turns.text_en: It's cold outside, right? How about a hot bowl of noodles? We have beef noodles and egg noodles.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Order your noodles.

options.text_zh: 我要一碗牛肉面，谢谢！
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yào yī wǎn niúròu miàn, xièxie!
options.text_en: I'll have a bowl of beef noodles, thanks!
options.feedback: Orders from his menu and thanks him -- he noticed you were cold and offered warmth.

options.text_zh: 牛肉面吧。
options.text_pinyin: Niúròu miàn ba.
options.text_en: Beef noodles, I guess.
options.feedback: Picks a choice but sounds halfhearted -- he warmly welcomed you in from the cold.

options.text_zh: 哪个好吃？
options.text_pinyin: Nǎge hǎochī?
options.text_en: Which one is good?
options.feedback: He offered two options and you asked which is better -- he owns the shop, both are his. Just pick one.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好的！很快。你第一次来我们这里吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Hǎo de! Hěn kuài. Nǐ dì yī cì lái wǒmen zhèlǐ ma?
turns.text_en: Okay! It'll be quick. Is this your first time here?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell him about your visit.

options.text_zh: 是第一次。朋友说这里的面很好吃。
options.text_pinyin: Shì dì yī cì. Péngyou shuō zhèlǐ de miàn hěn hǎochī.
options.text_en: Yes, first time. A friend said the noodles here are really good.
options.feedback: Answers honestly and pays a compliment -- a friend's recommendation will make the owner proud.

options.text_zh: 对，第一次来。
options.text_pinyin: Duì, dì yī cì lái.
options.text_en: Yes, first time.
options.feedback: Confirms but doesn't add anything -- he's making friendly conversation and this doesn't give much back.

options.text_zh: 你这个店开了多久了？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhège diàn kāi le duō jiǔ le?
options.text_en: How long has your shop been open?
options.feedback: He asked if this is your first time and you asked about his business history -- answer his question first.

tree.cultural_note: Tiny noodle shops (面馆) are the soul of Chinese street food culture. Owners often chat with customers like old friends. Coming in from the cold for a bowl of hot noodles is one of life's simple comforts.

tree.setup: Late at night, you step onto your balcony. Your neighbor is also on theirs, looking up at the sky.
tree.setup_zh: 很晚了，你走到阳台上。你的邻居也在阳台上，看着天空。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 今天晚上的星星好多啊。你也看到了吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Jīntiān wǎnshang de xīngxing hǎo duō a. Nǐ yě kàn dào le ma?
turns.text_en: There are so many stars tonight. Can you see them too?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share your observation.

options.text_zh: 看到了！好漂亮。平时看不到这么多。
options.text_pinyin: Kàn dào le! Hǎo piàoliang. Píngshí kàn bú dào zhème duō.
options.text_en: I see them! So beautiful. Usually you can't see this many.
options.feedback: Responds to the rare starry night with wonder -- both of you noticed something special at the same time.

options.text_zh: 是啊，很漂亮。
options.text_pinyin: Shì a, hěn piàoliang.
options.text_en: Yes, very beautiful.
options.feedback: Agrees but doesn't share the sense of rarity -- she pointed out how many there are tonight specifically.

options.text_zh: 你也没睡觉吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yě méi shuìjiào ma?
options.text_en: You can't sleep either?
options.feedback: She's excited about the stars and you assumed she's up because of insomnia -- reframes a beautiful moment as a problem.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 是啊。我小时候在老家，每天晚上都能看到。在城市里很难看到。
turns.text_pinyin: Shì a. Wǒ xiǎo shíhou zài lǎojiā, měi tiān wǎnshang dōu néng kàn dào. Zài chéngshì lǐ hěn nán kàn dào.
turns.text_en: Yes. When I was a child in my hometown, I could see them every night. In the city it's hard to see them.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to their memory.

options.text_zh: 你的老家一定很美。今晚很特别。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de lǎojiā yīdìng hěn měi. Jīn wǎn hěn tèbié.
options.text_en: Your hometown must be beautiful. Tonight is special.
options.feedback: Honors her memory of home and brings the conversation back to this moment you're sharing together.

options.text_zh: 在城市里确实很难看到。
options.text_pinyin: Zài chéngshì lǐ quèshí hěn nán kàn dào.
options.text_en: It really is hard to see them in the city.
options.feedback: Agrees with her point but doesn't respond to the personal memory she shared about her hometown.

options.text_zh: 你老家在哪里？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ lǎojiā zài nǎlǐ?
options.text_en: Where's your hometown?
options.feedback: She shared a tender childhood memory about stars at home, and you asked for the location -- a factual question when she was being emotional.

tree.cultural_note: Light pollution in Chinese cities makes starry nights rare. When the stars do appear, they stir nostalgia for rural hometowns (老家) where the sky was always clear. These balcony conversations between neighbors are fleeting but meaningful.

tree.setup: You discover a small second-hand bookshop in a quiet alley. The owner is reading behind the counter.
tree.setup_zh: 你在一条安静的小巷里发现了一家二手书店。老板在柜台后面看书。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 欢迎！随便看看。你喜欢看什么样的书？
turns.text_pinyin: Huānyíng! Suíbiàn kànkan. Nǐ xǐhuan kàn shénme yàng de shū?
turns.text_en: Welcome! Feel free to browse. What kind of books do you like?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell him what kind of books interest you.

options.text_zh: 我喜欢看故事书。有没有比较简单的中文小说？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuan kàn gùshi shū. Yǒu méiyǒu bǐjiào jiǎndān de Zhōngwén xiǎoshuō?
options.text_en: I like storybooks. Do you have any relatively simple Chinese novels?
options.feedback: Honest about your level — opens a helpful conversation.

options.text_zh: 我只是随便看看。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ zhǐshì suíbiàn kànkan.
options.text_en: I'm just browsing.
options.feedback: He invited you to browse — but also asked what you like. This dodges his question.

options.text_zh: 你们有英文书吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐmen yǒu Yīngwén shū ma?
options.text_en: Do you have English books?
options.feedback: On-topic, but misses the chance to practice — he's asking about your Chinese reading interests.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 有的。这本不错，是一个很温暖的故事。很多外国朋友都喜欢。
turns.text_pinyin: Yǒu de. Zhè běn búcuò, shì yī ge hěn wēnnuǎn de gùshi. Hěn duō wàiguó péngyou dōu xǐhuan.
turns.text_en: Yes. This one is nice — it's a warm story. Many foreign friends like it.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his recommendation.

options.text_zh: 看起来很有意思。多少钱一本？
options.text_pinyin: Kàn qǐlái hěn yǒu yìsi. Duōshao qián yī běn?
options.text_en: It looks interesting. How much per copy?
options.feedback: Engaged — shows genuine interest.

options.text_zh: 我不想买东西。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bù xiǎng mǎi dōngxi.
options.text_en: I don't want to buy anything.
options.feedback: Dismissive when he's being helpful.

options.text_zh: 这本书看起来很旧了。有没有新的？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè běn shū kàn qǐlái hěn jiù le. Yǒu méiyǒu xīn de?
options.text_en: This book looks really old. Do you have a new one?
options.feedback: He pauses — it's a second-hand bookshop. That's the whole point.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这本只要十块钱。二手书便宜。你要是喜欢，下次来我帮你找更多。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè běn zhǐ yào shí kuài qián. Èrshǒu shū piányi. Nǐ yàoshi xǐhuan, xià cì lái wǒ bāng nǐ zhǎo gèng duō.
turns.text_en: This one is only ten yuan. Second-hand books are cheap. If you like it, next time I'll find more for you.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Accept his kind offer.

options.text_zh: 太好了！我一定会再来。谢谢你推荐。
options.text_pinyin: Tài hǎo le! Wǒ yīdìng huì zài lái. Xièxie nǐ tuījiàn.
options.text_en: Great! I'll definitely come back. Thanks for the recommendation.
options.feedback: Warm and grateful — builds a connection.

options.text_zh: 好吧。我再想想。
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo ba. Wǒ zài xiǎngxiang.
options.text_en: Okay. Let me think about it.
options.feedback: Noncommittal — he just offered you a great deal and future help.

options.text_zh: 十块钱有点贵。能不能便宜一点？
options.text_pinyin: Shí kuài qián yǒudiǎn guì. Néng bù néng piányi yīdiǎn?
options.text_en: Ten yuan is a bit expensive. Can you make it cheaper?
options.feedback: Ten yuan for a book is already very cheap — haggling here misses the warmth of his offer.

tree.cultural_note: Second-hand bookshops (二手书店) are quiet havens in Chinese cities. Owners often curate personal collections and love recommending books. The low prices make reading accessible, and returning customers become friends.

title: Morning Tai Chi in the Park

tree.setup: You're jogging in the park and notice a group of elderly people doing tai chi. One of them waves you over.
tree.setup_zh: 你在公园跑步，看到一群老人在打太极拳。其中一位向你招手。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 年轻人，过来一起练练！太极拳对身体很好。
turns.text_pinyin: Niánqīng rén, guòlái yīqǐ liàn lian! Tàijí quán duì shēntǐ hěn hǎo.
turns.text_en: Young person, come practice with us! Tai chi is very good for the body.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the invitation.

options.text_zh: 好啊！但是我没学过，可以教我吗？
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo a! Dànshì wǒ méi xué guò, kěyǐ jiāo wǒ ma?
options.text_en: Sure! But I've never learned — can you teach me?
options.feedback: Open and enthusiastic — willing to try something new.

options.text_zh: 我跑步比较快。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ pǎobù bǐjiào kuài.
options.text_en: I run pretty fast.
options.feedback: He's inviting you to do tai chi, not run.

options.text_zh: 这个公园有厕所吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhège gōngyuán yǒu cèsuǒ ma?
options.text_en: Is there a restroom in this park?
options.feedback: Ignores his kind invitation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 当然可以！你跟着我做就行了。慢慢来，不着急。
turns.text_pinyin: Dāngrán kěyǐ! Nǐ gēnzhe wǒ zuò jiù xíng le. Mànmàn lái, bù zháojí.
turns.text_en: Of course! Just follow me. Take it slow, no rush.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Try and share how it feels.

options.text_zh: 感觉很放松。难怪你们每天都来。
options.text_pinyin: Gǎnjué hěn fàngsōng. Nánguài nǐmen měi tiān dōu lái.
options.text_en: It feels very relaxing. No wonder you all come every day.
options.feedback: Genuine observation — connects to their daily practice.

options.text_zh: 我觉得跑步更好。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde pǎobù gèng hǎo.
options.text_en: I think running is better.
options.feedback: Dismisses what they're sharing with you.

options.text_zh: 动作太慢了，我觉得没什么用。
options.text_pinyin: Dòngzuò tài màn le, wǒ juéde méi shénme yòng.
options.text_en: The movements are too slow — I don't think it's very useful.
options.feedback: He looks at you quietly, then back at the sky. The others keep moving without a word.

tree.cultural_note: Morning tai chi (太极拳) groups in parks are a beloved Chinese tradition. The elderly practitioners often welcome newcomers warmly. The phrase 慢慢来 (take it slow) reflects a core philosophy of patience in Chinese wellness culture.

tree.setup: Your landlady invites you to help make dumplings for the weekend. You sit together at the kitchen table.
tree.setup_zh: 房东阿姨邀请你周末一起包饺子。你们坐在厨房桌子旁。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你包过饺子吗？不难的，我教你。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ bāo guò jiǎozi ma? Bù nán de, wǒ jiāo nǐ.
turns.text_en: Have you ever made dumplings? It's not hard — I'll teach you.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell her about your experience with dumplings.

options.text_zh: 没包过，但是我很想学！看起来很有意思。
options.text_pinyin: Méi bāo guò, dànshì wǒ hěn xiǎng xué! Kàn qǐlái hěn yǒu yìsi.
options.text_en: Never made them, but I really want to learn! It looks fun.
options.feedback: She claps her hands once. "好！就知道你愿意学。来来来，坐这边。"

options.text_zh: 包过，不过我包的不好看。
options.text_pinyin: Bāo guò, búguò wǒ bāo de bù hǎokàn.
options.text_en: I have, but mine don't look very good.
options.feedback: She waves her hand like that doesn't matter. "好看不好看没关系，好吃就行！"

options.text_zh: 我不太会做饭。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bú tài huì zuò fàn.
options.text_en: I'm not very good at cooking.
options.feedback: She looks a little deflated, then pushes the dough toward you anyway. "没关系，饺子不算做饭。你就帮我捏就行。"

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看，先把馅放在皮上，然后这样捏。你试试。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn, xiān bǎ xiàn fàng zài pí shàng, ránhòu zhèyàng niē. Nǐ shìshi.
turns.text_en: See, first put the filling on the wrapper, then pinch like this. You try.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: You try making one. It doesn't look great. Say something.

options.text_zh: 我的饺子不太好看，但是很好玩！
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ de jiǎozi bú tài hǎokàn, dànshì hěn hǎowán!
options.text_en: My dumpling doesn't look great, but it's really fun!
options.feedback: She laughs and picks it up to inspect it. "哈！这个长得奇怪——不过没关系，煮熟了一样吃！"

options.text_zh: 好难啊，我不行。
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo nán a, wǒ bù xíng.
options.text_en: This is too hard, I can't do it.
options.feedback: She frowns and puts her hands over yours to guide them. "怎么不行？再试一次。看我怎么捏的。"

options.text_zh: 你包的比我好看多了。我应该看个视频学。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ bāo de bǐ wǒ hǎokàn duō le. Wǒ yīnggāi kàn ge shìpín xué.
options.text_en: Yours look way better than mine. I should watch a video to learn.
options.feedback: She waves her hand, a little hurt. "什么视频？我在这儿教你呢！跟我学就行了。"

tree.cultural_note: Making dumplings (包饺子) together is one of the most intimate domestic activities in Chinese life. It's often how neighbors and landlords bond with tenants. The imperfect shapes of a beginner's dumplings always bring laughter and warmth.

tree.setup: On a cold morning, you find a single knitted glove on a bench. A woman nearby seems to be looking for something.
tree.setup_zh: 一个冷天的早上，你在长椅上发现了一只手套。旁边有位女士好像在找什么。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你有没有看到一只红色的手套？我刚才坐在这里……
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu kàn dào yī zhī hóngsè de shǒutào? Wǒ gāngcái zuò zài zhèlǐ……
turns.text_en: Have you seen a red glove? I was sitting here just now...

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Help her find the glove.

options.text_zh: 是这只吗？我在长椅上看到的。
options.text_pinyin: Shì zhè zhī ma? Wǒ zài chángyǐ shàng kàn dào de.
options.text_en: Is it this one? I found it on the bench.
options.feedback: Helpful and direct — solves her problem immediately.

options.text_zh: 我觉得这个长椅上的东西不是手套。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde zhège chángyǐ shàng de dōngxi bú shì shǒutào.
options.text_en: I don't think this thing on the bench is a glove.
options.feedback: She looks confused — it's clearly a red glove sitting right there.

options.text_zh: 你要去哪里？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yào qù nǎlǐ?
options.text_en: Where are you going?
options.feedback: Doesn't help her find the glove.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 就是这只！太感谢了！这是我妈妈织的，对我很重要。
turns.text_pinyin: Jiù shì zhè zhī! Tài gǎnxiè le! Zhè shì wǒ māma zhī de, duì wǒ hěn zhòngyào.
turns.text_en: That's the one! Thank you so much! My mom knitted it — it's very important to me.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her gratitude.

options.text_zh: 太好了，找到就好。妈妈织的东西很珍贵。
options.text_pinyin: Tài hǎo le, zhǎo dào jiù hǎo. Māma zhī de dōngxi hěn zhēnguì.
options.text_en: Wonderful, glad you found it. Things mom made are precious.
options.feedback: Empathetic — recognizes the sentimental value.

options.text_zh: 下次你应该小心一点，别再丢了。
options.text_pinyin: Xià cì nǐ yīnggāi xiǎoxīn yīdiǎn, bié zài diū le.
options.text_en: Next time you should be more careful and not lose it again.
options.feedback: She nods slowly, smile fading a little — she was thanking you, not asking for advice.

options.text_zh: 我不喜欢红色。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bù xǐhuan hóngsè.
options.text_en: I don't like red.
options.feedback: Insensitive when she's emotional about her mom's glove.

tree.cultural_note: Handmade gifts from family carry deep sentimental value in Chinese culture. A knitted glove from a mother represents love and care. Finding and returning such items creates small but meaningful connections between strangers.

tree.setup: At a night market, you see an artist drawing portraits. He has an empty seat in front of him.
tree.setup_zh: 在夜市上，你看到一位画家在画人像。他面前有一个空位。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 来，坐下吧！我给你画一张，十五分钟就好。
turns.text_pinyin: Lái, zuò xià ba! Wǒ gěi nǐ huà yī zhāng, shíwǔ fēnzhōng jiù hǎo.
turns.text_en: Come, sit down! I'll draw you one — only fifteen minutes.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his offer.

options.text_zh: 好啊，我从来没有让人画过。要多少钱？
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo a, wǒ cónglái méiyǒu ràng rén huà guò. Yào duōshao qián?
options.text_en: Sure, I've never had anyone draw me before. How much?
options.feedback: Curious and practical — tries something new.

options.text_zh: 我不喜欢夜市。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bù xǐhuan yèshì.
options.text_en: I don't like night markets.
options.feedback: You're already at the night market.

options.text_zh: 你是哪里人？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ shì nǎlǐ rén?
options.text_en: Where are you from?
options.feedback: Doesn't respond to his offer to draw you.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 三十块。你别动，放轻松就好。你在中国住了多长时间了？
turns.text_pinyin: Sānshí kuài. Nǐ bié dòng, fàng qīngsōng jiù hǎo. Nǐ zài Zhōngguó zhù le duō cháng shíjiān le?
turns.text_en: Thirty yuan. Don't move — just relax. How long have you been living in China?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell him about your time in China while he draws.

options.text_zh: 差不多半年了。我越来越喜欢这里的生活。
options.text_pinyin: Chàbuduō bàn nián le. Wǒ yuè lái yuè xǐhuan zhèlǐ de shēnghuó.
options.text_en: About half a year. I like the life here more and more.
options.feedback: Reflective and genuine — shares a real feeling.

options.text_zh: 你画得快不快？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ huà de kuài bú kuài?
options.text_en: Do you draw fast?
options.feedback: He already said fifteen minutes.

options.text_zh: 我想吃烤串。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng chī kǎo chuàn.
options.text_en: I want to eat skewers.
options.feedback: He asked about your time in China.

tree.cultural_note: Night market portrait artists are a charming tradition. They capture a moment in time while conversation flows naturally. The casual intimacy of sitting still while a stranger draws you creates a unique connection.

tree.setup: You're at a small clinic with a minor cold. An elderly man next to you starts chatting.
tree.setup_zh: 你因为小感冒去了一家小诊所。旁边的一位老人开始跟你聊天。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你也感冒了吗？最近很多人都生病了。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ yě gǎnmào le ma? Zuìjìn hěn duō rén dōu shēngbìng le.
turns.text_en: You caught a cold too? A lot of people have been getting sick lately.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell him about how you feel.

options.text_zh: 是啊，有点咳嗽。不太严重，但是想让医生看看。
options.text_pinyin: Shì a, yǒudiǎn késou. Bú tài yánzhòng, dànshì xiǎng ràng yīshēng kànkan.
options.text_en: Yes, a bit of a cough. Not too serious, but I want the doctor to check.
options.feedback: Calm and reasonable — not overdramatic.

options.text_zh: 我不喜欢医院。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bù xǐhuan yīyuàn.
options.text_en: I don't like hospitals.
options.feedback: He asked about your symptoms.

options.text_zh: 你的衣服很好看。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de yīfu hěn hǎokàn.
options.text_en: Your clothes look nice.
options.feedback: A strange thing to say in a clinic waiting room.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 多喝热水，好好休息，很快就会好的。你一个人来的？
turns.text_pinyin: Duō hē rè shuǐ, hǎohāo xiūxi, hěn kuài jiù huì hǎo de. Nǐ yī ge rén lái de?
turns.text_en: Drink more hot water, rest well, and you'll recover soon. Did you come alone?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Answer his question.

options.text_zh: 是一个人来的。谢谢您的关心，我会多喝水的。
options.text_pinyin: Shì yī ge rén lái de. Xièxie nín de guānxīn, wǒ huì duō hē shuǐ de.
options.text_en: Yes, I came alone. Thank you for your concern — I'll drink more water.
options.feedback: Grateful and polite — accepts his care gracefully.

options.text_zh: 我不想喝水。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bù xiǎng hē shuǐ.
options.text_en: I don't want to drink water.
options.feedback: Dismisses his sincere advice.

options.text_zh: 这个诊所很小。
options.text_pinyin: Zhège zhěnsuǒ hěn xiǎo.
options.text_en: This clinic is very small.
options.feedback: He asked if you came alone.

tree.cultural_note: 「多喝热水」(drink more hot water) is the quintessential Chinese health advice, given for nearly every ailment. It reflects a deeply caring culture where strangers offer unsolicited but well-meaning health tips. Accepting the advice graciously is the polite response.

tree.setup: Your bicycle has a flat tire. You find a small repair shop run by an old man under a tree.
tree.setup_zh: 你的自行车轮胎没气了。你找到了一个老爷爷在树下开的修车铺。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 自行车坏了？让我看看。哦，是轮胎没气了，小问题。
turns.text_pinyin: Zìxíngchē huài le? Ràng wǒ kànkan. Ó, shì lúntāi méi qì le, xiǎo wèntí.
turns.text_en: Bike broken? Let me see. Oh, the tire is flat — small problem.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Ask about the repair.

options.text_zh: 太好了！修好要多长时间？
options.text_pinyin: Tài hǎo le! Xiū hǎo yào duō cháng shíjiān?
options.text_en: Great! How long will it take to fix?
options.feedback: Practical and relieved — gets to the point.

options.text_zh: 我想买一辆新的。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng mǎi yī liàng xīn de.
options.text_en: I want to buy a new one.
options.feedback: He just said it's a small problem.

options.text_zh: 你在这里多少年了？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zài zhèlǐ duōshao nián le?
options.text_en: How many years have you been here?
options.feedback: Your bike needs fixing first.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 十分钟就好。你坐这儿等一会儿。你是骑车上班吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Shí fēnzhōng jiù hǎo. Nǐ zuò zhèr děng yīhuǐr. Nǐ shì qí chē shàngbān ma?
turns.text_en: Ten minutes. Sit here and wait. Do you bike to work?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Chat while he fixes your bike.

options.text_zh: 对，每天骑车去。又方便又可以锻炼身体。
options.text_pinyin: Duì, měi tiān qí chē qù. Yòu fāngbiàn yòu kěyǐ duànliàn shēntǐ.
options.text_en: Yes, I bike every day. It's convenient and good exercise.
options.feedback: Friendly conversation — shares your routine naturally.

options.text_zh: 我不想等。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bù xiǎng děng.
options.text_en: I don't want to wait.
options.feedback: Impatient when he said only ten minutes.

options.text_zh: 你有孩子吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu háizi ma?
options.text_en: Do you have children?
options.feedback: He asked about your commute.

tree.cultural_note: Roadside bicycle repair shops (修车铺) are a disappearing but beloved part of Chinese urban life. Often run by elderly men under trees or awnings, they fix any bike cheaply and quickly. The chat while waiting is part of the experience.

tree.setup: A new café opened in your neighborhood. You go in to try it. The barista greets you warmly.
tree.setup_zh: 你的小区新开了一家咖啡店。你走进去尝尝。咖啡师热情地跟你打招呼。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 欢迎光临！我们刚开的。今天所有咖啡打八折。你想喝什么？
turns.text_pinyin: Huānyíng guānglín! Wǒmen gāng kāi de. Jīntiān suǒyǒu kāfēi dǎ bā zhé. Nǐ xiǎng hē shénme?
turns.text_en: Welcome! We just opened. All coffee is 20% off today. What would you like?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Order something.

options.text_zh: 那我要一杯热的拿铁。这个地方看起来很舒服。
options.text_pinyin: Nà wǒ yào yī bēi rè de ná tiě. Zhège dìfang kàn qǐlái hěn shūfu.
options.text_en: I'll have a hot latte then. This place looks really cozy.
options.feedback: Friendly and complimentary — good first impression.

options.text_zh: 以前这里是什么？
options.text_pinyin: Yǐqián zhèlǐ shì shénme?
options.text_en: What was here before?
options.feedback: She asked what you'd like to drink.

options.text_zh: 我不喜欢打折。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bù xǐhuan dǎzhé.
options.text_en: I don't like discounts.
options.feedback: An odd thing to say about a promotional offer.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好的！你是住在附近吗？希望你以后经常来。
turns.text_pinyin: Hǎo de! Nǐ shì zhù zài fùjìn ma? Xīwàng nǐ yǐhòu jīngcháng lái.
turns.text_en: Okay! Do you live nearby? I hope you'll come often.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell her about yourself.

options.text_zh: 对，我就住在旁边。走路两分钟就到了。以后一定常来。
options.text_pinyin: Duì, wǒ jiù zhù zài pángbiān. Zǒu lù liǎng fēnzhōng jiù dào le. Yǐhòu yīdìng cháng lái.
options.text_en: Yes, I live right next door. Two-minute walk. I'll definitely come often.
options.feedback: Warm and encouraging — supports a new local business.

options.text_zh: 你这里有WiFi吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhèlǐ yǒu WiFi ma?
options.text_en: Do you have WiFi?
options.feedback: She asked where you live and hoped you'd return.

options.text_zh: 我不太出门。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bú tài chūmén.
options.text_en: I don't go out much.
options.feedback: Contradicts the fact that you just came in.

tree.cultural_note: New neighborhood cafés in China work hard to build regular customers. The personal touch — chatting, discounts, remembering orders — makes them feel like extensions of home. 打八折 means 80% of the original price (20% off).

tree.setup: After dinner, you see a neighbor putting out food for stray cats in the courtyard. You stop to watch.
tree.setup_zh: 吃完晚饭后，你看到邻居在院子里给流浪猫放吃的。你停下来看。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你也喜欢猫吧？这些猫每天晚上都来。它们认识我了。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ yě xǐhuan māo ba? Zhèxiē māo měi tiān wǎnshang dōu lái. Tāmen rènshi wǒ le.
turns.text_en: You like cats too, right? These cats come every night. They know me now.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share your feelings about the cats.

options.text_zh: 它们看起来很信任你。你喂了多长时间了？
options.text_pinyin: Tāmen kàn qǐlái hěn xìnrèn nǐ. Nǐ wèi le duō cháng shíjiān le?
options.text_en: They seem to trust you a lot. How long have you been feeding them?
options.feedback: Observant and warm — notices the bond.

options.text_zh: 流浪猫很脏。
options.text_pinyin: Liúlàng māo hěn zāng.
options.text_en: Stray cats are dirty.
options.feedback: Insensitive to someone who cares for them.

options.text_zh: 你家有空调吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ jiā yǒu kōngtiáo ma?
options.text_en: Does your place have AC?
options.feedback: She's talking about the cats.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 快两年了。刚开始它们很怕人，现在看到我就跑过来。那只灰色的最亲人。
turns.text_pinyin: Kuài liǎng nián le. Gāng kāishǐ tāmen hěn pà rén, xiànzài kàn dào wǒ jiù pǎo guòlái. Nà zhī huīsè de zuì qīn rén.
turns.text_en: Almost two years. At first they were scared of people — now they run to me. The grey one is the friendliest.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her story.

options.text_zh: 两年了！你真有耐心。我可以帮你一起喂吗？
options.text_pinyin: Liǎng nián le! Nǐ zhēn yǒu nàixīn. Wǒ kěyǐ bāng nǐ yīqǐ wèi ma?
options.text_en: Two years! You're so patient. Can I help you feed them?
options.feedback: Admiring and willing to join — builds community.

options.text_zh: 我不想养猫。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bù xiǎng yǎng māo.
options.text_en: I don't want to keep a cat.
options.feedback: She's not asking you to adopt one.

options.text_zh: 明天我要上班。
options.text_pinyin: Míngtiān wǒ yào shàngbān.
options.text_en: I have work tomorrow.
options.feedback: Sounds like you want to leave.

tree.cultural_note: Community cat feeders (喂流浪猫的人) are unsung heroes of Chinese neighborhoods. Their patience transforms fearful strays into friendly companions. This quiet care reflects the iyashikei spirit — small acts of kindness that heal.

title: The School Gate at Pickup Time

tree.setup: You walk past a school gate at pickup time. A parent waiting outside starts chatting with you.
tree.setup_zh: 你路过一所学校的门口，正是放学时间。一位等孩子的家长跟你聊天。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你也来接孩子吗？还是路过？
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ yě lái jiē háizi ma? Háishi lùguò?
turns.text_en: Are you here to pick up a child too? Or just passing by?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Explain why you're here.

options.text_zh: 我路过的。这里每天都这么热闹吗？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ lùguò de. Zhèlǐ měi tiān dōu zhème rènao ma?
options.text_en: I'm just passing by. Is it this lively every day?
options.feedback: Friendly and curious — engages naturally.

options.text_zh: 我不认识你。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bú rènshi nǐ.
options.text_en: I don't know you.
options.feedback: Unfriendly response to a casual conversation.

options.text_zh: 这个学校好不好？
options.text_pinyin: Zhège xuéxiào hǎo bù hǎo?
options.text_en: Is this school good?
options.feedback: Doesn't answer whether you're picking up a child or passing by.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 每天都这样！孩子们出来的时候最开心了。你看，那边卖糖葫芦的老爷爷也每天都来。
turns.text_pinyin: Měi tiān dōu zhèyàng! Háizimen chūlái de shíhou zuì kāixīn le. Nǐ kàn, nàbiān mài tánghúlu de lǎoyéye yě měi tiān dōu lái.
turns.text_en: Every day! The kids are happiest when they come out. See, the old man selling candied hawthorns over there comes every day too.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: React to the scene.

options.text_zh: 真的很温馨。小时候我也很期待放学。
options.text_pinyin: Zhēn de hěn wēnxīn. Xiǎo shíhou wǒ yě hěn qīdài fàngxué.
options.text_en: It's really heartwarming. When I was little, I also looked forward to getting out of school.
options.feedback: Reflective — connects to a universal childhood feeling.

options.text_zh: 糖葫芦好吃吗？
options.text_pinyin: Tánghúlu hǎochī ma?
options.text_en: Are candied hawthorns tasty?
options.feedback: Misses the emotional beat of the moment.

options.text_zh: 我要走了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yào zǒu le.
options.text_en: I need to go.
options.feedback: Leaves abruptly during a warm moment.

tree.cultural_note: School pickup time (放学) is a daily ritual that transforms quiet streets into joyful scenes. Grandparents, parents, and snack vendors all converge. The tanghulu (糖葫芦) seller is an iconic figure — a thread of continuity across generations.

tree.setup: You're on a slow train through the countryside. The passenger across from you opens a conversation.
tree.setup_zh: 你坐慢车穿过乡下。对面的乘客跟你聊了起来。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你去哪里？这趟车走得很慢，不过可以看风景。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ qù nǎlǐ? Zhè tàng chē zǒu de hěn màn, búguò kěyǐ kàn fēngjǐng.
turns.text_en: Where are you going? This train is very slow, but you can see the scenery.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell him your destination and how you feel about the trip.

options.text_zh: 我去下一站。慢一点也好，窗外的风景真好看。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ qù xià yī zhàn. Màn yīdiǎn yě hǎo, chuāng wài de fēngjǐng zhēn hǎokàn.
options.text_en: I'm going to the next stop. Slow is fine — the scenery outside is beautiful.
options.feedback: Relaxed and present — enjoys the journey.

options.text_zh: 我不喜欢坐火车。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bù xǐhuan zuò huǒchē.
options.text_en: I don't like taking trains.
options.feedback: You're already on the train.

options.text_zh: 你的行李很多。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de xíngli hěn duō.
options.text_en: You have a lot of luggage.
options.feedback: A bit rude and doesn't answer his question.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 是啊，现在大家都坐高铁了。但是我觉得慢车有慢车的好。能看到很多小地方。
turns.text_pinyin: Shì a, xiànzài dàjiā dōu zuò gāotiě le. Dànshì wǒ juéde màn chē yǒu màn chē de hǎo. Néng kàn dào hěn duō xiǎo dìfang.
turns.text_en: Yeah, everyone takes the high-speed train now. But I think slow trains have their own charm. You can see lots of small places.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share your perspective on slow travel.

options.text_zh: 我也这样觉得。有时候慢一点，反而能看到更多。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yě zhèyàng juéde. Yǒu shíhou màn yīdiǎn, fǎn'ér néng kàn dào gèng duō.
options.text_en: I feel the same. Sometimes going slower, you actually see more.
options.feedback: — connects with his philosophy.

options.text_zh: 高铁比较贵。
options.text_pinyin: Gāotiě bǐjiào guì.
options.text_en: High-speed trains are more expensive.
options.feedback: He's talking about the charm of slow travel, not cost.

options.text_zh: 我想睡觉了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng shuìjiào le.
options.text_en: I want to sleep.
options.feedback: Ends the conversation abruptly.

tree.cultural_note: China's slow trains (慢车/绿皮车) are a nostalgic counterpoint to the high-speed rail network. They stop at small towns the bullet trains skip, carrying farmers, students, and travelers who prefer the unhurried pace. Conversations between strangers flow easily.

title: A Rainy Day at the Temple

tree.setup: You duck into a small Buddhist temple to escape the rain. A monk is sweeping the courtyard slowly, unbothered by the rain.
tree.setup_zh: 你躲进一座小寺庙避雨。一位僧人在院子里慢慢扫地，一点也不介意下雨。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 进来避雨吧。不着急，雨很快就停了。要不要喝杯茶？
turns.text_pinyin: Jìnlái bì yǔ ba. Bù zháojí, yǔ hěn kuài jiù tíng le. Yào bú yào hē bēi chá?
turns.text_en: Come in out of the rain. No rush — it'll stop soon. Would you like a cup of tea?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Accept his offer.

options.text_zh: 好的，谢谢您。这里好安静，我很喜欢。
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo de, xièxie nín. Zhèlǐ hǎo ānjìng, wǒ hěn xǐhuan.
options.text_en: Yes, thank you. It's so peaceful here — I really like it.
options.feedback: Gracious and reflective — appreciates the calm.

options.text_zh: 雨什么时候停？
options.text_pinyin: Yǔ shénme shíhou tíng?
options.text_en: When will the rain stop?
options.feedback: He just said no rush, and this sounds impatient.

options.text_zh: 附近有地铁站吗？
options.text_pinyin: Fùjìn yǒu dìtiě zhàn ma?
options.text_en: Is there a subway station nearby?
options.feedback: You just arrived and he's offering tea.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 雨天来寺庙的人不多。你平时喜欢去什么样的地方？
turns.text_pinyin: Yǔ tiān lái sìmiào de rén bù duō. Nǐ píngshí xǐhuan qù shénme yàng de dìfang?
turns.text_en: Not many people visit temples on rainy days. What kind of places do you usually like to go?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share what kind of places you enjoy.

options.text_zh: 我喜欢安静的地方。书店、公园……像这里一样的。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuan ānjìng de dìfang. Shūdiàn, gōngyuán…… xiàng zhèlǐ yīyàng de.
options.text_en: I like quiet places. Bookshops, parks... places like this.
options.feedback: Honest and resonant — fits the temple atmosphere.

options.text_zh: 我喜欢人多热闹的地方，比如商场。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuan rén duō rènao de dìfang, bǐrú shāngchǎng.
options.text_en: I like busy, lively places — like shopping malls.
options.feedback: He nods politely, but the temple falls very quiet for a moment.

options.text_zh: 我没有时间。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ méiyǒu shíjiān.
options.text_en: I don't have time.
options.feedback: You literally just sat down for tea.

tree.cultural_note: Buddhist temples welcome anyone seeking shelter or quiet. Monks often offer tea and conversation without any religious expectation. Rain amplifies the meditative quality of these spaces — the sound of rain on old tiles is its own kind of music.

tree.setup: Your neighbor has a small vegetable garden on the rooftop. She invites you up to see it.
tree.setup_zh: 你的邻居在楼顶有一个小菜园。她邀请你上去看看。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 快看，这些都是我自己种的。有西红柿、黄瓜，还有一些葱。
turns.text_pinyin: Kuài kàn, zhèxiē dōu shì wǒ zìjǐ zhǒng de. Yǒu xīhóngshì, huángguā, hái yǒu yīxiē cōng.
turns.text_en: Look, I grew all of these myself. Tomatoes, cucumbers, and some scallions.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: React to her garden.

options.text_zh: 太厉害了！你每天都要浇水吗？
options.text_pinyin: Tài lìhai le! Nǐ měi tiān dōu yào jiāo shuǐ ma?
options.text_en: That's amazing! Do you water them every day?
options.feedback: Admiring and curious — asks about her routine.

options.text_zh: 我不会种菜。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bú huì zhǒng cài.
options.text_en: I don't know how to grow vegetables.
options.feedback: Doesn't engage with what she's showing you.

options.text_zh: 楼顶好热。
options.text_pinyin: Lóudǐng hǎo rè.
options.text_en: The rooftop is very hot.
options.feedback: She's proudly showing her garden.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 差不多每天。你要不要拿一些回去？自己种的，没有农药，吃着放心。
turns.text_pinyin: Chàbuduō měi tiān. Nǐ yào bú yào ná yīxiē huíqù? Zìjǐ zhǒng de, méiyǒu nóngyào, chī zhe fàngxīn.
turns.text_en: Almost every day. Want to take some home? Home-grown, no pesticides — you can eat with peace of mind.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her generosity.

options.text_zh: 真的可以吗？太谢谢你了！我今晚用它做一道菜。
options.text_pinyin: Zhēn de kěyǐ ma? Tài xièxie nǐ le! Wǒ jīn wǎn yòng tā zuò yī dào cài.
options.text_en: Really? Thank you so much! I'll make a dish with them tonight.
options.feedback: Grateful and specific — shows you'll use the gift.

options.text_zh: 我一般在超市买菜。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yībān zài chāoshì mǎi cài.
options.text_en: I usually buy vegetables at the supermarket.
options.feedback: Dismisses her offer of fresh produce.

options.text_zh: 你种了多少年了？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhǒng le duōshao nián le?
options.text_en: How many years have you been growing?
options.feedback: She just offered you vegetables and you didn't respond.

tree.cultural_note: Rooftop gardens (楼顶菜园) are common in Chinese apartment buildings. Growing your own vegetables is a point of pride, and sharing the harvest with neighbors strengthens community bonds. 放心 (peace of mind) about food safety is a major value.

tree.setup: You need a passport photo. You find a small, old-fashioned photo studio run by an elderly couple.
tree.setup_zh: 你需要拍证件照。你找到了一家老式照相馆，老板是一对老夫妻。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 拍证件照？好，你坐这里。不要紧张，笑一笑。
turns.text_pinyin: Pāi zhèngjiàn zhào? Hǎo, nǐ zuò zhèlǐ. Bú yào jǐnzhāng, xiào yī xiào.
turns.text_en: Passport photo? Okay, sit here. Don't be nervous — smile a little.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: React to the photo session.

options.text_zh: 好的。我每次拍照都不太自然，不好意思。
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo de. Wǒ měi cì pāi zhào dōu bú tài zìrán, bù hǎo yìsi.
options.text_en: Okay. I'm always a bit unnatural in photos, sorry about that.
options.feedback: Honest and endearing — relatable admission.

options.text_zh: 你们这里有WiFi吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐmen zhèlǐ yǒu WiFi ma?
options.text_en: Do you have WiFi here?
options.feedback: You're about to have your photo taken.

options.text_zh: 外面的路很难找。
options.text_pinyin: Wàimiàn de lù hěn nán zhǎo.
options.text_en: The road outside was hard to find.
options.feedback: He's trying to help you pose.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 没关系，很多人都这样。你看旁边，对，就这样。好了！你要看看吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Méi guānxi, hěn duō rén dōu zhèyàng. Nǐ kàn pángbiān, duì, jiù zhèyàng. Hǎo le! Nǐ yào kànkan ma?
turns.text_en: No worries, lots of people are like that. Look to the side — yes, just like that. Done! Want to see?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Look at the photo and respond.

options.text_zh: 拍得真好！比我以前拍的好多了。谢谢您。
options.text_pinyin: Pāi de zhēn hǎo! Bǐ wǒ yǐqián pāi de hǎo duō le. Xièxie nín.
options.text_en: It came out great! Much better than ones I've had before. Thank you.
options.feedback: Genuine compliment — makes the photographer happy.

options.text_zh: 可以便宜一点吗？
options.text_pinyin: Kěyǐ piányi yīdiǎn ma?
options.text_en: Can it be cheaper?
options.feedback: He's showing you the result, not quoting a price.

options.text_zh: 我不太满意。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bú tài mǎnyì.
options.text_en: I'm not very satisfied.
options.feedback: Unlikely for such a kind interaction, and impolite.

tree.cultural_note: Old-fashioned photo studios (照相馆) are disappearing but still cherished. The owners' patience and warmth turn a mundane task into a human moment. Their skill with lighting often produces surprisingly good portraits.

tree.setup: You step outside early and find the neighborhood wrapped in fog. A security guard is at his post, drinking tea.
tree.setup_zh: 你一大早出门，发现整个小区都被雾笼罩了。保安在他的岗位上喝茶。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这么早出门？今天雾很大，走路小心点。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhème zǎo chūmén? Jīntiān wù hěn dà, zǒu lù xiǎoxīn diǎn.
turns.text_en: Out this early? The fog is heavy today — be careful walking.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his concern.

options.text_zh: 谢谢提醒。雾好大，对面都看不清。但是很漂亮。
options.text_pinyin: Xièxie tíxǐng. Wù hǎo dà, duìmiàn dōu kàn bù qīng. Dànshì hěn piàoliang.
options.text_en: Thanks for the reminder. The fog is so thick I can't see across the street. But it's beautiful.
options.feedback: Grateful and observant — finds beauty in the fog.

options.text_zh: 嗯，我知道。我看了天气预报，今天有雾。
options.text_pinyin: Ǹg, wǒ zhīdào. Wǒ kàn le tiānqì yùbào, jīntiān yǒu wù.
options.text_en: Yeah, I know. I checked the weather forecast — there's fog today.
options.feedback: He nods, but you just stated what he can already see. He was looking out for you.

options.text_zh: 你喝的是什么茶？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ hē de shì shénme chá?
options.text_en: What tea are you drinking?
options.feedback: He's warning you about the fog.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 是挺漂亮的。我在这里工作十几年了，每年冬天都有这样的雾。像住在云里一样。
turns.text_pinyin: Shì tǐng piàoliang de. Wǒ zài zhèlǐ gōngzuò shí jǐ nián le, měi nián dōngtiān dōu yǒu zhèyàng de wù. Xiàng zhù zài yún lǐ yīyàng.
turns.text_en: It is beautiful. I've worked here over ten years — every winter there's fog like this. Like living in the clouds.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his poetic observation.

options.text_zh: 「住在云里」，说得真好。您在这里一定看过很多风景。
options.text_pinyin: 「Zhù zài yún lǐ」, shuō de zhēn hǎo. Nín zài zhèlǐ yīdìng kàn guò hěn duō fēngjǐng.
options.text_en: Living in the clouds — beautifully put. You must have seen many sights here.
options.feedback: Appreciative — honors his words and experience.

options.text_zh: 十几年太长了。
options.text_pinyin: Shí jǐ nián tài cháng le.
options.text_en: Ten-plus years is too long.
options.feedback: Dismissive of his dedication.

options.text_zh: 我得走了，不然要迟到了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ děi zǒu le, bùrán yào chídào le.
options.text_en: I need to go or I'll be late.
options.feedback: Cuts short a lovely moment.

tree.cultural_note: Security guards (保安) are the quiet sentinels of Chinese neighborhoods. Often overlooked, they observe the seasons change from their posts year after year. Their observations can be surprisingly poetic — they see the neighborhood in ways residents miss.

tree.setup: You're studying at a café. A university student at the next table politely asks you a question.
tree.setup_zh: 你在咖啡店学习。旁边桌的大学生礼貌地问你一个问题。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 不好意思打扰你。你的中文书看起来很有意思。你也在学中文吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Bù hǎo yìsi dǎrǎo nǐ. Nǐ de Zhōngwén shū kàn qǐlái hěn yǒu yìsi. Nǐ yě zài xué Zhōngwén ma?
turns.text_en: Sorry to bother you. Your Chinese book looks interesting. Are you studying Chinese too?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell her about your studies.

options.text_zh: 是的，我在学中文。学了差不多半年了，还不太好。
options.text_pinyin: Shì de, wǒ zài xué Zhōngwén. Xué le chàbuduō bàn nián le, hái bú tài hǎo.
options.text_en: Yes, I'm studying Chinese. About half a year now — still not very good.
options.feedback: Humble and open — invites conversation.

options.text_zh: 这不是中文书。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè bú shì Zhōngwén shū.
options.text_en: This isn't a Chinese book.
options.feedback: The setup says it's a Chinese book.

options.text_zh: 中文太难了，我已经不想学了。
options.text_pinyin: Zhōngwén tài nán le, wǒ yǐjīng bù xiǎng xué le.
options.text_en: Chinese is too hard — I don't want to study it anymore.
options.feedback: She looks startled — she was just trying to make conversation, not hear you give up.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 半年已经很不错了！我也在写关于语言的作业。你觉得中文最难的是什么？
turns.text_pinyin: Bàn nián yǐjīng hěn búcuò le! Wǒ yě zài xiě guānyú yǔyán de zuòyè. Nǐ juéde Zhōngwén zuì nán de shì shénme?
turns.text_en: Half a year is already great! I'm writing a homework assignment about language too. What do you think is the hardest part of Chinese?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share what you find most challenging.

options.text_zh: 声调最难。有时候我说的，别人听不懂。
options.text_pinyin: Shēngdiào zuì nán. Yǒu shíhou wǒ shuō de, biérén tīng bù dǒng.
options.text_en: Tones are the hardest. Sometimes people can't understand what I say.
options.feedback: Honest and specific — a relatable language-learning struggle.

options.text_zh: 都不难。
options.text_pinyin: Dōu bù nán.
options.text_en: None of it is hard.
options.feedback: Not believable and shuts down conversation.

options.text_zh: 你的作业什么时候交？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de zuòyè shénme shíhou jiāo?
options.text_en: When is your homework due?
options.feedback: Deflects her question.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese university students are often curious about foreigners learning their language. These café encounters lead to genuine exchanges about the challenges and joys of language learning. Mutual vulnerability about learning creates fast bonds.

tree.setup: The elevator in your apartment building is broken. You meet your neighbor on the stairs, both of you climbing with groceries.
tree.setup_zh: 你住的楼的电梯坏了。你在楼梯上遇到邻居，你们都提着菜。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 电梯又坏了！我住十二楼，每次都累死了。你住几楼？
turns.text_pinyin: Diàntī yòu huài le! Wǒ zhù shí'èr lóu, měi cì dōu lèi sǐ le. Nǐ zhù jǐ lóu?
turns.text_en: The elevator's broken again! I live on the twelfth floor — I'm exhausted every time. What floor do you live on?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell her your floor and commiserate.

options.text_zh: 我住八楼。也够累的了。你提的东西好多。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ zhù bā lóu. Yě gòu lèi de le. Nǐ tí de dōngxi hǎo duō.
options.text_en: I'm on the eighth floor. That's tiring enough. You've got a lot of stuff.
options.feedback: Empathetic — acknowledges shared struggle.

options.text_zh: 我喜欢爬楼梯。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuan pá lóutī.
options.text_en: I like climbing stairs.
options.feedback: Dismisses her complaint in an annoying way.

options.text_zh: 你买了什么菜？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ mǎi le shénme cài?
options.text_en: What vegetables did you buy?
options.feedback: She's talking about the broken elevator problem.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 没办法，只能慢慢爬。你要不要我帮你拿一个袋子？
turns.text_pinyin: Méi bànfǎ, zhǐ néng mànmàn pá. Nǐ yào bú yào wǒ bāng nǐ ná yī ge dàizi?
turns.text_en: Nothing we can do — just climb slowly. Want me to carry one of your bags?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her kind offer.

options.text_zh: 不用不用，我还好。我们一起慢慢走吧。
options.text_pinyin: Bú yòng bú yòng, wǒ hái hǎo. Wǒmen yīqǐ mànmàn zǒu ba.
options.text_en: No no, I'm fine. Let's walk up slowly together.
options.feedback: Considerate — declines but suggests togetherness.

options.text_zh: 你应该搬到低楼层。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi bān dào dī lóucéng.
options.text_en: You should move to a lower floor.
options.feedback: Unhelpful advice when she just offered to help you.

options.text_zh: 电梯什么时候修好？
options.text_pinyin: Diàntī shénme shíhou xiū hǎo?
options.text_en: When will the elevator be fixed?
options.feedback: She can't answer that, and she just offered to help you.

tree.cultural_note: Broken elevators in Chinese apartment buildings create unexpected neighborly moments. The shared inconvenience bonds people — carrying groceries up many flights together turns strangers into friends. 没办法 (nothing we can do) is a resigned but warm acceptance.

tree.setup: You visit a flea market and find a vendor selling old photographs. He picks one up and tells you about it.
tree.setup_zh: 你去了一个跳蚤市场，看到一位摊主在卖老照片。他拿起一张跟你说。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看这张照片，是八十年代的。那时候这条街完全不一样。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn zhè zhāng zhàopiàn, shì bāshí niándài de. Nà shíhou zhè tiáo jiē wánquán bù yīyàng.
turns.text_en: Look at this photo — it's from the 1980s. This street looked completely different back then.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Show interest in the photo.

options.text_zh: 真的吗？看起来很不一样。那时候这里有什么？
options.text_pinyin: Zhēn de ma? Kàn qǐlái hěn bù yīyàng. Nà shíhou zhèlǐ yǒu shénme?
options.text_en: Really? It looks so different. What was here back then?
options.feedback: Curious and engaged — invites a story.

options.text_zh: 照片的颜色不太好了，太旧了。
options.text_pinyin: Zhàopiàn de yánsè bú tài hǎo le, tài jiù le.
options.text_en: The colors in the photo have faded — it's too old.
options.feedback: He looks down at the photo, then back at you. That's the whole beauty of it.

options.text_zh: 我不喜欢老东西。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bù xǐhuan lǎo dōngxi.
options.text_en: I don't like old things.
options.feedback: Rude at a vintage market.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 那时候都是小平房，门口有大树。到了晚上大家就搬椅子出来聊天。现在都是高楼了。
turns.text_pinyin: Nà shíhou dōu shì xiǎo píngfáng, ménkǒu yǒu dà shù. Dào le wǎnshang dàjiā jiù bān yǐzi chūlái liáotiān. Xiànzài dōu shì gāolóu le.
turns.text_en: Back then it was all small houses with big trees out front. In the evenings, everyone brought chairs out to chat. Now it's all high-rises.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his memory.

options.text_zh: 听起来那时候邻居之间更亲近。我想看看更多的老照片。
options.text_pinyin: Tīng qǐlái nà shíhou línjū zhī jiān gèng qīnjìn. Wǒ xiǎng kànkan gèng duō de lǎo zhàopiàn.
options.text_en: It sounds like neighbors were closer back then. I'd like to see more old photos.
options.feedback: — perceives the loss and wants to learn more.

options.text_zh: 现在的房子更大更方便，应该比以前好吧。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài de fángzi gèng dà gèng fāngbiàn, yīnggāi bǐ yǐqián hǎo ba.
options.text_en: Houses now are bigger and more convenient — that's probably better than before, right?
options.feedback: He sets the photo down gently. You answered the surface, not the feeling underneath.

options.text_zh: 我要走了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yào zǒu le.
options.text_en: I need to go.
options.feedback: Leaves abruptly during his story.

tree.cultural_note: China's rapid urbanization transformed neighborhoods within a generation. Old photographs at flea markets preserve memories of a slower pace — small houses, shared courtyards, and evening conversations under trees. These images carry deep nostalgia.

tree.setup: Late afternoon, you notice a man fixing a broken streetlight on your block. You stop to watch.
tree.setup_zh: 傍晚时分，你看到一个人在你家这条街修坏了的路灯。你停下来看。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这个灯坏了好几天了，今天终于来修了。你住在这条街上吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Zhège dēng huài le hǎo jǐ tiān le, jīntiān zhōngyú lái xiū le. Nǐ zhù zài zhè tiáo jiē shàng ma?
turns.text_en: This light has been broken for days — finally came to fix it today. Do you live on this street?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell him you've noticed the broken light.

options.text_zh: 对，我住这里。我也注意到了，晚上这里特别黑。
options.text_pinyin: Duì, wǒ zhù zhèlǐ. Wǒ yě zhùyì dào le, wǎnshang zhèlǐ tèbié hēi.
options.text_en: Yes, I live here. I noticed too — it's really dark here at night.
options.feedback: Observant — validates his work.

options.text_zh: 你修了多长时间了？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ xiū le duō cháng shíjiān le?
options.text_en: How long have you been fixing it?
options.feedback: He just started and asked if you live here.

options.text_zh: 我住在这里，但是我平时不太注意路灯。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ zhù zài zhèlǐ, dànshì wǒ píngshí bú tài zhùyì lùdēng.
options.text_en: I live here, but I don't usually pay attention to the streetlights.
options.feedback: He glances up from his work — honest, but you did notice, and the prompt asked you to say so.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 是啊，路灯很重要。大家回家的路上需要光。这个工作不起眼，但是我觉得有意思。
turns.text_pinyin: Shì a, lùdēng hěn zhòngyào. Dàjiā huí jiā de lù shàng xūyào guāng. Zhège gōngzuò bù qǐyǎn, dànshì wǒ juéde yǒu yìsi.
turns.text_en: Yeah, streetlights are important. People need light on their way home. This job isn't glamorous, but I find it meaningful.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his perspective on his work.

options.text_zh: 我觉得这个工作很了不起。谢谢你让我们回家的路亮起来。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde zhège gōngzuò hěn liǎobuqǐ. Xièxie nǐ ràng wǒmen huí jiā de lù liàng qǐlái.
options.text_en: I think this work is admirable. Thank you for lighting our way home.
options.feedback: Sincere — recognizes the value of unseen labor.

options.text_zh: 你工资高不高？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ gōngzī gāo bù gāo?
options.text_en: Is your salary high?
options.feedback: This reduces his heartfelt words to money.

options.text_zh: 我不怕黑。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bú pà hēi.
options.text_en: I'm not afraid of the dark.
options.feedback: Misses his point entirely.

tree.cultural_note: The people who maintain urban infrastructure — streetlight repairers, plumbers, electricians — are often invisible. In the iyashikei spirit, recognizing the dignity and meaning in 'small' work transforms how we see our neighborhoods.

tree.setup: At the post office, the clerk notices you received a letter from abroad. She chats with you while you sign.
tree.setup_zh: 在邮局，工作人员发现你收到了一封国外的信。她一边让你签名一边跟你聊。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 国外来的信！现在很少有人寄信了。是家人寄的吧？
turns.text_pinyin: Guówài lái de xìn! Xiànzài hěn shǎo yǒu rén jì xìn le. Shì jiārén jì de ba?
turns.text_en: A letter from abroad! Very few people send letters anymore. From family, right?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell her about the letter.

options.text_zh: 是我妈妈寄的。她不太会用手机，喜欢写信。
options.text_pinyin: Shì wǒ māma jì de. Tā bú tài huì yòng shǒujī, xǐhuan xiě xìn.
options.text_en: It's from my mom. She's not great with phones — she likes writing letters.
options.feedback: Warm and personal — shares a sweet detail.

options.text_zh: 我不知道是谁寄的。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bù zhīdào shì shéi jì de.
options.text_en: I don't know who sent it.
options.feedback: You can see who it's from on the envelope.

options.text_zh: 寄信很贵吗？
options.text_pinyin: Jì xìn hěn guì ma?
options.text_en: Is it expensive to send a letter?
options.feedback: She asked who sent it.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你妈妈真好。我妈妈以前也喜欢给我写信。收到信的感觉和收到消息不一样。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ māma zhēn hǎo. Wǒ māma yǐqián yě xǐhuan gěi wǒ xiě xìn. Shōu dào xìn de gǎnjué hé shōu dào xiāoxi bù yīyàng.
turns.text_en: Your mom is so sweet. My mom used to write me letters too. Getting a letter feels different from getting a message.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Agree and share your feelings.

options.text_zh: 对，信是慢的，但是更有温度。拿在手里感觉不一样。
options.text_pinyin: Duì, xìn shì màn de, dànshì gèng yǒu wēndù. Ná zài shǒu lǐ gǎnjué bù yīyàng.
options.text_en: Yes, letters are slow, but they have more warmth. Holding them in your hand feels different.
options.feedback: And genuine — captures the magic of handwritten letters.

options.text_zh: 对，信比较慢。现在发消息更快更方便。
options.text_pinyin: Duì, xìn bǐjiào màn. Xiànzài fā xiāoxi gèng kuài gèng fāngbiàn.
options.text_en: Yeah, letters are slow. Sending messages now is faster and more convenient.
options.feedback: She pauses, letter still in her hand. You agreed with the fact but missed the feeling.

options.text_zh: 你们邮局几点关门？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐmen yóujú jǐ diǎn guānmén?
options.text_en: What time does the post office close?
options.feedback: She's sharing a personal memory.

tree.cultural_note: Handwritten letters carry emotional weight that digital messages cannot replicate. In China, where WeChat dominates communication, receiving a physical letter is increasingly rare and precious. Post office clerks often notice and appreciate these moments.

tree.setup: On a windy afternoon, you walk to a hill where a grandfather is flying a kite with his granddaughter.
tree.setup_zh: 一个有风的下午，你走到一个小山坡，看到一位爷爷在和孙女放风筝。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你也来放风筝吗？今天的风很好！
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ yě lái fàng fēngzhēng ma? Jīntiān de fēng hěn hǎo!
turns.text_en: Are you here to fly kites too? The wind is great today!

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell him why you're here.

options.text_zh: 我来散步的。你们的风筝飞得好高！
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ lái sànbù de. Nǐmen de fēngzhēng fēi de hǎo gāo!
options.text_en: I'm here for a walk. Your kite is flying so high!
options.feedback: Friendly and observant — compliments their kite.

options.text_zh: 我没有风筝。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ méiyǒu fēngzhēng.
options.text_en: I don't have a kite.
options.feedback: He asked if you're here to fly kites, and a simple no would be better.

options.text_zh: 这个山不高。
options.text_pinyin: Zhège shān bù gāo.
options.text_en: This hill isn't very high.
options.feedback: Irrelevant to the conversation about kites.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这个风筝是我自己做的。小时候我爷爷教我做的。现在我教我孙女。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhège fēngzhēng shì wǒ zìjǐ zuò de. Xiǎo shíhou wǒ yéye jiāo wǒ zuò de. Xiànzài wǒ jiāo wǒ sūnnǚ.
turns.text_en: I made this kite myself. My grandfather taught me when I was little. Now I'm teaching my granddaughter.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the generational story.

options.text_zh: 三代人传下来的，真好。她一定很开心。
options.text_pinyin: Sān dài rén chuán xiàlái de, zhēn hǎo. Tā yīdìng hěn kāixīn.
options.text_en: Three generations passing it down — that's wonderful. She must be so happy.
options.feedback: Moved — recognizes the beauty of the tradition.

options.text_zh: 买一个比较方便。
options.text_pinyin: Mǎi yī ge bǐjiào fāngbiàn.
options.text_en: Buying one is more convenient.
options.feedback: The grandfather glances at his granddaughter, then back at you — a quiet pause where the warmth of the moment slips away.

options.text_zh: 我该回去了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ gāi huíqù le.
options.text_en: I should head back.
options.feedback: Leaves during a touching story.

tree.cultural_note: Kite flying is an ancient Chinese tradition dating back over 2,000 years. Hand-making kites and teaching the craft to grandchildren represents the passage of knowledge and love between generations. Windy hills are gathering places for this timeless activity.

tree.setup: You're hanging laundry on the shared balcony. Your neighbor comes out with her basket too.
tree.setup_zh: 你在共用阳台上晾衣服。你的邻居也端着一盆衣服出来了。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 今天阳光真好，正好晾衣服。你来中国以后习惯了吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Jīntiān yángguāng zhēn hǎo, zhènghǎo liàng yīfu. Nǐ lái Zhōngguó yǐhòu xíguàn le ma?
turns.text_en: Great sunshine today — perfect for drying clothes. Have you gotten used to life in China?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share how you've adjusted.

options.text_zh: 越来越习惯了。以前在家我都用烘干机，现在觉得晾衣服也很好。
options.text_pinyin: Yuè lái yuè xíguàn le. Yǐqián zài jiā wǒ dōu yòng hōnggān jī, xiànzài juéde liàng yīfu yě hěn hǎo.
options.text_en: More and more used to it. Back home I always used a dryer, but now I think air-drying is nice too.
options.feedback: Open and reflective — shows genuine adaptation.

options.text_zh: 我不喜欢晾衣服。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bù xǐhuan liàng yīfu.
options.text_en: I don't like hanging laundry.
options.feedback: She asked a broader question about adjusting.

options.text_zh: 你的衣服很多。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de yīfu hěn duō.
options.text_en: You have a lot of clothes.
options.feedback: An odd observation that ignores her question.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 是吧？阳光晒过的衣服有一种味道，很好闻。我觉得这是最简单的幸福。
turns.text_pinyin: Shì ba? Yángguāng shài guò de yīfu yǒu yī zhǒng wèidào, hěn hǎo wén. Wǒ juéde zhè shì zuì jiǎndān de xìngfú.
turns.text_en: Right? Sun-dried clothes have a certain smell — very pleasant. I think it's the simplest kind of happiness.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her observation about simple happiness.

options.text_zh: 你说得真好。我也开始喜欢这些小事了。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ shuō de zhēn hǎo. Wǒ yě kāishǐ xǐhuan zhèxiē xiǎo shì le.
options.text_en: Beautifully said. I'm starting to love these little things too.
options.feedback: Genuine — embraces the small joys of daily life.

options.text_zh: 烘干机更快。
options.text_pinyin: Hōnggān jī gèng kuài.
options.text_en: A dryer is faster.
options.feedback: Misses her point about simple pleasures.

options.text_zh: 我要去买菜了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yào qù mǎi cài le.
options.text_en: I need to go buy vegetables.
options.feedback: Leaves during a meaningful moment.

tree.cultural_note: Air-drying laundry in the sun is a universal practice in China. Shared balcony time creates natural neighbor interactions. The smell of sun-dried clothes (阳光的味道) is often described as one of life's simplest and purest pleasures.

tree.setup: You stop at a small breakfast stall on your way to class. The owner is steaming baozi and the whole street smells wonderful.
tree.setup_zh: 你上课路上在一个小早餐摊停下来。老板正在蒸包子，整条街都飘着香味。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 早啊！今天的包子刚蒸好，热的。你想吃什么馅的？
turns.text_pinyin: Zǎo a! Jīntiān de bāozi gāng zhēng hǎo, rè de. Nǐ xiǎng chī shénme xiàn de?
turns.text_en: Morning! Today's baozi just finished steaming, nice and hot. What filling would you like?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Order something and respond to her warmth.

options.text_zh: 早上好！给我两个菜包子吧。每天早上闻到这个香味，一天都会很开心。
options.text_pinyin: Zǎoshang hǎo! Gěi wǒ liǎng ge cài bāozi ba. Měi tiān zǎoshang wén dào zhège xiāngwèi, yī tiān dōu huì hěn kāixīn.
options.text_en: Good morning! Two vegetable baozi please. Smelling this every morning makes the whole day feel happy.
options.feedback: Warm and appreciative — connects a simple smell to daily joy.

options.text_zh: 给我一个吧。我赶时间，能快一点吗？
options.text_pinyin: Gěi wǒ yī ge ba. Wǒ gǎn shíjiān, néng kuài yīdiǎn ma?
options.text_en: Give me one. I'm in a hurry — can you be quick?
options.feedback: She hands it over with a smaller smile. You ordered, but rushed right past her warmth.

options.text_zh: 我不太饿。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bú tài è.
options.text_en: I'm not that hungry.
options.feedback: You stopped at the stall, so this doesn't make sense.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 是啊，我每天早上四点就起来做包子了。虽然很累，但是看到大家吃得开心，我也开心。
turns.text_pinyin: Shì a, wǒ měi tiān zǎoshang sì diǎn jiù qǐlái zuò bāozi le. Suīrán hěn lèi, dànshì kàn dào dàjiā chī de kāixīn, wǒ yě kāixīn.
turns.text_en: Yeah, I get up at four every morning to make them. It's tiring, but seeing everyone enjoy their food makes me happy too.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her dedication.

options.text_zh: 四点啊！你真的很辛苦。谢谢你，因为有你，我们每天早上都有这么好吃的早餐。
options.text_pinyin: Sì diǎn a! Nǐ zhēn de hěn xīnkǔ. Xièxie nǐ, yīnwèi yǒu nǐ, wǒmen měi tiān zǎoshang dōu yǒu zhème hǎochī de zǎocān.
options.text_en: Four o'clock! You really work hard. Thank you — because of you, we have such delicious breakfast every morning.
options.feedback: Genuine gratitude — sees and honors her effort.

options.text_zh: 你应该多休息。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi duō xiūxi.
options.text_en: You should rest more.
options.feedback: Unsolicited advice rather than appreciation.

options.text_zh: 你有没有肉包子？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu ròu bāozi?
options.text_en: Do you have meat baozi?
options.feedback: Ignores her personal sharing to ask about menu.

tree.cultural_note: Breakfast stalls (早餐摊) are a cornerstone of Chinese morning life. Vendors often start work before dawn, and their regulars form a quiet community of familiar faces. The steam rising from bamboo baskets in the cool morning air is one of China's most iconic everyday scenes.

tree.setup: You're sitting on a bench in the park reading. An elderly man sits down next to you with a thermos of tea.
tree.setup_zh: 你坐在公园的长椅上看书。一位老人拿着保温杯坐在你旁边。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 年轻人，你在看什么书？现在很少看到有人在公园看书了。
turns.text_pinyin: Niánqīng rén, nǐ zài kàn shénme shū? Xiànzài hěn shǎo kàn dào yǒu rén zài gōngyuán kàn shū le.
turns.text_en: Young person, what are you reading? It's rare to see someone reading in the park these days.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell him about your book and why you like reading here.

options.text_zh: 我在看一本中文书。在公园看书很舒服，有风，有树，很安静。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ zài kàn yī běn Zhōngwén shū. Zài gōngyuán kàn shū hěn shūfu, yǒu fēng, yǒu shù, hěn ānjìng.
options.text_en: I'm reading a Chinese book. Reading in the park is so comfortable — there's wind, trees, and quiet.
options.feedback: Simple and peaceful — captures why the park is a good place to read.

options.text_zh: 这本书是我朋友给我的。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè běn shū shì wǒ péngyou gěi wǒ de.
options.text_en: This book was given to me by a friend.
options.feedback: Doesn't answer what the book is or why you're reading here.

options.text_zh: 因为家里太热了。
options.text_pinyin: Yīnwèi jiālǐ tài rè le.
options.text_en: Because it's too hot at home.
options.feedback: A practical complaint rather than genuine connection.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 是啊，公园是个好地方。我每天都来这里坐坐，喝喝茶，看看大家。这样的日子很好。
turns.text_pinyin: Shì a, gōngyuán shì ge hǎo dìfang. Wǒ měi tiān dōu lái zhèlǐ zuòzuo, hēhe chá, kànkan dàjiā. Zhèyàng de rìzi hěn hǎo.
turns.text_en: Yes, the park is a good place. I come here every day to sit, drink tea, and watch people. Days like this are good.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his contentment with simple things.

options.text_zh: 我觉得你说得对。有时候最简单的事情是最好的——喝茶，看书，听鸟叫。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde nǐ shuō de duì. Yǒu shíhou zuì jiǎndān de shìqing shì zuì hǎo de — hē chá, kàn shū, tīng niǎo jiào.
options.text_en: I think you're right. Sometimes the simplest things are the best — drinking tea, reading, listening to birds.
options.feedback: Meets him in the stillness — affirms that simple pleasures are enough.

options.text_zh: 你不觉得无聊吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ bù juéde wúliáo ma?
options.text_en: Don't you get bored?
options.feedback: Projects restlessness onto his peaceful routine.

options.text_zh: 这个公园很大吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhège gōngyuán hěn dà ma?
options.text_en: Is this park big?
options.feedback: A factual question that misses the emotional moment.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese parks serve as communal living rooms, especially for retirees. Carrying a thermos (保温杯) of tea is standard for older men who spend mornings in the park. The phrase 这样的日子很好 captures a philosophy of contentment with the ordinary that runs deep in Chinese daily life.

tree.setup: It's raining outside. You and your roommate are sitting by the window drinking tea and watching the rain.
tree.setup_zh: 外面下雨了。你和你的室友坐在窗边喝茶，看着窗外的雨。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 下雨天哪儿也去不了。不过我觉得下雨天待在家里也很好。
turns.text_pinyin: Xià yǔ tiān nǎr yě qù bù liǎo. Búguò wǒ juéde xià yǔ tiān dāi zài jiālǐ yě hěn hǎo.
turns.text_en: Can't go anywhere on a rainy day. But I think staying home on rainy days is nice too.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share what you enjoy about rainy days.

options.text_zh: 我也喜欢下雨天。听雨的声音，喝热茶，很舒服。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yě xǐhuan xià yǔ tiān. Tīng yǔ de shēngyīn, hē rè chá, hěn shūfu.
options.text_en: I like rainy days too. Listening to the rain, drinking hot tea — it's comfortable.
options.feedback: Peaceful and present — finds comfort in the rain.

options.text_zh: 我想出去玩。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng chūqù wán.
options.text_en: I want to go out and have fun.
options.feedback: Rejects the cozy rainy-day mood.

options.text_zh: 明天会不会下雨？
options.text_pinyin: Míngtiān huì bú huì xià yǔ?
options.text_en: Will it rain tomorrow?
options.feedback: Jumps to weather forecasting instead of enjoying the moment.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对，就是这种感觉。小时候下雨，我妈妈会给我做热汤。现在一下雨我就想喝汤。
turns.text_pinyin: Duì, jiù shì zhè zhǒng gǎnjué. Xiǎo shíhou xià yǔ, wǒ māma huì gěi wǒ zuò rè tāng. Xiànzài yī xià yǔ wǒ jiù xiǎng hē tāng.
turns.text_en: Exactly, that feeling. When it rained as a kid, my mom would make me hot soup. Now whenever it rains, I crave soup.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her memory about rain and soup.

options.text_zh: 这真好。有些味道和记忆是一起的。你妈妈做的什么汤？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè zhēn hǎo. Yǒuxiē wèidào hé jìyì shì yīqǐ de. Nǐ māma zuò de shénme tāng?
options.text_en: That's lovely. Some tastes and memories go together. What soup did your mom make?
options.feedback: Gentle and curious — honors the connection between food and memory.

options.text_zh: 我不太喜欢喝汤。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bú tài xǐhuan hē tāng.
options.text_en: I don't really like soup.
options.feedback: Dismisses her fond memory with a personal preference.

options.text_zh: 你妈妈在哪里？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ māma zài nǎlǐ?
options.text_en: Where is your mom?
options.feedback: Too direct; could touch on something sensitive.

tree.cultural_note: Rainy days in China often evoke feelings of nostalgia and coziness. Hot soup (热汤) is closely associated with maternal care. The connection between weather, food, and childhood memories is a common thread in Chinese daily conversation, where sensory experiences carry deep emotional weight.

tree.setup: You're walking along the canal path when a friendly older woman on a bicycle slows down beside you.
tree.setup_zh: 你沿着河边小路走的时候，一位骑自行车的阿姨在你旁边慢了下来。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你好！你也喜欢在这里散步吗？这条路很漂亮，对不对？
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ hǎo! Nǐ yě xǐhuan zài zhèlǐ sànbù ma? Zhè tiáo lù hěn piàoliang, duì bú duì?
turns.text_en: Hello! Do you like walking here too? This path is beautiful, isn't it?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share what you like about this path.

options.text_zh: 是的，我很喜欢这里。河边的树很好看，走着走着心情就好了。
options.text_pinyin: Shì de, wǒ hěn xǐhuan zhèlǐ. Hé biān de shù hěn hǎokàn, zǒu zhe zǒu zhe xīnqíng jiù hǎo le.
options.text_en: Yes, I love it here. The trees by the river are beautiful — walking here just lifts my mood.
options.feedback: Natural and heartfelt — walking as a gentle remedy.

options.text_zh: 我只是路过。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ zhǐ shì lùguò.
options.text_en: I'm just passing through.
options.feedback: Shuts down the friendly conversation.

options.text_zh: 你的自行车很好看。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de zìxíngchē hěn hǎokàn.
options.text_en: Your bicycle is nice.
options.feedback: Comments on her bike instead of the scenery she mentioned.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对！我每天下午都骑车来这里。风吹在脸上，听到水的声音，比什么都好。
turns.text_pinyin: Duì! Wǒ měi tiān xiàwǔ dōu qí chē lái zhèlǐ. Fēng chuī zài liǎn shàng, tīng dào shuǐ de shēngyīn, bǐ shénme dōu hǎo.
turns.text_en: Right! I ride here every afternoon. Wind on my face, the sound of water — better than anything.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her love of the outdoors.

options.text_zh: 风和水的声音——这就够了，对吗？我觉得最好的东西都是不要钱的。
options.text_pinyin: Fēng hé shuǐ de shēngyīn — zhè jiù gòu le, duì ma? Wǒ juéde zuì hǎo de dōngxi dōu shì bú yào qián de.
options.text_en: Wind and the sound of water — that's enough, right? I think the best things are free.
options.feedback: Captures the iyashikei spirit — the best things cost nothing.

options.text_zh: 你不怕晒黑吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ bú pà shài hēi ma?
options.text_en: Aren't you afraid of getting tan?
options.feedback: Introduces a worry into a joyful moment.

options.text_zh: 骑车比走路快。
options.text_pinyin: Qí chē bǐ zǒu lù kuài.
options.text_en: Biking is faster than walking.
options.feedback: Speed isn't the point of her ride.

tree.cultural_note: Cycling along rivers and canals remains a beloved daily ritual in many Chinese cities. The phrase 比什么都好 (better than anything) is a common way to express that simple sensory experiences — wind, water, sunlight — surpass material comforts.

tree.setup: You pass a small flower stall near the subway exit. The seller, a middle-aged woman, is arranging sunflowers in a bucket.
tree.setup_zh: 你在地铁出口旁边经过一个小花摊。卖花的阿姨正在把向日葵放进桶里。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 看看花吧！今天的向日葵特别好看。买一束回家，家里就会很亮。
turns.text_pinyin: Kànkan huā ba! Jīntiān de xiàngrìkuí tèbié hǎokàn. Mǎi yī shù huí jiā, jiālǐ jiù huì hěn liàng.
turns.text_en: Come look at the flowers! Today's sunflowers are especially beautiful. Buy a bunch and your home will light right up.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her enthusiasm about the flowers.

options.text_zh: 真的很好看！它们的颜色好像太阳一样。给我一束吧。
options.text_pinyin: Zhēn de hěn hǎokàn! Tāmen de yánsè hǎoxiàng tàiyáng yīyàng. Gěi wǒ yī shù ba.
options.text_en: They really are beautiful! Their color is just like the sun. I'll take a bunch.
options.feedback: Warm and present — lets the flowers brighten the moment.

options.text_zh: 好看是好看，不过花放几天就死了，有点浪费。
options.text_pinyin: Hǎokàn shì hǎokàn, búguò huā fàng jǐ tiān jiù sǐ le, yǒudiǎn làngfèi.
options.text_en: They are pretty, but flowers die in a few days — seems like a waste.
options.feedback: She stops arranging and looks at you. The sunflowers are right there, bright as ever.

options.text_zh: 我家没有花瓶。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā méiyǒu huāpíng.
options.text_en: I don't have a vase at home.
options.feedback: A practical objection that kills the gentle moment.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 是吧？我每天看着这些花，心情都很好。有花的日子和没有花的日子，是不一样的。
turns.text_pinyin: Shì ba? Wǒ měi tiān kàn zhe zhèxiē huā, xīnqíng dōu hěn hǎo. Yǒu huā de rìzi hé méiyǒu huā de rìzi, shì bù yīyàng de.
turns.text_en: Right? Looking at these flowers every day puts me in a good mood. Days with flowers and days without — they're different.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her philosophy about flowers and daily life.

options.text_zh: 可不是嘛。一束花就能让一个普通的日子变得特别。谢谢你每天在这里卖花。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ shuō de duì. Yī shù huā jiù néng ràng yī ge pǔtōng de rìzi biàn de tèbié. Xièxie nǐ měi tiān zài zhèlǐ mài huā.
options.text_en: You're right. A bunch of flowers can make an ordinary day special. Thank you for being here selling flowers every day.
options.feedback: Affirms her quiet role in beautifying ordinary life.

options.text_zh: 向日葵能放几天？
options.text_pinyin: Xiàngrìkuí néng fàng jǐ tiān?
options.text_en: How many days do sunflowers last?
options.feedback: A practical question that ignores her poetic observation.

options.text_zh: 卖花赚钱吗？
options.text_pinyin: Mài huā zhuàn qián ma?
options.text_en: Do you make money selling flowers?
options.feedback: Intrusive question about her income.

tree.cultural_note: Small flower stalls near subway exits and street corners are common in Chinese cities. Many sellers develop a personal philosophy about flowers and daily life. The idea that 有花的日子 (days with flowers) are qualitatively different reflects a widespread Chinese appreciation for bringing natural beauty into urban living.

tree.setup: You're strolling through a small night market. A noodle seller waves at you from behind his steaming pot.
tree.setup_zh: 你在一个小夜市散步。一个卖面条的大叔在冒着蒸汽的锅后面朝你招手。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 来一碗面条吧！天冷了，吃碗热面最舒服了。
turns.text_pinyin: Lái yī wǎn miàntiáo ba! Tiān lěng le, chī wǎn rè miàn zuì shūfu le.
turns.text_en: Come have a bowl of noodles! It's getting cold — a hot bowl of noodles is the most comfortable thing.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his friendly invitation.

options.text_zh: 好啊！闻起来好香。在这里吃面条，看着灯笼，感觉很温暖。
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo a! Wén qǐlái hǎo xiāng. Zài zhèlǐ chī miàntiáo, kàn zhe dēnglong, gǎnjué hěn wēnnuǎn.
options.text_en: Sure! It smells wonderful. Eating noodles here, looking at the lanterns — it feels so warm.
options.feedback: Present and appreciative — embraces the night market atmosphere.

options.text_zh: 你的面条辣不辣？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de miàntiáo là bú là?
options.text_en: Are your noodles spicy?
options.feedback: A functional question that skips his warmth.

options.text_zh: 好啊。你们的面条跟外面饭店的一样吗？
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo a. Nǐmen de miàntiáo gēn wàimiàn fàndiàn de yīyàng ma?
options.text_en: Sure. Are your noodles the same as at regular restaurants?
options.feedback: He stirs the pot a bit slower. He's been here twenty years — this isn't a chain restaurant.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我在这里卖面条卖了二十年了。以前夜市很大，现在小了很多。但是还是有人来，我就还在。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ zài zhèlǐ mài miàntiáo mài le èrshí nián le. Yǐqián yèshì hěn dà, xiànzài xiǎo le hěn duō. Dànshì háishi yǒu rén lái, wǒ jiù hái zài.
turns.text_en: I've been selling noodles here for twenty years. The night market used to be much bigger, but it's gotten smaller. As long as people come, I'm still here.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his quiet perseverance.

options.text_zh: 二十年了，那这里一定有很多你的回忆。我觉得有你在，这个夜市就是温暖的。
options.text_pinyin: Èrshí nián le, nà zhèlǐ yīdìng yǒu hěn duō nǐ de huíyì. Wǒ juéde yǒu nǐ zài, zhège yèshì jiù shì wēnnuǎn de.
options.text_en: Twenty years — this place must be full of your memories. I think as long as you're here, this night market is warm.
options.feedback: Honors his decades of presence — he is the warmth of the market.

options.text_zh: 为什么夜市变小了？
options.text_pinyin: Wèishéme yèshì biàn xiǎo le?
options.text_en: Why did the night market get smaller?
options.feedback: Investigates decline instead of honoring his staying.

options.text_zh: 你想过换个工作吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ xiǎng guò huàn ge gōngzuò ma?
options.text_en: Have you thought about changing jobs?
options.feedback: Suggests leaving when he's expressing commitment.

tree.cultural_note: Night markets (夜市) have been shrinking in many Chinese cities due to urban development, but loyal vendors remain. The phrase 还是有人来，我就还在 captures a quiet stubbornness and devotion that defines many small business owners who see their stalls as places of human connection, not just commerce.

tree.setup: It suddenly starts raining as you leave the library. A classmate you've seen before but never talked to offers to share her umbrella.
tree.setup_zh: 你离开图书馆的时候突然下起了雨。一个你见过但没说过话的同学让你一起用她的伞。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你没带伞吗？我们一起走吧，我的伞够大的。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ méi dài sǎn ma? Wǒmen yīqǐ zǒu ba, wǒ de sǎn gòu dà de.
turns.text_en: You didn't bring an umbrella? Let's walk together — mine is big enough.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Accept her offer warmly.

options.text_zh: 谢谢你！真的太好了。我今天没想到会下雨。你去哪个方向？
options.text_pinyin: Xièxie nǐ! Zhēn de tài hǎo le. Wǒ jīntiān méi xiǎng dào huì xià yǔ. Nǐ qù nǎge fāngxiàng?
options.text_en: Thank you! That's so kind. I didn't expect rain today. Which direction are you heading?
options.feedback: Grateful and natural — opens the door to conversation.

options.text_zh: 没关系，我可以跑回去。
options.text_pinyin: Méi guānxi, wǒ kěyǐ pǎo huíqù.
options.text_en: It's fine, I can run back.
options.feedback: Declines a kind offer unnecessarily.

options.text_zh: 雨会停吗？
options.text_pinyin: Yǔ huì tíng ma?
options.text_en: Will the rain stop?
options.feedback: Avoids engaging with her gesture.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我也往南走。你是不是经常来图书馆？我好像总能看到你。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ yě wǎng nán zǒu. Nǐ shì bú shì jīngcháng lái túshūguǎn? Wǒ hǎoxiàng zǒng néng kàn dào nǐ.
turns.text_en: I'm heading south too. Do you come to the library often? I feel like I always see you there.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share why you spend time at the library.

options.text_zh: 对，我很喜欢那里。图书馆很安静，在那里学习的时候，时间过得特别快。你也经常去吗？
options.text_pinyin: Duì, wǒ hěn xǐhuan nàlǐ. Túshūguǎn hěn ānjìng, zài nàlǐ xuéxí de shíhou, shíjiān guò de tèbié kuài. Nǐ yě jīngcháng qù ma?
options.text_en: Yes, I love it there. The library is so quiet — when I study there, time flies. Do you go often too?
options.feedback: Open and reciprocal — builds the friendship naturally.

options.text_zh: 是啊，我要考试。
options.text_pinyin: Shì a, wǒ yào kǎoshì.
options.text_en: Yeah, I have exams.
options.feedback: This reduces the library to exam prep, misses the connection.

options.text_zh: 是啊，我去图书馆是因为那里有WiFi。
options.text_pinyin: Shì a, wǒ qù túshūguǎn shì yīnwèi nàlǐ yǒu WiFi.
options.text_en: Yeah, I go to the library because they have WiFi there.
options.feedback: She nods but the conversation stalls — she was hoping to connect, not hear about WiFi.

tree.cultural_note: Sharing an umbrella (共伞) is one of the most natural and common small kindnesses in China. It often becomes the starting point for friendships. The phrase 好像总能看到你 is a gentle way of saying 'I've noticed you' without being intrusive — a very Chinese way to acknowledge a quiet familiarity.

tree.setup: You're helping your landlord clean out a storage room. She finds an old photograph and pauses, smiling.
tree.setup_zh: 你在帮房东打扫储物间。她找到了一张老照片，停下来，微笑着。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看，这是二十年前的照片。这是我和我丈夫刚搬到这里的时候。那时候这里还是新的。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn, zhè shì èrshí nián qián de zhàopiàn. Zhè shì wǒ hé wǒ zhàngfu gāng bān dào zhèlǐ de shíhou. Nà shíhou zhèlǐ háishi xīn de.
turns.text_en: Look, this is a photo from twenty years ago. This is when my husband and I first moved here. Everything was still new then.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her memory with care.

options.text_zh: 你们看起来好开心啊。二十年了，这个家一定有很多故事。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐmen kàn qǐlái hǎo kāixīn a. Èrshí nián le, zhège jiā yīdìng yǒu hěn duō gùshì.
options.text_en: You both look so happy. Twenty years — this home must have so many stories.
options.feedback: Tender and interested — sees the life within the walls.

options.text_zh: 这个房间真的很乱。
options.text_pinyin: Zhège fángjiān zhēn de hěn luàn.
options.text_en: This room is really messy.
options.feedback: Ignores her emotional moment to comment on the mess.

options.text_zh: 照片在哪里拍的？
options.text_pinyin: Zhàopiàn zài nǎlǐ pāi de?
options.text_en: Where was the photo taken?
options.feedback: A factual question that misses the warmth of the moment.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 是啊，很多故事。孩子在这里长大，过年在这里吃饭。虽然房子旧了，但是我不想搬走。
turns.text_pinyin: Shì a, hěn duō gùshì. Háizi zài zhèlǐ zhǎng dà, guò nián zài zhèlǐ chī fàn. Suīrán fángzi jiù le, dànshì wǒ bù xiǎng bān zǒu.
turns.text_en: Yes, many stories. The children grew up here, we had New Year's dinner here. The house is old now, but I don't want to move.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to why she wants to stay.

options.text_zh: 我觉得老房子有一种温暖，新房子没有的。因为这里有你们的生活。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde lǎo fángzi yǒu yī zhǒng wēnnuǎn, xīn fángzi méiyǒu de. Yīnwèi zhèlǐ yǒu nǐmen de shēnghuó.
options.text_en: I think old houses have a warmth that new ones don't. Because your life is here.
