options.feedback: Understands that a home is made of memories, not materials.

options.text_zh: 新房子会更舒服。
options.text_pinyin: Xīn fángzi huì gèng shūfu.
options.text_en: A new house would be more comfortable.
options.feedback: Misses why she values this place.

options.text_zh: 这个房子多大？
options.text_pinyin: Zhège fángzi duō dà?
options.text_en: How big is this house?
options.feedback: A practical question during a sentimental moment.

tree.cultural_note: In China, homes carry deep emotional weight. Many older people resist moving even when offered newer apartments because their homes hold decades of family memories. The phrase 虽然旧了但不想搬走 captures a common sentiment: the wear of a home is not deterioration but evidence of a life fully lived within it.

tree.setup: You walk into a small tea shop and notice a cat sleeping on a chair by the window. The shop owner smiles as she sees you looking at it.
tree.setup_zh: 你走进一家小茶馆，看到一只猫在窗边的椅子上睡觉。老板娘看到你在看猫，笑了。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 那是小橘。它每天都在那里睡觉。你可以坐它旁边，它不怕人的。
turns.text_pinyin: Nà shì Xiǎo Jú. Tā měi tiān dōu zài nàlǐ shuìjiào. Nǐ kěyǐ zuò tā pángbiān, tā bú pà rén de.
turns.text_en: That's Xiao Ju. She sleeps there every day. You can sit next to her — she's not afraid of people.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the cat and the tea shop atmosphere.

options.text_zh: 它好可爱！在这里喝茶，旁边有只猫，感觉特别放松。
options.text_pinyin: Tā hǎo kě'ài! Zài zhèlǐ hē chá, pángbiān yǒu zhī māo, gǎnjué tèbié fàngsōng.
options.text_en: She's so cute! Drinking tea here with a cat beside me — it feels especially relaxing.
options.feedback: Warm and present — embraces the tea-and-cat serenity.

options.text_zh: 猫毛会不会掉到茶里？
options.text_pinyin: Māo máo huì bú huì diào dào chá lǐ?
options.text_en: Won't cat hair fall into the tea?
options.feedback: A hygiene worry that kills the charm.

options.text_zh: 你们这里有什么茶？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐmen zhèlǐ yǒu shénme chá?
options.text_en: What teas do you have here?
options.feedback: Jumps to the menu without acknowledging the cat.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 它来的时候还是一只小小的流浪猫。现在它觉得这里是它的家了。客人都很喜欢它。
turns.text_pinyin: Tā lái de shíhou háishi yī zhī xiǎo xiǎo de liúlàng māo. Xiànzài tā juéde zhèlǐ shì tā de jiā le. Kèren dōu hěn xǐhuan tā.
turns.text_en: When she arrived she was a tiny stray. Now she thinks this is her home. All the customers love her.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the cat's story.

options.text_zh: 它找到了一个好家。我觉得它在这里很幸福——有人喝茶，有阳光，还有你照顾它。
options.text_pinyin: Tā zhǎo dào le yī ge hǎo jiā. Wǒ juéde tā zài zhèlǐ hěn xìngfú — yǒu rén hē chá, yǒu yángguāng, hái yǒu nǐ zhàogù tā.
options.text_en: She found a good home. I think she's happy here — people drinking tea, sunlight, and you taking care of her.
options.feedback: Sees the fullness of a simple life — tea, sun, care.

options.text_zh: 流浪猫很多，你不能都收养啊。
options.text_pinyin: Liúlàng māo hěn duō, nǐ bù néng dōu shōuyǎng a.
options.text_en: There are lots of strays — you can't adopt them all.
options.feedback: Introduces a problem when she's sharing something gentle.

options.text_zh: 它吃什么？
options.text_pinyin: Tā chī shénme?
options.text_en: What does she eat?
options.feedback: A practical detail that misses the emotional story.

tree.cultural_note: Cats in Chinese tea shops and bookstores are beloved fixtures. The concept of a stray finding a permanent home in a small shop resonates deeply. 小橘 (little orange) is one of the most popular names for orange tabby cats in China, and shop cats often become minor local celebrities.

tree.setup: It's Sunday morning. Your neighbor is watering plants on her balcony. You step out onto yours with a cup of coffee.
tree.setup_zh: 星期天早上。你的邻居在阳台上给植物浇水。你端着一杯咖啡走到自己的阳台上。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 早啊！今天不用上班，真好。你看，我的花开了！
turns.text_pinyin: Zǎo a! Jīntiān bú yòng shàng bān, zhēn hǎo. Nǐ kàn, wǒ de huā kāi le!
turns.text_en: Morning! No work today — so nice. Look, my flowers bloomed!

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her excitement about the flowers.

options.text_zh: 真漂亮！粉色的花，太好看了。你种了多长时间了？
options.text_pinyin: Zhēn piàoliang! Fěnsè de huā, tài hǎokàn le. Nǐ zhǒng le duō cháng shíjiān le?
options.text_en: So beautiful! Pink flowers — gorgeous. How long have you been growing them?
options.feedback: Shares her excitement genuinely — shows real interest.

options.text_zh: 我不会种花。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bú huì zhǒng huā.
options.text_en: I don't know how to grow flowers.
options.feedback: Turns her joy into your limitation.

options.text_zh: 今天天气怎么样？
options.text_pinyin: Jīntiān tiānqì zěnmeyàng?
options.text_en: How's the weather today?
options.feedback: Generic weather talk that ignores her blooming flowers.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 三个月了！每天浇水，等啊等，终于开了。有时候等待也是一种快乐。
turns.text_pinyin: Sān ge yuè le! Měi tiān jiāo shuǐ, děng a děng, zhōngyú kāi le. Yǒu shíhou děngdài yě shì yī zhǒng kuàilè.
turns.text_en: Three months! Watering every day, waiting and waiting, and finally they bloomed. Sometimes waiting is a kind of joy too.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her idea about waiting.

options.text_zh: 等待也是快乐——我觉得你说得太好了。慢慢来的东西，最后都是最美的。
options.text_pinyin: Děngdài yě shì kuàilè — wǒ juéde nǐ shuō de tài hǎo le. Mànman lái de dōngxi, zuìhòu dōu shì zuì měi de.
options.text_en: Waiting is joy — I think that's beautifully said. Things that come slowly are always the most beautiful in the end.
options.feedback: Affirms the beauty of patience — slow is beautiful.

options.text_zh: 三个月太久了吧。
options.text_pinyin: Sān ge yuè tài jiǔ le ba.
options.text_en: Three months is too long.
options.feedback: Dismisses her patience as a waste.

options.text_zh: 你种的是什么花？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhǒng de shì shénme huā?
options.text_en: What kind of flowers are they?
options.feedback: Asks for a label when she's sharing a philosophy.

tree.cultural_note: Balcony gardening (阳台种花) is a cherished hobby in Chinese apartment living. The patient tending of plants mirrors a deeper cultural value: that worthwhile things require daily care and unhurried time. Sunday mornings, free from work, are when these small rituals shine brightest.

tree.setup: You're on the bus going home. An older woman sitting next to you notices you looking out the window at the sunset.
tree.setup_zh: 你坐公交车回家。旁边的阿姨看到你一直在看窗外的夕阳。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 今天的夕阳真好看。你是不是也在看？
turns.text_pinyin: Jīntiān de xīyáng zhēn hǎokàn. Nǐ shì bú shì yě zài kàn?
turns.text_en: Today's sunset is really beautiful. Are you watching it too?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share your feelings about the sunset.

options.text_zh: 是的，太漂亮了。每天都有夕阳，但是每天都不一样。今天的颜色特别好看。
options.text_pinyin: Shì de, tài piàoliang le. Měi tiān dōu yǒu xīyáng, dànshì měi tiān dōu bù yīyàng. Jīntiān de yánsè tèbié hǎokàn.
options.text_en: Yes, it's so beautiful. There's a sunset every day, but each one is different. Today's colors are especially lovely.
options.feedback: Noticing that each day's sunset is unique — an eye for the ordinary.

options.text_zh: 我在看手机。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ zài kàn shǒujī.
options.text_en: I'm looking at my phone.
options.feedback: Denies a shared moment of beauty.

options.text_zh: 还有几站到？
options.text_pinyin: Hái yǒu jǐ zhàn dào?
options.text_en: How many more stops?
options.feedback: Bus logistics during a sunset moment.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对，每天都不一样。我女儿总是说我「每天都拍夕阳」。但是我觉得，好看的东西就应该记下来。
turns.text_pinyin: Duì, měi tiān dōu bù yīyàng. Wǒ nǚ'ér zǒng shì shuō wǒ 'měi tiān dōu pāi xīyáng'. Dànshì wǒ juéde, hǎokàn de dōngxi jiù yīnggāi jì xiàlái.
turns.text_en: Right, every day is different. My daughter always says I 'take sunset photos every day.' But I think beautiful things should be recorded.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her habit of photographing sunsets.

options.text_zh: 我觉得这个习惯很好。以后你可以看这些照片，想起今天，想起每一个美好的傍晚。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde zhège xíguàn hěn hǎo. Yǐhòu nǐ kěyǐ kàn zhèxiē zhàopiàn, xiǎng qǐ jīntiān, xiǎng qǐ měi yī ge měihǎo de bàngwǎn.
options.text_en: I think that's a wonderful habit. Later you can look at those photos and remember today — remember every beautiful evening.
options.feedback: Validates her small ritual — every photo is a saved evening.

options.text_zh: 手机拍的不好看。
options.text_pinyin: Shǒujī pāi de bù hǎokàn.
options.text_en: Phone photos don't look good.
options.feedback: Critiques her tool instead of understanding the meaning.

options.text_zh: 你女儿多大了？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ nǚ'ér duō dà le?
options.text_en: How old is your daughter?
options.feedback: Changes subject away from the sunset conversation.

tree.cultural_note: Older Chinese women often develop deeply personal rituals around beauty — photographing sunsets, pressing flowers, saving autumn leaves. The gentle teasing from children (每天都拍夕阳) is affectionate, not dismissive. These daily recordings of beauty represent a form of quiet gratitude for ordinary life.

tree.setup: You're eating congee at a small shop on a cold morning. The owner notices you warming your hands on the bowl.
tree.setup_zh: 天冷的早上，你在一家小店喝粥。老板看到你用碗暖手。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 冷吧？粥要慢慢喝，不要急。热粥暖肚子，也暖心。
turns.text_pinyin: Lěng ba? Zhōu yào mànman hē, bú yào jí. Rè zhōu nuǎn dùzi, yě nuǎn xīn.
turns.text_en: Cold, right? Drink the congee slowly, no rush. Hot congee warms the belly and the heart.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his kind words.

options.text_zh: 谢谢大叔。这碗粥真的很暖和。冷天喝一碗热粥，觉得什么都好了。
options.text_pinyin: Xièxie dàshū. Zhè wǎn zhōu zhēn de hěn nuǎnhuo. Lěng tiān hē yī wǎn rè zhōu, juéde shénme dōu hǎo le.
options.text_en: Thank you, uncle. This congee is really warming. A bowl of hot congee on a cold day — everything feels better.
options.feedback: Simple gratitude — a bowl of congee as a remedy for everything.

options.text_zh: 这个粥里有什么？
options.text_pinyin: Zhège zhōu lǐ yǒu shénme?
options.text_en: What's in this congee?
options.feedback: Ingredient check instead of appreciating his care.

options.text_zh: 今天零下几度了。
options.text_pinyin: Jīntiān líng xià jǐ dù le.
options.text_en: It's below zero today.
options.feedback: A temperature report instead of warmth.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我妈妈以前也是这样说的——「冷了就喝粥」。粥不贵，也不复杂，但是能让人舒服。简单的东西最好。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ māma yǐqián yě shì zhèyàng shuō de — 'lěng le jiù hē zhōu'. Zhōu bú guì, yě bù fùzá, dànshì néng ràng rén shūfu. Jiǎndān de dōngxi zuì hǎo.
turns.text_en: My mom used to say the same thing — 'When it's cold, drink congee.' Congee isn't expensive or complicated, but it makes you feel good. Simple things are the best.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his philosophy about simplicity.

options.text_zh: 你妈妈说得对。最简单的东西，给人最多的温暖。你的粥也是这样。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ māma shuō de duì. Zuì jiǎndān de dōngxi, gěi rén zuì duō de wēnnuǎn. Nǐ de zhōu yě shì zhèyàng.
options.text_en: Your mom was right. The simplest things give the most warmth. Your congee is just like that.
options.feedback: Connects his mother's wisdom to his present work — his congee carries her warmth.

options.text_zh: 你每天都这么早开门吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ měi tiān dōu zhème zǎo kāi mén ma?
options.text_en: Do you open this early every day?
options.feedback: Business hours question during a warm moment.

options.text_zh: 我还想要一碗。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ hái xiǎng yào yī wǎn.
options.text_en: I want another bowl.
options.feedback: Ordering more food instead of connecting.

tree.cultural_note: Congee (粥) is one of China's most essential comfort foods, often the first thing served to someone who is cold, sick, or tired. The phrase 冷了就喝粥 is maternal wisdom passed through generations. A bowl of plain congee represents care in its most distilled form — nothing fancy, just warmth.

tree.setup: You're in a field near the river watching an old man fly a kite. He notices you watching and waves you over.
tree.setup_zh: 你在河边的一片空地上看一位老人放风筝。他看到你在看，朝你招手。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 来，你要不要试试？放风筝很简单，就是要耐心。
turns.text_pinyin: Lái, nǐ yào bú yào shìshi? Fàng fēngzhēng hěn jiǎndān, jiù shì yào nàixīn.
turns.text_en: Come, do you want to try? Flying a kite is simple — you just need patience.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his invitation.

options.text_zh: 我想试试！我以前没放过风筝。看起来很好玩，风筝飞得好高啊。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng shìshi! Wǒ yǐqián méi fàng guò fēngzhēng. Kàn qǐlái hěn hǎowán, fēngzhēng fēi de hǎo gāo a.
options.text_en: I'd love to try! I've never flown a kite before. It looks fun — the kite is flying so high!
options.feedback: Open and enthusiastic — willing to learn something new.

options.text_zh: 我不会。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bú huì.
options.text_en: I don't know how.
options.feedback: He just offered to teach you.

options.text_zh: 今天风大不大？
options.text_pinyin: Jīntiān fēng dà bú dà?
options.text_en: Is the wind strong today?
options.feedback: Asks about conditions instead of accepting his offer.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好！你拿着线，慢慢放。放风筝就像做人——不能太紧，也不能太松。要跟风配合。
turns.text_pinyin: Hǎo! Nǐ ná zhe xiàn, mànman fàng. Fàng fēngzhēng jiù xiàng zuò rén — bù néng tài jǐn, yě bù néng tài sōng. Yào gēn fēng pèihé.
turns.text_en: Good! Hold the string and let it out slowly. Flying a kite is like being a person — can't be too tight, can't be too loose. You have to work with the wind.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his kite-flying philosophy.

options.text_zh: 跟风配合——我喜欢这个说法。不是控制风，是和风一起。谢谢你教我。
options.text_pinyin: Gēn fēng pèihé — wǒ xǐhuan zhège shuōfǎ. Bú shì kòngzhì fēng, shì hé fēng yīqǐ. Xièxie nǐ jiāo wǒ.
options.text_en: Work with the wind — I like that. Not controlling the wind, but moving with it. Thank you for teaching me.
options.feedback: Catches the deeper meaning — living is about harmony, not control.

options.text_zh: 这个风筝你在哪里买的？
options.text_pinyin: Zhège fēngzhēng nǐ zài nǎlǐ mǎi de?
options.text_en: Where did you buy this kite?
options.feedback: Shopping question during a life lesson.

options.text_zh: 我怕线断了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ pà xiàn duàn le.
options.text_en: I'm afraid the string will break.
options.feedback: Worry instead of wonder.

tree.cultural_note: Kite-flying (放风筝) is one of China's oldest recreational traditions. Elders in parks often use it as a meditation practice. The metaphor of 跟风配合 (working with the wind) reflects Daoist philosophy — harmony with natural forces rather than dominance over them.

tree.setup: Early morning, you see the same street sweeper you pass every day. Today she has a small radio playing music softly.
tree.setup_zh: 一大早，你看到每天都看到的那个扫地阿姨。今天她有一个小收音机，轻轻地放着音乐。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 早啊！又是你最早出门。你每天都起得这么早吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Zǎo a! Yòu shì nǐ zuì zǎo chūmén. Nǐ měi tiān dōu qǐ de zhème zǎo ma?
turns.text_en: Morning! You're the earliest one out again. Do you always get up this early?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Greet her and acknowledge her daily routine.

options.text_zh: 早上好！我喜欢早上的空气。不过你比我更早——每天谢谢你把路扫得这么干净。
options.text_pinyin: Zǎoshang hǎo! Wǒ xǐhuan zǎoshang de kōngqì. Búguò nǐ bǐ wǒ gèng zǎo — měi tiān xièxie nǐ bǎ lù sǎo de zhème gānjìng.
options.text_en: Good morning! I like the morning air. But you're even earlier — thank you for keeping the road so clean every day.
options.feedback: Sees her work and thanks her — simple but meaningful.

options.text_zh: 我要去上班。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yào qù shàng bān.
options.text_en: I'm going to work.
options.feedback: A flat statement that doesn't engage her warmth.

options.text_zh: 你的收音机声音太大了。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de shōuyīnjī shēngyīn tài dà le.
options.text_en: Your radio is too loud.
options.feedback: A complaint that spoils a gentle scene.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 不客气。我喜欢早上的时候，街上没什么人，很安静。一边扫地一边听歌，时间过得很快。
turns.text_pinyin: Bú kèqi. Wǒ xǐhuan zǎoshang de shíhou, jiē shàng méi shénme rén, hěn ānjìng. Yībiān sǎo dì yībiān tīng gē, shíjiān guò de hěn kuài.
turns.text_en: You're welcome. I like the mornings when there's nobody on the street — so quiet. Sweeping while listening to songs, time passes quickly.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to how she finds peace in her work.

options.text_zh: 一边扫地一边听歌，听起来很安静很美好。我觉得你是每天第一个让这条街变漂亮的人。
options.text_pinyin: Yībiān sǎo dì yībiān tīng gē, tīng qǐlái hěn ānjìng hěn měihǎo. Wǒ juéde nǐ shì měi tiān dì yī ge ràng zhè tiáo jiē biàn piàoliang de rén.
options.text_en: Sweeping while listening to songs — that sounds peaceful and beautiful. I think you're the first person to make this street beautiful every day.
options.feedback: Elevates her work into something beautiful — she's the street's first artist.

options.text_zh: 你不觉得这个工作很辛苦吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ bù juéde zhège gōngzuò hěn xīnkǔ ma?
options.text_en: Don't you think this job is hard?
options.feedback: Projects difficulty onto her contentment.

options.text_zh: 你听什么歌？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ tīng shénme gē?
options.text_en: What songs do you listen to?
options.feedback: A detail question that doesn't match the mood.

tree.cultural_note: Street sweepers (环卫工人) are among the earliest risers in Chinese cities, often starting before dawn. Though their work is underappreciated, many develop a deep relationship with the streets they tend. The small radio playing music in the quiet dawn is a common sight — their private soundtrack to invisible labor.

tree.setup: You're sitting in a quiet courtyard. An old woman is sitting under a big tree, fanning herself slowly.
tree.setup_zh: 你坐在一个安静的院子里。一位老奶奶坐在一棵大树下，慢慢地扇扇子。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 坐这里吧，树下凉快。这棵树是我丈夫种的，已经三十年了。
turns.text_pinyin: Zuò zhèlǐ ba, shù xià liángkuai. Zhè kē shù shì wǒ zhàngfu zhǒng de, yǐjīng sānshí nián le.
turns.text_en: Sit here — it's cool under the tree. My husband planted this tree. It's been thirty years.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the tree and its history.

options.text_zh: 三十年了！这棵树好大。在这里坐着真的很舒服，谢谢您。
options.text_pinyin: Sānshí nián le! Zhè kē shù hǎo dà. Zài zhèlǐ zuò zhe zhēn de hěn shūfu, xièxie nín.
options.text_en: Thirty years! The tree is so big. Sitting here is really comfortable — thank you.
options.feedback: Respectful and present — appreciates the shade and its history.

options.text_zh: 这是什么树？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè shì shénme shù?
options.text_en: What kind of tree is this?
options.feedback: A botanical question that misses the personal meaning.

options.text_zh: 今天真热。
options.text_pinyin: Jīntiān zhēn rè.
options.text_en: It's really hot today.
options.feedback: A weather complaint instead of honoring her story.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 是啊。他说「种一棵树，以后就有地方乘凉了」。他走了以后，我每天都在树下坐。好像他还在。
turns.text_pinyin: Shì a. Tā shuō 'zhǒng yī kē shù, yǐhòu jiù yǒu dìfang chéngliáng le'. Tā zǒu le yǐhòu, wǒ měi tiān dōu zài shù xià zuò. Hǎoxiàng tā hái zài.
turns.text_en: Yes. He said, 'Plant a tree, and later there'll be shade to enjoy.' After he passed, I sit under the tree every day. It feels like he's still here.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond gently to her memory.

options.text_zh: 他种的树还在给你凉快——我觉得他的心意也还在。这棵树就像他留给你的礼物。
options.text_pinyin: Tā zhǒng de shù hái zài gěi nǐ liángkuai — wǒ juéde tā de xīnyì yě hái zài. Zhè kē shù jiù xiàng tā liú gěi nǐ de lǐwù.
options.text_en: The tree he planted still gives you shade — I think his care is still here too. This tree is like a gift he left for you.
options.feedback: The tree as a living gift — his care outlasts his life.

options.text_zh: 他是什么时候去世的？
options.text_pinyin: Tā shì shénme shíhou qùshì de?
options.text_en: When did he pass away?
options.feedback: Pries into grief instead of honoring the memory.

options.text_zh: 你一个人住吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yī ge rén zhù ma?
options.text_en: Do you live alone?
options.feedback: An intrusive question during a tender moment.

tree.cultural_note: Planting trees for future shade (前人栽树，后人乘凉) is a Chinese proverb about generosity that outlasts a single lifetime. Courtyards with old trees are treasured spaces in Chinese homes, and the tree often becomes a symbol of the family member who planted it — a living memorial.

tree.setup: You're at a café and notice a girl at the next table folding paper cranes. She has a whole line of them in front of her.
tree.setup_zh: 你在咖啡馆，看到旁边桌的女孩在折纸鹤。她面前已经有了一排纸鹤。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你在看我的纸鹤吗？我在给朋友折的，她下个星期生日。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ zài kàn wǒ de zhǐhè ma? Wǒ zài gěi péngyou zhé de, tā xià ge xīngqī shēngrì.
turns.text_en: Are you looking at my paper cranes? I'm folding them for a friend — her birthday is next week.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her handmade gift.

options.text_zh: 好漂亮啊！自己折的礼物最有心意。你的朋友一定会很开心。
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo piàoliang a! Zìjǐ zhé de lǐwù zuì yǒu xīnyì. Nǐ de péngyou yīdìng huì hěn kāixīn.
options.text_en: They're so pretty! A handmade gift has the most heart. Your friend will be so happy.
options.feedback: Appreciates the care behind the craft — handmade means heartfelt.

options.text_zh: 你为什么不买个礼物？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ wèishéme bù mǎi ge lǐwù?
options.text_en: Why don't you just buy a gift?
options.feedback: Suggests buying is better than making.

options.text_zh: 你折了多少个？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhé le duōshǎo ge?
options.text_en: How many have you folded?
options.feedback: Counts cranes instead of seeing the gesture.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我希望她会喜欢。现在大家都在网上买东西，自己做的东西不多了。但是我觉得，花时间做一个东西，这个东西就有了意义。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ xīwàng tā huì xǐhuan. Xiànzài dàjiā dōu zài wǎng shàng mǎi dōngxi, zìjǐ zuò de dōngxi bù duō le. Dànshì wǒ juéde, huā shíjiān zuò yī ge dōngxi, zhège dōngxi jiù yǒu le yìyì.
turns.text_en: I hope she'll like them. Everyone buys things online now — handmade things are rare. But I think when you spend time making something, it gains meaning.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her idea about handmade meaning.

options.text_zh: 嗯……买的东西有价格，但是做的东西有时间和心在里面。这是不一样的。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ shuō de duì. Mǎi de dōngxi yǒu jiàgé, dànshì zuò de dōngxi yǒu shíjiān hé xīn zài lǐmiàn. Zhè shì bù yīyàng de.
options.text_en: You're right. Bought things have a price, but made things have time and heart inside them. That's different.
options.feedback: Distinguishes price from meaning — time spent is love stored.

options.text_zh: 你可以教我折吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ jiāo wǒ zhé ma?
options.text_en: Can you teach me to fold them?
options.feedback: Shifts to a request instead of engaging her thought.

options.text_zh: 网上买更方便。
options.text_pinyin: Wǎng shàng mǎi gèng fāngbiàn.
options.text_en: Buying online is more convenient.
options.feedback: Argues for convenience against her point about meaning.

tree.cultural_note: Paper crane folding (折纸鹤) is a beloved tradition. Folding 1,000 cranes (千纸鹤) is believed to make a wish come true. Handmade gifts carry special weight in Chinese culture — the time spent making them is considered a form of devotion that bought gifts cannot match.

tree.setup: You're on the rooftop of your apartment building. Your neighbor is already there, looking up at the stars.
tree.setup_zh: 你在公寓楼的天台上。你的邻居已经在那里了，正在看星星。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你也上来了？今天晚上星星好多。在城市里很少能看到这么多星星。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ yě shàng lái le? Jīntiān wǎnshang xīngxing hǎo duō. Zài chéngshì lǐ hěn shǎo néng kàn dào zhème duō xīngxing.
turns.text_en: You came up too? So many stars tonight. It's rare to see this many in the city.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share your feelings about the stars.

options.text_zh: 真的好美。平时都在看手机，忘了抬头看看天。今天晚上真好。
options.text_pinyin: Zhēn de hǎo měi. Píngshí dōu zài kàn shǒujī, wàng le táitóu kànkan tiān. Jīntiān wǎnshang zhēn hǎo.
options.text_en: It's really beautiful. Usually I'm looking at my phone and forget to look up at the sky. Tonight is wonderful.
options.feedback: Honest and reflective — the stars remind us to look up.

options.text_zh: 我上来吹风的。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ shàng lái chuī fēng de.
options.text_en: I came up for some air.
options.feedback: Misses the star-gazing moment.

options.text_zh: 是啊，不过城市的空气不好，看不到多少星星。
options.text_pinyin: Shì a, búguò chéngshì de kōngqì bù hǎo, kàn bú dào duōshao xīngxing.
options.text_en: Yeah, but the city air is bad — you can't really see many stars.
options.feedback: He looks at you, then back up. You're both looking at a sky full of stars right now.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 是啊。我小时候在农村，每天晚上都能看到星星。那时候觉得很平常，现在觉得是最珍贵的。
turns.text_pinyin: Shì a. Wǒ xiǎo shíhou zài nóngcūn, měi tiān wǎnshang dōu néng kàn dào xīngxing. Nà shíhou juéde hěn píngcháng, xiànzài juéde shì zuì zhēnguì de.
turns.text_en: Yeah. When I was little in the countryside, I could see stars every night. Back then it felt ordinary — now I realize it was the most precious thing.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his realization about what's precious.

options.text_zh: 最珍贵的东西，往往是我们以为很平常的。等我们发现的时候，有时候已经不容易看到了。
options.text_pinyin: Zuì zhēnguì de dōngxi, wǎngwǎng shì wǒmen yǐwéi hěn píngcháng de. Děng wǒmen fāxiàn de shíhou, yǒu shíhou yǐjīng bù róngyì kàn dào le.
options.text_en: The most precious things are often what we thought were ordinary. By the time we realize it, sometimes they're already hard to find.
options.feedback: Quiet wisdom — the ordinary becomes precious in hindsight.

options.text_zh: 你可以回农村看啊。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ huí nóngcūn kàn a.
options.text_en: You can go back to the countryside to see them.
options.feedback: Offers a simple solution to a deeper reflection.

options.text_zh: 城市的灯光太亮了。
options.text_pinyin: Chéngshì de dēngguāng tài liàng le.
options.text_en: The city lights are too bright.
options.feedback: A factual explanation instead of emotional connection.

tree.cultural_note: Many Chinese city-dwellers grew up in rural areas and carry deep nostalgia for the night sky of their childhood. The phrase 觉得很平常 (it felt ordinary) speaks to a universal human experience: not knowing the value of something until it's gone. Rooftop stargazing in cities has become a quiet counter-movement among young people seeking reconnection.

tree.setup: You wander into a tiny bookshop. The owner, a quiet middle-aged man, is reading behind the counter.
tree.setup_zh: 你走进一家很小的书店。老板，一个安静的中年男人，在柜台后面看书。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 慢慢看，不着急。如果你想找什么书，可以告诉我。
turns.text_pinyin: Mànman kàn, bù zháojí. Rúguǒ nǐ xiǎng zhǎo shénme shū, kěyǐ gàosu wǒ.
turns.text_en: Take your time, no rush. If you're looking for something, just let me know.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell him why you came in.

options.text_zh: 谢谢。我没有想找什么特别的书，只是觉得这里很舒服，想进来看看。
options.text_pinyin: Xièxie. Wǒ méiyǒu xiǎng zhǎo shénme tèbié de shū, zhǐ shì juéde zhèlǐ hěn shūfu, xiǎng jìnlái kànkan.
options.text_en: Thank you. I'm not looking for anything specific — I just felt this place was welcoming and wanted to browse.
options.feedback: Honest and open — drawn in by the atmosphere, not a purchase.

options.text_zh: 你们有英文书吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐmen yǒu Yīngwén shū ma?
options.text_en: Do you have English books?
options.feedback: A transactional question that misses the mood.

options.text_zh: 这个书店很小。
options.text_pinyin: Zhège shūdiàn hěn xiǎo.
options.text_en: This bookshop is very small.
options.feedback: An observation that could feel dismissive.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 那就好。现在大家都在网上买书，来书店的人越来越少了。但是我觉得，在书店里找书和在网上找书是不一样的。有时候你不知道你要什么，走进来，一本书就找到了你。
turns.text_pinyin: Nà jiù hǎo. Xiànzài dàjiā dōu zài wǎng shàng mǎi shū, lái shūdiàn de rén yuè lái yuè shǎo le. Dànshì wǒ juéde, zài shūdiàn lǐ zhǎo shū hé zài wǎng shàng zhǎo shū shì bù yīyàng de. Yǒu shíhou nǐ bù zhīdào nǐ yào shénme, zǒu jìnlái, yī běn shū jiù zhǎo dào le nǐ.
turns.text_en: Good. Everyone buys books online now, fewer and fewer people come to bookshops. But I think finding a book in a shop and finding one online are different. Sometimes you don't know what you want, you walk in, and a book finds you.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his idea about books finding you.

options.text_zh: 「一本书找到了你」——我很喜欢这个说法。在网上你搜索，但是在书店里你遇见。这种遇见是最好的。
options.text_pinyin: 'Yī běn shū zhǎo dào le nǐ' — wǒ hěn xǐhuan zhège shuōfǎ. Zài wǎng shàng nǐ sōusuǒ, dànshì zài shūdiàn lǐ nǐ yùjiàn. Zhè zhǒng yùjiàn shì zuì hǎo de.
options.text_en: 'A book finds you' — I love that phrase. Online you search, but in a bookshop you encounter. That kind of encounter is the best.
options.feedback: Captures the magic of browsing — encounter over algorithm.

options.text_zh: 网上买比较便宜。
options.text_pinyin: Wǎng shàng mǎi bǐjiào piányi.
options.text_en: Buying online is cheaper.
options.feedback: Argues price in a conversation about serendipity.

options.text_zh: 你打算一直开这个书店吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ dǎsuàn yīzhí kāi zhège shūdiàn ma?
options.text_en: Do you plan to keep this bookshop open?
options.feedback: Raises the specter of closure during a philosophical exchange.

tree.cultural_note: Small independent bookshops in China have been disappearing as online shopping dominates. Those that survive often become quiet sanctuaries. The idea that 书找到了你 (a book finds you) reflects a Chinese appreciation for 缘分 (yuánfèn) — meaningful encounters that feel fated rather than chosen.

tree.setup: You're leaning on the railing of a small stone bridge at dusk. A fisherman below is packing up for the day.
tree.setup_zh: 黄昏的时候，你靠在一座小石桥的栏杆上。桥下的一个钓鱼的人正在收拾东西。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 今天没钓到什么鱼。不过没关系，坐了一下午，很舒服。
turns.text_pinyin: Jīntiān méi diào dào shénme yú. Búguò méi guānxi, zuò le yī xiàwǔ, hěn shūfu.
turns.text_en: Didn't catch much today. But it doesn't matter — I sat here all afternoon and it was comfortable.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his relaxed attitude about not catching fish.

options.text_zh: 我觉得钓鱼最好的不是钓到鱼，是坐在那里的时间。水的声音，风的声音，什么都不想。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde diàoyú zuì hǎo de bú shì diào dào yú, shì zuò zài nàlǐ de shíjiān. Shuǐ de shēngyīn, fēng de shēngyīn, shénme dōu bù xiǎng.
options.text_en: I think the best part of fishing isn't catching fish — it's the time spent sitting there. The sound of water, the sound of wind, thinking of nothing.
options.feedback: Understands that fishing is really about stillness, not the catch.

options.text_zh: 一条鱼也没有吗？
options.text_pinyin: Yī tiáo yú yě méiyǒu ma?
options.text_en: Not even one fish?
options.feedback: Focuses on the catch, which he already said doesn't matter.

options.text_zh: 你明天还来吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ míngtiān hái lái ma?
options.text_en: Will you come again tomorrow?
options.feedback: Looks ahead instead of being present in this moment.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 说得对。年轻的时候我钓鱼是为了吃鱼。现在我钓鱼是为了安静。年纪大了就知道了——安静是最好的。
turns.text_pinyin: Shuō de duì. Niánqīng de shíhou wǒ diàoyú shì wèile chī yú. Xiànzài wǒ diàoyú shì wèile ānjìng. Niánjì dà le jiù zhīdào le — ānjìng shì zuì hǎo de.
turns.text_en: Exactly. When I was young I fished to eat fish. Now I fish for the quiet. When you get older you understand — quiet is the best.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his wisdom about quiet.

options.text_zh: 我虽然还年轻，但是我也开始觉得安静是最好的了。谢谢你告诉我这些。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ suīrán hái niánqīng, dànshì wǒ yě kāishǐ juéde ānjìng shì zuì hǎo de le. Xièxie nǐ gàosu wǒ zhèxiē.
options.text_en: I'm still young, but I'm starting to feel the same — quiet is the best. Thank you for sharing that with me.
options.feedback: Humble and receptive — learning from an elder's earned wisdom.

options.text_zh: 年轻人比较忙。
options.text_pinyin: Niánqīng rén bǐjiào máng.
options.text_en: Young people are busier.
options.feedback: Creates a generational divide instead of bridging it.

options.text_zh: 你钓了多少年了？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ diào le duōshǎo nián le?
options.text_en: How many years have you been fishing?
options.feedback: Asks for a number during a moment of shared wisdom.

tree.cultural_note: Fishing in China has long been associated with philosophical contemplation. The legendary Daoist figure Jiang Ziya famously fished with a straight hook, caring nothing for the catch. The transition from fishing-for-food to fishing-for-quiet mirrors a common Chinese life arc: from striving to stillness.

tree.setup: You hear a familiar melody coming from a small shop. The shopkeeper is humming while arranging shelves.
tree.setup_zh: 你听到一家小店里传来一首熟悉的旋律。店主一边整理货架一边哼着歌。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 啊，你听到了吗？这首歌是我年轻时候最喜欢的。已经很多年了。
turns.text_pinyin: A, nǐ tīng dào le ma? Zhè shǒu gē shì wǒ niánqīng shíhou zuì xǐhuan de. Yǐjīng hěn duō nián le.
turns.text_en: Ah, did you hear it? This song was my favorite when I was young. It's been so many years.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his connection with the old song.

options.text_zh: 旋律很好听。老歌有一种特别的感觉——听到的时候，好像回到了以前。
options.text_pinyin: Xuánlǜ hěn hǎotīng. Lǎo gē yǒu yī zhǒng tèbié de gǎnjué — tīng dào de shíhou, hǎoxiàng huí dào le yǐqián.
options.text_en: The melody is lovely. Old songs have a special feeling — when you hear them, it's like going back in time.
options.feedback: Connects music to time travel — songs as portals to the past.

options.text_zh: 这首歌我听过。现在有很多新的翻唱版本。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè shǒu gē wǒ tīng guò. Xiànzài yǒu hěn duō xīn de fānchàng bǎnběn.
options.text_en: I've heard this song. There are a lot of new cover versions now.
options.feedback: He nods politely, but his eyes drift. He wasn't talking about covers — he was talking about his youth.

options.text_zh: 我没听过这首歌。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ méi tīng guò zhè shǒu gē.
options.text_en: I've never heard this song.
options.feedback: Closes off the conversation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对，就是这种感觉。一首歌就能让我想起那时候的朋友，那时候的日子。音乐真的很神奇。
turns.text_pinyin: Duì, jiù shì zhè zhǒng gǎnjué. Yī shǒu gē jiù néng ràng wǒ xiǎng qǐ nà shíhou de péngyou, nà shíhou de rìzi. Yīnyuè zhēn de hěn shénqí.
turns.text_en: Exactly that feeling. One song and I remember the friends from back then, the days from back then. Music is truly magical.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the magic of music and memory.

options.text_zh: 是啊，音乐可以保存时间。虽然那些日子过去了，但是歌还在，记忆也还在。
options.text_pinyin: Shì a, yīnyuè kěyǐ bǎocún shíjiān. Suīrán nàxiē rìzi guòqù le, dànshì gē hái zài, jìyì yě hái zài.
options.text_en: Yes, music can preserve time. Even though those days are gone, the songs remain, and so do the memories.
options.feedback: Music as a time capsule — the days pass but the melody stays.

options.text_zh: 你现在还跟那些朋友联系吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ xiànzài hái gēn nàxiē péngyou liánxì ma?
options.text_en: Are you still in touch with those friends?
options.feedback: Pries into relationships during a musical moment.

options.text_zh: 我比较喜欢新歌。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bǐjiào xǐhuan xīn gē.
options.text_en: I prefer newer songs.
options.feedback: Dismisses his nostalgia with a preference.

tree.cultural_note: Old songs (老歌) hold enormous cultural significance in China, where music from the 1980s and 1990s is associated with a period of opening and optimism. Hearing an old song can trigger powerful memories. The phrase 音乐很神奇 speaks to a widely held belief that music has an almost mystical power to preserve emotions across decades.

tree.setup: You're walking on a tree-lined road in autumn. A little girl and her grandmother are collecting fallen leaves.
tree.setup_zh: 秋天，你在一条两边都是树的路上走。一个小女孩和她的奶奶在捡落叶。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看这些叶子的颜色！红的、黄的、橙色的——每一片都不一样。我孙女说要带回去画画。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn zhèxiē yèzi de yánsè! Hóng de, huáng de, chéngsè de — měi yī piàn dōu bù yīyàng. Wǒ sūnnǚ shuō yào dài huíqù huàhuà.
turns.text_en: Look at the colors of these leaves! Red, yellow, orange — each one is different. My granddaughter says she wants to take them home to paint.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the grandmother and her granddaughter's activity.

options.text_zh: 每一片都不一样——真的好美。你孙女有一双好眼睛，能看到叶子的美。
options.text_pinyin: Měi yī piàn dōu bù yīyàng — zhēn de hǎo měi. Nǐ sūnnǚ yǒu yī shuāng hǎo yǎnjing, néng kàn dào yèzi de měi.
options.text_en: Each one different — it's really beautiful. Your granddaughter has good eyes to see the beauty in leaves.
options.feedback: Appreciates the child's eye for natural beauty.

options.text_zh: 叶子掉了就是死了。
options.text_pinyin: Yèzi diào le jiù shì sǐ le.
options.text_en: Fallen leaves are dead.
options.feedback: Grim observation that spoils a gentle moment.

options.text_zh: 你们家住在附近吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐmen jiā zhù zài fùjìn ma?
options.text_en: Do you live nearby?
options.feedback: An unrelated question during leaf-collecting.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 她的眼睛里什么都是美的。小孩子就是这样——他们觉得世界上什么都好玩，什么都好看。我们大人有时候忘了怎么看。
turns.text_pinyin: Tā de yǎnjing lǐ shénme dōu shì měi de. Xiǎo háizi jiù shì zhèyàng — tāmen juéde shìjiè shàng shénme dōu hǎowán, shénme dōu hǎokàn. Wǒmen dàrén yǒu shíhou wàng le zěnme kàn.
turns.text_en: In her eyes, everything is beautiful. Children are like that — they think everything in the world is fun and lovely. We adults sometimes forget how to look.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her observation about children and adults.

options.text_zh: 哈哈，是的。今天跟你们一起看这些叶子，我也想起了小时候的感觉。谢谢你们。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ shuō de duì. Jīntiān gēn nǐmen yīqǐ kàn zhèxiē yèzi, wǒ yě xiǎng qǐ le xiǎo shíhou de gǎnjué. Xièxie nǐmen.
options.text_en: You're right. Looking at these leaves with you today, I remembered what it felt like when I was little. Thank you.
options.feedback: The grandmother and child helped them remember how to see.

options.text_zh: 大人太忙了。
options.text_pinyin: Dàrén tài máng le.
options.text_en: Adults are too busy.
options.feedback: A generic excuse rather than genuine reflection.

options.text_zh: 她画画画得好吗？
options.text_pinyin: Tā huàhuà huà de hǎo ma?
options.text_en: Is she good at painting?
options.feedback: Evaluates skill instead of appreciating wonder.

tree.cultural_note: Collecting autumn leaves with grandchildren is a common scene in Chinese parks. The grandmother's insight — that adults forget how to see — echoes a Buddhist and Daoist idea about beginner's mind. Children's fresh perception is considered a kind of wisdom that adults spend their lives trying to recover.

tree.setup: You're sitting on the steps outside your building as the last light of the day fades. Your downstairs neighbor sits down with two cups of tea and offers you one.
tree.setup_zh: 你坐在楼下的台阶上，看着最后的阳光慢慢消失。楼下的邻居端着两杯茶走来，递给你一杯。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 给你一杯。我看你坐在这里，觉得你可能也想喝杯茶。这个时间的光最好看。
turns.text_pinyin: Gěi nǐ yī bēi. Wǒ kàn nǐ zuò zài zhèlǐ, juéde nǐ kěnéng yě xiǎng hē bēi chá. Zhège shíjiān de guāng zuì hǎokàn.
turns.text_en: Here, one for you. I saw you sitting here and thought you might want some tea. The light at this hour is the most beautiful.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Accept the tea and respond to the light.

options.text_zh: 谢谢你！你真好。是啊，这个时候的天空颜色特别温柔。我最喜欢这个时间。
options.text_pinyin: Xièxie nǐ! Nǐ zhēn hǎo. Shì a, zhège shíhou de tiānkōng yánsè tèbié wēnróu. Wǒ zuì xǐhuan zhège shíjiān.
options.text_en: Thank you! That's so kind. Yes, the sky at this hour is so gentle in color. It's my favorite time.
options.feedback: Gracious and present — meets her kindness with shared appreciation.

options.text_zh: 我不太喝茶。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bú tài hē chá.
options.text_en: I don't really drink tea.
options.feedback: Declines a gesture of kindness over a preference.

options.text_zh: 快天黑了。
options.text_pinyin: Kuài tiān hēi le.
options.text_en: It's getting dark soon.
options.feedback: States the obvious instead of savoring the moment.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我丈夫以前也喜欢这个时间。他说「一天里最好的几分钟」。以前我们经常一起坐在这里，看天慢慢暗下来。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ zhàngfu yǐqián yě xǐhuan zhège shíjiān. Tā shuō 'yī tiān lǐ zuì hǎo de jǐ fēnzhōng'. Yǐqián wǒmen jīngcháng yīqǐ zuò zài zhèlǐ, kàn tiān mànman àn xiàlái.
turns.text_en: My husband used to love this time too. He called it 'the best few minutes of the day.' We used to sit here together and watch the sky slowly darken.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her memory with gentleness.

options.text_zh: 一天里最好的几分钟——他说得真好。今天谢谢你和我一起看这个最后的光。
options.text_pinyin: Yī tiān lǐ zuì hǎo de jǐ fēnzhōng — tā shuō de zhēn hǎo. Jīntiān xièxie nǐ hé wǒ yīqǐ kàn zhège zuìhòu de guāng.
options.text_en: The best few minutes of the day — he said it perfectly. Thank you for watching the last light with me today.
options.feedback: Carries his phrase forward into this moment — the light is shared again.

options.text_zh: 你丈夫去哪了？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhàngfu qù nǎ le?
options.text_en: Where did your husband go?
options.feedback: Pries into loss during a tender moment.

options.text_zh: 我们应该进去了，蚊子出来了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒmen yīnggāi jìnqù le, wénzi chūlái le.
options.text_en: We should go in — the mosquitoes are coming out.
options.feedback: Mosquitoes break the spell of a poignant moment.

tree.cultural_note: Dusk (黄昏) carries special emotional weight in Chinese culture, often associated with nostalgia and gentle sadness. Sharing tea at this hour is an act of quiet companionship. The neighbor continuing her husband's ritual — sitting at dusk — by inviting someone else to share it is a beautiful way of keeping a memory alive through action.

tree.setup: You step out into the courtyard of your apartment building on a cool morning. Your neighbor, Auntie Li, is watering her potted plants. She looks up and smiles.
tree.setup_zh: 一个凉爽的早晨，你走进公寓楼的院子。你的邻居李阿姨正在给盆栽浇水。她抬头看到你，微笑了。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 早啊！今天起得真早。你看，我的茉莉花开了，好香。
turns.text_pinyin: Zǎo a! Jīntiān qǐ de zhēn zǎo. Nǐ kàn, wǒ de mòlìhuā kāi le, hǎo xiāng.
turns.text_en: Morning! You're up early today. Look, my jasmine bloomed — it smells wonderful.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to Auntie Li's greeting and her flowers.

options.text_zh: 真漂亮！我也想种一些花，但是我不太会照顾它们。
options.text_pinyin: Zhēn piàoliang! Wǒ yě xiǎng zhòng yīxiē huā, dànshì wǒ bú tài huì zhàogù tāmen.
options.text_en: So pretty! I want to grow some flowers too, but I'm not very good at taking care of them.
options.feedback: Warm and honest — shows interest and opens the conversation naturally.

options.text_zh: 嗯，不错。
options.text_pinyin: En, búcuò.
options.text_en: Mm, not bad.
options.feedback: Too brief — Auntie Li is being warm; a short reply feels distant.

options.text_zh: 我不喜欢花，我对花粉过敏。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bù xǐhuan huā, wǒ duì huāfěn guòmǐn.
options.text_en: I don't like flowers, I'm allergic to pollen.
options.feedback: Honest but shuts down the conversation quickly.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 种花不难的！茉莉花很好养，你只要每天浇一点水，放在有阳光的地方就行了。你要不要我给你一盆小的？
turns.text_pinyin: Zhòng huā bù nán de! Mòlìhuā hěn hǎo yǎng, nǐ zhǐyào měi tiān jiāo yīdiǎn shuǐ, fàng zài yǒu yángguāng de dìfang jiù xíng le. Nǐ yào bú yào wǒ gěi nǐ yī pén xiǎo de?
turns.text_en: Growing flowers isn't hard! Jasmine is easy to care for — you just need to water it a little every day and put it somewhere sunny. Would you like me to give you a small pot?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her offer of a jasmine plant.

options.text_zh: 真的吗？那太好了！谢谢李阿姨，我一定好好照顾它。
options.text_pinyin: Zhēn de ma? Nà tài hǎo le! Xièxie Lǐ āyí, wǒ yīdìng hǎohǎo zhàogù tā.
options.text_en: Really? That would be great! Thank you, Auntie Li — I'll take good care of it.
options.feedback: Grateful and warm — exactly the right tone for accepting a neighbor's kindness.

options.text_zh: 不用了，我怕养不活。
options.text_pinyin: Bú yòng le, wǒ pà yǎng bù huó.
options.text_en: No need, I'm afraid I'd kill it.
options.feedback: Polite refusal, but misses a chance to connect with a kind neighbor.

options.text_zh: 我周末太忙了，没时间。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ zhōumò tài máng le, méi shíjiān.
options.text_en: I'm too busy on weekends, no time.
options.feedback: Abrupt — she's offering something small and easy, not asking for a big commitment.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好！明天我帮你准备一盆。对了，你早饭吃了吗？我刚蒸了一些包子。
turns.text_pinyin: Hǎo! Míngtiān wǒ bāng nǐ zhǔnbèi yī pén. Duìle, nǐ zǎofàn chī le ma? Wǒ gāng zhēng le yīxiē bāozi.
turns.text_en: Great! I'll prepare one for you tomorrow. By the way, have you eaten breakfast? I just steamed some baozi.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her breakfast offer.

options.text_zh: 还没呢！李阿姨做的包子一定很好吃。我能尝一个吗？
options.text_pinyin: Hái méi ne! Lǐ āyí zuò de bāozi yīdìng hěn hǎochī. Wǒ néng cháng yī ge ma?
options.text_en: Not yet! Auntie Li's baozi must be delicious. Can I try one?
options.feedback: Friendly and appreciative — builds the neighborly bond.

options.text_zh: 谢谢，我已经吃过了。
options.text_pinyin: Xièxie, wǒ yǐjīng chī guò le.
options.text_en: Thanks, I've already eaten.
options.feedback: Polite but declines the connection.

options.text_zh: 我不吃早饭。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bù chī zǎofàn.
options.text_en: I don't eat breakfast.
options.feedback: Blunt — dismisses her kindness without warmth.

tree.cultural_note: In Chinese neighborhoods, especially older communities, sharing food and plants between neighbors is a common form of social bonding. Auntie Li's offer of jasmine and baozi reflects the warmth of everyday community life.

tree.setup: You walk into a small neighborhood tea house on a rainy afternoon. The owner, Uncle Zhang, is sitting behind the counter, arranging tea leaves. The room smells of oolong.
tree.setup_zh: 一个下雨的下午，你走进一家小茶馆。老板张叔叔坐在柜台后面，正在整理茶叶。屋子里弥漫着乌龙茶的香味。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 外面雨下得不小吧？快进来坐。你想喝什么茶？我们有绿茶、红茶，还有今年新到的龙井。
turns.text_pinyin: Wàimiàn yǔ xià de bù xiǎo ba? Kuài jìnlái zuò. Nǐ xiǎng hē shénme chá? Wǒmen yǒu lǜchá, hóngchá, hái yǒu jīnnián xīn dào de Lóngjǐng.
turns.text_en: It's raining pretty hard out there, isn't it? Come in, sit down. What tea would you like? We have green tea, black tea, and this year's new Longjing.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Choose a tea and respond to Uncle Zhang.

options.text_zh: 下雨天喝龙井一定很舒服。我想试试新到的龙井。
options.text_pinyin: Xià yǔ tiān hē Lóngjǐng yīdìng hěn shūfu. Wǒ xiǎng shìshi xīn dào de Lóngjǐng.
options.text_en: Drinking Longjing on a rainy day must be wonderful. I'd like to try the new Longjing.
options.feedback: Shows appreciation for the moment — connects the weather to the tea choice.

options.text_zh: 给我一杯绿茶吧。
options.text_pinyin: Gěi wǒ yī bēi lǜchá ba.
options.text_en: Give me a cup of green tea.
options.feedback: Simple and fine, but misses the chance to engage with his recommendation.

options.text_zh: 你们有咖啡吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐmen yǒu kāfēi ma?
options.text_en: Do you have coffee?
options.feedback: Asking for coffee in a tea house misses the atmosphere.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好眼光！这个龙井是杭州西湖边上的，朋友寄过来的。你闻一闻——有没有闻到一点花香？
turns.text_pinyin: Hǎo yǎnguāng! Zhège Lóngjǐng shì Hángzhōu Xīhú biān shàng de, péngyou jì guòlái de. Nǐ wén yī wén — yǒu méiyǒu wén dào yīdiǎn huāxiāng?
turns.text_en: Good eye! This Longjing is from near West Lake in Hangzhou — a friend sent it to me. Smell it — can you catch a hint of floral fragrance?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond after smelling the tea.

options.text_zh: 嗯，真的有一点花香！还有一点……像草地的味道？很清新。
options.text_pinyin: En, zhēn de yǒu yīdiǎn huāxiāng! Hái yǒu yīdiǎn…… xiàng cǎodì de wèidào? Hěn qīngxīn.
options.text_en: Mm, there really is a floral note! And also a bit like... fresh grass? Very refreshing.
options.feedback: Engaging and curious — shows you're paying attention to the experience.

options.text_zh: 嗯，不错，挺香的。
options.text_pinyin: En, búcuò, tǐng xiāng de.
options.text_en: Mm, not bad, smells nice.
options.feedback: Polite but generic — doesn't explore the moment.

options.text_zh: 我分不出来，我对茶不太了解。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ fēn bù chūlái, wǒ duì chá bú tài liǎojiě.
options.text_en: I can't tell, I don't know much about tea.
options.feedback: Honest but closes the door on learning more.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 没错！好的龙井就是这样——清新、自然。你慢慢喝，不着急。下雨天，最适合坐在这里喝茶，看看外面的雨。
turns.text_pinyin: Méi cuò! Hǎo de Lóngjǐng jiù shì zhèyàng — qīngxīn, zìrán. Nǐ mànmàn hē, bù zháojí. Xià yǔ tiān, zuì shìhé zuò zài zhèlǐ hē chá, kànkan wàimiàn de yǔ.
turns.text_en: Exactly! Good Longjing is just like that — fresh and natural. Take your time, no rush. Rainy days are perfect for sitting here, drinking tea, and watching the rain outside.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his invitation to slow down.

options.text_zh: 是啊，平时太忙了，很少有时间这样安静地坐一坐。谢谢张叔叔，这里很舒服。
options.text_pinyin: Shì a, píngshí tài máng le, hěn shǎo yǒu shíjiān zhèyàng ānjìng de zuò yi zuò. Xièxie Zhāng shūshu, zhèlǐ hěn shūfu.
options.text_en: Yeah, I'm usually so busy, rarely have time to sit quietly like this. Thank you, Uncle Zhang — it's very comfortable here.
options.feedback: Reflective and grateful — matches the quiet atmosphere perfectly.

options.text_zh: 这个茶多少钱一杯？
options.text_pinyin: Zhège chá duōshǎo qián yī bēi?
options.text_en: How much is this tea per cup?
options.feedback: Abrupt shift to money during a peaceful moment.

options.text_zh: 我一会儿还有事，先走了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yīhuìr hái yǒu shì, xiān zǒu le.
options.text_en: I have things to do later, I'll head out first.
options.feedback: Rushing away when he's inviting you to slow down.

tree.cultural_note: Tea houses are quiet havens in Chinese daily life. The ritual of smelling tea before drinking, and the practice of sitting quietly on rainy days, reflect the Chinese appreciation for slowness and presence. Uncle Zhang sharing a friend's gift tea is a sign of warmth and trust.

tree.setup: You just finished making dumplings at home and have too many. You decide to bring some to your neighbor, Xiao Wang, who recently moved in and doesn't know many people yet.
tree.setup_zh: 你刚在家包完饺子，包得太多了。你决定送一些给刚搬来的邻居小王，他还不太认识附近的人。

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Knock on your neighbor's door and offer the dumplings.

options.text_zh: 小王，你好！我刚包了一些饺子，包多了。你要不要尝尝？
options.text_pinyin: Xiǎo Wáng, nǐ hǎo! Wǒ gāng bāo le yīxiē jiǎozi, bāo duō le. Nǐ yào bú yào chángchang?
options.text_en: Xiao Wang, hi! I just made some dumplings — made too many. Would you like to try some?
options.feedback: Friendly and natural — the 'made too many' framing takes pressure off the neighbor.

options.text_zh: 这是给你的饺子。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè shì gěi nǐ de jiǎozi.
options.text_en: These dumplings are for you.
options.feedback: Direct but a bit abrupt — no warmth or context.

options.text_zh: 你吃饭了吗？我做了饺子。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ chīfàn le ma? Wǒ zuò le jiǎozi.
options.text_en: Have you eaten? I made dumplings.
options.feedback: Classic Chinese greeting that leads naturally to the offer.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 哎，太客气了！闻起来好香啊。你自己包的？里面是什么馅儿的？
turns.text_pinyin: Āi, tài kèqi le! Wén qǐlái hǎo xiāng a. Nǐ zìjǐ bāo de? Lǐmiàn shì shénme xiànr de?
turns.text_en: Oh, you're too kind! They smell amazing. Did you make them yourself? What filling is it?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell him about the filling.

options.text_zh: 是猪肉白菜的，我妈妈教我的做法。你要是不吃猪肉的话，我下次可以包素的。
options.text_pinyin: Shì zhūròu báicài de, wǒ māma jiāo wǒ de zuòfǎ. Nǐ yàoshi bù chī zhūròu dehuà, wǒ xià cì kěyǐ bāo sù de.
options.text_en: They're pork and cabbage — my mom's recipe. If you don't eat pork, I can make vegetarian ones next time.
options.feedback: And considerate — shows cultural awareness about dietary preferences.

options.text_zh: 猪肉白菜的。
options.text_pinyin: Zhūròu báicài de.
options.text_en: Pork and cabbage.
options.feedback: Answers the question but doesn't build the conversation.

options.text_zh: 你自己看吧，我不记得了。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zìjǐ kàn ba, wǒ bú jìde le.
options.text_en: See for yourself, I don't remember.
options.feedback: Odd — you just made them; not remembering seems disconnected.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 猪肉白菜是我最喜欢的！太好了。你做饭很厉害啊。我一个人住，平时都是点外卖，很少自己做饭。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhūròu báicài shì wǒ zuì xǐhuan de! Tài hǎo le. Nǐ zuòfàn hěn lìhai a. Wǒ yī ge rén zhù, píngshí dōu shì diǎn wàimài, hěn shǎo zìjǐ zuòfàn.
turns.text_en: Pork and cabbage is my favorite! Great. You're really good at cooking. I live alone and usually just order delivery — I rarely cook myself.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his comment about always ordering delivery.

options.text_zh: 下次我包饺子的时候叫你一起来包吧！很简单的，我可以教你。
options.text_pinyin: Xià cì wǒ bāo jiǎozi de shíhou jiào nǐ yīqǐ lái bāo ba! Hěn jiǎndān de, wǒ kěyǐ jiāo nǐ.
options.text_en: Next time I make dumplings, come join me! It's simple — I can teach you.
options.feedback: Warm and inclusive — turns a gift into a friendship.

options.text_zh: 你应该学做饭，总吃外卖不健康。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi xué zuòfàn, zǒng chī wàimài bù jiànkāng.
options.text_en: You should learn to cook — always ordering delivery isn't healthy.
options.feedback: Preachy and unsolicited advice — dampens the friendly mood.

options.text_zh: 是啊，自己做比外卖好吃多了。
options.text_pinyin: Shì a, zìjǐ zuò bǐ wàimài hǎochī duō le.
options.text_en: Yeah, homemade food is much tastier than delivery.
options.feedback: Agreeable but doesn't extend the connection.

tree.cultural_note: Sharing homemade food with neighbors — especially dumplings — is one of the most common and meaningful gestures of community in China. The phrase 「包多了」 (made too many) is a polite way to offer food without making the other person feel like they owe you anything.

tree.setup: You're walking through your neighborhood when an older gentleman stops you. He's looking a bit lost, holding a piece of paper with an address.
tree.setup_zh: 你在小区里散步时，一位老先生拦住了你。他看起来有点迷路，手里拿着一张写着地址的纸条。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 不好意思，小伙子，请问一下，中心路的邮局怎么走？我找了半天了。
turns.text_pinyin: Bù hǎoyìsi, xiǎohuǒzi, qǐngwèn yīxià, Zhōngxīn Lù de yóujú zěnme zǒu? Wǒ zhǎo le bàntiān le.
turns.text_en: Excuse me, young person, could you tell me how to get to the post office on Zhongxin Road? I've been looking for a while.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Give him directions or offer to help.

options.text_zh: 邮局啊，离这里不远。你往前走，到第二个路口往左拐，再走五分钟就到了。
options.text_pinyin: Yóujú a, lí zhèlǐ bù yuǎn. Nǐ wǎng qián zǒu, dào dì èr ge lùkǒu wǎng zuǒ guǎi, zài zǒu wǔ fēnzhōng jiù dào le.
options.text_en: The post office? It's not far. Go straight, turn left at the second intersection, then walk five more minutes and you're there.
options.feedback: Clear and helpful — gives specific directions with landmarks.

options.text_zh: 我不太确定，你可以用手机地图查一下。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bú tài quèdìng, nǐ kěyǐ yòng shǒujī dìtú chá yīxià.
options.text_en: I'm not sure — you could check on your phone's map.
options.feedback: Not very helpful — an older person asking for directions may not use phone maps easily.

options.text_zh: 我也是新来的，不太认识路。对不起啊。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yě shì xīn lái de, bú tài rènshi lù. Duìbuqǐ a.
options.text_en: I'm also new here, I don't know the roads well. Sorry.
options.feedback: Honest, but could still offer to help in another way.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 往左拐？好的。谢谢你啊。那路口有没有什么标志？我眼神不太好。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǎng zuǒ guǎi? Hǎo de. Xièxie nǐ a. Nà lùkǒu yǒu méiyǒu shénme biāozhì? Wǒ yǎnshén bú tài hǎo.
turns.text_en: Turn left? Okay, thank you. Is there any landmark at the intersection? My eyesight isn't great.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Help him identify the intersection.

options.text_zh: 那个路口有一个红色的水果店，很好认。您要是找不到，我可以带您过去。
options.text_pinyin: Nàge lùkǒu yǒu yī ge hóngsè de shuǐguǒ diàn, hěn hǎo rèn. Nín yàoshi zhǎo bú dào, wǒ kěyǐ dài nín guòqù.
options.text_en: There's a red fruit shop at that intersection — easy to spot. If you can't find it, I can walk you there.
options.feedback: Detailed and thoughtful — offers a landmark and backup help.

options.text_zh: 路口就在前面，你走走就看到了。
options.text_pinyin: Lùkǒu jiù zài qiánmiàn, nǐ zǒuzǒu jiù kàndào le.
options.text_en: The intersection is up ahead — just walk and you'll see it.
options.feedback: Vague — he said his eyesight is poor, so more detail would help.

options.text_zh: 我不记得了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bú jìde le.
options.text_en: I don't remember.
options.feedback: Unhelpful — you just gave him specific directions.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 红色的水果店，好，我记住了。你真热心，谢谢你！现在的年轻人很少这么有耐心了。
turns.text_pinyin: Hóngsè de shuǐguǒ diàn, hǎo, wǒ jì zhù le. Nǐ zhēn rèxīn, xièxie nǐ! Xiànzài de niánqīng rén hěn shǎo zhème yǒu nàixīn le.
turns.text_en: Red fruit shop, got it. You're really warm-hearted, thank you! Young people nowadays are rarely this patient.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his compliment.

options.text_zh: 没什么，应该的。您慢慢走，路上小心。
options.text_pinyin: Méi shénme, yīnggāi de. Nín mànmàn zǒu, lù shàng xiǎoxīn.
options.text_en: It's nothing, it's what I should do. Take your time, be careful on the road.
options.feedback: Humble and caring — a perfect gentle farewell.

options.text_zh: 谢谢，再见。
options.text_pinyin: Xièxie, zàijiàn.
options.text_en: Thanks, bye.
options.feedback: Polite but a bit too quick.

options.text_zh: 那当然了！
options.text_pinyin: Nà dāngrán le!
options.text_en: Of course!
options.feedback: Comes across as boastful rather than humble.

tree.cultural_note: Helping strangers with directions is a valued social behavior in China. The phrase 「应该的」 (it's what I should do) is a humble way to accept thanks, reflecting the cultural value of modesty. The older generation often comments on young people's manners — receiving such praise gracefully is important.

tree.setup: Your friend Xiao Chen sends you a message asking if you're free this weekend. You meet at the usual park bench to discuss plans.
tree.setup_zh: 你的朋友小陈发消息问你周末有没有空。你们在平时常去的公园长椅上碰面，讨论周末的计划。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好久没出去玩了！这个周末你想做什么？我们可以去爬山，或者去那个新开的商场逛逛。
turns.text_pinyin: Hǎojiǔ méi chūqù wán le! Zhège zhōumò nǐ xiǎng zuò shénme? Wǒmen kěyǐ qù pá shān, huòzhě qù nàge xīn kāi de shāngchǎng guàngguang.
turns.text_en: It's been so long since we went out! What do you want to do this weekend? We could go hiking, or check out that new mall.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Suggest what to do this weekend.

options.text_zh: 我觉得爬山比较好。天气这么好，我们可以带一些吃的，在山上野餐。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde pá shān bǐjiào hǎo. Tiānqì zhème hǎo, wǒmen kěyǐ dài yīxiē chī de, zài shān shàng yěcān.
options.text_en: I think hiking is better. The weather's so nice — we could bring some food and have a picnic on the mountain.
options.feedback: Enthusiastic and adds a nice detail about a picnic.

options.text_zh: 都可以，你决定吧。
options.text_pinyin: Dōu kěyǐ, nǐ juédìng ba.
options.text_en: Either is fine, you decide.
options.feedback: Easy-going but doesn't contribute to the planning.

options.text_zh: 我想在家休息，你自己去吧。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng zài jiā xiūxi, nǐ zìjǐ qù ba.
options.text_en: I want to rest at home — you go by yourself.
options.feedback: Dismissive — she's excited to spend time together.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好主意！山上野餐一定很舒服。那我们要不要叫小李一起？她上次说也想出去走走。
turns.text_pinyin: Hǎo zhǔyi! Shān shàng yěcān yīdìng hěn shūfu. Nà wǒmen yào bú yào jiào Xiǎo Lǐ yīqǐ? Tā shàng cì shuō yě xiǎng chūqù zǒuzou.
turns.text_en: Good idea! A mountaintop picnic would be lovely. Should we invite Xiao Li too? She mentioned wanting to get out last time.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to inviting Xiao Li.

options.text_zh: 当然好啊！人多更热闹。你给她发个消息吧。
options.text_pinyin: Dāngrán hǎo a! Rén duō gèng rènao. Nǐ gěi tā fā ge xiāoxi ba.
options.text_en: Of course! The more the merrier. Send her a message.
options.feedback: Inclusive and enthusiastic — good friend energy.

options.text_zh: 好吧，都行。
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo ba, dōu xíng.
options.text_en: Sure, whatever.
options.feedback: Agreeable but unenthusiastic.

options.text_zh: 不要了，就我们两个人去吧。
options.text_pinyin: Bú yào le, jiù wǒmen liǎng ge rén qù ba.
options.text_en: Nah, let's just go the two of us.
options.feedback: Not wrong, but could seem exclusive.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 太好了！那我们周六早上九点在地铁站见？记得穿运动鞋，山路不太好走。
turns.text_pinyin: Tài hǎo le! Nà wǒmen zhōuliù zǎoshang jiǔ diǎn zài dìtiě zhàn jiàn? Jìde chuān yùndòng xié, shān lù bú tài hǎo zǒu.
turns.text_en: Great! Then shall we meet at the subway station at 9 on Saturday morning? Remember to wear sneakers — the mountain paths aren't easy to walk.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Confirm the plan.

options.text_zh: 好的，九点见！我带水果和面包，你带水，怎么样？
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo de, jiǔ diǎn jiàn! Wǒ dài shuǐguǒ hé miànbāo, nǐ dài shuǐ, zěnmeyàng?
options.text_en: Okay, see you at nine! I'll bring fruit and bread, you bring water — how's that?
options.feedback: Takes initiative on logistics — shows you're a good planner.

options.text_zh: 好的，不见不散。
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo de, bú jiàn bú sàn.
options.text_en: Okay, see you there for sure.
options.feedback: Nice expression but doesn't add to the plan.

options.text_zh: 九点太早了，十点行吗？
options.text_pinyin: Jiǔ diǎn tài zǎo le, shí diǎn xíng ma?
options.text_en: Nine is too early — how about ten?
options.feedback: Reasonable to negotiate but slightly deflates the momentum.

tree.cultural_note: Making plans with friends in China often involves multiple rounds of suggestions and confirmations. The phrase 「不见不散」 means 'we won't leave until we see each other' — a common way to confirm plans. Splitting who brings what for outings is a natural part of group planning.

tree.setup: You wander into a cozy neighborhood bookstore. The owner, a gentle woman in her forties named Sister Fang, is shelving new arrivals. Soft music plays in the background.
tree.setup_zh: 你走进一家温馨的小书店。老板方姐四十多岁，正在摆放新到的书。背景里放着轻柔的音乐。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 欢迎来看看！今天新到了一些小说和旅行书。你平时喜欢看什么类型的书？
turns.text_pinyin: Huānyíng lái kànkan! Jīntiān xīn dào le yīxiē xiǎoshuō hé lǚxíng shū. Nǐ píngshí xǐhuan kàn shénme lèixíng de shū?
turns.text_en: Welcome, have a look! We got some new novels and travel books today. What kind of books do you usually like?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell her what kind of books you like.

options.text_zh: 我最近对旅行书很感兴趣。有没有关于中国小城市的？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ zuìjìn duì lǚxíng shū hěn gǎn xìngqù. Yǒu méiyǒu guānyú Zhōngguó xiǎo chéngshì de?
options.text_en: I've been interested in travel books lately. Do you have any about small Chinese cities?
options.feedback: Specific and engaging — gives her something to work with.

options.text_zh: 我随便看看。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ suíbiàn kànkan.
options.text_en: I'm just browsing.
options.feedback: Fine but doesn't open a conversation.

options.text_zh: 我不怎么看书。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bù zěnme kàn shū.
options.text_en: I don't really read much.
options.feedback: Odd thing to say in a bookstore.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 有的！这本写的是云南的大理，特别美。还有一本是关于贵州的小村庄的，作者住在那里一年，写得很温暖。
turns.text_pinyin: Yǒu de! Zhè běn xiě de shì Yúnnán de Dàlǐ, tèbié měi. Hái yǒu yī běn shì guānyú Guìzhōu de xiǎo cūnzhuāng de, zuòzhě zhù zài nàlǐ yī nián, xiě de hěn wēnnuǎn.
turns.text_en: Yes! This one is about Dali in Yunnan — really beautiful. And this one is about a small village in Guizhou — the author lived there for a year, very warmly written.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her recommendations.

options.text_zh: 贵州那本听起来很有意思！我一直想去贵州看看。我可以翻一翻吗？
options.text_pinyin: Guìzhōu nà běn tīng qǐlái hěn yǒu yìsi! Wǒ yīzhí xiǎng qù Guìzhōu kànkan. Wǒ kěyǐ fān yi fān ma?
options.text_en: The Guizhou one sounds really interesting! I've always wanted to visit Guizhou. Can I flip through it?
options.feedback: Shows genuine interest and connects the book to a personal wish.

options.text_zh: 多少钱？
options.text_pinyin: Duōshǎo qián?
options.text_en: How much?
options.feedback: Jumps straight to price without engaging with the content.

options.text_zh: 两本都不错，我都要。
options.text_pinyin: Liǎng běn dōu búcuò, wǒ dōu yào.
options.text_en: Both look good, I'll take both.
options.feedback: Generous but skips the browsing experience.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 当然可以！你坐那边的椅子上慢慢看。我给你倒杯茶。在书店里不用着急，想看多久就看多久。
turns.text_pinyin: Dāngrán kěyǐ! Nǐ zuò nàbiān de yǐzi shàng mànmàn kàn. Wǒ gěi nǐ dào bēi chá. Zài shūdiàn lǐ bú yòng zháojí, xiǎng kàn duōjiǔ jiù kàn duōjiǔ.
turns.text_en: Of course! Sit in that chair over there and take your time. I'll pour you some tea. No rush in a bookstore — read as long as you like.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her kindness.

options.text_zh: 谢谢方姐！这种书店现在越来越少了，我很喜欢这里的感觉。
options.text_pinyin: Xièxie Fāng jiě! Zhè zhǒng shūdiàn xiànzài yuè lái yuè shǎo le, wǒ hěn xǐhuan zhèlǐ de gǎnjué.
options.text_en: Thank you, Sister Fang! Bookstores like this are becoming rarer — I really like the feeling here.
options.feedback: Appreciative and thoughtful — acknowledges the value of small bookstores.

options.text_zh: 好的，谢谢。
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo de, xièxie.
options.text_en: Okay, thanks.
options.feedback: Polite but doesn't match her warmth.

options.text_zh: 你们几点关门？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐmen jǐ diǎn guānmén?
options.text_en: What time do you close?
options.feedback: Practical but misses the warm moment.

tree.cultural_note: Independent bookstores in China often serve tea and encourage lingering. They represent a quieter alternative to the fast-paced digital world. Many book lovers treasure these spaces as community gathering points.

tree.setup: You're waiting at a bus stop when it suddenly starts to drizzle. The woman next to you, a friendly office worker, opens her umbrella and scoots closer.
tree.setup_zh: 你在公交站等车，突然开始下小雨了。旁边的女士是一个看起来很友好的上班族，她打开了伞，靠近了你一些。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 下雨了！你没带伞吧？过来，一起撑我的。早上出门的时候天还好好的呢。
turns.text_pinyin: Xià yǔ le! Nǐ méi dài sǎn ba? Guòlái, yīqǐ chēng wǒ de. Zǎoshang chūmén de shíhou tiān hái hǎohǎo de ne.
turns.text_en: It's raining! You didn't bring an umbrella, did you? Come over, share mine. It was perfectly fine this morning when I left.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her offer to share the umbrella.

options.text_zh: 谢谢你！早上天气预报说不下雨，我就没带。最近天气变化真大。
options.text_pinyin: Xièxie nǐ! Zǎoshang tiānqì yùbào shuō bú xià yǔ, wǒ jiù méi dài. Zuìjìn tiānqì biànhuà zhēn dà.
options.text_en: Thank you! The weather forecast this morning said no rain, so I didn't bring one. The weather's been so unpredictable lately.
options.feedback: Grateful and conversational — opens up a natural topic.

options.text_zh: 没关系，我不怕淋雨。
options.text_pinyin: Méi guānxi, wǒ bú pà lín yǔ.
options.text_en: It's fine, I don't mind getting wet.
options.feedback: Independent but refuses a kind gesture.

options.text_zh: 哦。
options.text_pinyin: Ó.
options.text_en: Oh.
options.feedback: Barely a response — doesn't engage at all.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对啊，天气预报这几天都不太准。上周说会下雪，结果出太阳了。你觉得这个季节是不是越来越奇怪了？
turns.text_pinyin: Duì a, tiānqì yùbào zhè jǐ tiān dōu bú tài zhǔn. Shàng zhōu shuō huì xià xuě, jiéguǒ chū tàiyáng le. Nǐ juéde zhège jìjié shì bú shì yuè lái yuè qíguài le?
turns.text_en: Right, the forecast has been off these past few days. Last week they said it would snow, and it turned out sunny. Don't you think this season is getting stranger and stranger?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share your thoughts on the changing weather.

options.text_zh: 是啊，我记得小时候每个季节都很分明，现在春天有时候像冬天一样冷。
options.text_pinyin: Shì a, wǒ jìde xiǎo shíhou měi ge jìjié dōu hěn fēnmíng, xiànzài chūntiān yǒu shíhou xiàng dōngtiān yīyàng lěng.
options.text_en: Yeah, I remember when I was young the seasons were really distinct. Now spring sometimes feels as cold as winter.
options.feedback: And personal — connects the weather to memory.

options.text_zh: 可能吧。
options.text_pinyin: Kěnéng ba.
options.text_en: Maybe.
options.feedback: Too brief for a friendly conversation.

options.text_zh: 我不太关心天气，反正每天都得出门。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bú tài guānxīn tiānqì, fǎnzhèng měi tiān dōu děi chūmén.
options.text_en: I don't really care about weather — I have to go out every day anyway.
options.feedback: Practical but closes the conversation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对！以前秋天很长，现在感觉一下子就从夏天跳到冬天了。哦，车来了！你坐几路？
turns.text_pinyin: Duì! Yǐqián qiūtiān hěn cháng, xiànzài gǎnjué yīxiàzi jiù cóng xiàtiān tiào dào dōngtiān le. Ó, chē lái le! Nǐ zuò jǐ lù?
turns.text_en: Right! Autumn used to be long — now it feels like it jumps straight from summer to winter. Oh, the bus is here! Which line are you taking?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Answer and say goodbye.

options.text_zh: 我坐十二路。谢谢你的伞！希望明天天气好一点。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ zuò shí'èr lù. Xièxie nǐ de sǎn! Xīwàng míngtiān tiānqì hǎo yīdiǎn.
options.text_en: I'm taking the 12. Thanks for the umbrella! Hope the weather's better tomorrow.
options.feedback: Warm farewell that circles back to the shared experience.

options.text_zh: 十二路。再见。
options.text_pinyin: Shí'èr lù. Zàijiàn.
options.text_en: The 12. Bye.
options.feedback: Answers but doesn't acknowledge her kindness.

options.text_zh: 我不坐这班车。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bú zuò zhè bān chē.
options.text_en: I'm not taking this bus.
options.feedback: Fine but doesn't close the interaction warmly.

tree.cultural_note: Sharing an umbrella with a stranger is a common act of everyday kindness in China. Casual weather talk at bus stops creates brief but genuine connections between strangers. The phrase 「天气预报不准」 is a universal conversation starter.

tree.setup: You're sitting in a quiet café reading a book. The barista, a young guy named Ah Ming, comes over to refill your water and starts chatting.
tree.setup_zh: 你坐在一家安静的咖啡馆里看书。咖啡师阿明过来给你加水，顺便聊了起来。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看的什么书啊？看你看了好久了，一定很好看。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn de shénme shū a? Kàn nǐ kàn le hǎojiǔ le, yīdìng hěn hǎokàn.
turns.text_en: What book are you reading? You've been at it for a while — it must be good.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Tell him about your book.

options.text_zh: 是一本关于旅行的书，写的是一个人在西藏的故事。你喜欢看书吗？
options.text_pinyin: Shì yī běn guānyú lǚxíng de shū, xiě de shì yī ge rén zài Xīzàng de gùshi. Nǐ xǐhuan kàn shū ma?
options.text_en: It's a travel book — about someone's journey in Tibet. Do you like reading?
options.feedback: Shares the book and returns the question — natural conversation flow.

options.text_zh: 一本小说。
options.text_pinyin: Yī běn xiǎoshuō.
options.text_en: A novel.
options.feedback: Answers but doesn't invite further conversation.

options.text_zh: 没什么，随便看看。
options.text_pinyin: Méi shénme, suíbiàn kànkan.
options.text_en: Nothing special, just reading casually.
options.feedback: Dismissive — he's being friendly.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 西藏！我一直想去但是还没去过。我平时也喜欢看书，不过最近太忙了，只能在休息的时候看一点。你经常来这里看书吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Xīzàng! Wǒ yīzhí xiǎng qù dànshì hái méi qù guò. Wǒ píngshí yě xǐhuan kàn shū, búguò zuìjìn tài máng le, zhǐ néng zài xiūxi de shíhou kàn yīdiǎn. Nǐ jīngcháng lái zhèlǐ kàn shū ma?
turns.text_en: Tibet! I've always wanted to go but haven't yet. I like reading too, but I've been so busy lately I can only read during breaks. Do you come here often to read?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about your café habit.

options.text_zh: 嗯，差不多每个周末都来。这里很安静，而且咖啡很好喝。是你做的吗？
options.text_pinyin: En, chàbuduō měi ge zhōumò dōu lái. Zhèlǐ hěn ānjìng, érqiě kāfēi hěn hǎohē. Shì nǐ zuò de ma?
options.text_en: Yeah, almost every weekend. It's quiet here, and the coffee is great. Did you make it?
options.feedback: Compliments the café and the barista — builds rapport.

options.text_zh: 偶尔来。
options.text_pinyin: Ǒu'ěr lái.
options.text_en: Occasionally.
options.feedback: Brief — doesn't sustain the conversation.

options.text_zh: 我觉得这里有点吵。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde zhèlǐ yǒudiǎn chǎo.
options.text_en: I think it's a bit noisy here.
options.feedback: Negative comment to the person who works here.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 是我做的！谢谢你喜欢。我学了两年了。对了，我们下周有一个读书会，每个人带一本自己喜欢的书来分享。你有兴趣吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Shì wǒ zuò de! Xièxie nǐ xǐhuan. Wǒ xué le liǎng nián le. Duìle, wǒmen xià zhōu yǒu yī ge dúshūhuì, měi ge rén dài yī běn zìjǐ xǐhuan de shū lái fēnxiǎng. Nǐ yǒu xìngqù ma?
turns.text_en: I made it! Thanks for liking it. I've been learning for two years. By the way, we have a book club next week — everyone brings a book they like to share. Are you interested?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the book club invitation.

options.text_zh: 听起来很有意思！什么时间？我想参加。
options.text_pinyin: Tīng qǐlái hěn yǒu yìsi! Shénme shíjiān? Wǒ xiǎng cānjiā.
options.text_en: Sounds interesting! What time? I'd like to join.
options.feedback: Open and enthusiastic — a great way to make new connections.

options.text_zh: 我再想想吧。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ zài xiǎngxiang ba.
options.text_en: Let me think about it.
options.feedback: Non-committal but not rude.

options.text_zh: 我不喜欢跟很多人在一起。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bù xǐhuan gēn hěn duō rén zài yīqǐ.
options.text_en: I don't like being around a lot of people.
options.feedback: Honest but shuts down a kind invitation.

tree.cultural_note: Café culture is growing in Chinese cities, especially among young people. Many cafés host book clubs, art workshops, and other community events. The phrase 「读书会」 reflects the tradition of shared learning and discussion.

tree.setup: You open your apartment door and find a small orange cat sitting in the hallway. Your upstairs neighbor, Xiao Liu, comes running down the stairs looking worried.
tree.setup_zh: 你打开家门，发现走廊里坐着一只小橘猫。楼上的邻居小刘跑下楼来，看起来很着急。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 啊，橘子在这里！它又跑出来了。对不起，它没有打扰你吧？
turns.text_pinyin: A, Júzi zài zhèlǐ! Tā yòu pǎo chūlái le. Duìbuqǐ, tā méiyǒu dǎrǎo nǐ ba?
turns.text_en: Oh, Juzi is here! It ran out again. Sorry, it didn't bother you, did it?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about the cat.

options.text_zh: 没有没有，它特别可爱！我刚才还在想它是谁家的。它叫橘子啊？名字真好听。
options.text_pinyin: Méiyǒu méiyǒu, tā tèbié kě'ài! Wǒ gāngcái hái zài xiǎng tā shì shuí jiā de. Tā jiào Júzi a? Míngzi zhēn hǎotīng.
options.text_en: Not at all, it's so cute! I was just wondering whose cat it was. Its name is Juzi? What a nice name.
options.feedback: Warm and reassuring — puts the neighbor at ease and shows interest.

options.text_zh: 没事。
options.text_pinyin: Méi shì.
options.text_en: It's fine.
options.feedback: Reassuring but doesn't build the conversation.

options.text_zh: 你应该把门关好，别让猫跑出来。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi bǎ mén guān hǎo, bié ràng māo pǎo chūlái.
options.text_en: You should keep your door shut so the cat doesn't get out.
options.feedback: Critical — she's already worried, no need to lecture.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 谢谢你不介意！它就是喜欢到处跑。我养了它一年了，它特别喜欢跟人玩。你喜欢猫吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Xièxie nǐ bù jièyì! Tā jiù shì xǐhuan dàochù pǎo. Wǒ yǎng le tā yī nián le, tā tèbié xǐhuan gēn rén wán. Nǐ xǐhuan māo ma?
turns.text_en: Thanks for not minding! It just loves running around. I've had it for a year — it really likes playing with people. Do you like cats?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share whether you like cats.

options.text_zh: 喜欢！我小时候家里也养过猫。我可以摸摸它吗？
options.text_pinyin: Xǐhuan! Wǒ xiǎo shíhou jiālǐ yě yǎng guò māo. Wǒ kěyǐ mōmo tā ma?
options.text_en: I love them! We had a cat when I was little too. Can I pet it?
options.feedback: Enthusiastic and polite — asks before touching the cat.

options.text_zh: 还行吧，我没养过。
options.text_pinyin: Hái xíng ba, wǒ méi yǎng guò.
options.text_en: They're okay, I've never had one.
options.feedback: Neutral — doesn't engage much.

options.text_zh: 我更喜欢狗。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ gèng xǐhuan gǒu.
options.text_en: I prefer dogs.
options.feedback: Honest but slightly dismissive in this context.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 当然可以！你看，它已经在蹭你的腿了，说明它喜欢你。以后你想它了，随时可以上来看它。我家在五楼，五零二。
turns.text_pinyin: Dāngrán kěyǐ! Nǐ kàn, tā yǐjīng zài cèng nǐ de tuǐ le, shuōmíng tā xǐhuan nǐ. Yǐhòu nǐ xiǎng tā le, suíshí kěyǐ shànglái kàn tā. Wǒ jiā zài wǔ lóu, wǔ líng èr.
turns.text_en: Of course! Look, it's already rubbing against your leg — that means it likes you. In the future, whenever you miss it, feel free to come up and visit. I'm on the 5th floor, 502.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her invitation.

options.text_zh: 太好了！我一定会去看橘子的。对了，我叫……很高兴认识你和橘子。
options.text_pinyin: Tài hǎo le! Wǒ yīdìng huì qù kàn Júzi de. Duìle, wǒ jiào…… hěn gāoxìng rènshi nǐ hé Júzi.
options.text_en: Great! I'll definitely come visit Juzi. By the way, my name is... Nice to meet you and Juzi.
options.feedback: Warm introduction — a pet brings two neighbors together.

options.text_zh: 好的，谢谢。
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo de, xièxie.
options.text_en: Okay, thanks.
options.feedback: Polite but brief.

options.text_zh: 我可能没时间。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ kěnéng méi shíjiān.
options.text_en: I might not have time.
options.feedback: Dismisses a kind neighbor's invitation.

tree.cultural_note: Pets, especially cats, are increasingly popular in Chinese cities. Orange cats (橘猫) are beloved for their friendly personalities. Pets often serve as conversation starters between neighbors who might otherwise not interact. Sharing apartment numbers is a sign of neighborly trust.

tree.setup: You're having lunch with your colleague Xiao Yang. She's excited about a TV drama she's been watching.
tree.setup_zh: 你和同事小杨一起吃午饭。她最近在追一部电视剧，很兴奋地聊了起来。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你最近有没有看那部新的电视剧？就是那个讲一家人在小城市开饭馆的，叫《小城味道》。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ zuìjìn yǒu méiyǒu kàn nà bù xīn de diànshìjù? Jiù shì nàge jiǎng yī jiā rén zài xiǎo chéngshì kāi fànguǎn de, jiào "Xiǎo Chéng Wèidào".
turns.text_en: Have you been watching that new TV drama? The one about a family running a restaurant in a small city — called 'Small City Flavors.'

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her about the show.

options.text_zh: 听说过但是还没看。好看吗？讲的是什么故事？
options.text_pinyin: Tīngshuō guò dànshì hái méi kàn. Hǎokàn ma? Jiǎng de shì shénme gùshi?
options.text_en: I've heard of it but haven't watched it yet. Is it good? What's the story about?
options.feedback: Shows interest and invites her to share — good conversational move.

options.text_zh: 我不看电视。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bú kàn diànshì.
options.text_en: I don't watch TV.
options.feedback: Shuts down her enthusiasm completely.

options.text_zh: 没有，最近太忙了。
options.text_pinyin: Méiyǒu, zuìjìn tài máng le.
options.text_en: No, I've been too busy lately.
options.feedback: Honest but doesn't engage with her excitement.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 特别好看！讲的是一个妈妈带着两个孩子，在小城市开了一个小饭馆。每一集都有不同的客人来，每个人都有自己的故事。很温暖，看完心情特别好。
turns.text_pinyin: Tèbié hǎokàn! Jiǎng de shì yī ge māma dài zhe liǎng ge háizi, zài xiǎo chéngshì kāi le yī ge xiǎo fànguǎn. Měi yī jí dōu yǒu bùtóng de kèrén lái, měi ge rén dōu yǒu zìjǐ de gùshi. Hěn wēnnuǎn, kàn wán xīnqíng tèbié hǎo.
turns.text_en: It's really good! It's about a mother and her two kids who open a small restaurant in a small city. Each episode has different customers, each with their own story. It's very warm — you feel great after watching.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her description.

options.text_zh: 听你这么一说，我也想看了！我喜欢这种温暖的故事。一共有多少集？
options.text_pinyin: Tīng nǐ zhème yī shuō, wǒ yě xiǎng kàn le! Wǒ xǐhuan zhè zhǒng wēnnuǎn de gùshi. Yīgòng yǒu duōshǎo jí?
options.text_en: The way you describe it makes me want to watch it too! I like warm stories like that. How many episodes total?
options.feedback: Engaged and curious — mirrors her enthusiasm.

options.text_zh: 好吧，我有空看看。
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo ba, wǒ yǒu kòng kànkan.
options.text_en: Okay, I'll check it out when I have time.
options.feedback: Mildly interested but not enthusiastic.

options.text_zh: 我比较喜欢看动作片。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bǐjiào xǐhuan kàn dòngzuò piàn.
options.text_en: I prefer action movies.
options.feedback: Redirects away from what she wants to share.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 二十集，每集才四十分钟，很快就看完了。要不要今天晚上我们一起看两集？我可以带零食过来！
turns.text_pinyin: Èrshí jí, měi jí cái sìshí fēnzhōng, hěn kuài jiù kàn wán le. Yào bú yào jīntiān wǎnshang wǒmen yīqǐ kàn liǎng jí? Wǒ kěyǐ dài língshí guòlái!
turns.text_en: Twenty episodes, each only forty minutes — you'll finish it quickly. Want to watch a couple episodes together tonight? I can bring snacks!

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her invitation to watch together.

options.text_zh: 好啊！那我准备茶，你带零食。八点可以吗？
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo a! Nà wǒ zhǔnbèi chá, nǐ dài língshí. Bā diǎn kěyǐ ma?
options.text_en: Sure! I'll prepare tea, you bring snacks. Is eight okay?
options.feedback: Enthusiastic and practical — makes it a plan.

options.text_zh: 今天可能不行，改天吧。
options.text_pinyin: Jīntiān kěnéng bù xíng, gǎi tiān ba.
