options.text_pinyin: Jiā zài shū lǐ de dōngxi yǒu shíhou bǐ shū běnshēn gèng yǒu yìsi. Nàxiē chēpiào hé zhǐtiáo jiù xiàng yīge rén shēnghuó de hénjì, suīrán nǐ bú rènshi tā, dàn néng gǎnjué dào tā céngjīng zài zhèlǐ.
options.text_en: The things tucked in books are sometimes more interesting than the books themselves. Those tickets and notes are like traces of someone's life — even though you don't know them, you can feel they were here.
options.feedback: Observant and thoughtful — sees secondhand books as vessels of human presence.

options.text_zh: 这些旧书应该整理一下，把里面夹的东西都清理干净，这样看起来更整齐，也好卖一些。
options.text_pinyin: Zhèxiē jiù shū yīnggāi zhěnglǐ yīxià, bǎ lǐmiàn jiā de dōngxi dōu qīnglǐ gānjìng, zhèyàng kàn qǐlái gèng zhěngqí, yě hǎo mài yīxiē.
options.text_en: You should tidy these old books up — clear out everything tucked inside so they look neater and sell better.
options.feedback: Lao Zhao pauses, then quietly says: 'If I took those things out, these books would just be paper.'

options.text_zh: 你这里有英文书吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhèlǐ yǒu Yīngwén shū ma?
options.text_en: Do you have English books here?
options.feedback: Language preference question that ignores the charm of the moment.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 是啊。我最喜欢的是一本旧小说里夹的一封信，是一个人写给自己女儿的。信上说「爸爸不在的时候，看看这本书，就像爸爸在你旁边一样。」后来这本书到了我这里，那个女儿可能已经长大了。
turns.text_pinyin: Shì a. Wǒ zuì xǐhuān de shì yī běn jiù xiǎoshuō lǐ jiā de yī fēng xìn, shì yīge rén xiě gěi zìjǐ nǚ'ér de. Xìn shàng shuō 'bàba bú zài de shíhou, kàn kàn zhè běn shū, jiù xiàng bàba zài nǐ pángbiān yīyàng.' Hòulái zhè běn shū dào le wǒ zhèlǐ, nàge nǚ'ér kěnéng yǐjīng zhǎng dà le.
turns.text_en: Yeah. My favorite is a letter tucked in an old novel, written by someone to his daughter. It said, 'When dad isn't around, read this book, and it'll be like dad is right beside you.' Then the book ended up here — that daughter has probably grown up by now.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to this story.

options.text_zh: 那封信还在书里吗？也许有一天她会来找这本书。有些东西丢了以后，你才知道它有多重要。
options.text_pinyin: Nà fēng xìn hái zài shū lǐ ma? Yěxǔ yǒu yītiān tā huì lái zhǎo zhè běn shū. Yǒuxiē dōngxi diū le yǐhòu, nǐ cái zhīdào tā yǒu duō zhòngyào.
options.text_en: Is the letter still in the book? Maybe one day she'll come looking for it. Some things — you only realize how important they are after you've lost them.
options.feedback: Hopeful and tender — imagines a reunion while acknowledging loss.

options.text_zh: 这本小说是谁写的？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè běn xiǎoshuō shì shéi xiě de?
options.text_en: Who wrote this novel?
options.feedback: Focuses on the wrong detail; the letter matters, not the author.

options.text_zh: 你这里有空调吗？有点热。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhèlǐ yǒu kōngtiáo ma? Yǒudiǎn rè.
options.text_en: Do you have air conditioning here? It's a bit hot.
options.feedback: Comfort complaint during an emotional story.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 还在。我不会把它拿出来。也许你说得对，也许她永远不会来。但只要信还在书里，这个故事就还没有结束。
turns.text_pinyin: Hái zài. Wǒ bú huì bǎ tā ná chūlái. Yěxǔ nǐ shuō de duì, yěxǔ tā yǒngyuǎn bú huì lái. Dàn zhǐyào xìn hái zài shū lǐ, zhège gùshì jiù hái méiyǒu jiéshù.
turns.text_en: Still there. I won't take it out. Maybe you're right, maybe she'll never come. But as long as the letter stays in the book, the story hasn't ended.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his philosophy of keeping the letter.

options.text_zh: 这就是你开这家书店的原因吧。不只是卖书，是帮这些书保留它们的记忆，等着也许有一天会被需要它的人找到。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè jiù shì nǐ kāi zhè jiā shūdiàn de yuányīn ba. Bù zhǐ shì mài shū, shì bāng zhèxiē shū bǎoliú tāmen de jìyì, děng zhe yěxǔ yǒu yītiān huì bèi xūyào tā de rén zhǎo dào.
options.text_en: That's why you run this bookshop, isn't it. Not just selling books, but helping them preserve their memories, waiting for the day when the person who needs them might find them.
options.feedback: Sees the deeper purpose — reframes the bookshop as a place of safekeeping.

options.text_zh: 现在大家都看电子书了，纸质书越来越少了。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài dàjiā dōu kàn diànzǐ shū le, zhǐzhì shū yuè lái yuè shǎo le.
options.text_en: Everyone reads e-books now, paper books are getting fewer and fewer.
options.feedback: Industry observation that misses the emotional core.

options.text_zh: 你每天几点关门？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ měi tiān jǐ diǎn guānmén?
options.text_en: What time do you close every day?
options.feedback: Logistical question at a poignant moment.

tree.cultural_note: Secondhand bookshops (二手书店) in China often occupy marginal spaces in old buildings, run by passionate individuals rather than profit. The Chinese tradition of leaving personal items in books — letters, tickets, pressed flowers — creates accidental time capsules. These shops serve as quiet sanctuaries for those who appreciate the intersection of reading and human memory.

tree.setup: You're walking through a morning market at dawn. An elderly vegetable seller, Grandma Sun, is arranging her fresh produce. The air smells of earth and green onions.
tree.setup_zh: 你在清晨走过一个早市。一位卖菜的老奶奶孙奶奶正在摆她的新鲜蔬菜。空气中弥漫着泥土和葱的味道。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 小伙子，看看我的菜，今天早上刚从地里摘的。你看这个黄瓜，还带着露水呢。现在超市里的菜都是冷库里放了好几天的，哪有这么新鲜。
turns.text_pinyin: Xiǎohuǒzi, kàn kàn wǒ de cài, jīntiān zǎoshang gāng cóng dì lǐ zhāi de. Nǐ kàn zhège huángguā, hái dài zhe lùshuǐ ne. Xiànzài chāoshì lǐ de cài dōu shì lěngkù lǐ fàng le hǎo jǐ tiān de, nǎ yǒu zhème xīnxiān.
turns.text_en: Young man, take a look at my vegetables, picked from the field this very morning. See this cucumber — it still has dew on it. Supermarket vegetables sit in cold storage for days; nothing this fresh.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to Grandma Sun about her vegetables.

options.text_zh: 确实不一样，还能看到露水。小时候我奶奶也种菜，早上摘的西红柿，咬一口就有阳光的味道。那个味道在超市里买不到。
options.text_pinyin: Quèshí bù yīyàng, hái néng kàn dào lùshuǐ. Xiǎo shíhou wǒ nǎinai yě zhòng cài, zǎoshang zhāi de xīhóngshì, yǎo yī kǒu jiù yǒu yángguāng de wèidào. Nàge wèidào zài chāoshì lǐ mǎi bú dào.
options.text_en: It really is different — you can still see the dew. When I was little my grandma grew vegetables too. A tomato picked in the morning tasted like sunshine. You can't buy that taste in a supermarket.
options.feedback: Personal and sensory — connects her produce to childhood memories and taste.

options.text_zh: 看起来不错，不过我一般都在超市买菜，比较方便，而且有包装，干净一些。
options.text_pinyin: Kàn qǐlái búcuò, búguò wǒ yībān dōu zài chāoshì mǎi cài, bǐjiào fāngbiàn, érqiě yǒu bāozhuāng, gānjìng yīxiē.
options.text_en: They look nice, but I usually buy vegetables at the supermarket — it's more convenient, and they're packaged, a bit cleaner.
options.feedback: Grandma Sun shakes her head gently: 'Packaged... you think plastic makes things cleaner than morning dew?'

options.text_zh: 你每天几点来摆摊？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ měi tiān jǐ diǎn lái bǎi tān?
options.text_en: What time do you come to set up your stall every day?
options.feedback: Schedule question that bypasses the warmth of the conversation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 阳光的味道！你这个比喻好。现在很多年轻人都不来早市了，都在手机上买菜。方便是方便，但你摸不到菜，闻不到味道。买菜不只是买菜啊。
turns.text_pinyin: Yángguāng de wèidào! Nǐ zhège bǐyù hǎo. Xiànzài hěn duō niánqīng rén dōu bù lái zǎoshì le, dōu zài shǒujī shàng mǎi cài. Fāngbiàn shì fāngbiàn, dàn nǐ mō bú dào cài, wén bú dào wèidào. Mǎi cài bù zhǐ shì mǎi cài a.
turns.text_en: The taste of sunshine! What a nice metaphor. Nowadays many young people don't come to morning markets anymore — they buy vegetables on their phones. Convenient, sure, but you can't touch the vegetables, can't smell them. Buying vegetables isn't just about buying vegetables.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about what buying vegetables really means.

options.text_zh: 您说得对，来早市不只是买菜，也是跟人说说话，闻闻这些味道，感受一下早晨的生活。这些事情手机做不到。
options.text_pinyin: Nín shuō de duì, lái zǎoshì bù zhǐ shì mǎi cài, yě shì gēn rén shuō shuō huà, wén wen zhèxiē wèidào, gǎnshòu yīxià zǎochén de shēnghuó. Zhèxiē shìqíng shǒujī zuò bú dào.
options.text_en: You're right. Coming to the morning market isn't just about buying vegetables — it's about talking to people, smelling these scents, feeling the morning come alive. A phone can't do those things.
options.feedback: Affirms the human dimension of market life — sensory, social, irreplaceable.

options.text_zh: 手机买菜确实挺方便的，送到家门口。
options.text_pinyin: Shǒujī mǎi cài quèshí tǐng fāngbiàn de, sòng dào jiā ménkǒu.
options.text_en: Buying vegetables by phone is really convenient, delivered to your door.
options.feedback: Defends the thing she's lamenting, tone-deaf to her point.

options.text_zh: 你种菜用什么肥料？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhòng cài yòng shénme féiliào?
options.text_en: What fertilizer do you use to grow vegetables?
options.feedback: Technical farming question that misses the social observation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 就是啊。我在这个早市卖了二十年菜了，认识很多老顾客。他们来买菜的时候，我们聊聊天，说说家里的事。有时候菜没卖完，但心情很好。
turns.text_pinyin: Jiù shì a. Wǒ zài zhège zǎoshì mài le èrshí nián cài le, rènshi hěn duō lǎo gùkè. Tāmen lái mǎi cài de shíhou, wǒmen liáo liáo tiān, shuō shuō jiā lǐ de shì. Yǒu shíhou cài méi mài wán, dàn xīnqíng hěn hǎo.
turns.text_en: Exactly. I've been selling vegetables at this morning market for twenty years. I know so many regular customers. When they come to buy, we chat, talk about our families. Sometimes I don't sell everything, but I'm in a good mood.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her twenty years at the market.

options.text_zh: 二十年，您已经不只是卖菜的人了，您是这个早市的一部分。那些老顾客来找您，也许买菜只是一个理由，真正想要的是跟您聊聊天。
options.text_pinyin: Èrshí nián, nín yǐjīng bù zhǐ shì mài cài de rén le, nín shì zhège zǎoshì de yī bùfèn. Nàxiē lǎo gùkè lái zhǎo nín, yěxǔ mǎi cài zhǐ shì yīge lǐyóu, zhēnzhèng xiǎng yào de shì gēn nín liáo liáo tiān.
options.text_en: Twenty years — you're not just a vegetable seller anymore, you're part of this morning market. Those regulars come to you, and maybe buying vegetables is just an excuse. What they really want is a chat with you.
options.feedback: Sees her as a community anchor — buying vegetables as pretext for connection.

options.text_zh: 你以后打算退休吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǐhòu dǎsuàn tuìxiū ma?
options.text_en: Do you plan to retire?
options.feedback: Jumps ahead instead of appreciating the present.

options.text_zh: 我想买两斤西红柿。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng mǎi liǎng jīn xīhóngshì.
options.text_en: I'd like two jin of tomatoes.
options.feedback: Transactional response to an emotional share.

tree.cultural_note: Morning markets (早市) are a vital part of Chinese urban and semi-urban life, typically running from dawn until mid-morning. They serve as community gathering spaces where buying food is intertwined with social connection. The rise of grocery delivery apps has diminished foot traffic, leading many vendors to reflect on what is lost when commerce becomes purely transactional.

tree.setup: You're helping a neighbor, Grandpa Liu, sort through old boxes in his apartment. He finds a faded photograph and stops to look at it.
tree.setup_zh: 你在帮邻居刘爷爷整理家里的旧箱子。他找到了一张旧照片，停下来看。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看这张照片，是我三十年前在工厂门口拍的。那时候我们每天早上一起骑自行车上班，路上说说笑笑的。现在那个工厂已经变成了一个公园。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn zhè zhāng zhàopiàn, shì wǒ sānshí nián qián zài gōngchǎng ménkǒu pāi de. Nà shíhou wǒmen měi tiān zǎoshang yīqǐ qí zìxíngchē shàngbān, lù shàng shuō shuō xiào xiào de. Xiànzài nàge gōngchǎng yǐjīng biàn chéng le yīge gōngyuán.
turns.text_en: Look at this photo — it was taken thirty years ago at the factory entrance. Back then we'd ride bicycles to work together every morning, chatting and laughing along the way. Now that factory has become a park.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the old photograph.

options.text_zh: 照片里的人看起来都很开心。有时候最好的时光不是因为条件好，而是因为身边有一起走路的人。
options.text_pinyin: Zhàopiàn lǐ de rén kàn qǐlái dōu hěn kāixīn. Yǒu shíhou zuì hǎo de shíguāng bú shì yīnwèi tiáojiàn hǎo, ér shì yīnwèi shēnbiān yǒu yīqǐ zǒu lù de rén.
options.text_en: Everyone in the photo looks so happy. Sometimes the best times aren't because conditions were good, but because you had people walking beside you.
options.feedback: Perceptive — sees the happiness as relational, not material.

options.text_zh: 那个工厂生产什么？
options.text_pinyin: Nàge gōngchǎng shēngchǎn shénme?
options.text_en: What did the factory produce?
options.feedback: Industrial detail that misses the emotional weight of the memory.

options.text_zh: 这张照片可以修一修，让颜色更好看。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè zhāng zhàopiàn kěyǐ xiū yī xiū, ràng yánsè gèng hǎokàn.
options.text_en: You could restore this photo, make the colors look better.
options.feedback: Technical fix when he's sharing a memory.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对，就是这样。那时候工资不高，住的房子也小，但下班以后大家坐在院子里聊天，孩子们一起玩。现在条件好了，但邻居之间连名字都不知道。
turns.text_pinyin: Duì, jiù shì zhèyàng. Nà shíhou gōngzī bù gāo, zhù de fángzi yě xiǎo, dàn xiàbān yǐhòu dàjiā zuò zài yuànzi lǐ liáo tiān, háizimen yīqǐ wán. Xiànzài tiáojiàn hǎo le, dàn línjū zhī jiān lián míngzì dōu bù zhīdào.
turns.text_en: Exactly. Back then wages were low, houses were small, but after work everyone sat in the courtyard chatting and the kids played together. Now conditions are better, but neighbors don't even know each other's names.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his observation about community loss.

options.text_zh: 也许以前的生活虽然简单，但人和人之间的距离很近。现在我们住的房子大了，但心里的距离也远了。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ yǐqián de shēnghuó suīrán jiǎndān, dàn rén hé rén zhī jiān de jùlí hěn jìn. Xiànzài wǒmen zhù de fángzi dà le, dàn xīn lǐ de jùlí yě yuǎn le.
options.text_en: Maybe life was simpler before, but people were close to each other. Now our houses are bigger, but the distance in our hearts has grown too.
options.feedback: Captures the paradox — material improvement paired with emotional distance.

options.text_zh: 你那些老同事还联系吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ nàxiē lǎo tóngshì hái liánxì ma?
options.text_en: Are you still in touch with those old colleagues?
options.feedback: Practical follow-up that doesn't engage with the broader observation.

options.text_zh: 现在的公园好不好玩？
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài de gōngyuán hǎo bù hǎowán?
options.text_en: Is the park fun now?
options.feedback: Ignores the nostalgic reflection entirely.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 心里的距离远了……你说得对。我有时候想，进步到底是什么？如果生活越来越方便，但人越来越孤单，这算进步吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Xīn lǐ de jùlí yuǎn le…… nǐ shuō de duì. Wǒ yǒu shíhou xiǎng, jìnbù dàodǐ shì shénme? Rúguǒ shēnghuó yuè lái yuè fāngbiàn, dàn rén yuè lái yuè gūdān, zhè suàn jìnbù ma?
turns.text_en: The distance in our hearts... you're right. Sometimes I wonder, what is progress really? If life gets more and more convenient, but people get more and more lonely, does that count as progress?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his question about progress.

options.text_zh: 真正的进步应该包括人和人之间的关系。东西多了、速度快了，但如果我们失去了坐在院子里聊天的感觉，那不算完整的进步。
options.text_pinyin: Zhēnzhèng de jìnbù yīnggāi bāokuò rén hé rén zhī jiān de guānxì. Dōngxi duō le, sùdù kuài le, dàn rúguǒ wǒmen shīqù le zuò zài yuànzi lǐ liáo tiān de gǎnjué, nà bú suàn wánzhěng de jìnbù.
options.text_en: Real progress should include relationships between people. More stuff, faster speed — but if we lose the feeling of chatting in a courtyard, that's not complete progress.
options.feedback: Definition of progress — includes the human dimension.

options.text_zh: 现在科技很发达，可以视频聊天。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài kējì hěn fādá, kěyǐ shìpín liáo tiān.
options.text_en: Technology is very advanced now, you can video chat.
options.feedback: Tech solution that misses the point about in-person connection.

options.text_zh: 你要不要把这些旧箱子扔了？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yào bú yào bǎ zhèxiē jiù xiāngzi rēng le?
options.text_en: Do you want to throw away these old boxes?
options.feedback: Cleanup logistics during a meaningful conversation.

tree.cultural_note: The transformation of state-owned factories (国营工厂) into parks and commercial spaces is a common phenomenon in Chinese cities. The factory-era work unit (单位) system created tight-knit communities where colleagues were also neighbors. Many older Chinese people feel nostalgic for this communal lifestyle, even while acknowledging the material improvements of modern life.

tree.setup: It snowed last night. You're sitting in a small teahouse with a quiet middle-aged woman, Ms. He, who works as a librarian. The world outside the window is white and still.
tree.setup_zh: 昨晚下了雪。你坐在一家小茶馆里，和一位安静的中年女性何女士一起喝茶。她是一名图书管理员。窗外的世界又白又静。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看窗外，整个世界都安静下来了。雪有这个能力——把所有的声音都吸进去。每次下雪我都觉得，世界终于休息了一会儿。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn chuāng wài, zhěnggè shìjiè dōu ānjìng xiàlái le. Xuě yǒu zhège nénglì — bǎ suǒyǒu de shēngyīn dōu xī jìnqù. Měi cì xià xuě wǒ dōu juéde, shìjiè zhōngyú xiūxi le yīhuìr.
turns.text_en: Look outside — the whole world has gone quiet. Snow has this ability — it absorbs all the sound. Every time it snows I feel like the world has finally rested for a moment.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her observation about snow.

options.text_zh: 是啊，雪好像给整个城市盖了一条被子。平时那些吵闹的声音都不见了，只剩下安静。这种安静让人觉得时间慢了下来。
options.text_pinyin: Shì a, xuě hǎoxiàng gěi zhěnggè chéngshì gài le yī tiáo bèizi. Píngshí nàxiē chǎonào de shēngyīn dōu bújiàn le, zhǐ shèng xià ānjìng. Zhè zhǒng ānjìng ràng rén juéde shíjiān màn le xiàlái.
options.text_en: Yes, it's as if snow has tucked the whole city under a blanket. All the usual noise disappears, leaving only quiet. This kind of quiet makes you feel like time has slowed down.
options.feedback: Image — the blanket metaphor matches the iyashikei warmth.

options.text_zh: 你觉得今天会不会再下雪？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ juéde jīntiān huì bú huì zài xià xuě?
options.text_en: Do you think it will snow again today?
options.feedback: Weather forecast question that ignores the poetic observation.

options.text_zh: 下雪天路上很危险，你要小心开车。
options.text_pinyin: Xià xuě tiān lù shàng hěn wēixiǎn, nǐ yào xiǎoxīn kāi chē.
options.text_en: Roads are dangerous in the snow, you should drive carefully.
options.feedback: Safety advice that kills the contemplative mood.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 时间慢了下来……我喜欢这个说法。在图书馆工作，我最喜欢的就是早上刚开门的时候。还没有人来，阳光照在书架上，整个空间都是安静的。那种感觉和下雪很像。
turns.text_pinyin: Shíjiān màn le xiàlái…… wǒ xǐhuān zhège shuōfǎ. Zài túshūguǎn gōngzuò, wǒ zuì xǐhuān de jiù shì zǎoshang gāng kāi mén de shíhou. Hái méiyǒu rén lái, yángguāng zhào zài shūjià shàng, zhěnggè kōngjiān dōu shì ānjìng de. Nà zhǒng gǎnjué hé xià xuě hěn xiàng.
turns.text_en: Time slowing down... I like that way of putting it. Working at the library, my favorite moment is when we first open in the morning. Nobody's come yet, sunlight falls on the bookshelves, the whole space is quiet. That feeling is a lot like snow.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about her library mornings.

options.text_zh: 图书馆早上的阳光和书架，下雪后的安静——这些都是不花钱的美好。我觉得你很幸运，每天都能感受到这样的时刻。
options.text_pinyin: Túshūguǎn zǎoshang de yángguāng hé shūjià, xià xuě hòu de ānjìng — zhèxiē dōu shì bù huā qián de měihǎo. Wǒ juéde nǐ hěn xìngyùn, měi tiān dōu néng gǎnshòu dào zhèyàng de shíkè.
options.text_en: Morning sunlight on bookshelves, post-snow quietness — these are beautiful things that cost nothing. I think you're fortunate to experience moments like these every day.
options.feedback: Connects her two images and recognizes the privilege of noticing beauty.

options.text_zh: 你们图书馆有多少本书？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐmen túshūguǎn yǒu duōshǎo běn shū?
options.text_en: How many books does your library have?
options.feedback: Inventory question that misses the atmosphere she's describing.

options.text_zh: 图书馆的工资高不高？
options.text_pinyin: Túshūguǎn de gōngzī gāo bù gāo?
options.text_en: Is the salary good at the library?
options.feedback: Salary question during a moment of aesthetic appreciation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 幸运……也许吧。不过我觉得这不完全是运气。很多人也有这样的时刻，只是太忙了没注意到。美好的东西一直都在，只是我们有没有停下来看。
turns.text_pinyin: Xìngyùn…… yěxǔ ba. Búguò wǒ juéde zhè bù wánquán shì yùnqi. Hěn duō rén yě yǒu zhèyàng de shíkè, zhǐ shì tài máng le méi zhùyì dào. Měihǎo de dōngxi yīzhí dōu zài, zhǐ shì wǒmen yǒu méiyǒu tíng xiàlái kàn.
turns.text_en: Fortunate... maybe. But I don't think it's entirely luck. Many people have moments like these too, they're just too busy to notice. Beautiful things are always there — it's just whether we stop to look.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her philosophy of noticing.

options.text_zh: 就是。也许我们不需要去很远的地方找美好的东西。就在每天的生活里——早上的阳光，窗外的雪，一杯热茶。关键是要愿意停下来。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ shuō de duì. Yěxǔ wǒmen bù xūyào qù hěn yuǎn de dìfāng zhǎo měihǎo de dōngxi. Jiù zài měi tiān de shēnghuó lǐ — zǎoshang de yángguāng, chuāng wài de xuě, yī bēi rè chá. Guānjiàn shì yào yuànyì tíng xiàlái.
options.text_en: You're right. Maybe we don't need to go far to find beautiful things. They're right in daily life — morning sunlight, snow outside, a cup of hot tea. The key is being willing to stop.
options.feedback: Grounded and warm — brings the conversation back to the present moment.

options.text_zh: 这个茶叶是什么牌子的？
options.text_pinyin: Zhège cháyè shì shénme páizi de?
options.text_en: What brand is this tea?
options.feedback: Consumer question during a philosophical moment.

options.text_zh: 你平时周末做什么？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ píngshí zhōumò zuò shénme?
options.text_en: What do you usually do on weekends?
options.feedback: Small talk that disrupts the contemplative thread.

tree.cultural_note: The Chinese appreciation for snow (赏雪) has deep cultural roots, often associated with tea drinking, poetry, and quiet contemplation. The idea that beauty exists in ordinary daily moments (日常之美) resonates with classical Chinese aesthetics, where finding profound meaning in simple things — a cup of tea, a snowfall, morning light — is considered a mark of wisdom.

tree.setup: You're eating at a tiny noodle shop run by a husband and wife. The wife, Auntie Fang, notices you eating alone and starts chatting. The shop has only six tables.
tree.setup_zh: 你在一家夫妻开的小面馆吃饭。老板娘方阿姨注意到你一个人吃饭，开始跟你聊天。店里只有六张桌子。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 一个人吃啊？没关系，一个人吃也挺好的。你知道吗，来我们店的人，很多都是一个人。有的人不是没有朋友，就是想安安静静吃一碗面。
turns.text_pinyin: Yīge rén chī a? Méi guānxi, yīge rén chī yě tǐng hǎo de. Nǐ zhīdào ma, lái wǒmen diàn de rén, hěn duō dōu shì yīge rén. Yǒude rén bú shì méiyǒu péngyǒu, jiù shì xiǎng ān'ānjìngjìng chī yī wǎn miàn.
turns.text_en: Eating alone? That's fine, eating alone can be nice. You know, many people who come to our shop are alone. It's not that they don't have friends — they just want to quietly eat a bowl of noodles.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about eating alone.

options.text_zh: 我也觉得一个人吃饭有一个人的好处。不用聊天，不用考虑别人想吃什么，就专心感受每一口的味道。这碗面真的很好吃。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yě juéde yīge rén chīfàn yǒu yīge rén de hǎochù. Bú yòng liáo tiān, bú yòng kǎolǜ biérén xiǎng chī shénme, jiù zhuānxīn gǎnshòu měi yī kǒu de wèidào. Zhè wǎn miàn zhēn de hěn hǎochī.
options.text_en: I think eating alone has its own advantages too. No need to chat, no need to consider what others want to eat — just focusing on the taste of every bite. This bowl of noodles is really delicious.
options.feedback: Affirms solitary dining as positive — ends with genuine appreciation for the food.

options.text_zh: 你们这个面是什么面？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐmen zhège miàn shì shénme miàn?
options.text_en: What kind of noodles are these?
options.feedback: Menu question that skips past her thoughtful observation.

options.text_zh: 附近还有别的饭馆吗？
options.text_pinyin: Fùjìn hái yǒu bié de fànguǎn ma?
options.text_en: Are there other restaurants nearby?
options.feedback: Asking about competitors to the person cooking your food.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 谢谢你。这个面的做法是我婆婆教我的。她说好的面不需要太多东西，汤底要慢慢熬，面要手工做。简单的东西做好了，就是最好的。
turns.text_pinyin: Xièxie nǐ. Zhège miàn de zuòfǎ shì wǒ pópo jiāo wǒ de. Tā shuō hǎo de miàn bù xūyào tài duō dōngxi, tāng dǐ yào mànman áo, miàn yào shǒugōng zuò. Jiǎndān de dōngxi zuò hǎo le, jiù shì zuì hǎo de.
turns.text_en: Thank you. My mother-in-law taught me how to make these noodles. She said good noodles don't need too many ingredients — the broth should be simmered slowly, the noodles made by hand. Simple things done well — that's the best.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her cooking philosophy.

options.text_zh: 「简单的东西做好了就是最好的」——不只是说做面吧，也是一种生活的道理。不用追求太复杂的东西，把小事认真做好就够了。
options.text_pinyin: 'Jiǎndān de dōngxi zuò hǎo le jiù shì zuì hǎo de' — bù zhǐ shì shuō zuò miàn ba, yě shì yī zhǒng shēnghuó de dàolǐ. Bú yòng zhuīqiú tài fùzá de dōngxi, bǎ xiǎo shì rènzhēn zuò hǎo jiù gòu le.
options.text_en: 'Simple things done well is the best' — not just about noodles, right? It's a life philosophy. No need for complexity; doing small things well is enough.
options.feedback: Extends the cooking wisdom into life philosophy — appreciates depth in simplicity.

options.text_zh: 你婆婆还在吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ pópo hái zài ma?
options.text_en: Is your mother-in-law still alive?
options.feedback: Potentially painful question that shifts away from the positive message.

options.text_zh: 你们每天能卖多少碗面？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐmen měi tiān néng mài duōshǎo wǎn miàn?
options.text_en: How many bowls of noodles do you sell each day?
options.feedback: Business metrics during a moment of shared wisdom.

tree.cultural_note: Small family-run noodle shops (面馆) are a cornerstone of Chinese food culture. Many recipes are passed down through generations, often from mother-in-law to daughter-in-law. The philosophy of 简单做好 (doing simple things well) reflects a broader Chinese cultural value that mastery lies not in complexity but in the patient perfection of fundamentals.

tree.setup: You're sitting on a park bench in the late afternoon. A retired calligrapher, Mr. Xu, sits down beside you. He's carrying a brush and a bottle of water, and begins writing characters on the ground.
tree.setup_zh: 你在下午坐在公园的长椅上。一位退休的书法家徐先生坐到你旁边。他带着一支毛笔和一瓶水，开始在地上写字。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你不介意吧？我每天下午都来这里用水写字。写完了就干了，干了就没了。有人说这是浪费时间，但我觉得这就是最好的练习。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ bù jièyì ba? Wǒ měi tiān xiàwǔ dōu lái zhèlǐ yòng shuǐ xiě zì. Xiě wán le jiù gān le, gān le jiù méi le. Yǒu rén shuō zhè shì làngfèi shíjiān, dàn wǒ juéde zhè jiù shì zuì hǎo de liànxí.
turns.text_en: You don't mind, do you? I come here every afternoon to write characters with water. Once written they dry, once dry they're gone. Some people say it's a waste of time, but I think it's the best kind of practice.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his water calligraphy.

options.text_zh: 写了就没了？那要是写了一个特别好的字呢，也没了？
options.text_pinyin: Xiě le jiù méi le? Nà yàoshi xiě le yí gè tèbié hǎo de zì ne, yě méi le?
options.text_en: Written and then gone? But what if you write a really good one — that's gone too?
options.feedback: He stops mid-stroke and looks at you. 'That's the hardest part to get used to. But also the best part.'

options.text_zh: 我小时候也学过书法，后来就没练了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ xiǎo shíhou yě xué guò shūfǎ, hòulái jiù méi liàn le.
options.text_en: I studied calligraphy as a kid too, but I stopped practicing.
options.feedback: He nods politely. 'A lot of people stopped.' He goes back to writing. He was talking about the water, not the history.

options.text_zh: 水干得快吗？天热的时候是不是写不完就没了？
options.text_pinyin: Shuǐ gān de kuài ma? Tiān rè de shíhou shì bú shì xiě bù wán jiù méi le?
options.text_en: Does the water dry fast? On hot days does it vanish before you finish?
options.feedback: He answers — '夏天确实干得快' — but you can tell he wanted to talk about something less practical.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对，特别好的也没了。年轻的时候我总想写出完美的字，写了一幅不满意就很生气。后来我想通了——字好不好其实不重要，写的那几分钟，心是安静的。
turns.text_pinyin: Duì, tèbié hǎo de yě méi le. Niánqīng de shíhou wǒ zǒng xiǎng xiě chū wánměi de zì, xiě le yī fú bù mǎnyì jiù hěn shēngqì. Hòulái wǒ xiǎng tōng le — zì hǎo bù hǎo qíshí bú zhòngyào, xiě de nà jǐ fēnzhōng, xīn shì ānjìng de.
turns.text_en: Right, even the good ones vanish. When I was young I always wanted to write perfect characters — I'd get angry if a piece wasn't good enough. Eventually I figured it out: whether the character is good doesn't really matter. Those few minutes of writing — the heart is quiet.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to what he said about letting go of perfection.

options.text_zh: 我也想这样。但是工作上不行，什么都要有结果。也许这就是你来公园写字的原因——这里不需要结果。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yě xiǎng zhèyàng. Dànshì gōngzuò shàng bùxíng, shénme dōu yào yǒu jiéguǒ. Yěxǔ zhè jiùshì nǐ lái gōngyuán xiě zì de yuányīn — zhèlǐ bù xūyào jiéguǒ.
options.text_en: I wish I could be like that. But at work everything needs a result. Maybe that's why you come to the park to write — here, no result is needed.
options.feedback: He puts the brush down for a moment. 'You already understand. You just need a place that lets you forget about results for a while.'

options.text_zh: 你以前做什么工作？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǐqián zuò shénme gōngzuò?
options.text_en: What did you do for work before?
options.feedback: He answers — he was a middle school teacher — but the moment about letting go has passed.

options.text_zh: 我觉得写得好不好还是很重要的吧。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde xiě de hǎo bù hǎo háishi hěn zhòngyào de ba.
options.text_en: I think whether you write well still matters though.
options.feedback: He looks at you a beat too long. 'You sound like the old me.' He picks up the brush and writes another character. It dries before you can decide if it was good.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对，不需要结果的地方。来，你想试试吗？不用怕写不好。写完了，风一吹就没了。
turns.text_pinyin: Duì, bù xūyào jiéguǒ de dìfāng. Lái, nǐ xiǎng shì shì ma? Bú yòng pà xiě bù hǎo. Xiě wán le, fēng yī chuī jiù méi le.
turns.text_en: Right, a place where no result is needed. Come, want to try? Don't be afraid of writing badly. Once it's done, the wind blows and it's gone.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: He's offering you the brush.

options.text_zh: 好。我写一个「水」字吧，用水写「水」，挺有意思的。
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo. Wǒ xiě yí gè 'shuǐ' zì ba, yòng shuǐ xiě 'shuǐ', tǐng yǒu yìsi de.
options.text_en: Sure. I'll write the character for 'water' — writing 'water' with water, that's kind of funny.
options.feedback: He laughs — a real laugh, not a polite one. 'That's good. Nobody's thought of that before.'

options.text_zh: 不了，我的字太丑了，你看了会难过的。
options.text_pinyin: Bù le, wǒ de zì tài chǒu le, nǐ kàn le huì nánguò de.
options.text_en: No thanks, my writing is so ugly it would make you sad.
options.feedback: He shakes his head. 'It'll be gone in two minutes. Who's going to be sad?' But he doesn't push.

options.text_zh: 我手上有点脏，下次吧。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ shǒu shàng yǒu diǎn zāng, xià cì ba.
options.text_en: My hands are a bit dirty, maybe next time.
options.feedback: He glances at the water bottle. 'You're writing with water on pavement.' But he lets it go. '那下次见。'

tree.cultural_note: Water calligraphy (地书, dìshū) is a beloved practice in Chinese parks, where retirees write characters on pavement with large brushes dipped in water. The ephemeral nature of the practice — characters that vanish as they dry — embodies Buddhist and Daoist ideas about impermanence and non-attachment. It's also a social activity, often drawing small crowds of appreciative onlookers.

tree.setup: It's late evening on a nearly empty subway car. You and a young graphic designer, Xiao Lin, are the only passengers. The train hums softly through the tunnels.
tree.setup_zh: 深夜，地铁车厢里几乎没有人。你和一个年轻的设计师小林是仅有的乘客。列车在隧道里轻轻地响。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你有没有发现，晚上坐地铁的感觉完全不一样？白天挤在人群里，你只想快点到站。现在车厢空了，反而不想下车了。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu fāxiàn, wǎnshang zuò dìtiě de gǎnjué wánquán bù yīyàng? Báitiān jǐ zài rénqún lǐ, nǐ zhǐ xiǎng kuài diǎn dào zhàn. Xiànzài chēxiāng kōng le, fǎn'ér bù xiǎng xià chē le.
turns.text_en: Have you noticed how riding the subway at night feels completely different? During the day, packed in with everyone, you just want to reach your stop. Now that the car is empty, I don't even want to get off.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about the empty subway.

options.text_zh: 我也有这种感觉。白天地铁是工具，晚上地铁变成了一个移动的小房间。窗外是黑的隧道，车里只有灯光和安静。像一个属于自己的空间。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yě yǒu zhè zhǒng gǎnjué. Báitiān dìtiě shì gōngjù, wǎnshang dìtiě biàn chéng le yīge yídòng de xiǎo fángjiān. Chuāng wài shì hēi de suìdào, chē lǐ zhǐ yǒu dēngguāng hé ānjìng. Xiàng yīge shǔyú zìjǐ de kōngjiān.
options.text_en: I feel the same way. During the day the subway is a tool; at night it becomes a moving little room. Outside is the dark tunnel, inside is just light and quiet. Like a space that belongs to you.
options.feedback: Transforms mundane transit into a personal sanctuary.

options.text_zh: 你从哪一站上的车？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ cóng nǎ yī zhàn shàng de chē?
options.text_en: Which station did you get on at?
options.feedback: Logistical question that misses the atmospheric observation.

options.text_zh: 晚上坐地铁安全吗？
options.text_pinyin: Wǎnshang zuò dìtiě ānquán ma?
options.text_en: Is it safe to ride the subway at night?
options.feedback: Safety question that disrupts the contemplative mood.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 属于自己的空间，说得好。我做设计的，白天一直盯着电脑屏幕。到了晚上坐在这里，看着窗户上自己的倒影，才有时间想一想今天到底做了什么。
turns.text_pinyin: Shǔyú zìjǐ de kōngjiān, shuō de hǎo. Wǒ zuò shèjì de, báitiān yīzhí dīng zhe diànnǎo píngmù. Dào le wǎnshang zuò zài zhèlǐ, kàn zhe chuānghù shàng zìjǐ de dàoyǐng, cái yǒu shíjiān xiǎng yī xiǎng jīntiān dàodǐ zuò le shénme.
turns.text_en: A space that belongs to you — well put. I'm a designer, I stare at a computer screen all day. At night, sitting here, looking at my reflection in the window, I finally have time to think about what I actually did today.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about reflection in the window.

options.text_zh: 地铁窗户上的倒影很有意思——你看到的是自己，但背景一直在变。好像在提醒你，人还是那个人，但每一天都不一样。
options.text_pinyin: Dìtiě chuānghù shàng de dàoyǐng hěn yǒu yìsi — nǐ kàn dào de shì zìjǐ, dàn bèijǐng yīzhí zài biàn. Hǎoxiàng zài tíxǐng nǐ, rén háishì nàge rén, dàn měi yī tiān dōu bù yīyàng.
options.text_en: The reflection in the subway window is interesting — you see yourself, but the background keeps changing. As if reminding you that you're still the same person, but every day is different.
options.feedback: Observation — uses the subway window as a metaphor for identity and time.

options.text_zh: 你做什么类型的设计？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zuò shénme lèixíng de shèjì?
options.text_en: What type of design do you do?
options.feedback: Career question that breaks the reflective mood.

options.text_zh: 你每天加班到很晚吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ měi tiān jiābān dào hěn wǎn ma?
options.text_en: Do you work overtime until late every day?
options.feedback: Work schedule question during a contemplative exchange.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese subway systems are among the busiest in the world during rush hours. The contrast between the daytime crush and the late-night emptiness creates a distinct atmosphere that many urban workers cherish. The practice of looking at one's reflection in dark subway windows is a universally recognized moment of urban solitude.

tree.setup: Your upstairs neighbor, Uncle Chen, has invited you to see his rooftop garden. He grows vegetables and flowers in recycled containers. The sunset paints the sky orange.
tree.setup_zh: 楼上的邻居陈叔叔邀请你去看他的楼顶花园。他用回收的容器种蔬菜和花。夕阳把天空染成了橙色。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 很多人不知道我在这里种了一个花园。我用旧桶、旧箱子，什么都可以种。你看这些西红柿，再过两个星期就可以吃了。
turns.text_pinyin: Hěn duō rén bù zhīdào wǒ zài zhèlǐ zhòng le yīge huāyuán. Wǒ yòng jiù tǒng, jiù xiāngzi, shénme dōu kěyǐ zhòng. Nǐ kàn zhèxiē xīhóngshì, zài guò liǎng ge xīngqī jiù kěyǐ chī le.
turns.text_en: Most people don't know I've grown a garden up here. I use old buckets, old boxes — you can grow things in anything. Look at these tomatoes, two more weeks and they'll be ready to eat.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the rooftop garden.

options.text_zh: 在城市的楼顶种菜，这个想法很好。在这么高的地方，离天空很近，离地面很远，好像有了一个秘密的世界。
options.text_pinyin: Zài chéngshì de lóudǐng zhòng cài, zhège xiǎngfǎ hěn hǎo. Zài zhème gāo de dìfāng, lí tiānkōng hěn jìn, lí dìmiàn hěn yuǎn, hǎoxiàng yǒu le yīge mìmì de shìjiè.
options.text_en: Growing vegetables on a city rooftop — what a wonderful idea. Up this high, close to the sky and far from the ground, it's like having a secret world.
options.feedback: Captures the magic of the space — sees it as more than gardening.

options.text_zh: 物业允许你在楼顶种东西吗？
options.text_pinyin: Wùyè yǔnxǔ nǐ zài lóudǐng zhòng dōngxi ma?
options.text_en: Does the property management allow you to grow things on the roof?
options.feedback: Regulatory concern that deflates the wonder.

options.text_zh: 楼顶上蚊子多不多？
options.text_pinyin: Lóudǐng shàng wénzi duō bù duō?
options.text_en: Are there a lot of mosquitoes on the roof?
options.feedback: Pest question during a moment of beauty.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 秘密的世界——哈哈，是啊。我退休以后不知道做什么，每天在家看电视，觉得自己没用了。后来我想，我小时候在农村长大，最会做的事就是种东西。所以我就在楼顶开始种了。
turns.text_pinyin: Mìmì de shìjiè — hāhā, shì a. Wǒ tuìxiū yǐhòu bù zhīdào zuò shénme, měi tiān zài jiā kàn diànshì, juéde zìjǐ méi yòng le. Hòulái wǒ xiǎng, wǒ xiǎo shíhou zài nóngcūn zhǎng dà, zuì huì zuò de shì jiù shì zhòng dōngxi. Suǒyǐ wǒ jiù zài lóudǐng kāishǐ zhòng le.
turns.text_en: A secret world — ha, yeah. After I retired I didn't know what to do. Watching TV at home every day, feeling useless. Then I thought, I grew up in the countryside, and what I know best is growing things. So I started growing on the rooftop.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his story of finding purpose after retirement.

options.text_zh: 用他的工具继续做他教你的事——这是最好的纪念。他不在了，但手艺还活着。每修好一块表，就是他的影响在继续。
options.text_pinyin: Yòng tā de gōngjù jìxù zuò tā jiāo nǐ de shì — zhè shì zuì hǎo de jìniàn. Tā bú zài le, dàn shǒuyì hái huó zhe. Měi xiū hǎo yī kuài biǎo, jiù shì tā de yǐngxiǎng zài jìxù.
options.text_en: Using his tools to keep doing what he taught you — that's the best memorial. He's gone, but the craft lives on. Every watch you fix is his influence continuing.
options.feedback: Validates his struggle and reframes his garden as a form of meaningful living.

options.text_zh: 你种的菜够自己吃吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhòng de cài gòu zìjǐ chī ma?
options.text_en: Is what you grow enough for yourself?
options.feedback: Practical yield question that misses the emotional story.

options.text_zh: 退休金够花吗？
options.text_pinyin: Tuìxiū jīn gòu huā ma?
options.text_en: Is the pension enough?
options.feedback: Financial question during a conversation about purpose.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 用另一种方式活着……我喜欢这个说法。你知道吗，每天早上我第一件事就是上来看看它们。浇浇水，拔拔草。看着一颗种子慢慢长大，你会觉得时间是有意义的。
turns.text_pinyin: Yòng lìng yī zhǒng fāngshì huó zhe…… wǒ xǐhuān zhège shuōfǎ. Nǐ zhīdào ma, měi tiān zǎoshang wǒ dì yī jiàn shì jiù shì shàng lái kàn kàn tāmen. Jiāo jiāo shuǐ, bá bá cǎo. Kàn zhe yī kē zhǒngzi mànman zhǎng dà, nǐ huì juéde shíjiān shì yǒu yìyì de.
turns.text_en: Living in a different way... I like that. You know, every morning the first thing I do is come up to check on them. Water a little, pull some weeds. Watching a seed slowly grow — it makes you feel that time has meaning.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his morning ritual.

options.text_zh: 看着种子慢慢长大，这就是最好的生活节奏吧。不用急，不用赶，每天做一点，慢慢来。谢谢你带我上来看，这个花园让我心情好了很多。
options.text_pinyin: Kàn zhe zhǒngzi mànman zhǎng dà, zhè jiù shì zuì hǎo de shēnghuó jiézòu ba. Bú yòng jí, bú yòng gǎn, měi tiān zuò yīdiǎn, mànman lái. Xièxie nǐ dài wǒ shàng lái kàn, zhège huāyuán ràng wǒ xīnqíng hǎo le hěn duō.
options.text_en: Watching seeds grow slowly — that's the best pace of life. No rushing, no hurrying, doing a little each day, taking it slow. Thank you for bringing me up here. This garden has made me feel much better.
options.feedback: Warm and grateful — embraces the pace and expresses genuine appreciation.

options.text_zh: 你冬天怎么办？天气太冷了种不了吧？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ dōngtiān zěnme bàn? Tiānqì tài lěng le zhòng bù liǎo ba?
options.text_en: What do you do in winter? Too cold to grow anything, right?
options.feedback: Seasonal logistics instead of savoring the moment.

options.text_zh: 我也想种菜，但是没时间。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yě xiǎng zhòng cài, dànshì méi shíjiān.
options.text_en: I want to grow vegetables too, but I don't have time.
options.feedback: Self-focused response that misses the invitation to appreciate.

tree.cultural_note: Rooftop gardening in Chinese cities has become increasingly popular, especially among retirees who grew up in rural areas. For many, it reconnects them with the agricultural rhythms of their childhood. The practice addresses both the practical desire for fresh food and the emotional need for purpose after retirement, a significant issue in China's rapidly aging society.

tree.setup: You wander into a small record store in an old neighborhood. The owner, Lao Ma, is playing a vinyl record. The warm crackle fills the quiet shop.
tree.setup_zh: 你走进了一个老街区里的小唱片店。老板老马正在放一张黑胶唱片。温暖的沙沙声充满了安静的小店。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你听，这种沙沙的声音，数字音乐里没有。很多人觉得这是噪音，但我觉得这就是音乐的呼吸。就像老房子会发出声音一样，有生命的东西都会。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ tīng, zhè zhǒng shāsha de shēngyīn, shùzì yīnyuè lǐ méiyǒu. Hěn duō rén juéde zhè shì zàoyīn, dàn wǒ juéde zhè jiù shì yīnyuè de hūxī. Jiù xiàng lǎo fángzi huì fāchū shēngyīn yīyàng, yǒu shēngmìng de dōngxi dōu huì.
turns.text_en: Listen — that crackling sound, you won't find it in digital music. Many people think it's noise, but I think it's music breathing. Just like old houses make sounds — anything alive does.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his observation about vinyl.

options.text_zh: 音乐的呼吸——我喜欢这个说法。数字音乐太干净了，干净到感觉不到它是真实的。这种沙沙声反而让人觉得温暖，好像音乐就在你旁边。
options.text_pinyin: Yīnyuè de hūxī — wǒ xǐhuān zhège shuōfǎ. Shùzì yīnyuè tài gānjìng le, gānjìng dào gǎnjué bú dào tā shì zhēnshí de. Zhè zhǒng shāsha shēng fǎn'ér ràng rén juéde wēnnuǎn, hǎoxiàng yīnyuè jiù zài nǐ pángbiān.
options.text_en: Music breathing — I love that way of putting it. Digital music is too clean, so clean it doesn't feel real. This crackling actually feels warm, as if the music is right beside you.
options.feedback: Engages with the sensory idea — imperfection as warmth and presence.

options.text_zh: 其实现在的数字音乐音质已经很好了，比唱片清楚多了，而且也方便。这种沙沙声应该算是一种缺点吧。
options.text_pinyin: Qíshí xiànzài de shùzì yīnyuè yīnzhì yǐjīng hěn hǎo le, bǐ chàngpiàn qīngchǔ duō le, érqiě yě fāngbiàn. Zhè zhǒng shāsha shēng yīnggāi suàn shì yī zhǒng quēdiǎn ba.
options.text_en: Actually, digital music has great sound quality now — much clearer than vinyl, and more convenient. That crackling sound is really a defect, isn't it?
options.feedback: Lao Ma smiles patiently: 'A defect? Then wrinkles on a face are defects too. But they tell you someone has lived.'

options.text_zh: 现在谁还买唱片啊？都用手机听了。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài shéi hái mǎi chàngpiàn a? Dōu yòng shǒujī tīng le.
options.text_en: Who still buys records? Everyone listens on their phone.
options.feedback: Dismissive of his passion.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对。而且你知道吗，听唱片有一个好处——你不能跳过。从第一首到最后一首，你必须完整地听。现在的人听音乐，不喜欢就下一首。但有些歌，你要听几遍才能真正听懂。
turns.text_pinyin: Duì. Érqiě nǐ zhīdào ma, tīng chàngpiàn yǒu yīge hǎochù — nǐ bù néng tiào guò. Cóng dì yī shǒu dào zuìhòu yī shǒu, nǐ bìxū wánzhěng de tīng. Xiànzài de rén tīng yīnyuè, bù xǐhuān jiù xià yī shǒu. Dàn yǒuxiē gē, nǐ yào tīng jǐ biàn cái néng zhēnzhèng tīng dǒng.
turns.text_en: Exactly. And you know, listening to records has one advantage — you can't skip. From first track to last, you have to listen all the way through. People now, if they don't like a song, they skip. But some songs, you need to hear them several times before you truly understand.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about patience in listening.

options.text_zh: 对，我们太习惯跳过不喜欢的东西了。但最好的东西不是一开始就喜欢的，是慢慢理解以后才发现它的好。音乐是这样，人也是。
options.text_pinyin: Duì, wǒmen tài xíguàn tiào guò bù xǐhuan de dōngxi le. Dàn zuì hǎo de dōngxi bú shì yī kāishǐ jiù xǐhuan de, shì mànman lǐjiě yǐhòu cái fāxiàn tā de hǎo. Yīnyuè shì zhèyàng, rén yě shì.
options.text_en: Right, we're too used to skipping what we don't like. But the best things aren't what you like at first — they reveal themselves after you slowly understand them. Music is like that, people too.
options.feedback: Extends the music insight to human relationships — patience as a virtue.

options.text_zh: 你这里有流行音乐的唱片吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhèlǐ yǒu liúxíng yīnyuè de chàngpiàn ma?
options.text_en: Do you have pop music records here?
options.feedback: Genre request during a discussion about listening deeply.

options.text_zh: 你觉得哪个牌子的耳机最好？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ juéde nǎge páizi de ěrjī zuì hǎo?
options.text_en: What brand of headphones do you think is best?
options.feedback: Equipment question that misses the point about patience and attention.

tree.cultural_note: Vinyl record shops have experienced a quiet resurgence in Chinese cities, often run by music enthusiasts rather than businesspeople. The Chinese concept of 慢生活 (slow living) aligns with the vinyl listening experience — deliberate, unhurried, requiring full attention. These shops serve as cultural refuges for people seeking an alternative to the speed of digital consumption.

tree.setup: You're taking a walk after dinner along a river. Your colleague Xiao Wei, who usually seems stressed at work, is walking beside you. The evening is warm and the river reflects city lights.
tree.setup_zh: 你在晚饭后沿着河边散步。平时在工作中总是看起来很紧张的同事小韦走在你旁边。晚上很温暖，河水倒映着城市的灯光。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 谢谢你约我出来散步。在办公室的时候，我总觉得喘不过气来。但走在河边，听着水的声音，好像所有的压力都变小了。
turns.text_pinyin: Xièxie nǐ yuē wǒ chūlái sànbù. Zài bàngōngshì de shíhou, wǒ zǒng juéde chuǎn bú guò qì lái. Dàn zǒu zài hé biān, tīng zhe shuǐ de shēngyīn, hǎoxiàng suǒyǒu de yālì dōu biàn xiǎo le.
turns.text_en: Thanks for inviting me for a walk. In the office I always feel like I can't breathe. But walking by the river, listening to the water, it's like all the pressure shrinks.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to Xiao Wei about the pressure.

options.text_zh: 没事没事。不过我这边风特别大，都快穿外套了。陈姐，你那边风没这么大吧？
options.text_pinyin: Méi shì méi shì. Búguò wǒ zhè biān fēng tèbié dà, dōu kuài chuān wàitào le. Chén jiě, nǐ nà biān fēng méi zhème dà ba?
options.text_en: No problem, no problem. But the airflow over here is really strong, I'm almost putting on a jacket. Sister Chen, the breeze isn't this strong on your side, right?
options.feedback: Compassionate and wise — distinguishes between fixing problems and changing your relationship to them.

options.text_zh: 你工作压力大是因为什么？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ gōngzuò yālì dà shì yīnwèi shénme?
options.text_en: What causes your work stress?
options.feedback: Probing the source of stress when she's trying to escape it.

options.text_zh: 你有没有想过换工作？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu xiǎng guò huàn gōngzuò?
options.text_en: Have you thought about changing jobs?
options.feedback: Career advice when she needs presence, not solutions.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对啊。事情还在，但我会不一样。我以前一直觉得，只有把所有事情都做完了才能休息。但事情永远做不完，我却越来越累。
turns.text_pinyin: Duì a. Shìqíng hái zài, dàn wǒ huì bù yīyàng. Wǒ yǐqián yīzhí juéde, zhǐyǒu bǎ suǒyǒu shìqíng dōu zuò wán le cái néng xiūxi. Dàn shìqíng yǒngyuǎn zuò bù wán, wǒ què yuè lái yuè lèi.
turns.text_en: Right. The things are still there, but I'll be different. I used to think I could only rest after finishing everything. But things never end, and I just get more and more tired.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about the trap of endless work.

options.text_zh: 热水是好主意。陈姐，我看到一个研究说26度效率最高。咱们折中一下调到24？你那边不会太热，我也不用穿棉袄了。
options.text_pinyin: Rè shuǐ shì hǎo zhǔyì. Chén jiě, wǒ kàn dào yī ge yánjiū shuō èrshíliù dù xiàolǜ zuì gāo. Zánmen zhézhōng yīxià tiáo dào èrshísì? Nǐ nà biān bú huì tài rè, wǒ yě bú yòng chuān miánaǒ le.
options.text_en: Hot water is a good idea. Sister Chen, a study says 26 degrees is best for productivity. How about we compromise at 24? You won't be too hot, and I won't need a padded jacket.
options.feedback: Reframes rest as necessity, not luxury — a healing perspective.

options.text_zh: 你应该跟领导说，工作量太大了。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi gēn lǐngdǎo shuō, gōngzuòliàng tài dà le.
options.text_en: You should tell your boss the workload is too heavy.
options.feedback: Workplace advice that shifts from emotional support to problem-solving.

options.text_zh: 你试过瑜伽吗？听说对减压很有用。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ shì guò yújiā ma? Tīngshuō duì jiǎnyā hěn yǒuyòng.
options.text_en: Have you tried yoga? I hear it's good for stress relief.
options.feedback: Wellness recommendation instead of genuine emotional engagement.

tree.cultural_note: The Chinese tradition of 饭后散步 (post-dinner walking) is deeply embedded in daily life, often expressed in the proverb 饭后百步走，活到九十九 (walk a hundred steps after eating, live to ninety-nine). For many urban workers facing intense workplace pressure (996 culture), evening walks along rivers and parks serve as essential decompression rituals.

tree.setup: You've brought a broken watch to a tiny repair shop run by Master Qian, an elderly watchmaker. His shop is barely bigger than a closet, filled with tiny tools and ticking clocks.
tree.setup_zh: 你带着一块坏了的手表来到钱师傅的小修理铺。他是一位年迈的修表匠。他的店比一个柜子大不了多少，里面满是小工具和滴答作响的钟表。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 放这儿吧。我先看看。现在很少有人修表了，都说买新的更便宜。但有些东西的价值不在于多少钱，而在于它跟了你多少年。
turns.text_pinyin: Fàng zhèr ba. Wǒ xiān kàn kàn. Xiànzài hěn shǎo yǒu rén xiū biǎo le, dōu shuō mǎi xīn de gèng piányi. Dàn yǒuxiē dōngxi de jiàzhí bú zàiyú duōshǎo qián, ér zàiyú tā gēn le nǐ duōshǎo nián.
turns.text_en: Set it down here. Let me take a look. Few people repair watches anymore — they all say buying new is cheaper. But some things' value isn't about money, it's about how many years they've been with you.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about the value of old things.

options.text_zh: 是的，这块表是我爸爸给我的。虽然不贵，但每次看到它，我就想到他。这种东西买新的代替不了。
options.text_pinyin: Shì de, zhè kuài biǎo shì wǒ bàba gěi wǒ de. Suīrán bú guì, dàn měi cì kàn dào tā, wǒ jiù xiǎng dào tā. Zhè zhǒng dōngxi mǎi xīn de dàitì bù liǎo.
options.text_en: Yes, this watch was from my father. It's not expensive, but every time I see it, I think of him. This kind of thing can't be replaced by buying new.
options.feedback: Personal and genuine — connects the watch to emotional meaning.

options.text_zh: 不过说实话，修好了也不一定走得准。现在的电子表又便宜又准，功能也多。也许买个新的更实际。
options.text_pinyin: Búguò shuō shíhuà, xiū hǎo le yě bù yīdìng zǒu de zhǔn. Xiànzài de diànzǐ biǎo yòu piányi yòu zhǔn, gōngnéng yě duō. Yěxǔ mǎi ge xīn de gèng shíjì.
options.text_en: But honestly, even repaired it might not keep good time. Digital watches today are cheap, accurate, and full of features. Maybe buying new is more practical.
options.feedback: Master Qian sets down his loupe and looks at you: 'If you only wanted accuracy, you wouldn't have walked into my shop.'

options.text_zh: 现在大家都用手机看时间了。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài dàjiā dōu yòng shǒujī kàn shíjiān le.
options.text_en: Everyone uses their phone to check time now.
options.feedback: Technology observation that dismisses both the watch and his craft.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你爸爸给你的……那我一定帮你修好。我也有这样的东西。这把小工具是我师傅留给我的，用了四十年了。他走了以后，我每天还是用它，好像他还在教我一样。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ bàba gěi nǐ de…… nà wǒ yīdìng bāng nǐ xiū hǎo. Wǒ yě yǒu zhèyàng de dōngxi. Zhè bǎ xiǎo gōngjù shì wǒ shīfu liú gěi wǒ de, yòng le sìshí nián le. Tā zǒu le yǐhòu, wǒ měi tiān háishì yòng tā, hǎoxiàng tā hái zài jiāo wǒ yīyàng.
turns.text_en: From your father... then I'll definitely fix it for you. I have things like that too. This small tool was left to me by my master, I've used it for forty years. After he passed, I still use it every day — as if he's still teaching me.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his connection to his master.

options.text_zh: 挡风板是好主意，我去问问。陈姐，咱俩为了两度温差谈了十分钟，这效率才是真正需要研究的。
options.text_pinyin: Dǎngfēng bǎn shì hǎo zhǔyì, wǒ qù wènwen. Chén jiě, zánmen liǎ wèile liǎng dù wēnchā tán le shí fēnzhōng, zhè xiàolǜ cái shì zhēnzhèng xūyào yánjiū de.
options.text_en: The wind deflector is a good idea, I'll ask about it. Sister Chen, we spent ten minutes negotiating over two degrees — now that's the efficiency worth studying.
options.feedback: Moving and true — frames craftsmanship as living memory.

options.text_zh: 你收过徒弟吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ shōu guò túdì ma?
options.text_en: Have you ever taken on apprentices?
options.feedback: Succession question that shifts from the emotional moment.

options.text_zh: 修表难不难学？
options.text_pinyin: Xiū biǎo nán bù nán xué?
options.text_en: Is watch repair hard to learn?
options.feedback: Vocational question during a tender exchange.

tree.cultural_note: Traditional repair shops (修理铺) are disappearing from Chinese cities as disposable consumer culture takes hold. The master-apprentice (师徒) relationship in Chinese crafts is deeply personal, with tools and techniques passed down as both professional knowledge and emotional inheritance. The concept of 手艺 (shǒuyì, handicraft) carries moral weight — it represents dedication, patience, and a form of identity.

tree.setup: It's a hot afternoon in the office. Your colleague Sister Chen sits near the AC unit and has turned it down to 20 degrees. You sit far from the AC and are freezing. Neither of you will say what this is actually about.
tree.setup_zh: 办公室里一个炎热的下午。你的同事陈姐坐在空调旁边，把温度调到了20度。你坐得离空调很远，已经冻得不行了。你们谁都不会说出这件事的真正含义。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 哎，今天好热啊。我刚才把空调调低了一点，你不介意吧？太热了影响工作效率。我这边靠窗，太阳一直晒。
turns.text_pinyin: Āi, jīntiān hǎo rè a. Wǒ gāngcái bǎ kōngtiáo tiáo dī le yīdiǎn, nǐ bú jièyì ba? Tài rè le yǐngxiǎng gōngzuò xiàolǜ. Wǒ zhè biān kào chuāng, tàiyáng yīzhí shài.
turns.text_en: Oh, it's so hot today. I turned the AC down a bit just now, you don't mind, right? Being too hot affects work efficiency. I'm by the window, the sun keeps hitting me.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: You're wearing a sweater in July. You definitely mind. But she framed it as a question you can't say no to.

options.text_zh: 没事没事。不过我这边好像风特别大，我都快要穿外套了。陈姐，你那边的风没这边大吧？
options.text_pinyin: Méi shì méi shì. Búguò wǒ zhè biān hǎoxiàng fēng tèbié dà, wǒ dōu kuài yào chuān wàitào le. Chén jiě, nǐ nà biān de fēng méi zhè biān dà ba?
options.text_en: No problem, no problem. But the airflow over here seems really strong, I'm almost putting on a jacket. Sister Chen, the breeze isn't as strong on your side, is it?
options.feedback: You've matched her move. She cited 'work efficiency,' you cited 'airflow.' Nobody has said 'I'm cold, turn it up.' The negotiation has begun.

options.text_zh: 太冷了，你能不能调高一点？
options.text_pinyin: Tài lěng le, nǐ néng bù néng tiáo gāo yīdiǎn?
options.text_en: It's too cold, can you turn it up a bit?
options.feedback: She spent two sentences building a case. You've just made a direct request with no justification. In this office, that's bringing a knife to a chess match.

options.text_zh: 好的，你觉得合适就行。
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo de, nǐ juéde héshì jiù xíng.
options.text_en: Sure, whatever you think is best.
options.feedback: You've surrendered the temperature of your workspace for the rest of the afternoon. She'll remember this concession.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 是吗？那可能是出风口的位置问题。不过呢，空调设在20度，其实整体温度大概是24、25度的样子，很正常的。你要是冷的话……可以喝点热水。
turns.text_pinyin: Shì ma? Nà kěnéng shì chūfēngkǒu de wèizhì wèntí. Búguò ne, kōngtiáo shè zài èrshí dù, qíshí zhěngtǐ wēndù dàgài shì èrshísì, èrshíwǔ dù de yàngzi, hěn zhèngcháng de. Nǐ yàoshi lěng de huà ... kěyǐ hē diǎn rè shuǐ.
turns.text_en: Really? That might be the vent position. But you know, setting it at 20 means the overall room temperature is about 24, 25 degrees — very normal. If you're cold... you could drink some hot water.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: She just told you to drink hot water. The universal Chinese response to all problems. She's not budging.

options.text_zh: 我理解。电话说完就完了，但信可以拿在手里看很多遍。看着他的字，就像他在你面前。信和电话不一样，信是留得住的。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ lǐjiě. Diànhuà shuō wán jiù wán le, dàn xìn kěyǐ ná zài shǒu lǐ kàn hěn duō biàn. Kàn zhe tā de zì, jiù xiàng tā zài nǐ miànqián. Xìn hé diànhuà bù yīyàng, xìn shì liú de zhù de.
options.text_en: I understand. A phone call ends, but a letter you can hold and read over and over. His handwriting feels like he's right there. Letters and calls are different — letters stay.
options.feedback: You've escalated from feelings to data, matched her 'efficiency' framing, and proposed a number. The padded jacket image makes it funny without making it hostile.

options.text_zh: 好吧，我去倒点热水。
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo ba, wǒ qù dào diǎn rè shuǐ.
options.text_en: Alright, I'll go pour some hot water.
options.feedback: You're literally doing what she suggested. The temperature stays at 20. She controls the thermostat for the rest of the summer.

options.text_zh: 陈姐，你那边热是因为靠窗，你要不要换个位子？
options.text_pinyin: Chén jiě, nǐ nà biān rè shì yīnwèi kào chuāng, nǐ yào bú yào huàn ge wèizi?
options.text_en: Sister Chen, you're hot because you're by the window. Want to switch seats?
options.feedback: You've proposed she move desks. In most offices, seat assignments are decided by seniority, not thermostat disputes. She's been at that desk for six years.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 26度？哪个研究？……好吧好吧，24就24。不过明天如果太阳更大，我还是得调低一点。你也可以跟行政说给你那边加个挡风板。这样公平吧？
turns.text_pinyin: Èrshíliù dù? Nǎge yánjiū? ... Hǎo ba hǎo ba, èrshísì jiù èrshísì. Búguò míngtiān rúguǒ tàiyáng gèng dà, wǒ háishì děi tiáo dī yīdiǎn. Nǐ yě kěyǐ gēn xíngzhèng shuō gěi nǐ nà biān jiā ge dǎngfēng bǎn. Zhèyàng gōngpíng ba?
turns.text_en: 26 degrees? Which study? ... Fine fine, 24 it is. But tomorrow if the sun's stronger, I'll still need to turn it down. You could also ask admin to put a wind deflector on your side. Fair enough?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: She's conceded two degrees but already laid groundwork for tomorrow's re-negotiation.

options.text_zh: 挡风板是个好主意，我去问问。陈姐，你看咱俩为了两度的温差谈了十分钟，这个效率才是真正需要研究的。
options.text_pinyin: Dǎngfēng bǎn shì ge hǎo zhǔyì, wǒ qù wènwen. Chén jiě, nǐ kàn zánmen liǎ wèile liǎng dù de wēnchā tán le shí fēnzhōng, zhège xiàolǜ cái shì zhēnzhèng xūyào yánjiū de.
options.text_en: The wind deflector is a good idea, I'll ask about it. Sister Chen, look at us — ten minutes of negotiation over two degrees. Now that's the efficiency that really needs studying.
options.feedback: Accepts the deal and names the absurdity without making it personal. She laughs because it's true.

options.text_zh: 好的，谢谢陈姐。那明天再说吧。
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo de, xièxie Chén jiě. Nà míngtiān zài shuō ba.
options.text_en: Okay, thanks Sister Chen. We'll talk about it tomorrow then.
options.feedback: You've accepted a ceasefire that expires in 24 hours. Tomorrow the same ten-minute negotiation starts from zero.

options.text_zh: 24度还是有点低。25行不行？
options.text_pinyin: Èrshísì dù háishì yǒudiǎn dī. Èrshíwǔ xíng bù xíng?
options.text_en: 24 is still a bit low. How about 25?
options.feedback: She just conceded. Immediately pushing for more turns a compromise into an escalation. Pick up the win.

tree.cultural_note: Office AC temperature disputes are a universal workplace phenomenon, but in Chinese offices they carry additional subtext. Seniority often determines who controls the thermostat. The suggestion to 'drink hot water' (喝热水) is such a stereotypical Chinese response to any discomfort that it has become a meme. The real negotiation is rarely about temperature — it's about whose comfort takes priority in a shared space.

tree.setup: You notice an old woman, Grandma Zhou, checking a rusty mailbox outside an apartment building. She looks disappointed. You've seen her check it every day this week.
tree.setup_zh: 你注意到一位老奶奶周奶奶在公寓楼外面查看一个生锈的信箱。她看起来有些失望。你这个星期每天都看到她来查。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 又没有信。我孙子在国外工作，以前每个月给我写一封信。现在都打电话了，方便是方便，但我还是想收到一封手写的信。
turns.text_pinyin: Yòu méiyǒu xìn. Wǒ sūnzi zài guówài gōngzuò, yǐqián měi ge yuè gěi wǒ xiě yī fēng xìn. Xiànzài dōu dǎ diànhuà le, fāngbiàn shì fāngbiàn, dàn wǒ háishì xiǎng shōu dào yī fēng shǒuxiě de xìn.
turns.text_en: No letter again. My grandson works abroad — he used to write me a letter every month. Now it's all phone calls. Convenient, sure, but I still want to receive a handwritten letter.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about handwritten letters.

options.text_zh: 我理解。电话说完就完了，但一封信可以拿在手里看很多遍。看着他的字，就好像他就在你面前一样。信和电话不一样，信是留得住的。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ lǐjiě. Diànhuà shuō wán jiù wán le, dàn yī fēng xìn kěyǐ ná zài shǒu lǐ kàn hěn duō biàn. Kàn zhe tā de zì, jiù hǎoxiàng tā jiù zài nǐ miànqián yīyàng. Xìn hé diànhuà bù yīyàng, xìn shì liú de zhù de.
options.text_en: I understand. A phone call ends when it ends, but a letter you can hold and read again and again. Looking at his handwriting, it's like he's right in front of you. Letters and calls are different — letters stay.
options.feedback: Tender and precise — identifies what makes letters irreplaceable.

options.text_zh: 你孙子在哪个国家？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ sūnzi zài nǎge guójiā?
options.text_en: Which country is your grandson in?
options.feedback: Geography question when she's talking about missing connection.

options.text_zh: 你可以让他发微信给你啊。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ ràng tā fā Wēixìn gěi nǐ a.
options.text_en: You could have him send you WeChat messages.
options.feedback: Tech solution that misses why she values physical letters.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 留得住的，对。我把他以前写的信都放在一个铁盒子里。有时候想他了，就拿出来看看。看着他从小到大字越写越好，就像看着他长大一样。
turns.text_pinyin: Liú de zhù de, duì. Wǒ bǎ tā yǐqián xiě de xìn dōu fàng zài yīge tiě hézi lǐ. Yǒu shíhou xiǎng tā le, jiù ná chūlái kàn kàn. Kàn zhe tā cóng xiǎo dào dà zì yuè xiě yuè hǎo, jiù xiàng kàn zhe tā zhǎng dà yīyàng.
turns.text_en: They stay, yes. I keep all his old letters in a tin box. When I miss him, I take them out and read them. Watching his handwriting improve from childhood to now — it's like watching him grow up.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the tin box of letters.

options.text_zh: 一个铁盒子装着他成长的过程，比照片还珍贵。每封信都是他当时的样子——心情、想法、字迹。这些手机里存不了。
options.text_pinyin: Yī ge tiě hézi zhuāng zhe tā chéngzhǎng de guòchéng, bǐ zhàopiàn hái zhēnguì. Měi fēng xìn dōu shì tā dāngshí de yàngzi — xīnqíng, xiǎngfǎ, zìjì. Zhèxiē shǒujī lǐ cún bù liǎo.
options.text_en: A tin box holding his growth, more precious than any photo. Each letter is who he was then — his mood, thoughts, handwriting. A phone can't store these things.
options.feedback: Sees the letters as a growth record — values the physicality of handwriting.

options.text_zh: 你孙子多大了？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ sūnzi duō dà le?
options.text_en: How old is your grandson?
options.feedback: Age question during a tender moment about memory.

options.text_zh: 铁盒子会不会生锈？你应该换一个。
options.text_pinyin: Tiě hézi huì bú huì shēngxiù? Nǐ yīnggāi huàn yīge.
options.text_en: Won't the tin box rust? You should get a new one.
options.feedback: Container maintenance advice during an emotional story.

tree.cultural_note: The decline of letter writing in China mirrors a global trend, but carries particular weight for elderly Chinese who may not be comfortable with smartphones. For grandparents with family members abroad (留学生 or overseas workers), handwritten letters represented a tangible connection. The Chinese postal system's distinctive green mailboxes are becoming increasingly empty as digital communication replaces physical mail.

tree.setup: You're cycling slowly along a river path in the early morning. A retired factory worker, Uncle Jiang, is cycling beside you. The air is fresh and birds are singing.
tree.setup_zh: 你在清晨沿着河边的小路慢慢骑自行车。一位退休工人蒋叔叔骑在你旁边。空气新鲜，鸟儿在唱歌。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你也是早起骑车的人啊？我每天早上五点半就出来了。这个时候路上没什么人，风吹在脸上，整个人都清醒了。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ yě shì zǎo qǐ qí chē de rén a? Wǒ měi tiān zǎoshang wǔ diǎn bàn jiù chūlái le. Zhège shíhou lù shàng méi shénme rén, fēng chuī zài liǎn shàng, zhěnggè rén dōu qīngxǐng le.
turns.text_en: You're an early morning cyclist too? I come out at five-thirty every day. At this hour there's barely anyone on the road, the wind on your face, your whole body wakes up.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about early morning cycling.

options.text_zh: 五点半，那你看到的是大多数人看不到的城市。这个时候的光线最美，空气最新鲜，好像整个世界刚刚醒来，还没有开始忙碌。
options.text_pinyin: Wǔ diǎn bàn, nà nǐ kàn dào de shì dà duōshù rén kàn bú dào de chéngshì. Zhège shíhou de guāngxiàn zuì měi, kōngqì zuì xīnxiān, hǎoxiàng zhěnggè shìjiè gāng gāng xǐng lái, hái méiyǒu kāishǐ mánglù.
options.text_en: Five-thirty — then you see a city most people never see. The light at this hour is the most beautiful, the air the freshest. It's like the whole world has just woken up and hasn't started being busy yet.
options.feedback: Appreciates the privilege of early mornings — sees what others miss.

options.text_zh: 你骑的什么牌子的自行车？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ qí de shénme páizi de zìxíngchē?
options.text_en: What brand of bicycle do you ride?
options.feedback: Equipment question that ignores the morning atmosphere.

options.text_zh: 你不觉得太早了吗？我一般八点才起床。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ bù juéde tài zǎo le ma? Wǒ yībān bā diǎn cái qǐchuáng.
options.text_en: Don't you think that's too early? I usually don't get up until eight.
options.feedback: Self-focused comparison that misses the beauty he's describing.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对，还没有开始忙碌。我年轻的时候在工厂上班，每天都很忙，从来没有时间看看周围的风景。退休以后才发现，原来我住的地方这么美。
turns.text_pinyin: Duì, hái méiyǒu kāishǐ mánglù. Wǒ niánqīng de shíhou zài gōngchǎng shàngbān, měi tiān dōu hěn máng, cónglái méiyǒu shíjiān kàn kàn zhōuwéi de fēngjǐng. Tuìxiū yǐhòu cái fāxiàn, yuánlái wǒ zhù de dìfāng zhème měi.
turns.text_en: Right, before the busyness starts. When I was young I worked at the factory, always busy, never time to look at the scenery around me. Only after retiring did I discover how beautiful the place I live actually is.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about discovering beauty nearby.

options.text_zh: 我们总是觉得美好的东西在远方，其实就在身边。只是忙的时候看不到。也许退休给了你一双新的眼睛，让你重新看见这个世界。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒmen zǒng shì juéde měihǎo de dōngxi zài yuǎnfāng, qíshí jiù zài shēnbiān. Zhǐ shì máng de shíhou kàn bú dào. Yěxǔ tuìxiū gěi le nǐ yī shuāng xīn de yǎnjīng, ràng nǐ chóngxīn kànjiàn zhège shìjiè.
options.text_en: We always think beautiful things are far away, but they're right beside us. We just can't see them when we're busy. Maybe retirement gave you new eyes to see the world again.
options.feedback: Reframe — retirement as gaining new vision, not losing purpose.

options.text_zh: 你以前在工厂做什么工作？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǐqián zài gōngchǎng zuò shénme gōngzuò?
options.text_en: What did you do at the factory?
options.feedback: Career question when he's talking about rediscovering life.

options.text_zh: 这条路有多长？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè tiáo lù yǒu duō cháng?
options.text_en: How long is this path?
options.feedback: Distance question during a philosophical exchange.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 一双新的眼睛，说得好。我老伴说我退休以后变了一个人，以前从来不注意花什么时候开，现在每天告诉她今天哪棵树开花了。
turns.text_pinyin: Yī shuāng xīn de yǎnjīng, shuō de hǎo. Wǒ lǎobàn shuō wǒ tuìxiū yǐhòu biàn le yīge rén, yǐqián cónglái bù zhùyì huā shénme shíhou kāi, xiànzài měi tiān gàosù tā jīntiān nǎ kē shù kāi huā le.
turns.text_en: New eyes — well said. My wife says I've become a different person since retiring. I never used to notice when flowers bloomed; now I tell her every day which tree is flowering.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his transformation.

options.text_zh: 每天告诉她哪棵树开花了——这是我今天听到的最温暖的事情。能注意到这些小事，才是真正的生活。
options.text_pinyin: Měi tiān gàosù tā nǎ kē shù kāi huā le — zhè shì wǒ jīntiān tīng dào de zuì wēnnuǎn de shìqíng. Néng zhùyì dào zhèxiē xiǎo shì, cái shì zhēnzhèng de shēnghuó.
options.text_en: Telling her which tree is flowering every day — that's the warmest thing I've heard today. Being able to notice these small things — that's truly living.
options.feedback: Genuine warmth — recognizes the beauty in his daily practice of attention.

options.text_zh: 你太太也骑自行车吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ tàitai yě qí zìxíngchē ma?
options.text_en: Does your wife ride a bicycle too?
options.feedback: Activity question that sidesteps the lovely image.

options.text_zh: 退休以后时间多了，当然有空注意这些。上班的人哪有时间看花？还是太闲了吧。
options.text_pinyin: Tuìxiū yǐhòu shíjiān duō le, dāngrán yǒu kòng zhùyì zhèxiē. Shàngbān de rén nǎ yǒu shíjiān kàn huā? Háishì tài xián le ba.
options.text_en: After retirement you have plenty of time, of course you can notice these things. Working people can't afford to look at flowers. You're just less busy, that's all.
options.feedback: Uncle Jiang goes quiet for a moment, then says softly: 'I worked for forty years and never saw a single bloom. Was I living then, or am I living now?'

tree.cultural_note: Early morning cycling and exercise is deeply embedded in Chinese culture. Parks and riverpaths fill with retirees before dawn for tai chi, walking, and cycling. The concept of 慢生活 (slow living) gained popularity as a counter-narrative to China's rapid modernization. The image of noticing which trees are blooming reflects a traditional Chinese aesthetic value — 知时 (knowing the seasons) — that connects daily awareness to the natural world.

tree.setup: You're in a quiet old neighborhood. A woman, Auntie Zheng, is hanging laundry on a line between two trees. Sunlight filters through the damp clothes, making soft shadows on the ground.
tree.setup_zh: 你在一个安静的老小区里。郑阿姨正在两棵树之间的晾衣绳上晾衣服。阳光透过湿衣服，在地上投下柔和的影子。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 现在的年轻人都用烘干机，谁还在外面晾衣服啊。但我就是喜欢把衣服晾在太阳下面，收下来的时候有阳光的味道。
turns.text_pinyin: Xiànzài de niánqīng rén dōu yòng hōnggān jī, shéi hái zài wàimiàn liàng yīfu a. Dàn wǒ jiù shì xǐhuān bǎ yīfu liàng zài tàiyáng xiàmiàn, shōu xiàlái de shíhou yǒu yángguāng de wèidào.
turns.text_en: Young people all use dryers now — who still hangs laundry outside? But I just like putting clothes out in the sun. When you take them in, they smell like sunshine.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about sun-dried laundry.

options.text_zh: 阳光的味道，烘干机做不出来。有些东西是机器代替不了的。我小时候最喜欢的就是把脸埋进刚晒好的被子里，那种暖暖的感觉。
options.text_pinyin: Yángguāng de wèidào, hōnggān jī zuò bù chūlái. Yǒuxiē dōngxi shì jīqì dàitì bù liǎo de. Wǒ xiǎo shíhou zuì xǐhuān de jiù shì bǎ liǎn mái jìn gāng shài hǎo de bèizi lǐ, nà zhǒng nuǎnnuǎn de gǎnjué.
options.text_en: The smell of sunshine — a dryer can't make that. Some things machines just can't replace. When I was little, my favorite thing was burying my face in a freshly sun-dried quilt, that warm feeling.
options.feedback: Sensory and nostalgic — connects to childhood through smell and warmth.

options.text_zh: 用烘干机不是更方便吗？
options.text_pinyin: Yòng hōnggān jī bú shì gèng fāngbiàn ma?
options.text_en: Isn't using a dryer more convenient?
options.feedback: Argues for the thing she's explicitly choosing against.

options.text_zh: 下午会不会下雨？衣服别淋湿了。
options.text_pinyin: Xiàwǔ huì bú huì xià yǔ? Yīfu bié lín shī le.
options.text_en: Will it rain this afternoon? Don't let the clothes get wet.
options.feedback: Weather concern that interrupts her appreciation of simple pleasures.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对，就是那种感觉！我女儿老说我太老了，跟不上时代。但我觉得有些习惯不需要改。不是所有旧的东西都是不好的。
turns.text_pinyin: Duì, jiù shì nà zhǒng gǎnjué! Wǒ nǚ'ér lǎo shuō wǒ tài lǎo le, gēn bú shàng shídài. Dàn wǒ juéde yǒuxiē xíguàn bù xūyào gǎi. Bú shì suǒyǒu jiù de dōngxi dōu shì bù hǎo de.
turns.text_en: Yes, that's exactly it! My daughter always says I'm too old, can't keep up with the times. But I think some habits don't need changing. Not everything old is bad.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her daughter's criticism.

options.text_zh: 不是跟不上时代，是时代跟不上你。有些东西不需要更新，它已经是最好的版本了。
options.text_pinyin: bú shì gēn bu shàng shídài, shì shídài gēn bu shàng nǐ. yǒuxiē dōngxi bù xūyào gēngxīn, tā yǐjīng shì zuì hǎo de bǎnběn le.
options.text_en: It's not that you can't keep up with the times — the times can't keep up with you. Some things don't need updating; they're already the best version.
options.feedback: Reframes 'old-fashioned' as intentional — validates her choice.

options.text_zh: 你女儿在哪里工作？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ nǚ'ér zài nǎlǐ gōngzuò?
options.text_en: Where does your daughter work?
options.feedback: Career question unrelated to the conversation about old habits.

options.text_zh: 你也可以学学用手机，其实不难。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yě kěyǐ xué xué yòng shǒujī, qíshí bù nán.
options.text_en: You could learn to use a phone too, it's not hard.
options.feedback: Tech advice that sides with the daughter's criticism.

tree.cultural_note: Hanging laundry (晾衣服) outdoors is a quintessential daily scene in Chinese neighborhoods, especially in older residential areas. The practice is both practical (taking advantage of natural sunlight) and sensory (the smell of sun-dried fabric is universally loved). Intergenerational tension between 'keeping up with the times' and preserving old habits is a common theme in Chinese family dynamics.

tree.setup: You're walking through a quiet night market that's winding down. Most stalls have closed. A lantern maker, Master Gao, is still sitting by his display of paper lanterns, their warm light glowing softly.
tree.setup_zh: 你走过一个正在收摊的安静夜市。大多数摊位已经关了。一位灯笼师傅高师傅还坐在他的纸灯笼旁边，灯笼的暖光柔柔地亮着。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 不急，我不急着走。每天晚上这些灯笼亮着的时候，是我最开心的时候。你看这些光，每一个都是我自己做的。机器做的灯笼也好看，但光不一样。
turns.text_pinyin: Bù jí, wǒ bù jí zhe zǒu. Měi tiān wǎnshang zhèxiē dēnglóng liàng zhe de shíhou, shì wǒ zuì kāixīn de shíhou. Nǐ kàn zhèxiē guāng, měi yīge dōu shì wǒ zìjǐ zuò de. Jīqì zuò de dēnglóng yě hǎokàn, dàn guāng bù yīyàng.
turns.text_en: No rush, I'm not in a hurry. Every evening when these lanterns are lit, that's my happiest moment. Look at this light — each one is handmade by me. Machine-made lanterns look fine too, but the light is different.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about the handmade lanterns.

options.text_zh: 确实不一样。手工做的灯笼，光是暖的、柔的、不均匀的——就像一个有温度的东西。机器做的太完美了，反而少了那种温暖。
options.text_pinyin: Quèshí bù yīyàng. Shǒugōng zuò de dēnglóng, guāng shì nuǎn de, róu de, bù jūnyún de — jiù xiàng yīge yǒu wēndù de dōngxi. Jīqì zuò de tài wánměi le, fǎn'ér shǎo le nà zhǒng wēnnuǎn.
options.text_en: It really is different. Handmade lanterns give light that's warm, soft, uneven — like something with warmth in it. Machine-made ones are too perfect, which actually takes away that warmth.
options.feedback: Sees beauty in imperfection — the uneven light as a sign of humanity.

options.text_zh: 不过现在有LED灯了，比纸灯笼亮多了，而且不怕风也不怕雨，用起来更方便。
options.text_pinyin: Búguò xiànzài yǒu LED dēng le, bǐ zhǐ dēnglóng liàng duō le, érqiě bú pà fēng yě bú pà yǔ, yòng qǐlái gèng fāngbiàn.
options.text_en: But we have LED lights now — much brighter than paper lanterns, and they're not afraid of wind or rain. Much more practical.
options.feedback: Master Gao looks at his lanterns and says quietly: 'Brighter, yes. But no one has ever stood still to look at an LED.'

options.text_zh: 你在这里摆摊多久了？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zài zhèlǐ bǎi tān duō jiǔ le?
options.text_en: How long have you had a stall here?
options.feedback: Duration question instead of engaging with the lanterns.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看得很仔细。我做灯笼做了三十多年了。小时候跟我爸爸学的。他说，做灯笼不是做东西，是做光。你要想着，晚上有个人走在路上，看到了你的灯，心里就暖了一点。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn de hěn zǐxì. Wǒ zuò dēnglóng zuò le sānshí duō nián le. Xiǎo shíhou gēn wǒ bàba xué de. Tā shuō, zuò dēnglóng bú shì zuò dōngxi, shì zuò guāng. Nǐ yào xiǎng zhe, wǎnshang yǒu ge rén zǒu zài lù shàng, kàn dào le nǐ de dēng, xīn lǐ jiù nuǎn le yīdiǎn.
turns.text_en: You observe carefully. I've been making lanterns for over thirty years. Learned from my father when I was little. He said, making lanterns isn't making things — it's making light. Think about it: at night someone walks down the road, sees your lantern, and their heart warms just a little.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his father's philosophy.

options.text_zh: 不是做东西，是做光——你爸爸很有智慧。每个灯笼都是给陌生人的温暖，你不认识他们，但你的光陪他们走了一段路。
options.text_pinyin: Bú shì zuò dōngxi, shì zuò guāng — nǐ bàba hěn yǒu zhìhuì. Měi ge dēnglóng dōu shì gěi mòshēng rén de wēnnuǎn, nǐ bú rènshi tāmen, dàn nǐ de guāng péi tāmen zǒu le yī duàn lù.
options.text_en: Not making things, making light — your father was wise. Every lantern is warmth for strangers. You don't know them, but your light walks with them for a stretch.
options.feedback: Elevates the craft to philosophy — light as anonymous companionship.

options.text_zh: 你爸爸还在做灯笼吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ bàba hái zài zuò dēnglóng ma?
options.text_en: Does your father still make lanterns?
options.feedback: Potentially painful question that shifts from the wisdom.

options.text_zh: 你能教我做灯笼吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ néng jiāo wǒ zuò dēnglóng ma?
options.text_en: Can you teach me to make lanterns?
options.feedback: Request that jumps past the contemplative moment.

tree.cultural_note: Paper lanterns (灯笼) hold deep cultural significance in China, symbolizing hope, warmth, and celebration. Traditional lantern-making is a disappearing craft, with fewer artisans passing down the skill. The philosophy of '做光' (making light) reflects a broader Chinese value of contributing beauty to the community without seeking recognition — a quiet form of service.

tree.setup: You're in a nearly empty cafe on a rainy afternoon. A young freelance writer, Xiao Bai, is staring at her laptop without typing. She sighs and looks out the window.
tree.setup_zh: 在一个下雨的下午，你在一家几乎没人的咖啡馆里。一个年轻的自由写手小白盯着她的笔记本电脑却没有打字。她叹了口气，看着窗外。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你是不是也在等灵感？我已经坐了两个小时了，一个字都没写出来。但奇怪的是，我也不想离开。也许灵感就像这场雨，你不知道什么时候会来。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ shì bú shì yě zài děng línggǎn? Wǒ yǐjīng zuò le liǎng ge xiǎoshí le, yīge zì dōu méi xiě chūlái. Dàn qíguài de shì, wǒ yě bù xiǎng líkāi. Yěxǔ línggǎn jiù xiàng zhè chǎng yǔ, nǐ bù zhīdào shénme shíhou huì lái.
turns.text_en: Are you also waiting for inspiration? I've been sitting here two hours without writing a single word. But strangely, I don't want to leave either. Maybe inspiration is like this rain — you don't know when it'll come.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about waiting for inspiration.

options.text_zh: 灵感可能不是等来的，是在等的过程中慢慢长出来的。你看雨、发呆、喝咖啡——这些不是浪费时间，是在悄悄给灵感准备土壤。
options.text_pinyin: Línggǎn kěnéng bú shì děng lái de, shì zài děng de guòchéng zhōng mànman zhǎng chūlái de. Nǐ kàn yǔ, fādāi, hē kāfēi — zhèxiē bú shì làngfèi shíjiān, shì zài qiāoqiāo gěi línggǎn zhǔnbèi tǔrǎng.
options.text_en: Maybe inspiration isn't something you wait for — it grows while you're waiting. Watching rain, daydreaming, drinking coffee — not wasted time, but quietly preparing soil for inspiration.
options.feedback: Reframes inactivity as creative preparation — a comforting perspective.

options.text_zh: 你在写什么？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zài xiě shénme?
options.text_en: What are you writing?
options.feedback: Project question when she's talking about creative struggle.

options.text_zh: 你试过去图书馆写吗？那里可能更安静。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ shì guò qù túshūguǎn xiě ma? Nàlǐ kěnéng gèng ānjìng.
options.text_en: Have you tried writing at a library? It might be quieter.
options.feedback: Venue suggestion that misses the deeper issue.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 给灵感准备土壤……我喜欢这个说法。你知道吗，有时候我觉得写不出来是因为我太着急了。越着急越写不出来，越写不出来越着急。像一个困住自己的圆圈。
turns.text_pinyin: Gěi línggǎn zhǔnbèi tǔrǎng…… wǒ xǐhuān zhège shuōfǎ. Nǐ zhīdào ma, yǒu shíhou wǒ juéde xiě bù chūlái shì yīnwèi wǒ tài zháojí le. Yuè zháojí yuè xiě bù chūlái, yuè xiě bù chūlái yuè zháojí. Xiàng yīge kùn zhù zìjǐ de yuánquān.
turns.text_en: Preparing soil for inspiration... I like that. You know, sometimes I think the reason I can't write is that I'm too anxious about it. The more anxious, the less I can write; the less I write, the more anxious. It's like a circle trapping myself.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the anxiety cycle.

options.text_zh: 打破这个圆圈的方法也许不是更努力地写，而是允许自己不写。不写也没关系。听了雨声、喝了好咖啡，这也是有价值的一天。
options.text_pinyin: Dǎpò zhège yuánquān de fāngfǎ yěxǔ bú shì gèng nǔlì de xiě, ér shì yǔnxǔ zìjǐ bù xiě. Bù xiě yě méi guānxi. Tīng le yǔ shēng, hē le hǎo kāfēi, zhè yě shì yǒu jiàzhí de yī tiān.
options.text_en: Maybe breaking the circle isn't writing harder, but allowing yourself not to write. Not writing is okay. You heard rain, drank good coffee — that's still a worthwhile day.
