options.feedback: Compassionate and liberating — gives permission to rest without guilt.

options.text_zh: 你每天写多少字？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ měi tiān xiě duōshǎo zì?
options.text_en: How many words do you write per day?
options.feedback: Productivity metric during a conversation about creative anxiety.

options.text_zh: 你可以先写个大纲，再慢慢写。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ xiān xiě ge dàgāng, zài mànman xiě.
options.text_en: You could write an outline first, then fill it in.
options.feedback: Writing advice when she needs emotional support, not technique.

tree.cultural_note: Cafes (咖啡馆) have become important creative spaces in Chinese cities, especially for freelance workers and writers. The concept of 发呆 (fādāi, zoning out / daydreaming) is increasingly valued in Chinese culture as a legitimate form of rest — even designated '发呆' spaces have appeared in some Chinese cities. The creative anxiety Xiao Bai describes resonates with many young Chinese professionals navigating the tension between productivity culture and creative work.

tree.setup: You're sitting on a hill overlooking the city as the sun sets. An elderly painter, Lao Yao, is sketching the skyline with colored pencils. His drawings are simple but warm.
tree.setup_zh: 你坐在一座小山上看着日落。一位年迈的画家老姚正在用彩色铅笔画城市的天际线。他的画简单但温暖。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我每天都来这里画日落。你知道吗，每天的日落都不一样。今天的天空有一点紫色，昨天是橙色的。大多数人觉得日落就是日落，但如果你仔细看，每一天都是独一无二的。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ měi tiān dōu lái zhèlǐ huà rìluò. Nǐ zhīdào ma, měi tiān de rìluò dōu bù yīyàng. Jīntiān de tiānkōng yǒu yīdiǎn zǐsè, zuótiān shì chéngsè de. Dà duōshù rén juéde rìluò jiù shì rìluò, dàn rúguǒ nǐ zǐxì kàn, měi yī tiān dōu shì dúyīwú'èr de.
turns.text_en: I come here every day to paint the sunset. You know, every sunset is different. Today's sky has a bit of purple; yesterday was orange. Most people think a sunset is just a sunset, but if you look carefully, every day is unique.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about the uniqueness of each sunset.

options.text_zh: 每天都来画日落，画了多少幅了？每一幅都是那一天唯一的记录。就算没人看到那天的天空，你把它留住了。
options.text_pinyin: Měi tiān dōu lái huà rìluò, huà le duōshǎo fú le? Měi yī fú dōu shì nà yī tiān wéiyī de jìlù. Jiùsuàn méi rén kàn dào nà tiān de tiānkōng, nǐ bǎ tā liú zhù le.
options.text_en: Coming every day to paint sunsets — how many paintings is that by now? Each one is the only record of that day. Even if nobody else saw that sky, you preserved it.
options.feedback: Sees the paintings as unique daily records — values his quiet dedication.

options.text_zh: 你用什么牌子的铅笔？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yòng shénme páizi de qiānbǐ?
options.text_en: What brand of pencils do you use?
options.feedback: Equipment question during a moment of artistic reflection.

options.text_zh: 你的画卖不卖？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de huà mài bú mài?
options.text_en: Do you sell your paintings?
options.feedback: Commercial question that commodifies a personal practice.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 已经画了一千多幅了。我老伴走了以后，这是我每天最重要的事。她以前最喜欢看日落。我现在帮她看，然后画下来。好像她还在我身边一样。
turns.text_pinyin: Yǐjīng huà le yī qiān duō fú le. Wǒ lǎobàn zǒu le yǐhòu, zhè shì wǒ měi tiān zuì zhòngyào de shì. Tā yǐqián zuì xǐhuān kàn rìluò. Wǒ xiànzài bāng tā kàn, ránhòu huà xiàlái. Hǎoxiàng tā hái zài wǒ shēnbiān yīyàng.
turns.text_en: Over a thousand by now. After my wife passed, this became the most important thing I do each day. She loved watching sunsets. Now I watch for her, then paint it. As if she's still beside me.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his way of remembering his wife.

options.text_zh: 帮她看日落……这是我听过的最深的爱。一千多幅画，就是一千多个傍晚，你都在陪着她。她一定能感受到。
options.text_pinyin: Bāng tā kàn rìluò…… zhè shì wǒ tīng guò de zuì shēn de ài. Yī qiān duō fú huà, jiù shì yī qiān duō ge bàngwǎn, nǐ dōu zài péi zhe tā. Tā yīdìng néng gǎnshòu dào.
options.text_en: Watching sunsets for her... that's the deepest love I've ever heard of. Over a thousand paintings means over a thousand evenings you've been keeping her company. She must be able to feel it.
options.feedback: Deeply moved and respectful — honors his devotion without sentimentality.

options.text_zh: 你有没有想过办一个画展？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu xiǎng guò bàn yīge huàzhǎn?
options.text_en: Have you thought about holding an exhibition?
options.feedback: Career suggestion during a deeply personal revelation.

options.text_zh: 你太太是什么时候走的？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ tàitai shì shénme shíhou zǒu de?
options.text_en: When did your wife pass away?
options.feedback: Date question that probes pain instead of honoring the tribute.

tree.cultural_note: The practice of continuing daily rituals in memory of a deceased spouse is deeply rooted in Chinese culture. The concept of 陪伴 (péibàn, companionship) extends beyond physical presence — many Chinese believe that maintaining shared habits keeps the connection alive. Painting sunsets for a departed wife embodies the idea that love doesn't end with death but transforms into quiet daily devotion.

tree.setup: You've wandered into an old courtyard in a quiet part of the city. A retired music teacher, Mr. Luo, is sitting there playing an erhu. The music is slow and gentle.
tree.setup_zh: 你走进了城市安静角落的一个老院子。一位退休的音乐老师罗先生正坐在那里拉二胡。音乐缓慢而温柔。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 啊，进来坐吧，不用客气。我每天下午在这里拉一会儿。不是为了给谁听，就是觉得这个院子太安静了，需要一点声音。
turns.text_pinyin: A, jìnlái zuò ba, bú yòng kèqi. Wǒ měi tiān xiàwǔ zài zhèlǐ lā yīhuìr. Bú shì wèi le gěi shéi tīng, jiù shì juéde zhège yuànzi tài ānjìng le, xūyào yīdiǎn shēngyīn.
turns.text_en: Ah, come in and sit. No need to be polite. I play a bit here every afternoon. Not for anyone in particular — I just feel this courtyard is too quiet and needs a little sound.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his playing.

options.text_zh: 给安静的院子一点声音——这个理由很美。音乐和安静其实不矛盾，好的音乐会让安静变得更丰富，而不是打破它。
options.text_pinyin: Gěi ānjìng de yuànzi yīdiǎn shēngyīn — zhège lǐyóu hěn měi. Yīnyuè hé ānjìng qíshí bù máodùn, hǎo de yīnyuè huì ràng ānjìng biàn de gèng fēngfù, ér bú shì dǎpò tā.
options.text_en: Giving a quiet courtyard some sound — what a beautiful reason. Music and quiet aren't actually contradictory; good music makes the quiet richer, rather than breaking it.
options.feedback: Perceptive — music as enriching silence rather than replacing it.

options.text_zh: 你拉了多少年二胡了？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ lā le duōshǎo nián èrhú le?
options.text_en: How many years have you played the erhu?
options.feedback: Biographical question instead of engaging with his reason for playing.

options.text_zh: 这个院子是谁的？
options.text_pinyin: Zhège yuànzi shì shéi de?
options.text_en: Whose courtyard is this?
options.feedback: Ownership question during a musical moment.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 确实。我教了三十年音乐，最难教学生的就是这个道理——音乐不是噪音，好的音乐知道什么时候停下来。就像说话一样，最重要的不是你说了什么，是你在什么时候不说。
turns.text_pinyin: Quèshí. Wǒ jiāo le sānshí nián yīnyuè, zuì nán jiāo xuéshēng de jiù shì zhège dàolǐ — yīnyuè bú shì zàoyīn, hǎo de yīnyuè zhīdào shénme shíhou tíng xiàlái. Jiù xiàng shuōhuà yīyàng, zuì zhòngyào de bú shì nǐ shuō le shénme, shì nǐ zài shénme shíhou bù shuō.
turns.text_en: Indeed. I taught music for thirty years, and the hardest thing to teach students was this — music isn't noise, good music knows when to pause. It's like speaking: the most important thing isn't what you say, it's when you stay silent.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his insight about silence in music.

options.text_zh: 知道什么时候不说——这个道理不只适用于音乐。在生活中也是一样，有时候最好的回应不是说话，而是安静地陪在旁边。
options.text_pinyin: Zhīdào shénme shíhou bù shuō — zhège dàolǐ bù zhǐ shìyòng yú yīnyuè. Zài shēnghuó zhōng yě shì yīyàng, yǒu shíhou zuì hǎo de huíyìng bú shì shuōhuà, ér shì ānjìng de péi zài pángbiān.
options.text_en: Knowing when not to speak — that applies beyond music. In life too, sometimes the best response isn't words, but quietly being there beside someone.
options.feedback: Extends the musical principle to human relationships — presence over speech.

options.text_zh: 你的学生现在都在做什么？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de xuéshēng xiànzài dōu zài zuò shénme?
options.text_en: What are your students doing now?
options.feedback: Alumni question during a deep insight.

options.text_zh: 二胡难不难学？
options.text_pinyin: Èrhú nán bù nán xué?
options.text_en: Is the erhu hard to learn?
options.feedback: Difficulty question that misses the philosophical point.

tree.cultural_note: The erhu (二胡), a two-stringed Chinese fiddle, is one of the most expressive instruments in Chinese music. Its tone is often compared to the human voice, capable of conveying deep emotion. The concept of silence within music (留白, leaving blank space) parallels the Chinese painting technique of the same name — the idea that what you leave out is as important as what you include.

tree.setup: You stop at a small fruit stand run by a cheerful woman, Sister Mei. She's arranging peaches with care, turning each one so the best side faces out.
tree.setup_zh: 你在一个小水果摊前停下来。老板梅姐是一位开朗的女性。她正在仔细地摆放桃子，把每一个最好看的那面朝外。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看这些桃子，每一个我都转了一下，让它最好看的那面对着客人。有人说我太认真了，但我觉得水果也有自己最好的样子，应该让别人看到。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn zhèxiē táozi, měi yīge wǒ dōu zhuǎn le yīxià, ràng tā zuì hǎokàn de nà miàn duì zhe kèrén. Yǒu rén shuō wǒ tài rènzhēn le, dàn wǒ juéde shuǐguǒ yě yǒu zìjǐ zuì hǎo de yàngzi, yīnggāi ràng biérén kàn dào.
turns.text_en: Look at these peaches — I turned each one to show its best side to customers. Some people say I'm too serious about it, but I think fruit has its best face too, and it should be seen.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her care with the peaches.

options.text_zh: 这不是太认真，这是用心。大多数人只是把水果堆在那里，但你认真对待每一个桃子。做小事也能做得很美。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè bú shì tài rènzhēn, zhè shì yòngxīn. Dà duōshù rén zhǐ shì bǎ shuǐguǒ duī zài nàlǐ, dàn nǐ rènzhēn duìdài měi yīge táozi. Zuò xiǎo shì yě néng zuò de hěn měi.
options.text_en: That's not being too serious — that's being thoughtful. Most people just pile fruit up, but you treat each peach with care. Even small things can be done beautifully.
options.feedback: Validates her attention to detail as craft rather than obsession.

options.text_zh: 这些桃子甜不甜？
options.text_pinyin: Zhèxiē táozi tián bù tián?
options.text_en: Are these peaches sweet?
options.feedback: Taste question that ignores her careful arrangement.

options.text_zh: 其实客人买水果主要看新不新鲜，摆得好不好看应该没那么重要吧。反正买回去就吃了。
options.text_pinyin: Qíshí kèrén mǎi shuǐguǒ zhǔyào kàn xīn bù xīnxiān, bǎi de hǎo bù hǎokàn yīnggāi méi nàme zhòngyào ba. Fǎnzhèng mǎi huíqù jiù chī le.
options.text_en: Honestly, customers mostly care if fruit is fresh. How it's arranged probably doesn't matter much — they'll eat it as soon as they get home anyway.
options.feedback: Sister Mei pauses mid-turn: 'If that were true, nobody would ever arrange flowers either.'

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 用心，对。我妈妈以前也是卖水果的。她教我说，「不管做什么，都要让别人看到你的心意。」哪怕只是摆一个水果摊，你也能让路过的人觉得今天的生活好了一点。
turns.text_pinyin: Yòngxīn, duì. Wǒ māma yǐqián yě shì mài shuǐguǒ de. Tā jiāo wǒ shuō, 'bùguǎn zuò shénme, dōu yào ràng biérén kàn dào nǐ de xīnyì.' Nǎpà zhǐ shì bǎi yīge shuǐguǒ tān, nǐ yě néng ràng lùguò de rén juéde jīntiān de shēnghuó hǎo le yīdiǎn.
turns.text_en: Thoughtful, yes. My mother sold fruit too. She taught me: 'No matter what you do, let people see your care in it.' Even if it's just a fruit stand, you can make someone passing by feel life is a little better today.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her mother's teaching.

options.text_zh: 你妈妈说得真好。让路过的人觉得生活好了一点——这就是最了不起的事。不用做大事，每天认真做好小事，就在让世界更好。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ māma shuō de zhēn hǎo. Ràng lùguò de rén juéde shēnghuó hǎo le yīdiǎn — zhè jiù shì zuì liǎobuqǐ de shì. Bú yòng zuò dà shì, měi tiān rènzhēn zuò hǎo xiǎo shì, jiù zài ràng shìjiè gèng hǎo.
options.text_en: Your mother said it perfectly. Making passersby feel life is a little better — that's remarkable. No need for big things; doing small things well each day already makes the world better.
options.feedback: Honors the philosophy of small acts mattering — grounded and genuine.

options.text_zh: 你妈妈还在卖水果吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ māma hái zài mài shuǐguǒ ma?
options.text_en: Is your mother still selling fruit?
options.feedback: Status question that shifts from the teaching.

options.text_zh: 你这里有西瓜吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhèlǐ yǒu xīguā ma?
options.text_en: Do you have watermelon?
options.feedback: Inventory question during a heartfelt exchange.

tree.cultural_note: The care with which Chinese fruit vendors arrange their displays reflects a broader cultural value of 用心 (yòngxīn, putting heart into something). Small-scale fruit vendors, often family businesses passed between generations, are a vital part of Chinese street life. The philosophy that even the humblest work can brighten someone's day connects to the Confucian idea that moral excellence is practiced in ordinary, daily actions.

tree.setup: You walk past an old school and peek inside an empty classroom. A former teacher, Mrs. Huang, is standing there looking at the blackboard. School ended years ago but the building hasn't been torn down.
tree.setup_zh: 你路过一所旧学校，往一间空教室里看。一位以前的老师黄老师站在那里看着黑板。学校几年前就关了，但建筑还没有拆。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你也进来看看吧。我以前在这里教了二十年书。你看黑板上还有粉笔的痕迹，虽然已经看不清写的什么了。每次路过，我都会进来站一会儿。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ yě jìnlái kàn kàn ba. Wǒ yǐqián zài zhèlǐ jiāo le èrshí nián shū. Nǐ kàn hēibǎn shàng hái yǒu fěnbǐ de hénjì, suīrán yǐjīng kàn bù qīng xiě de shénme le. Měi cì lùguò, wǒ dōu huì jìnlái zhàn yīhuìr.
turns.text_en: Come in and look. I taught here for twenty years. See — there are still chalk traces on the blackboard, though you can't make out what was written anymore. Every time I pass by, I come in and stand for a while.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the empty classroom.

options.text_zh: 虽然教室空了，但你教过的那些学生还带着你给他们的东西走在外面的世界里。黑板上的字可以擦掉，但你教给他们的东西擦不掉。
options.text_pinyin: Suīrán jiàoshì kōng le, dàn nǐ jiāo guò de nàxiē xuéshēng hái dài zhe nǐ gěi tāmen de dōngxi zǒu zài wàimiàn de shìjiè lǐ. Hēibǎn shàng de zì kěyǐ cā diào, dàn nǐ jiāo gěi tāmen de dōngxi cā bú diào.
options.text_en: The classroom is empty, but the students you taught are out in the world carrying what you gave them. Words on a blackboard can be erased, but what you taught them can't.
options.feedback: Comforting and true — reframes emptiness as legacy.

options.text_zh: 这个学校为什么关了？
options.text_pinyin: Zhège xuéxiào wèishénme guān le?
options.text_en: Why did this school close?
options.feedback: Administrative question during an emotional return.

options.text_zh: 这个建筑以后会变成什么？
options.text_pinyin: Zhège jiànzhù yǐhòu huì biàn chéng shénme?
options.text_en: What will this building become in the future?
options.feedback: Development question that ignores her feelings.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 擦不掉……谢谢你这么说。有时候我想，当老师到底留下了什么？不像盖房子的人，你能看到你建的楼。我留下的东西看不见，只能相信它在那里。
turns.text_pinyin: Cā bú diào…… xièxie nǐ zhème shuō. Yǒu shíhou wǒ xiǎng, dāng lǎoshī dàodǐ liú xià le shénme? Bù xiàng gài fángzi de rén, nǐ néng kàn dào nǐ jiàn de lóu. Wǒ liú xià de dōngxi kàn bújiàn, zhǐ néng xiāngxìn tā zài nàlǐ.
turns.text_en: Can't be erased... thank you for saying that. Sometimes I wonder, what did being a teacher actually leave behind? Unlike someone who builds houses — you can see the buildings. What I left behind is invisible. I can only believe it's there.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her doubt about her legacy.

options.text_zh: 教室空了，但学生还带着您给的东西走在外面。黑板上的字能擦掉，但您教他们的东西擦不掉。
options.text_pinyin: Jiàoshì kōng le, dàn xuéshēng hái dài zhe nín gěi de dōngxi zǒu zài wàimiàn. Hēibǎn shàng de zì néng cā diào, dàn nín jiāo tāmen de dōngxi cā bú diào.
options.text_en: The classroom is empty, but your students are out in the world carrying what you gave them. Words on a blackboard can be erased, but what you taught them can't.
options.feedback: Reassuring and concrete — gives her invisible legacy tangible possibility.

options.text_zh: 你退休以后做什么？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ tuìxiū yǐhòu zuò shénme?
options.text_en: What do you do since retiring?
options.feedback: Retirement activities question during a profound moment.

options.text_zh: 你教的是什么科目？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ jiāo de shì shénme kēmù?
options.text_en: What subject did you teach?
options.feedback: Subject question that misses her existential reflection.

tree.cultural_note: The closing of rural and small-town schools (撤校并校) has been a widespread phenomenon in China as populations shift to cities. For teachers who spent decades in these schools, the empty buildings represent both personal loss and social change. The Chinese concept of 师恩 (shī'ēn, the grace of a teacher) holds that a teacher's influence is lifelong and often invisible — shaping character in ways that only reveal themselves years later.

tree.setup: You're at a small restaurant, sitting by the window. The cook, Lao Deng, brings your food and lingers to chat. Outside, autumn leaves are falling slowly.
tree.setup_zh: 你在一家小饭馆靠窗的位置坐着。厨师老邓送来你的菜，停下来聊天。窗外，秋天的树叶慢慢飘落。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这个位置是我们店里最好的。很多客人喜欢坐这里，不是因为菜好吃，是因为可以看外面的树。秋天的时候，叶子一片一片掉下来，比电视好看多了。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhège wèizhì shì wǒmen diàn lǐ zuì hǎo de. Hěn duō kèrén xǐhuān zuò zhèlǐ, bú shì yīnwèi cài hǎochī, shì yīnwèi kěyǐ kàn wàimiàn de shù. Qiūtiān de shíhou, yèzi yī piàn yī piàn diào xiàlái, bǐ diànshì hǎokàn duō le.
turns.text_en: This is the best seat in our restaurant. Many customers like sitting here — not because the food is good, but because you can watch the tree outside. In autumn, the leaves fall one by one. Much more beautiful than TV.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about the window view.

options.text_zh: 比电视好看——因为它是真的。每一片叶子掉下来的方式都不一样，而且掉了就是掉了，不会重播。真实的东西总是比屏幕上的更动人。
options.text_pinyin: Bǐ diànshì hǎokàn — yīnwèi tā shì zhēn de. Měi yī piàn yèzi diào xiàlái de fāngshì dōu bù yīyàng, érqiě diào le jiù shì diào le, bú huì chóngbō. Zhēnshí de dōngxi zǒng shì bǐ píngmù shàng de gèng dòngrén.
options.text_en: More beautiful than TV — because it's real. Every leaf falls differently, and once it falls, it's fallen, no replay. Real things are always more moving than what's on a screen.
options.feedback: Appreciates the irreplaceable quality of real-time nature.

options.text_zh: 菜也很好吃啊。
options.text_pinyin: Cài yě hěn hǎochī a.
options.text_en: The food is delicious too.
options.feedback: Deflects from his lovely observation with a compliment.

options.text_zh: 你们有没有包间？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐmen yǒu méiyǒu bāojiān?
options.text_en: Do you have a private room?
options.feedback: Seating logistics during a poetic moment.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 不会重播，对。你说的太好了。我年轻的时候觉得秋天很伤感，叶子掉了就没了。但现在我想，掉了才能长新的。每年秋天的叶子都不一样，就像每年的自己也不一样。
turns.text_pinyin: Bú huì chóngbō, duì. Nǐ shuō de tài hǎo le. Wǒ niánqīng de shíhou juéde qiūtiān hěn shānggǎn, yèzi diào le jiù méi le. Dàn xiànzài wǒ xiǎng, diào le cái néng zhǎng xīn de. Měi nián qiūtiān de yèzi dōu bù yīyàng, jiù xiàng měi nián de zìjǐ yě bù yīyàng.
turns.text_en: No replay, right. That's beautifully put. When I was young I thought autumn was sad — leaves fall and they're gone. But now I think, they have to fall for new ones to grow. Every autumn's leaves are different, just like every year you're a different person.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his changed perspective on autumn.

options.text_zh: 看不见的东西有时候比看得见的更重要。一个学生因为您一句话改变了想法，因为您的鼓励找到了自信——这些影响也许传了很多年了。
options.text_pinyin: Kàn bújiàn de dōngxi yǒu shíhou bǐ kàn de jiàn de gèng zhòngyào. Yī ge xuéshēng yīnwèi nín yī jù huà gǎibiàn le xiǎngfǎ, yīnwèi nín de gǔlì zhǎo dào le zìxìn — zhèxiē yǐngxiǎng yěxǔ chuán le hěn duō nián le.
options.text_en: Invisible things are sometimes more important than visible ones. A student changed their thinking because of something you said, found confidence through your encouragement — those effects may have been rippling for years.
options.feedback: Mature perspective — connects natural cycles to personal growth.

options.text_zh: 你这里冬天也有客人吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhèlǐ dōngtiān yě yǒu kèrén ma?
options.text_en: Do you have customers in winter too?
options.feedback: Business question that breaks the seasonal reflection.

options.text_zh: 你种的是什么树？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhòng de shì shénme shù?
options.text_en: What kind of tree is it?
options.feedback: Botanical question during a philosophical exchange.

tree.cultural_note: Autumn holds special significance in Chinese culture, often associated with both melancholy (秋愁) and philosophical reflection. The falling of leaves (落叶) is a pervasive metaphor in Chinese poetry for loss, change, and renewal. Many Chinese people develop a more positive relationship with autumn's symbolism as they age — seeing letting go not as loss but as making space for growth.

tree.setup: You're on an overnight train, lying in a bunk bed. The passenger below you, an older traveler named Old Xu, can't sleep. The train rocks gently and moonlight comes through the curtain.
tree.setup_zh: 你在夜行列车的卧铺上。下铺的旅客，一位叫老徐的长者，也睡不着。列车轻轻地摇晃，月光从窗帘的缝隙里透进来。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你也睡不着啊？火车上睡不着不一定是坏事。你听那个声音——车轮碰到铁轨的声音，像一首很慢的歌。小时候坐火车，我最喜欢听这个声音。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ yě shuì bù zháo a? Huǒchē shàng shuì bù zháo bù yīdìng shì huài shì. Nǐ tīng nàge shēngyīn — chēlún pèng dào tiěguǐ de shēngyīn, xiàng yī shǒu hěn màn de gē. Xiǎo shíhou zuò huǒchē, wǒ zuì xǐhuān tīng zhège shēngyīn.
turns.text_en: Can't sleep either? Not sleeping on a train isn't necessarily bad. Listen to that sound — wheels meeting rails, like a very slow song. When I rode trains as a child, this was my favorite sound.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about the train sounds.

options.text_zh: 比电视好看——因为是真的。每片叶子掉的方式都不一样，掉了就掉了，不会重播。真实的东西总比屏幕上的更动人。
options.text_pinyin: Bǐ diànshì hǎokàn — yīnwèi shì zhēn de. Měi piàn yèzi diào de fāngshì dōu bù yīyàng, diào le jiù diào le, bú huì chóngbō. Zhēnshí de dōngxi zǒng bǐ píngmù shàng de gèng dòngrén.
options.text_en: More beautiful than TV — because it's real. Every leaf falls differently, and once it falls, no replay. Real things are always more moving than what's on a screen.
options.feedback: Captures the in-between quality of train travel — liminal and poetic.

options.text_zh: 你要去哪里？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yào qù nǎlǐ?
options.text_en: Where are you going?
options.feedback: Destination question when he's savoring the journey itself.

options.text_zh: 你带了耳塞吗？可能戴上就能睡着了。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ dài le ěrsāi ma? Kěnéng dài shàng jiù néng shuì zháo le.
options.text_en: Did you bring earplugs? You might fall asleep with them.
options.feedback: Practical suggestion that treats sleeplessness as a problem rather than an experience.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 不属于任何地方……对。我年轻的时候最喜欢这种感觉。不用想工作，不用想家里的事。在火车上，你只是一个在移动的人。什么都不需要做，只要在那里就好。
turns.text_pinyin: Bù shǔyú rènhé dìfāng…… duì. Wǒ niánqīng de shíhou zuì xǐhuān zhè zhǒng gǎnjué. Bú yòng xiǎng gōngzuò, bú yòng xiǎng jiā lǐ de shì. Zài huǒchē shàng, nǐ zhǐ shì yīge zài yídòng de rén. Shénme dōu bù xūyào zuò, zhǐyào zài nàlǐ jiù hǎo.
turns.text_en: Belonging nowhere... exactly. When I was young that was my favorite feeling. No thinking about work, no thinking about home. On a train, you're just a person in motion. Nothing needs doing, just being there is enough.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about the freedom of just being.

options.text_zh: 年轻时怕失去，长大才明白失去也是生活的一部分。叶子落了是为了让树休息，也为了明年的新叶子。放下一些东西，才能接住新的。
options.text_pinyin: Niánqīng shí pà shīqù, zhǎng dà cái míngbái shīqù yě shì shēnghuó de yī bùfèn. Yèzi luò le shì wèi le ràng shù xiūxi, yě wèi le míngnián de xīn yèzi. Fàng xià yīxiē dōngxi, cái néng jiē zhù xīn de.
options.text_en: When young we fear loss; growing up we understand it's part of life. Leaves fall so the tree can rest and for next year's new leaves. You let go of some things to catch new ones.
options.feedback: Elevates idleness on a train to philosophical freedom.

options.text_zh: 高铁比这个快多了，你怎么不坐高铁？
options.text_pinyin: Gāotiě bǐ zhège kuài duō le, nǐ zěnme bú zuò gāotiě?
options.text_en: The high-speed train is much faster, why didn't you take that?
options.feedback: Efficiency question that misses why he chose the slow train.

options.text_zh: 我觉得火车上的被子不太干净。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde huǒchē shàng de bèizi bú tài gānjìng.
options.text_en: I think the blankets on trains aren't very clean.
options.feedback: Hygiene complaint during a contemplative exchange.

tree.cultural_note: Overnight trains (夜行列车) were once the primary mode of long-distance travel in China. While high-speed rail has replaced most routes, older travelers often prefer the slower pace. The rhythmic sound of wheels on tracks (哐当哐当) is deeply nostalgic for many Chinese, associated with family journeys and the meditative quality of unhurried travel.

tree.setup: You're visiting a ceramics studio. The potter, Sister Ye, is shaping a teapot on her wheel. The studio smells of wet clay and is filled with warm light.
tree.setup_zh: 你在参观一个陶瓷工作室。陶艺师叶姐正在转盘上做一把茶壶。工作室里弥漫着湿泥土的气味，充满了温暖的光线。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看，做茶壶最难的不是形状，是厚薄。太厚了茶凉得慢，太薄了容易碎。要找到一个刚刚好的厚度。做人也是这样，太硬了会碎，太软了撑不住。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn, zuò cháhú zuì nán de bú shì xíngzhuàng, shì hòubáo. Tài hòu le chá liáng de màn, tài báo le róngyì suì. Yào zhǎo dào yīge gāng gāng hǎo de hòudù. Zuòrén yě shì zhèyàng, tài yìng le huì suì, tài ruǎn le chēng bú zhù.
turns.text_en: Look — the hardest part of making a teapot isn't the shape, it's the thickness. Too thick and the tea cools slowly; too thin and it breaks easily. You have to find the just-right thickness. Being a person is the same — too hard and you'll break, too soft and you can't hold up.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her comparison.

options.text_zh: 刚刚好的厚度——这需要很多经验才能找到吧。不管是做茶壶还是做人，都要知道什么时候该坚强，什么时候该柔软。
options.text_pinyin: Gāng gāng hǎo de hòudù — zhè xūyào hěn duō jīngyàn cái néng zhǎo dào ba. Bùguǎn shì zuò cháhú háishì zuòrén, dōu yào zhīdào shénme shíhou gāi jiānqiáng, shénme shíhou gāi róuruǎn.
options.text_en: Just-right thickness — that must take a lot of experience to find. Whether making teapots or being a person, you need to know when to be strong and when to be soft.
options.feedback: Engages with the metaphor naturally — extends it to emotional wisdom.

options.text_zh: 你做一把茶壶要多长时间？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zuò yī bǎ cháhú yào duō cháng shíjiān?
options.text_en: How long does it take to make a teapot?
options.feedback: Production time question during a philosophical metaphor.

options.text_zh: 厚薄应该可以用机器控制吧？现在的技术很先进，机器做的比手工更精确，也更统一。
options.text_pinyin: Hòubáo yīnggāi kěyǐ yòng jīqì kòngzhì ba? Xiànzài de jìshù hěn xiānjìn, jīqì zuò de bǐ shǒugōng gèng jīngquè, yě gèng tǒngyī.
options.text_en: Can't you control the thickness with machines? Modern technology is advanced — machines are more precise and consistent than handwork.
options.feedback: Sister Ye keeps her hands on the wheel: 'A machine can make a perfect pot. But it can't make one that remembers how it felt to be shaped.'

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对，需要时间。我年轻的时候做的茶壶不是太厚就是太薄。师傅说我太着急了，泥巴能感觉到你的心情——你紧张，它就紧张；你放松，它才会听你的话。
turns.text_pinyin: Duì, xūyào shíjiān. Wǒ niánqīng de shíhou zuò de cháhú bú shì tài hòu jiù shì tài báo. Shīfu shuō wǒ tài zháojí le, níba néng gǎnjué dào nǐ de xīnqíng — nǐ jǐnzhāng, tā jiù jǐnzhāng; nǐ fàngsōng, tā cái huì tīng nǐ de huà.
turns.text_en: Right, it takes time. When I was young, my teapots were always either too thick or too thin. My master said I was too anxious — the clay can feel your mood. When you're tense, it's tense. Only when you relax will it listen to you.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about the clay sensing mood.

options.text_zh: 像慢歌——确实是。还有车厢轻轻摇晃，像被什么东西推着走。火车上的时间不属于任何地方，离开了出发地，还没到目的地。
options.text_pinyin: Xiàng màn gē — quèshí shì. Hái yǒu chēxiāng qīng qīng yáohuàng, xiàng bèi shénme dōngxi tuī zhe zǒu. Huǒchē shàng de shíjiān bù shǔyú rènhé dìfāng, líkāi le chūfā dì, hái méi dào mùdìdì.
options.text_en: Like a slow song — it really is. The gentle swaying of the car, like being pushed along. Time on a train belongs nowhere — left the departure, haven't reached the destination.
options.feedback: Trusts the craft wisdom and generalizes it — calmness as creative prerequisite.

options.text_zh: 你师傅是谁？很有名吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ shīfu shì shéi? Hěn yǒumíng ma?
options.text_en: Who was your master? Were they famous?
options.feedback: Fame question that misses the practical wisdom being shared.

options.text_zh: 这应该是经验的问题吧。多练习几年，手法熟练了，自然就不会紧张了。跟心情应该没什么关系。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè yīnggāi shì jīngyàn de wèntí ba. Duō liànxí jǐ nián, shǒufǎ shúliàn le, zìrán jiù bú huì jǐnzhāng le. Gēn xīnqíng yīnggāi méi shénme guānxi.
options.text_en: That's probably just a matter of experience. Practice enough years, your technique gets smooth, and you naturally stop being tense. Mood probably has nothing to do with it.
options.feedback: Sister Ye smiles: 'I've been doing this twenty years, and the clay still knows when I've had a bad day.'

tree.cultural_note: Chinese pottery, especially Yixing teapot making (宜兴紫砂壶), is a revered craft with centuries of tradition. The idea that clay responds to the maker's emotional state is a commonly held belief among potters. The master-apprentice system in Chinese ceramics emphasizes not just technical skill but cultivating inner calm (心静), reflecting the Daoist principle that the creator's spirit enters the creation.

tree.setup: You duck into a tiny corner store to buy water. The owner, Uncle Qin, has run this shop for decades. The shelves are old but everything is neatly arranged. A small radio plays soft music.
tree.setup_zh: 你走进一家小小的街角小店买水。老板秦叔叔开了这家店几十年了。货架很旧但东西摆得很整齐。一台小收音机在放轻音乐。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 进来坐坐，不用急着走。现在这种小店越来越少了，大家都去便利店和超市了。但我觉得，小店和便利店最大的区别不是东西，是人。你进便利店，没人跟你说话。
turns.text_pinyin: Jìnlái zuò zuò, bú yòng jí zhe zǒu. Xiànzài zhè zhǒng xiǎo diàn yuè lái yuè shǎo le, dàjiā dōu qù biànlì diàn hé chāoshì le. Dàn wǒ juéde, xiǎo diàn hé biànlì diàn zuì dà de qūbié bú shì dōngxi, shì rén. Nǐ jìn biànlì diàn, méi rén gēn nǐ shuōhuà.
turns.text_en: Come in and sit, no rush. These little shops are getting rarer — everyone goes to convenience stores and supermarkets. But I think the biggest difference between a corner shop and a convenience store isn't the goods, it's the people. You walk into a convenience store and nobody talks to you.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about the difference between small shops and chain stores.

options.text_zh: 嗯，是啊。在便利店，你只是一个「顾客」。在你的店里，你是一个人，有名字，有故事。这种区别看起来很小，但其实很重要。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ shuō de duì. Zài biànlì diàn, nǐ zhǐ shì yīge 'gùkè'. Zài nǐ de diàn lǐ, nǐ shì yīge rén, yǒu míngzì, yǒu gùshì. Zhè zhǒng qūbié kàn qǐlái hěn xiǎo, dàn qíshí hěn zhòngyào.
options.text_en: You're right. In a convenience store, you're just a 'customer.' In your shop, you're a person — with a name and a story. That difference seems small, but it's actually very important.
options.feedback: Names the distinction precisely — personhood vs. consumer identity.

options.text_zh: 你这里有充电线卖吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhèlǐ yǒu chōngdiàn xiàn mài ma?
options.text_en: Do you sell charging cables?
options.feedback: Product question that proves his point about transactional behavior.

options.text_zh: 便利店确实挺方便的。
options.text_pinyin: Biànlì diàn quèshí tǐng fāngbiàn de.
options.text_en: Convenience stores are really convenient though.
options.feedback: Defends the alternative he's critiquing.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对。我认识这条街上大部分的人。张大爷每天来买一包烟，我知道他女儿下个月结婚。隔壁的小姑娘考上了大学，她来买水的时候我祝贺了她。这些事情超市做不到。
turns.text_pinyin: Duì. Wǒ rènshi zhè tiáo jiē shàng dà bùfèn de rén. Zhāng dàye měi tiān lái mǎi yī bāo yān, wǒ zhīdào tā nǚ'ér xià ge yuè jiéhūn. Gébì de xiǎo gūniáng kǎo shàng le dàxué, tā lái mǎi shuǐ de shíhou wǒ zhùhè le tā. Zhèxiē shìqíng chāoshì zuò bú dào.
turns.text_en: Right. I know most people on this street. Uncle Zhang comes to buy a pack of cigarettes every day — I know his daughter's getting married next month. The girl next door got into university; when she came to buy water I congratulated her. A supermarket can't do these things.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his role in the community.

options.text_zh: 您不只是开店，您是这条街的记忆。谁家有什么事您都知道。这种店关了，少的不是买东西的地方，是让人觉得被看见的地方。
options.text_pinyin: Nín bù zhǐ shì kāi diàn, nín shì zhè tiáo jiē de jìyì. Shuí jiā yǒu shénme shì nín dōu zhīdào. Zhè zhǒng diàn guān le, shǎo de bú shì mǎi dōngxi de dìfāng, shì ràng rén juéde bèi kàn jiàn de dìfāng.
options.text_en: You're not just running a shop — you're this street's memory. You know what's happening in every family. If shops like this close, what's lost isn't a place to buy things, it's a place where people feel seen.
options.feedback: Elevates the corner store to a community institution — being seen as essential.

options.text_zh: 你这里的水比超市贵吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhèlǐ de shuǐ bǐ chāoshì guì ma?
options.text_en: Is water more expensive here than at a supermarket?
options.feedback: Price comparison when he's describing community bonds.

options.text_zh: 你每天几点开门？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ měi tiān jǐ diǎn kāi mén?
options.text_en: What time do you open every day?
options.feedback: Hours question during a meaningful exchange.

tree.cultural_note: Small neighborhood shops (小卖部 or 杂货店) have been a cornerstone of Chinese community life for generations. The shopkeeper often functions as an informal neighborhood hub — knowing everyone's business, celebrating their milestones, and providing a daily touchpoint. The rapid expansion of chain convenience stores and e-commerce threatens these human-scale commercial relationships.

tree.setup: You're sitting in a friend's apartment during a heavy rainstorm. Your friend, Xiao Wen, has turned off all the lights. You're both watching the rain through the window in the dark.
tree.setup_zh: 暴雨天，你坐在朋友的公寓里。你的朋友小文关了所有的灯。你们在黑暗中一起看着窗外的雨。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我最喜欢大雨天关灯看雨。你有没有注意到，每颗雨滴打在玻璃上的声音都不一样？有的重，有的轻，有的快，有的慢。像一个谁都不认识的乐队在演奏。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ zuì xǐhuān dà yǔ tiān guān dēng kàn yǔ. Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu zhùyì dào, měi kē yǔdī dǎ zài bōli shàng de shēngyīn dōu bù yīyàng? Yǒude zhòng, yǒude qīng, yǒude kuài, yǒude màn. Xiàng yīge shéi dōu bú rènshi de yuèduì zài yǎnzòu.
turns.text_en: I love turning off the lights and watching rain on heavy rain days. Have you noticed that every raindrop hitting the glass sounds different? Some heavy, some light, some fast, some slow. Like an unknown band performing.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about the rain sounds.

options.text_zh: 刚刚好的厚度——需要很多经验才能找到吧。不管做茶壶还是做人，都要知道什么时候该坚强，什么时候该柔软。
options.text_pinyin: Gāng gāng hǎo de hòudù — xūyào hěn duō jīngyàn cái néng zhǎo dào ba. Bùguǎn zuò cháhú háishì zuòrén, dōu yào zhīdào shénme shíhou gāi jiānqiáng, shénme shíhou gāi róuruǎn.
options.text_en: Just-right thickness — must take a lot of experience. Whether making teapots or being a person, you need to know when to be strong and when to be soft.
options.feedback: Builds on the metaphor beautifully — improvisation and heightened senses.

options.text_zh: 你不开灯不觉得害怕吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ bù kāi dēng bù juéde hàipà ma?
options.text_en: Aren't you scared with the lights off?
options.feedback: Fear question that misses the comfort of the practice.

options.text_zh: 这场雨下了多久了？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè chǎng yǔ xià le duō jiǔ le?
options.text_en: How long has this rain been going?
options.feedback: Duration question during an immersive experience.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对。你知道我为什么喜欢关灯吗？因为关了灯以后，手机屏幕的光就不吸引你了。你只能看窗外，听雨声。黑暗反而让你专心。现在我们太多光了，到处都是屏幕。
turns.text_pinyin: Duì. Nǐ zhīdào wǒ wèishénme xǐhuān guān dēng ma? Yīnwèi guān le dēng yǐhòu, shǒujī píngmù de guāng jiù bù xīyǐn nǐ le. Nǐ zhǐ néng kàn chuāng wài, tīng yǔ shēng. Hēi'àn fǎn'ér ràng nǐ zhuānxīn. Xiànzài wǒmen tài duō guāng le, dàochù dōu shì píngmù.
turns.text_en: Right. You know why I like turning off the lights? Because without lights, the phone screen stops attracting you. You can only look outside and listen to the rain. Darkness actually helps you focus. Nowadays we have too much light — screens everywhere.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about darkness and attention.

options.text_zh: 泥巴能感觉到你的心情——听起来很神奇，但我信。很多事你越放松越做得好。也许最好的东西都是在心里安静时做出来的。
options.text_pinyin: Níba néng gǎnjué dào nǐ de xīnqíng — tīng qǐlái hěn shénqí, dàn wǒ xìn. Hěn duō shì nǐ yuè fàngsōng yuè zuò de hǎo. Yěxǔ zuì hǎo de dōngxi dōu shì zài xīn lǐ ānjìng shí zuò chūlái de.
options.text_en: The clay can feel your mood — sounds magical, but I believe it. Many things work better the more relaxed you are. Maybe the best things are made when the heart is quiet.
options.feedback: Extends the observation to information overload — subtraction as clarity.

options.text_zh: 你平时用手机多吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ píngshí yòng shǒujī duō ma?
options.text_en: Do you use your phone a lot?
options.feedback: Phone usage question during a meditation on attention.

options.text_zh: 明天还会下雨吗？
options.text_pinyin: Míngtiān hái huì xià yǔ ma?
options.text_en: Will it rain tomorrow too?
options.feedback: Forecast question that pulls away from the present moment.

tree.cultural_note: The practice of 听雨 (tīng yǔ, listening to rain) has deep roots in Chinese literary culture, appearing in countless classical poems. The deliberate act of turning off lights and screens to be present with natural phenomena represents a modern adaptation of this ancient practice. In a society increasingly dominated by screens and artificial light, these moments of intentional darkness are becoming valued as a form of digital detox.

tree.setup: You've brought a jacket to be mended at a tiny tailor shop. The tailor, Grandma Feng, examines the tear carefully through thick glasses. Her shop is cluttered with fabric and thread.
tree.setup_zh: 你带了一件外套去一家小小的裁缝店修补。裁缝冯奶奶透过厚厚的眼镜仔细看着破口。她的小店里堆满了布料和线。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这件衣服料子还很好，不应该扔。现在的年轻人，衣服破了一点就扔了，多可惜。以前我们一件衣服要穿很多年，补了又补。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè jiàn yīfu liàozi hái hěn hǎo, bù yīnggāi rēng. Xiànzài de niánqīng rén, yīfu pò le yīdiǎn jiù rēng le, duō kěxī. Yǐqián wǒmen yī jiàn yīfu yào chuān hěn duō nián, bǔ le yòu bǔ.
turns.text_en: The fabric on this jacket is still good — it shouldn't be thrown away. Young people today, they toss clothes with the slightest tear. What a waste. We used to wear one piece of clothing for years, patching it again and again.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about mending vs. discarding.

options.text_zh: 我也觉得可惜。其实修补过的衣服有一种特别的美——它说明这件衣服被珍惜过，被用心对待过。新衣服没有这种故事。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yě juéde kěxī. Qíshí xiūbǔ guò de yīfu yǒu yī zhǒng tèbié de měi — tā shuōmíng zhè jiàn yīfu bèi zhēnxī guò, bèi yòngxīn duìdài guò. Xīn yīfu méiyǒu zhè zhǒng gùshì.
options.text_en: I think it's a waste too. Actually, mended clothes have a special beauty — they show the clothing was cherished and cared for. New clothes don't have that kind of story.
options.feedback: Finds beauty in repair — values care over novelty.

options.text_zh: 不过现在衣服那么便宜，修补花的时间可能比重新买一件还多。从效率来说，买新的确实更合理。
options.text_pinyin: Búguò xiànzài yīfu nàme piányi, xiūbǔ huā de shíjiān kěnéng bǐ chóngxīn mǎi yī jiàn hái duō. Cóng xiàolǜ lái shuō, mǎi xīn de quèshí gèng hélǐ.
options.text_en: But clothes are so cheap now — the time spent mending might be more than buying a new one. Efficiency-wise, buying new really is more reasonable.
options.feedback: Grandma Feng threads her needle without looking up: 'Efficiency. Is that what you say about everything you love?'

options.text_zh: 现在买新的也不贵啊。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài mǎi xīn de yě bú guì a.
options.text_en: Buying new isn't expensive nowadays.
options.feedback: Contradicts her philosophy of valuing what you have.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 有故事的衣服，说得好。我做了五十年裁缝了。以前结婚的时候，新娘的衣服都是找我做的。一针一针缝上去，每一针都有心意。现在什么都是机器做的，快是快了，但心意没了。
turns.text_pinyin: Yǒu gùshì de yīfu, shuō de hǎo. Wǒ zuò le wǔshí nián cáiféng le. Yǐqián jiéhūn de shíhou, xīnniáng de yīfu dōu shì zhǎo wǒ zuò de. Yī zhēn yī zhēn féng shàngqù, měi yī zhēn dōu yǒu xīnyì. Xiànzài shénme dōu shì jīqì zuò de, kuài shì kuài le, dàn xīnyì méi le.
turns.text_en: Clothes with stories — well said. I've been a tailor for fifty years. In the old days, brides would come to me for their wedding clothes. Sewn stitch by stitch, every stitch with care. Now everything is machine-made. Fast, sure, but the heart is gone.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond about handmade vs. machine-made.

options.text_zh: 机器快，但缝不进心意。您的每一针都是给那个人的，不是给「谁都行」的。这种手艺不只是技术，是一种在乎的方式。
options.text_pinyin: Jīqì kuài, dàn féng bú jìn xīnyì. Nín de měi yī zhēn dōu shì gěi nàge rén de, bú shì gěi 'shuí dōu xíng' de. Zhè zhǒng shǒuyì bù zhǐ shì jìshù, shì yī zhǒng zàihu de fāngshì.
options.text_en: Machines are fast, but they can't sew in care. Every stitch of yours is for that person, not for 'whoever.' This kind of craft isn't just skill — it's a way of caring.
options.feedback: Honors the personal nature of her craft — individuality as care.

options.text_zh: 你有没有收徒弟？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu shōu túdì?
options.text_en: Have you taken any apprentices?
options.feedback: Succession question during a craft philosophy moment.

options.text_zh: 你做的婚纱什么风格？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zuò de hūnshā shénme fēnggé?
options.text_en: What style wedding dresses do you make?
options.feedback: Style question that misses the deeper point about care.

tree.cultural_note: Traditional tailoring (裁缝) was once central to Chinese family life, with tailors making everything from everyday clothes to wedding garments. The concept of 心意 (xīnyì, heartfelt care/intention) in handmade goods reflects a belief that the maker's feelings are woven into the work. The rise of fast fashion has made traditional tailoring increasingly rare, though a counter-movement valuing slow, handmade clothing is emerging in Chinese cities.

tree.setup: You're sheltering from a sudden rain in a small temple garden. A retired teacher is there, watching the rain fall on lotus leaves in the pond.
tree.setup_zh: 你在一座小庙的花园里躲雨。一位退休的老师也在那里，看着雨落在池塘里的荷叶上。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 别着急走，下雨天的花园是最美的。你听——雨打在荷叶上的声音，每一滴都不一样。我年轻的时候太忙了，从来没有时间停下来听这些。
turns.text_pinyin: Bié zháojí zǒu, xià yǔ tiān de huāyuán shì zuì měi de. Nǐ tīng — yǔ dǎ zài héyè shàng de shēngyīn, měi yī dī dōu bù yīyàng. Wǒ niánqīng de shíhou tài máng le, cónglái méiyǒu shíjiān tíng xiàlái tīng zhèxiē.
turns.text_en: Don't rush off — a garden in the rain is most beautiful. Listen — the sound of rain on lotus leaves, each drop different. When I was young I was too busy, never had time to stop and listen to these things.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his invitation to listen to the rain.

options.text_zh: 您说得对，这个声音真的很特别。我平时下雨的时候都在想怎么不被淋湿，从来没有想过要去听雨的声音。今天是第一次。
options.text_pinyin: Nín shuō de duì, zhège shēngyīn zhēn de hěn tèbié. Wǒ píngshí xià yǔ de shíhou dōu zài xiǎng zěnme bú bèi lín shī, cónglái méiyǒu xiǎng guò yào qù tīng yǔ de shēngyīn. Jīntiān shì dì yī cì.
options.text_en: You're right, that sound is really special. Usually when it rains I'm just thinking about not getting wet — I've never thought about listening to the rain. Today is the first time.
options.feedback: Honest about being too preoccupied to notice beauty — a genuine shift.

options.text_zh: 雨什么时候能停？
options.text_pinyin: Yǔ shénme shíhou néng tíng?
options.text_en: When will the rain stop?
options.feedback: Wants the rain to end instead of listening to it.

options.text_zh: 这个寺庙有多少年历史了？
options.text_pinyin: Zhège sìmiào yǒu duōshǎo nián lìshǐ le?
options.text_en: How old is this temple?
options.feedback: A tourist question during a contemplative moment.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 第一次就很好。我以前教语文，教了三十年。课本里有很多关于自然的诗，但我那时候只是在教字和意思，没有真正地去感受。退休以后才发现，诗人写的那些东西，一直就在身边。
turns.text_pinyin: Dì yī cì jiù hěn hǎo. Wǒ yǐqián jiāo yǔwén, jiāo le sānshí nián. Kèběn lǐ yǒu hěn duō guānyú zìrán de shī, dàn wǒ nà shíhou zhǐ shì zài jiāo zì hé yìsi, méiyǒu zhēnzhèng de qù gǎnshòu. Tuìxiū yǐhòu cái fāxiàn, shīrén xiě de nàxiē dōngxi, yīzhí jiù zài shēnbiān.
turns.text_en: First time is already good. I used to teach Chinese literature for thirty years. Textbooks had so many poems about nature, but back then I was just teaching characters and meanings, never truly feeling them. Only after retirement did I discover that what the poets wrote was always right here around us.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his realization about literature and experience.

options.text_zh: 也许有些东西需要先活过才能真正读懂。您教了三十年的诗，但可能现在才真正成了诗里的人。这不是遗憾，是另一种收获。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ yǒuxiē dōngxi xūyào xiān huó guò cáinéng zhēnzhèng dú dǒng. Nín jiāo le sānshí nián de shī, dàn kěnéng xiànzài cái zhēnzhèng chéng le shī lǐ de rén. Zhè bú shì yíhàn, shì lìng yī zhǒng shōuhuò.
options.text_en: Some things need to be lived before they can be understood. You taught poetry for thirty years, but maybe only now have you become the person inside the poems. That's not regret — it's harvest.
options.feedback: Reframes his delay as fulfillment — understanding comes through living.

options.text_zh: 那您以前的学生理解那些诗吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nà nín yǐqián de xuéshēng lǐjiě nàxiē shī ma?
options.text_en: Did your students understand those poems?
options.feedback: Redirects to students instead of his personal revelation.

options.text_zh: 退休以后您都做什么？
options.text_pinyin: Tuìxiū yǐhòu nín dōu zuò shénme?
options.text_en: What do you do after retirement?
options.feedback: A schedule question during a philosophical exchange.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 另一种收获——这个说法我喜欢。你知道吗，其实现在我有时候会给自己朗读那些诗，在花园里，在雨天。不是为了教谁，就是为了自己。这才发现，诗原来是给自己读的。
turns.text_pinyin: Lìng yī zhǒng shōuhuò — zhège shuōfǎ wǒ xǐhuan. Nǐ zhīdào ma, qíshí xiànzài wǒ yǒu shíhou huì gěi zìjǐ lǎngdú nàxiē shī, zài huāyuán lǐ, zài yǔ tiān. Bú shì wèile jiāo shéi, jiù shì wèile zìjǐ. Zhè cái fāxiàn, shī yuánlái shì gěi zìjǐ dú de.
turns.text_en: Another kind of harvest — I like that phrase. You know, now sometimes I read those poems aloud to myself, in the garden, on rainy days. Not to teach anyone, just for myself. That's when I discovered: poems were always meant to be read for oneself.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his discovery of reading for himself.

options.text_zh: 在雨天的花园里给自己朗读——这本身就已经是一首诗了。我觉得您的学生如果知道您现在这样，一定会觉得，这才是最好的语文课。
options.text_pinyin: Zài yǔ tiān de huāyuán lǐ gěi zìjǐ lǎngdú — zhè běnshēn jiù yǐjīng shì yī shǒu shī le. Wǒ juéde nín de xuéshēng rúguǒ zhīdào nín xiànzài zhèyàng, yīdìng huì juéde, zhè cái shì zuì hǎo de yǔwén kè.
options.text_en: Reading aloud to yourself in a rainy garden — that itself is already a poem. I think if your students knew you were like this now, they'd say this is the best Chinese literature class of all.
options.feedback: His life has become the poem he used to teach — the highest form of understanding.

options.text_zh: 您可以录下来放到网上，很多人会喜欢听的。
options.text_pinyin: Nín kěyǐ lù xiàlái fàng dào wǎng shàng, hěn duō rén huì xǐhuan tīng de.
options.text_en: You could record them and post them online — lots of people would enjoy listening.
options.feedback: Turns a private ritual into content creation.

options.text_zh: 您最喜欢哪首诗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín zuì xǐhuan nǎ shǒu shī?
options.text_en: Which poem is your favorite?
options.feedback: Requests a title when the conversation has gone beyond specific poems.

tree.cultural_note: Rain on lotus leaves (雨打荷叶) is one of the most celebrated natural sounds in Chinese aesthetics, appearing in countless poems. The concept of 听雨 (listening to rain) is itself a genre of contemplation. The retired teacher's journey from teaching poems to living them mirrors a common Chinese wisdom: understanding (懂) is not the same as knowing (知道).

tree.setup: You visit a small calligraphy studio and find the teacher, Mr. Chen, slowly grinding ink on a stone. The room smells of ink and sandalwood.
tree.setup_zh: 你走进一间小小的书法工作室，看到陈老师正在慢慢地磨墨。房间里弥漫着墨汁和檀香的味道。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 不好意思，我在磨墨。坐吧，等我磨好。很多人觉得磨墨是浪费时间，但其实这是写字之前最重要的一步。磨墨的时候，心会慢慢静下来。
turns.text_pinyin: Bù hǎoyìsi, wǒ zài mó mò. Zuò ba, děng wǒ mó hǎo. Hěn duō rén juéde mó mò shì làngfèi shíjiān, dàn qíshí zhè shì xiě zì zhīqián zuì zhòngyào de yī bù. Mó mò de shíhou, xīn huì mànman jìng xiàlái.
turns.text_en: Sorry, I'm grinding ink. Have a seat, wait for me to finish. Many people think grinding ink is a waste of time, but it's actually the most important step before writing. While grinding ink, the heart slowly settles.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his practice of grinding ink.

options.text_zh: 心慢慢静下来——原来磨墨不只是准备墨汁，也是在准备写字的人。这个过程本身就是一种练习。
options.text_pinyin: Xīn mànman jìng xiàlái — yuánlái mó mò bù zhǐ shì zhǔnbèi mòzhī, yě shì zài zhǔnbèi xiě zì de rén. Zhège guòchéng běnshēn jiù shì yī zhǒng liànxí.
options.text_en: The heart slowly settles — so grinding ink isn't just preparing the ink, it's preparing the person who writes. The process itself is a form of practice.
options.feedback: Sees that preparation is not separate from the art — it is the art.

options.text_zh: 现在不是有墨水可以直接用吗？
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài bú shì yǒu mòshuǐ kěyǐ zhíjiē yòng ma?
options.text_en: Can't you just use bottled ink now?
options.feedback: Suggests a shortcut that misses the entire point.

options.text_zh: 磨墨要多长时间？
options.text_pinyin: Mó mò yào duō cháng shíjiān?
options.text_en: How long does grinding ink take?
options.feedback: Measures time when the practice is about losing track of it.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你理解得很好。我学书法的时候，师父说过一句话——「急着写出来的字，一笔都站不住」。书法不是手的事，是心的事。如果你心里乱，手再稳也写不好。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ lǐjiě de hěn hǎo. Wǒ xué shūfǎ de shíhou, shīfu shuō guò yī jù huà — 'jí zhe xiě chūlái de zì, yī bǐ dōu zhàn bú zhù'. Shūfǎ bú shì shǒu de shì, shì xīn de shì. Rúguǒ nǐ xīnlǐ luàn, shǒu zài wěn yě xiě bù hǎo.
turns.text_en: You understand well. When I was learning calligraphy, my teacher said — 'Characters written in haste can't stand on even one stroke.' Calligraphy isn't about the hand, it's about the heart. If your heart is chaotic, no amount of steady hands will save the writing.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the connection between inner state and outer expression.

options.text_zh: 「一笔都站不住」——这个画面特别生动。字是心的镜子，心乱了，字就歪了。这不只是书法的道理，好像什么事情都是这样。先安心，才能做好。
options.text_pinyin: 'Yī bǐ dōu zhàn bú zhù' — zhège huàmiàn tèbié shēngdòng. Zì shì xīn de jìngzi, xīn luàn le, zì jiù wāi le. Zhè bù zhǐ shì shūfǎ de dàolǐ, hǎoxiàng shénme shìqing dōu shì zhèyàng. Xiān ānxīn, cáinéng zuò hǎo.
options.text_en: 'Can't stand on one stroke' — what a vivid image. Characters mirror the heart; disordered heart, crooked strokes. This isn't about calligraphy — it's true of everything. Settle the heart first, then the work.
options.feedback: Extends calligraphy into a universal principle — inner order precedes outer quality.

options.text_zh: 您写了多少年书法了？
options.text_pinyin: Nín xiě le duōshǎo nián shūfǎ le?
options.text_en: How many years have you practiced calligraphy?
options.feedback: Asks for credentials during a philosophical moment.

options.text_zh: 您觉得楷书和行书哪个更难？
options.text_pinyin: Nín juéde kǎishū hé xíngshū nǎge gèng nán?
options.text_en: Which is harder, regular script or running script?
options.feedback: A technical comparison that derails the deeper conversation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 就是这样。我年轻的时候急着想把字练漂亮，每天写很多。但是越急越写不好。后来才明白，不是写得多就能写好，而是心够静才能写好。现在我每天只写几个字，但是每个字都是认真的。
turns.text_pinyin: Jiù shì zhèyàng. Wǒ niánqīng de shíhou jí zhe xiǎng bǎ zì liàn piàoliang, měi tiān xiě hěn duō. Dànshì yuè jí yuè xiě bù hǎo. Hòulái cái míngbai, bú shì xiě de duō jiù néng xiě hǎo, ér shì xīn gòu jìng cáinéng xiě hǎo. Xiànzài wǒ měi tiān zhǐ xiě jǐ ge zì, dànshì měi ge zì dōu shì rènzhēn de.
turns.text_en: Exactly. When I was young I rushed to make my characters beautiful, writing many each day. But the more I rushed, the worse they got. Only later did I understand: it's not writing more that makes you better, it's having a quiet enough heart. Now I write only a few characters a day, but each one is sincere.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his journey from quantity to quality.

options.text_zh: 每个字都是认真的——这比写一千个敷衍的字更有意义。我觉得这也是一种勇气，敢于少做，但是每一件都做到最好。
options.text_pinyin: Měi ge zì dōu shì rènzhēn de — zhè bǐ xiě yīqiān ge fūyǎn de zì gèng yǒu yìyì. Wǒ juéde zhè yě shì yī zhǒng yǒngqì, gǎn yú shǎo zuò, dànshì měi yī jiàn dōu zuò dào zuì hǎo.
options.text_en: Each character done sincerely — that means more than a thousand careless ones. I think that's also a kind of courage: daring to do less, but making each thing the best it can be.
options.feedback: Less as courage — doing fewer things with full attention is harder than doing many carelessly.

options.text_zh: 您的字可以卖吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín de zì kěyǐ mài ma?
options.text_en: Do you sell your calligraphy?
options.feedback: Introduces commerce into a discussion about sincerity.

options.text_zh: 我也想学书法，在哪里可以学？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yě xiǎng xué shūfǎ, zài nǎlǐ kěyǐ xué?
options.text_en: I want to learn calligraphy too — where can I study?
options.feedback: Asks for logistics during a meaningful philosophical exchange.

tree.cultural_note: Ink grinding (磨墨) is considered the meditative threshold of calligraphy. The four treasures of the study (文房四宝) — brush, ink, paper, and inkstone — are not just tools but partners in contemplation. The master's phrase 急着写出来的字一笔都站不住 encapsulates a core teaching: rushed work lacks structural integrity, in calligraphy and in life.

tree.setup: You're hiking and stop to rest on a small wooden bridge over a stream. A local farmer is sitting there eating his lunch.
tree.setup_zh: 你在爬山，停下来在一座小木桥上休息。一位当地的农民正坐在那里吃午饭。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 坐吧，这里有风，比山上凉快。你从城里来的吧？城里人都喜欢来爬山。我倒是每天都走这条路，已经走了几十年了，从来没觉得有什么特别。
turns.text_pinyin: Zuò ba, zhèlǐ yǒu fēng, bǐ shān shàng liángkuai. Nǐ cóng chéng lǐ lái de ba? Chéng lǐ rén dōu xǐhuan lái pá shān. Wǒ dào shì měi tiān dōu zǒu zhè tiáo lù, yǐjīng zǒu le jǐ shí nián le, cónglái méi juéde yǒu shénme tèbié.
turns.text_en: Sit down — there's a breeze here, cooler than up the mountain. You're from the city, right? City folks love hiking. I walk this road every day, have for decades — never thought it was anything special.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the contrast between his daily life and your visit.

options.text_zh: 也许就是因为我不是每天都能来，所以觉得特别美。但我觉得您每天走在这条路上，其实是很幸福的——只是习惯了就不觉得了。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ jiù shì yīnwèi wǒ bú shì měi tiān dōu néng lái, suǒyǐ juéde tèbié měi. Dàn wǒ juéde nín měi tiān zǒu zài zhè tiáo lù shàng, qíshí shì hěn xìngfú de — zhǐ shì xíguàn le jiù bù juéde le.
options.text_en: Maybe it's because I can't come every day that it feels so beautiful. But I think walking this path daily is actually a kind of happiness — you just stop noticing when you're used to it.
options.feedback: The visitor sees what the local has stopped seeing — both perspectives hold truth.

options.text_zh: 这里空气真好。
options.text_pinyin: Zhèlǐ kōngqì zhēn hǎo.
options.text_en: The air here is really good.
options.feedback: A surface observation that doesn't engage his reflection.

options.text_zh: 您种什么菜？
options.text_pinyin: Nín zhǒng shénme cài?
options.text_en: What vegetables do you grow?
options.feedback: Changes subject to farming details.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你这样一说，我倒是想起来了——我小时候第一次走到这座桥，看到下面的水那么清，高兴了好半天。后来走了太多次，就不看了。也许你说得对，幸福一直在，是我自己忘了看。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhèyàng yī shuō, wǒ dào shì xiǎng qǐlái le — wǒ xiǎo shíhou dì yī cì zǒu dào zhè zuò qiáo, kàn dào xiàmian de shuǐ nàme qīng, gāoxìng le hǎo bàntiān. Hòulái zǒu le tài duō cì, jiù bú kàn le. Yěxǔ nǐ shuō de duì, xìngfú yīzhí zài, shì wǒ zìjǐ wàng le kàn.
turns.text_en: Now that you say it, I remember — the first time I walked to this bridge as a child and saw how clear the water was below, I was happy for a long time. After too many crossings, I stopped looking. Maybe you're right — the happiness was always here, I just forgot to look.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his rediscovery.

options.text_zh: 水一直那么清，桥一直在。有时候我们需要一个外人的眼睛来帮我们重新看到身边的美。今天您帮了我，我也帮了您——挺好的。
options.text_pinyin: Shuǐ yīzhí nàme qīng, qiáo yīzhí zài. Yǒu shíhou wǒmen xūyào yī ge wàirén de yǎnjing lái bāng wǒmen chóngxīn kàn dào shēnbiān de měi. Jīntiān nín bāng le wǒ, wǒ yě bāng le nín — tǐng hǎo de.
options.text_en: The water's always been clear, the bridge always here. Sometimes we need an outsider's eyes to see the beauty around us again. Today you helped me, I helped you — that's nice.
options.feedback: Mutual gift — the stranger and the local each restore the other's sight.

options.text_zh: 您应该多带朋友来这里看看。
options.text_pinyin: Nín yīnggāi duō dài péngyou lái zhèlǐ kànkan.
options.text_en: You should bring friends here to see it.
options.feedback: Advice-giving instead of savoring the shared moment.

options.text_zh: 这条小溪流到哪里去？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè tiáo xiǎo xī liú dào nǎlǐ qù?
options.text_en: Where does this stream flow to?
options.feedback: A geography question during an emotional exchange.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 哈哈，说得好。以前我觉得你们城里人来爬山是「不懂生活」——有山有什么好看的？今天才知道，也许你们比我们更会「看」。谢谢你啊，年轻人。明天过桥的时候，我会记得往下看看的。
turns.text_pinyin: Hāhā, shuō de hǎo. Yǐqián wǒ juéde nǐmen chéng lǐ rén lái pá shān shì 'bù dǒng shēnghuó' — yǒu shān yǒu shénme hǎokàn de? Jīntiān cái zhīdào, yěxǔ nǐmen bǐ wǒmen gèng huì 'kàn'. Xièxie nǐ a, niánqīng rén. Míngtiān guò qiáo de shíhou, wǒ huì jìde wǎng xià kànkan de.
turns.text_en: Haha, well said. I used to think city folks coming to hike 'didn't understand life' — what's so special about mountains? Now I see, maybe you're better at 'looking' than we are. Thank you, young person. Tomorrow when I cross this bridge, I'll remember to look down.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his thanks and his promise to look.

options.text_zh: 那明天您往下看的时候，那条小溪就会变回您小时候第一次看到的样子。不是水变了，是眼睛变回来了。
options.text_pinyin: Nà míngtiān nín wǎng xià kàn de shíhou, nà tiáo xiǎo xī jiù huì biàn huí nín xiǎo shíhou dì yī cì kàn dào de yàngzi. Bú shì shuǐ biàn le, shì yǎnjing biàn huílái le.
options.text_en: Then tomorrow when you look down, that stream will look the way it did the first time you saw it as a child. Not because the water changed — because your eyes changed back.
options.feedback: The stream didn't change, the seeing did — a perfect closing thought.

options.text_zh: 我下次还会来爬山的！
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ xià cì hái huì lái pá shān de!
options.text_en: I'll come hiking again next time!
options.feedback: A future plan that doesn't match the depth of the conversation.

options.text_zh: 您午饭吃的什么？看起来很好吃。
options.text_pinyin: Nín wǔfàn chī de shénme? Kàn qǐlái hěn hǎochī.
options.text_en: What are you having for lunch? It looks tasty.
options.feedback: Pivots to food at the conclusion of a meaningful exchange.

tree.cultural_note: The contrast between city visitors and rural locals is a recurring theme in Chinese life. City dwellers often seek nature as escape, while locals may overlook what surrounds them daily. The concept of 重新看到 (seeing again) connects to Buddhist 初心 (beginner's mind) — the ability to perceive the familiar as if for the first time.

tree.setup: You're on an overnight train, unable to sleep. The person in the bunk across from you is also awake, quietly looking out the window at passing lights.
tree.setup_zh: 你在一辆夜间火车上，睡不着。对面铺位的人也没睡，静静地看着窗外闪过的灯光。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你也睡不着吗？夜车上最有意思的就是窗外的灯光——一闪一闪的，每一个光点后面都是一个人家。你说他们在做什么呢？
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ yě shuì bù zháo ma? Yè chē shàng zuì yǒu yìsi de jiù shì chuāng wài de dēngguāng — yī shǎn yī shǎn de, měi yī ge guāng diǎn hòumiàn dōu shì yī ge rénjiā. Nǐ shuō tāmen zài zuò shénme ne?
turns.text_en: Can't sleep either? The most interesting thing on a night train is the lights outside the window — flickering past, each point of light is someone's home. What do you think they're doing?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her imagination about the lights.

options.text_zh: 也许有人在给孩子讲故事，有人在洗碗，有人也在看窗外——看到我们的火车经过，想着那些人要去哪里。我们想他们，他们也许也在想我们。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ yǒu rén zài gěi háizi jiǎng gùshi, yǒu rén zài xǐ wǎn, yǒu rén yě zài kàn chuāng wài — kàn dào wǒmen de huǒchē jīngguò, xiǎng zhe nàxiē rén yào qù nǎlǐ. Wǒmen xiǎng tāmen, tāmen yěxǔ yě zài xiǎng wǒmen.
options.text_en: Maybe someone's telling their child a story, someone's washing dishes, someone's looking out their window — seeing our train pass, wondering where we're going. We think of them; maybe they think of us too.
options.feedback: Reciprocity — the watchers are being watched.

options.text_zh: 大概都睡了吧。
options.text_pinyin: Dàgài dōu shuì le ba.
options.text_en: They're probably all asleep.
options.feedback: Shuts down her imaginative game.

options.text_zh: 你要去哪里？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yào qù nǎlǐ?
options.text_en: Where are you headed?
options.feedback: Changes subject from the lights to travel logistics.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 哈，我们在想他们，他们也在想我们——这个想法真好。火车就是这样，把很多不认识的人放在一个空间里。明天大家就各自走各自的路了，但今晚我们一起看了同一片灯光。
turns.text_pinyin: Hā, wǒmen zài xiǎng tāmen, tāmen yě zài xiǎng wǒmen — zhège xiǎngfǎ zhēn hǎo. Huǒchē jiù shì zhèyàng, bǎ hěn duō bù rènshi de rén fàng zài yī ge kōngjiān lǐ. Míngtiān dàjiā jiù gèzì zǒu gèzì de lù le, dàn jīnwǎn wǒmen yīqǐ kàn le tóng yī piàn dēngguāng.
turns.text_en: Ha, we're thinking about them and they're thinking about us — what a lovely thought. That's what trains do — put strangers together in one space. Tomorrow everyone goes their separate ways, but tonight we watched the same lights together.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the temporary closeness of strangers on a train.

options.text_zh: 也许旅行最珍贵的不是到达目的地，而是路上遇到的人和风景。就算以后不再见面，今晚的灯光和这次对话，我都会记住。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ lǚxíng zuì zhēnguì de bú shì dàodá mùdìdì, ér shì lù shàng yù dào de rén hé fēngjǐng. Jiùsuàn yǐhòu bú zài jiànmiàn, jīnwǎn de dēngguāng hé zhè cì duìhuà, wǒ dōu huì jì zhù.
options.text_en: Maybe travel's most precious part isn't arriving, but the people and sights along the way. Even if we never meet again, I'll remember tonight's lights and this conversation.
options.feedback: The journey matters more than the destination — a night train philosophy.

options.text_zh: 你要不要加我微信？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yào bú yào jiā wǒ Wēixìn?
options.text_en: Want to add me on WeChat?
options.feedback: Tries to make permanent what is beautiful precisely because it's temporary.

options.text_zh: 我们应该试着睡了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒmen yīnggāi shì zhe shuì le.
options.text_en: We should try to sleep.
options.feedback: Breaks a rare moment of connection with practicality.

tree.cultural_note: Night trains (夜车) have long been spaces of intimate strangers in China. The brief, deep conversations between people who will never meet again carry a special quality — unguarded, honest, and ephemeral. The flickering lights outside are a shared canvas for imagination, a tradition of long-distance Chinese rail travel.

tree.setup: You visit a tea farmer's home in the mountains. She pours you a cup of tea from leaves she picked that morning.
tree.setup_zh: 你在山里拜访一位茶农的家。她用今天早上采的茶叶给你泡了一杯茶。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 尝尝看。这是今天早上太阳出来之前采的，露水还在叶子上。早上的茶叶最嫩，味道最好。你喝得出来吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Chángchang kàn. Zhè shì jīntiān zǎoshang tàiyáng chūlái zhīqián cǎi de, lùshuǐ hái zài yèzi shàng. Zǎoshang de cháyè zuì nèn, wèidào zuì hǎo. Nǐ hē de chūlái ma?
turns.text_en: Try it. These were picked before sunrise this morning, with dew still on the leaves. Morning tea leaves are the most tender, the best flavor. Can you taste the difference?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the tea and her care.

options.text_zh: 太好喝了。有一种很清新的味道，好像能尝到今天早上的山和空气。我在城里喝的茶从来没有这个味道。
options.text_pinyin: Tài hǎo hē le. Yǒu yī zhǒng hěn qīngxīn de wèidào, hǎoxiàng néng cháng dào jīntiān zǎoshang de shān hé kōngqì. Wǒ zài chéng lǐ hē de chá cónglái méiyǒu zhège wèidào.
options.text_en: It's wonderful. There's a fresh flavor — it's like tasting this morning's mountain and air. Tea in the city never tastes like this.
options.feedback: Tasting the landscape in the cup — connects flavor to place.

options.text_zh: 这是什么品种的茶？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè shì shénme pǐnzhǒng de chá?
options.text_en: What variety of tea is this?
options.feedback: Classifying instead of experiencing.

options.text_zh: 你每天都起那么早吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ měi tiān dōu qǐ nàme zǎo ma?
options.text_en: Do you get up that early every day?
options.feedback: Schedule question instead of tasting the moment.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 能尝出来就好。城里的茶是包装好的，已经离开山很久了。茶叶和人一样，离开家太久，味道就会变。最好的茶，就是在它长大的地方喝。水也是这里的山泉水。
turns.text_pinyin: Néng cháng chūlái jiù hǎo. Chéng lǐ de chá shì bāozhuāng hǎo de, yǐjīng líkāi shān hěn jiǔ le. Cháyè hé rén yīyàng, líkāi jiā tài jiǔ, wèidào jiù huì biàn. Zuì hǎo de chá, jiù shì zài tā zhǎng dà de dìfang hē. Shuǐ yě shì zhèlǐ de shān quán shuǐ.
turns.text_en: Good that you can tell. City tea is packaged, already far from the mountain. Tea leaves are like people — away from home too long, the flavor changes. The best tea is drunk where it grew. The water is from our mountain spring too.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her analogy about tea and people.

options.text_zh: 茶叶和人一样——这让我很感动。来这里之前我不知道茶的味道和它的家分不开。也许人也是这样，最好的状态是在属于自己的地方。
options.text_pinyin: Cháyè hé rén yīyàng — zhè ràng wǒ hěn gǎndòng. Lái zhèlǐ zhīqián wǒ bù zhīdào chá de wèidào hé tā de jiā fēn bù kāi. Yěxǔ rén yě shì zhèyàng, zuì hǎo de zhuàngtài shì zài shǔyú zìjǐ de dìfāng.
options.text_en: Tea and people are the same — that really moves me. Before coming here I didn't know tea's flavor was inseparable from its home. Maybe people are the same, at their best where they belong.
options.feedback: Flavor and belonging are inseparable — for tea and for people.

options.text_zh: 你的茶卖到哪里去？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de chá mài dào nǎlǐ qù?
options.text_en: Where do you sell your tea?
options.feedback: Commercial question during a poetic moment.

options.text_zh: 我可以买一些带回去吗？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ kěyǐ mǎi yīxiē dài huíqù ma?
options.text_en: Can I buy some to take back?
options.feedback: She just said tea tastes best where it grew.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你来这里喝这杯茶，比带走一百包茶叶都有意义。因为这个味道只属于这个地方、这个早晨、这一刻。以后你想起这杯茶，就会想起这座山、这片云。这才是茶真正的价值。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ lái zhèlǐ hē zhè bēi chá, bǐ dài zǒu yī bǎi bāo cháyè dōu yǒu yìyì. Yīnwèi zhège wèidào zhǐ shǔyú zhège dìfang, zhège zǎochén, zhè yī kè. Yǐhòu nǐ xiǎng qǐ zhè bēi chá, jiù huì xiǎng qǐ zhè zuò shān, zhè piàn yún. Zhè cái shì chá zhēnzhèng de jiàzhí.
turns.text_en: Coming here to drink this cup of tea is more meaningful than taking a hundred bags home. Because this flavor belongs only to this place, this morning, this moment. Later when you remember this tea, you'll remember this mountain, these clouds. That's the true value of tea.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the irreplaceability of this moment.

options.text_zh: 这杯茶我一定会记住。不是味道本身，而是整个画面——山、云、泉水、还有您。谢谢您让我明白了什么是「在场」。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè bēi chá wǒ yīdìng huì jì zhù. Bú shì wèidào běnshēn, ér shì zhěnggè huàmiàn — shān, yún, quánshuǐ, hái yǒu nín. Xièxie nín ràng wǒ míngbai le shénme shì 'zàichǎng'.
options.text_en: I'll always remember this cup of tea. Not the flavor itself, but the whole scene — the mountain, clouds, spring water, and you. Thank you for helping me understand what it means to 'be present.'
options.feedback: The tea is a portal to a full sensory memory — presence is the real gift.

options.text_zh: 那我多喝几杯吧！
options.text_pinyin: Nà wǒ duō hē jǐ bēi ba!
options.text_en: Then let me have a few more cups!
options.feedback: Tries to accumulate quantity after a lesson about irreplaceability.

options.text_zh: 您这里可以住宿吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín zhèlǐ kěyǐ zhùsù ma?
options.text_en: Can I stay overnight here?
options.feedback: Logistics question at the peak of the conversation.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese tea culture emphasizes 地域性 (terroir) — the inseparable relationship between tea and its landscape. Mountain spring water (山泉水) is considered essential for the truest expression of the leaf. The farmer's insight that tea's real value is in the moment of drinking echoes the Zen tea concept of 一期一会 — each encounter is once in a lifetime.

tree.setup: In a narrow alley, you find a workshop where an elderly man makes paper lanterns by hand. Soft light glows from dozens of finished lanterns hanging from the ceiling.
tree.setup_zh: 在一条窄巷子里，你找到一间手工做纸灯笼的作坊。几十个做好的灯笼挂在天花板上，散发着柔和的光。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 进来看看吧。小心头，灯笼多。这些都是我一个一个做的。快过年了，订单多，但是我不会赶工。每个灯笼都要认真做，不然点上灯以后，光就不均匀。
turns.text_pinyin: Jìnlái kànkan ba. Xiǎoxīn tóu, dēnglong duō. Zhèxiē dōu shì wǒ yī ge yī ge zuò de. Kuài guò nián le, dìngdān duō, dànshì wǒ bú huì gǎn gōng. Měi ge dēnglong dōu yào rènzhēn zuò, bùrán diǎn shàng dēng yǐhòu, guāng jiù bù jūnyún.
turns.text_en: Come in and look. Watch your head — lots of lanterns. I made each one by hand. New Year is coming, lots of orders, but I won't rush. Each lantern must be made carefully, otherwise when you light it, the glow won't be even.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his craftsmanship and the lantern light.

options.text_zh: 这里好美——像走进了一个光做成的世界。每个灯笼的光都这么柔和，我能感觉到您花了很多心思。不赶工这件事本身就很了不起。
options.text_pinyin: Zhèlǐ hǎo měi — xiàng zǒu jìn le yī ge guāng zuò chéng de shìjiè. Měi ge dēnglong de guāng dōu zhème róuhé, wǒ néng gǎnjué dào nín huā le hěn duō xīnsi. Bù gǎn gōng zhè jiàn shì běnshēn jiù hěn liǎobuqǐ.
options.text_en: It's beautiful here — like walking into a world made of light. Each lantern's glow is so soft, I can feel how much care you put in. Refusing to rush is itself remarkable.
options.feedback: Sees the workshop as an art world — and honors the patience behind it.

options.text_zh: 做一个灯笼要多长时间？
options.text_pinyin: Zuò yī ge dēnglong yào duō cháng shíjiān?
options.text_en: How long does one lantern take?
options.feedback: Measures production time instead of appreciating the art.

options.text_zh: 机器做的灯笼也很好看吧？
options.text_pinyin: Jīqì zuò de dēnglong yě hěn hǎokàn ba?
options.text_en: Machine-made lanterns look nice too, right?
options.feedback: Compares mass production to his handcraft.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 谢谢你懂。很多人觉得灯笼就是灯笼，买一个便宜的挂上就好了。但我爸教我——灯笼是用来「送光」的。过年的时候，家家户户挂灯笼，不只是好看，是把光送给经过的每一个人。所以灯笼的光一定要好。
turns.text_pinyin: Xièxie nǐ dǒng. Hěn duō rén juéde dēnglong jiù shì dēnglong, mǎi yī ge piányi de guà shàng jiù hǎo le. Dàn wǒ bà jiāo wǒ — dēnglong shì yòng lái 'sòng guāng' de. Guò nián de shíhou, jiājiā hùhù guà dēnglong, bù zhǐ shì hǎokàn, shì bǎ guāng sòng gěi jīngguò de měi yī ge rén. Suǒyǐ dēnglong de guāng yīdìng yào hǎo.
turns.text_en: Thank you for understanding. Many people think a lantern is just a lantern — buy a cheap one and hang it up. But my father taught me — lanterns are for 'giving light.' During New Year, every household hangs lanterns, not just for decoration, but to give light to everyone who passes by. So the lantern's light must be good.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the idea of lanterns giving light to passersby.

options.text_zh: 「送光」——这个词太美了。灯笼不只是照亮自己的家，是照亮路人的路。这样一想，您做的每个灯笼都是送给陌生人的礼物。
options.text_pinyin: 'Sòng guāng' — zhège cí tài měi le. Dēnglong bù zhǐ shì zhào liàng zìjǐ de jiā, shì zhào liàng lùrén de lù. Zhèyàng yī xiǎng, nín zuò de měi ge dēnglong dōu shì sòng gěi mòshēng rén de lǐwù.
options.text_en: 'Giving light' — what a beautiful phrase. Lanterns don't just illuminate your own home, they light the way for passersby. Seen that way, every lantern you make is a gift to strangers.
options.feedback: Lanterns as anonymous gifts of light — generosity without knowing the recipient.

options.text_zh: 您父亲也是做灯笼的吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín fùqīn yě shì zuò dēnglong de ma?
options.text_en: Did your father also make lanterns?
options.feedback: Family history question during a moment about light's meaning.

options.text_zh: 现在还有多少人手工做灯笼？
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài hái yǒu duōshǎo rén shǒugōng zuò dēnglong?
options.text_en: How many people still make lanterns by hand?
options.feedback: Industry survey during a philosophical conversation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对，就是这样。做灯笼的人和看到灯光的人可能一辈子都不会认识，但那一刻，光是连着的。我觉得这是最温暖的关系——不需要认识，不需要感谢，只是把光放在那里，有人看到就够了。
turns.text_pinyin: Duì, jiù shì zhèyàng. Zuò dēnglong de rén hé kàn dào dēngguāng de rén kěnéng yībèizi dōu bú huì rènshi, dàn nà yī kè, guāng shì lián zhe de. Wǒ juéde zhè shì zuì wēnnuǎn de guānxi — bù xūyào rènshi, bù xūyào gǎnxiè, zhǐ shì bǎ guāng fàng zài nàlǐ, yǒu rén kàn dào jiù gòu le.
turns.text_en: Exactly. The lantern maker and the person who sees the light may never meet, but in that moment, the light connects them. I think that's the warmest kind of relationship — no need to know each other, no need for thanks, just putting light there for someone to see.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his philosophy of anonymous connection through light.

options.text_zh: 不需要认识，不需要感谢——这种善意最纯粹。世界上也许有很多这样的光——做面的阿姨、扫街的叔叔、种树的人。您让我看到了这个。
options.text_pinyin: Bù xūyào rènshi, bù xūyào gǎnxiè — zhè zhǒng shànyì zuì chúncuì. Shìjiè shàng yěxǔ yǒu hěn duō zhèyàng de guāng — zuò miàn de āyí, sǎo jiē de shūshu, zhǒng shù de rén. Nín ràng wǒ kàn dào le zhège.
options.text_en: No need to know, no need to thank — that's the purest kindness. The world probably has many such lights — the auntie making noodles, the uncle sweeping streets, people planting trees. You helped me see that.
options.feedback: Extends the lantern metaphor to all invisible kindness — everyone is a light-giver.

options.text_zh: 我可以买一个灯笼吗？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ kěyǐ mǎi yī ge dēnglong ma?
options.text_en: Can I buy a lantern?
options.feedback: Purchase request during a profound exchange about anonymous giving.

options.text_zh: 您应该开个网店。
options.text_pinyin: Nín yīnggāi kāi ge wǎng diàn.
options.text_en: You should open an online store.
options.feedback: Business advice at the emotional peak.

tree.cultural_note: Red lanterns (红灯笼) are among China's most powerful symbols of warmth and community. The tradition of hanging lanterns during Spring Festival transforms dark streets into rivers of warm light. The craftsman's concept of 送光 (giving light) elevates a decorative object into an act of generosity — the lantern-maker as an anonymous benefactor to every nighttime passerby.

tree.setup: At a flea market, you pick up a chipped blue porcelain bowl. The seller, an older woman, notices your interest.
tree.setup_zh: 在跳蚤市场上，你拿起一个有缺口的蓝色瓷碗。卖家，一位年纪大的女人，注意到你的兴趣。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这个碗有一个小缺口，所以很便宜。但是你看这个蓝色——以前的碗才有这种颜色。现在的碗都太白了，没有温度。这个碗是用过的，被人好好用过的。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhège wǎn yǒu yī ge xiǎo quēkǒu, suǒyǐ hěn piányi. Dànshì nǐ kàn zhège lán sè — yǐqián de wǎn cái yǒu zhè zhǒng yánsè. Xiànzài de wǎn dōu tài bái le, méiyǒu wēndù. Zhège wǎn shì yòng guò de, bèi rén hǎohāo yòng guò de.
turns.text_en: This bowl has a small chip, so it's very cheap. But look at this blue — only old bowls have this color. Modern bowls are too white, no warmth. This bowl has been used — really used by someone.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the character of the old bowl.

options.text_zh: 这个蓝色真的很特别——像是有故事的颜色。缺口反而让它更真实。完美的碗到处都有，但有过生活痕迹的碗是独一无二的。
options.text_pinyin: Zhège lán sè zhēn de hěn tèbié — xiàng shì yǒu gùshi de yánsè. Quēkǒu fǎn'ér ràng tā gèng zhēnshí. Wánměi de wǎn dàochù dōu yǒu, dàn yǒu guò shēnghuó hénjì de wǎn shì dúyīwú'èr de.
options.text_en: This blue is really special — it's a color with stories. The chip actually makes it more real. Perfect bowls are everywhere, but one with marks of life is one of a kind.
options.feedback: The chip as proof of life — imperfection as authenticity.

options.text_zh: 能便宜一点吗？
options.text_pinyin: Néng piányi yīdiǎn ma?
options.text_en: Can you make it cheaper?
options.feedback: Haggles when she's sharing something deeper.

options.text_zh: 缺口会不会割手？
options.text_pinyin: Quēkǒu huì bú huì gē shǒu?
options.text_en: Will the chip cut my hand?
options.feedback: A safety concern that misses the beauty she's pointing out.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你说得真好。现在的人都要新的、完美的。旧的、有裂纹的就扔了。但我觉得，用了很多年的东西有一种暖，新东西没有的暖。因为它记住了被使用的每一天。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ shuō de zhēn hǎo. Xiànzài de rén dōu yào xīn de, wánměi de. Jiù de, yǒu lièwén de jiù rēng le. Dàn wǒ juéde, yòng le hěn duō nián de dōngxi yǒu yī zhǒng nuǎn, xīn dōngxi méiyǒu de nuǎn. Yīnwèi tā jì zhù le bèi shǐyòng de měi yī tiān.
turns.text_en: You put it well. People today want new, perfect things. Old things with cracks get thrown away. But I think things used for many years have a warmth that new things don't have. Because they remember every day they were used.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the warmth stored in old things.

options.text_zh: 这个蓝色真特别——像是有故事的颜色。缺口反而让它更真实。完美的碗到处有，有生活痕迹的碗才是独一无二的。
options.text_pinyin: Zhège lán sè zhēn tèbié — xiàng shì yǒu gùshi de yánsè. Quēkǒu fǎn'ér ràng tā gèng zhēnshí. Wánměi de wǎn dàochù yǒu, yǒu shēnghuó hénjì de wǎn cái shì dúyīwú'èr de.
options.text_en: This blue is really special — a color with stories. The chip makes it more real. Perfect bowls are everywhere, but one with marks of life is truly one of a kind.
options.feedback: Continuing the bowl's life story — new hands, new memories, same warmth.

options.text_zh: 你这里还有别的旧碗吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhèlǐ hái yǒu bié de jiù wǎn ma?
options.text_en: Do you have other old bowls?
options.feedback: Browsing for more items during a meaningful exchange.

options.text_zh: 日本有一种修碗的艺术叫金缮。
options.text_pinyin: Rìběn yǒu yī zhǒng xiū wǎn de yìshù jiào jīnshàn.
options.text_en: Japan has a bowl-repair art called kintsugi.
options.feedback: Pivots to another culture's practice instead of honoring this moment.

tree.cultural_note: In Chinese households, a well-used rice bowl (饭碗) can carry decades of family history. The concept that objects absorb the warmth (温度) of their users reflects an animistic thread in Chinese thinking — that daily use imbues objects with a kind of life. Flea markets (跳蚤市场) in China are treasure troves of such storied objects.

tree.setup: Your bicycle has a flat tire. You bring it to a small repair stand under a tree. The repairman works quietly, his hands black with grease.
tree.setup_zh: 你的自行车爆胎了。你推到一棵树下的修车摊。修车师傅安静地工作着，双手沾满了油污。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 坐着等一下，很快就好。这个内胎还能补，不用换新的。现在的人什么东西坏了就想换新的，其实很多东西修一修还能用很久。
turns.text_pinyin: Zuò zhe děng yīxià, hěn kuài jiù hǎo. Zhège nèitāi hái néng bǔ, bú yòng huàn xīn de. Xiànzài de rén shénme dōngxi huài le jiù xiǎng huàn xīn de, qíshí hěn duō dōngxi xiū yī xiū hái néng yòng hěn jiǔ.
turns.text_en: Sit and wait a moment, it'll be quick. This inner tube can still be patched — no need for a new one. People today want to replace everything that breaks, but many things can last a long time with a little repair.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his philosophy about repairing vs. replacing.

options.text_zh: 您说得对。我们太习惯扔东西了。其实修好一个东西比买一个新的更有意义——因为你给了它第二次生命。
options.text_pinyin: Nín shuō de duì. Wǒmen tài xíguàn rēng dōngxi le. Qíshí xiū hǎo yī ge dōngxi bǐ mǎi yī ge xīn de gèng yǒu yìyì — yīnwèi nǐ gěi le tā dì èr cì shēngmìng.
options.text_en: You're right. We're too used to throwing things away. Actually, repairing something is more meaningful than buying new — because you give it a second life.
options.feedback: Repair as resurrection — giving things a second chance at life.

options.text_zh: 不过补过的地方总是比原来的弱一点吧？我还是有点担心骑着骑着又坏了。换新的可能更放心一些。
options.text_pinyin: Búguò bǔ guò de dìfāng zǒng shì bǐ yuánlái de ruò yīdiǎn ba? Wǒ háishì yǒudiǎn dānxīn qí zhe qí zhe yòu huài le. Huàn xīn de kěnéng gèng fàngxīn yīxiē.
options.text_en: But a patched spot is always weaker than the original, right? I'm a bit worried it'll break again while riding. Replacing might give more peace of mind.
options.feedback: He presses the patch firmly and says: 'A scar doesn't mean something is weak. Sometimes it's the strongest part.'

options.text_zh: 补好以后还会再破吗？
