options.text_pinyin: Bǔ hǎo yǐhòu hái huì zài pò ma?
options.text_en: Will it puncture again after patching?
options.feedback: Doubts his repair instead of engaging his thought.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 第二次生命——哈，没想到补个胎还能说得这么有道理。我干这行三十多年了。以前每条街都有修车的，现在越来越少了。大家都骑共享单车，坏了就换一辆，没人修了。
turns.text_pinyin: Dì èr cì shēngmìng — hā, méi xiǎng dào bǔ ge tāi hái néng shuō de zhème yǒu dàolǐ. Wǒ gàn zhè háng sānshí duō nián le. Yǐqián měi tiáo jiē dōu yǒu xiū chē de, xiànzài yuè lái yuè shǎo le. Dàjiā dōu qí gòngxiǎng dānchē, huài le jiù huàn yī liàng, méi rén xiū le.
turns.text_en: Second life — ha, never thought patching a tire could sound so meaningful. I've been doing this over thirty years. Every street used to have a bike repairman — now fewer and fewer. Everyone rides shared bikes, if one breaks just switch to another, nobody repairs anymore.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the disappearing craft of bicycle repair.

options.text_zh: 但您还在这里——说明还有人需要您，还有人相信修比换好。也许不是每条街都需要修车师傅了，但这条街因为有您，不一样。
options.text_pinyin: Dàn nín hái zài zhèlǐ — shuōmíng hái yǒu rén xūyào nín, hái yǒu rén xiāngxìn xiū bǐ huàn hǎo. Yěxǔ bú shì měi tiáo jiē dōu xūyào xiū chē shīfù le, dàn zhè tiáo jiē yīnwèi yǒu nín, bù yīyàng.
options.text_en: But you're still here — that means someone needs you, someone believes repairing is better than replacing. Maybe not every street needs a repairman now, but this street is different because of you.
options.feedback: His presence defines the street — the repairman as guardian of care.

options.text_zh: 共享单车确实方便。
options.text_pinyin: Gòngxiǎng dānchē quèshí fāngbiàn.
options.text_en: Shared bikes are indeed convenient.
options.feedback: Validates the system that's displacing him.

options.text_zh: 您打算干到什么时候？
options.text_pinyin: Nín dǎsuàn gàn dào shénme shíhou?
options.text_en: How long do you plan to keep doing this?
options.feedback: Asks about retirement during a moment of affirmation.

tree.cultural_note: Bicycle repairmen (修车师傅) were once as common as mailboxes on Chinese streets. Their disappearance mirrors a broader shift from repair culture to replacement culture. The remaining repairmen — often under trees with simple tools — represent a vanishing ethic: that things worth using are worth fixing.

tree.setup: Your upstairs neighbor has transformed her tiny windowsill into a lush garden with herbs and small flowers. You stop to admire it from below.
tree.setup_zh: 楼上的邻居把她的小窗台变成了一个种满香草和小花的花园。你在楼下停下来欣赏。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你在看我的花园呢？都是些小东西——薄荷、罗勒、还有一盆小雏菊。地方虽然小，但是我觉得，只要用心，哪里都可以是花园。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ zài kàn wǒ de huāyuán ne? Dōu shì xiē xiǎo dōngxi — bòhe, luólè, hái yǒu yī pén xiǎo chújú. Dìfang suīrán xiǎo, dànshì wǒ juéde, zhǐyào yòngxīn, nǎlǐ dōu kěyǐ shì huāyuán.
turns.text_en: You're looking at my garden? Just small things — mint, basil, and a pot of daisies. The space is tiny, but I think as long as you put your heart in, anywhere can be a garden.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her windowsill garden philosophy.

options.text_zh: 从下面看真的很美。只有一个窗台，但是被您照顾得像一个真正的花园。我觉得「用心」才是最重要的——空间大不大不重要。
options.text_pinyin: Cóng xiàmian kàn zhēn de hěn měi. Zhǐyǒu yī ge chuāngtái, dànshì bèi nín zhàogù de xiàng yī ge zhēnzhèng de huāyuán. Wǒ juéde 'yòngxīn' cái shì zuì zhòngyào de — kōngjiān dà bú dà bù zhòngyào.
options.text_en: It looks really beautiful from below. Just a windowsill, but you've cared for it like a real garden. I think 'putting your heart in' is what matters most — the size of the space doesn't.
options.feedback: Care over space — the heart makes the garden, not the acreage.

options.text_zh: 薄荷可以泡水喝。
options.text_pinyin: Bòhe kěyǐ pào shuǐ hē.
options.text_en: You can steep mint in water.
options.feedback: A usage tip instead of appreciating her philosophy.

options.text_zh: 您不觉得窗台太小了吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín bù juéde chuāngtái tài xiǎo le ma?
options.text_en: Don't you think the windowsill is too small?
options.feedback: Questions the very thing she's transcended.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 谢谢你。我以前住农村的时候有一个大院子。搬到城市以后，只有这个窗台了。刚开始觉得很失落，但是后来想通了——大花园和小花园给人的快乐是一样的。只要有一棵植物在生长，你就会觉得生活是有希望的。
turns.text_pinyin: Xièxie nǐ. Wǒ yǐqián zhù nóngcūn de shíhou yǒu yī ge dà yuànzi. Bān dào chéngshì yǐhòu, zhǐyǒu zhège chuāngtái le. Gāng kāishǐ juéde hěn shīluò, dànshì hòulái xiǎng tōng le — dà huāyuán hé xiǎo huāyuán gěi rén de kuàilè shì yīyàng de. Zhǐyào yǒu yī kē zhíwù zài shēngzhǎng, nǐ jiù huì juéde shēnghuó shì yǒu xīwàng de.
turns.text_en: Thank you. I used to have a big courtyard when I lived in the countryside. After moving to the city, all I had was this windowsill. At first I felt lost, but then I figured it out — big gardens and small gardens give the same joy. As long as one plant is growing, you feel life has hope.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her insight about growth and hope.

options.text_zh: 只要有一棵植物在生长——这让我很感动。不管空间多小，有生命在生长就有希望。您的窗台花园从楼下也能感受到那种生命力。
options.text_pinyin: Zhǐyào yǒu yī kē zhíwù zài shēngzhǎng — zhè ràng wǒ hěn gǎndòng. Bùguǎn kōngjiān duō xiǎo, yǒu shēngmìng zài shēngzhǎng jiù yǒu xīwàng. Nín de chuāngtái huāyuán cóng lóu xià yě néng gǎnshòu dào nà zhǒng shēngmìnglì.
options.text_en: As long as one plant is growing — that moves me. No matter how small the space, if life is growing, there's hope. Your windowsill garden radiates that vitality even from down below.
options.feedback: Growth as hope, visible from the street — her garden gives to everyone.

options.text_zh: 您为什么搬到城市？
options.text_pinyin: Nín wèishéme bān dào chéngshì?
options.text_en: Why did you move to the city?
options.feedback: Asks about the move when she's already made peace with it.

options.text_zh: 您有没有想过在阳台上种菜？
options.text_pinyin: Nín yǒu méiyǒu xiǎng guò zài yángtái shàng zhòng cài?
options.text_en: Have you thought about growing vegetables on the balcony?
options.feedback: Suggests upgrading when her point is about sufficiency.

tree.cultural_note: Many urban Chinese residents, especially those from rural backgrounds, maintain windowsill or balcony gardens as a connection to their former life on the land. The phrase 想通了 (figured it out / came to terms with it) describes a mental shift from loss to acceptance, a valued emotional milestone in Chinese culture.

tree.setup: You meet a postman resting on a bench in a village. He tells you he's retiring next month after delivering mail here for twenty-five years.
tree.setup_zh: 你在村子里遇到一位坐在长椅上休息的邮递员。他告诉你下个月就要退休了，在这里送信已经二十五年了。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 二十五年了，这条路上每一家我都认识。谁家有几口人，谁家的狗叫什么名字，我都知道。下个月退休以后，我最不习惯的大概就是不用走这条路了。
turns.text_pinyin: Èrshíwǔ nián le, zhè tiáo lù shàng měi yī jiā wǒ dōu rènshi. Shéi jiā yǒu jǐ kǒu rén, shéi jiā de gǒu jiào shénme míngzi, wǒ dōu zhīdào. Xià ge yuè tuìxiū yǐhòu, wǒ zuì bù xíguàn de dàgài jiù shì bú yòng zǒu zhè tiáo lù le.
turns.text_en: Twenty-five years — I know every family on this road. How many people in each house, what their dogs are named, I know it all. After I retire next month, the hardest thing will probably be not walking this road anymore.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his deep connection with the route.

options.text_zh: 二十五年——这条路不只是邮路了，是您生活的一部分。每天走同一条路，但看到的是不同的季节、不同的故事。这种连接很珍贵。
options.text_pinyin: Èrshíwǔ nián — zhè tiáo lù bù zhǐ shì yóulù le, shì nín shēnghuó de yī bùfen. Měi tiān zǒu tóng yī tiáo lù, dàn kàn dào de shì bùtóng de jìjié, bùtóng de gùshi. Zhè zhǒng liánjiē hěn zhēnguì.
options.text_en: Twenty-five years — this road is more than a mail route, it's part of your life. Same road every day, but different seasons, different stories. That connection is precious.
options.feedback: The route as life itself — same path, ever-changing story.

options.text_zh: 现在还有人写信吗？
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài hái yǒu rén xiě xìn ma?
options.text_en: Do people still write letters?
options.feedback: Questions the relevance of his profession.

options.text_zh: 退休以后您打算做什么？
options.text_pinyin: Tuìxiū yǐhòu nín dǎsuàn zuò shénme?
options.text_en: What are you planning to do after retirement?
options.feedback: Jumps past his present feelings to future plans.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 嗯……不同的季节、不同的故事。春天的时候路边开花，夏天有人在门口乘凉，秋天满地落叶，冬天早上有霜。走了二十五年，这条路四季的样子我都记得。有时候觉得，我不是在送信，是在陪这条路过四季。
turns.text_pinyin: Ǹg... , bùtóng de jìjié, bùtóng de gùshi. Chūntiān de shíhou lù biān kāi huā, xiàtiān yǒu rén zài ménkǒu chéngliáng, qiūtiān mǎn dì luòyè, dōngtiān zǎoshang yǒu shuāng. Zǒu le èrshíwǔ nián, zhè tiáo lù sìjì de yàngzi wǒ dōu jìde. Yǒu shíhou juéde, wǒ bú shì zài sòng xìn, shì zài péi zhè tiáo lù guò sìjì.
turns.text_en: Mm... different seasons, different stories. Spring with roadside flowers, summer with people cooling off in doorways, autumn with fallen leaves everywhere, winter with morning frost. Twenty-five years, I remember this road in every season. Sometimes I feel I wasn't delivering mail — I was keeping this road company through the seasons.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the image of accompanying the road through seasons.

options.text_zh: 陪这条路过四季——太美了。路也会想念您的。二十五年来每天有人走过来带着信也带着关心。您不只是邮递员，您是这条路上的风景。
options.text_pinyin: Péi zhè tiáo lù guò sìjì — tài měi le. Lù yě huì xiǎngniàn nín de. Èrshíwǔ nián lái měi tiān yǒu rén zǒu guòlái dài zhe xìn yě dài zhe guānxīn. Nín bù zhǐ shì yóudìyuán, nín shì zhè tiáo lù shàng de fēngjǐng.
options.text_en: Keeping the road company through seasons — beautiful. The road will miss you too. Twenty-five years of someone walking by with letters and care. You're not just a postman — you're part of this road's scenery.
options.feedback: The postman as scenery — he belongs to the road as much as the trees do.

options.text_zh: 退休以后还可以来散步啊。
options.text_pinyin: Tuìxiū yǐhòu hái kěyǐ lái sànbù a.
options.text_en: You can still come walk here after retirement.
options.feedback: A practical suggestion during a reflective farewell.

options.text_zh: 新的邮递员会是谁？
options.text_pinyin: Xīn de yóudìyuán huì shì shéi?
options.text_en: Who will be the new postman?
options.feedback: Replacement logistics during an emotional moment.

tree.cultural_note: Rural postmen (乡村邮递员) in China have been celebrated as unsung connectors of communities. Walking the same route daily for decades, they become living archives of local life. The image of 陪路过四季 (keeping the road company through seasons) reflects a Chinese sensibility that daily rituals are not monotony but companionship with the world.

tree.setup: You're visiting a small village at noon. The streets are empty. An old man sits in front of a shop, the only person awake during the village's afternoon nap.
tree.setup_zh: 中午你来到一个小村庄。街上空空的。一位老人坐在一家小店前面，整个村庄午睡的时候只有他醒着。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 别奇怪，这个时间大家都在午睡呢。我们这里的规矩，中午十二点到两点，全村都安静下来。连狗都睡了。你要不要坐一会儿？反正也没地方去。
turns.text_pinyin: Bié qíguài, zhège shíjiān dàjiā dōu zài wǔshuì ne. Wǒmen zhèlǐ de guījǔ, zhōngwǔ shí'èr diǎn dào liǎng diǎn, quán cūn dōu ānjìng xiàlái. Lián gǒu dōu shuì le. Nǐ yào bú yào zuò yīhuìr? Fǎnzhèng yě méi dìfang qù.
turns.text_en: Don't be surprised — everyone's napping at this hour. The rule here: noon to two, the whole village goes quiet. Even the dogs sleep. Want to sit for a while? There's nowhere to go anyway.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the village's nap tradition.

options.text_zh: 连狗都睡了——太可爱了。整个村庄一起午睡，这种节奏真好。城市里中午大家都在工作，从来不停。我好像已经忘了午睡是什么感觉了。
options.text_pinyin: Lián gǒu dōu shuì le — tài kě'ài le. Zhěnggè cūnzhuāng yīqǐ wǔshuì, zhè zhǒng jiézòu zhēn hǎo. Chéngshì lǐ zhōngwǔ dàjiā dōu zài gōngzuò, cónglái bù tíng. Wǒ hǎoxiàng yǐjīng wàng le wǔshuì shì shénme gǎnjué le.
options.text_en: Even the dogs sleep — that's adorable. The whole village napping together, what a beautiful rhythm. In the city, everyone works through noon, never stopping. I think I've forgotten what a nap feels like.
options.feedback: Contrasts city relentlessness with village rhythm — napping as a lost art.

options.text_zh: 这个村子有多少人？
options.text_pinyin: Zhège cūnzi yǒu duōshǎo rén?
options.text_en: How many people in this village?
options.feedback: Census question during a scene of gentle stillness.

options.text_zh: 你为什么没有午睡？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ wèishéme méiyǒu wǔshuì?
options.text_en: Why aren't you napping?
options.feedback: Interrogates his wakefulness instead of enjoying the moment.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 城里人都忘了午睡，也忘了慢的意思。我们这里日出而作，日落而息，中午就休息。几百年了，一直是这样。不是因为懒，是因为我们跟着太阳走。太阳最热的时候，人就应该歇着。
turns.text_pinyin: Chéng lǐ rén dōu wàng le wǔshuì, yě wàng le màn de yìsi. Wǒmen zhèlǐ rì chū ér zuò, rì luò ér xī, zhōngwǔ jiù xiūxi. Jǐ bǎi nián le, yīzhí shì zhèyàng. Bú shì yīnwèi lǎn, shì yīnwèi wǒmen gēn zhe tàiyáng zǒu. Tàiyáng zuì rè de shíhou, rén jiù yīnggāi xiē zhe.
turns.text_en: City folks forgot napping, and forgot what 'slow' means. Here we work at sunrise, rest at sunset, and nap at noon. Hundreds of years, always like this. Not because we're lazy — because we follow the sun. When the sun is hottest, people should rest.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to following the sun's rhythm.

options.text_zh: 跟着太阳走——比跟着闹钟走有智慧多了。城市里我们按照工作表生活，你们按照自然生活。也许最健康的节奏不是人定的，是天定的。
options.text_pinyin: Gēn zhe tàiyáng zǒu — bǐ gēn zhe nàozhōng zǒu yǒu zhìhuì duō le. Chéngshì lǐ wǒmen ànzhào gōngzuò biǎo shēnghuó, nǐmen ànzhào zìrán shēnghuó. Yěxǔ zuì jiànkāng de jiézòu bú shì rén dìng de, shì tiān dìng de.
options.text_en: Following the sun — wiser than following an alarm clock. In the city we live by schedules, here you live by nature. Maybe the healthiest rhythm isn't set by people, but by the sky.
options.feedback: Natural rhythm over artificial schedule — the sky as the truest clock.

options.text_zh: 但是这样效率不是很低吗？
options.text_pinyin: Dànshì zhèyàng xiàolǜ bú shì hěn dī ma?
options.text_en: But isn't that inefficient?
options.feedback: Applies urban productivity logic to village wisdom.

options.text_zh: 年轻人也午睡吗？
options.text_pinyin: Niánqīng rén yě wǔshuì ma?
options.text_en: Do the young people nap too?
options.feedback: Demographic question during a moment about rhythm.

tree.cultural_note: The afternoon nap (午睡/午休) is a deeply rooted tradition in rural and even urban China. The phrase 日出而作，日落而息 (work at sunrise, rest at sunset) comes from an ancient folk song and represents an ideal of living in harmony with natural cycles. Many villages still observe collective noon quiet, a rhythm that predates clocks.

tree.setup: You're invited to make dumplings with your Chinese friend's grandmother. She rolls the wrappers with astonishing speed while chatting.
tree.setup_zh: 你被邀请和朋友的奶奶一起包饺子。她一边聊天一边飞快地擀饺子皮。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 来，你试试擀。不要怕，手放轻一点，慢慢来。我擀了六十年了，一开始也擀不好。我婆婆当年教我的时候说「饺子皮不怕丑，就怕没耐心」。
turns.text_pinyin: Lái, nǐ shìshi gǎn. Bú yào pà, shǒu fàng qīng yīdiǎn, mànman lái. Wǒ gǎn le liùshí nián le, yī kāishǐ yě gǎn bù hǎo. Wǒ pópo dāngnián jiāo wǒ de shíhou shuō 'jiǎozi pí bú pà chǒu, jiù pà méi nàixīn'.
turns.text_en: Come, try rolling. Don't be afraid — lighter hands, go slowly. I've been rolling for sixty years; I couldn't do it well at first either. My mother-in-law taught me and said, 'Dumpling wrappers don't mind being ugly — they only mind impatience.'

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her encouragement and her mother-in-law's saying.

options.text_zh: 「不怕丑，就怕没耐心」——这个说法太好了。不只是饺子皮，好像什么事情都是这样。做得不完美没关系，最重要的是愿意慢慢学。
options.text_pinyin: 'Bú pà chǒu, jiù pà méi nàixīn' — zhège shuōfǎ tài hǎo le. Bù zhǐ shì jiǎozi pí, hǎoxiàng shénme shìqing dōu shì zhèyàng. Zuò de bù wánměi méi guānxi, zuì zhòngyào de shì yuànyì mànman xué.
options.text_en: 'Not afraid of ugly, only afraid of impatience' — what a great saying. Not just for dumpling wrappers — it seems true for everything. It's okay to be imperfect, the important thing is willingness to learn slowly.
options.feedback: Extracts universal wisdom from a kitchen saying — patience over perfection.

options.text_zh: 我擀的好丑啊。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ gǎn de hǎo chǒu a.
options.text_en: Mine are so ugly.
options.feedback: Self-deprecation instead of engaging her wisdom.

options.text_zh: 用饺子机不是更快吗？
options.text_pinyin: Yòng jiǎozi jī bú shì gèng kuài ma?
options.text_en: Wouldn't a dumpling machine be faster?
options.feedback: Suggests a machine to a sixty-year veteran.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 哈哈，是的。我婆婆教了我很多，不只是包饺子。她说做人也一样——「不怕慢，就怕站」。只要你一直在学，一直在做，什么都能学会。你看，你擀的这个已经比刚才好多了。
turns.text_pinyin: Hāhā, shì de. Wǒ pópo jiāo le wǒ hěn duō, bù zhǐ shì bāo jiǎozi. Tā shuō zuòrén yě yīyàng — 'bú pà màn, jiù pà zhàn'. Zhǐyào nǐ yīzhí zài xué, yīzhí zài zuò, shénme dōu néng xué huì. Nǐ kàn, nǐ gǎn de zhège yǐjīng bǐ gāngcái hǎo duō le.
turns.text_en: Ha, yes. My mother-in-law taught me many things, not just dumpling-making. She said being a person is the same — 'Not afraid of slow, only afraid of standing still.' As long as you keep learning, keep doing, you can learn anything. Look, this one you rolled is already much better than before.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her encouragement and the wisdom passed through generations.

options.text_zh: 您婆婆的话从她传到您，今天又从您传到了我。包饺子不只是做饭——是一家人坐在一起，把爱和智慧像馅一样包进去，一代传一代。
options.text_pinyin: Nín pópo de huà cóng tā chuán dào nín, jīntiān yòu cóng nín chuán dào le wǒ. Bāo jiǎozi bù zhǐ shì zuòfàn — shì yī jiā rén zuò zài yīqǐ, bǎ ài hé zhìhuì xiàng xiàn yīyàng bāo jìnqù, yī dài chuán yī dài.
options.text_en: Your mother-in-law's words passed from her to you, and today from you to me. Making dumplings isn't just cooking — it's family sitting together, wrapping love and wisdom inside like filling, generation after generation.
options.feedback: Dumplings as vessels of wisdom — each generation wraps in their care.

options.text_zh: 什么馅的最好吃？
options.text_pinyin: Shénme xiàn de zuì hǎochī?
options.text_en: What filling tastes best?
options.feedback: A food preference question during an intergenerational moment.

options.text_zh: 您婆婆还在吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín pópo hái zài ma?
options.text_en: Is your mother-in-law still alive?
options.feedback: Could touch on grief, and misses the emotional flow.

tree.cultural_note: Making dumplings (包饺子) is one of China's most important family rituals, especially during Spring Festival. The round table, the shared labor, the passing of technique from mother-in-law to daughter-in-law — all carry deep emotional significance. The proverb 不怕慢就怕站 (not afraid of slow, only afraid of stopping) is kitchen wisdom that doubles as life philosophy.

tree.setup: You discover a 24-hour bookshop that stays open all night. At 1 AM, the night clerk is reading behind the counter. You're the only customer.
tree.setup_zh: 你发现了一家24小时营业的书店。凌晨一点，值夜班的店员在柜台后面看书。你是唯一的顾客。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 欢迎。晚上来书店的人不多，但总有一些。大多是睡不着的人，或者不想回家的人。书店就是这样——不管你几点来，灯都亮着。
turns.text_pinyin: Huānyíng. Wǎnshang lái shūdiàn de rén bù duō, dàn zǒng yǒu yīxiē. Dàduō shì shuì bù zháo de rén, huòzhě bù xiǎng huí jiā de rén. Shūdiàn jiù shì zhèyàng — bùguǎn nǐ jǐ diǎn lái, dēng dōu liàng zhe.
turns.text_en: Welcome. Not many come to the bookshop at night, but there are always some. Mostly people who can't sleep, or people who don't want to go home. That's what a bookshop is — no matter what time you come, the lights are on.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the idea of a bookshop that's always lit.

options.text_zh: 灯都亮着——这让我觉得很安心。凌晨一点的城市大部分都暗了，但是这里还亮着。好像在说「不管多晚，你都可以来」。
options.text_pinyin: Dēng dōu liàng zhe — zhè ràng wǒ juéde hěn ānxīn. Língchén yī diǎn de chéngshì dà bùfen dōu àn le, dànshì zhèlǐ hái liàng zhe. Hǎoxiàng zài shuō 'bùguǎn duō wǎn, nǐ dōu kěyǐ lái'.
options.text_en: The lights are always on — that makes me feel at ease. At 1 AM most of the city is dark, but this place is still lit. It's like it's saying, 'No matter how late, you can come.'
options.feedback: The bookshop as a beacon in the dark city — unconditional welcome.

options.text_zh: 晚上值班不累吗？
options.text_pinyin: Wǎnshang zhí bān bú lèi ma?
options.text_en: Isn't the night shift tiring?
options.feedback: Labor question during a poetic moment.

options.text_zh: 你们卖咖啡吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐmen mài kāfēi ma?
options.text_en: Do you sell coffee?
options.feedback: A menu question in a bookshop of the soul.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对，就是这个意思。有些人来了也不买书，就坐在角落里看一会儿，然后走了。我从来不赶他们。有时候一个人需要的不是一本书，而是一个亮着灯的地方可以待一会儿。
turns.text_pinyin: Duì, jiù shì zhège yìsi. Yǒuxiē rén lái le yě bù mǎi shū, jiù zuò zài jiǎoluò lǐ kàn yīhuìr, ránhòu zǒu le. Wǒ cónglái bù gǎn tāmen. Yǒu shíhou yī ge rén xūyào de bú shì yī běn shū, ér shì yī ge liàng zhe dēng de dìfang kěyǐ dāi yīhuìr.
turns.text_en: Exactly. Some people come and don't even buy a book — just sit in a corner for a while, then leave. I never ask them to go. Sometimes what a person needs isn't a book, but a lit place to sit for a while.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the bookshop as a sanctuary.

options.text_zh: 一个亮着灯的地方——比任何书都重要。凌晨大多数地方都关了，这里还开着。您不只是卖书，是在给人一个可以安心待着的地方。
options.text_pinyin: Yī ge liàng zhe dēng de dìfang — bǐ rènhé shū dōu zhòngyào. Língchén dàduōshù dìfang dōu guān le, zhèlǐ hái kāi zhe. Nín bù zhǐ shì mài shū, shì zài gěi rén yī ge kěyǐ ānxīn dāi zhe de dìfang.
options.text_en: A lit place — more important than any book. Most places close in the early hours, but this one stays open. You're not selling books — you're giving people a place to feel at peace.
options.feedback: The shop's real product isn't books — it's sanctuary.

options.text_zh: 但是他们不买书的话，你们怎么赚钱？
options.text_pinyin: Dànshì tāmen bù mǎi shū dehuà, nǐmen zěnme zhuàn qián?
options.text_en: But if they don't buy books, how do you make money?
options.feedback: Business model question during a moment about human shelter.

options.text_zh: 你在看什么书？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zài kàn shénme shū?
options.text_en: What book are you reading?
options.feedback: Redirects to his personal reading during a deeper exchange.

tree.cultural_note: 24-hour bookshops (24小时书店) have become cultural landmarks in Chinese cities like Beijing and Hangzhou. They explicitly welcome anyone, even those who never buy. The concept of a place that 灯都亮着 (keeps the lights on) regardless of commercial return reflects a belief that some spaces exist for human need, not profit.

tree.setup: You're climbing ancient stone steps up a mountain path. A monk passes you going down, carrying a basket of vegetables.
tree.setup_zh: 你在爬一条山路上的古老石阶。一位和尚提着一篮蔬菜从山上走下来，和你擦肩而过。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 慢慢走，不着急。这些石阶有三百多年了，每一级都被很多人踩过。你看，中间都凹下去了——那是几百年的脚印。
turns.text_pinyin: Mànman zǒu, bù zháojí. Zhèxiē shí jiē yǒu sānbǎi duō nián le, měi yī jí dōu bèi hěn duō rén cǎi guò. Nǐ kàn, zhōngjiān dōu āo xiàqù le — nà shì jǐ bǎi nián de jiǎoyìn.
turns.text_en: Walk slowly, no rush. These stone steps are over three hundred years old — each one has been stepped on by many people. Look, the centers are worn concave — those are hundreds of years of footprints.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the worn stone steps.

options.text_zh: 几百年的脚印——我从没这样看过石阶。每个凹下去的地方都是无数人走过的证据。我现在这一步也在加深这个印记。很奇妙。
options.text_pinyin: Jǐ bǎi nián de jiǎoyìn — wǒ cóng méi zhèyàng kàn guò shí jiē. Měi ge āo xiàqù de dìfang dōu shì wúshù rén zǒu guò de zhèngjù. Wǒ xiànzài zhè yī bù yě zài jiāshēn zhège yìnjì. Hěn qímiào.
options.text_en: Hundreds of years of footprints — I've never looked at stone steps this way. Every worn spot is evidence of countless people passing. My step right now deepens that mark. Magical.
options.feedback: Each step adds to centuries of passage — connecting to all who walked before.

options.text_zh: 山上有什么？
options.text_pinyin: Shān shàng yǒu shénme?
options.text_en: What's at the top?
options.feedback: Destination question during a reflection on the path itself.

options.text_zh: 这些台阶应该修一修了。
options.text_pinyin: Zhèxiē táijiē yīnggāi xiū yī xiū le.
options.text_en: These steps should be repaired.
options.feedback: Suggests erasing the very history he's pointing out.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 是的，你的脚印也在里面了。有时候我走这条路的时候会想——以前走这里的人看到的风景和我看到的一样吗？树可能不同了，但山没变，天没变，风也没变。
turns.text_pinyin: Shì de, nǐ de jiǎoyìn yě zài lǐmiàn le. Yǒu shíhou wǒ zǒu zhè tiáo lù de shíhou huì xiǎng — yǐqián zǒu zhèlǐ de rén kàn dào de fēngjǐng hé wǒ kàn dào de yīyàng ma? Shù kěnéng bùtóng le, dàn shān méi biàn, tiān méi biàn, fēng yě méi biàn.
turns.text_en: Yes, your footprint is in there now too. Sometimes when I walk this path I wonder — did the people who walked here before see the same scenery I see? The trees might be different, but the mountain hasn't changed, the sky hasn't changed, the wind hasn't changed.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to what changes and what doesn't.

options.text_zh: 山、天、风——这些是永恒的。人来了又走了，但每个走过这条路的人都和同一座山分享过一个瞬间。这就是这条路的意义——连接不同时代的人。
options.text_pinyin: Shān, tiān, fēng — zhèxiē shì yǒnghéng de. Rén lái le yòu zǒu le, dàn měi ge zǒu guò zhè tiáo lù de rén dōu hé tóng yī zuò shān fēnxiǎng guò yī ge shùnjiān. Zhè jiù shì zhè tiáo lù de yìyì — liánjiē bùtóng shídài de rén.
options.text_en: Mountain, sky, wind — these are eternal. People come and go, but everyone who walked this path shared a moment with the same mountain. That's this path's meaning — connecting people across eras.
options.feedback: The path as a bridge across time — centuries of shared moments.

options.text_zh: 您每天都走这条路吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín měi tiān dōu zǒu zhè tiáo lù ma?
options.text_en: Do you walk this path every day?
options.feedback: A routine question that doesn't match the depth.

options.text_zh: 这条路通向哪个寺庙？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè tiáo lù tōng xiàng nǎge sìmiào?
options.text_en: Which temple does this path lead to?
options.feedback: Destination logistics during a timeless reflection.

tree.cultural_note: Ancient mountain paths (石阶) in China bear the physical imprint of centuries of pilgrims, monks, and travelers. The concave wear on each step is considered beautiful, not deteriorated — proof of connection across time. Monks often walk these paths daily with a contemplative awareness that transforms routine into meditation.

tree.setup: At a tea house, the owner shows you a small clay teapot with a deep, glossy patina. She handles it with obvious reverence.
tree.setup_zh: 在一间茶馆里，老板给你看一把有着深色光泽的小紫砂壶。她拿着它的时候明显很敬重。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这把壶是我外婆留下来的，已经用了四十多年了。你看这个颜色——新的紫砂壶不是这样的。这种光泽是茶养出来的，每天泡茶，一点一点变的。我外婆说这叫「养壶」——不是你在用壶，是你在养它。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè bǎ hú shì wǒ wàipó liú xiàlái de, yǐjīng yòng le sìshí duō nián le. Nǐ kàn zhège yánsè — xīn de zǐshā hú bú shì zhèyàng de. Zhè zhǒng guāngzé shì chá yǎng chūlái de, měi tiān pào chá, yīdiǎn yīdiǎn biàn de. Wǒ wàipó shuō zhè jiào 'yǎng hú' — bú shì nǐ zài yòng hú, shì nǐ zài yǎng tā.
turns.text_en: This teapot was left to me by my grandmother — it's been used for over forty years. See this color — new Yixing teapots don't look like this. This sheen was nurtured by tea, brewed daily, changing bit by bit. My grandmother said it's called 'nurturing the pot' — you're not using the pot, you're raising it.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the idea of nurturing a teapot.

options.text_zh: 养壶——多美的一个词。不是「用」，是「养」。就像养一个有生命的东西，用耐心和时间。四十年的茶泡进去，这把壶已经有了灵魂。
options.text_pinyin: 'Yǎng hú' — duō měi de yī ge cí. Bú shì 'yòng', shì 'yǎng'. Jiù xiàng yǎng yī ge yǒu shēngmìng de dōngxi, yòng nàixīn hé shíjiān. Sìshí nián de chá pào jìnqù, zhè bǎ hú yǐjīng yǒu le línghún.
options.text_en: 'Nurturing the pot' — what a beautiful phrase. Not 'using,' but 'nurturing.' Like raising a living thing with patience and time. Forty years of tea steeped into it — this pot has a soul now.
options.feedback: The pot as a living being — raised, not used.

options.text_zh: 四十年太久了，壶里面肯定不太卫生了吧？也许应该换一把新的，这样泡茶更干净。
options.text_pinyin: Sìshí nián tài jiǔ le, hú lǐmiàn kěndìng bú tài wèishēng le ba? Yěxǔ yīnggāi huàn yī bǎ xīn de, zhèyàng pào chá gèng gānjìng.
options.text_en: Forty years is a long time — the inside can't be very hygienic anymore, right? Maybe you should switch to a new one so the tea is cleaner.
options.feedback: She cradles the pot protectively: 'You think those forty years are dirt? That's my grandmother's whole life in there.'

options.text_zh: 紫砂壶和普通茶壶有什么区别？
options.text_pinyin: Zǐshā hú hé pǔtōng cháhú yǒu shénme qūbié?
options.text_en: What's the difference between Yixing clay and regular teapots?
options.feedback: A technical comparison during an emotional sharing.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 可不是嘛。它有灵魂了。最神奇的是——现在就算不放茶叶，只倒热水进去，出来的水都有淡淡的茶味。四十年的茶已经渗进了壶里。我外婆的习惯、她每天泡茶的时间、她喝的那种茶——都在这把壶里了。
turns.text_pinyin: Kě bú shì ma. , tā yǒu línghún le. Zuì shénqí de shì — xiànzài jiùsuàn bù fàng cháyè, zhǐ dào rè shuǐ jìnqù, chūlái de shuǐ dōu yǒu dàndàn de cháwèi. Sìshí nián de chá yǐjīng shèn jìn le hú lǐ. Wǒ wàipó de xíguàn, tā měi tiān pào chá de shíjiān, tā hē de nà zhǒng chá — dōu zài zhè bǎ hú lǐ le.
turns.text_en: Isn't that the truth. it has a soul. The most magical thing is — now even without tea leaves, just pouring hot water in, the water comes out with a faint tea flavor. Forty years of tea have seeped into the clay. My grandmother's habits, the time she brewed every day, the tea she drank — they're all in this pot.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the pot holding memories of her grandmother.

options.text_zh: 这把壶不只是茶具——是您外婆的日记。每天的茶留下一点痕迹，四十年变成了现在这样。每次用它泡茶，其实是在跟外婆一起喝。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè bǎ hú bù zhǐ shì chájù — shì nín wàipó de rìjì. Měi tiān de chá liú xià yīdiǎn hénjì, sìshí nián biàn chéng le xiànzài zhèyàng. Měi cì yòng tā pào chá, qíshí shì zài gēn wàipó yīqǐ hē.
options.text_en: This pot isn't just a tea vessel — it's your grandmother's diary. Each day's tea left a trace, forty years became this. Every time you brew with it, you're having tea with her.
options.feedback: The pot as a daily diary — each cup is a reunion with grandmother.

options.text_zh: 您可以用这把壶泡什么茶？
options.text_pinyin: Nín kěyǐ yòng zhè bǎ hú pào shénme chá?
options.text_en: What tea can you brew in this pot?
options.feedback: A practical question during a moment about memory and love.

options.text_zh: 您有其他的紫砂壶吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín yǒu qítā de zǐshā hú ma?
options.text_en: Do you have other Yixing teapots?
options.feedback: Asks about her collection when this one holds all the meaning.

tree.cultural_note: Yixing clay teapots (紫砂壶) are treasured precisely because they absorb tea over decades. The concept of 养壶 (nurturing the pot) is one of China's most beautiful examples of patient care transforming an object. A well-nurtured pot becomes a taste archive of its owner's life — their preferences, their rituals, their years.

tree.setup: You go up to the rooftop to hang laundry and find your elderly neighbor already there, watching the clouds while her sheets dry in the wind.
tree.setup_zh: 你到天台上去晒衣服，发现楼上的老奶奶已经在那里了，一边看云一边等被单晒干。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 上来啦？今天的风正好，衣服很快就能干。你看那些云，走得好快。我小时候觉得云是活的，会走路。现在还是这样觉得。
turns.text_pinyin: Shàng lái la? Jīntiān de fēng zhènghǎo, yīfu hěn kuài jiù néng gān. Nǐ kàn nàxiē yún, zǒu de hǎo kuài. Wǒ xiǎo shíhou juéde yún shì huó de, huì zǒulù. Xiànzài háishi zhèyàng juéde.
turns.text_en: You're up! The wind is perfect today — clothes will dry quickly. Look at those clouds, moving so fast. As a child I thought clouds were alive, that they walked. I still think so.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her view of clouds as alive.

options.text_zh: 云是活的——我喜欢这个想法。有自己的方向和速度，每朵云都不会再来第二次。也许云真的是活的，只是大人不愿意这样想了。
options.text_pinyin: Yún shì huó de — wǒ xǐhuan zhège xiǎngfǎ. Yǒu zìjǐ de fāngxiàng hé sùdù, měi duǒ yún dōu bú huì zài lái dì èr cì. Yěxǔ yún zhēn de shì huó de, zhǐ shì dàrén bú yuànyì zhèyàng xiǎng le.
options.text_en: Clouds are alive — I love that. Their own direction and speed, each cloud never comes again. Maybe clouds really are alive — adults just don't want to think that way.
options.feedback: Each cloud unique and unrepeatable — childlike seeing as deeper truth.

options.text_zh: 今天风大，衣服可能会被吹跑。
options.text_pinyin: Jīntiān fēng dà, yīfu kěnéng huì bèi chuī pǎo.
options.text_en: It's windy today, the clothes might blow away.
options.feedback: A practical worry during a whimsical moment.

options.text_zh: 下午会下雨吗？
options.text_pinyin: Xiàwǔ huì xià yǔ ma?
options.text_en: Will it rain this afternoon?
options.feedback: Weather prediction instead of cloud-gazing.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 哈哈，你跟我想的一样。我老伴以前也喜欢来天台看云。他说「云是最自由的，没有人能管它去哪里」。我觉得天台是这栋楼最好的地方——离天最近。
turns.text_pinyin: Hāhā, nǐ gēn wǒ xiǎng de yīyàng. Wǒ lǎobàn yǐqián yě xǐhuan lái tiāntái kàn yún. Tā shuō 'yún shì zuì zìyóu de, méiyǒu rén néng guǎn tā qù nǎlǐ'. Wǒ juéde tiāntái shì zhè dòng lóu zuì hǎo de dìfang — lí tiān zuì jìn.
turns.text_en: Haha, you think just like me. My late husband also loved coming to the roof to watch clouds. He said, 'Clouds are the freest — nobody can tell them where to go.' I think the rooftop is the best place in this building — closest to the sky.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her memory of watching clouds together.

options.text_zh: 离天最近——也许也离他最近。每次您来天台看云，也许他也在某朵云里看着您。他说得对，云是自由的。也许那是最好的方式。
options.text_pinyin: Lí tiān zuì jìn — yěxǔ yě lí tā zuì jìn. Měi cì nín lái tiāntái kàn yún, yěxǔ tā yě zài mǒu duǒ yún lǐ kàn zhe nín. Tā shuō de duì, yún shì zìyóu de. Yěxǔ nà shì zuì hǎo de fāngshì.
options.text_en: Closest to the sky — and maybe closest to him. Every time you watch clouds from the roof, maybe he's watching from one. He was right, clouds are free. Maybe that's the best way.
options.feedback: Transforms the rooftop into a meeting place between here and elsewhere — tender and gentle.

options.text_zh: 您经常来天台吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín jīngcháng lái tiāntái ma?
options.text_en: Do you come to the roof often?
options.feedback: A frequency question during a moment about love and loss.

options.text_zh: 天台可以种花。
options.text_pinyin: Tiāntái kěyǐ zhòng huā.
options.text_en: You could grow flowers on the roof.
options.feedback: A gardening suggestion during an intimate memory.

tree.cultural_note: Rooftops (天台) in Chinese apartment buildings are shared spaces for laundry but also serve as open-air sanctuaries. For older residents especially, the rooftop is where daily chores meet contemplation. Watching clouds together is a quiet but deeply intimate activity, and the phrase 离天最近 takes on layered meaning when the other person has passed away.

tree.setup: You visit a village market and find an old woman selling small paper bags of seeds. She has dozens of varieties, each labeled in careful handwriting.
tree.setup_zh: 你在村子的集市上看到一位老人在卖一小袋一小袋的种子。她有几十种，每种上面都用工整的字写着标签。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这些种子都是我自己留的，不是买的。每年收成以后，我把最好的果实留下来取种子。这个习惯是我妈妈教我的，她的妈妈教她的。一代一代留下来的种子，比种子公司的好。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhèxiē zhǒngzi dōu shì wǒ zìjǐ liú de, bú shì mǎi de. Měi nián shōuchéng yǐhòu, wǒ bǎ zuì hǎo de guǒshí liú xiàlái qǔ zhǒngzi. Zhège xíguàn shì wǒ māma jiāo wǒ de, tā de māma jiāo tā de. Yī dài yī dài liú xiàlái de zhǒngzi, bǐ zhǒngzi gōngsī de hǎo.
turns.text_en: These seeds are all saved by me, not store-bought. Each year after harvest, I save seeds from the best fruit. My mother taught me this habit, and her mother taught her. Seeds passed down through generations are better than seed company ones.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the tradition of seed-saving.

options.text_zh: 一代一代留下来——这些不只是种子，是您家里几代人的记忆。每一颗种子都连着您妈妈、您外婆。种下去的时候，是在种她们的选择和智慧。
options.text_pinyin: Yī dài yī dài liú xiàlái — zhèxiē bù zhǐ shì zhǒngzi, shì nín jiālǐ jǐ dài rén de jìyì. Měi yī kē zhǒngzi dōu lián zhe nín māma, nín wàipó. Zhǒng xiàqù de shíhou, shì zài zhǒng tāmen de xuǎnzé hé zhìhuì.
options.text_en: Passed down generation by generation — these aren't just seeds, they're your family's memories across generations. Each seed is connected to your mother, your grandmother. When you plant them, you're planting their choices and wisdom.
options.feedback: Seeds as memory capsules — each one carries ancestral wisdom.

options.text_zh: 种子公司的品种不好吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhǒngzi gōngsī de pǐnzhǒng bù hǎo ma?
options.text_en: Are seed company varieties not good?
options.feedback: Debates commercial vs. saved seeds instead of honoring the tradition.

options.text_zh: 不过现在种子公司的品种产量更高，抗病能力也更强。科学培育的种子应该比老品种更好吧？
options.text_pinyin: Búguò xiànzài zhǒngzi gōngsī de pǐnzhǒng chǎnliàng gèng gāo, kàng bìng nénglì yě gèng qiáng. Kēxué péiyù de zhǒngzi yīnggāi bǐ lǎo pǐnzhǒng gèng hǎo ba?
options.text_en: But seed company varieties have higher yields and better disease resistance. Scientifically bred seeds should be better than old varieties, right?
options.feedback: She holds up a small paper bag: 'Higher yield? My grandmother's tomatoes made people close their eyes when they tasted them. Can your science do that?'

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你说得好。我最怕的就是这些种子断了。村里的年轻人都去城里了，没人种地了。如果我不留种子，这些品种就没有了——不是去超市买不到的那种「没有」，是从世界上消失了的「没有」。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ shuō de hǎo. Wǒ zuì pà de jiù shì zhèxiē zhǒngzi duàn le. Cūn lǐ de niánqīng rén dōu qù chéng lǐ le, méi rén zhòng dì le. Rúguǒ wǒ bù liú zhǒngzi, zhèxiē pǐnzhǒng jiù méiyǒu le — bú shì qù chāoshì mǎi bú dào de nà zhǒng 'méiyǒu', shì cóng shìjiè shàng xiāoshī le de 'méiyǒu'.
turns.text_en: Well said. What I fear most is these seeds being lost. The village young people have all gone to the city, nobody farms anymore. If I don't save seeds, these varieties will be gone — not 'gone' as in you can't find them at the supermarket, but 'gone' as in disappeared from the world.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the permanent loss she describes.

options.text_zh: 从世界上消失——这种「没有」不能接受。您不只是种菜，是在保护记忆和基因。每留一颗种子，就是在对未来说「这值得继续存在」。
options.text_pinyin: Cóng shìjiè shàng xiāoshī — zhè zhǒng 'méiyǒu' bù néng jiēshòu. Nín bù zhǐ shì zhòng cài, shì zài bǎohù jìyì hé jīyīn. Měi liú yī kē zhǒngzi, jiù shì zài duì wèilái shuō 'zhè zhíde jìxù cúnzài'.
options.text_en: Disappeared from the world — that kind of 'gone' is unacceptable. You're not just gardening, you're protecting memory and genes. Every seed saved says to the future: 'This deserves to continue.'
options.feedback: Seed-saving as a declaration to the future — existence is worth preserving.

options.text_zh: 有没有种子银行可以保存？
options.text_pinyin: Yǒu méiyǒu zhǒngzi yínháng kěyǐ bǎocún?
options.text_en: Is there a seed bank that could preserve them?
options.feedback: Institutional solution when she's talking about personal guardianship.

options.text_zh: 年轻人为什么不回来种地？
options.text_pinyin: Niánqīng rén wèishéme bù huílái zhòng dì?
options.text_en: Why don't the young people come back to farm?
options.feedback: Sociological question during an emotional revelation.

tree.cultural_note: Seed-saving (留种) is one of humanity's oldest agricultural practices and remains alive in Chinese villages. As younger generations leave for cities, the continuity of local seed varieties is genuinely threatened. Each lost variety represents irreversible genetic and cultural loss. Village seed keepers are often elderly women who see themselves as guardians of living heritage.

tree.setup: You wake up early at a countryside guesthouse and step outside into thick morning fog. The innkeeper is already sitting on the porch with tea.
tree.setup_zh: 你在一家乡村民宿早起，走到外面发现浓浓的晨雾。民宿老板已经坐在门廊上喝茶了。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 早。雾很大吧？不用怕，太阳出来就散了。我最喜欢早上有雾的时候——什么都看不清，世界好像只剩下声音了。你听，有鸟叫、有水声，但是看不到在哪里。
turns.text_pinyin: Zǎo. Wù hěn dà ba? Bú yòng pà, tàiyáng chūlái jiù sàn le. Wǒ zuì xǐhuan zǎoshang yǒu wù de shíhou — shénme dōu kàn bù qīng, shìjiè hǎoxiàng zhǐ shèng xià shēngyīn le. Nǐ tīng, yǒu niǎo jiào, yǒu shuǐ shēng, dànshì kàn bú dào zài nǎlǐ.
turns.text_en: Morning. Thick fog, right? Don't worry, it'll clear when the sun comes out. I love mornings with fog — you can't see anything clearly, the world seems reduced to just sound. Listen — birdsong, water, but you can't see where they are.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the fog and its effect on perception.

options.text_zh: 真的，平时用眼睛看的世界，现在只能用耳朵听。好像雾把视觉关掉了，让我们重新用耳朵感受。看不见反而更丰富了。
options.text_pinyin: Zhēn de, píngshí yòng yǎnjing kàn de shìjiè, xiànzài zhǐ néng yòng ěrduo tīng. Hǎoxiàng wù bǎ shìjué guān diào le, ràng wǒmen chóngxīn yòng ěrduo gǎnshòu. Kàn bú jiàn fǎn'ér gèng fēngfù le.
options.text_en: Really — a world I normally see, now I can only hear. The fog turned off sight, making us relearn with our ears. Not seeing actually makes things richer.
options.feedback: Fog as a teacher — removing one sense to awaken another.

options.text_zh: 几点太阳会出来？
options.text_pinyin: Jǐ diǎn tàiyáng huì chūlái?
options.text_en: What time will the sun come out?
options.feedback: Wants the fog gone instead of experiencing it.

options.text_zh: 这里经常有雾吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhèlǐ jīngcháng yǒu wù ma?
options.text_en: Is fog common here?
options.feedback: A weather pattern question during a sensory moment.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 看不见反而更丰富——说得好。有雾的时候，远处不存在了。你只能感受身边的东西——脚下的土地，手里的茶杯，空气里的湿润。雾让世界变小了，但变小了反而变深了。
turns.text_pinyin: Kàn bú jiàn fǎn'ér gèng fēngfù — shuō de hǎo. Yǒu wù de shíhou, yuǎnchù bù cúnzài le. Nǐ zhǐ néng gǎnshòu shēnbiān de dōngxi — jiǎo xià de tǔdì, shǒu lǐ de chábēi, kōngqì lǐ de shīrùn. Wù ràng shìjiè biàn xiǎo le, dàn biàn xiǎo le fǎn'ér biàn shēn le.
turns.text_en: Not seeing makes things richer — well said. In fog, the distance stops existing. You can only feel what's near — the ground beneath your feet, the teacup in your hands, the moisture in the air. Fog makes the world smaller, but smaller becomes deeper.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the idea of a smaller world being deeper.

options.text_zh: 变小了反而变深了——也许是我最需要学的东西。平时总想看更远、想更多。但这个早晨的雾在教我：只关注脚下和手里的东西，就够了。
options.text_pinyin: Biàn xiǎo le fǎn'ér biàn shēn le — yěxǔ shì wǒ zuì xūyào xué de dōngxi. Píngshí zǒng xiǎng kàn gèng yuǎn, xiǎng gèng duō. Dàn zhège zǎochén de wù zài jiāo wǒ: zhǐ guānzhù jiǎo xià hé shǒu lǐ de dōngxi, jiù gòu le.
options.text_en: Smaller becomes deeper — maybe that's what I most need to learn. Always trying to see farther, think more. But this morning's fog teaches me: just focus on what's at your feet and in your hands. That's enough.
options.feedback: The fog as life lesson — narrowing scope deepens experience.

options.text_zh: 我应该拍个照片。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yīnggāi pāi ge zhàopiàn.
options.text_en: I should take a photo.
options.feedback: Reaches for a camera during a moment about pure presence.

options.text_zh: 雾天开车很危险。
options.text_pinyin: Wù tiān kāi chē hěn wēixiǎn.
options.text_en: Driving in fog is dangerous.
options.feedback: A safety warning in a contemplative moment.

tree.cultural_note: Morning fog (晨雾) in the Chinese countryside is a beloved natural phenomenon, appearing in countless landscape paintings. The aesthetic concept of 留白 (leaving white space) in Chinese art parallels the fog's effect: what is hidden enhances what is present. The innkeeper's insight that a smaller world is a deeper world echoes Daoist principles of reduction leading to fullness.

tree.setup: Near the post office, you see an elderly man sitting at a small table with pen and paper. A sign reads: 'Letters written on your behalf.' An older woman has just finished dictating a letter to him.
tree.setup_zh: 邮局旁边，你看到一位老人坐在一张小桌子前，桌上有纸和笔。一个牌子写着「代写书信」。一位老太太刚给他口述完一封信。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你好奇？这位阿姨不识字，但她想给在北京的儿子写封信。我替她写。你知道吗，现在能写信的人越来越少了，会写字但不会表达的更多。写信不只是写字——要把别人心里的话变成纸上的字。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ hàoqí? Zhè wèi āyí bú shí zì, dàn tā xiǎng gěi zài Běijīng de érzi xiě fēng xìn. Wǒ tì tā xiě. Nǐ zhīdào ma, xiànzài néng xiě xìn de rén yuè lái yuè shǎo le, huì xiě zì dàn bú huì biǎodá de gèng duō. Xiě xìn bù zhǐ shì xiě zì — yào bǎ biéren xīnlǐ de huà biàn chéng zhǐ shàng de zì.
turns.text_en: Curious? This lady can't read or write, but she wants to send a letter to her son in Beijing. I write for her. You know, fewer and fewer people write letters now — and many who can write characters can't express feelings. Writing letters isn't just writing — it's turning someone's heart into words on paper.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his role as a letter writer.

options.text_zh: 把心里的话变成纸上的字——这工作太有意义了。您不只是写字，是在帮人传递表达不了的感情。这种信比任何微信消息都珍贵。
options.text_pinyin: Bǎ xīnlǐ de huà biàn chéng zhǐ shàng de zì — zhè gōngzuò tài yǒu yìyì le. Nín bù zhǐ shì xiě zì, shì zài bāng rén chuándì biǎodá bù liǎo de gǎnqíng. Zhè zhǒng xìn bǐ rènhé Wēixìn xiāoxi dōu zhēnguì.
options.text_en: Turning heart into words on paper — such meaningful work. You're not just writing, you're helping people convey feelings they can't express. These letters are more precious than any WeChat message.
options.feedback: Sees the letter writer as an emotional translator — heart to paper.

options.text_zh: 为什么她不打电话？
options.text_pinyin: Wèishéme tā bù dǎ diànhuà?
options.text_en: Why doesn't she just call?
options.feedback: Suggests a replacement that misses why she chose a letter.

options.text_zh: 但是现在可以用语音消息啊，她直接给儿子发一段语音就好了，不用写信那么麻烦。
options.text_pinyin: Dànshì xiànzài kěyǐ yòng yǔyīn xiāoxi a, tā zhíjiē gěi érzi fā yī duàn yǔyīn jiù hǎo le, bú yòng xiě xìn nàme máfan.
options.text_en: But she could just send a voice message now — just record one directly for her son. No need for all the trouble of writing a letter.
options.feedback: He sets down his pen: 'A voice message disappears after you hear it. A letter waits in a drawer until you need it again.'

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 比微信珍贵——你说到了点子上。刚才那位阿姨说「告诉他我很好，让他不要担心，冬天要穿厚一点」。就这三句话。但是你知道吗，她说这三句话的时候哭了两次。微信打字打不出眼泪，但是信上的字，是带着眼泪写的。
turns.text_pinyin: Bǐ Wēixìn zhēnguì — nǐ shuō dào le diǎnzi shàng. Gāngcái nà wèi āyí shuō 'gàosu tā wǒ hěn hǎo, ràng tā bú yào dānxīn, dōngtiān yào chuān hòu yīdiǎn'. Jiù zhè sān jù huà. Dànshì nǐ zhīdào ma, tā shuō zhè sān jù huà de shíhou kū le liǎng cì. Wēixìn dǎ zì dǎ bù chū yǎnlèi, dànshì xìn shàng de zì, shì dài zhe yǎnlèi xiě de.
turns.text_en: More precious than WeChat — you hit the point. That lady just said, 'Tell him I'm fine, tell him not to worry, dress warmly in winter.' Just those three sentences. But you know, she cried twice saying them. WeChat typing can't convey tears, but the words in a letter are written with tears.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the tears behind three simple sentences.

options.text_zh: 三句话，哭了两次。每句后面都是说不出口的想念。「我很好」是「别担心」，「穿厚一点」是「我没法在你身边」。您写下的不是字，是藏在简单话里的爱。
options.text_pinyin: Sān jù huà, kū le liǎng cì. Měi jù hòumiàn dōu shì shuō bù chūkǒu de xiǎngniàn. 'Wǒ hěn hǎo' shì 'bié dānxīn', 'chuān hòu yīdiǎn' shì 'wǒ méi fǎ zài nǐ shēnbiān'. Nín xiě xià de bú shì zì, shì cáng zài jiǎndān huà lǐ de ài.
options.text_en: Three sentences, cried twice. Behind each is unspoken longing. 'I'm fine' means 'don't worry.' 'Dress warmly' means 'I can't be by your side.' What you wrote isn't words — it's love hidden in simple phrases.
options.feedback: Decodes the mother's love — every simple sentence carries an ocean of feeling.

options.text_zh: 她儿子多久回来一次？
options.text_pinyin: Tā érzi duō jiǔ huílái yī cì?
options.text_en: How often does her son come home?
options.feedback: Investigates the son's schedule during a moment about a mother's love.

options.text_zh: 现在还有人收信吗？
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài hái yǒu rén shōu xìn ma?
options.text_en: Does anyone still receive letters?
options.feedback: Questions the medium's viability during an emotional peak.

tree.cultural_note: Professional letter writers (代笔人) once served an essential role in Chinese communities where illiteracy was common. Though increasingly rare, they still exist in some smaller cities and villages. The mother's three simple sentences — I'm fine, don't worry, dress warmly — are among the most universal expressions of Chinese parental love, where care is expressed through restraint and practical concern.

tree.setup: You notice a handmade wind chime hanging outside a small shop. The owner, a young woman, sees you listening to it.
tree.setup_zh: 你注意到一家小店外面挂着一个手工风铃。店主，一个年轻女人，看到你在听风铃的声音。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你在听风铃吗？这是我奶奶做的。她说风铃是「看得见的风」——平时风是看不见的，但是通过风铃，你就能看到风在那里，还能听到它说话。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ zài tīng fēnglíng ma? Zhè shì wǒ nǎinai zuò de. Tā shuō fēnglíng shì 'kàn de jiàn de fēng' — píngshí fēng shì kàn bú jiàn de, dànshì tōngguò fēnglíng, nǐ jiù néng kàn dào fēng zài nàlǐ, hái néng tīng dào tā shuōhuà.
turns.text_en: Listening to the wind chime? My grandmother made it. She said wind chimes are 'visible wind' — normally wind is invisible, but through a wind chime, you can see where the wind is, and even hear it speak.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the idea of wind chimes making wind visible.

options.text_zh: 「看得见的风」——这个说法太诗意了。风铃让无形的变成有形的，让安静的变成有声音的。您奶奶一定是一个很有智慧的人。
options.text_pinyin: 'Kàn de jiàn de fēng' — zhège shuōfǎ tài shīyì le. Fēnglíng ràng wúxíng de biàn chéng yǒuxíng de, ràng ānjìng de biàn chéng yǒu shēngyīn de. Nín nǎinai yīdìng shì yī ge hěn yǒu zhìhuì de rén.
options.text_en: 'Visible wind' — what a poetic phrase. Wind chimes make the formless visible, the silent audible. Your grandmother must have been a very wise person.
options.feedback: Honors the grandmother's poetic wisdom — making the invisible real.

options.text_zh: 这个风铃卖吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhège fēnglíng mài ma?
options.text_en: Is this wind chime for sale?
options.feedback: Tries to buy a family heirloom.

options.text_zh: 什么材料做的？
options.text_pinyin: Shénme cáiliào zuò de?
options.text_en: What material is it made of?
options.feedback: Asks about materials during a moment about meaning.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 她是。她还说过「如果你觉得生活太安静了，就挂一个风铃。它会告诉你，风一直都在，只是你没注意」。我奶奶去世以后，我把这个风铃挂在店门口。每次风吹的时候，我就觉得她在跟我说话。
turns.text_pinyin: Tā shì. Tā hái shuō guò 'rúguǒ nǐ juéde shēnghuó tài ānjìng le, jiù guà yī ge fēnglíng. Tā huì gàosu nǐ, fēng yīzhí dōu zài, zhǐ shì nǐ méi zhùyì'. Wǒ nǎinai qùshì yǐhòu, wǒ bǎ zhège fēnglíng guà zài diàn ménkǒu. Měi cì fēng chuī de shíhou, wǒ jiù juéde tā zài gēn wǒ shuōhuà.
turns.text_en: She was. She also said, 'If life feels too quiet, hang a wind chime. It'll tell you the wind has always been there, you just weren't paying attention.' After my grandmother passed, I hung this chime at the shop door. Every time the wind blows, I feel like she's talking to me.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the wind chime as her grandmother's voice.

options.text_zh: 她不在了，但风还在，风铃还在，她的声音就还在。也许她做风铃就是为了这个——留一个声音，让风替她继续说。
options.text_pinyin: Tā bú zài le, dàn fēng hái zài, fēnglíng hái zài, tā de shēngyīn jiù hái zài. Yěxǔ tā zuò fēnglíng jiù shì wèile zhège — liú yī ge shēngyīn, ràng fēng tì tā jìxù shuō.
options.text_en: She's gone, but the wind is still here, the chime is still here, so her voice is still here. Maybe that's why she made it — leaving a sound for the wind to keep speaking for her.
options.feedback: The wind chime as a voice that outlives its maker — carried by the wind forever.

options.text_zh: 你奶奶是什么时候去世的？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ nǎinai shì shénme shíhou qùshì de?
options.text_en: When did your grandmother pass away?
options.feedback: Pries into timing during a moment about enduring presence.

options.text_zh: 风铃声音会不会吵到邻居？
options.text_pinyin: Fēnglíng shēngyīn huì bú huì chǎo dào línjū?
options.text_en: Does the wind chime bother the neighbors?
options.feedback: A noise complaint during an intimate remembrance.

tree.cultural_note: Wind chimes (风铃) in Chinese culture serve both decorative and spiritual purposes. The idea that wind itself is always present but unnoticed until a chime reveals it parallels Daoist thought about the invisible forces shaping daily life. Hanging a deceased relative's handmade object where wind activates it creates a form of ongoing communication — the boundary between remembrance and presence dissolves.

tree.setup: You're waiting for the last bus of the night. The only other person at the stop is a tired-looking nurse still in her scrubs.
tree.setup_zh: 你在等末班车。站台上唯一的另一个人是一位看起来很累的护士，还穿着工作服。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 末班车总是这么安静。白天的公交车很吵、很挤，但是最后一班只有几个人。我每天都坐这班车回家。你也是刚下班吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Mò bān chē zǒng shì zhème ānjìng. Báitiān de gōngjiāo chē hěn chǎo, hěn jǐ, dànshì zuìhòu yī bān zhǐyǒu jǐ ge rén. Wǒ měi tiān dōu zuò zhè bān chē huí jiā. Nǐ yě shì gāng xià bān ma?
turns.text_en: The last bus is always this quiet. Daytime buses are noisy and crowded, but the last one has just a few people. I take this bus home every day. Did you just get off work too?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the quiet of the last bus and her daily routine.

options.text_zh: 是的。末班车有一种特别的安静，像是这座城市终于深呼吸了一下。每天最后一班车上的人，大概都有自己辛苦了一天的故事。
options.text_pinyin: Shì de. Mò bān chē yǒu yī zhǒng tèbié de ānjìng, xiàng shì zhè zuò chéngshì zhōngyú shēn hūxī le yīxià. Měi tiān zuìhòu yī bān chē shàng de rén, dàgài dōu yǒu zìjǐ xīnkǔ le yī tiān de gùshi.
options.text_en: Yes. The last bus has a special quiet — like the city finally took a deep breath. Everyone on the last bus probably has their own story of a hard day.
options.feedback: The last bus as the city's exhale — shared exhaustion as quiet solidarity.

options.text_zh: 末班车几点到？
options.text_pinyin: Mò bān chē jǐ diǎn dào?
options.text_en: What time does the last bus arrive?
options.feedback: A schedule check instead of engaging her reflection.

options.text_zh: 你不打车回家吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ bù dǎ chē huí jiā ma?
options.text_en: Don't you take a taxi home?
options.feedback: Suggests alternatives when she's sharing a ritual.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 辛苦了一天的故事——对，就是这样。我在医院工作，今天值了十二个小时的班。但是等末班车的这几分钟反而是我一天里最放松的时候。什么都不用做，就站在这里，等着。
turns.text_pinyin: Xīnkǔ le yī tiān de gùshi — duì, jiù shì zhèyàng. Wǒ zài yīyuàn gōngzuò, jīntiān zhí le shí'èr ge xiǎoshí de bān. Dànshì děng mò bān chē de zhè jǐ fēnzhōng fǎn'ér shì wǒ yī tiān lǐ zuì fàngsōng de shíhou. Shénme dōu bú yòng zuò, jiù zhàn zài zhèlǐ, děng zhe.
turns.text_en: Stories of a hard day — yes, exactly. I work at the hospital, did a twelve-hour shift today. But these few minutes waiting for the last bus are actually the most relaxing part of my day. Nothing to do, just stand here and wait.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to waiting as the most relaxing moment.

options.text_zh: 等待反而最放松——这几分钟没人需要你，没事要处理，只需要做一件事：等。也许等待本身就是一种休息，只是我们平时把它当成浪费时间。
options.text_pinyin: Děngdài fǎn'ér zuì fàngsōng — zhè jǐ fēnzhōng méi rén xūyào nǐ, méi shì yào chǔlǐ, zhǐ xūyào zuò yī jiàn shì: děng. Yěxǔ děngdài běnshēn jiù shì yī zhǒng xiūxi, zhǐ shì wǒmen píngshí bǎ tā dāng chéng làngfèi shíjiān.
options.text_en: Waiting is the most relaxing — nobody needs you for these minutes, nothing to handle, just one thing to do: wait. Maybe waiting itself is rest; we just usually treat it as wasted time.
options.feedback: Reframes waiting from waste to rest — doing nothing as the deepest relief.

options.text_zh: 十二个小时太长了。
options.text_pinyin: Shí'èr ge xiǎoshí tài cháng le.
options.text_en: Twelve hours is too long.
options.feedback: Comments on her schedule instead of her insight.

options.text_zh: 医院忙吗？
options.text_pinyin: Yīyuàn máng ma?
options.text_en: Is the hospital busy?
options.feedback: Workplace question during a moment of personal peace.

tree.cultural_note: The last bus (末班车) is a quiet institution in Chinese cities. Its passengers are often those who work the latest — nurses, restaurant workers, security guards. The few minutes waiting at the stop become an accidental meditation, a pause between duty and rest. For essential workers, these small in-between moments are often the only true break in a long day.

tree.setup: You're sitting with your colleague Xiaoyu at a quiet noodle shop after work. She's been staying late at the office every day and looks exhausted.
tree.setup_zh: 下班后你和同事小雨坐在一家安静的面馆里。她最近每天加班，看起来很疲惫。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我已经连续加了两个星期的班了。每天回家都快十点了，感觉生活只剩下工作。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ yǐjīng liánxù jiā le liǎng ge xīngqī de bān le. Měi tiān huí jiā dōu kuài shí diǎn le, gǎnjué shēnghuó zhǐ shèng xià gōngzuò.
turns.text_en: I've been working overtime for two straight weeks. I don't get home until nearly ten every night. It feels like life is nothing but work.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to Xiaoyu's exhaustion.

options.text_zh: 两个星期？那太辛苦了。你有没有试过跟领导说一下？长期这样下去对身体不好。
options.text_pinyin: Liǎng ge xīngqī? Nà tài xīnkǔ le. Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu shì guò gēn lǐngdǎo shuō yíxià? Chángqī zhèyàng xià qù duì shēntǐ bù hǎo.
options.text_en: Two weeks? That's really tough. Have you tried talking to your manager about it? It's not good for your health to keep going like this.
options.feedback: Empathetic and practical — shows concern and gently suggests action.

options.text_zh: 加班费多不多？
options.text_pinyin: Jiābān fèi duō bù duō?
options.text_en: Is the overtime pay good?
options.feedback: This reduces her exhaustion to a financial question.

options.text_zh: 我最近在追一部新的电视剧，特别好看。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ zuìjìn zài zhuī yī bù xīn de diànshìjù, tèbié hǎokàn.
options.text_en: I've been watching a new TV series recently, it's really good.
options.feedback: Ignores her distress entirely.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 说过了，但领导说项目快结束了，再坚持一下。每次都是「再坚持一下」。我都不记得上次周末做自己喜欢的事是什么时候了。
turns.text_pinyin: Shuō guò le, dàn lǐngdǎo shuō xiàngmù kuài jiéshù le, zài jiānchí yíxià. Měi cì dōu shì 'zài jiānchí yíxià'. Wǒ dōu bú jìde shàng cì zhōumò zuò zìjǐ xǐhuan de shì shì shénme shíhou le.
turns.text_en: I've tried, but the manager says the project is almost done, just hang in there a bit more. It's always 'just hang in there.' I can't even remember the last time I did something I enjoyed on a weekend.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share your thoughts on finding balance.

options.text_zh: 我觉得平衡不是把工作和生活分成两半，而是在忙的时候也能留一点空间给自己。哪怕只是睡前看十分钟书，或者早上多走一段路。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde pínghéng bú shì bǎ gōngzuò hé shēnghuó fēn chéng liǎng bàn, ér shì zài máng de shíhou yě néng liú yīdiǎn kōngjiān gěi zìjǐ. Nǎpà zhǐ shì shuì qián kàn shí fēnzhōng shū, huòzhě zǎoshang duō zǒu yí duàn lù.
options.text_en: Balance isn't splitting work and life in two — it's keeping a small space for yourself even when busy. Even reading ten minutes before bed, or taking a longer walk in the morning.
options.feedback: And realistic — reframes balance as small daily choices rather than grand gestures.

options.text_zh: 你应该辞职，换一个轻松的工作。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi cízhí, huàn yīge qīngsōng de gōngzuò.
options.text_en: You should quit and find an easier job.
options.feedback: Too abrupt — dismisses the complexity of her situation.

options.text_zh: 你中午吃了什么？这家面馆的牛肉面不错。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhōngwǔ chī le shénme? Zhè jiā miànguǎn de niúròu miàn búcuò.
options.text_en: What did you have for lunch? The beef noodles here are good.
options.feedback: Pivots away from the emotional conversation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 留一点空间给自己……你说得对。也许我可以先从小的改变开始。谢谢你愿意听我说这些。
turns.text_pinyin: Liú yīdiǎn kōngjiān gěi zìjǐ…… nǐ shuō de duì. Yěxǔ wǒ kěyǐ xiān cóng xiǎo de gǎibiàn kāishǐ. Xièxie nǐ yuànyì tīng wǒ shuō zhèxiē.
turns.text_en: Keep a small space for yourself... you're right. Maybe I can start with small changes first. Thank you for being willing to listen.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Offer a kind closing thought.

options.text_zh: 不用谢。其实我们都在学怎么照顾自己。下次累了就叫我出来吃面，至少别一个人扛着。
options.text_pinyin: Bú yòng xiè. Qíshí wǒmen dōu zài xué zěnme zhàogù zìjǐ. Xià cì lèi le jiù jiào wǒ chūlái chī miàn, zhìshǎo bié yīge rén káng zhe.
options.text_en: No need to thank me. We're all learning how to take care of ourselves. Next time you're tired, just call me to come out for noodles. At least don't carry it all alone.
options.feedback: Warm and genuine — normalizes struggle and offers ongoing support.

options.text_zh: 没事。我先走了，明天见。
options.text_pinyin: Méi shì. Wǒ xiān zǒu le, míngtiān jiàn.
options.text_en: It's fine. I'll head out first, see you tomorrow.
options.feedback: Dismissive — abrupt exit after a vulnerable conversation.

options.text_zh: 你要不要再来一碗面？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yào bú yào zài lái yī wǎn miàn?
options.text_en: Do you want another bowl of noodles?
options.feedback: Deflects from the emotional moment.

tree.cultural_note: The phrase「再坚持一下」(just hang in there a bit more) is commonly heard in Chinese workplaces. China's intense work culture, including the controversial '996' schedule (9am to 9pm, 6 days a week), has sparked growing discussion about work-life balance, especially among younger generations who increasingly question whether career success is worth sacrificing personal well-being.

tree.setup: You're sitting on a park bench with your friend Mingming. He just found out that his close friend Ahua has moved to another city for work.
tree.setup_zh: 你和朋友明明坐在公园的长椅上。他刚得知好朋友阿华因为工作搬到了另一个城市。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 阿华上个月搬去深圳了，你知道吗？他走之前也没怎么告别，就发了条朋友圈。
turns.text_pinyin: Ā Huá shàng ge yuè bān qù Shēnzhèn le, nǐ zhīdào ma? Tā zǒu zhīqián yě méi zěnme gàobié, jiù fā le tiáo péngyouquān.
turns.text_en: Ahua moved to Shenzhen last month, did you know? He didn't really say goodbye before he left, just posted on WeChat Moments.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: React to the news about Ahua leaving.

options.text_zh: 我看到了那条朋友圈，当时还以为他是出差。搬走了啊……好像这几年身边的朋友一个一个都离开了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ kàn dào le nà tiáo péngyouquān, dāngshí hái yǐwéi tā shì chūchāi. Bān zǒu le a…… hǎoxiàng zhè jǐ nián shēnbiān de péngyou yīge yīge dōu líkāi le.
options.text_en: I saw that post, I thought he was just on a business trip. So he moved... It feels like over the past few years, friends around us have left one by one.
options.feedback: Honest and relatable — acknowledges the gradual loss and connects it to a broader pattern.

options.text_zh: 其实搬去深圳也挺好的，那边机会多，发展快。他肯定会过得不错的。
options.text_pinyin: Qíshí bān qù Shēnzhèn yě tǐng hǎo de, nàbiān jīhuì duō, fāzhǎn kuài. Tā kěndìng huì guò de búcuò de.
options.text_en: Actually moving to Shenzhen is pretty good — lots of opportunities, fast development. He'll definitely do well.
options.feedback: Mingming isn't worried about Ahua's career prospects — he's grieving the distance. Jumping to optimism skips the feeling.

options.text_zh: 他工资涨了吗？
options.text_pinyin: Tā gōngzī zhǎng le ma?
options.text_en: Did he get a raise?
options.feedback: Focuses on money instead of the friendship.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 是啊。大学毕业那年我们五个人说好要留在同一个城市，结果现在只剩我们两个了。我不怪他们，每个人都有自己的路要走。只是有时候会突然想起以前一起吃夜宵的日子。
turns.text_pinyin: Shì a. Dàxué bìyè nà nián wǒmen wǔ ge rén shuō hǎo yào liú zài tóng yīge chéngshì, jiéguǒ xiànzài zhǐ shèng wǒmen liǎng ge le. Wǒ bú guài tāmen, měi ge rén dōu yǒu zìjǐ de lù yào zǒu. Zhǐ shì yǒu shíhou huì tūrán xiǎng qǐ yǐqián yīqǐ chī yèxiāo de rìzi.
turns.text_en: Yeah. The year we graduated, the five of us promised to stay in the same city. Now it's just us two. I don't blame them — everyone has their own path. I just sometimes suddenly remember the days we used to eat late-night snacks together.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on how friendship changes with distance.

options.text_zh: 距离确实会改变一些东西。联系越来越少，话题也不一样了。但我觉得好的友谊不一定要天天见面，有些朋友就算很久不联系，见面的时候还是跟以前一样。
options.text_pinyin: Jùlí quèshí huì gǎibiàn yīxiē dōngxi. Liánxì yuè lái yuè shǎo, huàtí yě bù yíyàng le. Dàn wǒ juéde hǎo de yǒuyì bú yídìng yào tiāntiān jiànmiàn, yǒuxiē péngyou jiùsuàn hěn jiǔ bù liánxì, jiànmiàn de shíhou háishì gēn yǐqián yíyàng.
options.text_en: Distance changes some things — less contact, different topics. But real friendship doesn't require seeing each other daily. Some friends, even after ages apart, still feel the same when you meet.
options.feedback: Balanced and comforting — acknowledges the loss while affirming what endures.

options.text_zh: 你想吃什么？我饿了。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ xiǎng chī shénme? Wǒ è le.
options.text_en: What do you want to eat? I'm hungry.
options.feedback: Interrupts a heartfelt moment.

options.text_zh: 你可以加他微信群聊啊。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ jiā tā Wēixìn qún liáo a.
options.text_en: You can add him to a WeChat group chat.
options.feedback: Too shallow — treats deep nostalgia as a tech problem to solve.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 就是。也许友谊不是看见面多少次，而是看心里有没有那个位置。走吧，今晚我请你吃夜宵，就当替阿华的那份也吃了。
turns.text_pinyin: Jiù shì. Yěxǔ yǒuyì bú shì kàn jiànmiàn duōshǎo cì, ér shì kàn xīn lǐ yǒu méiyǒu nàge wèizhì. Zǒu ba, jīn wǎn wǒ qǐng nǐ chī yèxiāo, jiù dāng tì Ā Huá de nà fèn yě chī le.
turns.text_en: Exactly. Maybe friendship isn't about how often you meet, but whether there's still a place in your heart. Come on, tonight I'll treat you to late-night snacks — consider it eating Ahua's share too.

tree.cultural_note: In China, the massive internal migration for work opportunities means close friend groups from university often scatter across the country after graduation. WeChat Moments (朋友圈) has become the primary way dispersed friends stay loosely connected, though many people note the difference between scrolling past someone's posts and actually sharing a meal together. Late-night snacks (夜宵) remain a symbol of close friendship in Chinese culture.

tree.setup: You're having tea with your neighbor Lao Li, who recently left his engineering job to open a small bakery. He seems both nervous and alive in a way you haven't seen before.
tree.setup_zh: 你正在和邻居老李喝茶。他最近辞掉了工程师的工作，开了一家小面包店。他看起来既紧张又充满活力，和以前很不一样。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 说实话，递辞职信那天我的手都在抖。干了十二年的工程师，突然要从头开始做面包，家里人都觉得我疯了。
turns.text_pinyin: Shuō shíhuà, dì cízhí xìn nà tiān wǒ de shǒu dōu zài dǒu. Gàn le shí'èr nián de gōngchéngshī, tūrán yào cóng tóu kāishǐ zuò miànbāo, jiālǐ rén dōu juéde wǒ fēng le.
turns.text_en: To be honest, my hands were shaking the day I handed in my resignation. After twelve years as an engineer, suddenly starting over making bread — my family thought I was crazy.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to Lao Li's courage in changing careers.

options.text_zh: 能做出这个决定本身就不容易。十二年不是一个小数字，放下需要很大的勇气。是什么让你最终决定要换的？
options.text_pinyin: Néng zuò chū zhège juédìng běnshēn jiù bù róngyì. Shí'èr nián bú shì yīge xiǎo shùzì, fàng xià xūyào hěn dà de yǒngqì. Shì shénme ràng nǐ zuìzhōng juédìng yào huàn de?
options.text_en: Making that decision itself isn't easy. Twelve years is no small number, and letting go takes real courage. What finally made you decide to switch?
options.feedback: Affirming and curious — validates the difficulty while inviting him to share more.

options.text_zh: 面包店赚钱吗？
options.text_pinyin: Miànbāo diàn zhuàn qián ma?
options.text_en: Does the bakery make money?
options.feedback: This reduces a life-changing decision to profitability.

options.text_zh: 你以前做什么工程？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǐqián zuò shénme gōngchéng?
options.text_en: What kind of engineering did you do before?
options.feedback: Looks backward instead of engaging with the emotional present.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 有一天晚上加班到十一点，回家路上经过一家面包店，闻到刚出炉的面包香。我站在那里闻了好久，突然想：我什么时候对自己的工作也有过这种感觉？答案是从来没有。
turns.text_pinyin: Yǒu yī tiān wǎnshang jiābān dào shíyī diǎn, huí jiā lù shang jīngguò yī jiā miànbāo diàn, wén dào gāng chūlú de miànbāo xiāng. Wǒ zhàn zài nàlǐ wén le hǎo jiǔ, tūrán xiǎng: wǒ shénme shíhou duì zìjǐ de gōngzuò yě yǒu guò zhè zhǒng gǎnjué? Dá'àn shì cónglái méiyǒu.
turns.text_en: One night I worked overtime until eleven. On the way home I passed a bakery and smelled freshly baked bread. I stood there smelling it for a long time, and suddenly thought: when have I ever felt this way about my own work? The answer was never.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share your thoughts on following what moves you.

options.text_zh: 那个瞬间你找到了答案。很多人一辈子都在做「应该做」的事，从来没问过自己真正想做什么。你至少迈出了那一步。
options.text_pinyin: Nàge shùnjiān nǐ zhǎo dào le dá'àn. Hěn duō rén yíbèizi dōu zài zuò 'yīnggāi zuò' de shì, cónglái méi wèn guò zìjǐ zhēnzhèng xiǎng zuò shénme. Nǐ zhìshǎo mài chū le nà yī bù.
options.text_en: In that moment you found your answer. Many people spend their whole lives doing what they 'should' do, never asking what they truly want. At least you took that step.
options.feedback: Encouraging without being hollow — acknowledges the universal struggle and honors his choice.

options.text_zh: 但是做面包很辛苦，要很早起床。
options.text_pinyin: Dànshì zuò miànbāo hěn xīnkǔ, yào hěn zǎo qǐchuáng.
options.text_en: But making bread is hard work, you have to get up very early.
options.feedback: Deflating — points out difficulties when he's sharing a moment of meaning.

options.text_zh: 你的面包店在哪条街？我下次去看看。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de miànbāo diàn zài nǎ tiáo jiē? Wǒ xià cì qù kànkan.
options.text_en: Which street is your bakery on? I'll visit next time.
options.feedback: Not wrong, but misses the emotional depth of what he's sharing.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 谢谢你这么说。现在每天凌晨四点起来揉面，确实累。但闻到面包出炉的时候，我觉得一切都值得。也许这就是他们说的「热爱」吧。
turns.text_pinyin: Xièxie nǐ zhème shuō. Xiànzài měi tiān língchén sì diǎn qǐlái róu miàn, quèshí lèi. Dàn wén dào miànbāo chūlú de shíhou, wǒ juéde yīqiè dōu zhíde. Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì tāmen shuō de 'rè'ài' ba.
turns.text_en: Thank you for saying that. Now I get up at four every morning to knead dough — it is tiring. But when I smell the bread coming out of the oven, I feel it's all worth it. Maybe this is what people call 'passion.'

tree.cultural_note: In Chinese culture, career changes later in life often face significant family pressure, as stable professional jobs (especially in engineering or government) are highly valued. The concept of following one's passion (热爱) is gaining ground among younger and middle-aged Chinese, though it still conflicts with traditional expectations of financial security and family duty.

tree.setup: You're sitting with your friend Xiaomei in a quiet bookshop café. She just watched a documentary about traditional craftspeople in rural China and can't stop thinking about it.
tree.setup_zh: 你和朋友小美坐在一家安静的书店咖啡馆里。她刚看了一部关于中国农村传统手艺人的纪录片，一直在回味。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我昨晚看了一部纪录片，讲的是贵州山里做蓝染布的老奶奶。她八十多岁了，还每天自己种靛草、染布。看完以后我心里特别安静。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ zuó wǎn kàn le yī bù jìlùpiàn, jiǎng de shì Guìzhōu shān lǐ zuò lán rǎn bù de lǎo nǎinai. Tā bāshí duō suì le, hái měi tiān zìjǐ zhòng diàn cǎo, rǎn bù. Kàn wán yǐhòu wǒ xīn lǐ tèbié ānjìng.
turns.text_en: I watched a documentary last night about an elderly grandmother in the mountains of Guizhou who makes indigo-dyed cloth. She's over eighty and still grows the indigo plants and dyes cloth every day. After watching it, I felt incredibly peaceful.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to Xiaomei's description of the documentary.

options.text_zh: 听起来很美。一个人一辈子专注做一件事，那种坚持本身就是一种艺术。你觉得是什么让你看了以后感到安静？
options.text_pinyin: Tīng qǐlái hěn měi. Yīge rén yíbèizi zhuānzhù zuò yī jiàn shì, nà zhǒng jiānchí běnshēn jiù shì yī zhǒng yìshù. Nǐ juéde shì shénme ràng nǐ kàn le yǐhòu gǎndào ānjìng?
options.text_en: That sounds beautiful. A person spending a whole lifetime focused on one thing — that persistence itself is a kind of art. What do you think made you feel peaceful after watching it?
options.feedback: Engages with the feeling she's describing.

options.text_zh: 纪录片在哪个平台上？要不要会员？
options.text_pinyin: Jìlùpiàn zài nǎge píngtái shàng? Yào bú yào huìyuán?
options.text_en: Which platform is the documentary on? Do you need a membership?
options.feedback: Jumps to logistics instead of connecting emotionally.

options.text_zh: 贵州的天气怎么样？我一直想去旅游。
options.text_pinyin: Guìzhōu de tiānqì zěnmeyàng? Wǒ yīzhí xiǎng qù lǚyóu.
options.text_en: What's the weather like in Guizhou? I've been wanting to travel there.
options.feedback: Derails into travel planning.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我想可能是因为她做这件事不是为了赚钱，也不是为了出名。她只是觉得这是她应该做的事。在我们这个什么都讲效率的时代，这种态度反而让人感动。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng kěnéng shì yīnwèi tā zuò zhè jiàn shì bú shì wèile zhuàn qián, yě bú shì wèile chūmíng. Tā zhǐ shì juéde zhè shì tā yīnggāi zuò de shì. Zài wǒmen zhège shénme dōu jiǎng xiàolǜ de shídài, zhè zhǒng tàidù fǎn'ér ràng rén gǎndòng.
turns.text_en: I think maybe it's because she does it not for money or fame. She just feels it's what she's meant to do. In our era where everything is about efficiency, that kind of attitude is actually moving.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share your thoughts on craftsmanship and modern life.

options.text_zh: 也许我们被效率绑住了。什么都要快，什么都要有结果。但有些事情的价值不在于结果，而在于做的过程。就像她染布，每一块布都是时间慢慢积累出来的。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ wǒmen bèi xiàolǜ bǎng zhù le. Shénme dōu yào kuài, shénme dōu yào yǒu jiéguǒ. Dàn yǒuxiē shìqíng de jiàzhí bú zàiyú jiéguǒ, ér zàiyú zuò de guòchéng. Jiù xiàng tā rǎn bù, měi yī kuài bù dōu shì shíjiān mànman jīlěi chūlái de.
options.text_en: Maybe we've been bound by efficiency. Everything fast, everything with results. But some things' value is in the process, not the outcome. Like her dyeing cloth — every piece is time slowly accumulated.
options.feedback: Connects the documentary's message to broader modern anxieties.

options.text_zh: 我觉得手工的东西太贵了，不实用。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde shǒugōng de dōngxi tài guì le, bù shíyòng.
options.text_en: I think handmade things are too expensive and not practical.
options.feedback: Applies a consumer mindset to a conversation about meaning.

options.text_zh: 你平时看纪录片多吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ píngshí kàn jìlùpiàn duō ma?
options.text_en: Do you watch documentaries often?
options.feedback: Generic question that doesn't engage with the depth of the discussion.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 时间慢慢积累出来的……你说得真好。我有时候觉得我们的生活跑得太快了，连停下来想一想都觉得是在浪费时间。也许我应该学她，找一件能让自己安静下来的事。
turns.text_pinyin: Shíjiān mànman jīlěi chūlái de…… nǐ shuō de zhēn hǎo. Wǒ yǒu shíhou juéde wǒmen de shēnghuó pǎo de tài kuài le, lián tíng xiàlái xiǎng yī xiǎng dōu juéde shì zài làngfèi shíjiān. Yěxǔ wǒ yīnggāi xué tā, zhǎo yī jiàn néng ràng zìjǐ ānjìng xiàlái de shì.
turns.text_en: Time slowly accumulated... that's beautifully put. Sometimes I feel our lives move too fast — even stopping to think feels like wasting time. Maybe I should learn from her and find something that can make me feel still.

tree.cultural_note: China has a rich tradition of artisanal crafts, many of which are disappearing as younger generations move to cities. Documentaries like「了不起的匠人」(The Great Shokunin) and「舌尖上的中国」(A Bite of China) have sparked renewed interest in traditional craftsmanship. Indigo dyeing (蓝染) is particularly significant in Guizhou's Miao and Dong ethnic minority communities, where the craft has been passed down for centuries.

tree.setup: You run into Aunt Zhang at the neighborhood convenience store. She's lived here for twenty years and has noticed how much the area has changed recently.
tree.setup_zh: 你在小区的便利店遇到了张阿姨。她在这里住了二十年，最近发现这片区域变化很大。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看那边，又开了一家咖啡店。以前那个位置是一家修鞋铺，老师傅修了三十年的鞋。上个月关门了，说是租金涨了三倍。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn nà biān, yòu kāi le yī jiā kāfēi diàn. Yǐqián nàge wèizhì shì yī jiā xiū xié pù, lǎo shīfu xiū le sānshí nián de xié. Shàng ge yuè guānmén le, shuō shì zūjīn zhǎng le sān bèi.
turns.text_en: Look over there, another coffee shop opened. That spot used to be a shoe repair shop — the old craftsman repaired shoes for thirty years. He closed last month because the rent tripled.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the neighborhood changes.

options.text_zh: 三十年……他肯定对这个地方有很深的感情。这样的小店越来越少了，取代它们的都是连锁店。方便是方便了，但总觉得少了点什么。
options.text_pinyin: Sānshí nián…… tā kěndìng duì zhège dìfang yǒu hěn shēn de gǎnqíng. Zhèyàng de xiǎo diàn yuè lái yuè shǎo le, qǔdài tāmen de dōu shì liánsuǒ diàn. Fāngbiàn shì fāngbiàn le, dàn zǒng juéde shǎo le diǎn shénme.
options.text_en: Thirty years... he must have had deep feelings for this place. These little shops are fewer and fewer, replaced by chain stores. It's more convenient, sure, but something always feels missing.
options.feedback: Empathetic and observant — honors the craftsman's connection to the neighborhood.

options.text_zh: 那家咖啡好喝吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nà jiā kāfēi hǎo hē ma?
options.text_en: Is the coffee at that place any good?
options.feedback: Ignores the loss she's describing.

options.text_zh: 现在的房租确实太贵了。你家的租金涨了吗？
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài de fángzū quèshí tài guì le. Nǐ jiā de zūjīn zhǎng le ma?
options.text_en: Rent is really too expensive now. Has your rent gone up?
options.feedback: Makes it about her personal finances rather than the community loss.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 少了人情味儿。以前修鞋的老师傅认识我们每一个人，知道谁家的孩子考上了大学，谁家的老人身体不好。咖啡店的年轻人可不会记住这些。
turns.text_pinyin: Shǎo le rénqíngwèir. Yǐqián xiū xié de lǎo shīfu rènshi wǒmen měi yīge rén, zhīdào shéi jiā de háizi kǎo shàng le dàxué, shéi jiā de lǎorén shēntǐ bù hǎo. Kāfēi diàn de niánqīng rén kě bú huì jìzhù zhèxiē.
turns.text_en: The human warmth is missing. The old cobbler knew every one of us — who had a kid that got into university, whose elderly parent wasn't doing well. The young people at the coffee shop won't remember any of that.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on community connection.

options.text_zh: 这种关系是用时间堆出来的，搬不走也买不到。也许我们可以主动跟新邻居多说说话？改变已经发生了，但人情味儿不一定非要跟着消失。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè zhǒng guānxì shì yòng shíjiān duī chūlái de, bān bù zǒu yě mǎi bú dào. Yěxǔ wǒmen kěyǐ zhǔdòng gēn xīn línjū duō shuōshuō huà? Gǎibiàn yǐjīng fāshēng le, dàn rénqíngwèir bú yídìng fēi yào gēn zhe xiāoshī.
options.text_en: That relationship is built over time — you can't move or buy it. Maybe we can talk more with new neighbors? Change has happened, but human warmth doesn't have to disappear with it.
options.feedback: Constructive and gentle — acknowledges the loss while proposing a way forward.

options.text_zh: 你有那个师傅的联系方式吗？我的鞋坏了。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu nàge shīfu de liánxì fāngshì ma? Wǒ de xié huài le.
options.text_en: Do you have that cobbler's contact info? My shoes are broken.
options.feedback: Practical request that misses the emotional weight.

options.text_zh: 时代在变嘛，我们也要跟上。
options.text_pinyin: Shídài zài biàn ma, wǒmen yě yào gēn shàng.
options.text_en: Times are changing, we have to keep up.
options.feedback: Dismissive — invalidates her feelings about community loss.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 嗯，是啊。也许我明天做一锅红烧肉，给楼上新搬来的那家人送一份去。总得有人先开口嘛。
turns.text_pinyin: Ǹg, shì a. Yěxǔ wǒ míngtiān zuò yī guō hóngshāo ròu, gěi lóu shàng xīn bān lái de nà jiā rén sòng yī fèn qù. Zǒng děi yǒu rén xiān kāikǒu ma.
turns.text_en: Yeah, exactly. Maybe tomorrow I'll make a pot of braised pork and bring some to the new family that moved in upstairs. Someone has to make the first move.

tree.cultural_note: 人情味儿 (rénqíngwèir, human warmth/personal touch) is a concept deeply cherished in Chinese community life. Traditional neighborhoods were held together by small daily interactions — the cobbler, the vegetable vendor, the tailor — people who knew your family's story. Rapid urban development has replaced many such community anchors with chain businesses, and the resulting sense of disconnection is a common topic of quiet lament.

tree.setup: You're in the kitchen with your friend Xiaowen. She's trying to recreate her grandmother's braised fish, working from memory since no recipe was ever written down.
tree.setup_zh: 你和朋友小文在厨房里。她正试着凭记忆重做奶奶的红烧鱼，因为食谱从来没有写下来过。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我奶奶做的红烧鱼，我再也做不出那个味道。她从来不量调料，都是看感觉放的。我小时候站在旁边看了无数次，但自己做的时候总是差一点。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ nǎinai zuò de hóngshāo yú, wǒ zài yě zuò bù chū nàge wèidào. Tā cónglái bù liáng tiáoliào, dōu shì kàn gǎnjué fàng de. Wǒ xiǎo shíhou zhàn zài pángbiān kàn le wúshù cì, dàn zìjǐ zuò de shíhou zǒng shì chà yīdiǎn.
turns.text_en: My grandmother's braised fish — I can never recreate that flavor. She never measured seasonings, she just went by feel. I watched her countless times as a child, but when I try it myself, something is always slightly off.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to Xiaowen about her grandmother's cooking.

options.text_zh: 也许差的那一点不是调料的问题，是记忆里的味道。小时候吃的东西总是特别好吃，因为那时候有奶奶在旁边。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ chà de nà yīdiǎn bú shì tiáoliào de wèntí, shì jìyì lǐ de wèidào. Xiǎo shíhou chī de dōngxi zǒng shì tèbié hǎochī, yīnwèi nà shíhou yǒu nǎinai zài pángbiān.
options.text_en: Maybe what's missing isn't about seasonings — it's the taste in your memory. Food from childhood always tastes especially good because grandmother was there beside you.
