options.feedback: Tender and insightful — connects taste to emotion and presence.

options.text_zh: 你可以在网上搜一下红烧鱼的做法。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ zài wǎng shàng sōu yíxià hóngshāo yú de zuòfǎ.
options.text_en: You can search for a braised fish recipe online.
options.feedback: Misses the emotional point about grandmother's unique touch.

options.text_zh: 你多做几次就好了，熟能生巧嘛。下次注意火候应该就差不多了。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ duō zuò jǐ cì jiù hǎo le, shú néng shēng qiǎo ma. Xià cì zhùyì huǒhòu yīnggāi jiù chàbuduō le.
options.text_en: Just practice a few more times — practice makes perfect. Pay attention to the heat next time and it should be close enough.
options.feedback: She's not looking for cooking tips. The gap isn't technique — it's that her grandmother is gone.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 是啊。有时候我做这道菜，不完全是为了味道，是想通过做饭跟她再靠近一点。她去世已经五年了，但每次闻到红烧的香味，我就想起她的厨房。
turns.text_pinyin: Shì a. Yǒu shíhou wǒ zuò zhè dào cài, bù wánquán shì wèile wèidào, shì xiǎng tōngguò zuòfàn gēn tā zài kào jìn yīdiǎn. Tā qùshì yǐjīng wǔ nián le, dàn měi cì wén dào hóngshāo de xiāngwèi, wǒ jiù xiǎng qǐ tā de chúfáng.
turns.text_en: Yeah. Sometimes when I cook this dish, it's not entirely about the flavor — it's wanting to feel closer to her through cooking. She passed away five years ago, but every time I smell braised food, I think of her kitchen.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share a comforting thought about keeping memories alive.

options.text_zh: 做饭就是你和她之间的纽带。也许味道不完全一样，但你每次做这道菜的时候，她的记忆就还活着。这比任何食谱都重要。
options.text_pinyin: Zuòfàn jiù shì nǐ hé tā zhī jiān de niǔdài. Yěxǔ wèidào bù wánquán yíyàng, dàn nǐ měi cì zuò zhè dào cài de shíhou, tā de jìyì jiù hái huó zhe. Zhè bǐ rènhé shípǔ dōu zhòngyào.
options.text_en: Cooking is the bond between you and her. Maybe the taste isn't exactly the same, but every time you make this dish, her memory stays alive. That matters more than any recipe.
options.feedback: Warm and meaningful — elevates the act of cooking beyond technique.

options.text_zh: 你应该把做法记下来，不然以后会忘记。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi bǎ zuòfǎ jì xiàlái, bùrán yǐhòu huì wàngjì.
options.text_en: You should write the recipe down so you don't forget it later.
options.feedback: Practical but misses the emotional depth of the moment.

options.text_zh: 下次你可以试试用别的鱼。
options.text_pinyin: Xià cì nǐ kěyǐ shìshì yòng bié de yú.
options.text_en: Next time you can try using a different kind of fish.
options.feedback: Focuses on ingredients when the conversation is about memory.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 谢谢你。来，尝尝今天做的。虽然跟奶奶的不一样，但也是我用心做的。也许有一天，我的孩子也会想念我做的这个味道。
turns.text_pinyin: Xièxie nǐ. Lái, chángchang jīntiān zuò de. Suīrán gēn nǎinai de bù yíyàng, dàn yě shì wǒ yòng xīn zuò de. Yěxǔ yǒu yī tiān, wǒ de háizi yě huì xiǎngniàn wǒ zuò de zhège wèidào.
turns.text_en: Thank you. Here, taste today's attempt. It's not the same as grandmother's, but I made it with heart. Maybe one day, my children will miss my version of this flavor too.

tree.cultural_note: In Chinese families, recipes are rarely written down — they're transmitted through observation and practice across generations. The phrase「看感觉放」(go by feel) describes an intuitive cooking style common among older Chinese cooks. When a family elder passes away, their dishes become irreplaceable, making cooking an act of remembrance and connection.

tree.setup: It's past midnight. You and your roommate Daming are sitting on the balcony, unable to sleep. The city below is finally quiet.
tree.setup_zh: 已经过了午夜。你和室友大明坐在阳台上，睡不着。楼下的城市终于安静了。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你有没有过那种感觉？白天忙的时候什么都不想，一到晚上安静下来，脑子里就开始翻来覆去想很多事。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu guò nà zhǒng gǎnjué? Báitiān máng de shíhou shénme dōu bù xiǎng, yī dào wǎnshang ānjìng xiàlái, nǎozi lǐ jiù kāishǐ fānlái fùqù xiǎng hěn duō shì.
turns.text_en: Have you ever had that feeling? During the busy day you don't think about anything, but as soon as it gets quiet at night, your mind starts turning things over and over.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share whether you relate to this feeling.

options.text_zh: 经常。尤其是那些白天没有时间处理的情绪，到了晚上都会冒出来。有时候不一定是坏事，安静的时候反而能看清一些东西。
options.text_pinyin: Jīngcháng. Yóuqí shì nàxiē báitiān méiyǒu shíjiān chǔlǐ de qíngxù, dào le wǎnshang dōu huì mào chūlái. Yǒu shíhou bú yídìng shì huài shì, ānjìng de shíhou fǎn'ér néng kàn qīng yīxiē dōngxi.
options.text_en: Often. Especially emotions I didn't have time to process during the day — they all surface at night. Sometimes it's not necessarily bad. In the quiet, you can actually see some things more clearly.
options.feedback: Honest and reflective — normalizes nighttime overthinking and reframes it gently.

options.text_zh: 你应该早点睡觉，别想太多。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi zǎo diǎn shuìjiào, bié xiǎng tài duō.
options.text_en: You should go to bed earlier and stop overthinking.
options.feedback: Dismissive — shuts down the reflective mood.

options.text_zh: 我一般睡前看手机，很快就睡着了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yībān shuì qián kàn shǒujī, hěn kuài jiù shuì zháo le.
options.text_en: I usually look at my phone before bed and fall asleep quickly.
options.feedback: Misses the deeper conversation about inner life.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 看清一些东西……也许吧。我最近一直在想，我现在过的生活是不是我真正想要的。不是说不好，就是觉得每天都差不多，好像在走一条别人画好的路。
turns.text_pinyin: Kàn qīng yīxiē dōngxi…… yěxǔ ba. Wǒ zuìjìn yīzhí zài xiǎng, wǒ xiànzài guò de shēnghuó shì bu shì wǒ zhēnzhèng xiǎng yào de. Bú shì shuō bù hǎo, jiù shì juéde měi tiān dōu chàbuduō, hǎoxiàng zài zǒu yī tiáo biéren huà hǎo de lù.
turns.text_en: See things more clearly... maybe. I've been thinking lately — is the life I'm living really the one I want? It's not that it's bad, it just feels like every day is the same, like I'm walking a path someone else drew.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his existential reflection.

options.text_zh: 我觉得有这种想法不代表你走错了路，只是说明你开始认真思考了。很多人连这一步都不会走。也许答案不需要今晚就找到，先问出来就已经很好了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde yǒu zhè zhǒng xiǎngfǎ bú dàibiǎo nǐ zǒu cuò le lù, zhǐ shì shuōmíng nǐ kāishǐ rènzhēn sīkǎo le. Hěn duō rén lián zhè yī bù dōu bú huì zǒu. Yěxǔ dá'àn bù xūyào jīn wǎn jiù zhǎo dào, xiān wèn chūlái jiù yǐjīng hěn hǎo le.
options.text_en: Having these thoughts doesn't mean you're on the wrong path — it means you've started thinking seriously. Many people never take that step. The answer doesn't need to come tonight. Asking the question is enough.
options.feedback: Patient and wise — validates the questioning without rushing to solutions.

options.text_zh: 你可以去旅游散散心。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ qù lǚyóu sànsàn xīn.
options.text_en: You could go traveling to clear your mind.
options.feedback: Surface-level — offers escape rather than engagement.

options.text_zh: 别想了，明天还要上班呢。
options.text_pinyin: Bié xiǎng le, míngtiān hái yào shàng bān ne.
options.text_en: Stop thinking about it, you have work tomorrow.
options.feedback: Dismissive — uses practicality to avoid depth.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 先问出来就已经很好了……谢谢你。夜里说的话好像比白天的更真。也许我们都需要这样的时刻，停下来听听自己心里的声音。
turns.text_pinyin: Xiān wèn chūlái jiù yǐjīng hěn hǎo le…… xièxie nǐ. Yè lǐ shuō de huà hǎoxiàng bǐ báitiān de gèng zhēn. Yěxǔ wǒmen dōu xūyào zhèyàng de shíkè, tíng xiàlái tīng tīng zìjǐ xīn lǐ de shēngyīn.
turns.text_en: Just asking is already enough... thank you. Words spoken at night feel more real than daytime ones. Maybe we all need moments like this — to stop and listen to the voice inside.

tree.cultural_note: In Chinese culture, there's a saying「夜深人静」(yè shēn rén jìng, deep night when people are quiet) — the idea that stillness and truth emerge in the late hours. Many young Chinese workers describe feeling trapped in routines shaped by societal expectations (stable job, marriage timeline, homeownership), and late-night conversations with close friends become rare spaces for honest self-reflection.

tree.setup: You're browsing a used bookstore with your friend Yichen. He picks up a worn copy of a book and holds it like an old friend.
tree.setup_zh: 你和朋友一辰在一家旧书店里。他拿起一本翻旧了的书，像握着一位老朋友。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这本书我大学的时候读了三遍。那时候我什么都不确定——专业选得对不对，以后要做什么。读完以后我没有找到答案，但不知道为什么，我不那么害怕了。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè běn shū wǒ dàxué de shíhou dú le sān biàn. Nà shíhou wǒ shénme dōu bú quèdìng — zhuānyè xuǎn de duì bú duì, yǐhòu yào zuò shénme. Dú wán yǐhòu wǒ méiyǒu zhǎo dào dá'àn, dàn bù zhīdào wèi shénme, wǒ bú nàme hàipà le.
turns.text_en: I read this book three times in university. Back then I was uncertain about everything — whether I'd chosen the right major, what I'd do with my life. After reading it I didn't find answers, but somehow, I wasn't as afraid anymore.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Ask about what made the book special.

options.text_zh: 不是给你答案，而是让你不再害怕没有答案——这种书才是真正有力量的。是什么内容让你有这种感觉？
options.text_pinyin: Bú shì gěi nǐ dá'àn, ér shì ràng nǐ bú zài hàipà méiyǒu dá'àn — zhè zhǒng shū cái shì zhēnzhèng yǒu lìliang de. Shì shénme nèiróng ràng nǐ yǒu zhè zhǒng gǎnjué?
options.text_en: Not giving you answers, but making you no longer afraid of not having them — that's what truly powerful books do. What was it about the content that made you feel that way?
options.feedback: Reframing — recognizes the book's real gift and asks to go deeper.

options.text_zh: 这本书叫什么名字？我回去在网上搜搜看。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè běn shū jiào shénme míngzì? Wǒ huíqù zài wǎng shàng sōu sōu kàn.
options.text_en: What's this book called? I'll look it up online when I get home.
options.feedback: He's sharing a formative emotional experience, and you respond with an information request. The question isn't what it's called — it's what it did to him.

options.text_zh: 我不太喜欢读书，我更喜欢看视频。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bú tài xǐhuan dú shū, wǒ gèng xǐhuan kàn shìpín.
options.text_en: I don't really like reading, I prefer watching videos.
options.feedback: Dismisses his emotional connection to books.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 书里有一句话我到现在还记得：「你不需要看到整条路，只需要看到下一步就够了。」那时候的我总想把未来全部想清楚，但其实没有人能做到。
turns.text_pinyin: Shū lǐ yǒu yī jù huà wǒ dào xiànzài hái jìde: 'Nǐ bù xūyào kàn dào zhěng tiáo lù, zhǐ xūyào kàn dào xià yī bù jiù gòu le.' Nà shíhou de wǒ zǒng xiǎng bǎ wèilái quánbù xiǎng qīngchǔ, dàn qíshí méiyǒu rén néng zuòdào.
turns.text_en: There's a line in the book I still remember: 'You don't need to see the whole road, just the next step is enough.' Back then I always wanted to figure out the entire future, but nobody can actually do that.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share how a book or idea has affected you.

options.text_zh: 我也有过类似的经历。有一本书让我明白，不完美是正常的，不需要什么都做到最好才开始行动。有时候一句话就够了，在对的时刻读到，整个人都不一样了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yě yǒu guò lèisì de jīnglì. Yǒu yī běn shū ràng wǒ míngbai, bù wánměi shì zhèngcháng de, bù xūyào shénme dōu zuòdào zuì hǎo cái kāishǐ xíngdòng. Yǒu shíhou yī jù huà jiù gòu le, zài duì de shíkè dú dào, zhěnggè rén dōu bù yíyàng le.
options.text_en: I've had a similar experience. A book helped me understand that imperfection is normal — you don't have to be perfect at everything before you start. Sometimes one sentence is enough. Read at the right moment, it changes you entirely.
options.feedback: Genuine and reciprocal — shares vulnerability while affirming his experience.

options.text_zh: 现在谁还看纸质书啊，都用电子书了。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài shéi hái kàn zhǐzhì shū a, dōu yòng diànzǐ shū le.
options.text_en: Who still reads paper books? Everyone uses e-books now.
options.feedback: Dismisses the tangible, emotional connection to physical books.

options.text_zh: 你大学读的什么专业？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ dàxué dú de shénme zhuānyè?
options.text_en: What did you major in at university?
options.feedback: Diverts from the meaningful thread about books and growth.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 在对的时刻读到……没错。也许这就是旧书店存在的意义吧。每本旧书都在等一个需要它的人。来，这本我买给你，也许你也会需要它。
turns.text_pinyin: Zài duì de shíkè dú dào…… méi cuò. Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì jiù shūdiàn cúnzài de yìyì ba. Měi běn jiù shū dōu zài děng yīge xūyào tā de rén. Lái, zhè běn wǒ mǎi gěi nǐ, yěxǔ nǐ yě huì xūyào tā.
turns.text_en: Read at the right moment... exactly. Maybe that's what used bookstores are for. Every old book is waiting for someone who needs it. Here, I'll buy this one for you — maybe you'll need it too.

tree.cultural_note: Used bookstores (旧书店) hold a special place in Chinese literary culture, especially in university towns. The practice of gifting books carries emotional weight — it's sharing a piece of your inner world. Many Chinese readers describe a single book or sentence that arrived at exactly the right moment in their lives, a concept resonant with the Buddhist idea of「缘分」(yuánfèn, fated connection).

title: A Place I Keep Returning To

tree.setup: You and your colleague Jing are eating lunch together. She mentions a small town in Yunnan that she visited years ago and keeps wanting to return to.
tree.setup_zh: 你和同事静一起吃午饭。她提到了多年前去过的云南小镇，一直想再回去。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我五年前去过云南的一个小镇，名字我都快忘了，但那里的感觉我一直记得。早上推开窗户就是山，空气里有松树的味道，时间好像过得特别慢。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ wǔ nián qián qù guò Yúnnán de yīge xiǎo zhèn, míngzi wǒ dōu kuài wàng le, dàn nàlǐ de gǎnjué wǒ yīzhí jìde. Zǎoshang tuī kāi chuānghu jiù shì shān, kōngqì lǐ yǒu sōngshù de wèidào, shíjiān hǎoxiàng guò de tèbié màn.
turns.text_en: Five years ago I visited a small town in Yunnan. I've almost forgotten the name, but I still remember how it felt. Every morning I'd open the window to mountains, the air smelled of pine trees, and time seemed to pass especially slowly.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to Jing's travel memory.

options.text_zh: 你记住的不是名字，而是感觉。也许那才是旅行真正留下来的东西——不是照片，是某个瞬间的安静。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ jìzhù de bú shì míngzi, ér shì gǎnjué. Yěxǔ nà cái shì lǚxíng zhēnzhèng liú xiàlái de dōngxi — bú shì zhàopiàn, shì mǒu ge shùnjiān de ānjìng.
options.text_en: What you remember isn't the name, but the feeling. Maybe that's what travel really leaves behind — not photos, but the stillness of a certain moment.
options.feedback: And true — captures what makes certain places unforgettable.

options.text_zh: 云南的机票贵不贵？
options.text_pinyin: Yúnnán de jīpiào guì bú guì?
options.text_en: Are flights to Yunnan expensive?
options.feedback: This reduces a lyrical memory to travel logistics.

options.text_zh: 你拍了很多照片吗？发给我看看。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ pāi le hěn duō zhàopiàn ma? Fā gěi wǒ kànkan.
options.text_en: Did you take a lot of photos? Send them to me.
options.feedback: She just said the feeling matters more than tangible records.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对，就是安静。我在那里住了一个小客栈，老板娘每天早上泡普洱茶。我们就坐在院子里，有一搭没一搭地聊天。什么都不急。回到城市以后我才发现，原来我已经很久没有过那种不着急的感觉了。
turns.text_pinyin: Duì, jiù shì ānjìng. Wǒ zài nàlǐ zhù le yīge xiǎo kèzhàn, lǎobǎnniáng měi tiān zǎoshang pào Pǔ'ěr chá. Wǒmen jiù zuò zài yuànzi lǐ, yǒu yī dā méi yī dā de liáotiān. Shénme dōu bù jí. Huí dào chéngshì yǐhòu wǒ cái fāxiàn, yuánlái wǒ yǐjīng hěn jiǔ méiyǒu guò nà zhǒng bù zháojí de gǎnjué le.
turns.text_en: Right, exactly — stillness. I stayed at a small guesthouse there. The owner brewed Pu'er tea every morning. We'd sit in the courtyard chatting casually. Nothing was urgent. After returning to the city, I realized I hadn't felt unhurried like that in a very long time.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on why we keep wanting to return to certain places.

options.text_zh: 也许我们想回去的不是那个地方，而是在那里的那个自己。那时候的你没有被催着赶着，能真正地活在当下。也许不用回去，在这里也能偶尔给自己那样的时刻。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ wǒmen xiǎng huí qù de bú shì nàge dìfang, ér shì zài nàlǐ de nàge zìjǐ. Nà shíhou de nǐ méiyǒu bèi cuī zhe gǎn zhe, néng zhēnzhèng de huó zài dāngxià. Yěxǔ bú yòng huí qù, zài zhèlǐ yě néng ǒu'ěr gěi zìjǐ nàyàng de shíkè.
options.text_en: Maybe what we want to return to isn't the place, but who we were there. You weren't being rushed — you could live in the present. You don't have to go back. Give yourself those moments here sometimes.
options.feedback: Profound and gentle — reframes nostalgia as something you can recreate within.

options.text_zh: 你应该再去一次，拍个视频发到网上。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi zài qù yī cì, pāi ge shìpín fā dào wǎng shàng.
options.text_en: You should go again and post a video online.
options.feedback: Commodifies the experience.

options.text_zh: 普洱茶好喝吗？我比较喜欢绿茶。
options.text_pinyin: Pǔ'ěr chá hǎo hē ma? Wǒ bǐjiào xǐhuan lǜchá.
options.text_en: Is Pu'er tea good? I prefer green tea.
options.feedback: Fixates on tea preference instead of the deeper meaning.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 在这里也能给自己那样的时刻……你说得对。也许今天中午就是这样的时刻。不急着回去上班，就坐在这里多聊一会儿。
turns.text_pinyin: Zài zhèlǐ yě néng gěi zìjǐ nàyàng de shíkè…… nǐ shuō de duì. Yěxǔ jīntiān zhōngwǔ jiù shì zhèyàng de shíkè. Bù jí zhe huí qù shàng bān, jiù zuò zài zhèlǐ duō liáo yīhuìr.
turns.text_en: Give yourself those moments here too... you're right. Maybe this lunchtime is one of those moments. No rush to get back to work — just sit here and chat a while longer.

tree.cultural_note: Yunnan province, with its diverse ethnic cultures and stunning mountain landscapes, holds a special place in Chinese imagination as a retreat from urban pressure. The phrase「有一搭没一搭地聊天」describes casual, unhurried conversation — chatting with no particular purpose, a rhythm increasingly rare in fast-paced city life. Small guesthouses (客栈) in towns like Dali and Lijiang have become symbols of a slower, more contemplative way of living.

tree.setup: You're helping your friend Xiaoli make dumplings at her apartment. It's the Winter Solstice, and she's continuing a family tradition even though she lives far from home.
tree.setup_zh: 冬至这天，你在朋友小丽的公寓里帮她包饺子。虽然她住在离家很远的地方，但还是坚持这个家庭传统。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我妈每年冬至都会包饺子。全家人围在一起，一边包一边聊天，那是我小时候最温暖的记忆。今年虽然只有我们两个人，但我还是想包。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ mā měi nián dōngzhì dōu huì bāo jiǎozi. Quánjiā rén wéi zài yīqǐ, yībiān bāo yībiān liáotiān, nà shì wǒ xiǎo shíhou zuì wēnnuǎn de jìyì. Jīn nián suīrán zhǐ yǒu wǒmen liǎng ge rén, dàn wǒ háishì xiǎng bāo.
turns.text_en: My mom makes dumplings every Winter Solstice. The whole family gathers together, chatting while wrapping. It's my warmest childhood memory. Even though it's just us two this year, I still want to make them.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to Xiaoli about keeping traditions alive.

options.text_zh: 两个人包也很好。传统不在于人多人少，而在于你愿不愿意继续做。你妈妈知道你今天在包饺子的话，一定会很高兴。
options.text_pinyin: Liǎng ge rén bāo yě hěn hǎo. Chuántǒng bú zàiyú rén duō rén shǎo, ér zàiyú nǐ yuàn bú yuànyì jìxù zuò. Nǐ māma zhīdào nǐ jīntiān zài bāo jiǎozi dehuà, yídìng huì hěn gāoxìng.
options.text_en: Making them with just two people is good too. Tradition isn't about how many people are there, but whether you're willing to keep doing it. If your mom knew you were making dumplings today, she'd be really happy.
options.feedback: Warm and reassuring — validates her effort to maintain the tradition.

options.text_zh: 直接买速冻饺子不是更方便吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhíjiē mǎi sùdòng jiǎozi bú shì gèng fāngbiàn ma?
options.text_en: Wouldn't it be easier to just buy frozen dumplings?
options.feedback: She's not making dumplings for convenience.

options.text_zh: 你妈妈包的什么馅儿的？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ māma bāo de shénme xiànr de?
options.text_en: What filling does your mom use?
options.feedback: Not terrible but diverts from the emotional weight of the moment.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我给她发了张照片，她回了一个笑脸，然后又发了一条语音说：「馅儿要多放点姜，别忘了。」每次都是这句话。有些东西虽然重复，但听到就觉得安心。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ gěi tā fā le zhāng zhàopiàn, tā huí le yīge xiàoliǎn, ránhòu yòu fā le yī tiáo yǔyīn shuō: 'Xiànr yào duō fàng diǎn jiāng, bié wàng le.' Měi cì dōu shì zhè jù huà. Yǒuxiē dōngxi suīrán chóngfù, dàn tīng dào jiù juéde ānxīn.
turns.text_en: I sent her a photo. She replied with a smiley face, then sent a voice message: 'Don't forget to add more ginger to the filling.' It's the same thing every time. Some things are repetitive, but hearing them brings comfort.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on the comfort of familiar words.

options.text_zh: 这种重复才是爱的方式。她不是在教你做饭，是在告诉你她还在关心你。不管你在哪里，她心里都留着你的位置。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè zhǒng chóngfù cái shì ài de fāngshì. Tā bú shì zài jiāo nǐ zuòfàn, shì zài gàosù nǐ tā hái zài guānxīn nǐ. Bùguǎn nǐ zài nǎlǐ, tā xīn lǐ dōu liú zhe nǐ de wèizhì.
options.text_en: That kind of repetition is how love works. She's not teaching you to cook — she's telling you she still cares. No matter where you are, she keeps a place for you in her heart.
options.feedback: Insight — decodes the emotional language hidden in everyday reminders.

options.text_zh: 你应该过年回家看看她。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi guò nián huí jiā kànkan tā.
options.text_en: You should go home for New Year's to see her.
options.feedback: Reasonable but generic — misses the beauty of what she's sharing right now.

options.text_zh: 姜放多了不好吃吧？
options.text_pinyin: Jiāng fàng duō le bù hǎochī ba?
options.text_en: Wouldn't too much ginger taste bad?
options.feedback: Takes the advice literally instead of understanding its emotional meaning.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 留着我的位置……嗯。你说到我心里去了。来，我们一起包吧。我教你我妈妈的手法，以后你也可以把这个传统传下去。
turns.text_pinyin: Liú zhe wǒ de wèizhì…… en. Nǐ shuō dào wǒ xīn lǐ qù le. Lái, wǒmen yīqǐ bāo ba. Wǒ jiāo nǐ wǒ māma de shǒufǎ, yǐhòu nǐ yě kěyǐ bǎ zhège chuántǒng chuán xià qù.
turns.text_en: Keeps a place for me... yes. You said exactly what I feel. Come, let's wrap them together. I'll teach you my mom's technique — someday you can pass this tradition on too.

tree.cultural_note: Making dumplings (包饺子) during the Winter Solstice (冬至) is a tradition rooted in northern Chinese culture. The communal act of wrapping dumplings — everyone gathered around the table, hands covered in flour — is as important as eating them. For Chinese people living far from home, recreating these rituals becomes a way of staying connected to family and identity.

tree.setup: You're sitting at your usual corner table in a small café. The owner, Sister Chen, brings you tea without asking — she knows your order by now.
tree.setup_zh: 你坐在一家小咖啡馆里你常坐的角落。老板陈姐不用问就端来了茶——她已经记住了你的习惯。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 老样子，一杯茉莉花茶。你今天看起来比平时安静，是不是有心事？
turns.text_pinyin: Lǎo yàngzi, yī bēi mòlìhuā chá. Nǐ jīntiān kàn qǐlái bǐ píngshí ānjìng, shì bu shì yǒu xīnshì?
turns.text_en: The usual — one jasmine tea. You look quieter than usual today. Is something on your mind?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to Sister Chen's observation.

options.text_zh: 也没什么大事。就是最近总觉得时间过得太快，好像每天都在赶，但又说不清在赶什么。
options.text_pinyin: Yě méi shénme dà shì. Jiù shì zuìjìn zǒng juéde shíjiān guò de tài kuài, hǎoxiàng měi tiān dōu zài gǎn, dàn yòu shuō bù qīng zài gǎn shénme.
options.text_en: Nothing major. I've just been feeling like time passes too fast lately. Like I'm rushing every day, but I can't even say what I'm rushing toward.
options.feedback: Honest and vulnerable — opens up naturally to someone who cares.

options.text_zh: 没有，我很好。今天的茶很香。
options.text_pinyin: Méiyǒu, wǒ hěn hǎo. Jīntiān de chá hěn xiāng.
options.text_en: No, I'm fine. Today's tea is fragrant.
options.feedback: Deflects — closes off a genuine moment of connection.

options.text_zh: 你们这里有WiFi吗？密码多少？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐmen zhèlǐ yǒu WiFi ma? Mìmǎ duōshǎo?
options.text_en: Do you have WiFi here? What's the password?
options.feedback: Ignores her warmth entirely.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 在赶什么都不知道……我以前也是这样。后来我开了这家店，才学会慢下来。你看外面那棵银杏树，现在正在变黄。你今天有没有抬头看过它？
turns.text_pinyin: Zài gǎn shénme dōu bù zhīdào…… wǒ yǐqián yě shì zhèyàng. Hòulái wǒ kāi le zhè jiā diàn, cái xuéhuì màn xiàlái. Nǐ kàn wàimiàn nà kē yínxìng shù, xiànzài zhèng zài biàn huáng. Nǐ jīntiān yǒu méiyǒu táitóu kàn guò tā?
turns.text_en: Don't even know what you're rushing toward... I used to be the same. After I opened this shop, I learned to slow down. Look at the ginkgo tree outside — it's turning yellow right now. Did you look up at it today?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on what Sister Chen is saying.

options.text_zh: 没有……我经过那棵树无数次了，好像从来没有认真看过。也许问题就在这里，我太习惯低着头走路，错过了很多本来就在眼前的东西。
options.text_pinyin: Méiyǒu…… wǒ jīngguò nà kē shù wúshù cì le, hǎoxiàng cónglái méiyǒu rènzhēn kàn guò. Yěxǔ wèntí jiù zài zhèlǐ, wǒ tài xíguàn dī zhe tóu zǒulù, cuòguò le hěn duō běnlái jiù zài yǎnqián de dōngxi.
options.text_en: No... I've walked past that tree countless times, but I don't think I've ever looked at it. Maybe that's the problem — I'm so used to walking with my head down that I miss what's in front of me.
options.feedback: Self-aware and poetic — takes her metaphor and applies it honestly.

options.text_zh: 银杏树的叶子可以泡茶吗？
options.text_pinyin: Yínxìng shù de yèzi kěyǐ pào chá ma?
options.text_en: Can you make tea from ginkgo leaves?
options.feedback: Misses the metaphorical point entirely.

options.text_zh: 我没时间看树，太忙了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ méi shíjiān kàn shù, tài máng le.
options.text_en: I don't have time to look at trees, I'm too busy.
options.feedback: Proves her point but without self-awareness — doubles down on the rushing.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 出去的时候抬头看看吧。慢一秒钟，世界就不一样了。我的茶不急，你也不急。
turns.text_pinyin: Chūqù de shíhou táitóu kànkan ba. Màn yī miǎozhōng, shìjiè jiù bù yíyàng le. Wǒ de chá bù jí, nǐ yě bù jí.
turns.text_en: Look up when you leave. One second slower, and the world looks different. My tea isn't in a hurry, and neither are you.

tree.cultural_note: In many Chinese neighborhoods, small shop owners serve as informal community anchors — they remember regulars' orders, notice when someone seems troubled, and offer quiet wisdom. The ginkgo tree (银杏树) is beloved in China for its golden autumn foliage, and ginkgo-lined streets become pilgrimage sites every fall. Sister Chen's invitation to slow down echoes a growing counter-movement to China's fast-paced urban life.

tree.setup: You're walking through a park with your friend Hao on the first cool day after summer. The leaves are just beginning to change.
tree.setup_zh: 夏天过后第一个凉爽的日子，你和朋友浩在公园里散步。树叶刚开始变色。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你闻到了吗？空气里有一种不一样的味道。夏天的热气突然就散了，好像一夜之间就变成了秋天。每年到这个时候我都会有一种说不清的感觉。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ wén dào le ma? Kōngqì lǐ yǒu yī zhǒng bù yíyàng de wèidào. Xiàtiān de rèqì tūrán jiù sàn le, hǎoxiàng yī yè zhī jiān jiù biàn chéng le qiūtiān. Měi nián dào zhège shíhou wǒ dōu huì yǒu yī zhǒng shuō bù qīng de gǎnjué.
turns.text_en: Can you smell it? There's something different in the air. Summer's heat just suddenly lifted, as if autumn arrived overnight. Every year around this time I get a feeling I can't quite describe.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share how the change of season makes you feel.

options.text_zh: 我也有。有点像轻微的忧伤，但不是难过。也许是因为秋天提醒我们时间在走，什么都留不住。但正因为留不住，所以才觉得珍贵。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yě yǒu. Yǒudiǎn xiàng qīngwēi de yōushāng, dàn bú shì nánguò. Yěxǔ shì yīnwèi qiūtiān tíxǐng wǒmen shíjiān zài zǒu, shénme dōu liú bú zhù. Dàn zhèng yīnwèi liú bú zhù, suǒyǐ cái juéde zhēnguì.
options.text_en: I feel it too. It's like a mild melancholy, but not sadness. Maybe it's because autumn reminds us that time is passing and nothing can be held onto. But precisely because we can't hold on, things feel precious.
options.feedback: Expressed — captures the bittersweet essence of seasonal change.

options.text_zh: 我比较喜欢夏天，可以去游泳。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bǐjiào xǐhuan xiàtiān, kěyǐ qù yóuyǒng.
options.text_en: I prefer summer — you can go swimming.
options.feedback: Misses the reflective mood he's setting.

options.text_zh: 天气预报说下周会更冷。
options.text_pinyin: Tiānqì yùbào shuō xià zhōu huì gèng lěng.
options.text_en: The weather forecast says next week will be colder.
options.feedback: Responds with data to a poetic observation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 珍贵……你说得好。我奶奶以前说，秋天是让人学会放手的季节。树叶落了不是因为树不要它们了，是因为该放下的时候就要放下。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhēnguì…… nǐ shuō de hǎo. Wǒ nǎinai yǐqián shuō, qiūtiān shì ràng rén xuéhuì fàngshǒu de jìjié. Shùyè luò le bú shì yīnwèi shù bú yào tāmen le, shì yīnwèi gāi fàng xià de shíhou jiù yào fàng xià.
turns.text_en: Precious... well said. My grandmother used to say autumn is the season for learning to let go. Leaves fall not because the tree doesn't want them, but because when it's time to release, you release.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the idea of letting go.

options.text_zh: 你奶奶真有智慧。我们总是怕失去，但也许失去也是一种成长。就像树落了叶子才能过冬，来年才会有新的叶子。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ nǎinai zhēn yǒu zhìhuì. Wǒmen zǒng shì pà shīqù, dàn yěxǔ shīqù yě shì yī zhǒng chéngzhǎng. Jiù xiàng shù luò le yèzi cái néng guòdōng, lái nián cái huì yǒu xīn de yèzi.
options.text_en: Your grandmother was truly wise. We're always afraid of losing things, but maybe loss is also a form of growth. Just like a tree sheds its leaves to survive winter, so new ones can come the following year.
options.feedback: Extends the metaphor naturally — connects seasonal wisdom to human experience.

options.text_zh: 你奶奶现在身体怎么样？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ nǎinai xiànzài shēntǐ zěnmeyàng?
options.text_en: How is your grandmother's health now?
options.feedback: Shifts to a personal inquiry instead of engaging with the wisdom shared.

options.text_zh: 秋天的银杏叶很好看，我们去拍照吧。
options.text_pinyin: Qiūtiān de yínxìng yè hěn hǎokàn, wǒmen qù pāi zhào ba.
options.text_en: Ginkgo leaves are beautiful in autumn, let's go take photos.
options.feedback: Jumps to activity planning during a contemplative moment.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 嗯。你看那片叶子，正好落下来了。它不急也不慌，就是慢慢地飘下来。也许我们也可以学它，放下的时候不用那么用力。
turns.text_pinyin: En. Nǐ kàn nà piàn yèzi, zhènghǎo luò xiàlái le. Tā bù jí yě bù huāng, jiù shì mànman de piāo xiàlái. Yěxǔ wǒmen yě kěyǐ xué tā, fàng xià de shíhou bú yòng nàme yòng lì.
turns.text_en: Mm. Look, that leaf just fell. It's not hurried or panicked — it simply drifts down slowly. Maybe we can learn from it. When it's time to let go, we don't have to try so hard.

tree.cultural_note: The concept of 立秋 (lìqiū, Start of Autumn) is one of the 24 solar terms in the traditional Chinese calendar. Chinese culture has a deep tradition of connecting seasonal change to human emotion — autumn is associated with nostalgia, letting go, and quiet reflection. The phrase「悲秋」(bēi qiū, autumn melancholy) appears throughout classical Chinese poetry.

tree.setup: You notice your colleague Xiaogang sitting alone in the break room, staring at his phone. He's been unusually quiet all week and missed the team dinner last night.
tree.setup_zh: 你注意到同事小刚一个人坐在休息室，盯着手机发呆。他这一整个星期都异常沉默，昨晚的团队聚餐也没参加。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 啊，是你。我没事，就是坐一会儿。最近事情有点多，脑子总是转不过来。
turns.text_pinyin: A, shì nǐ. Wǒ méi shì, jiù shì zuò yīhuìr. Zuìjìn shìqíng yǒudiǎn duō, nǎozi zǒng shì zhuǎn bú guòlái.
turns.text_en: Oh, it's you. I'm fine, just sitting for a bit. There's been a lot going on lately, my mind can't keep up.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Gently check in with Xiaogang.

options.text_zh: 不用跟我说没事。我不是来问工作的，就是看你最近不太像平时的你。如果想聊就聊，不想聊我陪你坐一会儿也行。
options.text_pinyin: Bú yòng gēn wǒ shuō méi shì. Wǒ bú shì lái wèn gōngzuò de, jiù shì kàn nǐ zuìjìn bú tài xiàng píngshí de nǐ. Rúguǒ xiǎng liáo jiù liáo, bù xiǎng liáo wǒ péi nǐ zuò yīhuìr yě xíng.
options.text_en: You don't have to tell me you're fine. I'm not here to ask about work — I noticed you haven't seemed like yourself. If you want to talk, we can. If not, I'll sit with you.
options.feedback: Perfect balance of care and space — offers presence without pressure.

options.text_zh: 昨天的聚餐你怎么没来？大家都在问你。
options.text_pinyin: Zuótiān de jùcān nǐ zěnme méi lái? Dàjiā dōu zài wèn nǐ.
options.text_en: How come you didn't come to last night's dinner? Everyone was asking about you.
options.feedback: Adds pressure — implies he owes the group an explanation.

options.text_zh: 你是不是工作压力太大了？要不要跟领导说说？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ shì bu shì gōngzuò yālì tài dà le? Yào bú yào gēn lǐngdǎo shuōshuō?
options.text_en: Are you under too much work pressure? Want to talk to the manager?
options.feedback: Jumps to solutions before understanding the problem — may feel intrusive.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 谢谢……其实不完全是工作的事。我爸最近身体不太好，但他不让我回家，说没什么大事。我在这边什么都做不了，心里很不安。
turns.text_pinyin: Xièxie…… qíshí bù wánquán shì gōngzuò de shì. Wǒ bà zuìjìn shēntǐ bú tài hǎo, dàn tā bú ràng wǒ huí jiā, shuō méi shénme dà shì. Wǒ zài zhè biān shénme dōu zuò bù liǎo, xīn lǐ hěn bù'ān.
turns.text_en: Thanks... it's not entirely about work. My dad hasn't been feeling well lately, but he won't let me come home — says it's nothing serious. I can't do anything from here, and I feel so uneasy.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Support him through his worry about his father.

options.text_zh: 远在外面担心家里人，这种感觉最难受。你爸不让你回去可能是不想让你担心，也可能是不想影响你的工作。但你的感受是真实的，不安就是不安，不用假装没事。
options.text_pinyin: Yuǎn zài wàimiàn dānxīn jiālǐ rén, zhè zhǒng gǎnjué zuì nánshòu. Nǐ bà bú ràng nǐ huí qù kěnéng shì bù xiǎng ràng nǐ dānxīn, yě kěnéng shì bù xiǎng yǐngxiǎng nǐ de gōngzuò. Dàn nǐ de gǎnshòu shì zhēnshí de, bù'ān jiù shì bù'ān, bú yòng jiǎzhuāng méi shì.
options.text_en: Worrying about family from far away — that's one of the hardest feelings. Your dad probably doesn't want you to worry. But your feelings are real. Unease is unease — you don't have to pretend.
options.feedback: Deeply empathetic — names the feeling, validates it, and gives him permission to not be okay.

options.text_zh: 你可以请几天假回去看看。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ qǐng jǐ tiān jiǎ huí qù kànkan.
options.text_en: You could take a few days off and go back to see him.
options.feedback: Jumps to logistics — not what he needs in this moment.

options.text_zh: 你爸多大年纪了？有没有去医院检查？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ bà duō dà niánjì le? Yǒu méiyǒu qù yīyuàn jiǎnchá?
options.text_en: How old is your dad? Has he been to the hospital?
options.feedback: Turns empathy into an interrogation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 谢谢你。能跟人说出来，感觉轻了一点。你不用帮我解决什么，有人愿意听就已经很好了。
turns.text_pinyin: Xièxie nǐ. Néng gēn rén shuō chūlái, gǎnjué qīng le yīdiǎn. Nǐ bú yòng bāng wǒ jiějué shénme, yǒu rén yuànyì tīng jiù yǐjīng hěn hǎo le.
turns.text_en: Thank you. Being able to say it out loud makes it feel a bit lighter. You don't need to solve anything for me — having someone willing to listen is already enough.

tree.cultural_note: In Chinese culture, the phrase「没事」(méi shì, it's nothing) is often used reflexively even when something is clearly wrong — it's a way of not burdening others. Chinese parents, especially fathers, often downplay health concerns to avoid worrying their children who work in distant cities. The guilt of being far from aging parents is one of the most common emotional struggles for China's migrant workforce.

tree.setup: It's Friday afternoon. Your friend Yiyi calls to ask about weekend plans. For once, neither of you has anything scheduled.
tree.setup_zh: 周五下午。朋友一一打电话问你周末有什么安排。这一次，你们两个都没有计划。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 周末你有什么计划？我这次什么都没安排。一开始还有点慌，后来想了想，什么都不做好像也挺好的。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhōumò nǐ yǒu shénme jìhuà? Wǒ zhè cì shénme dōu méi ānpái. Yī kāishǐ hái yǒudiǎn huāng, hòulái xiǎng le xiǎng, shénme dōu bú zuò hǎoxiàng yě tǐng hǎo de.
turns.text_en: Any plans for the weekend? I've got nothing scheduled this time. At first I felt a bit panicked, but then I thought about it — doing nothing might actually be nice.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the idea of an unplanned weekend.

options.text_zh: 什么都不做的周末才是真正的休息。我们平时连周末都排得满满的，好像不做点什么就对不起这两天假。其实有时候最需要的就是空白。
options.text_pinyin: Shénme dōu bú zuò de zhōumò cái shì zhēnzhèng de xiūxi. Wǒmen píngshí lián zhōumò dōu pái de mǎn mǎn de, hǎoxiàng bú zuò diǎn shénme jiù duìbuqǐ zhè liǎng tiān jiǎ. Qíshí yǒu shíhou zuì xūyào de jiù shì kòngbái.
options.text_en: A weekend with nothing to do is real rest. We usually pack even our weekends full, as if we'd be wasting those two days off. But sometimes what we need most is blank space.
options.feedback: Resonant and honest — names the guilt around unproductivity and reclaims it.

options.text_zh: 那我们去逛街吧！好久没去购物了。
options.text_pinyin: Nà wǒmen qù guàng jiē ba! Hǎo jiǔ méi qù gòuwù le.
options.text_en: Then let's go shopping! It's been a while.
options.feedback: Immediately fills the empty space she's trying to appreciate.

options.text_zh: 你可以打扫一下家里，做做家务。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ dǎsǎo yíxià jiālǐ, zuòzuo jiāwù.
options.text_en: You could clean the house and do some chores.
options.feedback: Turns rest into productivity — misses the point.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 空白！对，就是空白。我上次什么都不做是什么时候？好像已经想不起来了。我有点期待明天早上可以自然醒，不设闹钟，醒了就躺着看看窗外。
turns.text_pinyin: Kòngbái! Duì, jiù shì kòngbái. Wǒ shàng cì shénme dōu bú zuò shì shénme shíhou? Hǎoxiàng yǐjīng xiǎng bù qǐlái le. Wǒ yǒudiǎn qīdài míngtiān zǎoshang kěyǐ zìrán xǐng, bú shè nàozhōng, xǐng le jiù tǎng zhe kànkan chuāng wài.
turns.text_en: Blank space! Yes, exactly. When was the last time I did nothing? I can't even remember. I'm a little excited about waking up naturally tomorrow, no alarm, just lying there looking out the window.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Appreciate the simple pleasures she's describing.

options.text_zh: 自然醒，看看窗外——这些听起来很简单，但其实是很奢侈的事。我们太习惯被安排了，反而忘了最简单的快乐。希望明天你的窗外有好看的天空。
options.text_pinyin: Zìrán xǐng, kànkan chuāng wài — zhèxiē tīng qǐlái hěn jiǎndān, dàn qíshí shì hěn shēchǐ de shì. Wǒmen tài xíguàn bèi ānpái le, fǎn'ér wàng le zuì jiǎndān de kuàilè. Xīwàng míngtiān nǐ de chuāng wài yǒu hǎokàn de tiānkōng.
options.text_en: Waking up naturally, looking out the window — these sound simple, but they're actually luxuries. We're so used to being scheduled that we've forgotten the simplest joys. I hope tomorrow your window has a beautiful sky.
options.feedback: Gentle and true — elevates small moments to their deserved importance.

options.text_zh: 你不会无聊吗？我一个人待着就会无聊。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ bú huì wúliáo ma? Wǒ yīge rén dāi zhe jiù huì wúliáo.
options.text_en: Won't you get bored? I get bored when I'm alone.
options.feedback: Projects anxiety about stillness — undermines her attempt to embrace quiet.

options.text_zh: 天气预报说明天有雨。
options.text_pinyin: Tiānqì yùbào shuō míngtiān yǒu yǔ.
options.text_en: The forecast says rain tomorrow.
options.feedback: Mundane weather update during a moment of anticipation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 谢谢你。就算是下雨也没关系，听雨声也很好。周一见的时候我告诉你，我的「什么都不做」过得怎么样。
turns.text_pinyin: Xièxie nǐ. Jiùsuàn shì xià yǔ yě méi guānxi, tīng yǔ shēng yě hěn hǎo. Zhōuyī jiàn de shíhou wǒ gàosù nǐ, wǒ de 'shénme dōu bú zuò' guò de zěnmeyàng.
turns.text_en: Thank you. Even if it rains, that's fine — listening to rain is nice too. When we meet Monday, I'll tell you how my 'doing nothing' went.

tree.cultural_note: The anxiety of unstructured time reflects a broader cultural phenomenon in China, where productivity and busyness are often equated with worth. Young professionals frequently schedule every weekend with social obligations, hobbies, or side projects. The idea of deliberately doing nothing (发呆, fādāi) is gaining traction as a counterbalance, with some cities even hosting「发呆大赛」(staring into space competitions) to celebrate the art of doing nothing.

tree.setup: Your colleague Lao Wang just gave a brilliant presentation that clearly took weeks of preparation. You try to compliment him. He will not accept it. The denial will escalate far beyond anything reasonable.
tree.setup_zh: 你的同事老王刚做了一个非常精彩的演讲，明显准备了好几周。你想夸他。他不会接受的。否认的程度会远远超出合理范围。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 哎呀，终于讲完了。刚才PPT第三页的数据出了点问题，你注意到了吗？我昨天太晚了没来得及检查。
turns.text_pinyin: Āiyā, zhōngyú jiǎng wán le. Gāngcái PPT dì sān yè de shùjù chū le diǎn wèntí, nǐ zhùyì dào le ma? Wǒ zuótiān tài wǎn le méi láidejí jiǎnchá.
turns.text_en: Oh, finally done. Did you notice the data issue on slide three? I was up too late last night and didn't have time to check.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Nobody noticed anything on slide three. He's preemptively deflecting a compliment you haven't even given yet.

options.text_zh: 第三页？没人注意到。老王，说实话，整个演讲非常流畅，数据也很有说服力。你准备了很久吧？
options.text_pinyin: Dì sān yè? Méi rén zhùyì dào. Lǎo Wáng, shuō shíhuà, zhěnggè yǎnjiǎng fēicháng liúchàng, shùjù yě hěn yǒu shuōfúlì. Nǐ zhǔnbèi le hěn jiǔ ba?
options.text_en: Slide three? Nobody noticed. Lao Wang, honestly, the whole presentation was very smooth, and the data was convincing. You must have prepared for a long time?
options.feedback: You've dismissed his preemptive self-criticism and delivered the compliment directly. Now the real game begins.

options.text_zh: 是吗？我没注意到第三页有问题。下次可以多检查一下。
options.text_pinyin: Shì ma? Wǒ méi zhùyì dào dì sān yè yǒu wèntí. Xià cì kěyǐ duō jiǎnchá yīxià.
options.text_en: Really? I didn't notice any issue on slide three. You could check more carefully next time.
options.feedback: He invented a flaw, and you've offered improvement advice for it. He was fishing for reassurance, not a performance review.

options.text_zh: 辛苦了，赶紧休息一下吧。
options.text_pinyin: Xīnkǔ le, gǎnjǐn xiūxi yīxià ba.
options.text_en: You worked hard, get some rest.
options.feedback: Acknowledging effort without acknowledging quality. He heard 'you tried hard,' not 'you were good.'

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 准备了很久？没有没有，就随便弄的。主要是领导给的方向好，我就是把数据整理了一下而已。任何人拿到这些数据都能讲得比我好。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhǔnbèi le hěn jiǔ? Méiyǒu méiyǒu, jiù suíbiàn nòng de. Zhǔyào shì lǐngdǎo gěi de fāngxiàng hǎo, wǒ jiùshì bǎ shùjù zhěnglǐ le yīxià éryǐ. Rènhé rén ná dào zhèxiē shùjù dōu néng jiǎng de bǐ wǒ hǎo.
turns.text_en: Prepared for a long time? No no, I just threw it together. The direction from leadership was good, I just organized the data a bit. Anyone with this data could have presented it better than me.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: He's credited leadership, minimized his own work, and claimed anyone could do better. The deflection is escalating.

options.text_zh: 老王，你刚才说'随便弄的'，但我看到你上周每天加班到九点。你'随便弄'的标准好像比大多数人认真做的标准还高。
options.text_pinyin: Lǎo Wáng, nǐ gāngcái shuō 'suíbiàn nòng de', dàn wǒ kàn dào nǐ shàng zhōu měi tiān jiābān dào jiǔ diǎn. Nǐ 'suíbiàn nòng' de biāozhǔn hǎoxiàng bǐ dàduōshù rén rènzhēn zuò de biāozhǔn hái gāo.
options.text_en: Lao Wang, you just said you 'threw it together,' but I saw you working overtime until nine every day last week. Your standard for 'throwing it together' seems higher than most people's standard for serious work.
options.feedback: You've used evidence to pin down the deflection. He can't deny working late when you literally saw him. Now he has to find a new escape route.

options.text_zh: 也是，领导的方向确实定得好。你执行得也不错。
options.text_pinyin: Yě shì, lǐngdǎo de fāngxiàng quèshí dìng de hǎo. Nǐ zhíxíng de yě búcuò.
options.text_en: True, leadership did set a good direction. You executed it well too.
options.feedback: You've accepted his reframing. He's the executor now, not the creator. The compliment has been downgraded from 'brilliant' to 'competent.'

options.text_zh: 别谦虚了，大家都觉得你讲得好。
options.text_pinyin: Bié qiānxū le, dàjiā dōu juéde nǐ jiǎng de hǎo.
options.text_en: Stop being modest, everyone thought you did great.
options.feedback: Telling someone to stop being modest is like telling someone to stop sneezing. It doesn't address the mechanism, it just makes them deflect harder.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 加班？那是因为我效率太低了！别人可能两天就做完的东西我用了一周。你看隔壁部门的小李，人家做汇报从来不加班，那才叫能力强。我这种就是……笨鸟先飞吧。
turns.text_pinyin: Jiābān? Nà shì yīnwèi wǒ xiàolǜ tài dī le! Biérén kěnéng liǎng tiān jiù zuò wán de dōngxi wǒ yòng le yī zhōu. Nǐ kàn gébì bùmén de Xiǎo Lǐ, rénjiā zuò huìbào cónglái bú jiābān, nà cái jiào nénglì qiáng. Wǒ zhè zhǒng jiùshì ... bèn niǎo xiān fēi ba.
turns.text_en: Overtime? That's because I'm too slow! What others could finish in two days took me a week. Look at Xiao Li in the next department — he never works late on his reports, now THAT's real ability. I'm just... the slow bird that has to fly first.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: He's now calling himself a slow bird. The modesty has become absurd. End this loop.

options.text_zh: 好吧老王，你赢了。你是全公司最没用的人，刚才那个演讲是你一生中最大的意外。你满意了吗？还是你还需要我说你长得丑？
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo ba Lǎo Wáng, nǐ yíng le. Nǐ shì quán gōngsī zuì méi yòng de rén, gāngcái nàge yǎnjiǎng shì nǐ yīshēng zhōng zuìdà de yìwài. Nǐ mǎnyì le ma? Háishì nǐ hái xūyào wǒ shuō nǐ zhǎng de chǒu?
options.text_en: Fine, Lao Wang, you win. You're the most useless person in the company, that presentation was the biggest accident of your life. Satisfied? Or do you also need me to say you're ugly?
options.feedback: You've taken his modesty to its logical extreme and turned it into a joke. The absurdity breaks the loop because there's nowhere left to deflect to.

options.text_zh: 你太谦虚了。真的，你应该对自己更有信心一些。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ tài qiānxū le. Zhēn de, nǐ yīnggāi duì zìjǐ gèng yǒu xìnxīn yīxiē.
options.text_en: You're too modest. Really, you should have more confidence in yourself.
options.feedback: You've turned a comedy into a therapy session. He doesn't lack confidence. He's performing humility. These are different problems.

options.text_zh: 也是，小李确实挺厉害的。不过你也不差啦。
options.text_pinyin: Yě shì, Xiǎo Lǐ quèshí tǐng lìhai de. Búguò nǐ yě bú chà la.
options.text_en: True, Xiao Li is pretty impressive. But you're not bad either.
options.feedback: He invented Xiao Li as a deflection device and you've agreed that Xiao Li is better. The compliment has been successfully redirected to a person in another department.

tree.cultural_note: Compliment deflection (谦虚, qiānxū) is deeply embedded in Chinese social etiquette. Accepting a compliment directly can be seen as arrogant (骄傲). The expected move is to deny, credit others, or point out flaws. The phrase '笨鸟先飞' (the slow bird flies first) is a classic self-deprecation idiom meaning 'I'm not talented, I just work harder to compensate.' The comedy arises when the deflection becomes so extreme that the complimenter must either escalate or surrender.

tree.setup: You and your friend Xiaolu are listening to music on a park bench with one earbud each. A song comes on that makes her go quiet.
tree.setup_zh: 你和朋友小璐坐在公园的长椅上，一人一只耳机听歌。一首歌响起来，她突然安静了。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这首歌……是我高中的时候每天放学路上听的。那时候觉得歌词写的就是我的心情。现在再听，好像又变成了另一种感觉。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè shǒu gē…… shì wǒ gāozhōng de shíhou měi tiān fàngxué lù shang tīng de. Nà shíhou juéde gēcí xiě de jiù shì wǒ de xīnqíng. Xiànzài zài tīng, hǎoxiàng yòu biàn chéng le lìng yī zhǒng gǎnjué.
turns.text_en: This song... I used to listen to it every day walking home from school in high school. Back then I felt like the lyrics described exactly how I felt. Listening now, it seems to have become something different.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Ask about how the song feels different now.

options.text_zh: 同一首歌，不同的年纪听会有不同的感受。也许不是歌变了，是你变了。你现在听的时候在想什么？
options.text_pinyin: Tóng yī shǒu gē, bùtóng de niánjì tīng huì yǒu bùtóng de gǎnshòu. Yěxǔ bú shì gē biàn le, shì nǐ biàn le. Nǐ xiànzài tīng de shíhou zài xiǎng shénme?
options.text_en: The same song hits differently at different ages. Maybe it's not the song that changed, but you. What are you thinking about when you listen to it now?
options.feedback: Perceptive and gentle — opens space for her to reflect.

options.text_zh: 这是谁唱的？我想加到我的歌单里。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè shì shéi chàng de? Wǒ xiǎng jiā dào wǒ de gēdān lǐ.
options.text_en: Who sings this? I want to add it to my playlist.
options.feedback: Practical question that skips over her emotional moment.

options.text_zh: 你高中的时候成绩好吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ gāozhōng de shíhou chéngjì hǎo ma?
options.text_en: Were your grades good in high school?
options.feedback: Irrelevant tangent.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我在想那时候的自己。十七岁，什么都不确定，但什么都敢期待。现在确定的事多了，反而没有那种期待了。听这首歌就像在跟过去的自己说话。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ zài xiǎng nà shíhou de zìjǐ. Shíqī suì, shénme dōu bú quèdìng, dàn shénme dōu gǎn qīdài. Xiànzài quèdìng de shì duō le, fǎn'ér méiyǒu nà zhǒng qīdài le. Tīng zhè shǒu gē jiù xiàng zài gēn guòqù de zìjǐ shuōhuà.
turns.text_en: I'm thinking about who I was then. Seventeen, nothing certain, but willing to hope for everything. Now there's more certainty, but less expectation. Listening to this song feels like talking to my past self.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share a thought about music and memory.

options.text_zh: 音乐就是这样，它能保存那些文字说不清的感觉。你十七岁的自己听到了你现在的心情，也许她会觉得长大以后的你比她想象的更勇敢。
options.text_pinyin: Yīnyuè jiù shì zhèyàng, tā néng bǎocún nàxiē wénzì shuō bù qīng de gǎnjué. Nǐ shíqī suì de zìjǐ tīng dào le nǐ xiànzài de xīnqíng, yěxǔ tā huì juéde zhǎng dà yǐhòu de nǐ bǐ tā xiǎngxiàng de gèng yǒnggǎn.
options.text_en: That's what music does — it preserves feelings that words can't capture. If your seventeen-year-old self could hear your heart now, maybe she'd think the grown-up you is braver than she imagined.
options.feedback: And affirming — bridges past and present through the shared thread of music.

options.text_zh: 你现在还听流行歌曲吗？我比较喜欢古典音乐。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ xiànzài hái tīng liúxíng gēqǔ ma? Wǒ bǐjiào xǐhuan gǔdiǎn yīnyuè.
options.text_en: Do you still listen to pop music? I prefer classical.
options.feedback: Pivots to genre preferences during a vulnerable moment.

options.text_zh: 不要想太多了，就是一首歌而已。
options.text_pinyin: Bú yào xiǎng tài duō le, jiù shì yī shǒu gē éryǐ.
options.text_en: Don't overthink it, it's just a song.
options.feedback: Dismissive — shuts down a meaningful reflection.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 更勇敢……谢谢你。我觉得长大不是失去了期待，而是期待变得不一样了。来，下一首换你选，让我听一首属于你的歌。
turns.text_pinyin: Gèng yǒnggǎn…… xièxie nǐ. Wǒ juéde zhǎng dà bú shì shīqù le qīdài, ér shì qīdài biàn de bù yíyàng le. Lái, xià yī shǒu huàn nǐ xuǎn, ràng wǒ tīng yī shǒu shǔyú nǐ de gē.
turns.text_en: Braver... thank you. I think growing up isn't losing hope — it's that hope changes shape. Come on, you pick the next song. Let me hear one that belongs to you.

tree.cultural_note: Sharing earbuds (一人一只耳机) to listen to music together is a common intimate gesture among young Chinese friends and couples. Chinese pop music from different eras carries strong generational markers, and revisiting old songs often triggers powerful nostalgia. The phrase「跟过去的自己说话」(talking to your past self) reflects a popular theme in Chinese social media culture, where people reflect on their younger selves with a mix of tenderness and wistfulness.

title: The Little Place Around the Corner

tree.setup: You and your colleague Lao Zhou are trying to decide where to eat lunch. He mentions a small family-run restaurant that most people walk past without noticing.
tree.setup_zh: 你和同事老周在决定中午去哪儿吃。他提到了一家不起眼的家庭小餐馆。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我带你去一个地方。就在转角那条巷子里，没有招牌，门口只有一个小黑板写着今天的菜。老板夫妻俩自己做，每天就做二十份，卖完就关门。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ dài nǐ qù yīge dìfang. Jiù zài zhuǎnjiǎo nà tiáo xiàngzi lǐ, méiyǒu zhāopái, ménkǒu zhǐ yǒu yīge xiǎo hēibǎn xiě zhe jīntiān de cài. Lǎobǎn fūqī liǎ zìjǐ zuò, měi tiān jiù zuò èrshí fèn, mài wán jiù guānmén.
turns.text_en: Let me take you somewhere. It's in the alley around the corner — no sign, just a small blackboard at the door with today's menu. The husband and wife cook everything themselves, only twenty servings a day, and they close when it's sold out.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: React to his description of the restaurant.

options.text_zh: 只做二十份就关门？这种店才有意思。不是为了赚更多，而是做好自己能做到的量。这种态度在现在很少见了。
options.text_pinyin: Zhǐ zuò èrshí fèn jiù guānmén? Zhè zhǒng diàn cái yǒu yìsi. Bú shì wèile zhuàn gèng duō, ér shì zuò hǎo zìjǐ néng zuòdào de liàng. Zhè zhǒng tàidù zài xiànzài hěn shǎo jiàn le.
options.text_en: Only twenty servings and then they close? That's the kind of place I find interesting. Not about making more money, but about doing well within their capacity. That attitude is rare these days.
options.feedback: Appreciative — values quality and intention over scale.

options.text_zh: 没有招牌的店卫生条件怎么样？
options.text_pinyin: Méiyǒu zhāopái de diàn wèishēng tiáojiàn zěnmeyàng?
options.text_en: What are the hygiene conditions like at a place with no sign?
options.feedback: Practical concern that misses the charm he's describing.

options.text_zh: 我们去那个新开的连锁店吧，有优惠券。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒmen qù nàge xīn kāi de liánsuǒ diàn ba, yǒu yōuhuìquàn.
options.text_en: Let's go to that new chain restaurant, I have a coupon.
options.feedback: Chooses convenience over the experience he's offering.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 老板姓刘，大家叫他刘叔。他以前在大酒店当厨师，退休以后跟老婆开了这个小店。他说大酒店做的菜好看但没有灵魂，他想做自己真正想吃的东西。
turns.text_pinyin: Lǎobǎn xìng Liú, dàjiā jiào tā Liú shū. Tā yǐqián zài dà jiǔdiàn dāng chúshī, tuìxiū yǐhòu gēn lǎopo kāi le zhège xiǎo diàn. Tā shuō dà jiǔdiàn zuò de cài hǎokàn dàn méiyǒu línghún, tā xiǎng zuò zìjǐ zhēnzhèng xiǎng chī de dōngxi.
turns.text_en: The owner's surname is Liu, everyone calls him Uncle Liu. He used to be a chef at a big hotel. After retiring, he opened this little place with his wife. He says hotel food looks beautiful but has no soul — he wanted to make what he truly wants to eat.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to Uncle Liu's philosophy about food.

options.text_zh: 有灵魂的菜——我喜欢这个说法。大酒店的菜是给别人吃的，这里的菜是给自己做的。你能吃出来那种差别。走吧，我已经很期待了。
options.text_pinyin: Yǒu línghún de cài — wǒ xǐhuan zhège shuōfǎ. Dà jiǔdiàn de cài shì gěi biéren chī de, zhèlǐ de cài shì gěi zìjǐ zuò de. Nǐ néng chī chūlái nà zhǒng chābié. Zǒu ba, wǒ yǐjīng hěn qīdài le.
options.text_en: Food with soul — I love that way of putting it. Hotel food is made for others; the food here is made for themselves. You can taste the difference. Let's go, I'm already looking forward to it.
options.feedback: Enthusiastic and thoughtful — picks up on the philosophy behind the cooking.

options.text_zh: 退休了还开店？不累吗？
options.text_pinyin: Tuìxiū le hái kāi diàn? Bú lèi ma?
options.text_en: Opening a shop after retiring? Isn't that tiring?
options.feedback: Sees it as labor rather than passion.

options.text_zh: 二十份会不会不够？我们去晚了怎么办？
options.text_pinyin: Èrshí fèn huì bú huì bú gòu? Wǒmen qù wǎn le zěnme bàn?
options.text_en: Will twenty servings be enough? What if we get there late?
options.feedback: Practical but misses the soul of the conversation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 走！今天是周四，刘叔周四做酸菜鱼。他每次看到有人吃得干干净净，就站在厨房门口偷偷笑。那种笑容你在大酒店里看不到。
turns.text_pinyin: Zǒu! Jīntiān shì zhōusì, Liú shū zhōusì zuò suāncài yú. Tā měi cì kàn dào yǒu rén chī de gāngān jìngjìng, jiù zhàn zài chúfáng ménkǒu tōutōu xiào. Nà zhǒng xiàoróng nǐ zài dà jiǔdiàn lǐ kàn bú dào.
turns.text_en: Let's go! Today's Thursday — Uncle Liu makes pickled cabbage fish on Thursdays. Every time he sees someone finish every last bite, he stands at the kitchen door and smiles to himself. That kind of smile you'll never see in a big hotel.

tree.cultural_note: Small, family-run restaurants with no signage (无招牌小店) are a beloved part of Chinese food culture. They rely entirely on word-of-mouth and repeat customers. The concept of「有灵魂的菜」(food with soul) resonates deeply — many Chinese food enthusiasts believe the best meals come from cooks who care more about the food than the profit, a sentiment captured in food documentaries and social media food culture.

tree.setup: You run into your friend Xiaofang at a pharmacy. She's buying sleep supplements and looks tired.
tree.setup_zh: 你在药店遇到朋友小芳。她在买助眠保健品，看起来很疲惫。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 别笑我，我已经连续一个月睡不好了。不是睡不着，是躺在床上以后脑子停不下来。想明天的会议，想上周犯的错，想下个月的计划……越想越清醒。
turns.text_pinyin: Bié xiào wǒ, wǒ yǐjīng liánxù yīge yuè shuì bù hǎo le. Bú shì shuì bù zháo, shì tǎng zài chuáng shàng yǐhòu nǎozi tíng bú xiàlái. Xiǎng míngtiān de huìyì, xiǎng shàng zhōu fàn de cuò, xiǎng xià ge yuè de jìhuà…… yuè xiǎng yuè qīngxǐng.
turns.text_en: Don't laugh at me — I haven't slept well for a month straight. It's not that I can't fall asleep, it's that my mind won't stop after I lie down. Tomorrow's meeting, last week's mistake, next month's plans... the more I think, the more awake I get.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond with understanding to her sleep struggles.

options.text_zh: 我不会笑你。很多人都有这个问题，只是不说出来。脑子停不下来说明你白天一直在紧绷，到了晚上身体放松了，但心还没有放下来。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bú huì xiào nǐ. Hěn duō rén dōu yǒu zhège wèntí, zhǐ shì bù shuō chūlái. Nǎozi tíng bú xiàlái shuōmíng nǐ báitiān yīzhí zài jǐnbēng, dào le wǎnshang shēntǐ fàngsōng le, dàn xīn hái méiyǒu fàng xiàlái.
options.text_en: I won't laugh. Many people have this problem, they just don't talk about it. Your mind not stopping means you've been tense all day — at night your body relaxes, but your heart hasn't let go yet.
options.feedback: Empathetic and insightful — normalizes her experience and names the root cause.

options.text_zh: 你试过喝牛奶吗？睡前喝一杯热牛奶很有效。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ shì guò hē niúnǎi ma? Shuì qián hē yī bēi rè niúnǎi hěn yǒuxiào.
options.text_en: Have you tried drinking milk? A cup of warm milk before bed is really effective.
options.feedback: Generic advice — skips the emotional content.

options.text_zh: 你应该少看手机，蓝光对睡眠不好。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi shǎo kàn shǒujī, lánguāng duì shuìmián bù hǎo.
options.text_en: You should use your phone less — blue light is bad for sleep.
options.feedback: Lecturing — she knows the usual advice; she needs understanding.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 心还没有放下来……对，就是这种感觉。我好像已经不会休息了。就算躺着，心里也在工作。有时候我觉得累不是因为做得太多，而是因为一直没有真正停下来过。
turns.text_pinyin: Xīn hái méiyǒu fàng xiàlái…… duì, jiù shì zhè zhǒng gǎnjué. Wǒ hǎoxiàng yǐjīng bú huì xiūxi le. Jiùsuàn tǎng zhe, xīn lǐ yě zài gōngzuò. Yǒu shíhou wǒ juéde lèi bú shì yīnwèi zuò de tài duō, ér shì yīnwèi yīzhí méiyǒu zhēnzhèng tíng xiàlái guò.
turns.text_en: The heart hasn't let go... yes, exactly. I think I've forgotten how to rest. Even lying down, my mind is still working. Sometimes I feel tired not because I do too much, but because I've never truly stopped.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share a thought about what real rest means.

options.text_zh: 也许真正的休息不是什么都不做，而是允许自己不用什么都想清楚。你不需要在睡觉之前把所有问题都解决。明天的事明天再说，今晚只需要呼吸就好。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ zhēnzhèng de xiūxi bú shì shénme dōu bú zuò, ér shì yǔnxǔ zìjǐ bú yòng shénme dōu xiǎng qīngchǔ. Nǐ bù xūyào zài shuìjiào zhīqián bǎ suǒyǒu wèntí dōu jiějué. Míngtiān de shì míngtiān zài shuō, jīn wǎn zhǐ xūyào hūxī jiù hǎo.
options.text_en: Maybe real rest isn't doing nothing, but allowing yourself not to figure everything out. You don't need to solve all problems before bed. Tomorrow's matters can wait for tomorrow. Tonight, you just need to breathe.
options.feedback: Calming and practical — redefines rest as permission rather than technique.

options.text_zh: 要不你去看医生吧，可能需要吃药。
options.text_pinyin: Yào bù nǐ qù kàn yīshēng ba, kěnéng xūyào chī yào.
options.text_en: Maybe you should see a doctor, you might need medication.
options.feedback: Jumps to medicalization — not what she needs in this conversation.

options.text_zh: 我每天睡得很好，一躺下就睡着了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ měi tiān shuì de hěn hǎo, yī tǎng xià jiù shuì zháo le.
options.text_en: I sleep great every day, I fall asleep as soon as I lie down.
options.feedback: Insensitive — makes it about yourself when she's struggling.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 今晚只需要呼吸就好……嗯，我试试。谢谢你，你说的比这些保健品有用多了。
turns.text_pinyin: Jīn wǎn zhǐ xūyào hūxī jiù hǎo…… en, wǒ shìshì. Xièxie nǐ, nǐ shuō de bǐ zhèxiē bǎojiàn pǐn yǒuyòng duō le.
turns.text_en: Tonight, just breathe... okay, I'll try. Thank you — what you said is more useful than any of these supplements.

tree.cultural_note: Sleep problems are increasingly common among young Chinese professionals, driven by work pressure and constant digital connectivity. The phrase「内卷」(nèijuǎn, involution/rat race) captures the sense of endless competition that makes people unable to stop working even mentally. Sleep supplements (助眠保健品) have become a booming market in China, but many people recognize that the root issue is psychological rather than physical.

tree.setup: You and your friend Xiaochen are waiting for the subway. She takes out her earbuds and wants to share something she heard on a podcast this morning.
tree.setup_zh: 你和朋友小陈在等地铁。她摘下耳机，想跟你分享今天早上在播客里听到的一段话。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 今天早上听了一个播客，主持人说了一句话我一直在想：「我们花了太多时间让别人满意，却忘了问自己满不满意。」你觉得是这样吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Jīntiān zǎoshang tīng le yīge bōkè, zhǔchírén shuō le yī jù huà wǒ yīzhí zài xiǎng: 'Wǒmen huā le tài duō shíjiān ràng biéren mǎnyì, què wàng le wèn zìjǐ mǎn bù mǎnyì.' Nǐ juéde shì zhèyàng ma?
turns.text_en: I listened to a podcast this morning, and the host said something I can't stop thinking about: 'We spend too much time making others happy, and forget to ask if we're happy ourselves.' Do you think that's true?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share your reaction to the quote.

options.text_zh: 确实很多人是这样。从小就被教着要懂事、要考虑别人的感受，时间长了连自己想要什么都不清楚了。关键不是不关心别人，而是别忘了自己也需要被关心。
options.text_pinyin: Quèshí hěn duō rén shì zhèyàng. Cóng xiǎo jiù bèi jiāo zhe yào dǒngshì, yào kǎolǜ biéren de gǎnshòu, shíjiān cháng le lián zìjǐ xiǎng yào shénme dōu bù qīngchǔ le. Guānjiàn bú shì bù guānxīn biéren, ér shì bié wàng le zìjǐ yě xūyào bèi guānxīn.
options.text_en: A lot of people are like that. We're taught from childhood to be sensible and consider others, and over time you lose track of what you want. The point isn't to stop caring about others, but to not forget you need caring too.
options.feedback: Nuanced and thoughtful — avoids the trap of simple agreement and adds depth.

options.text_zh: 你听什么播客？推荐一下。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ tīng shénme bōkè? Tuījiàn yíxià.
options.text_en: What podcast do you listen to? Recommend it.
options.feedback: Skips the meaningful discussion to ask about the platform.

options.text_zh: 我不太听播客，我觉得太长了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bú tài tīng bōkè, wǒ juéde tài cháng le.
options.text_en: I don't really listen to podcasts, I think they're too long.
options.feedback: Dismisses the medium instead of engaging with the idea.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 不要忘了自己也需要被关心……你说得好。我最近就在学这个。以前同事让我帮忙，我从来不拒绝，哪怕自己已经忙不过来了。现在我在练习说「不」，虽然每次说完都觉得不好意思。
turns.text_pinyin: Bú yào wàng le zìjǐ yě xūyào bèi guānxīn…… nǐ shuō de hǎo. Wǒ zuìjìn jiù zài xué zhège. Yǐqián tóngshì ràng wǒ bāngmáng, wǒ cónglái bù jùjué, nǎpà zìjǐ yǐjīng máng bú guòlái le. Xiànzài wǒ zài liànxí shuō 'bù', suīrán měi cì shuō wán dōu juéde bù hǎoyìsi.
turns.text_en: Don't forget you need caring too... well said. I've been learning this lately. Before, whenever a colleague asked for help, I never refused, even when I was already overwhelmed. Now I'm practicing saying 'no,' though I feel awkward every time.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Encourage her practice of setting boundaries.

options.text_zh: 说「不」不是自私，是自我保护。你已经迈出了最难的那一步。慢慢来，不用一下子做到完美。照顾好自己也是对别人负责，耗尽的人帮不了任何人。
options.text_pinyin: Shuō "bù" bú shì zìsī, shì zìwǒ bǎohù. Nǐ yǐjīng mài chū le zuì nán de nà yī bù. Mànman lái, bú yòng yíxiàzi zuòdào wánměi. Zhàogù hǎo zìjǐ yě shì duì biéren fùzé, hào jìn de rén bāng bù liǎo rènhé rén.
options.text_en: Saying "no" isn't selfish — it's self-protection. You've already taken the hardest step. Take it slow, no need to be perfect right away. Taking care of yourself is being responsible to others too — a depleted person can't help anyone.
options.feedback: Affirming and logical — reframes boundary-setting as responsible rather than selfish.

options.text_zh: 你不好意思说不的话，可以发微信拒绝，这样就不用面对面了。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ bù hǎoyìsi shuō bù dehuà, kěyǐ fā Wēixìn jùjué, zhèyàng jiù bú yòng miàn duì miàn le.
options.text_en: If you feel awkward saying no in person, you could refuse by WeChat so you don't have to face them.
options.feedback: Avoidance technique — doesn't address the root issue.

options.text_zh: 同事之间互相帮忙很正常啊，不要太计较。
options.text_pinyin: Tóngshì zhī jiān hùxiāng bāngmáng hěn zhèngcháng a, bú yào tài jìjiào.
options.text_en: Helping colleagues out is normal, don't be too calculating.
options.feedback: Undermining — invalidates her effort to set healthy boundaries.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 一个耗尽的人帮不了任何人……这句话我要记住。谢谢你，每次跟你聊天都比听播客还有用。
turns.text_pinyin: Yīge hào jìn de rén bāng bù liǎo rènhé rén…… zhè jù huà wǒ yào jìzhù. Xièxie nǐ, měi cì gēn nǐ liáotiān dōu bǐ tīng bōkè hái yǒuyòng.
turns.text_en: A depleted person can't help anyone... I need to remember that. Thank you — every conversation with you is more useful than any podcast.

tree.cultural_note: Podcasts (播客) have grown rapidly in China, especially among urban professionals seeking thoughtful content during commutes. The concept of「懂事」(dǒngshì, being sensible/considerate) is deeply ingrained in Chinese upbringing — children are praised for putting others' needs first. This cultural expectation makes boundary-setting particularly challenging, as saying 'no' can feel like a breach of social harmony (和谐, héxié).

title: The Courtyard We Grew Up In

tree.setup: You and your childhood friend Xiaopeng happen to pass by the old neighborhood where you both grew up. The buildings are still there, but everything looks different.
tree.setup_zh: 你和发小小鹏碰巧路过你们小时候住的老小区。楼还在，但一切看起来都不一样了。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看，那个就是我们以前踢球的空地。现在变成停车场了。那时候觉得那块地好大好大，现在看其实就那么一小块。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn, nàge jiù shì wǒmen yǐqián tī qiú de kòngdì. Xiànzài biàn chéng tíngchēchǎng le. Nà shíhou juéde nà kuài dì hǎo dà hǎo dà, xiànzài kàn qíshí jiù nàme yī xiǎo kuài.
turns.text_en: Look, that's the open lot where we used to play soccer. It's a parking lot now. Back then it seemed so huge, but looking at it now, it's actually just a small patch.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share your feelings about revisiting the old neighborhood.

options.text_zh: 小时候什么都显得大，因为我们那么小。不只是这块空地，连回家的路都觉得特别长。现在走一遍，三分钟就到了。也许不是地方变小了，是我们长大了。
options.text_pinyin: Xiǎo shíhou shénme dōu xiǎn de dà, yīnwèi wǒmen nàme xiǎo. Bù zhǐ shì zhè kuài kòngdì, lián huí jiā de lù dōu juéde tèbié cháng. Xiànzài zǒu yī biàn, sān fēnzhōng jiù dào le. Yěxǔ bú shì dìfang biàn xiǎo le, shì wǒmen zhǎng dà le.
options.text_en: Everything seemed big when we were small, because we were so little. Even the walk home felt so long. Now I can walk it in three minutes. Maybe the place didn't shrink — we grew up.
options.feedback: Nostalgic and true — captures the shift in perspective that comes with growing up.

options.text_zh: 停车场挺方便的，这附近确实缺停车位。
options.text_pinyin: Tíngchēchǎng tǐng fāngbiàn de, zhè fùjìn quèshí quē tíngchē wèi.
options.text_en: A parking lot is convenient, this area really lacks parking spots.
options.feedback: Responds pragmatically to a nostalgic moment.

options.text_zh: 你小时候踢球踢得好吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ xiǎo shíhou tī qiú tī de hǎo ma?
options.text_en: Were you good at soccer when you were a kid?
options.feedback: Sidesteps the emotional weight of returning to this place.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 长大了……是啊。你记不记得那个小卖部的阿姨？我们每天放学都去买五毛钱的冰棍。她总是多给我们一根，说天热，多吃点。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhǎng dà le…… shì a. Nǐ jì bú jìde nàge xiǎomàibù de āyí? Wǒmen měi tiān fàngxué dōu qù mǎi wǔ máo qián de bīnggùn. Tā zǒng shì duō gěi wǒmen yī gēn, shuō tiān rè, duō chī diǎn.
turns.text_en: Grew up... yeah. Do you remember the lady at the corner shop? We'd buy fifty-cent popsicles every day after school. She always gave us an extra one, saying it's hot out, have some more.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on those small kindnesses.

options.text_zh: 我记得。那时候觉得那根多给的冰棍是全世界最好的东西。现在想想，那些小小的善意才是真正让童年温暖的东西。不是什么大事，就是有人愿意对你好一点。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ jìde. Nà shíhou juéde nà gēn duō gěi de bīnggùn shì quán shìjiè zuì hǎo de dōngxi. Xiànzài xiǎngxiǎng, nàxiē xiǎo xiǎo de shànyì cái shì zhēnzhèng ràng tóngnián wēnnuǎn de dōngxi. Bú shì shénme dà shì, jiù shì yǒu rén yuànyì duì nǐ hǎo yīdiǎn.
options.text_en: Back then that extra popsicle felt like the best thing in the world. Now I realize those small kindnesses truly made childhood warm. Nothing big — someone willing to be a little extra good to you.
options.feedback: Tender and wise — connects childhood memory to a universal truth about kindness.

options.text_zh: 现在的小孩都不吃冰棍了，都吃那种贵的冰淇淋了。时代不一样了。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài de xiǎohái dōu bù chī bīnggùn le, dōu chī nà zhǒng guì de bīngqílín le. Shídài bù yíyàng le.
options.text_en: Kids nowadays don't eat popsicles anymore — they all eat those fancy ice creams. Times have changed.
options.feedback: Xiaopeng is remembering a woman's small generosity, and you pivot to a generational comparison. The point isn't what kids eat now — it's what that extra popsicle meant.

options.text_zh: 那个小卖部还在吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nàge xiǎomàibù hái zài ma?
options.text_en: Is that corner shop still there?
options.feedback: Not terrible but focuses on the place rather than the feeling.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 有人愿意对你好一点……嗯。我们长大以后，好像越来越难做到这一点了。也许我们应该试试，做别人的那个「多给一根冰棍」的人。
turns.text_pinyin: Yǒu rén yuànyì duì nǐ hǎo yīdiǎn…… en. Wǒmen zhǎng dà yǐhòu, hǎoxiàng yuè lái yuè nán zuòdào zhè yīdiǎn le. Yěxǔ wǒmen yīnggāi shìshì, zuò biéren de nàge 'duō gěi yī gēn bīnggùn' de rén.
turns.text_en: Someone willing to be a little extra good... yeah. After growing up, it seems harder and harder to do that. Maybe we should try — be someone's 'extra popsicle' person.

tree.cultural_note: 小卖部 (xiǎomàibù, corner shops) were once ubiquitous in Chinese residential neighborhoods, run by familiar faces who knew every family. The five-cent popsicle (五毛钱冰棍) is a powerful symbol of 1990s and 2000s Chinese childhood. As neighborhoods modernize and small shops are replaced by convenience store chains, these personal connections fade — making childhood revisits particularly bittersweet.

tree.setup: You meet your neighbor Sister Lin stretching by the lake path at dawn. She's been running here every morning for three years.
tree.setup_zh: 清晨你在湖边的小路上遇到邻居林姐在拉伸。她已经在这里晨跑了三年。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 早啊！难得看到你这么早出来。我每天早上五点半就到这儿了。刚开始跑的时候很难坚持，但现在如果哪天不跑，反而觉得少了点什么。
turns.text_pinyin: Zǎo a! Nándé kàn dào nǐ zhème zǎo chūlái. Wǒ měi tiān zǎoshang wǔ diǎn bàn jiù dào zhèr le. Gāng kāishǐ pǎo de shíhou hěn nán jiānchí, dàn xiànzài rúguǒ nǎ tiān bù pǎo, fǎn'ér juéde shǎo le diǎn shénme.
turns.text_en: Morning! It's rare to see you out this early. I'm here by five-thirty every morning. When I first started running it was hard to stick with, but now if I skip a day, something feels missing.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Ask about what keeps her going.

options.text_zh: 三年，每天五点半——这不只是运动了，已经变成你生活的一部分。是什么让你一开始决定跑步的？
options.text_pinyin: Sān nián, měi tiān wǔ diǎn bàn — zhè bù zhǐ shì yùndòng le, yǐjīng biàn chéng nǐ shēnghuó de yī bùfen. Shì shénme ràng nǐ yī kāishǐ juédìng pǎobù de?
options.text_en: Three years, every day at five-thirty — that's not just exercise anymore, it's become part of your life. What made you decide to start running in the first place?
options.feedback: Engaged and curious — recognizes the commitment and asks for the story behind it.

options.text_zh: 跑步伤膝盖，你要注意。
options.text_pinyin: Pǎobù shāng xīgài, nǐ yào zhùyì.
options.text_en: Running hurts your knees, be careful.
options.feedback: Unsolicited advice — undermines her established routine.

options.text_zh: 我更喜欢在健身房锻炼。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ gèng xǐhuan zài jiànshēnfáng duànliàn.
options.text_en: I prefer working out at the gym.
options.feedback: Shifts focus to yourself.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 三年前我压力特别大，每天晚上都睡不好。有一天清晨醒得特别早，就出来走了走。走着走着就开始跑了。跑完以后出了一身汗，脑子突然安静了。那种安静是什么药都给不了的。
turns.text_pinyin: Sān nián qián wǒ yālì tèbié dà, měi tiān wǎnshang dōu shuì bù hǎo. Yǒu yī tiān qīngchén xǐng de tèbié zǎo, jiù chūlái zǒu le zǒu. Zǒu zhe zǒu zhe jiù kāishǐ pǎo le. Pǎo wán yǐhòu chū le yī shēn hàn, nǎozi tūrán ānjìng le. Nà zhǒng ānjìng shì shénme yào dōu gěi bù liǎo de.
turns.text_en: Three years ago I was under a lot of pressure and couldn't sleep at night. One dawn I woke up very early and just went out for a walk. Gradually the walking turned into running. After running, covered in sweat, my mind suddenly went quiet. That kind of quiet is something no medicine can give you.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her experience of finding peace through running.

options.text_zh: 用身体的疲劳换头脑的安静——也许这才是跑步的意义。不为减肥，不为比赛，就是跟自己待一会儿。清晨的湖边只有你和呼吸声，这种时刻太珍贵了。
options.text_pinyin: Yòng shēntǐ de píláo huàn tóunǎo de ānjìng — yěxǔ zhè cái shì pǎobù de yìyì. Bú wèi jiǎnféi, bú wèi bǐsài, jiù shì gēn zìjǐ dāi yīhuìr. Qīngchén de hú biān zhǐ yǒu nǐ hé hūxī shēng, zhè zhǒng shíkè tài zhēnguì le.
options.text_en: Trading physical tiredness for mental quiet — maybe that's what running really means. Not for weight loss or competition, just spending time with yourself. At dawn by the lake, just you and your breathing — too precious.
options.feedback: — redefines the purpose of exercise as inner peace.

options.text_zh: 你一般跑多少公里？配速怎么样？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yībān pǎo duōshǎo gōnglǐ? Pèisù zěnmeyàng?
options.text_en: How many kilometers do you usually run? What's your pace?
options.feedback: Quantifies a spiritual experience — misses the emotional point.

options.text_zh: 你应该去看医生，压力大可以吃药。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi qù kàn yīshēng, yālì dà kěyǐ chī yào.
options.text_en: You should see a doctor — medication can help with stress.
options.feedback: Medicalizes what she's already found a solution for.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 跟自己待一会儿……对，就是这个。你今天也来跑一圈吧？不用快，慢慢来就好。你会发现，天亮以前的世界特别温柔。
turns.text_pinyin: Gēn zìjǐ dāi yīhuìr…… duì, jiù shì zhège. Nǐ jīntiān yě lái pǎo yī quān ba? Bú yòng kuài, mànman lái jiù hǎo. Nǐ huì fāxiàn, tiān liàng yǐqián de shìjiè tèbié wēnróu.
turns.text_en: Spending time with yourself... yes, exactly that. Why don't you run a lap today too? No need to be fast, just go slowly. You'll find the world before dawn is especially gentle.

tree.cultural_note: Morning exercise culture (晨练) is deeply rooted in Chinese life, from elderly tai chi practitioners to young urban runners. Lakeside and park paths are shared spaces where generations cross paths at dawn. The phrase「天亮以前的世界」(the world before dawn) carries poetic weight — early morning is seen as a liminal, peaceful time before the day's demands begin.

tree.setup: You and your friend Xiaoxia are having tea after seeing a news story about a natural disaster in another province. The mood is heavy.
tree.setup_zh: 你和朋友小夏看到一条关于外省自然灾害的新闻后在喝茶。气氛有些沉重。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看到那个新闻了吗？那个村子被洪水冲了，好多人失去了家。视频里有个老人站在废墟前面，什么话都没说，就是站在那里。那个画面我一直忘不了。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn dào nàge xīnwén le ma? Nàge cūnzi bèi hóngshuǐ chōng le, hǎo duō rén shīqù le jiā. Shìpín lǐ yǒu ge lǎorén zhàn zài fèixū qiánmiàn, shénme huà dōu méi shuō, jiù shì zhàn zài nàlǐ. Nàge huàmiàn wǒ yīzhí wàng bù liǎo.
turns.text_en: Did you see that news? A village was hit by flooding, so many people lost their homes. In the video there's an elderly person standing in front of the ruins, not saying a word, just standing there. I can't get that image out of my head.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the heavy news.

options.text_zh: 有时候最沉重的画面反而是最安静的。那个老人什么都没说，但你能感受到他失去的一切。我觉得看到这些新闻的时候，让自己心疼一下也是一种尊重。
options.text_pinyin: Yǒu shíhou zuì chénzhòng de huàmiàn fǎn'ér shì zuì ānjìng de. Nàge lǎorén shénme dōu méi shuō, dàn nǐ néng gǎnshòu dào tā shīqù de yīqiè. Wǒ juéde kàn dào zhèxiē xīnwén de shíhou, ràng zìjǐ xīnténg yíxià yě shì yī zhǒng zūnzhòng.
options.text_en: Sometimes the heaviest images are the quietest. The old man said nothing, but you could feel everything he lost. I think when we see news like this, allowing ourselves to feel the pain is itself a form of respect.
options.feedback: Compassionate and thoughtful — honors both the suffering and the act of witnessing.

options.text_zh: 这种新闻看多了就习惯了。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè zhǒng xīnwén kàn duō le jiù xíguàn le.
options.text_en: You get used to news like this after seeing it enough.
options.feedback: Desensitized response — normalizes indifference.

options.text_zh: 天灾没办法避免，只能靠政府了。
options.text_pinyin: Tiānzāi méi bànfǎ bìmiǎn, zhǐ néng kào zhèngfǔ le.
options.text_en: Natural disasters can't be avoided, we can only rely on the government.
options.feedback: Deflects to systems — doesn't engage with the emotional weight.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 让自己心疼一下……你说得对。我有时候会觉得自己关心这些没有用，毕竟我也帮不了什么。但看到那些画面无动于衷，好像也不对。
turns.text_pinyin: Ràng zìjǐ xīnténg yíxià…… nǐ shuō de duì. Wǒ yǒu shíhou huì juéde zìjǐ guānxīn zhèxiē méiyǒu yòng, bìjìng wǒ yě bāng bù liǎo shénme. Dàn kàn dào nàxiē huàmiàn wúdòng yú zhōng, hǎoxiàng yě bú duì.
turns.text_en: Allowing yourself to feel the pain... you're right. Sometimes I feel like my caring is useless since I can't really help. But being unmoved by those images doesn't feel right either.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on the value of caring even when you can't help directly.

options.text_zh: 关心不一定有用才有价值。你会心疼，说明你还没麻木。在信息这么多的时代，保持这种感受力本身就很珍贵。做不了大事，做小事也行——记住他们就够了。
options.text_pinyin: Guānxīn bú yídìng yǒuyòng cái yǒu jiàzhí. Nǐ huì xīnténg, shuōmíng nǐ hái méi mámù. Zài xìnxī zhème duō de shídài, bǎochí zhè zhǒng gǎnshòu lì běnshēn jiù hěn zhēnguì. Zuò bù liǎo dà shì, zuò xiǎo shì yě xíng — jìzhù tāmen jiù gòu le.
options.text_en: Caring doesn't need to be useful to have value. That you feel the pain means you haven't gone numb. In an age of so much information, keeping that sensitivity is precious. Can't do big things? Small things work too — just remembering them is enough.
options.feedback: Layered and gentle — validates compassion as meaningful regardless of its practical impact.

options.text_zh: 你可以去当志愿者啊。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ qù dāng zhìyuànzhě a.
options.text_en: You could go volunteer.
options.feedback: Jumps to action — misses that she's processing her feelings first.

options.text_zh: 别想了，我们改天再聊。吃饭去吧。
options.text_pinyin: Bié xiǎng le, wǒmen gǎi tiān zài liáo. Chīfàn qù ba.
options.text_en: Stop thinking about it, let's talk another day. Let's go eat.
options.feedback: Avoidant — pushes away a conversation that matters.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 记住他们……嗯。也许这就够了。谢谢你没有让我觉得关心这些是多余的。
turns.text_pinyin: Jìzhù tāmen…… en. Yěxǔ zhè jiù gòu le. Xièxie nǐ méiyǒu ràng wǒ juéde guānxīn zhèxiē shì duōyú de.
turns.text_en: Remember them... yes. Maybe that's enough. Thank you for not making me feel like caring about this is unnecessary.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese social media amplifies both awareness of and fatigue from tragic news. The tension between compassion and helplessness (「我也帮不了什么」) is a common feeling. Charitable donation platforms like 腾讯公益 (Tencent Charity) have made micro-donations accessible, but the emotional processing of tragedy — allowing yourself to feel without being overwhelmed — remains a personal challenge in the information age.

tree.setup: Your upstairs neighbor Aunt Wang has started growing herbs and vegetables on her small balcony. She invites you up to see her little garden.
tree.setup_zh: 楼上的王阿姨在小阳台上开始种香草和蔬菜。她邀请你上去看看她的小花园。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看，这是我上个月种的小葱，已经长出来了。还有这盆薄荷，闻一闻，是不是很香？在这么小的阳台上，我种出了一个小世界。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn, zhè shì wǒ shàng ge yuè zhòng de xiǎocōng, yǐjīng zhǎng chūlái le. Háiyǒu zhè pén bòhe, wén yī wén, shì bu shì hěn xiāng? Zài zhème xiǎo de yángtái shàng, wǒ zhòng chū le yīge xiǎo shìjiè.
turns.text_en: Look, these are the scallions I planted last month — they've already come up. And this pot of mint, smell it — isn't it fragrant? On this tiny balcony, I've grown a little world.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Appreciate Aunt Wang's balcony garden.

options.text_zh: 真的很漂亮！在这么小的地方种出这些，一定花了很多心思。我觉得种东西最美的地方不是收获，而是每天看着它们长大的过程。
options.text_pinyin: Zhēn de hěn piàoliang! Zài zhème xiǎo de dìfang zhòng chū zhèxiē, yídìng huā le hěn duō xīnsi. Wǒ juéde zhòng dōngxi zuì měi de dìfang bú shì shōuhuò, ér shì měi tiān kàn zhe tāmen zhǎng dà de guòchéng.
options.text_en: It's really beautiful! Growing all this in such a small space must have taken a lot of thought. I think the most beautiful part of growing things isn't the harvest, but watching them grow day by day.
options.feedback: Warm and observant — values the process over the product.

options.text_zh: 超市买菜不是更方便吗？
options.text_pinyin: Chāoshì mǎi cài bú shì gèng fāngbiàn ma?
options.text_en: Isn't it more convenient to buy vegetables at the supermarket?
options.feedback: She's not gardening for convenience.

options.text_zh: 阳台上种菜会不会招虫子？
options.text_pinyin: Yángtái shàng zhòng cài huì bú huì zhāo chóngzi?
options.text_en: Won't growing vegetables on the balcony attract bugs?
options.feedback: Practical concern that dampens her enthusiasm.

turns.speaker: npc
