turns.text_zh: 对啊。每天看着它们长大确实是最开心的事。退休以后我一直不知道做什么，直到有一天在花市看到一包种子。我想，试试吧。没想到一种就停不下来了。
turns.text_pinyin: Duì a. , měi tiān kàn zhe tāmen zhǎng dà quèshí shì zuì kāixīn de shì. Tuìxiū yǐhòu wǒ yīzhí bù zhīdào zuò shénme, zhídào yǒu yī tiān zài huāshì kàn dào yī bāo zhǒngzǐ. Wǒ xiǎng, shìshì ba. Méi xiǎng dào yī zhòng jiù tíng bú xiàlái le.
turns.text_en: Right. watching them grow every day is truly the happiest thing. After retiring I didn't know what to do, until one day I saw a packet of seeds at the flower market. I thought, why not try? I never expected that once I started, I couldn't stop.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on how small beginnings can transform life.

options.text_zh: 有时候最大的改变就从一粒种子开始。不需要一片大田，一个小花盆就够了。人生也一样——从手边的小事开始，慢慢就会长出意想不到的东西。
options.text_pinyin: Yǒu shíhou zuì dà de gǎibiàn jiù cóng yī lì zhǒngzǐ kāishǐ. Bù xūyào yī piàn dà tián, yīge xiǎo huāpén jiù gòu le. Rénshēng yě yíyàng — cóng shǒubiān de xiǎo shì kāishǐ, mànman jiù huì zhǎng chū yìxiǎng bú dào de dōngxi.
options.text_en: Sometimes the biggest changes start from a single seed. You don't need a vast field — a small pot is enough. Life is the same — start from the small things at hand, and something unexpected will grow.
options.feedback: Metaphor — extends the garden into a life philosophy.

options.text_zh: 你种的菜够吃吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhòng de cài gòu chī ma?
options.text_en: Do you grow enough vegetables to eat?
options.feedback: Quantifies something that's about meaning, not yield.

options.text_zh: 你有没有考虑过种花？花比菜好看。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu kǎolǜ guò zhòng huā? Huā bǐ cài hǎokàn.
options.text_en: Have you considered growing flowers? Flowers look better than vegetables.
options.feedback: Judgment about aesthetics — dismisses her choice.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 从手边能做的小事开始……嗯，就像种一粒种子一样。来，我送你一盆薄荷苗，你也在你的窗台上种一个小世界吧。
turns.text_pinyin: Cóng shǒubiān néng zuò de xiǎo shì kāishǐ…… en, jiù xiàng zhòng yī lì zhǒngzǐ yíyàng. Lái, wǒ sòng nǐ yī pén bòhe miáo, nǐ yě zài nǐ de chuāngtái shàng zhòng yīge xiǎo shìjiè ba.
turns.text_en: Start from the small things within reach... yes, like planting a seed. Here, let me give you a pot of mint — you can grow your own little world on your windowsill too.

tree.cultural_note: Balcony gardening (阳台种菜) has become increasingly popular in Chinese cities, especially among retirees and young people seeking connection to nature in high-rise apartments. Flower markets (花市) are vibrant community spaces in many Chinese cities. The act of gifting a plant cutting or seedling to a neighbor carries warmth and symbolizes sharing — a quiet act of community building.

tree.setup: You and your friend Yueming stop in front of a painting at a small local gallery. It's a simple landscape — mountains, mist, a small boat. But something about it holds you both still.
tree.setup_zh: 你和朋友月明在一家小画廊前停了下来。一幅简单的风景画——山、雾、一只小船。但不知道为什么，你们两个都站住了。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你有没有觉得，好的画不是让你想说话，而是让你想安静下来？这幅画我已经看了五分钟了，好像什么都没想，但又好像想了很多。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu juéde, hǎo de huà bú shì ràng nǐ xiǎng shuōhuà, ér shì ràng nǐ xiǎng ānjìng xiàlái? Zhè fú huà wǒ yǐjīng kàn le wǔ fēnzhōng le, hǎoxiàng shénme dōu méi xiǎng, dàn yòu hǎoxiàng xiǎng le hěn duō.
turns.text_en: Don't you think a good painting doesn't make you want to speak, but makes you want to be quiet? I've been looking at this painting for five minutes. It's as if I'm thinking nothing, yet somehow thinking about everything.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share your feeling about the painting.

options.text_zh: 山只画了一半，船那么小，雾把剩下的都藏起来了。正因为看不到的部分，你才会去想象。好的艺术不会告诉你一切，而是给你空间。
options.text_pinyin: Shān zhǐ huà le yībàn, chuán nàme xiǎo, wù bǎ shèng xià de dōu cáng qǐlái le. Zhèng yīnwèi kàn bú dào de bùfen, nǐ cái huì qù xiǎngxiàng. Hǎo de yìshù bú huì gàosù nǐ yīqiè, ér shì gěi nǐ kōngjiān.
options.text_en: The mountain is half-painted, the boat is tiny, mist hides the rest. It's precisely because of the unseen parts that you imagine. Good art doesn't tell you everything — it gives you space.
options.feedback: Perceptive — connects the aesthetic of negative space to the experience of viewing.

options.text_zh: 确实挺好看的。你知道这个画家是谁吗？有名吗？
options.text_pinyin: Quèshí tǐng hǎokàn de. Nǐ zhīdào zhège huàjiā shì shéi ma? Yǒumíng ma?
options.text_en: It is pretty. Do you know who the painter is? Are they famous?
options.feedback: Yueming is describing an experience of stillness, and you redirect to the painter's credentials. The painting's power doesn't depend on who made it.

options.text_zh: 我更喜欢颜色鲜艳的画，这个太淡了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ gèng xǐhuan yánsè xiānyàn de huà, zhège tài dàn le.
options.text_en: I prefer paintings with brighter colors, this one is too pale.
options.feedback: Dismissive — judges rather than engages.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 给你空间……说得真好。也许这就是中国画和西洋画最大的区别吧。西洋画想让你看到一切，中国画想让你自己去填满那些留白。
turns.text_pinyin: Gěi nǐ kōngjiān…… shuō de zhēn hǎo. Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì Zhōngguó huà hé xīyáng huà zuì dà de qūbié ba. Xīyáng huà xiǎng ràng nǐ kàn dào yīqiè, Zhōngguó huà xiǎng ràng nǐ zìjǐ qù tiánmǎn nàxiē liúbái.
turns.text_en: Gives you space... beautifully said. Maybe that's the biggest difference between Chinese and Western painting. Western painting wants you to see everything; Chinese painting wants you to fill in the blank spaces yourself.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on what blank space means beyond art.

options.text_zh: 生活也需要留白。我们总想把每天都填满，但有时候最有意义的时刻反而是什么都没发生的时候。就像这幅画里的雾——看不见的部分最有味道。
options.text_pinyin: Shēnghuó yě xūyào liúbái. Wǒmen zǒng xiǎng bǎ měi tiān dōu tiánmǎn, dàn yǒu shíhou zuì yǒu yìyì de shíkè fǎn'ér shì shénme dōu méi fāshēng de shíhou. Jiù xiàng zhè fú huà lǐ de wù — kàn bú jiàn de bùfen zuì yǒu wèidào.
options.text_en: Life needs blank space too. We always want to fill every day, but sometimes the most meaningful moments are when nothing happens. Like the mist in this painting — the invisible part has the most flavor.
options.feedback: Philosophical and natural — extends the art principle into life wisdom.

options.text_zh: 你学过画画吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ xué guò huà huà ma?
options.text_en: Have you studied painting?
options.feedback: Shifts to personal history instead of continuing the reflection.

options.text_zh: 我们去看看旁边那幅画吧。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒmen qù kànkan pángbiān nà fú huà ba.
options.text_en: Let's go look at the painting next to this one.
options.feedback: Moves on too quickly — doesn't honor the moment of stillness.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 生活也需要留白……我以后每次觉得太忙的时候，就来看看这幅画。让自己站一站，什么都不做，就够了。
turns.text_pinyin: Shēnghuó yě xūyào liúbái…… wǒ yǐhòu měi cì juéde tài máng de shíhou, jiù lái kànkan zhè fú huà. Ràng zìjǐ zhàn yī zhàn, shénme dōu bú zuò, jiù gòu le.
turns.text_en: Life needs blank space too... from now on, whenever I feel too busy, I'll come look at this painting. Just stand for a while, doing nothing. That will be enough.

tree.cultural_note: 留白 (liúbái, leaving blank/negative space) is a central concept in Chinese ink painting, calligraphy, and aesthetics. Rather than filling the canvas, the artist deliberately leaves empty space, inviting the viewer's imagination to complete the scene. This principle extends beyond art into Chinese philosophy — the idea that emptiness creates meaning, echoing Daoist concepts of「无」(wú, nothingness) as essential to understanding「有」(yǒu, being).

tree.setup: Your roommate Xiaojie is sitting at the kitchen table, staring at a pot of badly burnt rice. She looks embarrassed but also a little amused.
tree.setup_zh: 室友小洁坐在厨房桌前，盯着一锅烧糊了的饭。她看起来有点尴尬，但又有点想笑。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你别笑，这已经是我这个星期第三次把饭煮糊了。我真的不明白，明明按照教程做的，为什么每次结果都不一样？做饭这件事怎么这么难？
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ bié xiào, zhè yǐjīng shì wǒ zhège xīngqī dì sān cì bǎ fàn zhǔ hú le. Wǒ zhēn de bù míngbai, míngmíng ànzhào jiàochéng zuò de, wèi shénme měi cì jiéguǒ dōu bù yíyàng? Zuòfàn zhè jiàn shì zěnme zhème nán?
turns.text_en: Don't laugh — this is the third time this week I've burnt the rice. I really don't understand. I followed the tutorial exactly, so why is the result different every time? How can cooking be this hard?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her frustration with warmth.

options.text_zh: 做饭不是考试，没有标准答案。每个炉子的火力不同，每次放的水也不一样。失败几次很正常，说明你在学。你敢自己做饭，已经比很多人强了。
options.text_pinyin: Zuòfàn bú shì kǎoshì, méiyǒu biāozhǔn dá'àn. Měi ge lúzi de huǒlì bù tóng, měi cì fàng de shuǐ yě bù yíyàng. Shībài jǐ cì hěn zhèngcháng, shuōmíng nǐ zài xué. Nǐ gǎn zìjǐ zuòfàn, yǐjīng bǐ hěn duō rén qiáng le.
options.text_en: Cooking isn't an exam — no standard answer. Every stove has different heat, and the water amount is never the same. Failing a few times is normal, it means you're learning. Daring to cook on your own already puts you ahead of many.
options.feedback: Encouraging and grounded — normalizes failure and reframes her effort positively.

options.text_zh: 为什么不用电饭锅？很简单的。
options.text_pinyin: Wèi shénme bú yòng diànfànguō? Hěn jiǎndān de.
options.text_en: Why don't you use a rice cooker? It's really simple.
options.feedback: Problem-solving before empathy — she needs encouragement first.

options.text_zh: 你应该去上一个做饭的课。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi qù shàng yīge zuòfàn de kè.
options.text_en: You should take a cooking class.
options.feedback: Suggesting she needs formal training — adds pressure rather than support.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你这么一说我好像好受一点了。我从小都是吃妈妈做的饭，来大城市以后才发现自己什么都不会做。每次做糊了就会想她，觉得她做饭的样子那么轻松，其实背后一定也练了很久。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhème yī shuō wǒ hǎoxiàng hǎo shòu yīdiǎn le. Wǒ cóng xiǎo dōu shì chī māma zuò de fàn, lái dà chéngshì yǐhòu cái fāxiàn zìjǐ shénme dōu bú huì zuò. Měi cì zuò hú le jiù huì xiǎng tā, juéde tā zuòfàn de yàngzi nàme qīngsōng, qíshí bèihòu yídìng yě liàn le hěn jiǔ.
turns.text_en: Hearing you say that makes me feel a bit better. Growing up I always ate my mom's cooking. It wasn't until I came to the big city that I realized I couldn't cook anything. Every time I burn something, I think of her — how effortless she made it look. She must have practiced for a long time behind the scenes.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her thoughts about her mother.

options.text_zh: 你妈妈做饭看起来轻松，是因为做了几十年。每道菜都是从烧糊开始的。你现在经历的就是她当年经历的。下次做饭前给她打个电话让她教你，她一定很高兴。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ māma zuòfàn kàn qǐlái qīngsōng, shì yīnwèi zuò le jǐ shí nián. Měi dào cài dōu shì cóng shāo hú kāishǐ de. Nǐ xiànzài jīnglì de jiù shì tā dāngnián jīnglì de. Xià cì zuòfàn qián gěi tā dǎ ge diànhuà ràng tā jiāo nǐ, tā yídìng hěn gāoxìng.
options.text_en: Your mom makes it look easy because she's been doing it for decades. Every dish started from burnt attempts. What you're going through now is what she went through. Call her before cooking next time and let her teach you — she'd love it.
options.feedback: Warm and connective — links her struggle to her mother's journey and suggests a beautiful reconnection.

options.text_zh: 你可以叫外卖啊，不用自己做。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ jiào wàimài a, bú yòng zìjǐ zuò.
options.text_en: You can just order delivery, no need to cook yourself.
options.feedback: Gives up on her behalf — dismisses her desire to learn.

options.text_zh: 我也不太会做饭，但我觉得没什么大不了的。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yě bú tài huì zuòfàn, dàn wǒ juéde méi shénme dà bùliǎo de.
options.text_en: I can't cook well either, but I don't think it's a big deal.
options.feedback: Minimizes what she's feeling — she's connecting cooking to missing her mother.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 给她打个电话……嗯，好主意。也许下次我一边跟她视频一边做，就算做糊了，两个人一起笑也很好。
turns.text_pinyin: Gěi tā dǎ ge diànhuà…… en, hǎo zhǔyì. Yěxǔ xià cì wǒ yībiān gēn tā shìpín yībiān zuò, jiùsuàn zuò hú le, liǎng ge rén yīqǐ xiào yě hěn hǎo.
turns.text_en: Call her... yeah, good idea. Maybe next time I'll video call her while cooking. Even if I burn it again, laughing together would be nice.

tree.cultural_note: For many young Chinese who move to big cities for work, learning to cook is a rite of passage that often triggers homesickness. The ability to cook was traditionally a valued skill in Chinese culture, especially for women, though modern urban life has made delivery apps (外卖) a common replacement. Video-calling parents during cooking has become a heartwarming trend on Chinese social media.

tree.setup: You and your close friend Xiaolan are sitting by the river, watching boats pass. You've been sitting in comfortable silence for a while.
tree.setup_zh: 你和好朋友小兰坐在河边看船经过。你们已经安静地坐了好一会儿了。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你知道我最喜欢跟你在一起的什么吗？就是可以不说话。跟别人在一起不说话会觉得尴尬，但跟你不说话反而觉得很舒服。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhīdào wǒ zuì xǐhuan gēn nǐ zài yīqǐ de shénme ma? Jiù shì kěyǐ bù shuōhuà. Gēn biéren zài yīqǐ bù shuōhuà huì juéde gāngà, dàn gēn nǐ bù shuōhuà fǎn'ér juéde hěn shūfu.
turns.text_en: You know what I like most about being with you? That we don't have to talk. Being silent with others feels awkward, but being silent with you actually feels comfortable.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to this observation about your friendship.

options.text_zh: 我也是这么觉得的。好的朋友不需要一直填满沉默。能一起安静地坐着，说明我们之间已经不需要用话来证明什么了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yě shì zhème juéde de. Hǎo de péngyou bù xūyào yīzhí tiánmǎn chénmò. Néng yīqǐ ānjìng de zuò zhe, shuōmíng wǒmen zhī jiān yǐjīng bù xūyào yòng huà lái zhèngmíng shénme le.
options.text_en: I feel the same way. Good friends don't need to constantly fill the silence. Being able to sit quietly together means we no longer need words to prove anything between us.
options.feedback: Reciprocal and genuine — affirms the depth of the friendship.

options.text_zh: 是吗？我以为你不说话是因为心情不好。
options.text_pinyin: Shì ma? Wǒ yǐwéi nǐ bù shuōhuà shì yīnwèi xīnqíng bù hǎo.
options.text_en: Really? I thought you were quiet because you're in a bad mood.
options.feedback: Misreads the moment — projects worry when she's expressing appreciation.

options.text_zh: 我们聊点什么吧，太安静了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒmen liáo diǎn shénme ba, tài ānjìng le.
options.text_en: Let's chat about something, it's too quiet.
options.feedback: Contradicts what she just said she values about the friendship.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 不需要用话来证明什么……对。我觉得这个世界太吵了，所有人都在说话，但真正有人愿意听的时候很少。你是那种愿意听的人，所以跟你在一起不用伪装。
turns.text_pinyin: Bù xūyào yòng huà lái zhèngmíng shénme…… duì. Wǒ juéde zhège shìjiè tài chǎo le, suǒyǒu rén dōu zài shuōhuà, dàn zhēnzhèng yǒu rén yuànyì tīng de shíhou hěn shǎo. Nǐ shì nà zhǒng yuànyì tīng de rén, suǒyǐ gēn nǐ zài yīqǐ bú yòng wěizhuāng.
turns.text_en: No need to prove anything with words... right. I think this world is too noisy. Everyone is talking, but rarely does someone truly listen. You're the kind of person who's willing to listen, so I don't have to pretend around you.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share what her friendship means to you.

options.text_zh: 能让一个人不用伪装，这也许是友谊能给的最好的东西。我也感谢你愿意在我面前做最真实的自己。这条河以后不管怎么变，我们能一起坐在这里就很好了。
options.text_pinyin: Néng ràng yīge rén bú yòng wěizhuāng, zhè yěxǔ shì yǒuyì néng gěi de zuì hǎo de dōngxi. Wǒ yě gǎnxiè nǐ yuànyì zài wǒ miànqián zuò zuì zhēnshí de zìjǐ. Zhè tiáo hé yǐhòu bùguǎn zěnme biàn, wǒmen néng yīqǐ zuò zài zhèlǐ jiù hěn hǎo le.
options.text_en: Letting someone drop the pretense — maybe that's the best gift friendship can offer. I'm also grateful you're willing to be your truest self around me. No matter how this river changes, being able to sit here together is enough.
options.feedback: Moving and mutual — returns the vulnerability she offered.

options.text_zh: 你在跟别人在一起的时候会伪装吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zài gēn biéren zài yīqǐ de shíhou huì wěizhuāng ma?
options.text_en: Do you pretend around other people?
options.feedback: Probes instead of reciprocates — turns an intimate moment into an interview.

options.text_zh: 对了，你今晚有什么安排？
options.text_pinyin: Duì le, nǐ jīn wǎn yǒu shénme ānpái?
options.text_en: By the way, do you have any plans tonight?
options.feedback: Abruptly shifts from depth to logistics.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我们能一起坐在这里就很好了……嗯。那就再坐一会儿吧，听听水的声音。什么都不用说。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒmen néng yīqǐ zuò zài zhèlǐ jiù hěn hǎo le…… en. Nà jiù zài zuò yīhuìr ba, tīng tīng shuǐ de shēngyīn. Shénme dōu bú yòng shuō.
turns.text_en: Being able to sit here together is enough... yes. Let's sit a while longer then, listening to the water. Nothing needs to be said.

tree.cultural_note: In Chinese philosophy, particularly in Daoist thought, the concept of「此时无声胜有声」(silence speaks louder than words) values shared quiet as a mark of deep connection. Modern Chinese social life often emphasizes group activities and lively conversation, making friendships that allow comfortable silence especially precious. The image of two friends sitting by water evokes classical Chinese poetry's tradition of contemplative companionship.

tree.setup: You and your friend Mingyu are walking home after a late dinner. The streetlights cast long shadows. He mentions a recurring dream he's been having.
tree.setup_zh: 你和朋友明宇晚饭后一起走路回家。路灯拉出长长的影子。他提到了一个反复做的梦。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你会不会觉得我奇怪？我最近一直做一个同样的梦。梦里我走在一条很长的路上，两边都是麦田，风吹着麦子发出沙沙的声音。我不知道要去哪里，但我不着急。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ huì bú huì juéde wǒ qíguài? Wǒ zuìjìn yīzhí zuò yīge tóngyàng de mèng. Mèng lǐ wǒ zǒu zài yī tiáo hěn cháng de lù shang, liǎng biān dōu shì màitián, fēng chuī zhe màizi fā chū shāshā de shēngyīn. Wǒ bù zhīdào yào qù nǎlǐ, dàn wǒ bù zháojí.
turns.text_en: Would you think I'm strange? I've been having the same dream over and over. In the dream I'm walking on a very long road, wheat fields on both sides, wind making the wheat rustle. I don't know where I'm going, but I'm not in a hurry.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his dream.

options.text_zh: 一点都不奇怪。梦有时候比现实更诚实。也许你的梦在说，你不需要知道目的地，路上的感觉对了就好。你在梦里不着急，也许是因为醒着的时候一直在赶路。
options.text_pinyin: Yīdiǎn dōu bù qíguài. Mèng yǒu shíhou bǐ xiànshí gèng chéngshí. Yěxǔ nǐ de mèng zài shuō, nǐ bù xūyào zhīdào mùdìdì, lù shang de gǎnjué duì le jiù hǎo. Nǐ zài mèng lǐ bù zháojí, yěxǔ shì yīnwèi xǐng zhe de shíhou yīzhí zài gǎn lù.
options.text_en: Not strange at all. Dreams are sometimes more honest than reality. Maybe your dream is saying you don't need to know the destination — the feeling on the road being right is enough. You're unhurried in the dream, maybe because you're always rushing when awake.
options.feedback: Deep and gentle — reads the dream as a message from his subconscious.

options.text_zh: 你是不是压力太大了？做梦跟压力有关系。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ shì bu shì yālì tài dà le? Zuòmèng gēn yālì yǒu guānxi.
options.text_en: Are you under too much stress? Dreams are related to stress.
options.feedback: Medicalizes a beautiful dream — misses the peaceful quality he's describing.

options.text_zh: 我很少做梦，或者说醒来就忘了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ hěn shǎo zuòmèng, huòzhě shuō xǐng lái jiù wàng le.
options.text_en: I rarely dream, or I forget as soon as I wake up.
options.feedback: Self-referential — doesn't engage with what he shared.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 清醒的时候一直在赶路……你说得太对了。白天我总是在想下一步要做什么，但梦里的我只是在走。没有手机，没有计划，只有风和麦田。也许那才是我真正想要的生活。
turns.text_pinyin: Qīngxǐng de shíhou yīzhí zài gǎn lù…… nǐ shuō de tài duì le. Báitiān wǒ zǒng shì zài xiǎng xià yī bù yào zuò shénme, dàn mèng lǐ de wǒ zhǐ shì zài zǒu. Méiyǒu shǒujī, méiyǒu jìhuà, zhǐ yǒu fēng hé màitián. Yěxǔ nà cái shì wǒ zhēnzhèng xiǎng yào de shēnghuó.
turns.text_en: Always rushing when awake... you're so right. During the day I'm always thinking about what to do next, but in the dream I'm just walking. No phone, no plans, just wind and wheat fields. Maybe that's the life I truly want.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Offer a thought about listening to what dreams tell us.

options.text_zh: 你不用搬到麦田去住，但可以在生活里创造一些「梦里的时刻」——关掉手机，出去走走，让自己有不知道去哪里的时间。梦给了你线索，告诉你心里在渴望什么。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ bú yòng bān dào màitián qù zhù, dàn kěyǐ zài shēnghuó lǐ chuàngzào yīxiē "mèng lǐ de shíkè" — guān diào shǒujī, chūqù zǒuzou, ràng zìjǐ yǒu bù zhīdào qù nǎlǐ de shíjiān. Mèng gěi le nǐ xiànsuǒ, gàosù nǐ xīn lǐ zài kěwàng shénme.
options.text_en: You don't have to move to a wheat field, but you can create "dream moments" in life — turn off your phone, go walk, give yourself time without direction. The dream gave you a clue about what your heart longs for.
options.feedback: Practical wisdom — bridges the dream world and daily life beautifully.

options.text_zh: 你可以试试睡觉前少喝水，也许就不做梦了。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ shìshì shuìjiào qián shǎo hē shuǐ, yěxǔ jiù bú zuòmèng le.
options.text_en: Try drinking less water before bed, maybe you won't dream.
options.feedback: Misses the entire point — he likes the dream.

options.text_zh: 梦没有什么意义，只是大脑在整理信息。
options.text_pinyin: Mèng méiyǒu shénme yìyì, zhǐ shì dànǎo zài zhěnglǐ xìnxī.
options.text_en: Dreams don't have meaning, the brain is just organizing information.
options.feedback: Reductive — dismisses a meaningful personal experience.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 梦里的时刻……我喜欢这个说法。也许今晚回去以后，我在梦里会走到一个地方。不管去哪里，至少路上有风。
turns.text_pinyin: Mèng lǐ de shíkè…… wǒ xǐhuan zhège shuōfǎ. Yěxǔ jīn wǎn huí qù yǐhòu, wǒ zài mèng lǐ huì zǒu dào yīge dìfang. Bùguǎn qù nǎlǐ, zhìshǎo lù shang yǒu fēng.
turns.text_en: Dream moments... I like that phrase. Maybe tonight when I go back, I'll walk somewhere in my dream. Wherever it is, at least there'll be wind along the way.

tree.cultural_note: Dream interpretation (解梦, jiěmèng) has a long history in Chinese culture, from the ancient classic「周公解梦」(Duke of Zhou's Dream Interpretations) to modern psychological interest. The image of wheat fields and wind evokes rural China's pastoral landscapes, which hold nostalgic power for urban workers whose families often come from farming communities. Dreams of open spaces are often interpreted as the subconscious seeking freedom from constraint.

tree.setup: You run into your colleague Xiaotian at the coffee machine early in the morning. The office is still empty.
tree.setup_zh: 一大早你在咖啡机前遇到了同事小天。办公室还没有人。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你也这么早？我每天都提前一个小时来。不是为了加班，是因为这一个小时是我一天里最安静的时候。没有人打扰，邮件还没开始轰炸，我可以安安静静地喝杯咖啡，想想今天要做什么。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ yě zhème zǎo? Wǒ měi tiān dōu tíqián yīge xiǎoshí lái. Bú shì wèile jiābān, shì yīnwèi zhè yīge xiǎoshí shì wǒ yī tiān lǐ zuì ānjìng de shíhou. Méiyǒu rén dǎrǎo, yóujiàn hái méi kāishǐ hōngzhà, wǒ kěyǐ ānān jìngjìng de hē bēi kāfēi, xiǎngxiǎng jīntiān yào zuò shénme.
turns.text_en: You're here this early too? I come an hour early every day. Not for overtime — because this hour is the quietest part of my day. Nobody interrupts, emails haven't started bombarding, I can have a peaceful coffee and think about what I need to do today.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share your thoughts on finding quiet time.

options.text_zh: 提前一个小时来换一份安静——这个交换很值得。一天开始之前跟自己待一会儿，整个人都不一样。你有没有觉得这一个小时比后面八个小时还重要？
options.text_pinyin: Tíqián yīge xiǎoshí lái huàn yī fèn ānjìng — zhège jiāohuàn hěn zhíde. Yī tiān kāishǐ zhīqián gēn zìjǐ dāi yīhuìr, zhěnggè rén dōu bù yíyàng. Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu juéde zhè yīge xiǎoshí bǐ hòumiàn bā ge xiǎoshí hái zhòngyào?
options.text_en: Coming an hour early in exchange for quiet — that trade is worth it. Having time with yourself before the day starts changes everything. Don't you think this one hour matters more than the eight that follow?
options.feedback: — reframes early arrival as a deliberate choice rather than overwork.

options.text_zh: 你可以在家喝咖啡啊，不用这么早来。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ zài jiā hē kāfēi a, bú yòng zhème zǎo lái.
options.text_en: You could drink coffee at home, no need to come in this early.
options.feedback: The office quiet is the point, not the coffee.

options.text_zh: 你不困吗？我如果这么早起肯定很困。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ bú kùn ma? Wǒ rúguǒ zhème zǎo qǐ kěndìng hěn kùn.
options.text_en: Aren't you sleepy? I'd definitely be exhausted getting up this early.
options.feedback: Self-focused — centers on your own habits rather than engaging with his.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 比后面八个小时还重要……真的是这样。我有一个小本子，每天早上到了以后会写三件我感谢的事。不是什么大事，可能只是昨天吃了一顿好饭，或者路上看到一朵花。写完以后心情就会好很多。
turns.text_pinyin: Bǐ hòumiàn bā ge xiǎoshí hái zhòngyào…… zhēn de shì zhèyàng. Wǒ yǒu yīge xiǎo běnzi, měi tiān zǎoshang dào le yǐhòu huì xiě sān jiàn wǒ gǎnxiè de shì. Bú shì shénme dà shì, kěnéng zhǐ shì zuótiān chī le yī dùn hǎo fàn, huòzhě lù shang kàn dào yī duǒ huā. Xiě wán yǐhòu xīnqíng jiù huì hǎo hěn duō.
turns.text_en: More important than the eight that follow... it really is. I have a small notebook — every morning after I arrive, I write three things I'm grateful for. Nothing big, maybe just a good meal yesterday, or a flower I saw on the way. After writing, my mood improves a lot.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his gratitude practice.

options.text_zh: 每天写三件感谢的事，听着简单，但坚持下来不容易。感恩不是因为日子特别好，而是学会在平凡中发现值得珍惜的东西。一朵花、一顿饭，都是生活在跟你说话。
options.text_pinyin: Měi tiān xiě sān jiàn gǎnxiè de shì, tīng zhe jiǎndān, dàn jiānchí xiàlái bù róngyì. Gǎn'ēn bú shì yīnwèi rìzi tèbié hǎo, ér shì xuéhuì zài píngfán zhōng fāxiàn zhíde zhēnxī de dōngxi. Yī duǒ huā, yī dùn fàn, dōu shì shēnghuó zài gēn nǐ shuōhuà.
options.text_en: Writing three grateful things daily sounds simple but isn't easy to keep up. Gratitude isn't because life is great — it's learning to find what's worth cherishing in ordinary days. A flower, a meal — life talking to you.
options.feedback: Eloquent and affirming — deepens his practice into philosophy.

options.text_zh: 你试过用手机APP记吗？比本子方便。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ shì guò yòng shǒujī APP jì ma? Bǐ běnzi fāngbiàn.
options.text_en: Have you tried using a phone app? It's more convenient than a notebook.
options.feedback: Focuses on the tool rather than the meaning.

options.text_zh: 我觉得我每天都差不多，没什么特别值得感谢的。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde wǒ měi tiān dōu chàbuduō, méi shénme tèbié zhíde gǎnxiè de.
options.text_en: I feel like every day is the same, nothing especially worth being grateful for.
options.feedback: Cynical response — undermines his positive practice.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 生活在跟你说话……我要把这句话写在今天的本子里。谢谢你，今天的第一杯咖啡遇到你，这也是一件值得感谢的事。
turns.text_pinyin: Shēnghuó zài gēn nǐ shuōhuà…… wǒ yào bǎ zhè jù huà xiě zài jīntiān de běnzi lǐ. Xièxie nǐ, jīntiān de dì yī bēi kāfēi yù dào nǐ, zhè yě shì yī jiàn zhíde gǎnxiè de shì.
turns.text_en: Life speaking to you... I'm going to write that in today's notebook. Thank you — running into you over my first coffee today is also something worth being grateful for.

tree.cultural_note: The concept of gratitude journaling has gained popularity in China alongside the broader wellness and mindfulness movement. The practice resonates with traditional Chinese values of contentment (知足, zhīzú) — knowing when you have enough. The idea of arriving early not to work but to find peace reflects a quiet resistance to「996」culture, reclaiming space within the system rather than fighting it directly.

tree.setup: You and your friend Xiaoqian are walking through a weekend farmer's market. The sounds of vendors calling out prices and neighbors chatting fill the air.
tree.setup_zh: 你和朋友小倩走在周末的农贸市场里。小贩叫卖的声音和邻居聊天的声音充满了空气。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我特别喜欢来菜市场。你听，每个摊位都在喊价，但仔细听的话，里面还有人在聊家常、谈天气。超市很安静，但菜市场有一种活着的感觉。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ tèbié xǐhuan lái càishìchǎng. Nǐ tīng, měi ge tānwèi dōu zài hǎn jià, dàn zǐxì tīng dehuà, lǐmiàn hái yǒu rén zài liáo jiācháng, tán tiānqì. Chāoshì hěn ānjìng, dàn càishìchǎng yǒu yī zhǒng huó zhe de gǎnjué.
turns.text_en: I especially love coming to the farmers' market. Listen — every stall is calling out prices, but if you listen carefully, people are chatting about family, talking about weather. Supermarkets are quiet, but markets have a feeling of being alive.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her love of the market atmosphere.

options.text_zh: 活着的感觉——你说得太好了。菜市场是城市最真实的地方。没有包装，没有灯光设计，就是人和人最直接的交流。买一把菜聊几句话，比任何社交软件都真实。
options.text_pinyin: Huó zhe de gǎnjué — nǐ shuō de tài hǎo le. Càishìchǎng shì chéngshì zuì zhēnshí de dìfang. Méiyǒu bāozhuāng, méiyǒu dēngguāng shèjì, jiù shì rén hé rén zuì zhíjiē de jiāoliú. Mǎi yī bǎ cài liáo jǐ jù huà, bǐ rènhé shèjiāo ruǎnjiàn dōu zhēnshí.
options.text_en: A feeling of being alive — perfectly put. The farmers' market is a city's most authentic place. No packaging, no designed lighting, just the most direct exchange between people. Buying greens and chatting a few words is more real than any social media.
options.feedback: Affirming and eloquent — celebrates the market's unpolished humanity.

options.text_zh: 但是菜市场的菜没有超市的干净。
options.text_pinyin: Dànshì càishìchǎng de cài méiyǒu chāoshì de gānjìng.
options.text_en: But market vegetables aren't as clean as supermarket ones.
options.feedback: Compares on the wrong dimension.

options.text_zh: 现在网上买菜更方便，不用出门。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài wǎng shàng mǎi cài gèng fāngbiàn, bú yòng chūmén.
options.text_en: Buying groceries online is more convenient now, no need to go out.
options.feedback: She comes for the experience, not convenience.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看那个卖豆腐的大姐，她认识每一个常来的客人。刚才她问一个阿姨的孙子考试考得怎么样。这种关系在超市里是不可能有的。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn nàge mài dòufu de dàjiě, tā rènshi měi yīge cháng lái de kèren. Gāngcái tā wèn yīge āyí de sūnzi kǎoshì kǎo de zěnmeyàng. Zhè zhǒng guānxì zài chāoshì lǐ shì bù kěnéng yǒu de.
turns.text_en: Look at the tofu seller over there — she knows every regular customer. Just now she asked one auntie how her grandson did on his exams. That kind of relationship can't exist in a supermarket.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on why these small connections matter.

options.text_zh: 这就是社区的温度。卖豆腐的大姐记住了谁家孙子在考试，说明她不只是在卖豆腐，是在关心邻居。我们都需要被真实的人记住，而不是在网上被点赞。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè jiù shì shèqū de wēndù. Mài dòufu de dàjiě jìzhù le shéi jiā sūnzi zài kǎoshì, shuōmíng tā bù zhǐ shì zài mài dòufu, shì zài guānxīn línjū. Wǒmen dōu xūyào bèi zhēnshí de rén jìzhù, ér bú shì zài wǎng shàng bèi diǎnzàn.
options.text_en: That's community warmth. The tofu seller remembering whose grandson has exams shows she's not just selling tofu — she's caring about neighbors. We all need to be remembered by real people, not just liked online.
options.feedback: Moving and sharp — contrasts digital and real-world connection beautifully.

options.text_zh: 她的豆腐好吃吗？我们要不要买一块？
options.text_pinyin: Tā de dòufu hǎochī ma? Wǒmen yào bú yào mǎi yī kuài?
options.text_en: Is her tofu good? Should we buy some?
options.feedback: Shifts to the transactional — misses the human observation.

options.text_zh: 她可能只是为了让客人多买东西吧。
options.text_pinyin: Tā kěnéng zhǐ shì wèile ràng kèren duō mǎi dōngxi ba.
options.text_en: She's probably just doing it to get customers to buy more.
options.feedback: Cynical — assumes commercial motive for genuine warmth.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 被一个真实的人记住……嗯。走吧，我们去买点菜。我要跟那个大姐聊聊天，让她也记住我们。
turns.text_pinyin: Bèi yīge zhēnshí de rén jìzhù…… en. Zǒu ba, wǒmen qù mǎi diǎn cài. Wǒ yào gēn nàge dàjiě liáo liáo tiān, ràng tā yě jìzhù wǒmen.
turns.text_en: Being remembered by a real person... yes. Come on, let's go buy some vegetables. I want to chat with that seller and get her to remember us too.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese wet markets and farmers' markets (菜市场) are considered by many to be the heart of neighborhood life. The relationship between regular vendors and customers goes beyond commerce — vendors often remember family details, offer cooking advice, and throw in extra produce for loyal customers. In contrast to the sterile efficiency of supermarkets and online grocery delivery, the market represents a communal space that is increasingly valued as cities modernize.

tree.setup: You notice your colleague Xiaodong eating a simple bowl of noodles at his desk during lunch, staring out the window. He seems far away in thought.
tree.setup_zh: 午餐时你看到同事小东在桌前吃一碗简单的面条，望着窗外发呆。他好像在想很远的事。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 今天食堂没有我想吃的，就自己煮了一碗面。味道差远了。我妈做的手擀面，从揉面到切面都是自己来的。那个味道，在这个城市里怎么找都找不到。
turns.text_pinyin: Jīntiān shítáng méiyǒu wǒ xiǎng chī de, jiù zìjǐ zhǔ le yī wǎn miàn. Wèidào chà yuǎn le. Wǒ mā zuò de shǒugǎn miàn, cóng róu miàn dào qiē miàn dōu shì zìjǐ lái de. Nàge wèidào, zài zhège chéngshì lǐ zěnme zhǎo dōu zhǎo bú dào.
turns.text_en: The cafeteria didn't have what I wanted today, so I cooked a bowl of noodles myself. The taste is nowhere close. My mom's handmade noodles — she does everything from kneading the dough to cutting the noodles herself. That flavor, I can't find it anywhere in this city.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his homesickness.

options.text_zh: 找不到的味道其实不是面的味道，是家的味道。不管做多少次，调料放得再像，差的那一点永远是距离和思念。你离家多久了？
options.text_pinyin: Zhǎo bú dào de wèidào qíshí bú shì miàn de wèidào, shì jiā de wèidào. Bùguǎn zuò duōshǎo cì, tiáoliào fàng de zài xiàng, chà de nà yīdiǎn yǒngyuǎn shì jùlí hé sīniàn. Nǐ lí jiā duō jiǔ le?
options.text_en: What you can't find isn't the taste of the noodles — it's the taste of home. No matter how similar the seasonings, what's missing is the distance and the longing. How long have you been away?
options.feedback: Perceptive and tender — names what he can't articulate.

options.text_zh: 你可以让你妈妈把做法写下来发给你。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ ràng nǐ māma bǎ zuòfǎ xiě xiàlái fā gěi nǐ.
options.text_en: You could ask your mom to write down the recipe and send it to you.
options.feedback: Practical but misses the emotional core — it's not about the recipe.

options.text_zh: 附近有一家面馆味道不错，下次我带你去。
options.text_pinyin: Fùjìn yǒu yī jiā miànguǎn wèidào búcuò, xià cì wǒ dài nǐ qù.
options.text_en: There's a noodle shop nearby that's pretty good, I'll take you next time.
options.feedback: Tries to solve the unsolvable — no restaurant can replace his mother's noodles.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 快三年了。回去的次数越来越少，每次回去待的时间也越来越短。有时候觉得我在这个城市越来越像一个本地人了，但一碗面就能让我想起我不是。
turns.text_pinyin: Kuài sān nián le. Huí qù de cìshù yuè lái yuè shǎo, měi cì huí qù dāi de shíjiān yě yuè lái yuè duǎn. Yǒu shíhou juéde wǒ zài zhège chéngshì yuè lái yuè xiàng yīge běndì rén le, dàn yī wǎn miàn jiù néng ràng wǒ xiǎng qǐ wǒ bú shì.
turns.text_en: Almost three years. I go back less and less often, and each visit is shorter. Sometimes I feel like I'm becoming more and more like a local in this city, but a single bowl of noodles reminds me I'm not.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Offer comfort about belonging and identity.

options.text_zh: 你不需要选择做哪里的人。在这个城市生活，心里还留着家乡的位置就好。想家不是软弱，是证明你有值得想念的地方。每次做面的努力，都是在跟家保持连接。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ bù xūyào xuǎnzé zuò nǎlǐ de rén. Zài zhège chéngshì shēnghuó, xīn lǐ hái liú zhe jiāxiāng de wèizhì jiù hǎo. Xiǎng jiā bú shì ruǎnruò, shì zhèngmíng nǐ yǒu zhíde xiǎngniàn de dìfang. Měi cì zuò miàn de nǔlì, dōu shì zài gēn jiā bǎochí liánjiē.
options.text_en: You don't have to choose where you belong. Live in this city while keeping a place for home in your heart. Missing home isn't weakness — it proves you have somewhere worth missing. Every time you try to make noodles, you're staying connected to home.
options.feedback: Compassionate and wise — reframes homesickness as a sign of roots rather than rootlessness.

options.text_zh: 三年不算久，以后你会习惯的。
options.text_pinyin: Sān nián bú suàn jiǔ, yǐhòu nǐ huì xíguàn de.
options.text_en: Three years isn't that long, you'll get used to it.
options.feedback: Dismissive — minimizes his feelings of displacement.

options.text_zh: 你为什么不考虑回老家工作？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ wèi shénme bù kǎolǜ huí lǎojiā gōngzuò?
options.text_en: Why don't you consider going back to work in your hometown?
options.feedback: Oversimplified — doesn't account for the complexity of his situation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 跟家保持连接……谢谢你。也许今晚我给我妈打个电话，让她在视频那边陪我再煮一次面。就算味道还是不一样，至少能听到她的声音。
turns.text_pinyin: Gēn jiā bǎochí liánjiē…… xièxie nǐ. Yěxǔ jīn wǎn wǒ gěi wǒ mā dǎ ge diànhuà, ràng tā zài shìpín nà biān péi wǒ zài zhǔ yī cì miàn. Jiùsuàn wèidào háishì bù yíyàng, zhìshǎo néng tīng dào tā de shēngyīn.
turns.text_en: Staying connected to home... thank you. Maybe tonight I'll call my mom and have her keep me company on video while I cook noodles again. Even if the taste is still different, at least I'll hear her voice.

tree.cultural_note: China's internal migration has created hundreds of millions of「异乡人」(yìxiāng rén, people living away from home). Food is the most powerful trigger for homesickness — the phrase「家乡的味道」(the taste of hometown) carries enormous emotional weight. Hand-pulled and hand-cut noodles (手擀面) represent the irreplaceable personal touch of a mother's cooking. Video-calling parents while cooking has become a bittersweet modern ritual of maintaining family bonds across distance.

tree.setup: You and your friend Yanan are sitting in a tea house. She recently moved from Chengdu to Shanghai for work and is adjusting to the differences.
tree.setup_zh: 你和朋友亚南坐在茶馆里。她最近因为工作从成都搬到了上海，正在适应两个城市的不同。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 上海什么都快。走路快，说话快，吃饭也快。在成都的时候，我们下午可以在茶馆坐一整个下午，没有人觉得奇怪。这里好像每个人都在赶下一件事。
turns.text_pinyin: Shànghǎi shénme dōu kuài. Zǒulù kuài, shuōhuà kuài, chīfàn yě kuài. Zài Chéngdū de shíhou, wǒmen xiàwǔ kěyǐ zài cháguǎn zuò yī zhěnggè xiàwǔ, méiyǒu rén juéde qíguài. Zhèlǐ hǎoxiàng měi ge rén dōu zài gǎn xià yī jiàn shì.
turns.text_en: Everything in Shanghai is fast. Walking fast, talking fast, eating fast. In Chengdu, we could sit in a tea house all afternoon and nobody thought it was strange. Here everyone seems to be rushing to the next thing.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her comparison of the two cities.

options.text_zh: 每个城市都有自己的节奏。上海是奔跑的，成都是散步的。不是哪个更好，而是你要在这个节奏里找到自己的步伐。你不一定要跟着上海的速度走。
options.text_pinyin: Měi ge chéngshì dōu yǒu zìjǐ de jiézòu. Shànghǎi shì bēnpǎo de, Chéngdū shì sànbù de. Bú shì nǎge gèng hǎo, ér shì nǐ yào zài zhège jiézòu lǐ zhǎo dào zìjǐ de bùfá. Nǐ bú yídìng yào gēn zhe Shànghǎi de sùdù zǒu.
options.text_en: Every city has its own rhythm. Shanghai runs; Chengdu strolls. It's not about which is better, but finding your own pace within this rhythm. You don't have to match Shanghai's speed.
options.feedback: Balanced and wise — avoids judging either city and empowers her to set her own pace.

options.text_zh: 上海发展机会多，值得。
options.text_pinyin: Shànghǎi fāzhǎn jīhuì duō, zhíde.
options.text_en: Shanghai has more career opportunities, it's worth it.
options.feedback: Dismisses her feelings by emphasizing the practical trade-off.

options.text_zh: 那你为什么不回成都呢？
options.text_pinyin: Nà nǐ wèi shénme bù huí Chéngdū ne?
options.text_en: Then why don't you go back to Chengdu?
options.feedback: Oversimplifies — she came for a reason and is processing, not seeking escape.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 找到属于自己的步伐……我觉得你说得对。也许我可以在上海的忙碌里保留一些成都的慢。比如周末的时候不安排任何事，就像在成都那样，找一家安静的茶馆坐一坐。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhǎo dào shǔyú zìjǐ de bùfá…… wǒ juéde nǐ shuō de duì. Yěxǔ wǒ kěyǐ zài Shànghǎi de mánglù lǐ bǎoliú yīxiē Chéngdū de màn. Bǐrú zhōumò de shíhou bù ānpái rènhé shì, jiù xiàng zài Chéngdū nàyàng, zhǎo yī jiā ānjìng de cháguǎn zuò yī zuò.
turns.text_en: Finding my own pace... I think you're right. Maybe I can keep some of Chengdu's slowness within Shanghai's busyness. Like on weekends, not scheduling anything, just like in Chengdu — finding a quiet tea house and sitting.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Encourage her to blend the best of both cities.

options.text_zh: 对，你不用完全变成一个上海人。把成都的心带在身上，到哪里都是你的城市。一个人可以同时属于两个地方。
options.text_pinyin: Duì, nǐ bú yòng wánquán biàn chéng yīge Shànghǎi rén. Bǎ Chéngdū de xīn dài zài shēn shàng, dào nǎlǐ dōu shì nǐ de chéngshì. Yīge rén kěyǐ tóngshí shǔyú liǎng ge dìfang.
options.text_en: Right, you don't have to completely become a Shanghainese person. Carry Chengdu in your heart, and wherever you go becomes your city. A person can belong to two places at once.
options.feedback: Affirming and poetic — dissolves the binary of choosing one identity.

options.text_zh: 上海也有很多好吃的啊。
options.text_pinyin: Shànghǎi yě yǒu hěn duō hǎochī de a.
options.text_en: Shanghai has lots of good food too.
options.feedback: Food isn't the issue she's processing.

options.text_zh: 你可以去找一些成都老乡，周末一起聚一聚。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ qù zhǎo yīxiē Chéngdū lǎoxiāng, zhōumò yīqǐ jù yī jù.
options.text_en: You can find some fellow Chengdu people and get together on weekends.
options.feedback: Not wrong but doesn't engage with the deeper identity question.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 同时属于两个地方……我以前觉得这是一种矛盾，但你让我觉得这是一种丰富。谢谢你。下次我带你去一家我发现的茶馆，就在弄堂深处，有成都的感觉。
turns.text_pinyin: Tóngshí shǔyú liǎng ge dìfang…… wǒ yǐqián juéde zhè shì yī zhǒng máodùn, dàn nǐ ràng wǒ juéde zhè shì yī zhǒng fēngfù. Xièxie nǐ. Xià cì wǒ dài nǐ qù yī jiā wǒ fāxiàn de cháguǎn, jiù zài lòngtáng shēn chù, yǒu Chéngdū de gǎnjué.
turns.text_en: Belonging to two places at once... I used to think that was a contradiction, but you've made me see it as richness. Thank you. Next time I'll take you to a tea house I discovered, deep in an alley — it has a Chengdu feeling.

tree.cultural_note: The contrast between Chengdu and Shanghai is a frequent topic in Chinese culture. Chengdu is famous for its relaxed「慢生活」(màn shēnghuó, slow living) culture — tea houses, mahjong, and the philosophy that life should be enjoyed. Shanghai represents ambition, speed, and cosmopolitan energy. Many Chinese professionals who migrate between these poles grapple with dual identity, carrying the rhythm of their hometown within the tempo of their adopted city.

tree.setup: A sudden rain traps you and a stranger under the same awning near a river. She's holding a closed book and smiling at the rain.
tree.setup_zh: 一场突然的雨把你和一个陌生人困在了河边的同一个雨棚下。她手里拿着一本合上的书，对着雨微笑。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你不着急走吗？很多人一下雨就跑。我反而喜欢这种时候——被迫停下来，什么都做不了，只能等。你有没有觉得，被迫等待其实是一种解放？
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ bù zháojí zǒu ma? Hěn duō rén yī xià yǔ jiù pǎo. Wǒ fǎn'ér xǐhuan zhè zhǒng shíhou — bèi pò tíng xiàlái, shénme dōu zuò bù liǎo, zhǐ néng děng. Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu juéde, bèi pò děngdài qíshí shì yī zhǒng jiěfàng?
turns.text_en: You're not in a hurry to go? Most people run when it rains. I actually like these moments — forced to stop, unable to do anything, just wait. Don't you think being forced to wait is actually a kind of liberation?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the stranger's perspective on rain.

options.text_zh: 是啊。平时我们不会给自己「什么都不做」的许可。但是雨帮我们做了这个决定——不是我们选择停下来，是天让我们停下来。这种被动反而让人放松。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ shuō de duì. Píngshí wǒmen bú huì gěi zìjǐ 'shénme dōu bú zuò' de xǔkě. Dànshì yǔ bāng wǒmen zuò le zhège juédìng — bú shì wǒmen xuǎnzé tíng xiàlái, shì tiān ràng wǒmen tíng xiàlái. Zhè zhǒng bèidòng fǎn'ér ràng rén fàngsōng.
options.text_en: You're right. Usually we don't give ourselves permission to do nothing. But rain makes that decision for us — we didn't choose to stop, the sky made us stop. That passivity is actually relaxing.
options.feedback: — picks up her philosophical thread and extends it naturally.

options.text_zh: 你没有带伞吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ méiyǒu dài sǎn ma?
options.text_en: Didn't you bring an umbrella?
options.feedback: Practical but flat — misses the contemplative opening she's offering.

options.text_zh: 雨应该很快就停了吧。
options.text_pinyin: Yǔ yīnggāi hěn kuài jiù tíng le ba.
options.text_en: The rain should stop soon.
options.feedback: Impatient — contradicts the spirit of enjoying the pause.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 天让我们停下来……说得好。你看河面上的雨点，每一滴落下来都画一个圆，然后就消失了。好像什么都没留下，但水面一直在动。也许很多事情就是这样——看不到结果，但影响一直在。
turns.text_pinyin: Tiān ràng wǒmen tíng xiàlái…… shuō de hǎo. Nǐ kàn hé miàn shàng de yǔdiǎn, měi yī dī luò xiàlái dōu huà yīge yuán, ránhòu jiù xiāoshī le. Hǎoxiàng shénme dōu méi liú xià, dàn shuǐ miàn yīzhí zài dòng. Yěxǔ hěn duō shìqíng jiù shì zhèyàng — kàn bú dào jiéguǒ, dàn yǐngxiǎng yīzhí zài.
turns.text_en: The sky made us stop... well said. Look at the raindrops on the river. Each one draws a circle when it falls, then disappears. It seems like nothing was left behind, but the surface keeps moving. Maybe many things are like that — you can't see the result, but the ripples continue.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on her observation about ripples.

options.text_zh: 这让我想到人与人的相遇。今天我们在这儿聊几句，明天可能就忘了。但说过的话会在心里留下痕迹，像水面的波纹。我们永远不知道自己给别人留下了什么。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè ràng wǒ xiǎng dào rén yǔ rén de xiāngyù. Jīntiān wǒmen zài zhèr liáo jǐ jù, míngtiān kěnéng jiù wàng le. Dàn shuō guò de huà huì zài xīn lǐ liú xià hénjì, xiàng shuǐ miàn de bōwén. Wǒmen yǒngyuǎn bù zhīdào zìjǐ gěi biéren liú xià le shénme.
options.text_en: That makes me think of encounters between people. We chat here today, maybe forget by tomorrow. But words leave traces in our hearts, like ripples on water. We never know what we leave behind in others.
options.feedback: — connects the natural observation to human connection, perfect for a brief encounter.

options.text_zh: 你经常一个人来河边吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ jīngcháng yīge rén lái hé biān ma?
options.text_en: Do you come to the river alone often?
options.feedback: Shifts to personal details instead of continuing the philosophical thread.

options.text_zh: 雨小一点了，我先走了。
options.text_pinyin: Yǔ xiǎo yīdiǎn le, wǒ xiān zǒu le.
options.text_en: The rain is lighter now, I'll head out.
options.feedback: Abrupt — exits a rare meaningful encounter too quickly.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我们永远不知道自己给别人留下了什么……嗯，说得真好。看，雨停了。谢谢你陪我等了这场雨。也许下次下雨的时候，我会想起今天这段对话。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒmen yǒngyuǎn bù zhīdào zìjǐ gěi biéren liú xià le shénme…… en, shuō de zhēn hǎo. Kàn, yǔ tíng le. Xièxie nǐ péi wǒ děng le zhè chǎng yǔ. Yěxǔ xià cì xià yǔ de shíhou, wǒ huì xiǎng qǐ jīntiān zhè duàn duìhuà.
turns.text_en: We never know what we leave behind... yes, beautifully said. Look, the rain stopped. Thank you for waiting out this rain with me. Maybe next time it rains, I'll remember today's conversation.

tree.cultural_note: Rain holds deep poetic significance in Chinese culture. Classical poets like Li Shangyin and Du Fu used rain as a metaphor for emotion, longing, and the passage of time. The image of strangers meeting during rain — sharing an awning, exchanging a few words — resonates with the concept of「一期一会」(borrowed from Japanese, but shared in Chinese aesthetic sensibility), the idea that each encounter is unique and unrepeatable.

tree.setup: Your friend Xiaolu found a box of old printed photos while cleaning her apartment. She's sitting cross-legged on the floor, sorting through them.
tree.setup_zh: 朋友小璐在整理公寓时发现了一箱旧照片。她盘腿坐在地上，一张一张地翻看。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看这张——这是我小学毕业的时候，全班合照。那时候我们都觉得小学毕业是天大的事，以为离开以后就再也见不到了。结果长大了才发现，真正让人难过的离别都是悄悄发生的。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn zhè zhāng — zhè shì wǒ xiǎoxué bìyè de shíhou, quán bān hézhào. Nà shíhou wǒmen dōu juéde xiǎoxué bìyè shì tiān dà de shì, yǐwéi líkāi yǐhòu jiù zài yě jiàn bú dào le. Jiéguǒ zhǎng dà le cái fāxiàn, zhēnzhèng ràng rén nánguò de líbié dōu shì qiāoqiāo fāshēng de.
turns.text_en: Look at this one — this is from my elementary school graduation, the whole class photo. Back then we all thought graduation was the biggest deal, that we'd never see each other again. But growing up, I realized the partings that truly make you sad all happen quietly.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her observation about partings.

options.text_zh: 悄悄发生的离别……是啊。不是谁突然消失了，而是联系慢慢少了，有一天你发现已经很久没有说过话了。这种离别没有告别的机会，所以更让人遗憾。
options.text_pinyin: Qiāoqiāo fāshēng de líbié…… shì a. Bú shì shéi tūrán xiāoshī le, ér shì liánxì mànman shǎo le, yǒu yī tiān nǐ fāxiàn yǐjīng hěn jiǔ méiyǒu shuō guò huà le. Zhè zhǒng líbié méiyǒu gàobié de jīhuì, suǒyǐ gèng ràng rén yíhàn.
options.text_en: Partings that happen quietly... yes. It's not that someone suddenly disappears, but contact slowly fades, and one day you realize it's been ages since you spoke. These partings have no chance for goodbye, which makes them more regrettable.
options.feedback: Resonant and precise — names the mechanism of gradual loss.

options.text_zh: 现在都用手机拍照了，不用洗照片了。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài dōu yòng shǒujī pāi zhào le, bú yòng xǐ zhàopiàn le.
options.text_en: Now everyone uses phones, no need to print photos.
options.feedback: Compares technology when she's sharing emotion.

options.text_zh: 你还联系班里的同学吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ hái liánxì bān lǐ de tóngxué ma?
options.text_en: Are you still in touch with your classmates?
options.feedback: Not terrible but misses the philosophical depth of what she said.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看这些照片，每一张背后都有一个故事，但大部分我已经记不清了。有时候看到一张照片里的人，我知道我们以前很好，但想不起来我们一起做过什么。记忆真的很脆弱。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn zhèxiē zhàopiàn, měi yī zhāng bèihòu dōu yǒu yīge gùshi, dàn dà bùfen wǒ yǐjīng jì bù qīng le. Yǒu shíhou kàn dào yī zhāng zhàopiàn lǐ de rén, wǒ zhīdào wǒmen yǐqián hěn hǎo, dàn xiǎng bù qǐlái wǒmen yīqǐ zuò guò shénme. Jìyì zhēn de hěn cuìruò.
turns.text_en: Look at these photos — each one has a story behind it, but most I can't remember clearly. Sometimes I see a person in a photo and know we used to be close, but I can't recall what we did together. Memory is really fragile.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Offer a comforting thought about memory and photos.

options.text_zh: 也许记不清细节没关系。你记得你们曾经很好，这就是最重要的记忆。照片的意义不是让你记住一切，而是提醒你那段时光真实存在过。故事模糊了，温度还在。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ jì bù qīng xìjié méi guānxi. Nǐ jìde nǐmen céngjīng hěn hǎo, zhè jiù shì zuì zhòngyào de jìyì. Zhàopiàn de yìyì bú shì ràng nǐ jìzhù yīqiè, ér shì tíxǐng nǐ nà duàn shíguāng zhēnshí cúnzài guò. Gùshi móhu le, wēndù hái zài.
options.text_en: Maybe not remembering the details is okay. You remember you were close — that's the most important memory. Photos don't exist to make you remember everything, but to remind you that time really happened. Stories fade, but the warmth remains.
options.feedback: Gentle and comforting — redefines what memory preservation means.

options.text_zh: 你应该把这些照片扫描存到电脑里，不然会褪色。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi bǎ zhèxiē zhàopiàn sǎomiáo cún dào diànnǎo lǐ, bùrán huì tuìsè.
options.text_en: You should scan these photos and save them on your computer, otherwise they'll fade.
options.feedback: Practical advice that misses the emotional moment.

options.text_zh: 你小时候好可爱啊！
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ xiǎo shíhou hǎo kě'ài a!
options.text_en: You were so cute as a kid!
options.feedback: Superficial — doesn't engage with the deeper reflection on memory and loss.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 温度还在……嗯。谢谢你。我不会把这些照片扔掉的。就算记不清了，至少我知道自己曾经被这么多人温暖过。
turns.text_pinyin: Wēndù hái zài…… en. Xièxie nǐ. Wǒ bú huì bǎ zhèxiē zhàopiàn rēng diào de. Jiùsuàn jì bù qīng le, zhìshǎo wǒ zhīdào zìjǐ céngjīng bèi zhème duō rén wēnnuǎn guò.
turns.text_en: The warmth remains... yes. Thank you. I won't throw these photos away. Even if I can't remember clearly, at least I know I was once warmed by so many people.

tree.cultural_note: Physical printed photographs carry special emotional weight in China, where the transition from film to digital happened rapidly in the 2000s. Many families have boxes of old photos from an era before smartphones, each one deliberately taken and developed. The phrase「温度还在」(the warmth remains) speaks to a Chinese understanding of memory as emotional rather than factual — what matters is not the precise details but the feeling that persists.

title: The Bookshop That Stays Open Late

tree.setup: You discover a small 24-hour bookshop while walking home late at night. The owner, a quiet man in his fifties, is reading behind the counter.
tree.setup_zh: 深夜走路回家时你发现了一家二十四小时小书店。五十多岁的老板安静地坐在柜台后面读书。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 欢迎。这么晚了还有人来，我很高兴。这家店已经开了二十年了，白天来的人越来越少，反而是夜里会来一些需要安静地方的人。
turns.text_pinyin: Huānyíng. Zhème wǎn le hái yǒu rén lái, wǒ hěn gāoxìng. Zhè jiā diàn yǐjīng kāi le èrshí nián le, báitiān lái de rén yuè lái yuè shǎo, fǎn'ér shì yè lǐ huì lái yīxiē xūyào ānjìng dìfang de rén.
turns.text_en: Welcome. I'm glad someone comes even this late. This shop has been open for twenty years. Fewer people come during the day, but at night, people who need a quiet place find their way here.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Ask about why he keeps the bookshop open 24 hours.

options.text_zh: 二十年……在这个时代坚持开一家实体书店，一定不容易。为什么选择开到深夜？不只是为了生意吧？
options.text_pinyin: Èrshí nián…… zài zhège shídài jiānchí kāi yī jiā shítǐ shūdiàn, yídìng bù róngyì. Wèi shénme xuǎnzé kāi dào shēnyè? Bù zhǐ shì wèile shēngyì ba?
options.text_en: Twenty years... persisting with a physical bookshop in this era can't be easy. Why choose to stay open late into the night? It's not just about business, is it?
options.feedback: Curious and respectful — sees the deeper purpose behind his choice.

options.text_zh: 你这里有畅销书榜吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhèlǐ yǒu chàngxiāo shū bǎng ma?
options.text_en: Do you have a bestseller list?
options.feedback: Treats a late-night sanctuary like a commercial store.

options.text_zh: 深夜开门不害怕吗？
options.text_pinyin: Shēnyè kāimén bú hàipà ma?
options.text_en: Aren't you afraid staying open at night?
options.feedback: Focuses on safety rather than the spirit of the place.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 不只是为了生意。深夜来的人大多不是为了买书，是为了找一个能待着的地方。有加班到很晚不想回空房子的人，有吵架后需要冷静的人，也有只是睡不着想翻翻书的人。我觉得一个城市需要这样的地方。
turns.text_pinyin: Bù zhǐ shì wèile shēngyì. Shēnyè lái de rén dàduō bú shì wèile mǎi shū, shì wèile zhǎo yīge néng dāi zhe de dìfang. Yǒu jiābān dào hěn wǎn bù xiǎng huí kōng fángzi de rén, yǒu chǎojià hòu xūyào lěngjìng de rén, yě yǒu zhǐ shì shuì bù zháo xiǎng fānfan shū de rén. Wǒ juéde yīge chéngshì xūyào zhèyàng de dìfang.
turns.text_en: Not just business. Most people who come at night aren't here to buy books — they're looking for somewhere to be. Some worked late and don't want to go back to an empty apartment. Some need to cool down after a fight. Some just can't sleep and want to browse. I think a city needs places like this.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on the role of this kind of space.

options.text_zh: 对啊，一个城市需要这样的地方——不卖东西，不问你为什么来，只是亮着灯等你。这家店不只是书店，是一盏留给深夜里需要温暖的人的灯。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ shuō de duì, yīge chéngshì xūyào zhèyàng de dìfang — bú mài dōngxi, bú wèn nǐ wèi shénme lái, zhǐ shì liàng zhe dēng děng nǐ. Zhè jiā diàn bù zhǐ shì shūdiàn, shì yī zhǎn liú gěi shēnyè lǐ xūyào wēnnuǎn de rén de dēng.
options.text_en: A city needs places like this — not selling anything, not asking why you came, leaving the light on and waiting. This shop isn't a bookstore — it's a lamp left on for people who need warmth at night.
options.feedback: And deeply appreciative — captures the essence of what he's created.

options.text_zh: 但这样的店怎么赚钱呢？房租那么贵。
options.text_pinyin: Dàn zhèyàng de diàn zěnme zhuàn qián ne? Fángzū nàme guì.
options.text_en: But how does a shop like this make money? Rent is so expensive.
options.feedback: Pragmatic but deflating — he clearly keeps it open for reasons beyond profit.

options.text_zh: 你平时看什么类型的书？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ píngshí kàn shénme lèixíng de shū?
options.text_en: What kind of books do you usually read?
options.feedback: Not wrong but shifts away from the deeper conversation about purpose.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 一盏灯……谢谢你这么说。你今晚随便坐，想看什么都可以。不用买，就待着就好。这里的灯会一直亮着的。
turns.text_pinyin: Yī zhǎn dēng…… xièxie nǐ zhème shuō. Nǐ jīn wǎn suíbiàn zuò, xiǎng kàn shénme dōu kěyǐ. Bú yòng mǎi, jiù dāi zhe jiù hǎo. Zhèlǐ de dēng huì yīzhí liàng zhe de.
turns.text_en: A lamp... thank you for saying that. Sit anywhere tonight, read whatever you like. No need to buy, just being here is enough. The light here will always be on.

tree.cultural_note: 24-hour bookshops (二十四小时书店) have become a cultural phenomenon in Chinese cities, most famously Sanlian Taofen Bookstore in Beijing. They serve as refuges for night owls, insomniacs, and people who simply need a quiet space. The concept resonates deeply in a culture where urban loneliness is widespread, and these shops are often described as「深夜里的一盏灯」(a light in the deep night) — spaces that offer belonging without conditions.

tree.setup: Your friend Xiaomeng's birthday is next week. She's the kind of person who never wants a fuss, but you know she secretly appreciates small, meaningful gestures.
tree.setup_zh: 朋友小梦下周过生日。她是那种从来不想麻烦别人的人，但你知道她其实很喜欢小小的、有心意的举动。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你别给我办什么生日会啊。我真的不喜欢那种大家围在一起唱生日歌的场面，太尴尬了。而且我也不想让朋友们花钱给我买礼物。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ bié gěi wǒ bàn shénme shēngrì huì a. Wǒ zhēn de bù xǐhuan nà zhǒng dàjiā wéi zài yīqǐ chàng shēngrì gē de chǎngmiàn, tài gāngà le. Érqiě wǒ yě bù xiǎng ràng péngyoumen huā qián gěi wǒ mǎi lǐwù.
turns.text_en: Don't throw me a birthday party. I really don't like the scene of everyone standing around singing happy birthday — it's too awkward. And I don't want friends spending money on gifts for me.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Suggest something small and meaningful instead.

options.text_zh: 不办party，也不买贵的礼物。但我可以做一件小事——那天早上给你写一封手写的信，把认识以来想跟你说但没说过的话写下来。你觉得怎么样？
options.text_pinyin: Bú bàn party, yě bù mǎi guì de lǐwù. Dàn wǒ kěyǐ zuò yī jiàn xiǎo shì — nà tiān zǎoshang gěi nǐ xiě yī fēng shǒuxiě de xìn, bǎ rènshi yǐlái xiǎng gēn nǐ shuō dàn méi shuō guò de huà xiě xiàlái. Nǐ juéde zěnmeyàng?
options.text_en: No party, no expensive gifts. But I can do one small thing — write you a handwritten letter that morning, putting down all the things I've wanted to say since we met but never did. What do you think?
options.feedback: Calibrated — respects her boundaries while offering something deeply personal.

options.text_zh: 那我们就出去吃一顿大餐吧！我请客。
options.text_pinyin: Nà wǒmen jiù chūqù chī yī dùn dàcān ba! Wǒ qǐngkè.
options.text_en: Then let's go out for a big dinner! My treat.
options.feedback: Still spending money and making it an event — doesn't respect her wish for simplicity.

options.text_zh: 好吧，那就什么都不做了。
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo ba, nà jiù shénme dōu bú zuò le.
options.text_en: Okay, then we'll do nothing.
options.feedback: Takes her at face value — misses that she'd appreciate something thoughtful.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 手写的信？你……你真的会这么做吗？我已经很多年没有收到过手写的东西了。现在所有人都用手机，打几个字发出去就算了。你说的那些没说过的话是什么？
turns.text_pinyin: Shǒuxiě de xìn? Nǐ…… nǐ zhēn de huì zhème zuò ma? Wǒ yǐjīng hěn duō nián méiyǒu shōu dào guò shǒuxiě de dōngxi le. Xiànzài suǒyǒu rén dōu yòng shǒujī, dǎ jǐ ge zì fā chūqù jiù suàn le. Nǐ shuō de nàxiē méi shuō guò de huà shì shénme?
turns.text_en: A handwritten letter? You'd... really do that? I haven't received anything handwritten in years. Everyone uses phones now, type a few characters and send it. What are these unsaid things you mentioned?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Give a hint of what you'd write.

options.text_zh: 等你收到再看吧。但先告诉你一点——我想谢谢你做了一个不需要很多话就能让人安心的人。有些人在你身边，你会觉得世界安静了一点。你就是那种人。
options.text_pinyin: Děng nǐ shōu dào zài kàn ba. Dàn xiān gàosù nǐ yīdiǎn — wǒ xiǎng xièxie nǐ zuò le yīge bù xūyào hěn duō huà jiù néng ràng rén ānxīn de rén. Yǒuxiē rén zài nǐ shēnbiān, nǐ huì juéde shìjiè ānjìng le yīdiǎn. Nǐ jiù shì nà zhǒng rén.
options.text_en: Wait till you get it. But I can tell you a little — I want to thank you for being someone who makes people feel at ease without needing many words. Some people, when they're near you, make the world feel quieter. You're that kind of person.
options.feedback: Moving and specific — gives her a preview that shows genuine attention to who she is.

options.text_zh: 到时候你就知道了。现在不告诉你。
options.text_pinyin: Dào shíhou nǐ jiù zhīdào le. Xiànzài bú gàosù nǐ.
options.text_en: You'll find out when the time comes. Not telling you now.
options.feedback: Playful but misses the chance to give her something meaningful now.

options.text_zh: 就是一些平时不好意思说的话。
options.text_pinyin: Jiù shì yīxiē píngshí bù hǎoyìsi shuō de huà.
options.text_en: Just some things I'm usually too embarrassed to say.
options.feedback: Vague — doesn't give her the warmth she's clearly hoping for.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你让我觉得世界安静了一点……天哪，如果你把这些写在信里，我肯定会哭的。谢谢你。这已经是我收到的最好的生日礼物了，虽然信还没写。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ ràng wǒ juéde shìjiè ānjìng le yīdiǎn…… tiān na, rúguǒ nǐ bǎ zhèxiē xiě zài xìn lǐ, wǒ kěndìng huì kū de. Xièxie nǐ. Zhè yǐjīng shì wǒ shōu dào de zuì hǎo de shēngrì lǐwù le, suīrán xìn hái méi xiě.
turns.text_en: You make the world feel quieter... oh my, if you write that in the letter, I'll definitely cry. Thank you. This is already the best birthday gift I've received, even though the letter hasn't been written yet.

tree.cultural_note: In modern Chinese culture, handwritten letters have become rare and therefore deeply treasured. The contrast between a WeChat message and a handwritten letter represents the difference between convenience and intention. Chinese birthdays are traditionally celebrated with longevity noodles (长寿面) rather than cake, though Western-style birthday celebrations have become common. The concept of giving emotional rather than material gifts is increasingly valued among younger Chinese.

title: Things I Forgot to Say Thank You For

tree.setup: You and your friend Xiaoyu are walking through a quiet park in the evening. She mentions something she's been thinking about.
tree.setup_zh: 傍晚你和朋友小雨在安静的公园里散步。她提到了一些她最近在想的事。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我今天突然想到一件事——我们平时会因为大事跟人说谢谢，但那些小的、不起眼的帮助，好像从来没有感谢过。比如我妈每天早上帮我放好杯子和水，我从来没有对她说过谢谢。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ jīntiān tūrán xiǎng dào yī jiàn shì — wǒmen píngshí huì yīnwèi dà shì gēn rén shuō xièxie, dàn nàxiē xiǎo de, bù qǐyǎn de bāngzhù, hǎoxiàng cónglái méiyǒu gǎnxiè guò. Bǐrú wǒ mā měi tiān zǎoshang bāng wǒ fàng hǎo bēizi hé shuǐ, wǒ cónglái méiyǒu duì tā shuō guò xièxie.
turns.text_en: I had a sudden thought today — we say thank you to people for big things, but those small, inconspicuous acts of help we never seem to acknowledge. Like my mom puts out a cup and water for me every morning, and I've never once thanked her for it.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her reflection on gratitude.

options.text_zh: 也许正因为这些事太小太日常了，我们反而忽略了。但这些小事最费心——她不是偶尔做一次，是每天都做，不求回报。最深的爱往往藏在最平常的事里。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ zhèng yīnwèi zhèxiē shì tài xiǎo tài rìcháng le, wǒmen fǎn'ér hūlüè le. Dàn zhèxiē xiǎo shì zuì fèi xīn — tā bú shì ǒu'ěr zuò yī cì, shì měi tiān dōu zuò, bù qiú huíbào. Zuì shēn de ài wǎngwǎng cáng zài zuì píngcháng de shì lǐ.
options.text_en: Maybe precisely because these things are so small and daily, we overlook them. But they take the most care — she doesn't do it once in a while, she does it every day, expecting nothing back. The deepest love hides in the most ordinary things.
options.feedback: Profound and gentle — illuminates the invisible labor of love.

options.text_zh: 那你下次记得说谢谢就好了。
options.text_pinyin: Nà nǐ xià cì jìde shuō xièxie jiù hǎo le.
options.text_en: Just remember to say thank you next time.
options.feedback: Oversimplified — treats a deep realization as a simple action item.

options.text_zh: 父母做这些是应该的吧。
options.text_pinyin: Fùmǔ zuò zhèxiē shì yīnggāi de ba.
options.text_en: Isn't it just what parents are supposed to do?
options.feedback: Dismissive — takes parental love for granted.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 最深的爱藏在最平常的事里……你说得我眼眶都湿了。我以后要学会看见这些小事。不一定要说出「谢谢」两个字，但至少心里要知道。
turns.text_pinyin: Zuì shēn de ài cáng zài zuì píngcháng de shì lǐ…… nǐ shuō de wǒ yǎnkuàng dōu shī le. Wǒ yǐhòu yào xuéhuì kànjiàn zhèxiē xiǎo shì. Bú yídìng yào shuō chū 'xièxie' liǎng ge zì, dàn zhìshǎo xīn lǐ yào zhīdào.
turns.text_en: The deepest love hidden in the most ordinary things... you're making my eyes water. From now on I want to learn to see these small things. I don't necessarily have to say the words 'thank you,' but at least know it in my heart.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share something you're grateful for but haven't expressed.

options.text_zh: 我也有。一个朋友每次跟我说话都认真看着我的眼睛，让我觉得每句话都重要。我从没告诉过她，但这个习惯让我在最不自信的时候也觉得自己值得被听到。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yě yǒu. Yīge péngyou měi cì gēn wǒ shuōhuà dōu rènzhēn kàn zhe wǒ de yǎnjīng, ràng wǒ juéde měi jù huà dōu zhòngyào. Wǒ cóng méi gàosù guò tā, dàn zhège xíguàn ràng wǒ zài zuì bú zìxìn de shíhou yě juéde zìjǐ zhíde bèi tīng dào.
options.text_en: I have one too. A friend always looks me in the eyes when we talk, making every word feel important. I've never told her, but that habit makes me feel worth hearing even at my least confident.
options.feedback: Vulnerable and reciprocal — matches her openness with genuine personal sharing.

options.text_zh: 我们应该多跟家人说说心里话。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒmen yīnggāi duō gēn jiārén shuōshuō xīnlǐ huà.
options.text_en: We should speak from the heart more with our families.
options.feedback: Generic advice — doesn't match the personal depth she offered.

options.text_zh: 你回去可以给你妈买个礼物。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ huí qù kěyǐ gěi nǐ mā mǎi ge lǐwù.
options.text_en: You could buy your mom a gift when you go back.
options.feedback: Materializes gratitude — misses the point about seeing, not spending.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 值得被听到的……你那个朋友一定不知道她给了你多大的力量。也许这就是最好的善意——做的人不觉得特别，但收到的人一辈子记得。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhíde bèi tīng dào de…… nǐ nàge péngyou yídìng bù zhīdào tā gěi le nǐ duō dà de lìliang. Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì zuì hǎo de shànyì — zuò de rén bù juéde tèbié, dàn shōu dào de rén yíbèizi jìde.
turns.text_en: Worth being heard... your friend probably has no idea how much strength she gave you. Maybe that's the best kind of kindness — the person doing it doesn't think it's special, but the one receiving it remembers for a lifetime.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese culture traditionally emphasizes implicit gratitude over explicit verbal thanks — love and care are shown through actions rather than words. The phrase「心里知道」(know it in your heart) captures this cultural approach to gratitude. Young Chinese people are increasingly exploring the emotional intelligence of naming and expressing gratitude, influenced by both Western psychological frameworks and a renewed appreciation for traditional filial piety (孝, xiào).

tree.setup: You're visiting your friend Lao Zhang at his pottery studio. He's working on a piece that has taken him three months to finish.
tree.setup_zh: 你去朋友老张的陶艺工作室拜访他。他正在做一件花了三个月的作品。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这个杯子我做了三个月。不是因为复杂，是因为每做一步都要等——等泥干、等窑温、等釉色慢慢出来。做陶最重要的不是技术，是耐心。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhège bēizi wǒ zuò le sān ge yuè. Bú shì yīnwèi fùzá, shì yīnwèi měi zuò yī bù dōu yào děng — děng ní gān, děng yáo wēn, děng yòu sè mànman chūlái. Zuò táo zuì zhòngyào de bú shì jìshù, shì nàixīn.
turns.text_en: This cup took me three months. Not because it's complex, but because every step requires waiting — waiting for the clay to dry, for the kiln temperature, for the glaze color to emerge slowly. The most important thing in pottery isn't technique, it's patience.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his philosophy of patience.

options.text_zh: 三个月做一个杯子。在现在这个什么都要快的世界里，这本身就是一种态度。你没有催它，而是等它准备好。也许最好的东西都不能被催出来。
options.text_pinyin: Sān ge yuè zuò yīge bēizi. Zài xiànzài zhège shénme dōu yào kuài de shìjiè lǐ, zhè běnshēn jiù shì yī zhǒng tàidù. Nǐ méiyǒu cuī tā, ér shì děng tā zhǔnbèi hǎo. Yěxǔ zuì hǎo de dōngxi dōu bù néng bèi cuī chūlái.
options.text_en: Three months for one cup. In a world where everything has to be fast, that's a statement in itself. You didn't rush it, you waited until it was ready. Maybe the best things can't be hurried.
options.feedback: Resonant — connects his craft philosophy to a broader life truth.

options.text_zh: 三个月？你一个月能做几个？
options.text_pinyin: Sān ge yuè? Nǐ yīge yuè néng zuò jǐ ge?
options.text_en: Three months? How many can you make in a month?
options.feedback: Quantifies artistry — applies production metrics to a craft.

options.text_zh: 我觉得用机器做更快，效果也差不多。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde yòng jīqì zuò gèng kuài, xiàoguǒ yě chàbuduō.
options.text_en: I think machine-made ones are faster and look about the same.
options.feedback: Dismissive — equates handcraft with industrial production.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 不能被催出来……对。每次我想赶的时候，作品就会出问题。泥没干就上釉，釉会裂。火候不够就开窑，颜色不对。陶在教我一件事：有些事就是需要时间，急不来。
turns.text_pinyin: Bù néng bèi cuī chūlái…… duì. Měi cì wǒ xiǎng gǎn de shíhou, zuòpǐn jiù huì chū wèntí. Ní méi gān jiù shàng yòu, yòu huì liè. Huǒhòu bú gòu jiù kāi yáo, yánsè bú duì. Táo zài jiāo wǒ yī jiàn shì: yǒuxiē shì jiù shì xūyào shíjiān, jí bù lái.
turns.text_en: Can't be hurried... right. Every time I try to rush, the piece goes wrong. Apply glaze before the clay is dry and it cracks. Open the kiln too early and the color is off. Pottery teaches me something: some things simply need time, you can't rush them.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Extend the metaphor to life.

options.text_zh: 人也是一样的。有时候我们对自己太急了——想赶快成功、赶快弄清楚自己要什么。但也许我们也需要「等泥干」的过程。给自己时间，让事情自然地发生。
options.text_pinyin: Rén yě shì yíyàng de. Yǒu shíhou wǒmen duì zìjǐ tài jí le — xiǎng gǎnkuài chénggōng, gǎnkuài nòng qīngchǔ zìjǐ yào shénme. Dàn yěxǔ wǒmen yě xūyào 'děng ní gān' de guòchéng. Gěi zìjǐ shíjiān, ràng shìqíng zìrán de fāshēng.
options.text_en: People are the same. We're too impatient with ourselves — wanting to succeed quickly, figure things out fast. But maybe we need the 'waiting for clay to dry' process. Give ourselves time. Let things happen naturally.
options.feedback: Perfect metaphor extension — makes the pottery lesson personal and universal.

options.text_zh: 你有没有想过开陶艺课？很多人会感兴趣的。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu xiǎng guò kāi táoyì kè? Hěn duō rén huì gǎn xìngqù de.
options.text_en: Have you thought about teaching pottery classes? A lot of people would be interested.
options.feedback: Entrepreneurial thinking — misses the contemplative moment.

options.text_zh: 道理是这样，但现实中谁有这个时间慢慢等呢？工作压力那么大。
options.text_pinyin: Dàolǐ shì zhèyàng, dàn xiànshí zhōng shéi yǒu zhège shíjiān mànman děng ne? Gōngzuò yālì nàme dà.
options.text_en: That makes sense in theory, but who has time to wait slowly in real life? Work pressure is so intense.
options.feedback: Lao Zhang just offered a metaphor for patience in life, and you dismiss it as impractical. That's exactly the mindset the metaphor was trying to soften.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 等泥干的过程……你说得好。来，这个杯子送给你。下次喝茶的时候，记住这三个月——提醒自己不用那么急。
turns.text_pinyin: Děng ní gān de guòchéng…… nǐ shuō de hǎo. Lái, zhège bēizi sòng gěi nǐ. Xià cì hē chá de shíhou, jìzhù zhè sān ge yuè — tíxǐng zìjǐ bú yòng nàme jí.
turns.text_en: The 'waiting for clay to dry' process... well said. Here, this cup is for you. Next time you drink tea, remember these three months — a reminder not to rush.

tree.cultural_note: Pottery (陶艺) has experienced a renaissance in Chinese urban culture as young professionals seek hands-on, meditative activities. Traditional Chinese ceramics, from Jingdezhen porcelain to Yixing clay teapots, represent centuries of patient craftsmanship. The concept of「急不来」(can't be rushed) connects to the Daoist principle of「无为」(wúwéi, non-forcing) — allowing things to unfold at their natural pace.

tree.setup: An elderly tea master, Teacher Xu, invites you to sit at her tea table. She moves slowly, washing the cups, warming the pot, measuring the leaves. There is no hurry.
tree.setup_zh: 一位年长的茶艺师徐老师请你在她的茶桌前坐下。她动作很慢，洗杯、温壶、量茶叶。一切都不急。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 泡茶最重要的第一步不是放茶叶，是洗杯子。你看我洗这个杯子的时候，不只是在洗干净，也是在让自己安静下来。茶不急，人也不应该急。
turns.text_pinyin: Pào chá zuì zhòngyào de dì yī bù bú shì fàng cháyè, shì xǐ bēizi. Nǐ kàn wǒ xǐ zhège bēizi de shíhou, bù zhǐ shì zài xǐ gānjìng, yě shì zài ràng zìjǐ ānjìng xiàlái. Chá bù jí, rén yě bù yīnggāi jí.
turns.text_en: The most important first step of brewing tea isn't adding leaves — it's washing the cups. When I wash this cup, I'm not just cleaning it, I'm also letting myself settle down. Tea isn't in a hurry, and neither should we be.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to Teacher Xu's first lesson.

options.text_zh: 洗杯子是在为茶做准备，也是在为自己做准备。如果心没有静下来，再好的茶也喝不出味道。原来泡茶从洗杯子那一刻就开始了。
options.text_pinyin: Xǐ bēizi shì zài wèi chá zuò zhǔnbèi, yě shì zài wèi zìjǐ zuò zhǔnbèi. Rúguǒ xīn méiyǒu jìng xiàlái, zài hǎo de chá yě hē bù chū wèidào. Yuánlái pào chá cóng xǐ bēizi nà yī kè jiù kāishǐ le.
options.text_en: Washing the cups is preparing for the tea, and preparing yourself. If the heart hasn't settled, even the finest tea won't taste right. So brewing really begins from the moment you wash the cups.
options.feedback: Attentive and receptive — absorbs the lesson and articulates it back.

options.text_zh: 什么茶最贵？
options.text_pinyin: Shénme chá zuì guì?
options.text_en: What tea is the most expensive?
options.feedback: Commodifies the experience.

options.text_zh: 可以快一点吗？我等一下还有事。
options.text_pinyin: Kěyǐ kuài yīdiǎn ma? Wǒ děng yíxià hái yǒu shì.
options.text_en: Can you go a bit faster? I have something to do later.
options.feedback: Violates the entire spirit of the tea ceremony — rushing defeats the purpose.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你学得很快。第一泡茶我们不喝，倒掉。不是因为不好，是因为第一泡是茶叶在醒来。就像人一样，刚起床的时候不是最好的状态。给它一点时间，它会展开自己。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ xué de hěn kuài. Dì yī pào chá wǒmen bù hē, dào diào. Bú shì yīnwèi bù hǎo, shì yīnwèi dì yī pào shì cháyè zài xǐng lái. Jiù xiàng rén yíyàng, gāng qǐchuáng de shíhou bú shì zuì hǎo de zhuàngtài. Gěi tā yīdiǎn shíjiān, tā huì zhǎnkāi zìjǐ.
turns.text_en: You learn quickly. The first brew we don't drink — we pour it out. Not because it's bad, but because the first brew is the tea leaves waking up. Just like people, we're not at our best right after waking. Give it some time, and it will unfold.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on the metaphor of tea leaves unfolding.

options.text_zh: 给它时间让它展开自己——这不只是在说茶叶吧。我觉得人也一样，有的人很快就绽放了，有的人需要慢慢泡。不能因为第一印象就判断一个人的好坏。
options.text_pinyin: Gěi tā shíjiān ràng tā zhǎnkāi zìjǐ — zhè bù zhǐ shì zài shuō cháyè ba. Wǒ juéde rén yě yíyàng, yǒu de rén hěn kuài jiù zhànfàng le, yǒu de rén xūyào mànman pào. Bù néng yīnwèi dì yī yìnxiàng jiù pànduàn yīge rén de hǎo huài.
options.text_en: Giving it time to unfold itself — you're not just talking about tea leaves, are you? I think people are the same. Some bloom quickly, others need slow steeping. You can't judge someone by the first impression.
options.feedback: Shows genuine insight — extends the tea metaphor to human relationships.

options.text_zh: 原来第一泡不能喝，我以前都不知道。
options.text_pinyin: Yuánlái dì yī pào bù néng hē, wǒ yǐqián dōu bù zhīdào.
options.text_en: I didn't know you couldn't drink the first brew.
options.feedback: Surface-level — treats it as a factoid rather than a lesson.

options.text_zh: 这个茶闻起来好香！
options.text_pinyin: Zhège chá wén qǐlái hǎo xiāng!
options.text_en: This tea smells wonderful!
options.feedback: Appreciative but doesn't engage with the philosophical layer.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你理解了。茶道不是教你怎么泡茶，是教你怎么看人、看世界。来，第二泡好了。慢慢喝，让茶在嘴里待一会儿。好东西都值得多等一等。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ lǐjiě le. Chádào bú shì jiāo nǐ zěnme pào chá, shì jiāo nǐ zěnme kàn rén, kàn shìjiè. Lái, dì èr pào hǎo le. Mànman hē, ràng chá zài zuǐ lǐ dāi yīhuìr. Hǎo dōngxi dōu zhíde duō děng yī děng.
turns.text_en: You understand. The way of tea doesn't teach you how to brew — it teaches you how to see people, how to see the world. Come, the second brew is ready. Drink slowly, let the tea rest in your mouth a while. Good things are always worth waiting for.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese tea ceremony (茶道 or 茶艺) is not merely about making tea — it's a meditative practice that cultivates patience, presence, and appreciation. The act of discarding the first brew (洗茶, xǐ chá) symbolizes both cleansing and awakening. In Chinese philosophy, tea has been linked to self-cultivation for over a thousand years, with the saying「茶如人生」(tea is like life) — bitter at first, sweet in the aftertaste.

tree.setup: Your friend Xiaohe recently decluttered her apartment and now has very few possessions. You visit her simple, open living space.
tree.setup_zh: 朋友小禾最近清理了公寓，现在东西很少。你来到她简洁开阔的家。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你觉得空吗？上个月我把不需要的东西都送掉或者捐了。以前这里堆得满满的，现在只留下真正用得上的。说来奇怪，东西少了以后我反而觉得空间大了，心里也宽了。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ juéde kōng ma? Shàng ge yuè wǒ bǎ bù xūyào de dōngxi dōu sòng diào huòzhě juān le. Yǐqián zhèlǐ duī de mǎn mǎn de, xiànzài zhǐ liú xià zhēnzhèng yòng de shàng de. Shuō lái qíguài, dōngxi shǎo le yǐhòu wǒ fǎn'ér juéde kōngjiān dà le, xīn lǐ yě kuān le.
turns.text_en: Does it feel empty to you? Last month I gave away or donated everything I didn't need. This place used to be packed full. Now I only keep what I actually use. Strangely, with fewer things, I feel like the space got bigger and my mind feels more open.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share your thoughts on having fewer things.

options.text_zh: 不觉得空，觉得通透。我觉得我们拥有的东西太多的时候，东西反而在拥有我们——你得整理它、维护它、担心它。少一点东西，就多一点自由。
options.text_pinyin: Bù juéde kōng, juéde tōngtòu. Wǒ juéde wǒmen yōngyǒu de dōngxi tài duō de shíhou, dōngxi fǎn'ér zài yōngyǒu wǒmen — nǐ děi zhěnglǐ tā, wéihù tā, dānxīn tā. Shǎo yīdiǎn dōngxi, jiù duō yīdiǎn zìyóu.
options.text_en: It doesn't feel empty — it feels clear. I think when we own too many things, the things end up owning us. You have to organize them, maintain them, worry about them. Fewer things means more freedom.
options.feedback: Sharp reversal — things owning us is a powerful reframe.

options.text_zh: 你不怕以后需要的时候没有了吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ bú pà yǐhòu xūyào de shíhou méiyǒu le ma?
options.text_en: Aren't you afraid you'll need something later and it won't be there?
options.feedback: Projects scarcity anxiety — contradicts the freedom she's found.

options.text_zh: 你那些东西都送给谁了？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ nàxiē dōngxi dōu sòng gěi shéi le?
options.text_en: Who did you give all your stuff to?
options.feedback: Focuses on the logistics rather than the philosophy.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 东西在拥有我们……你说得太好了。我以前买了很多衣服，但每天早上站在衣柜前反而不知道穿什么。现在只有十几件，早上起来拿一件就走，特别轻松。选择少了，反而更自在了。
turns.text_pinyin: Dōngxi zài yōngyǒu wǒmen…… nǐ shuō de tài hǎo le. Wǒ yǐqián mǎi le hěn duō yīfu, dàn měi tiān zǎoshang zhàn zài yīguì qián fǎn'ér bù zhīdào chuān shénme. Xiànzài zhǐ yǒu shí jǐ jiàn, zǎoshang qǐlái ná yī jiàn jiù zǒu, tèbié qīngsōng. Xuǎnzé shǎo le, fǎn'ér gèng zìzai le.
turns.text_en: Things owning us... that's so true. I used to have so many clothes, but every morning standing in front of the closet I couldn't decide what to wear. Now I have just a dozen pieces — I grab one in the morning and go. So much easier. Fewer choices, more freedom.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on choice overload.

options.text_zh: 选择太多其实是一种负担。不只是衣服，信息和机会也是。我们觉得选择多是好事，但太多反而让人焦虑。真正的自由不是无限的选择，而是知道自己要什么。
options.text_pinyin: Xuǎnzé tài duō qíshí shì yī zhǒng fùdān. Bù zhǐ shì yīfu, xìnxī hé jīhuì yě shì. Wǒmen juéde xuǎnzé duō shì hǎo shì, dàn tài duō fǎn'ér ràng rén jiāolǜ. Zhēnzhèng de zìyóu bú shì wúxiàn de xuǎnzé, ér shì zhīdào zìjǐ yào shénme.
options.text_en: Too many choices is actually a burden. Not just clothes — information and opportunities too. We think more options is better, but too many causes anxiety. Real freedom isn't infinite choices — it's knowing what you want.
options.feedback: Deep and applicable — extends minimalism from material to existential.

options.text_zh: 我做不到，我太喜欢买东西了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ zuò bú dào, wǒ tài xǐhuan mǎi dōngxi le.
options.text_en: I could never do that, I love shopping too much.
options.feedback: Self-focused — doesn't engage with her journey.

options.text_zh: 十几件衣服够换洗吗？
options.text_pinyin: Shí jǐ jiàn yīfu gòu huànxǐ ma?
options.text_en: Are a dozen pieces enough to rotate?
options.feedback: Practical concern that misses the philosophical point.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 知道自己要什么……这也许就是极简主义真正的意义——不是为了少，而是为了清楚。清楚什么是重要的，然后把不重要的放下。谢谢你，今天的对话就像我的家一样，简单但有分量。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhīdào zìjǐ yào shénme…… zhè yěxǔ jiù shì jíjiǎn zhǔyì zhēnzhèng de yìyì — bú shì wèile shǎo, ér shì wèile qīngchǔ. Qīngchǔ shénme shì zhòngyào de, ránhòu bǎ bú zhòngyào de fàng xià. Xièxie nǐ, jīntiān de duìhuà jiù xiàng wǒ de jiā yíyàng, jiǎndān dàn yǒu fènliàng.
turns.text_en: Knowing what you want... maybe that's what minimalism truly means — not about having less, but about having clarity. Being clear about what matters, then letting go of what doesn't. Thank you — today's conversation is like my home: simple but substantial.

tree.cultural_note: Minimalism (极简主义) has gained significant traction in China's urban centers, partly as a reaction to the rapid consumerism of the past decades. The concept resonates with traditional Chinese philosophical ideas — Daoist simplicity (简朴, jiǎnpǔ) and Buddhist non-attachment.
