The phrase「断舍离」(duàn shě lí, break-discard-separate), borrowed from a Japanese decluttering philosophy, has become a common term in Chinese lifestyle discussions.

tree.setup: You notice your colleague Xiaowen writing a letter by hand during lunch break. In the age of instant messaging, it catches your attention.
tree.setup_zh: 午休时你注意到同事小文在手写一封信。在即时通讯的时代，这引起了你的注意。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 别觉得奇怪。我每个月都会给住在乡下的外婆写一封信。她不会用智能手机，家里的座机也经常打不通。写信是我们之间唯一稳定的联系方式。
turns.text_pinyin: Bié juéde qíguài. Wǒ měi ge yuè dōu huì gěi zhù zài xiāngxia de wàipó xiě yī fēng xìn. Tā bú huì yòng zhìnéng shǒujī, jiālǐ de zuòjī yě jīngcháng dǎ bù tōng. Xiě xìn shì wǒmen zhī jiān wéiyī wěndìng de liánxì fāngshì.
turns.text_en: Don't think it's strange. I write a letter to my grandmother in the countryside every month. She can't use a smartphone, and the landline at home often doesn't connect. Letters are the only reliable way we stay in touch.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her letter-writing habit.

options.text_zh: 一点都不奇怪，我觉得很美。写信需要时间、需要想一想要说什么——这种慢本身就是一种用心。你外婆收到信的时候一定特别开心。
options.text_pinyin: Yīdiǎn dōu bù qíguài, wǒ juéde hěn měi. Xiě xìn xūyào shíjiān, xūyào xiǎng yī xiǎng yào shuō shénme — zhè zhǒng màn běnshēn jiù shì yī zhǒng yòng xīn. Nǐ wàipó shōu dào xìn de shíhou yídìng tèbié kāixīn.
options.text_en: Not strange at all — I think it's beautiful. Writing letters takes time, takes thinking about what to say. That slowness itself is a form of care. Your grandmother must be so happy when she receives them.
options.feedback: Warm and appreciative — honors the intentionality behind letter writing.

options.text_zh: 你可以教她用微信视频啊。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ jiāo tā yòng Wēixìn shìpín a.
options.text_en: You could teach her to use WeChat video.
options.feedback: She's not writing letters because she has no other option; the ritual matters.

options.text_zh: 现在还有人写信吗？感觉像上个世纪的事了。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài hái yǒu rén xiě xìn ma? Gǎnjué xiàng shàng ge shìjì de shì le.
options.text_en: Do people still write letters? Feels like something from last century.
options.feedback: Dismissive — treats a meaningful practice as outdated.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 她每次回信都写得很慢，字歪歪扭扭的，因为她只上过三年学。但她每封信的最后都会写：「外婆想你，吃饱穿暖。」这八个字我看了一百多遍，每次看到都会鼻子酸。
turns.text_pinyin: Tā měi cì huí xìn dōu xiě de hěn màn, zì wāiwāi niūniū de, yīnwèi tā zhǐ shàng guò sān nián xué. Dàn tā měi fēng xìn de zuìhòu dōu huì xiě: 'Wàipó xiǎng nǐ, chī bǎo chuān nuǎn.' Zhè bā ge zì wǒ kàn le yībǎi duō biàn, měi cì kàn dào dōu huì bízi suān.
turns.text_en: She writes back very slowly, her characters crooked and wobbly because she only had three years of schooling. But at the end of every letter she writes: 'Grandma misses you — eat well and dress warmly.' I've read those eight characters over a hundred times, and every time my nose stings.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the beauty of her grandmother's letters.

options.text_zh: 八个字比一千条微信消息都重。那些字是她一笔一笔写的，每一笔都带着对你的牵挂。手写的字有温度、有重量，是握在手里的爱。这些信你好好保存。
options.text_pinyin: Bā ge zì bǐ yīqiān tiáo Wēixìn xiāoxi dōu zhòng. Nàxiē zì shì tā yī bǐ yī bǐ xiě de, měi yī bǐ dōu dài zhe duì nǐ de qiānguà. Shǒuxiě de zì yǒu wēndù, yǒu zhòngliàng, shì wò zài shǒu lǐ de ài. Zhèxiē xìn nǐ hǎohǎo bǎocún.
options.text_en: Eight characters weigh more than a thousand WeChat messages. She wrote each stroke by hand, every stroke carrying her concern for you. Handwritten words have warmth, have weight — love you can hold. Keep these letters safe.
options.feedback: Deeply moving — captures the physical and emotional weight of handwritten words.

options.text_zh: 你外婆身体怎么样？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ wàipó shēntǐ zěnmeyàng?
options.text_en: How is your grandmother's health?
options.feedback: Not wrong but shifts from the beautiful detail she shared.

options.text_zh: 她的字写得好看吗？
options.text_pinyin: Tā de zì xiě de hǎokàn ma?
options.text_en: Is her handwriting nice?
options.feedback: She just said the characters are crooked, but that's what makes them precious.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 握在手里的爱……对，就是这种感觉。谢谢你能理解。我先把这封信写完，然后去邮局寄。虽然要走一个星期才能到，但我知道她每天都在等。
turns.text_pinyin: Wò zài shǒu lǐ de ài…… duì, jiù shì zhè zhǒng gǎnjué. Xièxie nǐ néng lǐjiě. Wǒ xiān bǎ zhè fēng xìn xiě wán, ránhòu qù yóujú jì. Suīrán yào zǒu yīge xīngqī cái néng dào, dàn wǒ zhīdào tā měi tiān dōu zài děng.
turns.text_en: Love you can hold in your hands... yes, that's exactly the feeling. Thank you for understanding. Let me finish this letter, then I'll go to the post office. It takes a week to arrive, but I know she's waiting every day.

tree.cultural_note: In rural China, many elderly people never learned to use digital technology, making physical letters their only connection to family members working in distant cities. China Post still delivers letters to remote villages, though the volume has dropped dramatically. The phrase「吃饱穿暖」(eat well, dress warmly) is a classic expression of Chinese parental and grandparental love — practical care distilled into the most essential wishes.

tree.setup: You and your friend Xiaojun are walking along the river when he stops at a specific bench and sits down with a familiar sigh.
tree.setup_zh: 你和朋友小军沿着河边走，他在一把特定的长椅前停下来，熟悉地叹了口气坐下。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这把长椅是我在这个城市最喜欢的地方。每次遇到想不通的事，我就来这里坐一会儿。不做什么，就看看水，听听风。坐完以后好像什么都没解决，但心里会舒服一点。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè bǎ chángyǐ shì wǒ zài zhège chéngshì zuì xǐhuan de dìfang. Měi cì yù dào xiǎng bù tōng de shì, wǒ jiù lái zhèlǐ zuò yīhuìr. Bú zuò shénme, jiù kànkan shuǐ, tīng tīng fēng. Zuò wán yǐhòu hǎoxiàng shénme dōu méi jiějué, dàn xīn lǐ huì shūfu yīdiǎn.
turns.text_en: This bench is my favorite place in the whole city. Whenever I can't figure something out, I come sit here for a while. I don't do anything — just watch the water, listen to the wind. When I leave, nothing seems solved, but my heart feels a bit better.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his attachment to this place.

options.text_zh: 有时候我们不需要解决问题，只需要一个能让自己安静下来的地方。这把长椅不会给你答案，但它给了你空间。有这样一个地方，是一种幸运。
options.text_pinyin: Yǒu shíhou wǒmen bù xūyào jiějué wèntí, zhǐ xūyào yīge néng ràng zìjǐ ānjìng xiàlái de dìfang. Zhè bǎ chángyǐ bú huì gěi nǐ dá'àn, dàn tā gěi le nǐ kōngjiān. Yǒu zhèyàng yīge dìfang, shì yī zhǒng xìngyùn.
options.text_en: Sometimes we don't need to solve problems, we just need a place that lets us be quiet. This bench won't give you answers, but it gives you space. Having a place like this is a kind of luck.
options.feedback: Gentle and affirming — values the role of sacred personal spaces.

options.text_zh: 你应该找人聊聊，别一个人闷着。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi zhǎo rén liáoliao, bié yīge rén mēn zhe.
options.text_en: You should talk to someone, don't just keep it inside.
options.feedback: Well-intentioned but invalidates his coping method.

options.text_zh: 这个长椅看起来很旧了，应该换一个新的。
options.text_pinyin: Zhège chángyǐ kàn qǐlái hěn jiù le, yīnggāi huàn yīge xīn de.
options.text_en: This bench looks old, they should replace it.
options.feedback: Completely misses the emotional significance — treats it as furniture.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 给了我空间……对。我觉得每个人都需要这样一个「自己的地方」。不需要大，不需要漂亮，只要到了那里就觉得能呼吸了。你有没有这样的地方？
turns.text_pinyin: Gěi le wǒ kōngjiān…… duì. Wǒ juéde měi ge rén dōu xūyào zhèyàng yīge 'zìjǐ de dìfang'. Bù xūyào dà, bù xūyào piàoliang, zhǐyào dào le nàlǐ jiù juéde néng hūxī le. Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu zhèyàng de dìfang?
turns.text_en: Gave me space... right. I think everyone needs a 'place of their own' like this. Doesn't need to be big or beautiful, just somewhere that when you arrive, you feel like you can breathe. Do you have a place like that?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share your own quiet place.

options.text_zh: 有。我家楼顶有一个小天台，晚上没人去。我有时候会上去看星星，虽然城市里能看到的很少。但站在那里吹着风，觉得自己很小，问题也跟着变小了。
options.text_pinyin: Yǒu. Wǒ jiā lóudǐng yǒu yīge xiǎo tiāntái, wǎnshang méi rén qù. Wǒ yǒu shíhou huì shàng qù kàn xīngxīng, suīrán chéngshì lǐ néng kàn dào de hěn shǎo. Dàn zhàn zài nàlǐ chuī zhe fēng, juéde zìjǐ hěn xiǎo, wèntí yě gēn zhe biàn xiǎo le.
options.text_en: Yes. There's a small rooftop above my building, nobody goes at night. Sometimes I look at stars — you can't see many in the city. But standing there in the wind, I feel small, and my problems shrink too.
options.feedback: Reciprocal and poetic — matches his vulnerability with your own.

options.text_zh: 我没有特别喜欢的地方，到处都差不多。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ méiyǒu tèbié xǐhuan de dìfang, dàochù dōu chàbuduō.
options.text_en: I don't have a special place, everywhere is about the same.
options.feedback: Flat — doesn't reciprocate his openness.

options.text_zh: 我一般去健身房释放压力。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yībān qù jiànshēnfáng shìfàng yālì.
options.text_en: I usually go to the gym to release stress.
options.feedback: Not wrong but shifts from contemplation to activity — different mode.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 问题也跟着变小了……哈哈，我喜欢这个说法。也许有一天我们可以交换一下——你来我的河边，我去你的天台。看看对方的世界是什么样的。
turns.text_pinyin: Wèntí yě gēn zhe biàn xiǎo le…… hāhā, wǒ xǐhuan zhège shuōfǎ. Yěxǔ yǒu yī tiān wǒmen kěyǐ jiāohuàn yíxià — nǐ lái wǒ de hé biān, wǒ qù nǐ de tiāntái. Kànkan duìfāng de shìjiè shì shénme yàng de.
turns.text_en: Problems shrink along... haha, I like that. Maybe one day we can trade — you come to my river, I go to your rooftop. See what each other's world looks like.

tree.cultural_note: In dense Chinese cities, finding personal space is a challenge. Many people develop an attachment to a specific bench, rooftop, or quiet corner that becomes their sanctuary. The concept of having a「自己的地方」(a place of one's own) resonates deeply in a culture where shared living spaces are common and privacy is often limited.

tree.setup: You're sitting with your mother at the kitchen table. She's peeling an apple for you, and you notice her hands have more lines and spots than you remember.
tree.setup_zh: 你和妈妈坐在厨房桌前。她在给你削苹果，你注意到她的手上比你记忆中多了很多纹路和斑点。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看什么呢？削个苹果有什么好看的。来，吃吧。我小时候我妈也是这样给我削的，一圈一圈，皮不断。现在我的手没有她那么稳了。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn shénme ne? Xuē ge píngguǒ yǒu shénme hǎokàn de. Lái, chī ba. Wǒ xiǎo shíhou wǒ mā yě shì zhèyàng gěi wǒ xuē de, yī quān yī quān, pí bú duàn. Xiànzài wǒ de shǒu méiyǒu tā nàme wěn le.
turns.text_en: What are you looking at? What's so interesting about peeling an apple? Here, eat. When I was little, my mother peeled them for me the same way — round and round, the skin unbroken. My hands aren't as steady as hers anymore.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to your mother's comment about her hands.

options.text_zh: 我在看你的手。小时候觉得你的手什么都能做——做饭、缝衣服、牵着我过马路。现在你的手上多了很多纹路，但每一条都是为我们操劳的痕迹。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ zài kàn nǐ de shǒu. Xiǎo shíhou juéde nǐ de shǒu shénme dōu néng zuò — zuòfàn, féng yīfu, qiān zhe wǒ guò mǎlù. Xiànzài nǐ de shǒu shàng duō le hěn duō wénlù, dàn měi yī tiáo dōu shì wèi wǒmen cāoláo de hénjì.
options.text_en: I'm looking at your hands. When I was little, I thought they could do everything — cook, sew, hold mine crossing the road. Now there are more lines, but each is a mark of your work for us.
options.feedback: Tender and specific — sees her hands as a map of love and labor.

options.text_zh: 你应该多用护手霜。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi duō yòng hùshǒu shuāng.
options.text_en: You should use more hand cream.
options.feedback: Practical but misses the emotional depth of the moment.

options.text_zh: 苹果真甜，在哪儿买的？
options.text_pinyin: Píngguǒ zhēn tián, zài nǎr mǎi de?
options.text_en: The apple is really sweet, where did you buy it?
options.feedback: Sidesteps a rare intimate moment.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 看你说的。我老了嘛，手自然就这样了。不过你说得对，这双手确实没闲过。从你出生到现在，一直在忙。但只要你们过得好，忙一点也值得。
turns.text_pinyin: Kàn nǐ shuō de. Wǒ lǎo le ma, shǒu zìrán jiù zhèyàng le. Búguò nǐ shuō de duì, zhè shuāng shǒu quèshí méi xián guò. Cóng nǐ chūshēng dào xiànzài, yīzhí zài máng. Dàn zhǐyào nǐmen guò de hǎo, máng yīdiǎn yě zhíde.
turns.text_en: Listen to you. I'm getting old, of course my hands look like this. But you're right, these hands have never been idle. From the day you were born until now, always busy. But as long as you're all doing well, being busy is worth it.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Express what you've never said about her aging.

options.text_zh: 妈，我有时候害怕你变老。不是因为你不漂亮了，是因为我突然意识到时间一直在走，而我陪你的时间越来越少了。以后我会多回来的。
options.text_pinyin: Mā, wǒ yǒu shíhou hàipà nǐ biàn lǎo. Bú shì yīnwèi nǐ bú piàoliang le, shì yīnwèi wǒ tūrán yìshí dào shíjiān yīzhí zài zǒu, ér wǒ péi nǐ de shíjiān yuè lái yuè shǎo le. Yǐhòu wǒ huì duō huí lái de.
options.text_en: Mom, sometimes I'm afraid of you getting older. Not because you're less beautiful, but because I suddenly realize time keeps moving, and the time I spend with you is less and less. I'll come back more often.
options.feedback: Raw and honest — names the fear of loss that most children feel but rarely express.

options.text_zh: 你要多注意身体，按时吃饭。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yào duō zhùyì shēntǐ, ànshí chīfàn.
options.text_en: Take care of your health, eat your meals on time.
options.feedback: Role-reversed caring that avoids the emotional core.

options.text_zh: 你不老，看起来还很年轻。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ bù lǎo, kàn qǐlái hái hěn niánqīng.
options.text_en: You're not old, you still look very young.
options.feedback: Flattering but dishonest — deflects instead of meeting her honestly.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 傻孩子，说什么害怕。妈在呢。来，再吃一块苹果。只要你还肯让我削苹果，我就不算老。
turns.text_pinyin: Shǎ háizi, shuō shénme hàipà. Mā zài ne. Lái, zài chī yī kuài píngguǒ. Zhǐyào nǐ hái kěn ràng wǒ xuē píngguǒ, wǒ jiù bú suàn lǎo.
turns.text_en: Silly child, what's there to be afraid of. Mom's right here. Come on, eat another piece of apple. As long as you still let me peel apples for you, I'm not old.

tree.cultural_note: In Chinese culture, peeling fruit for someone is one of the most common daily expressions of love — parents peel fruit for children well into adulthood. The image of a mother's hands changing over time carries deep emotional resonance. The phrase「只要你们过得好」(as long as you're all doing well) encapsulates the selfless parental love (父母之爱) that is central to Chinese family values.

tree.setup: Your colleague Xiaohui mentions she spends every Saturday morning volunteering at an elderly care center. She's never talked about it before.
tree.setup_zh: 同事小慧提到她每个周六上午都在养老院做志愿者。她以前从来没说过这件事。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你别发朋友圈啊。我去养老院不是为了让别人知道。那里有个九十岁的爷爷，每次看到我来就特别高兴。他没有孩子来看他，我就陪他下下棋，听他讲以前的事。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ bié fā péngyouquān a. Wǒ qù yǎnglǎoyuàn bú shì wèile ràng biéren zhīdào. Nàlǐ yǒu ge jiǔshí suì de yéye, měi cì kàn dào wǒ lái jiù tèbié gāoxìng. Tā méiyǒu háizi lái kàn tā, wǒ jiù péi tā xià xia qí, tīng tā jiǎng yǐqián de shì.
turns.text_en: Don't post about this on Moments. I don't go to the care center for other people to know. There's a ninety-year-old grandfather there who gets so happy every time he sees me come. No children visit him, so I keep him company, play chess, and listen to his stories.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her quiet kindness.

options.text_zh: 不让别人知道的善意也许是最纯粹的。你做这件事不是为了被看见，而是因为那个爷爷需要被看见。这种安静的陪伴对他来说一定比任何药都管用。
options.text_pinyin: Bú ràng biéren zhīdào de shànyì yěxǔ shì zuì chúncuì de. Nǐ zuò zhè jiàn shì bú shì wèile bèi kànjiàn, ér shì yīnwèi nàge yéye xūyào bèi kànjiàn. Zhè zhǒng ānjìng de péibàn duì tā lái shuō yídìng bǐ rènhé yào dōu guǎnyòng.
options.text_en: Kindness that nobody knows about might be the purest kind. You do this not to be seen, but because that grandfather needs to be seen. That quiet companionship must work better for him than any medicine.
options.feedback: Honors both her humility and the old man's need — sees the giving in both directions.

options.text_zh: 你可以在网上发一下，鼓励更多人去当志愿者。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ zài wǎng shàng fā yíxià, gǔlì gèng duō rén qù dāng zhìyuànzhě.
options.text_en: You could post about it online to encourage more people to volunteer.
options.feedback: Well-intentioned but contradicts her explicit wish for privacy.

options.text_zh: 每周六都去？不会太累吗？
options.text_pinyin: Měi zhōuliù dōu qù? Bú huì tài lèi ma?
options.text_en: Every Saturday? Isn't that tiring?
options.feedback: Frames it as a burden rather than a gift.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你知道他跟我说过什么吗？他说：「小慧，你来的时候，这一天就不一样了。」就这一句话，让我觉得所有的坚持都是值得的。有时候一个人需要的不是什么大的帮助，只是有人记得他的存在。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhīdào tā gēn wǒ shuō guò shénme ma? Tā shuō: 'Xiǎo Huì, nǐ lái de shíhou, zhè yī tiān jiù bù yíyàng le.' Jiù zhè yī jù huà, ràng wǒ juéde suǒyǒu de jiānchí dōu shì zhíde de. Yǒu shíhou yīge rén xūyào de bú shì shénme dà de bāngzhù, zhǐ shì yǒu rén jìde tā de cúnzài.
turns.text_en: You know what he said to me once? He said: 'Xiaohui, the day you come is different from other days.' Just that one sentence made me feel all the persistence was worth it. Sometimes what a person needs isn't big help — just someone who remembers they exist.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on what it means to be remembered.

options.text_zh: 有人记得他的存在——这句话让我很感动。我们都怕被遗忘。你每周六去那里，是在告诉他：你很重要，你不是一个人。这比任何物质上的帮助都珍贵。
options.text_pinyin: Yǒu rén jìde tā de cúnzài — zhè jù huà ràng wǒ hěn gǎndòng. Wǒmen dōu pà bèi yíwàng. Nǐ měi zhōuliù qù nàlǐ, shì zài gàosù tā: nǐ hěn zhòngyào, nǐ bú shì yīge rén. Zhè bǐ rènhé wùzhì shàng de bāngzhù dōu zhēnguì.
options.text_en: Someone remembers they exist — that line moves me deeply. We all fear being forgotten. Going there every Saturday, you're telling him: you matter, you're not alone. That's more precious than any material help.
options.feedback: Deeply compassionate — names the universal fear and the antidote she provides.

options.text_zh: 养老院的条件怎么样？
options.text_pinyin: Yǎnglǎoyuàn de tiáojiàn zěnmeyàng?
options.text_en: What are the conditions like at the care center?
options.feedback: Shifts to facility assessment.

options.text_zh: 你有没有想过以后做社工？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu xiǎng guò yǐhòu zuò shègōng?
options.text_en: Have you thought about becoming a social worker?
options.feedback: Career-focused — not what this conversation is about.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你不是一个人……谢谢你能理解。这周六你要不要跟我一起去？不用做什么特别的，就去坐坐，听他讲讲故事。有些人的故事，需要有人听。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ bú shì yīge rén…… xièxie nǐ néng lǐjiě. Zhè zhōuliù nǐ yào bú yào gēn wǒ yīqǐ qù? Bú yòng zuò shénme tèbié de, jiù qù zuòzuo, tīng tā jiǎngjiang gùshi. Yǒuxiē rén de gùshi, xūyào yǒu rén tīng.
turns.text_en: You're not alone... thank you for understanding. Do you want to come with me this Saturday? You don't need to do anything special — just sit, listen to his stories. Some people's stories need someone to hear them.

tree.cultural_note: China's aging population has made elderly care a growing social concern. Many elderly people in care centers (养老院) receive few family visits, leading to profound loneliness. The concept of「安静的帮助」(quiet help) resonates with the Confucian ideal of modesty in good deeds —「做好事不留名」(do good without seeking recognition). Youth volunteering at care centers has become more common, though the emotional labor involved is often underappreciated.

tree.setup: You and your friend Xiaoting are watching the sunset from a hilltop at the edge of the city. The sky is painted in gold and pink.
tree.setup_zh: 你和朋友小婷在城市边缘的小山顶上看日落。天空被染成了金色和粉色。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你知道我为什么喜欢看日落吗？因为它每天都不一样。同样的太阳、同样的地方，但颜色永远不重复。就像每一天的结束都是独一无二的。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhīdào wǒ wèi shénme xǐhuan kàn rìluò ma? Yīnwèi tā měi tiān dōu bù yíyàng. Tóngyàng de tàiyáng, tóngyàng de dìfang, dàn yánsè yǒngyuǎn bù chóngfù. Jiù xiàng měi yī tiān de jiéshù dōu shì dúyī wú'èr de.
turns.text_en: Do you know why I like watching sunsets? Because they're different every day. Same sun, same spot, but the colors never repeat. Just like the ending of each day is one of a kind.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share what sunsets make you think about.

options.text_zh: 永远不重复……也许这就是它最美的地方。你看到的这一刻，过了就没有了。日落提醒我们，有些美只存在于当下。如果不停下来看，就真的错过了。
options.text_pinyin: Yǒngyuǎn bù chóngfù…… yěxǔ zhè jiù shì tā zuì měi de dìfang. Nǐ kàn dào de zhè yī kè, guò le jiù méiyǒu le. Rìluò tíxǐng wǒmen, yǒuxiē měi zhǐ cúnzài yú dāngxià. Rúguǒ bù tíng xiàlái kàn, jiù zhēn de cuòguò le.
options.text_en: Never repeating... maybe that's what makes it most beautiful. This moment you're seeing will be gone after it passes. Sunsets remind us that some beauty only exists in the present. If you don't stop to look, you truly miss it.
options.feedback: And present — captures the fleeting nature of beauty.

options.text_zh: 我觉得日出比日落更好看。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde rìchū bǐ rìluò gèng hǎokàn.
options.text_en: I think sunrise is more beautiful than sunset.
options.feedback: Comparative — breaks the contemplative mood by debating preferences.

options.text_zh: 你拍照了吗？发给我。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ pāi zhào le ma? Fā gěi wǒ.
options.text_en: Did you take a photo? Send it to me.
options.feedback: Commodifies the moment — a photo can't capture what she's describing.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 有些美只存在于当下……对。我有时候会想，如果我今天没有来这里，这个日落就没有人看到。不是说它不存在了，而是没有人为它停下脚步。美需要有人去看见。
turns.text_pinyin: Yǒuxiē měi zhǐ cúnzài yú dāngxià…… duì. Wǒ yǒu shíhou huì xiǎng, rúguǒ wǒ jīntiān méiyǒu lái zhèlǐ, zhège rìluò jiù méiyǒu rén kàn dào. Bú shì shuō tā bù cúnzài le, ér shì méiyǒu rén wèi tā tíng xià jiǎobù. Měi xūyào yǒu rén qù kànjiàn.
turns.text_en: Some beauty only exists in the present... right. Sometimes I think, if I hadn't come here today, nobody would have seen this sunset. Not that it wouldn't exist, but no one would have stopped for it. Beauty needs someone to see it.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on beauty needing a witness.

options.text_zh: 也许这就是为什么我们需要彼此。不只是为了陪伴，也是为了一起看见。一个人看日落是美的，两个人看日落是幸福的。谢谢你今天叫我来。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì wèi shénme wǒmen xūyào bǐcǐ. Bù zhǐ shì wèile péibàn, yě shì wèile yīqǐ kànjiàn. Yīge rén kàn rìluò shì měi de, liǎng ge rén kàn rìluò shì xìngfú de. Xièxie nǐ jīntiān jiào wǒ lái.
options.text_en: Maybe that's why we need each other. Not just for company, but to see together. Watching a sunset alone is beautiful; watching it together is happiness. Thank you for inviting me today.
options.feedback: Warm and complete — connects the beauty of nature to the beauty of friendship.

options.text_zh: 天要黑了，我们走吧，路不好走。
options.text_pinyin: Tiān yào hēi le, wǒmen zǒu ba, lù bù hǎo zǒu.
options.text_en: It's getting dark, let's go, the path is hard to walk.
options.feedback: Practical but breaks the spell of the moment.

options.text_zh: 明天还会有日落的，不用太感慨。
options.text_pinyin: Míngtiān hái huì yǒu rìluò de, bú yòng tài gǎnkǎi.
options.text_en: There'll be another sunset tomorrow, no need to get so emotional.
options.feedback: Dismissive — contradicts her point about each sunset being unique.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 一起看见……嗯。你看，最后一点光正在消失。别急着走，再等一等——最美的颜色往往出现在太阳完全落下以后。
turns.text_pinyin: Yīqǐ kànjiàn…… en. Nǐ kàn, zuìhòu yīdiǎn guāng zhèng zài xiāoshī. Bié jí zhe zǒu, zài děng yī děng — zuì měi de yánsè wǎngwǎng chūxiàn zài tàiyáng wánquán luò xià yǐhòu.
turns.text_en: Seeing together... yes. Look, the last bit of light is disappearing. Don't rush to leave, wait a little more — the most beautiful colors often appear after the sun has fully set.

tree.cultural_note: In Chinese poetry, sunset (日落/夕阳) carries a melancholic beauty, famously captured in Li Shangyin's verse「夕阳无限好，只是近黄昏」(the setting sun is infinitely beautiful, but it's already near dusk). The afterglow (晚霞) — the colors that emerge after the sun drops below the horizon — is considered the most stunning part of sunset. The tradition of watching sunsets together holds romantic and philosophical significance.

tree.setup: Your friend Xiaoyan has been through a difficult breakup. You meet her at a café three months later. She looks different — calmer, but with a quiet sadness still in her eyes.
tree.setup_zh: 朋友小燕经历了一次痛苦的分手。三个月后你在咖啡馆遇到她。她看起来不一样了——更平静了，但眼睛里还有一种安静的悲伤。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你不用小心翼翼的，我没有那么脆弱了。这三个月我想了很多，也哭了很多。现在不能说完全好了，但至少能正常地吃饭、睡觉、上班了。愈合这件事好像没有终点，只有慢慢变好的过程。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ bú yòng xiǎoxīn yìyì de, wǒ méiyǒu nàme cuìruò le. Zhè sān ge yuè wǒ xiǎng le hěn duō, yě kū le hěn duō. Xiànzài bù néng shuō wánquán hǎo le, dàn zhìshǎo néng zhèngcháng de chīfàn, shuìjiào, shàng bān le. Yùhé zhè jiàn shì hǎoxiàng méiyǒu zhōngdiǎn, zhǐ yǒu mànman biàn hǎo de guòchéng.
turns.text_en: You don't have to tiptoe around me — I'm not that fragile anymore. These three months I've thought a lot and cried a lot. I can't say I'm completely okay, but at least I can eat, sleep, and work normally again. Healing doesn't seem to have an endpoint, just a process of slowly getting better.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her honest assessment of healing.

options.text_zh: 能说出「还没有完全好」本身就是一种进步。很多人会假装没事，但你没有。你允许自己慢慢来，这才是真正的勇敢。不用给自己设一个「好了」的期限。
options.text_pinyin: Néng shuō chū 'hái méiyǒu wánquán hǎo' běnshēn jiù shì yī zhǒng jìnbù. Hěn duō rén huì jiǎzhuāng méi shì, dàn nǐ méiyǒu. Nǐ yǔnxǔ zìjǐ mànman lái, zhè cái shì zhēnzhèng de yǒnggǎn. Bú yòng gěi zìjǐ shè yīge 'hǎo le' de qīxiàn.
options.text_en: Being able to say 'not completely okay yet' is itself progress. Many people pretend they're fine, but you didn't. Allowing yourself to take it slowly — that's real courage. You don't need to set a deadline for 'being okay.'
options.feedback: Validating and wise — redefines courage as honesty rather than toughness.

options.text_zh: 时间会治好一切的，别想太多。
options.text_pinyin: Shíjiān huì zhì hǎo yīqiè de, bié xiǎng tài duō.
options.text_en: Time heals everything, don't overthink it.
options.feedback: Cliché — dismisses her careful processing.

options.text_zh: 你有没有考虑再找一个？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu kǎolǜ zài zhǎo yīge?
options.text_en: Have you thought about finding someone new?
options.feedback: Tone-deaf — rushes past healing toward replacement.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 不用给自己设期限……你说得对。我一直在逼自己快点好起来，好像不难过了才算正常。但也许难过也是正常的一部分。
turns.text_pinyin: Bú yòng gěi zìjǐ shè qīxiàn…… nǐ shuō de duì. Wǒ yīzhí zài bī zìjǐ kuài diǎn hǎo qǐlái, hǎoxiàng bù nánguò le cái suàn zhèngcháng. Dàn yěxǔ nánguò yě shì zhèngcháng de yī bùfen.
turns.text_en: No deadline for healing... you're right. I've been pushing myself to get better quickly, as if not being sad is what counts as normal. But maybe sadness is a normal part too.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Offer a gentle perspective on sadness as part of healing.

options.text_zh: 难过说明你真正爱过。如果没有感觉就不会有痛，但也不会有那些美好的记忆。伤口会慢慢结疤，但疤痕不是缺陷，是你经历过的证明。你比你以为的更坚强。
options.text_pinyin: Nánguò shuōmíng nǐ zhēnzhèng ài guò. Rúguǒ méiyǒu gǎnjué jiù bú huì yǒu tòng, dàn yě bú huì yǒu nàxiē měihǎo de jìyì. Shāngkǒu huì mànman jiē bā, dàn bāhén bú shì quēxiàn, shì nǐ jīnglì guò de zhèngmíng. Nǐ bǐ nǐ yǐwéi de gèng jiānqiáng.
options.text_en: Sadness means you truly loved. Without feeling there'd be no pain, but there'd be no beautiful memories either. Wounds slowly scar, but scars aren't flaws — they're proof of what you've lived through. You're stronger than you think.
options.feedback: Compassionate and affirming — reframes scars as evidence of a life fully lived.

options.text_zh: 你要不要去旅游散散心？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yào bú yào qù lǚyóu sànsàn xīn?
options.text_en: Do you want to go traveling to take your mind off things?
options.feedback: Offers escape rather than engagement with her process.

options.text_zh: 他也不值得你这么难过。
options.text_pinyin: Tā yě bù zhíde nǐ zhème nánguò.
options.text_en: He wasn't worth being this sad over.
options.feedback: Invalidating — judges her feelings instead of honoring them.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 疤痕是经历过的证明……谢谢你。你知道吗，这三个月你是第一个不叫我「振作起来」的人。有时候最好的安慰不是说「没关系」，而是说「你可以慢慢来」。
turns.text_pinyin: Bāhén shì jīnglì guò de zhèngmíng…… xièxie nǐ. Nǐ zhīdào ma, zhè sān ge yuè nǐ shì dì yīge bú jiào wǒ 'zhènzuò qǐlái' de rén. Yǒu shíhou zuì hǎo de ānwèi bú shì shuō 'méi guānxi', ér shì shuō 'nǐ kěyǐ mànman lái'.
turns.text_en: Scars are proof of experience... thank you. You know, in these three months you're the first person who didn't tell me to 'get over it.' Sometimes the best comfort isn't saying 'it's okay,' but saying 'you can take your time.'

tree.cultural_note: In Chinese culture, the phrase「振作起来」(zhènzuò qǐlái, pull yourself together) is commonly used to comfort someone, but it can inadvertently pressure people to suppress their emotions. The growing mental health awareness in China has led to a shift toward gentler approaches to emotional support. The metaphor of scars (疤痕) as markers of experience rather than damage reflects a more nuanced understanding of emotional recovery.

tree.setup: It's raining hard and you're stuck at a convenience store entrance. A stranger silently hands you a spare umbrella before walking away.
tree.setup_zh: 雨下得很大，你被困在便利店门口。一个陌生人默默递给你一把备用伞，然后就走了。你跟旁边同样在躲雨的人说起了这件事。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 那个人刚才直接给你一把伞就走了？连名字都没留？现在还有这样的人啊。我已经很久没遇到过这种不图回报的好意了。
turns.text_pinyin: Nàge rén gāngcái zhíjiē gěi nǐ yī bǎ sǎn jiù zǒu le? Lián míngzi dōu méi liú? Xiànzài hái yǒu zhèyàng de rén a. Wǒ yǐjīng hěn jiǔ méi yù dào guò zhè zhǒng bù tú huíbào de hǎoyì le.
turns.text_en: That person just gave you an umbrella and walked away? Didn't even leave a name? People like that still exist. It's been a long time since I've encountered kindness that asks for nothing in return.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on the stranger's kindness.

options.text_zh: 我也很惊讶。但也许这种善意一直存在，只是我们太习惯了互相防备，反而看不到了。一把伞不值多少钱，但在雨里递给一个陌生人的那一刻，它变得特别珍贵。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yě hěn jīngyà. Dàn yěxǔ zhè zhǒng shànyì yīzhí cúnzài, zhǐ shì wǒmen tài xíguàn le hùxiāng fángbèi, fǎn'ér kàn bú dào le. Yī bǎ sǎn bù zhí duōshǎo qián, dàn zài yǔ lǐ dì gěi yīge mòshēngrén de nà yī kè, tā biàn de tèbié zhēnguì.
options.text_en: Maybe this kindness has always been there — we've become so used to guarding against each other that we can't see it. An umbrella isn't worth much, but handed to a stranger in the rain, it becomes precious.
options.feedback: — questions our default suspicion and elevates a simple act.

options.text_zh: 他可能是卖伞的吧。
options.text_pinyin: Tā kěnéng shì mài sǎn de ba.
options.text_en: He was probably selling umbrellas.
options.feedback: Cynical — assumes commercial motive for a pure gesture.

options.text_zh: 你认识他吗？也许是你的邻居。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ rènshi tā ma? Yěxǔ shì nǐ de línjū.
options.text_en: Do you know him? Maybe he's your neighbor.
options.feedback: Tries to rationalize instead of appreciating.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 确实。也许善意一直都在，只是我们忘了看。你打算怎么办？那把伞以后还给他吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Quèshí. Yěxǔ shànyì yīzhí dōu zài, zhǐ shì wǒmen wàng le kàn. Nǐ dǎsuàn zěnme bàn? Nà bǎ sǎn yǐhòu hái gěi tā ma?
turns.text_en: Indeed. Maybe kindness has always been there, we just forgot to look. What are you going to do? Return the umbrella to him later?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share what you plan to do with the umbrella.

options.text_zh: 我不知道怎么还给他。但也许最好的还法不是还给他，而是下次下雨的时候，把这把伞递给另一个需要的人。让这份善意继续传下去。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bù zhīdào zěnme hái gěi tā. Dàn yěxǔ zuì hǎo de huán fǎ bú shì hái gěi tā, ér shì xià cì xià yǔ de shíhou, bǎ zhè bǎ sǎn dì gěi lìng yīge xūyào de rén. Ràng zhè fèn shànyì jìxù chuán xià qù.
options.text_en: I don't know how to return it. But maybe the best way isn't giving it back — it's the next time it rains, handing this umbrella to someone else who needs it. Let the kindness keep passing forward.
options.feedback: — transforms a single act of kindness into a chain of generosity.

options.text_zh: 我把它放在家里吧，以后可以用。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bǎ tā fàng zài jiālǐ ba, yǐhòu kěyǐ yòng.
options.text_en: I'll keep it at home, I can use it later.
options.feedback: Keeps the kindness instead of passing it forward.

options.text_zh: 算了吧，一把伞而已。
options.text_pinyin: Suàn le ba, yī bǎ sǎn éryǐ.
options.text_en: Forget it, it's just an umbrella.
options.feedback: Minimizes the significance of the gesture.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 让善意传下去……你让我想起了一句话：「你不知道你的一个小小的举动会改变谁的一天。」也许那个人已经忘了今天递伞这件事，但你会记得。这就够了。
turns.text_pinyin: Ràng shànyì chuán xià qù…… nǐ ràng wǒ xiǎng qǐ le yī jù huà: 'Nǐ bù zhīdào nǐ de yīge xiǎo xiǎo de jǔdòng huì gǎibiàn shéi de yī tiān.' Yěxǔ nàge rén yǐjīng wàng le jīntiān dì sǎn zhè jiàn shì, dàn nǐ huì jìde. Zhè jiù gòu le.
turns.text_en: Letting kindness pass forward... you remind me of a saying: 'You never know whose day your small act will change.' Maybe that person has already forgotten handing over the umbrella today, but you'll remember. That's enough.

tree.cultural_note: The concept of「传递善意」(passing kindness forward) has deep roots in Chinese culture, connected to the Buddhist idea of cultivating merit (积德, jī dé) through good deeds. The umbrella is a powerful symbol in Chinese culture — sharing an umbrella (共伞) represents intimacy and care, and the act of giving away an umbrella to a stranger embodies the highest form of selfless generosity.

tree.setup: You and your friend Xiaoming are walking home after a long but uneventful day. The evening air is cool and the streets are quiet.
tree.setup_zh: 你和朋友小明在漫长但平淡的一天后走路回家。傍晚的空气凉爽，街道很安静。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 今天什么特别的事都没发生。开了两个会，写了几封邮件，吃了一碗不好不坏的面。但不知道为什么，走在回家的路上，我觉得今天其实还不错。
turns.text_pinyin: Jīntiān shénme tèbié de shì dōu méi fāshēng. Kāi le liǎng ge huì, xiě le jǐ fēng yóujiàn, chī le yī wǎn bù hǎo bù huài de miàn. Dàn bù zhīdào wèi shénme, zǒu zài huí jiā de lù shang, wǒ juéde jīntiān qíshí hái búcuò.
turns.text_en: Nothing special happened today. Had two meetings, wrote a few emails, ate a bowl of so-so noodles. But I don't know why, walking home now, I feel like today was actually not bad.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Explore why an ordinary day can feel good.

options.text_zh: 也许正因为没什么特别的事，你才有空间感受普通的日子。不是每天都要有大事发生。有时候一天结束时觉得平安、舒服，就已经是好日子了。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ zhèng yīnwèi méi shénme tèbié de shì, nǐ cái yǒu kōngjiān gǎnshòu pǔtōng de rìzi. Bú shì měi tiān dōu yào yǒu dà shì fāshēng. Yǒu shíhou yī tiān jiéshù shí juéde píng'ān, shūfu, jiù yǐjīng shì hǎo rìzi le.
options.text_en: Maybe it's precisely because nothing special happened that you had space to feel an ordinary day. Not every day needs big events. Sometimes ending the day feeling safe and comfortable is already a good day.
options.feedback: Quietly profound — redefines what makes a day good.

options.text_zh: 你今天那碗面在哪儿吃的？好吃吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ jīntiān nà wǎn miàn zài nǎr chī de? Hǎochī ma?
options.text_en: Where did you eat that bowl of noodles? Was it good?
options.feedback: Fixates on detail rather than engaging with the feeling.

options.text_zh: 没什么特别的不就是普通的一天吗？有什么好高兴的？
options.text_pinyin: Méi shénme tèbié de bú jiù shì pǔtōng de yī tiān ma? Yǒu shénme hǎo gāoxìng de?
options.text_en: Nothing special just means it was an ordinary day. What's there to be happy about?
options.feedback: Cynical — dismisses the quiet contentment he's found.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 平安和舒服就是一个好日子……以前的我肯定不会这么想。以前总觉得一天没有成就什么就是浪费了。现在慢慢觉得，安安稳稳地过完一天，本身就是一种成就。
turns.text_pinyin: Píng'ān hé shūfu jiù shì yīge hǎo rìzi…… yǐqián de wǒ kěndìng bú huì zhème xiǎng. Yǐqián zǒng juéde yī tiān méiyǒu chéngjiù shénme jiù shì làngfèi le. Xiànzài mànman juéde, ān'ān wěn wěn de guò wán yī tiān, běnshēn jiù shì yī zhǒng chéngjiù.
turns.text_en: Safe and comfortable is a good day... the old me definitely wouldn't have thought this way. I used to feel like a day without accomplishment was wasted. Now I'm slowly realizing that getting through a day peacefully is itself an achievement.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Close the day with a gentle thought.

options.text_zh: 也许这就是成长——不再需要每天都有烟花，而是学会欣赏安静的夜晚。今天没有什么特别的事，但我们走在一起回家，聊着这些，这本身就挺好的。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì chéngzhǎng — bú zài xūyào měi tiān dōu yǒu yānhuā, ér shì xuéhuì xīnshǎng ānjìng de yèwǎn. Jīntiān méiyǒu shénme tèbié de shì, dàn wǒmen zǒu zài yīqǐ huí jiā, liáo zhe zhèxiē, zhè běnshēn jiù tǐng hǎo de.
options.text_en: Maybe this is growth — no longer needing fireworks every day, but learning to appreciate quiet evenings. Nothing special happened today, but we're walking home together talking about these things. That itself is pretty good.
options.feedback: Perfect closing — names growth as quiet acceptance and honors the moment.

options.text_zh: 明天应该会更好的。
options.text_pinyin: Míngtiān yīnggāi huì gèng hǎo de.
options.text_en: Tomorrow should be better.
options.feedback: Implies today wasn't good enough — contradicts what he just realized.

options.text_zh: 到家了，明天见。
options.text_pinyin: Dào jiā le, míngtiān jiàn.
options.text_en: We're home, see you tomorrow.
options.feedback: Abrupt — exits a warm moment without reciprocating.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 走在一起回家就挺好的……嗯。到了。今天谢谢你陪我走了这段路。明天见，希望明天也是一个平安的日子。
turns.text_pinyin: Zǒu zài yīqǐ huí jiā jiù tǐng hǎo de…… en. Dào le. Jīntiān xièxie nǐ péi wǒ zǒu le zhè duàn lù. Míngtiān jiàn, xīwàng míngtiān yě shì yīge píng'ān de rìzi.
turns.text_en: Walking home together is pretty good... yes. We're here. Thank you for walking this stretch with me today. See you tomorrow — I hope tomorrow is also a peaceful day.

tree.cultural_note: The Chinese greeting「平安」(píng'ān, peace and safety) is one of the most deeply valued wishes in Chinese culture — not excitement or success, but simply safety and well-being. The idea that a quiet, uneventful day is itself valuable represents a maturation that many Chinese people describe experiencing as they move beyond the competitive pressures of youth into a more contemplative phase of life.

tree.setup: You're sitting in a hutong courtyard teahouse in Beijing. The elderly owner, Lao Chen, is lamenting how developers are eyeing the last stretch of his alley. You've been coming here for months.
tree.setup_zh: 你坐在北京胡同四合院茶馆里。年迈的老板老陈正在感叹开发商盯上了他胡同的最后一段。你已经来这儿好几个月了。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看对面那堵墙上的'拆'字了吗？上周才喷的。我在这条胡同住了五十年，眼看着一条条街变成了商业区。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn duìmiàn nà dǔ qiáng shàng de 'chāi' zì le ma? Shàng zhōu cái pēn de. Wǒ zài zhè tiáo hútòng zhù le wǔshí nián, yǎnkàn zhe yī tiáo tiáo jiē biàn chéng le shāngyè qū.
turns.text_en: Did you see the '拆' character painted on the wall across the way? Just sprayed last week. I've lived in this hutong fifty years, watching street after street turn into commercial zones.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to Lao Chen about what's happening to the neighborhood.

options.text_zh: 我注意到了。每次来这边都觉得少了点什么，但又说不清楚。可能不是少了建筑，是少了某种气氛吧。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ zhùyì dào le. Měi cì lái zhè biān dōu juéde shǎo le diǎn shénme, dàn yòu shuō bù qīngchǔ. Kěnéng bú shì shǎo le jiànzhù, shì shǎo le mǒu zhǒng qìfēn ba.
options.text_en: I've noticed. Every time I come here I feel like something's missing, but I can't quite put my finger on it. Maybe it's not the buildings that are gone — it's a certain atmosphere.
options.feedback: Observational and empathetic — matches the reflective tone Lao Chen is setting.

options.text_zh: 不过发展也是好事啊。新的商业区能带来就业，经济也能发展起来。
options.text_pinyin: Búguò fāzhǎn yě shì hǎo shì a. Xīn de shāngyè qū néng dàilái jiùyè, jīngjì yě néng fāzhǎn qǐlái.
options.text_en: But development is a good thing too. New commercial zones bring jobs and boost the economy.
options.feedback: Lao Chen is watching fifty years of his life get painted with a demolition character, and you offer him a macroeconomics talking point. He knows the arguments — he's mourning.

options.text_zh: 你知道附近哪儿有停车场吗？我今天开车来的。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhīdào fùjìn nǎr yǒu tíngchēchǎng ma? Wǒ jīntiān kāi chē lái de.
options.text_en: Do you know where there's a parking lot nearby? I drove here today.
options.feedback: Completely ignores the emotional weight of the conversation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 气氛？你说得对。以前邻居之间的关系不是靠微信维持的，是靠每天早上在门口碰面、借一把盐、帮忙看孩子。现在搬走的人越来越多，留下来的都是老人。
turns.text_pinyin: Qìfēn? Nǐ shuō de duì. Yǐqián línjū zhī jiān de guānxì bú shì kào Wēixìn wéichí de, shì kào měi tiān zǎoshang zài ménkǒu pèngmiàn, jiè yī bǎ yán, bāngmáng kàn háizi. Xiànzài bān zǒu de rén yuè lái yuè duō, liú xiàlái de dōu shì lǎorén.
turns.text_en: Atmosphere? You're right. Neighbor relationships used to be maintained not through WeChat, but by running into each other at the doorstep every morning, borrowing a pinch of salt, helping watch the kids. Now more and more people move away, and only the elderly stay behind.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Ask Lao Chen about his own situation — will he stay or go?

options.text_zh: 那您呢？如果真的拆了，您打算怎么办？搬去那种统一规划的回迁房？
options.text_pinyin: Nà nín ne? Rúguǒ zhēn de chāi le, nín dǎsuàn zěnme bàn? Bān qù nà zhǒng tǒngyī guīhuà de huíqiān fáng?
options.text_en: What about you? If they really demolish this, what will you do? Move into one of those standardized relocation apartments?
options.feedback: Directly engages with his personal stakes and shows you understand the relocation housing system.

options.text_zh: 你听说过最近那个新的购物中心吗？好像很不错。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ tīngshuō guò zuìjìn nàge xīn de gòuwù zhōngxīn ma? Hǎoxiàng hěn búcuò.
options.text_en: Have you heard about that new shopping mall? It seems pretty nice.
options.feedback: Mentioning a new mall to someone grieving the loss of traditional neighborhoods is oblivious.

options.text_zh: 我最近在学做饭，你有什么好的食谱推荐吗？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ zuìjìn zài xué zuòfàn, nǐ yǒu shénme hǎo de shípǔ tuījiàn ma?
options.text_en: I've been learning to cook recently, do you have any good recipes to recommend?
options.feedback: Ignores the entire thread of conversation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 回迁房？那种地方我去参观过。二十层楼，每户长得一模一样，邻居之间连门都不开。说是改善生活条件，其实是把社区拆散了。
turns.text_pinyin: Huíqiān fáng? Nà zhǒng dìfang wǒ qù cānguān guò. Èrshí céng lóu, měi hù zhǎng de yīmú yīyàng, línjū zhī jiān lián mén dōu bù kāi. Shuō shì gǎishàn shēnghuó tiáojiàn, qíshí shì bǎ shèqū chāi sàn le.
turns.text_en: Relocation housing? I've visited those places. Twenty-story towers, every unit identical, neighbors don't even open their doors to each other. They say it's improving living conditions, but really they're dismantling the community.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on the tension between development and preservation.

options.text_zh: 这就是矛盾所在吧。条件确实需要改善——老房子的确不好住。但改善不等于推倒重来。有没有可能在保留社区结构的前提下进行改造呢？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè jiù shì máodùn suǒzài ba. Tiáojiàn quèshí xūyào gǎishàn — lǎo fángzi díquè bù hǎo zhù. Dàn gǎishàn bù děngyú tuīdǎo chónglái. Yǒu méiyǒu kěnéng zài bǎoliú shèqū jiégòu de qiántí xià jìnxíng gǎizào ne?
options.text_en: That's exactly the tension, isn't it. Conditions do need improving — old houses genuinely aren't comfortable to live in. But improving doesn't mean tearing everything down and starting over. Is there a way to renovate while preserving the community structure?
options.feedback: Nuanced response that acknowledges both sides without being dismissive of either — exactly the kind of conversation Lao Chen would appreciate.

options.text_zh: 对了，你这里有WiFi密码吗？
options.text_pinyin: Duì le, nǐ zhèlǐ yǒu WiFi mìmǎ ma?
options.text_en: Oh right, do you have the WiFi password here?
options.feedback: Completely derails a meaningful exchange.

options.text_zh: 我觉得北京的地铁系统真的很方便，你经常坐地铁吗？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde Běijīng de dìtiě xìtǒng zhēn de hěn fāngbiàn, nǐ jīngcháng zuò dìtiě ma?
options.text_en: I think Beijing's subway system is really convenient, do you ride it often?
options.feedback: Unrelated to the ongoing discussion about neighborhood demolition.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 有人提过微改造，就是保留原来的格局，只是把管道、电线、厕所这些升级。可是这种方案赚不了多少钱，开发商没兴趣。归根结底还是一个利益问题。
turns.text_pinyin: Yǒu rén tí guò wēi gǎizào, jiù shì bǎoliú yuánlái de géjú, zhǐ shì bǎ guǎndào, diànxiàn, cèsuǒ zhèxiē shēngjí. Kěshì zhè zhǒng fāng'àn zhuàn bù liǎo duōshǎo qián, kāifāshāng méi xìngqù. Guīgēn jiédǐ háishì yīge lìyì wèntí.
turns.text_en: Some people have proposed micro-renovation — keeping the original layout but just upgrading the pipes, wiring, and toilets. But that kind of plan doesn't make much money, so developers aren't interested. At the end of the day, it's a question of profit.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the systemic nature of the problem.

options.text_zh: 所以问题不在于技术上能不能做到，而在于现行制度下，谁有动力去做。居民想留，开发商想拆，政府夹在中间。这种博弈里，声音最小的往往是住了最久的人。
options.text_pinyin: Suǒyǐ wèntí bú zàiyú jìshù shàng néng bù néng zuòdào, ér zàiyú xiànxíng zhìdù xià, shéi yǒu dònglì qù zuò. Jūmín xiǎng liú, kāifāshāng xiǎng chāi, zhèngfǔ jiā zài zhōngjiān. Zhè zhǒng bóyì lǐ, shēngyīn zuì xiǎo de wǎngwǎng shì zhù le zuì jiǔ de rén.
options.text_en: So the problem isn't technical feasibility, but who has the incentive. Residents want to stay, developers want to demolish, government is caught between. In this game, the quietest voices are the people who've lived there longest.
options.feedback: Structural analysis that names the power dynamics directly — shows real understanding of how urban development works in China.

options.text_zh: 你有没有养过猫？胡同里的猫特别多。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu yǎng guò māo? Hútòng lǐ de māo tèbié duō.
options.text_en: Have you ever had a cat? There are so many cats in the hutongs.
options.feedback: A non-sequitur that abandons the substantive discussion.

options.text_zh: 我觉得应该多建公园，你觉得呢？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde yīnggāi duō jiàn gōngyuán, nǐ juéde ne?
options.text_en: I think they should build more parks, what do you think?
options.feedback: Vaguely related to urban planning but doesn't engage with the specific issue at hand.

tree.cultural_note: The painted character '拆' (chāi, demolish) on buildings is one of the most recognizable symbols of urban transformation in China. Hutong residents facing demolition often receive relocation compensation, but the social fabric — decades of neighbor relationships, shared courtyards, and informal community support — cannot be rebuilt in high-rise towers. The tension between modernization and preservation remains one of urban China's defining debates.

tree.setup: You're at a government service center trying to get a document notarized. You've already been sent to three different windows. The clerk at Window 7, Xiao Liu, has just told you that you need the original household registration booklet, not the copy that Window 3 said was fine.
tree.setup_zh: 你在政务服务中心办理文件公证。你已经被指到三个不同的窗口了。7号窗口的小刘刚告诉你需要户口本原件，而不是3号窗口说可以用的复印件。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你好，请问办什么业务？……哦，公证。你带了户口本原件吗？复印件不行，必须是原件。……3号窗口说可以？那你去3号窗口办啊。他们要是能办，就不会把你送到我这儿来了，对吧？
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ hǎo, qǐngwèn bàn shénme yèwù? ... Ó, gōngzhèng. Nǐ dài le hùkǒuběn yuánjiàn ma? Fùyìnjiàn bù xíng, bìxū shì yuánjiàn. ... Sān hào chuāngkǒu shuō kěyǐ? Nà nǐ qù sān hào chuāngkǒu bàn a. Tāmen yàoshi néng bàn, jiù bú huì bǎ nǐ sòng dào wǒ zhèr lái le, duì ba?
turns.text_en: Hello, what business are you here for? ... Oh, notarization. Did you bring the original household registration booklet? Copies won't do, must be the original. ... Window 3 said copies are fine? Then go to Window 3. If they could handle it, they wouldn't have sent you here, right?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Point out the circular logic without losing your composure entirely.

options.text_zh: 等一下，让我理清一下。3号窗口说他们办不了，让我来7号。7号说材料不对，让我去3号。如果我现在回3号，他们会不会又把我送到7号来？我们是在办公证还是在玩一个很无聊的游戏？
options.text_pinyin: Děng yīxià, ràng wǒ lǐqīng yīxià. Sān hào chuāngkǒu shuō tāmen bàn bù liǎo, ràng wǒ lái qī hào. Qī hào shuō cáiliào bú duì, ràng wǒ qù sān hào. Rúguǒ wǒ xiànzài huí sān hào, tāmen huì bú huì yòu bǎ wǒ sòng dào qī hào lái? Wǒmen shì zài bàn gōngzhèng háishì zài wán yīge hěn wúliáo de yóuxì?
options.text_en: Window 3 says they can't do it, sends me to 7. Window 7 says wrong materials, sends me to 3. If I go back now, will they send me to 7 again?
options.feedback: Names the loop out loud. She can hear how absurd it sounds when someone says it back to her.

options.text_zh: 我理解每个窗口有不同的规定。那我应该先去哪里拿到原件的证明，然后再回来找你？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ lǐjiě měi ge chuāngkǒu yǒu bùtóng de guīdìng. Nà wǒ yīnggāi xiān qù nǎlǐ ná dào yuánjiàn de zhèngmíng, ránhòu zài huílái zhǎo nǐ?
options.text_en: I understand each window has different rules. So where should I go first to get the original verified, then come back to you?
options.feedback: You're trying to solve the maze by following its rules. She just told you the rules point in a circle.

options.text_zh: 好的，没关系，那我回家拿原件吧。大概需要多久能办完？
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo de, méi guānxi, nà wǒ huí jiā ná yuánjiàn ba. Dàgài xūyào duō jiǔ néng bàn wán?
options.text_en: Okay, no problem, I'll go home and get the original. About how long will the whole process take?
options.feedback: You've accepted the premise and are already planning your next trip. She hasn't even confirmed that the original will be enough.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 游戏？我们这是正规流程。你看啊——3号管初审，我们管复核。初审的标准跟复核的标准不一样，这不是很正常吗？你去医院，挂号和看病也不是一个窗口吧？
turns.text_pinyin: Yóuxì? Wǒmen zhè shì zhèngguī liúchéng. Nǐ kàn a — sān hào guǎn chūshěn, wǒmen guǎn fùhé. Chūshěn de biāozhǔn gēn fùhé de biāozhǔn bù yīyàng, zhè bú shì hěn zhèngcháng ma? Nǐ qù yīyuàn, guàhào hé kànbìng yě bú shì yīge chuāngkǒu ba?
turns.text_en: Game? This is proper procedure. Look — Window 3 handles preliminary review, we handle secondary review. The standards for preliminary and secondary review are different. Isn't that normal? When you go to the hospital, registration and seeing the doctor aren't at the same window either, right?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: She's used a surprisingly good analogy. Engage with it.

options.text_zh: 对，但医院挂号的时候会告诉你要带什么。不会挂完号到了诊室，医生说'你挂的科不对，回去重新挂'，然后挂号处说'你得先看完医生才能改科室'。你们这个就是这样的。
options.text_pinyin: Duì, dàn yīyuàn guàhào de shíhou huì gàosù nǐ yào dài shénme. Bú huì guà wán hào dào le zhěnshì, yīshēng shuō 'nǐ guà de kē bú duì, huíqù chóngxīn guà', ránhòu guàhào chù shuō 'nǐ děi xiān kàn wán yīshēng cái néng gǎi kēshì'. Nǐmen zhège jiù shì zhèyàng de.
options.text_en: But at the hospital they tell you what to bring when you register. They don't let you walk to the exam room, then say 'wrong department, re-register,' and registration says 'see the doctor first.' That's what you're doing here.
options.feedback: Uses her own analogy to show the circularity. Hard to argue with your own metaphor turned against you.

options.text_zh: 你说得有道理，流程确实是分步骤的。那我现在具体需要做什么？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ shuō de yǒu dàolǐ, liúchéng quèshí shì fēn bùzhòu de. Nà wǒ xiànzài jùtǐ xūyào zuò shénme?
options.text_en: You make a good point, procedures do have steps. So what exactly do I need to do now?
options.feedback: She compared a broken system to a functioning one and you accepted the comparison. Now you're back to following instructions that lead in a circle.

options.text_zh: 我觉得你们应该把初审和复核的要求统一，这样办事人就不会跑来跑去了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde nǐmen yīnggāi bǎ chūshěn hé fùhé de yāoqiú tǒngyī, zhèyàng bànshì rén jiù bú huì pǎo lái pǎo qù le.
options.text_en: I think you should unify the preliminary and secondary review requirements, so people don't have to run back and forth.
options.feedback: You've proposed a structural reform to a window clerk. She nods politely, but the suggestion will reach exactly nobody with the power to change it.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: ……好吧，你这个医院的比喻倒是把我说住了。行，我帮你查一下，看能不能在系统里直接调你的户口信息。本来不该我做这个，但你都跑了三趟了。不过你得在这儿签一个'材料补交承诺书'——对，就是承诺你会补交你已经交过但我们不认的材料。
turns.text_pinyin: ... Hǎo ba, nǐ zhège yīyuàn de bǐyù dǎoshì bǎ wǒ shuō zhù le. Xíng, wǒ bāng nǐ chá yīxià, kàn néng bù néng zài xìtǒng lǐ zhíjiē diào nǐ de hùkǒu xìnxī. Běnlái bù gāi wǒ zuò zhège, dàn nǐ dōu pǎo le sān tàng le. Búguò nǐ děi zài zhèr qiān yīge 'cáiliào bǔjiāo chéngnuòshū' — duì, jiù shì chéngnuò nǐ huì bǔjiāo nǐ yǐjīng jiāo guò dàn wǒmen bù rèn de cáiliào.
turns.text_en: ... Fine, your hospital metaphor got me. Okay, let me check if I can pull your household registration info directly from the system. This isn't really my job, but you've made three trips already. Though you'll need to sign a 'supplementary materials pledge form' — yes, that's a pledge to re-submit materials you already submitted but that we don't accept.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: She's actually helping, but the solution is almost as absurd as the problem.

options.text_zh: 我签。我签任何东西。你要我承诺补交我交过的东西，我承诺。你要我承诺以后不抱怨，我也承诺。在这个大厅里待了四个小时之后，我的底线已经不存在了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ qiān. Wǒ qiān rènhé dōngxi. Nǐ yào wǒ chéngnuò bǔjiāo wǒ jiāo guò de dōngxi, wǒ chéngnuò. Nǐ yào wǒ chéngnuò yǐhòu bù bàoyuàn, wǒ yě chéngnuò. Zài zhège dàtīng lǐ dāi le sì ge xiǎoshí zhīhòu, wǒ de dǐxiàn yǐjīng bù cúnzài le.
options.text_en: I'll sign. I'll sign anything. Re-submit what I already submitted? I pledge. Pledge not to complain? I pledge that too. After four hours in this hall, my standards no longer exist.
options.feedback: Total surrender delivered with the exhausted clarity of someone who has been defeated by paperwork. She's trying not to laugh.

options.text_zh: 谢谢你帮我想办法，小刘。像你这样的工作人员真的不多。
options.text_pinyin: Xièxie nǐ bāng wǒ xiǎng bànfǎ, Xiǎo Liú. Xiàng nǐ zhèyàng de gōngzuò rényuán zhēn de bù duō.
options.text_en: Thank you for finding a way to help, Xiao Liu. There aren't many staff members like you.
options.feedback: Genuine gratitude, but you've just been asked to sign a form promising to re-submit documents you already submitted. The absurdity sailed right past you.

options.text_zh: 这个承诺书有法律效力吗？我要不要先看一下内容？
options.text_pinyin: Zhège chéngnuòshū yǒu fǎlǜ xiàolì ma? Wǒ yào bú yào xiān kàn yīxià nèiróng?
options.text_en: Does this pledge form have legal standing? Should I read the contents first?
options.feedback: Treating a form about re-submitting already-submitted materials as a serious legal document. The bureaucracy has won. You're thinking inside the box it built around you.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese government service centers (政务服务中心) have modernized significantly, but the experience of being shuttled between windows (窗口) with inconsistent requirements remains a common frustration. The phrase '上面的规定' (regulations from above) simultaneously acknowledges absurdity while absolving the clerk. The 'supplementary materials pledge form' is only slightly exaggerated — Chinese bureaucracy genuinely features forms about forms, reviews of reviews, and confirmations that you will confirm.

tree.setup: It's raining in Shanghai. You're standing inside a convenience store watching the shared umbrella rack outside. A woman, Wei, is standing next to you. You both just watched someone walk up, take a shared umbrella, and leave. Now a second person has arrived, seen the rack is one umbrella lighter, and is eyeing the remaining ones.
tree.setup_zh: 上海下雨了。你站在便利店里看着外面的共享雨伞架。一个叫小薇的女生站在你旁边。你们刚看到一个人走过来，拿了一把共享雨伞就走了。现在第二个人到了，看到架子上少了一把，正在打量剩下的。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看你看——那个穿灰衣服的，拿伞之前看了一下四周。他知道这不太对，但他还是拿了。他安慰自己的方式大概是'反正别人也拿了'。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn nǐ kàn — nàge chuān huī yīfu de, ná sǎn zhīqián kàn le yīxià sìzhōu. Tā zhīdào zhè bú tài duì, dàn tā háishì ná le. Tā ānwèi zìjǐ de fāngshì dàgài shì 'fǎnzhèng biérén yě ná le'.
turns.text_en: Look, look — the one in gray, he looked around before taking the umbrella. He knows it's not quite right, but he still took it. His way of comforting himself is probably 'well, other people took them too.'

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: You're both watching this unfold like a nature documentary. Join the commentary.

options.text_zh: 注意他走路的姿势——前十步走得很快，心虚。等过了路口，就放慢了，那把伞已经从'共享雨伞'变成'我的伞'了。身份转换完成。
options.text_pinyin: Zhùyì tā zǒulù de zīshì — qián shí bù zǒu de hěn kuài, xīnxū. Děng guò le lùkǒu, jiù fàngmàn le, nà bǎ sǎn yǐjīng cóng 'gòngxiǎng yǔsǎn' biàn chéng 'wǒ de sǎn' le. Shēnfèn zhuǎnhuàn wánchéng.
options.text_en: Watch his walk — first ten steps, he's moving fast, guilty. Once he's past the intersection, he slows down. That umbrella has already gone from 'shared umbrella' to 'my umbrella.' Identity transfer complete.
options.feedback: You've matched her nature-documentary tone perfectly. The 'identity transfer' observation lands because it's specific and true.

options.text_zh: 其实这种共享雨伞的模式本身就有问题，设计者高估了人们的归还率。
options.text_pinyin: Qíshí zhè zhǒng gòngxiǎng yǔsǎn de móshì běnshēn jiù yǒu wèntí, shèjìzhě gāogū le rénmen de guīhuán lǜ.
options.text_en: Actually the shared umbrella model itself is flawed, the designers overestimated people's return rate.
options.feedback: She's doing live commentary on a heist. You've switched to a business school case study.

options.text_zh: 也许他真的急着用，下这么大的雨，也能理解吧。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ tā zhēn de jí zhe yòng, xià zhème dà de yǔ, yě néng lǐjiě ba.
options.text_en: Maybe he really needed it urgently, it's raining so hard, it's understandable.
options.feedback: She's not judging him. She's narrating. You've rushed to his defense before anyone accused him of anything.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 哈哈哈，'身份转换完成'！——等等，你看，又来一个。这个更好——她看到架子上只剩两把了，你看她的表情，她在算。她在想'如果我不拿，等一下别人也会拿走的，那我不就亏了？'
turns.text_pinyin: Hāhāhā, 'shēnfèn zhuǎnhuàn wánchéng'! — Děngdeng, nǐ kàn, yòu lái yīge. Zhège gèng hǎo — tā kàn dào jiàzi shàng zhǐ shèng liǎng bǎ le, nǐ kàn tā de biǎoqíng, tā zài suàn. Tā zài xiǎng 'rúguǒ wǒ bù ná, děng yīxià biérén yě huì ná zǒu de, nà wǒ bú jiù kuī le?'
turns.text_en: Hahaha, 'identity transfer complete'! — Wait, look, another one. This one's even better — she sees there are only two left on the rack. Look at her face, she's calculating. She's thinking 'if I don't take one, someone else will take it anyway, so wouldn't I be the loser?'

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: The scarcity is accelerating the behavior. Narrate what happens next.

options.text_zh: 经典的囚徒困境，现场直播版。你看——她拿了。而且她拿了以后看了一眼最后那一把，你说她在想什么？她在想要不要把最后一把也拿走，'帮朋友带一把'。
options.text_pinyin: Jīngdiǎn de qiútú kùnjìng, xiànchǎng zhíbō bǎn. Nǐ kàn — tā ná le. Érqiě tā ná le yǐhòu kàn le yī yǎn zuìhòu nà yī bǎ, nǐ shuō tā zài xiǎng shénme? Tā zài xiǎng yào bú yào bǎ zuìhòu yī bǎ yě ná zǒu, 'bāng péngyou dài yī bǎ'.
options.text_en: Classic prisoner's dilemma, live. She took one. After taking it she glanced at the last one. What do you think? She's wondering whether to grab the last one too, 'for a friend.'
options.feedback: The glance at the last umbrella is the comic detail that makes this. Everyone's been that person doing the mental math.

options.text_zh: 我们应该出去告诉她那些伞是要归还的，不是免费拿的。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒmen yīnggāi chūqù gàosù tā nàxiē sǎn shì yào guīhuán de, bú shì miǎnfèi ná de.
options.text_en: We should go out and tell her those umbrellas are supposed to be returned, not taken for free.
options.feedback: You've broken the fourth wall of the nature documentary. She was enjoying the observation, not building a case for intervention.

options.text_zh: 这种行为其实反映了一种社会信任的缺失，值得深思。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè zhǒng xíngwéi qíshí fǎnyìng le yī zhǒng shèhuì xìnrèn de quēshī, zhíde shēnsī.
options.text_en: This kind of behavior actually reflects a lack of social trust, it's worth reflecting on deeply.
options.feedback: She's laughing. You've handed her a sociology abstract. The tone gap is visible from space.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 她没拿最后一把！你看——她犹豫了一下，走了。她留下了最后一把。也许这就是人性的底线吧。全拿走太贪了，留一把给下一个人，自己就不算坏人了。哎——你看，第四个人来了。他在看最后那一把。
turns.text_pinyin: Tā méi ná zuìhòu yī bǎ! Nǐ kàn — tā yóuyù le yīxià, zǒu le. Tā liú xià le zuìhòu yī bǎ. Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì rénxìng de dǐxiàn ba. Quán ná zǒu tài tān le, liú yī bǎ gěi xià yīge rén, zìjǐ jiù bú suàn huài rén le. Āi — nǐ kàn, dì sì ge rén lái le. Tā zài kàn zuìhòu nà yī bǎ.
turns.text_en: She didn't take the last one! Look — she hesitated, then left. She left the last one. Maybe that's the floor of human nature. Taking all of them is too greedy; leave one for the next person and you're not a bad person. Oh — look, a fourth person just showed up. He's looking at the last one.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: The last umbrella. The final act.

options.text_zh: 他不会拿的。最后一把有一种道德重力——拿走'其中一把'跟拿走'最后一把'感觉完全不一样。拿最后一把的人没法告诉自己'反正还有别的'。……他拿了。我对人类的信心又调整了一下。
options.text_pinyin: Tā bú huì ná de. Zuìhòu yī bǎ yǒu yī zhǒng dàodé zhònglì — ná zǒu 'qízhōng yī bǎ' gēn ná zǒu 'zuìhòu yī bǎ' gǎnjué wánquán bù yīyàng. Ná zuìhòu yī bǎ de rén méi fǎ gàosù zìjǐ 'fǎnzhèng hái yǒu biéde'. ... Tā ná le. Wǒ duì rénlèi de xìnxīn yòu tiáozhěng le yīxià.
options.text_en: He won't take it. The last one has moral gravity — taking 'one of them' versus 'the last one' feel completely different. The last-taker can't say 'there are still others.' ... He took it. Humanity confidence adjusted.
options.feedback: The prediction, the reversal, and 'adjusted my confidence slightly' — the understatement makes it land.

options.text_zh: 算了，我们也出去吧，反正伞都没了，雨也没那么大了。
options.text_pinyin: Suàn le, wǒmen yě chūqù ba, fǎnzhèng sǎn dōu méi le, yǔ yě méi nàme dà le.
options.text_en: Forget it, let's go too, there are no umbrellas left anyway, and the rain isn't that heavy anymore.
options.feedback: The climax of the show just happened and you're heading for the exit. She's still staring at the empty rack.

options.text_zh: 这就是为什么共享经济在中国很难成功。缺乏监管，谁都可以拿，当然就没了。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè jiù shì wèi shénme gòngxiǎng jīngjì zài Zhōngguó hěn nán chénggōng. Quēfá jiānguǎn, shéi dōu kěyǐ ná, dāngrán jiù méi le.
options.text_en: This is why the sharing economy struggles in China. No oversight, anyone can take them, of course they're gone.
options.feedback: You just watched four strangers perform a one-act play about human nature, and your takeaway is a policy recommendation.

tree.cultural_note: Shared umbrella schemes in Chinese cities (共享雨伞) became a minor cultural phenomenon and cautionary tale. Companies like Sharing E Umbrella lost nearly all 300,000 umbrellas within weeks, becoming a national joke about the gap between techno-optimism and actual behavior. The real comedy isn't in the theft itself but in the micro-negotiations people have with their own conscience — the glance around, the speed of the walk, the decision about whether to take the last one.

title: The Locked Room on the Third Floor

tree.setup: You've just moved into an old apartment building in Chengdu. Your neighbor across the hall, a retired detective named Uncle Fang, mentions that the apartment on the third floor has been locked for two years. Nobody knows who owns it, but sometimes people hear sounds at night.
tree.setup_zh: 你刚搬进成都一栋老公寓楼。对面的邻居方叔是个退休的侦探，提到三楼有间公寓锁了两年。没人知道谁是房主，但有时候晚上能听到声响。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你是新搬来的吧？跟你说个事儿——三楼靠右那间，你别好奇去敲门。锁了两年了，物业也不管。但有意思的是，电表一直在走。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ shì xīn bān lái de ba? Gēn nǐ shuō ge shìr — sān lóu kào yòu nà jiān, nǐ bié hàoqí qù qiāo mén. Suǒ le liǎng nián le, wùyè yě bù guǎn. Dàn yǒu yìsi de shì, diànbiǎo yīzhí zài zǒu.
turns.text_en: You just moved in, right? Let me tell you something — that unit on the right side of the third floor, don't go knocking out of curiosity. Been locked for two years, property management doesn't deal with it. But here's the interesting thing: the electric meter's been running the whole time.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to Uncle Fang's intriguing detail about the electric meter.

options.text_zh: 电表一直在走？那就说明里面有东西在用电。要么是有人定期来，要么是什么设备一直开着。方叔，你以前做侦探的——你觉得最可能的解释是什么？
options.text_pinyin: Diànbiǎo yīzhí zài zǒu? Nà jiù shuōmíng lǐmiàn yǒu dōngxi zài yòng diàn. Yàome shì yǒu rén dìngqī lái, yàome shì shénme shèbèi yīzhí kāi zhe. Fāng shū, nǐ yǐqián zuò zhēntàn de — nǐ juéde zuì kěnéng de jiěshì shì shénme?
options.text_en: The meter's been running? That means something inside is using electricity. Either someone comes by regularly, or some equipment is running continuously. Uncle Fang, you used to be a detective — what do you think the most likely explanation is?
options.feedback: Good deductive thinking — identifies the key implications and appeals to his expertise.

options.text_zh: 这栋楼的物业费贵不贵？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè dòng lóu de wùyè fèi guì bú guì?
options.text_en: Are the property management fees expensive in this building?
options.feedback: A man is telling you about a mystery and you're asking about HOA fees.

options.text_zh: 我还没把宽带装好，你知道哪个运营商比较好吗？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ hái méi bǎ kuāndài zhuāng hǎo, nǐ zhīdào nǎge yùnyíngshāng bǐjiào hǎo ma?
options.text_en: I haven't set up my internet yet, do you know which provider is better?
options.feedback: Practical question that kills the detective-story atmosphere.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我观察了一下。快递从来没有送到那个门牌号的。但是每个月月初，会有一个人来——穿得很普通，戴着口罩，提着一个黑色的袋子。待大概一个小时就走。我试着跟他打过招呼，他不理我。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ guānchá le yīxià. Kuàidì cónglái méiyǒu sòng dào nàge ménpái hào de. Dànshì měi ge yuè yuèchū, huì yǒu yīge rén lái — chuān de hěn pǔtōng, dài zhe kǒuzhào, tí zhe yīge hēisè de dàizi. Dāi dàgài yīge xiǎoshí jiù zǒu. Wǒ shì zhe gēn tā dǎ guò zhāohu, tā bù lǐ wǒ.
turns.text_en: I've been observing. Deliveries never come to that door number. But at the beginning of every month, someone shows up — dressed plainly, wearing a mask, carrying a black bag. Stays about an hour and leaves. I tried greeting him once; he ignored me.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reason through the clues with Uncle Fang.

options.text_zh: 月初来一次，每次带个袋子——如果是搬东西应该更频繁。你觉得有没有可能是在维护什么？比如浇花、检查设备之类的？而且他不想被认出来，所以才戴口罩、不跟人说话。
options.text_pinyin: Yuèchū lái yī cì, měi cì dài ge dàizi — rúguǒ shì bān dōngxi yīnggāi gèng pínfán. Nǐ juéde yǒu méiyǒu kěnéng shì zài wéihù shénme? Bǐrú jiāo huā, jiǎnchá shèbèi zhī lèi de? Érqiě tā bù xiǎng bèi rèn chūlái, suǒyǐ cái dài kǒuzhào, bù gēn rén shuōhuà.
options.text_en: Once a month, always with a bag — if he were moving things it'd be more frequent. Think he's maintaining something? Watering plants, checking equipment? And he doesn't want to be recognized — hence the mask, no talking.
options.feedback: Sound reasoning — eliminates the moving hypothesis and proposes maintenance, while noting the deliberate anonymity.

options.text_zh: 我最近在追一部电视剧，你看不看电视？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ zuìjìn zài zhuī yī bù diànshìjù, nǐ kàn bú kàn diànshì?
options.text_en: I've been following a TV series, do you watch TV?
options.feedback: You're in the middle of a real-life mystery and you pivot to TV.

options.text_zh: 成都的火锅真的好吃，你推荐哪家？
options.text_pinyin: Chéngdū de huǒguō zhēn de hǎochī, nǐ tuījiàn nǎ jiā?
options.text_en: Chengdu hotpot is really delicious, which restaurant do you recommend?
options.feedback: Derails the detective conversation entirely.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 维护……有道理。还有一个细节——我有一次正好在楼道里，听到他在打电话，声音很低，但我听到他说了一句'数据已经备份了'。你觉得这意味着什么？
turns.text_pinyin: Wéihù ... yǒu dàolǐ. Hái yǒu yīge xìjié — wǒ yǒu yī cì zhènghǎo zài lóudào lǐ, tīng dào tā zài dǎ diànhuà, shēngyīn hěn dī, dàn wǒ tīng dào tā shuō le yī jù 'shùjù yǐjīng bèifèn le'. Nǐ juéde zhè yìwèi zhe shénme?
turns.text_en: Maintenance... makes sense. One more detail — I happened to be in the hallway once and heard him on the phone, voice very low, but I caught him saying 'the data has been backed up.' What do you think that means?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Put the clues together.

options.text_zh: 电表一直走、每月来一次、'数据备份'——越来越像一个私人服务器机房。有人把服务器放在居民楼里，可能为了省钱，也可能不想留商业注册记录。不违法，但肯定不合规。
options.text_pinyin: Diànbiǎo yīzhí zǒu, měi yuè lái yī cì, 'shùjù bèifèn' — yuè lái yuè xiàng yīge sīrén fúwùqì jīfáng. Yǒu rén bǎ fúwùqì fàng zài jūmín lóu lǐ, kěnéng wèile shěng qián, yě kěnéng bù xiǎng liú shāngyè zhùcè jìlù. Bú wéifǎ, dàn kěndìng bù héguī.
options.text_en: Meter running, monthly visits, 'data backup' — increasingly looks like a private server room. Someone running servers in a residential building, maybe to save money or avoid a commercial trail. Not illegal, but definitely not compliant.
options.feedback: Excellent deduction — synthesizes all the clues into a coherent and plausible theory.

options.text_zh: 你退休之后平时做什么消遣？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ tuìxiū zhīhòu píngshí zuò shénme xiāoqiǎn?
options.text_en: What do you do for fun since retiring?
options.feedback: The man just dropped a major clue and you ask about hobbies.

options.text_zh: 最近天气挺热的，你觉得今年夏天会更热吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zuìjìn tiānqì tǐng rè de, nǐ juéde jīnnián xiàtiān huì gèng rè ma?
options.text_en: It's been pretty hot lately, do you think this summer will be hotter?
options.feedback: Weather small talk when you're piecing together a mystery.

tree.cultural_note: Running private server rooms in residential buildings (居民楼) is a real phenomenon in Chinese cities, where commercial data center space is expensive and regulations are unevenly enforced. Retired people (especially former police or security professionals) often become de facto neighborhood watchmen, maintaining an observational awareness that stems from decades of professional habit. The detail of the electric meter (电表) is culturally specific — in older Chinese buildings, meters are often visible in public hallways, making neighbor surveillance both easy and normalized.

tree.setup: You're at a dinner party in Guangzhou. A fellow guest, Lin Jie, grew up in Guangzhou but her parents are from Sichuan. After someone casually asks her '你是哪里人？', she turns to you with a knowing look. She's heard this question a thousand times and has complex feelings about it.
tree.setup_zh: 你在广州参加一个晚餐聚会。另一位客人林洁在广州长大，但她父母是四川人。有人随口问她'你是哪里人？'之后，她带着一种心照不宣的表情转向你。她听过这个问题无数遍了，内心很复杂。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 每次被问'你是哪里人'，我都不知道该怎么回答。说广州吧，人家听我姓林、看我长相就说'你不像广东人'。说四川吧，我四川话都说不利索。你说这个问题到底在问什么？
turns.text_pinyin: Měi cì bèi wèn 'nǐ shì nǎlǐ rén', wǒ dōu bù zhīdào gāi zěnme huídá. Shuō Guǎngzhōu ba, rénjiā tīng wǒ xìng Lín, kàn wǒ zhǎngxiàng jiù shuō 'nǐ bú xiàng Guǎngdōng rén'. Shuō Sìchuān ba, wǒ Sìchuān huà dōu shuō bù lìsuǒ. Nǐ shuō zhège wèntí dàodǐ zài wèn shénme?
turns.text_en: Every time someone asks 'where are you from,' I don't know how to answer. If I say Guangzhou, people hear my surname Lin, look at my face, and say 'you don't seem Cantonese.' If I say Sichuan, I can't even speak Sichuanese fluently. What is this question really asking?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Engage with her question about what '你是哪里人' really means.

options.text_zh: 我觉得这个问题表面上问的是地理，其实问的是归属感。人们想找到一个标签把你放进去——方言、口味、性格，全套配齐。可是你的经历比任何一个标签都复杂，所以怎么回答都不准确。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde zhège wèntí biǎomiàn shàng wèn de shì dìlǐ, qíshí wèn de shì guīshǔ gǎn. Rénmen xiǎng zhǎodào yīge biāoqiān bǎ nǐ fàng jìnqù — fāngyán, kǒuwèi, xìnggé, quán tào pèi qí. Kěshì nǐ de jīnglì bǐ rènhé yīge biāoqiān dōu fùzá, suǒyǐ zěnme huídá dōu bù zhǔnquè.
options.text_en: On the surface it asks about geography, but it's asking about belonging. People want a label — dialect, food, personality, the whole package. But your experience is more complex than any single label, so no answer feels right.
options.feedback: Perceptive analysis that names the real function of the question — identity categorization — and validates her discomfort.

options.text_zh: 广州的早茶你喜欢去哪家？
options.text_pinyin: Guǎngzhōu de zǎochá nǐ xǐhuān qù nǎ jiā?
options.text_en: Which place do you like for morning tea in Guangzhou?
options.feedback: She's opening up about identity and you ask about dim sum.

options.text_zh: 你在哪里上的大学？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zài nǎlǐ shàng de dàxué?
options.text_en: Where did you go to college?
options.feedback: Generic biographical question that misses the philosophical depth she's offering.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对，就是这种感觉。最烦的是那种'哦，怪不得'的语气——好像你的一切行为都可以用籍贯来解释。我吃辣就是因为我爸妈是四川人？不能只是因为我喜欢吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Duì, jiù shì zhè zhǒng gǎnjué. Zuì fán de shì nà zhǒng 'ó, guàibùdé' de yǔqì — hǎoxiàng nǐ de yīqiè xíngwéi dōu kěyǐ yòng jíguàn lái jiěshì. Wǒ chī là jiù shì yīnwèi wǒ bàmā shì Sìchuān rén? Bù néng zhǐ shì yīnwèi wǒ xǐhuān ma?
turns.text_en: Right, that's exactly the feeling. The most annoying is that 'oh, no wonder' tone — as if all your behavior can be explained by where you're registered. I eat spicy food because my parents are from Sichuan? Can't it just be because I like it?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her frustration about being reduced to regional stereotypes.

options.text_zh: 这种'怪不得'思维真的很偷懒——把一个立体的人压缩成一个地名。而且它有一个隐含的假设：你的选择不是你自己做的，而是你的'基因'或'根'替你决定的。这其实是在否认你的主体性。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè zhǒng 'guàibùdé' sīwéi zhēn de hěn tōu lǎn — bǎ yīge lìtǐ de rén yāsuō chéng yīge dìmíng. Érqiě tā yǒu yīge yǐnhán de jiǎshè: nǐ de xuǎnzé bú shì nǐ zìjǐ zuò de, ér shì nǐ de 'jīyīn' huò 'gēn' tì nǐ juédìng de. Zhè qíshí shì zài fǒurèn nǐ de zhǔtǐ xìng.
options.text_en: That 'no wonder' thinking is intellectually lazy — compresses a three-dimensional person into a place name. It implies your choices aren't your own, they're determined by your 'genes.' It's denying your agency.
options.feedback: Sharp critique that names the philosophical problem — the denial of individual agency through regional determinism.

options.text_zh: 你平时做什么工作？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ píngshí zuò shénme gōngzuò?
options.text_en: What do you do for work?
options.feedback: Standard small talk that ignores the vulnerable conversation happening.

options.text_zh: 我听说广州最近房价降了，你怎么看？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ tīngshuō Guǎngzhōu zuìjìn fángjià jiàng le, nǐ zěnme kàn?
options.text_en: I heard Guangzhou's housing prices have dropped recently, what do you think?
options.feedback: Pivoting to real estate during a conversation about identity and belonging.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 主体性——对，就是这个词。有时候我觉得中国的地域文化认同特别强，强到可以覆盖一个人的个体身份。你呢？你作为外国人在中国，人家问你是哪里人的时候，会不会有类似的感觉？
turns.text_pinyin: Zhǔtǐ xìng — duì, jiù shì zhège cí. Yǒu shíhou wǒ juéde Zhōngguó de dìyù wénhuà rèntóng tèbié qiáng, qiáng dào kěyǐ fùgài yīge rén de gètǐ shēnfèn. Nǐ ne? Nǐ zuòwéi wàiguó rén zài Zhōngguó, rénjiā wèn nǐ shì nǎlǐ rén de shíhou, huì bú huì yǒu lèisì de gǎnjué?
turns.text_en: Agency — yes, that's the word. Sometimes I feel like regional cultural identity in China is especially strong, so strong it can override a person's individual identity. What about you? As a foreigner in China, when people ask where you're from, do you ever feel something similar?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Share your own experience with the question of belonging.

options.text_zh: 有过。不过反过来——在国外我是美国人，在美国被问'你的根在哪里'。好像每个人都必须有'原始版本'。也许有些人的身份就是拼凑出来的，不来自某个地方，而来自很多次选择的叠加。
options.text_pinyin: Yǒu guò. Búguò fǎn guòlái — zài guówài wǒ shì Měiguó rén, zài Měiguó bèi wèn 'nǐ de gēn zài nǎlǐ'. Hǎoxiàng měi ge rén dōu bìxū yǒu 'yuánshǐ bǎnběn'. Yěxǔ yǒuxiē rén de shēnfèn jiù shì pīncòu chūlái de, bù lái zì mǒu ge dìfang, ér lái zì hěn duō cì xuǎnzé de diéjiā.
options.text_en: I have. But reversed — abroad I'm American, in America I'm asked 'where are your roots.' As if everyone must have an 'original version.' Maybe some identities are assembled from many choices, not from one place.
options.feedback: Honest reciprocity — shares a parallel experience and offers a constructive reframing of identity as assembled rather than inherited.

options.text_zh: 你有没有去过国外？想不想出去旅游？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu qù guò guówài? Xiǎng bù xiǎng chūqù lǚyóu?
options.text_en: Have you ever been abroad? Do you want to travel?
options.feedback: Generic travel question when she's asked something deeply personal.

options.text_zh: 我觉得学中文挺难的，你觉得呢？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde xué Zhōngwén tǐng nán de, nǐ juéde ne?
options.text_en: I think learning Chinese is pretty hard, what do you think?
options.feedback: Self-referential language-learner comment that deflects from the real conversation.

tree.cultural_note: The question '你是哪里人？' (Where are you from?) functions differently in Chinese culture than in the West. It often refers to 籍贯 (ancestral origin/household registration), not where you grew up. This creates identity friction for millions of Chinese people who've migrated internally — second-generation migrants may feel they belong neither to their parents' hometown nor to the city where they were raised. The hukou (household registration) system reinforces this by tying social benefits to registered origin rather than current residence.

tree.setup: You're stuck in a slow elevator in a Shenzhen tech park with a product manager named Zhao Yue. She's just come from a meeting about an AI-powered elevator system that 'learns' building traffic patterns. She finds the whole concept absurd.
tree.setup_zh: 你被困在深圳科技园一部很慢的电梯里，同行的是一位叫赵悦的产品经理。她刚从一个关于AI智能电梯系统的会议出来，这个系统据说能'学习'大楼的人流规律。她觉得这个概念太荒谬了。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你知道他们现在要给电梯装AI吗？说是可以预测每层楼的用电梯需求，提前把空电梯派过去。我就问了一句：'那如果它预测错了呢？'会议室安静了十秒钟。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhīdào tāmen xiànzài yào gěi diàntī zhuāng AI ma? Shuō shì kěyǐ yùcè měi céng lóu de yòng diàntī xūqiú, tíqián bǎ kōng diàntī pài guòqù. Wǒ jiù wèn le yī jù: 'Nà rúguǒ tā yùcè cuò le ne?' Huìyìshì ānjìng le shí miǎo zhōng.
turns.text_en: Did you know they want to install AI in elevators now? Supposedly it can predict elevator demand on each floor and dispatch empty ones in advance. I just asked one question: 'What if it predicts wrong?' The meeting room went silent for ten seconds.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the AI elevator concept.

options.text_zh: 十秒钟的沉默说明他们从来没想过这问题。这是很多AI项目的通病——先假设预测是准的，再倒推产品价值。但电梯不像推荐算法，推荐错了划一下就过去了，电梯预测错了人就在走廊干等。
options.text_pinyin: Shí miǎo zhōng de chénmò shuōmíng tāmen cónglái méi xiǎng guò zhè wèntí. Zhè shì hěn duō AI xiàngmù de tōngbìng — xiān jiǎshè yùcè shì zhǔn de, zài dào tuī chǎnpǐn jiàzhí. Dàn diàntī bù xiàng tuījiàn suànfǎ, tuījiàn cuò le huá yīxià jiù guòqù le, diàntī yùcè cuò le rén jiù zài zǒuláng gān děng.
options.text_en: Ten seconds of silence means they never considered this. Common AI flaw — assume predictions are accurate, then reverse-engineer value. But elevators aren't recommendation algorithms; wrong predictions mean people waiting in hallways.
options.feedback: Sharp structural critique — distinguishes between low-cost and high-cost prediction failures.

options.text_zh: 这栋楼有多少层？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè dòng lóu yǒu duōshǎo céng?
options.text_en: How many floors does this building have?
options.feedback: She's making a point about tech absurdity and you ask about building specs.

options.text_zh: 你下班以后一般做什么？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ xiàbān yǐhòu yībān zuò shénme?
options.text_en: What do you usually do after work?
options.feedback: Generic after-work question that ignores the conversation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 而且你有没有发现，所有这些'智能'产品，最后都是在要求用户配合机器。以前是机器为人服务，现在变成了人得按机器的逻辑行动。你得在它'期望'你出现的时间出现在它'预测'你会去的楼层。
turns.text_pinyin: Érqiě nǐ yǒu méiyǒu fāxiàn, suǒyǒu zhèxiē 'zhìnéng' chǎnpǐn, zuìhòu dōu shì zài yāoqiú yònghù pèihé jīqì. Yǐqián shì jīqì wèi rén fúwù, xiànzài biàn chéng le rén děi àn jīqì de luójí xíngdòng. Nǐ děi zài tā 'qīwàng' nǐ chūxiàn de shíjiān chūxiàn zài tā 'yùcè' nǐ huì qù de lóucéng.
turns.text_en: And have you noticed — all these 'smart' products end up demanding that users accommodate the machine. It used to be machines serving people; now people have to act according to the machine's logic. You need to appear at the time it 'expects' you, on the floor it 'predicts' you'll go to.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Build on her observation about humans serving machines.

options.text_zh: 这是一种很微妙的权力翻转。卖这些系统时说的都是'提升效率''优化体验'，但实际上是在要求人类变得更可预测。如果你不符合模型的假设，不是模型有问题——是你有问题。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè shì yī zhǒng hěn wēimiào de quánlì fānzhuǎn. Mài zhèxiē xìtǒng shí shuō de dōu shì 'tíshēng xiàolǜ' 'yōuhuà tǐyàn', dàn shíjì shàng shì zài yāoqiú rénlèi biàn de gèng kě yùcè. Rúguǒ nǐ bù fúhé móxíng de jiǎshè, bú shì móxíng yǒu wèntí — shì nǐ yǒu wèntí.
options.text_en: A subtle power inversion. When selling these systems they say 'improving efficiency,' but they're demanding humans become more predictable. If you don't fit the model's assumptions, the model doesn't have a problem — you do.
options.feedback: Excellent escalation — names the ideological inversion at the heart of predictive tech.

options.text_zh: 你觉得5G以后网速会更快吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ juéde 5G yǐhòu wǎngsù huì gèng kuài ma?
options.text_en: Do you think internet speeds will be faster with 5G?
options.feedback: Surface-level tech question that misses the philosophical thread.

options.text_zh: 这个电梯怎么这么慢啊？
options.text_pinyin: Zhège diàntī zěnme zhème màn a?
options.text_en: Why is this elevator so slow?
options.feedback: Stating the obvious rather than engaging with the ideas.

turns.speaker: npc
