options.feedback: Baking as improvised conversation — reading dough's nightly personality.

options.text_zh: 你用什么牌子的面粉？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yòng shénme páizi de miànfěn?
options.text_en: What brand of flour do you use?
options.feedback: Ingredient brand question during a meditation on craft.

options.text_zh: 你不累吗？每天熬夜。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ bú lèi ma? Měi tiān áo yè.
options.text_en: Aren't you tired? Staying up every night.
options.feedback: Fatigue question misses the philosophical depth.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 即兴的对话——对。但这个对话有一个很特别的观众：早上来买面包的人。他们不知道今天晚上我和面团之间发生了什么谈判。他们只是拿起一个面包，咬一口，觉得好吃。他们不知道这个面包好吃是因为今天晚上的湿度比昨天高了百分之五，我多加了一点水、少放了一点糖。他们吃到的是结果。但结果背后是整晚的判断、调整和等待。也许这就是所有好的手艺的特点——使用者看到的是简单，但简单背后是无数看不见的复杂。
turns.text_pinyin: Jíxīng de duìhuà — duì. Dàn zhège duìhuà yǒu yī ge hěn tèbié de guānzhòng: zǎoshang lái mǎi miànbāo de rén. Tāmen bù zhīdào jīntiān wǎnshang wǒ hé miàntuán zhī jiān fāshēng le shénme tánpàn. Tāmen zhǐ shì ná qǐ yī ge miànbāo, yǎo yī kǒu, juéde hǎochī. Tāmen bù zhīdào zhège miànbāo hǎochī shì yīnwèi jīntiān wǎnshang de shīdù bǐ zuótiān gāo le bǎi fēn zhī wǔ, wǒ duō jiā le yīdiǎn shuǐ, shǎo fàng le yīdiǎn táng. Tāmen chī dào de shì jiéguǒ. Dàn jiéguǒ bèihòu shì zhěng wǎn de pànduàn, tiáozhěng hé děngdài. Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì suǒyǒu hǎo de shǒuyì de tèdiǎn — shǐyòngzhě kàn dào de shì jiǎndān, dàn jiǎndān bèihòu shì wúshù kàn bú jiàn de fùzá.
turns.text_en: Improvised conversation — yes. But with a special audience: people buying bread in the morning. They don't know tonight's negotiation. They pick up bread, take a bite, think it's good. They don't know it's because tonight's humidity was five percent higher, so I adjusted water and sugar. They eat the result. Behind the result is a whole night of judgment, adjustment, and waiting. Perhaps good craftsmanship means the user sees simplicity, but behind it lies invisible complexity.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the invisible complexity behind apparent simplicity.

options.text_zh: 使用者看到的是简单——这几乎定义了所有伟大的工作。最好的设计让你觉得'当然如此'，最好的翻译让你忘了它是翻译。手艺人做得越好，别人越觉得'这有什么难的'。
options.text_pinyin: Shǐyòngzhě kàn dào de shì jiǎndān — zhè jīhū dìngyì le suǒyǒu wěidà de gōngzuò. Zuì hǎo de shèjì ràng nǐ juéde 'dāngrán rúcǐ', zuì hǎo de fānyì ràng nǐ wàng le tā shì fānyì. Shǒuyìrén zuò de yuè hǎo, biérén yuè juéde 'zhè yǒu shénme nán de'.
options.text_en: The user sees simplicity — that almost defines all great work. The best design makes you think 'of course.' The best translation makes you forget it's translated. The better the craftsman, the more people think 'what's so hard about that.'
options.feedback: The paradox of mastery — excellence becomes invisible and therefore undervalued.

options.text_zh: 你考虑过开连锁店吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kǎolǜ guò kāi liánsuǒ diàn ma?
options.text_en: Have you considered opening a chain?
options.feedback: Business scaling during a meditation on invisible craft.

options.text_zh: 你的面包比超市的贵吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de miànbāo bǐ chāoshì de guì ma?
options.text_en: Is your bread more expensive than supermarket bread?
options.feedback: Price comparison during a philosophical exchange.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 做得越好，别人越觉得不难——你说到了我心里最痛的地方。但我不在意了。因为我发现了一件事：凌晨三点，当整个城市都在睡觉的时候，我一个人在揉面——那个时刻本身就是奖励。没有人看，没有人评价，没有人知道。只有面团和我。这种安静是白天找不到的。我不是在为了早上的顾客而工作——我是在享受凌晨三点只属于我和面团的那几个小时。如果你问我为什么能做二十二年，答案不是坚持——是享受。坚持是痛苦的。享受是自然的。
turns.text_pinyin: Zuò de yuè hǎo, biérén yuè juéde bù nán — nǐ shuō dào le wǒ xīnlǐ zuì tòng de dìfang. Dàn wǒ bú zàiyì le. Yīnwèi wǒ fāxiàn le yī jiàn shì: língchén sān diǎn, dāng zhěnggè chéngshì dōu zài shuìjiào de shíhou, wǒ yī ge rén zài róu miàn — nàge shíkè běnshēn jiù shì jiǎnglì. Méiyǒu rén kàn, méiyǒu rén píngjià, méiyǒu rén zhīdào. Zhǐyǒu miàntuán hé wǒ. Zhè zhǒng ānjìng shì báitiān zhǎo bú dào de. Wǒ bú shì zài wèi le zǎoshang de gùkè ér gōngzuò — wǒ shì zài xiǎngshòu língchén sān diǎn zhǐ shǔyú wǒ hé miàntuán de nà jǐ ge xiǎoshí. Rúguǒ nǐ wèn wǒ wèishéme néng zuò èrshí'èr nián, dá'àn bú shì jiānchí — shì xiǎngshòu. Jiānchí shì tòngkǔ de. Xiǎngshòu shì zìrán de.
turns.text_en: The better you do, the less others think it's hard — you've touched my sorest point. But I don't mind anymore. At 3 AM, the whole city sleeps, I'm alone kneading — that moment is the reward. No one watching, no one judging. Just dough and me. This quiet can't be found in daytime. If you ask why twenty-two years, the answer isn't perseverance — it's enjoyment. Perseverance is painful. Enjoyment is natural.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to enjoyment being sustainable where perseverance is not.

options.text_zh: 坚持是痛苦的，享受是自然的——两种持续方式。坚持靠意志力，而意志力有限。享受不需要意志力，它自己会生长。二十二年不是因为意志坚强，是因为凌晨三点和面团就是您想要的生活。
options.text_pinyin: Jiānchí shì tòngkǔ de, xiǎngshòu shì zìrán de — liǎng zhǒng chíxù fāngshì. Jiānchí kào yìzhìlì, ér yìzhìlì yǒuxiàn. Xiǎngshòu bù xūyào yìzhìlì, tā zìjǐ huì shēngzhǎng. Èrshí'èr nián bú shì yīnwèi yìzhì jiānqiáng, shì yīnwèi língchén sān diǎn hé miàntuán jiù shì nín xiǎng yào de shēnghuó.
options.text_en: Perseverance is painful, enjoyment is natural — two modes of sustaining. Perseverance runs on willpower, which is finite. Enjoyment needs no willpower — it grows itself. Twenty-two years isn't strong will — 3 AM and dough are the life you want.
options.feedback: Enjoyment as self-sustaining — love expressing itself naturally looks like hardship from outside.

options.text_zh: 你有没有想过换一份工作？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu xiǎng guò huàn yī fèn gōngzuò?
options.text_en: Have you ever thought about changing jobs?
options.feedback: Career change question after a declaration of love for the work.

options.text_zh: 你的面包最受欢迎的是哪种？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de miànbāo zuì shòu huānyíng de shì nǎ zhǒng?
options.text_en: Which bread is your most popular?
options.feedback: Product question at the philosophical climax.

tree.cultural_note: Night bakers represent a tradition of invisible labor (看不见的劳动) that sustains daily life across Chinese cities. The concept of listening to materials rather than commanding them — 'the dough doesn't listen to you' — reflects the Daoist principle of 顺势而为 (shùnshì ér wéi, acting in accordance with natural tendencies). The distinction between 坚持 (jiānchí, perseverance through willpower) and 享受 (xiǎngshòu, sustaining through enjoyment) connects to broader Chinese philosophical discussions about whether virtuous behavior should require effort (Confucian self-cultivation) or flow naturally (Daoist wu-wei).

tree.setup: You're sitting with a sociology professor, Dr. Fang, at a university tea room. She's been studying how Chinese society has transformed in the past thirty years.
tree.setup_zh: 你和社会学教授方老师坐在大学的茶室里。她一直在研究过去三十年中国社会的变迁。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 西方用了两百年完成的工业化、城市化和信息化，中国用了三十年。这意味着我们的父辈、我们自己和我们的下一代，几乎生活在三个完全不同的世界里。这种断裂的速度是前所未有的。
turns.text_pinyin: Xīfāng yòng le liǎng bǎi nián wánchéng de gōngyèhuà, chéngshìhuà hé xìnxīhuà, Zhōngguó yòng le sānshí nián. Zhè yìwèi zhe wǒmen de fùbèi, wǒmen zìjǐ hé wǒmen de xià yīdài, jīhū shēnghuó zài sān ge wánquán bùtóng de shìjiè lǐ. Zhè zhǒng duànliè de sùdù shì qiánsuǒwèiyǒu de.
turns.text_en: The industrialization, urbanization, and informatization that the West took two hundred years to complete, China accomplished in thirty. This means our parents' generation, our own, and the next generation are living in what are essentially three entirely different worlds. The speed of this rupture is unprecedented.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Engage with Dr. Fang's observation about compressed change.

options.text_zh: 三代人，三个世界——这解释了为什么代际理解那么困难。不是哪一代不够好，而是变化太快，每一代形成价值观的环境完全不同。我们用着相同的语言，但指向的经验完全是两回事。
options.text_pinyin: Sān dài rén, sān ge shìjiè — zhè jiěshì le wèi shénme dàijì lǐjiě nàme kùnnan. Bú shì nǎ yīdài bú gòu hǎo, ér shì biànhuà tài kuài, měi yīdài xíngchéng jiàzhíguān de huánjìng wánquán bùtóng. Wǒmen yòng zhe xiāngtóng de yǔyán, dàn zhǐxiàng de jīngyàn wánquán shì liǎng huí shì.
options.text_en: Three generations, three worlds — that explains why intergenerational understanding is so hard. Not that any generation is inadequate, but change was too fast, each generation's value-forming environment completely different. Same language, entirely separate experiences.
options.feedback: Analytically sharp — connects the macro observation to the interpersonal level.

options.text_zh: 变化快是好事啊，说明中国发展得快。
options.text_pinyin: Biànhuà kuài shì hǎo shì a, shuōmíng Zhōngguó fāzhǎn de kuài.
options.text_en: Rapid change is a good thing — it shows China is developing fast.
options.feedback: One-dimensional — ignores the human cost she's analyzing.

options.text_zh: 您做这个研究多久了？
options.text_pinyin: Nín zuò zhège yánjiū duō jiǔ le?
options.text_en: How long have you been doing this research?
options.feedback: Shifts from the substantive discussion to personal background.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 相同的语言，不同的经验——你概括得很精准。我研究中发现最触动我的一点是：在急剧变化的社会里，人们普遍失去了一种「连续性」的感觉。你的过去和你的现在之间没有一条平滑的线索，像是被切断了。
turns.text_pinyin: Xiāngtóng de yǔyán, bùtóng de jīngyàn — nǐ gàikuò de hěn jīngzhǔn. Wǒ yánjiū zhōng fāxiàn zuì chùdòng wǒ de yīdiǎn shì: zài jíjù biànhuà de shèhuì lǐ, rénmen pǔbiàn shīqù le yī zhǒng 'liánxùxìng' de gǎnjué. Nǐ de guòqù hé nǐ de xiànzài zhī jiān méiyǒu yī tiáo pínghuá de xiànsuǒ, xiàng shì bèi qiēduàn le.
turns.text_en: Same language, different experiences — precisely summarized. The most striking finding in my research is this: in a rapidly changing society, people universally lose a sense of 'continuity.' There's no smooth thread between your past and your present — it feels severed.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Explore the idea of lost continuity.

options.text_zh: 这种断裂感也许就是很多人说的'找不到自己'。你在一个环境里成长，形成了认知，但等你长大那个环境已经不在了。既不属于过去，也不完全适应现在。连续性被压缩，认同就碎片化了。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè zhǒng duànliè gǎn yěxǔ jiù shì hěn duō rén shuō de 'zhǎo bú dào zìjǐ'. Nǐ zài yīge huánjìng lǐ chéngzhǎng, xíngchéng le rènzhī, dàn děng nǐ zhǎng dà nàge huánjìng yǐjīng bú zài le. Jì bù shǔyú guòqù, yě bù wánquán shìyìng xiànzài. Liánxùxìng bèi yāsuō, rèntóng jiù suìpiànhuà le.
options.text_en: That rupture is what people mean by 'can't find themselves.' You grow up in one environment, form a worldview, but by adulthood it's gone. Belonging neither to the past nor the present. Continuity compressed, identity fragmented.
options.feedback: Sophisticated analysis — links macro social change to individual psychological experience.

options.text_zh: 我觉得适应能力强是中国人的优点。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde shìyìng nénglì qiáng shì Zhōngguó rén de yōudiǎn.
options.text_en: I think strong adaptability is a Chinese strength.
options.feedback: Deflects the critical analysis with national pride.

options.text_zh: 现在年轻人不是都挺好的吗？比以前生活条件好多了。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài niánqīng rén bú shì dōu tǐng hǎo de ma? Bǐ yǐqián shēnghuó tiáojiàn hǎo duō le.
options.text_en: Aren't young people doing fine now? Living conditions are much better than before.
options.feedback: Material improvement doesn't address the existential dislocation she's describing.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 碎片化的认同——这正是我论文的核心论点。但我也不想让你觉得这只是一个学术概念。它是真实的痛。我的每一个学生都在经历这种撕裂：父母希望他们稳定，社会要求他们竞争，内心渴望他们能做自己。三个方向同时在拉扯。
turns.text_pinyin: Suìpiànhuà de rèntóng — zhè zhèng shì wǒ lùnwén de héxīn lùndiǎn. Dàn wǒ yě bù xiǎng ràng nǐ juéde zhè zhǐ shì yīge xuéshù gàiniàn. Tā shì zhēnshí de tòng. Wǒ de měi yīge xuéshēng dōu zài jīnglì zhè zhǒng sīliè: fùmǔ xīwàng tāmen wěndìng, shèhuì yāoqiú tāmen jìngzhēng, nèixīn kěwàng tāmen néng zuò zìjǐ. Sān ge fāngxiàng tóngshí zài lāchě.
turns.text_en: Fragmented identity — that's exactly the core argument of my paper. But I don't want you to think this is merely an academic concept. It's real pain. Every one of my students experiences this tearing: parents want stability, society demands competition, and their inner selves long to be authentic. Three directions pulling simultaneously.

tree.cultural_note: China's「压缩式现代化」(compressed modernization) — achieving in decades what other nations took centuries — has created unique sociological phenomena. The concept of「断裂社会」(fractured society) proposed by Chinese sociologist Sun Liping describes how rapid change severs social bonds and shared narratives. Young Chinese often describe feeling caught between traditional filial expectations, competitive market demands, and personal aspirations — a tension with no historical precedent.

title: Neither Here Nor There
title_zh: 既不属于这里，也不属于那里

tree.setup: You're having dinner with your friend Zhihao, who grew up in a small town, studied in Beijing, and now works in Shanghai. He's been thinking about where he truly belongs.
tree.setup_zh: 你和朋友志浩在吃晚饭。他在小镇长大，在北京读书，现在在上海工作。他一直在想自己真正属于哪里。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 过年回老家，亲戚们说我变了，说我「城里人了」。但在上海，我永远是外地人。我发现自己在任何一个地方都不完全属于。回去的时候我是外来的，在这里我也是外来的。你说这算什么？
turns.text_pinyin: Guò nián huí lǎojiā, qīnqimen shuō wǒ biàn le, shuō wǒ 'chénglǐ rén le'. Dàn zài Shànghǎi, wǒ yǒngyuǎn shì wàidì rén. Wǒ fāxiàn zìjǐ zài rènhé yīge dìfang dōu bù wánquán shǔyú. Huí qù de shíhou wǒ shì wàilái de, zài zhèlǐ wǒ yě shì wàilái de. Nǐ shuō zhè suàn shénme?
turns.text_en: When I go home for New Year, relatives say I've changed — 'you're a city person now.' But in Shanghai, I'm forever an outsider. I've realized I don't fully belong anywhere. When I go back, I'm the outsider. Here, I'm also the outsider. What do you call that?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Engage with his identity crisis.

options.text_zh: 也许这不是你的问题，是时代的特征。你经历了两个世界，吸收了两套价值观。不完全属于哪一边，但两边都是你的一部分。也许不属于任何地方本身就是一种归属——你属于你自己的经历。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ zhè bú shì nǐ de wèntí, shì shídài de tèzhēng. Nǐ jīnglì le liǎng ge shìjiè, xīshōu le liǎng tào jiàzhíguān. Bù wánquán shǔyú nǎ yī biān, dàn liǎng biān dōu shì nǐ de yī bùfen. Yěxǔ bù shǔyú rènhé dìfang běnshēn jiù shì yī zhǒng guīshǔ — nǐ shǔyú nǐ zìjǐ de jīnglì.
options.text_en: Maybe this isn't your problem, it's a feature of the era. You've lived in two worlds, absorbed two value systems. Not fully belonging to either, but both are part of you. Maybe not belonging anywhere is itself belonging — you belong to your own experience.
options.feedback: Reframes displacement as a unique form of identity — sophisticated and comforting.

options.text_zh: 你在哪里交社保，就算哪里的人呗。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zài nǎlǐ jiāo shèbǎo, jiù suàn nǎlǐ de rén bei.
options.text_en: Wherever you pay social security, that's where you're from.
options.feedback: This reduces identity to bureaucracy — misses the existential depth.

options.text_zh: 很多人都是这样的，你不用太纠结。
options.text_pinyin: Hěn duō rén dōu shì zhèyàng de, nǐ bú yòng tài jiūjié.
options.text_en: Lots of people are like this, don't overthink it.
options.feedback: Minimizes a real struggle by normalizing without engaging.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 属于自己的经历……这个说法让我想到了一个比喻。也许我不是一棵树——树必须扎根在一个地方。也许我更像一条河——经过很多地方，每个地方都留下了一些东西，也带走了一些东西。
turns.text_pinyin: Shǔyú zìjǐ de jīnglì…… zhège shuōfǎ ràng wǒ xiǎng dào le yīge bǐyù. Yěxǔ wǒ bú shì yī kē shù — shù bìxū zhāgēn zài yīge dìfang. Yěxǔ wǒ gèng xiàng yī tiáo hé — jīngguò hěn duō dìfang, měi ge dìfang dōu liú xià le yīxiē dōngxi, yě dài zǒu le yīxiē dōngxi.
turns.text_en: Belonging to your own experience... that phrasing makes me think of a metaphor. Maybe I'm not a tree — trees must root in one place. Maybe I'm more like a river — passing through many places, leaving something behind at each, and carrying something away.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Extend the river metaphor.

options.text_zh: 河的力量在于它不停留。带着上游的泥土流过平原，最终汇入大海。你身上有小镇的真诚，北京的思辨，上海的韧性。这些不是矛盾，而是你这条河的地层——每经过一个地方，你就更丰富一层。
options.text_pinyin: Hé de lìliang zàiyú tā bù tíngliú. Dài zhe shàngyóu de nítǔ liú guò píngyuán, zuìzhōng huì rù dàhǎi. Nǐ shēn shàng yǒu xiǎo zhèn de zhēnchéng, Běijīng de sībiàn, Shànghǎi de rènxìng. Zhèxiē bú shì máodùn, ér shì nǐ zhè tiáo hé de dìcéng — měi jīngguò yīge dìfang, nǐ jiù gèng fēngfù yī céng.
options.text_en: A river's strength is that it doesn't stay. Carrying upstream soil across plains, flowing into the sea. You carry your small town's sincerity, Beijing's critical thinking, Shanghai's resilience. Not contradictions but your river's geological layers — richer with every place.
options.feedback: Masterful extension — turns his metaphor into affirmation of his complex identity.

options.text_zh: 你想得太深了，其实在哪里生活都差不多。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ xiǎng de tài shēn le, qíshí zài nǎlǐ shēnghuó dōu chàbuduō.
options.text_en: You're thinking too deeply, life is about the same everywhere.
options.feedback: Flattening — denies the significance of place and displacement.

options.text_zh: 你可以落户上海啊，买个房就有归属感了。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ luòhù Shànghǎi a, mǎi ge fáng jiù yǒu guīshǔ gǎn le.
options.text_en: You could get Shanghai hukou and buy an apartment — that'll give you a sense of belonging.
options.feedback: Material solution to an existential question — completely misses the depth.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 每经过一个地方就更丰富了一层……谢谢你。也许我不需要回答「我属于哪里」这个问题。也许更好的问题是：我经过的每一个地方，给了我什么？我带着这些继续往前流就好了。
turns.text_pinyin: Měi jīngguò yīge dìfang jiù gèng fēngfù le yī céng…… xièxie nǐ. Yěxǔ wǒ bù xūyào huídá 'wǒ shǔyú nǎlǐ' zhège wèntí. Yěxǔ gèng hǎo de wèntí shì: wǒ jīngguò de měi yīge dìfang, gěi le wǒ shénme? Wǒ dài zhe zhèxiē jìxù wǎng qián liú jiù hǎo le.
turns.text_en: Richer with every place passed through... thank you. Maybe I don't need to answer 'where do I belong.' Maybe the better question is: what has each place I've passed through given me? I'll carry all of it and keep flowing forward.

tree.cultural_note: China's hukou (户籍) system creates a legal distinction between local residents and migrants, reinforcing a sense of not belonging for the hundreds of millions who live and work away from their registered hometown. The phrase「外地人」(wàidì rén, outsider/non-local) carries real social weight in cities like Shanghai and Beijing. The tension between hometown identity and urban adaptation is one of the defining psychological experiences of China's massive internal migration.

tree.setup: You and a cultural commentator friend, Xiaowei, are discussing the phenomenon of widespread exhaustion among young Chinese professionals — the 'lying flat' and 'let it rot' trends.
tree.setup_zh: 你和做文化评论的朋友小为在讨论中国年轻人普遍的疲惫现象——「躺平」和「摆烂」的文化趋势。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 「躺平」不是懒惰，而是一种消极抵抗。当你发现无论多努力都无法改变结构性的不公，最理性的反应就是退出竞争。它不是放弃，而是拒绝按照一套不合理的规则继续玩下去。
turns.text_pinyin: 'Tǎng píng' bú shì lǎnduò, ér shì yī zhǒng xiāojí dǐkàng. Dāng nǐ fāxiàn wúlùn duō nǔlì dōu wúfǎ gǎibiàn jiégòuxìng de bùgōng, zuì lǐxìng de fǎnyìng jiù shì tuìchū jìngzhēng. Tā bú shì fàngqì, ér shì jùjué ànzhào yī tào bù hélǐ de guīzé jìxù wán xià qù.
turns.text_en: 'Lying flat' isn't laziness — it's passive resistance. When you discover that no matter how hard you try, you can't change structural injustice, the most rational response is to exit the competition. It's not giving up — it's refusing to keep playing by an unreasonable set of rules.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Engage with her analysis of 'lying flat.'

options.text_zh: 确实。但当大量年轻人同时选择退出，这本身就是一种信号。不是个人问题，而是系统问题的症状。如果一个社会让最有活力的群体感到无力，需要反思的不是年轻人，而是这个系统。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ shuō de duì. Dàn dāng dàliàng niánqīng rén tóngshí xuǎnzé tuìchū, zhè běnshēn jiù shì yī zhǒng xìnhào. Bú shì gèrén wèntí, ér shì xìtǒng wèntí de zhèngzhuàng. Rúguǒ yīge shèhuì ràng zuì yǒu huólì de qúntǐ gǎndào wúlì, xūyào fǎnsī de bú shì niánqīng rén, ér shì zhège xìtǒng.
options.text_en: You're right. But when large numbers of young people simultaneously choose to exit, that itself is a signal. Not individual — systemic. If a society makes its most energetic group feel powerless, what needs reflection isn't the youth, but the system.
options.feedback: Structural thinking — elevates the discussion from cultural description to systemic analysis.

options.text_zh: 年轻人就是不够努力，以前的人更辛苦。
options.text_pinyin: Niánqīng rén jiù shì bú gòu nǔlì, yǐqián de rén gèng xīnkǔ.
options.text_en: Young people just aren't working hard enough — previous generations had it tougher.
options.feedback: Generational blame — ignores the structural analysis she's offering.

options.text_zh: 躺平也挺好的，不用那么累。
options.text_pinyin: Tǎng píng yě tǐng hǎo de, bú yòng nàme lèi.
options.text_en: Lying flat is fine, at least you're not tired.
options.feedback: Oversimplifies — treats a complex sociological phenomenon as a lifestyle choice.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你提到了关键词——「系统」。但问题在于，系统的调整需要时间，而人的生命有限。你不可能让一代人等系统改好了再开始活。所以很多人选择了第三条路：既不完全躺平，也不全力内卷，而是找到一个自己能接受的平衡点。不追求最好的，但追求足够好的。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ tí dào le guānjiàn cí — 'xìtǒng'. Dàn wèntí zàiyú, xìtǒng de tiáozhěng xūyào shíjiān, ér rén de shēngmìng yǒuxiàn. Nǐ bù kěnéng ràng yīdài rén děng xìtǒng gǎi hǎo le zài kāishǐ huó. Suǒyǐ hěn duō rén xuǎnzé le dì sān tiáo lù: jì bù wánquán tǎng píng, yě bù quánlì nèijuǎn, ér shì zhǎo dào yīge zìjǐ néng jiēshòu de pínghéng diǎn. Bù zhuīqiú zuì hǎo de, dàn zhuīqiú zúgòu hǎo de.
turns.text_en: You've identified the key word — 'system.' But the problem is, systemic adjustment takes time, while human lives are finite. You can't ask a generation to wait until the system improves before they start living. So many people have chosen a third path: neither fully lying flat nor going all-in on the rat race, but finding a balance point they can accept. Not pursuing the best, but pursuing good enough.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the 'third path' concept.

options.text_zh: '足够好'也许才是最务实的智慧。完美主义是内卷的燃料——总觉得不够好，永远停不下来。如果一代人能重新定义'成功'，不用房车职位衡量，而用内心平静来衡量，这本身就是一种变革。
options.text_pinyin: 'Zúgòu hǎo' yěxǔ cái shì zuì wùshí de zhìhuì. Wánměi zhǔyì shì nèijuǎn de ránliào — zǒng juéde bú gòu hǎo, yǒngyuǎn tíng bú xiàlái. Rúguǒ yīdài rén néng chóngxīn dìngyì 'chénggōng', bú yòng fángchē zhíwèi héngliáng, ér yòng nèixīn píngjìng lái héngliáng, zhè běnshēn jiù shì yī zhǒng biàngé.
options.text_en: 'Good enough' is the most pragmatic wisdom. Perfectionism fuels the rat race — always feeling insufficient, never stopping. If a generation redefines 'success' — measuring by inner peace instead of houses and titles — that itself is revolution.
options.feedback: Conclusion — positions the cultural shift as a quiet revolution of values.

options.text_zh: 但是不努力怎么买房？现实是残酷的。
options.text_pinyin: Dànshì bù nǔlì zěnme mǎi fáng? Xiànshí shì cánkù de.
options.text_en: But how can you buy a house without working hard? Reality is harsh.
options.feedback: She's questioning whether homeownership should be the measure of success.

options.text_zh: 我觉得每个时代都有自己的问题，没什么特别的。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde měi ge shídài dōu yǒu zìjǐ de wèntí, méi shénme tèbié de.
options.text_en: Every era has its own problems, nothing special about this one.
options.feedback: Dismissive relativism — refuses to engage with the specific dynamics she's analyzing.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 通过重新定义来变革……我同意。历史上很多真正持久的社会变化不是通过对抗实现的，而是通过一代人默默改变了他们的价值排序。也许正在发生的不是衰退，而是转向。只是我们身在其中，还看不清它的方向。
turns.text_pinyin: Tōngguò chóngxīn dìngyì lái biàngé…… wǒ tóngyì. Lìshǐ shàng hěn duō zhēnzhèng chíjiǔ de shèhuì biànhuà bú shì tōngguò duìkàng shíxiàn de, ér shì tōngguò yīdài rén mòmò gǎibiàn le tāmen de jiàzhí páixù. Yěxǔ zhèng zài fāshēng de bú shì shuāituì, ér shì zhuǎnxiàng. Zhǐ shì wǒmen shēn zài qí zhōng, hái kàn bù qīng tā de fāngxiàng.
turns.text_en: Revolution through redefinition... I agree. Many of history's most lasting social changes weren't achieved through confrontation, but through a generation quietly reshuffling their value priorities. Perhaps what's happening isn't decline, but a turn. It's just that being inside it, we can't yet see where it's heading.

tree.cultural_note: 「躺平」(tǎng píng, lying flat) emerged in 2021 as a viral Chinese internet term describing young people's rejection of overwork culture. 「内卷」(nèijuǎn, involution) describes hyper-competition with diminishing returns. Together, these concepts represent a generational renegotiation of the social contract. While older generations see this as laziness, cultural analysts increasingly view it as a rational response to structural conditions — housing unaffordability, credential inflation, and the exhaustion of the growth model that once promised upward mobility for all.

tree.setup: You're talking with your former classmate Yilin, now a teacher herself, about what the education system gave you and what it left out.
tree.setup_zh: 你和曾经的同学一琳聊天。她现在自己也当了老师，你们在聊教育体系给了你们什么，又缺少了什么。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我教了五年书以后才意识到，我们的教育体系非常擅长一件事：教你怎么得到正确答案。但它几乎没有教过你怎么提出正确的问题。我的学生很会考试，但面对没有标准答案的情境，很多人会慌。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ jiāo le wǔ nián shū yǐhòu cái yìshí dào, wǒmen de jiàoyù tǐxì fēicháng shàncháng yī jiàn shì: jiāo nǐ zěnme dédào zhèngquè dá'àn. Dàn tā jīhū méiyǒu jiāo guò nǐ zěnme tí chū zhèngquè de wèntí. Wǒ de xuéshēng hěn huì kǎoshì, dàn miànduì méiyǒu biāozhǔn dá'àn de qíngjìng, hěn duō rén huì huāng.
turns.text_en: After five years of teaching, I realized our education system is extremely good at one thing: teaching you how to get the right answer. But it's barely taught anyone how to ask the right question. My students are excellent at exams, but when faced with situations that have no standard answer, many of them panic.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Engage with her critique of the education system.

options.text_zh: 这也许就是考试驱动教育的核心矛盾——生产了大量能解决已知问题的人，却很少培养能识别未知问题的人。现实世界充满了后者。问一个好问题比给出一个对答案更难，也更有价值。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè yěxǔ jiù shì kǎoshì qūdòng jiàoyù de héxīn máodùn — shēngchǎn le dàliàng néng jiějué yǐzhī wèntí de rén, què hěn shǎo péiyǎng néng shìbié wèizhī wèntí de rén. Xiànshí shìjiè chōngmǎn le hòuzhě. Wèn yīge hǎo wèntí bǐ gěi chū yīge duì dá'àn gèng nán, yě gèng yǒu jiàzhí.
options.text_en: That's the core contradiction of exam-driven education — producing people who solve known problems, rarely cultivating those who identify unknown ones. The real world is full of the latter. Asking a good question is harder and more valuable than giving a right answer.
options.feedback: Analytically precise — identifies the structural limitation rather than blaming individuals.

options.text_zh: 中国学生的基础知识比外国学生扎实多了。
options.text_pinyin: Zhōngguó xuéshēng de jīchǔ zhīshi bǐ wàiguó xuéshēng zhāshi duō le.
options.text_en: Chinese students have much stronger foundational knowledge than foreign students.
options.feedback: Defensive comparison — doesn't engage with the critique.

options.text_zh: 现在不是在搞教育改革吗？应该会慢慢好的。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài bú shì zài gǎo jiàoyù gǎigé ma? Yīnggāi huì mànman hǎo de.
options.text_en: Aren't there education reforms happening? It should gradually improve.
options.feedback: Deflects to policy instead of engaging with the deeper philosophical point.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 已知问题和未知问题——你说到了关键。还有一个问题是情感教育的缺失。我们学了十几年数学和英语，但没有一门课教我们怎么处理愤怒、怎么表达脆弱、怎么跟不同意你的人好好说话。这些恰恰是成年以后最需要的能力。
turns.text_pinyin: Yǐzhī wèntí hé wèizhī wèntí — nǐ shuō dào le guānjiàn. Hái yǒu yīge wèntí shì qínggǎn jiàoyù de quēshī. Wǒmen xué le shí jǐ nián shùxué hé yīngyǔ, dàn méiyǒu yī mén kè jiāo wǒmen zěnme chǔlǐ fènnù, zěnme biǎodá cuìruò, zěnme gēn bù tóngyì nǐ de rén hǎohǎo shuōhuà. Zhèxiē qiàqià shì chéngnián yǐhòu zuì xūyào de nénglì.
turns.text_en: Known and unknown problems — you've hit the key point. There's another issue: the absence of emotional education. We studied math and English for over a decade, but not a single course taught us how to handle anger, how to express vulnerability, or how to have a conversation with someone who disagrees with you. These are precisely the skills most needed in adult life.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the gap in emotional education.

options.text_zh: 完全同意。我们被教导要赢，但没人教怎么输；被教导要坚强，但没人教怎么示弱。结果很多人在情感上是文盲——知识丰富，但不会跟自己的感受相处。最重要的教育不是教你做事，是教做人。
options.text_pinyin: Wánquán tóngyì. Wǒmen bèi jiàodǎo yào yíng, dàn méi rén jiāo zěnme shū; bèi jiàodǎo yào jiānqiáng, dàn méi rén jiāo zěnme shìruò. Jiéguǒ hěn duō rén zài qínggǎn shàng shì wénmáng — zhīshi fēngfù, dàn bú huì gēn zìjǐ de gǎnshòu xiāngchǔ. Zuì zhòngyào de jiàoyù bú shì jiāo nǐ zuòshì, shì jiāo zuòrén.
options.text_en: Absolutely. Taught to win but nobody teaches losing; taught to be strong but nobody teaches vulnerability. Many are emotionally illiterate — rich in knowledge, unable to sit with feelings. The most important education isn't about doing, it's about being.
options.feedback: Parallel structure — 'taught to win but not to lose' crystallizes the gap beautifully.

options.text_zh: 这些应该是家长教的，不是学校的责任。
options.text_pinyin: Zhèxiē yīnggāi shì jiāzhǎng jiāo de, bú shì xuéxiào de zérèn.
options.text_en: These are things parents should teach, not the school's responsibility.
options.feedback: Passes the buck — many parents had the same gaps in their own education.

options.text_zh: 你可以在你的班上试着加入这些内容。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ zài nǐ de bān shàng shì zhe jiārù zhèxiē nèiróng.
options.text_en: You could try adding this content in your own class.
options.feedback: Practical but sidesteps the broader conversation about systemic change.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 教你怎么做人……这也许是教育最原始的目的，只是在追求效率和排名的过程中被忘记了。我现在每周会花一节课的时间不讲课本，就和学生聊：你们最近在想什么？你们害怕什么？也许这改变不了制度，但至少我的教室可以是一个允许脆弱存在的地方。
turns.text_pinyin: Jiāo nǐ zěnme zuòrén…… zhè yěxǔ shì jiàoyù zuì yuánshǐ de mùdì, zhǐ shì zài zhuīqiú xiàolǜ hé páimíng de guòchéng zhōng bèi wàngjì le. Wǒ xiànzài měi zhōu huì huā yī jié kè de shíjiān bù jiǎng kèběn, jiù hé xuéshēng liáo: nǐmen zuìjìn zài xiǎng shénme? Nǐmen hàipà shénme? Yěxǔ zhè gǎibiàn bù liǎo zhìdù, dàn zhìshǎo wǒ de jiàoshì kěyǐ shì yīge yǔnxǔ cuìruò cúnzài de dìfang.
turns.text_en: Teaching how to be human... perhaps that's education's original purpose, just forgotten in the pursuit of efficiency and rankings. Now I spend one class period each week not teaching the textbook — just talking with students: what have you been thinking about? What are you afraid of? Maybe it can't change the system, but at least my classroom can be a place where vulnerability is allowed to exist.

tree.cultural_note: China's education system, centered around the gaokao (高考, national college entrance exam), has been both praised for producing strong foundational skills and criticized for suppressing creativity and emotional development. The concept of「情感教育」(qínggǎn jiàoyù, emotional education) is gaining attention among progressive Chinese educators. The phrase「做人」(zuòrén, being a person) carries deep philosophical weight in Chinese — it encompasses ethics, emotional intelligence, and social wisdom, and was historically considered education's primary purpose.

tree.setup: You and a philosophy graduate student, Mingzhe, are walking through an old temple garden. He's been struggling with the question of meaning after finishing his thesis.
tree.setup_zh: 你和哲学研究生明哲在一座古庙的花园里散步。他写完论文后一直在纠结「意义」这个问题。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我花了三年研究「意义」这个概念，写了十万字的论文。但写完的那一刻我发现自己反而更困惑了。如果意义不是被发现的而是被创造的，那创造本身有没有意义？这就变成了一个无限循环。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ huā le sān nián yánjiū 'yìyì' zhège gàiniàn, xiě le shí wàn zì de lùnwén. Dàn xiě wán de nà yī kè wǒ fāxiàn zìjǐ fǎn'ér gèng kùnhuò le. Rúguǒ yìyì bú shì bèi fāxiàn de ér shì bèi chuàngzào de, nà chuàngzào běnshēn yǒu méiyǒu yìyì? Zhè jiù biàn chéng le yīge wúxiàn xúnhuán.
turns.text_en: I spent three years studying the concept of 'meaning' and wrote a 100,000-character thesis. But the moment I finished, I found myself more confused than before. If meaning isn't discovered but created, does the act of creating itself have meaning? It becomes an infinite loop.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Engage with his philosophical spiral.

options.text_zh: 也许问题不在于找不到答案，而在于你用思考去找一个需要用生活来回答的问题。意义不在论文里。它在你做一件事忘了问'有没有意义'的那个瞬间。种树时你不问意义，你只是在种。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ wèntí bú zàiyú zhǎo bú dào dá'àn, ér zàiyú nǐ yòng sīkǎo qù zhǎo yīge xūyào yòng shēnghuó lái huídá de wèntí. Yìyì bú zài lùnwén lǐ. Tā zài nǐ zuò yī jiàn shì wàng le wèn 'yǒu méiyǒu yìyì' de nàge shùnjiān. Zhòng shù shí nǐ bú wèn yìyì, nǐ zhǐ shì zài zhòng.
options.text_en: Maybe the problem isn't finding the answer, but using thinking to answer something only living can. Meaning isn't in the thesis. It's in the moment you do something and forget to ask 'does this have meaning.' Planting a tree, you don't ask — you just plant.
options.feedback: Cuts through the intellectual spiral with experiential wisdom — exactly what a philosopher stuck in abstraction needs.

options.text_zh: 你想太多了，人活着就是吃饭睡觉工作，不需要什么意义。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ xiǎng tài duō le, rén huó zhe jiù shì chīfàn shuìjiào gōngzuò, bù xūyào shénme yìyì.
options.text_en: You're overthinking — life is just eating, sleeping, and working, no need for meaning.
options.feedback: Anti-intellectual — dismisses a genuine existential struggle.

options.text_zh: 你的导师怎么说？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de dǎoshī zěnme shuō?
options.text_en: What does your thesis advisor say?
options.feedback: Defers to authority — doesn't engage personally.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 忘了问的那个瞬间……你让我想到了庄子说的「鱼之乐」。鱼在水里游的时候不会问自己快不快乐，它只是在游。也许意义就像快乐一样，你越追它越跑，但当你不再追的时候，它反而就在身边了。
turns.text_pinyin: Wàng le wèn de nàge shùnjiān…… nǐ ràng wǒ xiǎng dào le Zhuāngzǐ shuō de 'yú zhī lè'. Yú zài shuǐ lǐ yóu de shíhou bú huì wèn zìjǐ kuài bú kuàilè, tā zhǐ shì zài yóu. Yěxǔ yìyì jiù xiàng kuàilè yíyàng, nǐ yuè zhuī tā yuè pǎo, dàn dāng nǐ bú zài zhuī de shíhou, tā fǎn'ér jiù zài shēnbiān le.
turns.text_en: The moment you forget to ask... you remind me of Zhuangzi's 'joy of fish.' When a fish swims, it doesn't ask whether it's happy — it just swims. Maybe meaning is like happiness — the more you chase it, the more it runs. But when you stop chasing, it's already beside you.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Close with a thought about living the answer.

options.text_zh: 也许你的论文不是白写的。你花了三年去追一个问题，追到最后发现答案不在终点，而在追的过程本身。这三年你读了书、思考了、困惑了、挣扎了——这些就是意义。意义不是结论，是体验。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ nǐ de lùnwén bú shì bái xiě de. Nǐ huā le sān nián qù zhuī yīge wèntí, zhuī dào zuìhòu fāxiàn dá'àn bú zài zhōngdiǎn, ér zài zhuī de guòchéng běnshēn. Zhè sān nián nǐ dú le shū, sīkǎo le, kùnhuò le, zhēngzhá le — zhèxiē jiù shì yìyì. Yìyì bú shì jiélùn, shì tǐyàn.
options.text_en: Maybe your thesis wasn't written in vain. You spent three years chasing a question and discovered the answer isn't at the destination but in the chase.
options.feedback: Resolves his paradox — reframes the journey as the answer.

options.text_zh: 毕业了打算做什么工作？
options.text_pinyin: Bìyè le dǎsuàn zuò shénme gōngzuò?
options.text_en: What kind of job are you planning to get after graduation?
options.feedback: Pulls him out of a profound moment into career planning.

options.text_zh: 你可以继续读博，再研究几年。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ jìxù dú bó, zài yánjiū jǐ nián.
options.text_en: You could do a PhD and research for a few more years.
options.feedback: More of the same approach — staying in the intellectual loop rather than breaking out.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 意义不是结论，是体验……这比我论文里写的任何一句话都更接近真相。谢谢你。也许我应该少读一点书，多种一点树。
turns.text_pinyin: Yìyì bú shì jiélùn, shì tǐyàn…… zhè bǐ wǒ lùnwén lǐ xiě de rènhé yī jù huà dōu gèng jiējìn zhēnxiàng. Xièxie nǐ. Yěxǔ wǒ yīnggāi shǎo dú yīdiǎn shū, duō zhòng yīdiǎn shù.
turns.text_en: Meaning isn't a conclusion, it's experience... that's closer to the truth than anything in my thesis. Thank you. Maybe I should read fewer books and plant more trees.

tree.cultural_note: Zhuangzi's (庄子) parable of the「鱼之乐」(joy of fish) is one of the most famous passages in Chinese philosophy — Zhuangzi observes fish swimming and declares they are happy, challenged by his friend Huizi who asks how he can know what fish feel. The dialogue raises questions about empathy, knowledge, and the nature of happiness. The tension between intellectual understanding and lived experience is central to Chinese philosophical traditions, where both Daoist and Chan Buddhist thought emphasize direct experience (体验) over abstract reasoning.

title: The Village That Became a City

tree.setup: You visit your friend Xiaogang's hometown in Guangdong. What was once a fishing village is now surrounded by high-rise developments. He shows you the one remaining ancestral hall.
tree.setup_zh: 你去广东探访朋友小刚的老家。这里曾经是一个渔村，现在被高楼包围了。他带你看唯一剩下的祠堂。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这个祠堂是我们村最后一栋旧建筑了。三年前他们差点也拆了，是几个老人跪在推土机前面才保住的。那些开发商不明白，对我们来说这不只是一栋房子——我们家族五代人的名字都刻在里面。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhège cítáng shì wǒmen cūn zuìhòu yī dòng jiù jiànzhù le. Sān nián qián tāmen chàdiǎn yě chāi le, shì jǐ ge lǎorén guì zài tuītǔjī qiánmiàn cái bǎo zhù de. Nàxiē kāifāshāng bù míngbai, duì wǒmen lái shuō zhè bù zhǐ shì yī dòng fángzi — wǒmen jiāzú wǔ dài rén de míngzi dōu kè zài lǐmiàn.
turns.text_en: This ancestral hall is the last old building in our village. Three years ago they nearly demolished it too — a few elders had to kneel in front of the bulldozers to save it. The developers don't understand — to us this isn't just a building. Five generations of our family's names are carved inside.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the significance of the ancestral hall.

options.text_zh: 五代人的名字——那不是建筑，是根。拆掉它等于拆掉一个家族的记忆。城市化追求效率和利润，但有些东西不能用经济指标衡量。祠堂可能不值钱，但它承载着社区的身份认同。
options.text_pinyin: Wǔ dài rén de míngzi — nà bú shì jiànzhù, shì gēn. Chāi diào tā děngyú chāi diào yīge jiāzú de jìyì. Chéngshìhuà zhuīqiú xiàolǜ hé lìrùn, dàn yǒuxiē dōngxi bù néng yòng jīngjì zhǐbiāo héngliáng. Cítáng kěnéng bù zhíqián, dàn tā chéngzài zhe shèqū de shēnfèn rèntóng.
options.text_en: Five generations of names — that's not architecture, that's roots. Tearing it down tears down a family's memory. Urbanization pursues efficiency, but some things can't be measured economically. A hall may not be worth money, but it carries a community's identity.
options.feedback: Framing — separates economic value from cultural and existential value.

options.text_zh: 但是旧房子住着不方便，新房子条件更好。
options.text_pinyin: Dànshì jiù fángzi zhù zhe bù fāngbiàn, xīn fángzi tiáojiàn gèng hǎo.
options.text_en: But old buildings aren't convenient to live in — new ones have better conditions.
options.feedback: The hall isn't about living conditions but identity.

options.text_zh: 政府应该把它保护起来，申请文物。
options.text_pinyin: Zhèngfǔ yīnggāi bǎ tā bǎohù qǐlái, shēnqǐng wénwù.
options.text_en: The government should protect it and apply for heritage status.
options.feedback: Bureaucratic solution — doesn't engage with the emotional and philosophical dimension.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 承载身份认同……你说到点子上了。你知道最让我心疼的是什么吗？不是建筑被拆了，而是建筑被拆以后，年轻人连这个地方曾经是什么都不知道了。我侄子出生的时候，村子已经变成了小区。他不知道这里曾经有海风、有渔船、有晒鱼干的架子。记忆的断层比建筑的断层更可怕。
turns.text_pinyin: Chéngzài shēnfèn rèntóng…… nǐ shuō dào diǎnzi shàng le. Nǐ zhīdào zuì ràng wǒ xīnténg de shì shénme ma? Bú shì jiànzhù bèi chāi le, ér shì jiànzhù bèi chāi yǐhòu, niánqīng rén lián zhège dìfang céngjīng shì shénme dōu bù zhīdào le. Wǒ zhízǐ chūshēng de shíhou, cūnzi yǐjīng biàn chéng le xiǎoqū. Tā bù zhīdào zhèlǐ céngjīng yǒu hǎifēng, yǒu yúchuán, yǒu shài yúgān de jiàzi. Jìyì de duàncéng bǐ jiànzhù de duàncéng gèng kěpà.
turns.text_en: Carrying identity... you've hit the point. You know what pains me most? Not that the buildings were demolished, but that after they were, young people don't even know what this place once was. When my nephew was born, the village had already become an apartment complex. He doesn't know this place once had sea breeze, fishing boats, and racks for drying fish. The rupture of memory is more frightening than the rupture of buildings.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the concept of memory rupture.

options.text_zh: 你在做记忆的守护者——用故事把这个地方的样子传下去。建筑可以被拆，但只要有人还讲这些故事，渔村就还活着。你侄子不记得海风，但你可以告诉他。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zài zuò jìyì de shǒuhù zhě — yòng gùshi bǎ zhège dìfang de yàngzi chuán xià qù. Jiànzhù kěyǐ bèi chāi, dàn zhǐyào yǒu rén hái jiǎng zhèxiē gùshi, yúcūn jiù hái huó zhe. Nǐ zhízǐ bú jìde hǎifēng, dàn nǐ kěyǐ gàosù tā.
options.text_en: You're a guardian of memory — passing down what this place was through stories. Buildings can be torn down, but as long as someone tells these stories, the fishing village lives. Your nephew doesn't remember the sea breeze, but you can tell him.
options.feedback: Elevates his role from mourner to guardian — transforms grief into purpose.

options.text_zh: 时代在发展，这是不可避免的。
options.text_pinyin: Shídài zài fāzhǎn, zhè shì bù kě bìmiǎn de.
options.text_en: Times are developing, this is unavoidable.
options.feedback: Fatalistic — denies the possibility and value of preservation.

options.text_zh: 你可以拍些照片和视频记录下来。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ pāi xiē zhàopiàn hé shìpín jìlù xiàlái.
options.text_en: You could take photos and videos to document it.
options.feedback: Practical but insufficient — documentation is different from living memory.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 记忆的守护者……嗯。来，我带你进去看看。里面的墙上刻着我太爷爷的名字。他一辈子是渔民，可能从来没想过他的孙子会站在高楼环绕的祠堂里，跟一个朋友讲述他的故事。但他的名字还在。只要名字还在，根就还在。
turns.text_pinyin: Jìyì de shǒuhù zhě…… en. Lái, wǒ dài nǐ jìn qù kànkan. Lǐmiàn de qiáng shàng kè zhe wǒ tàiyéye de míngzi. Tā yíbèizi shì yúmín, kěnéng cónglái méi xiǎng guò tā de sūnzi huì zhàn zài gāolóu huánrào de cítáng lǐ, gēn yīge péngyou jiǎngshù tā de gùshi. Dàn tā de míngzi hái zài. Zhǐyào míngzi hái zài, gēn jiù hái zài.
turns.text_en: Guardian of memory... yes. Come, I'll take you inside. My great-grandfather's name is carved on the wall inside. He was a fisherman his whole life. He probably never imagined his grandson would stand in an ancestral hall surrounded by high-rises, telling his story to a friend. But his name is still there. As long as the name remains, the roots remain.

tree.cultural_note: Ancestral halls (祠堂, cítáng) are central to Chinese clan culture, particularly in Guangdong and Fujian provinces. They serve as sites of ancestor worship, family record-keeping, and community gatherings. China's rapid urbanization has demolished countless such structures. The phrase「记忆的断层」(rupture of memory) captures a widespread concern that urbanization erases not just buildings but the intergenerational transmission of local identity, dialect, customs, and family history.

tree.setup: You and your friend Lina are sitting in a late-night ramen shop. She just had another argument with her parents about her life choices.
tree.setup_zh: 你和朋友丽娜在一家深夜拉面店里。她刚和父母又吵了一架，关于她的人生选择。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我妈又打电话来催婚了。她不理解我为什么三十岁了还不结婚，为什么放着稳定的国企不待非要去创业。在她看来，我做的每一个选择都是错的。但她不知道，按照她的标准活着会让我窒息。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ mā yòu dǎ diànhuà lái cuī hūn le. Tā bù lǐjiě wǒ wèi shénme sānshí suì le hái bù jiéhūn, wèi shénme fàng zhe wěndìng de guóqǐ bú dāi fēi yào qù chuàngyè. Zài tā kàn lái, wǒ zuò de měi yīge xuǎnzé dōu shì cuò de. Dàn tā bù zhīdào, ànzhào tā de biāozhǔn huó zhe huì ràng wǒ zhìxī.
turns.text_en: My mom called again to push marriage. She doesn't understand why I'm thirty and not married, why I left a stable state-owned enterprise to start my own business. In her eyes, every choice I've made is wrong. But she doesn't know that living by her standards would suffocate me.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to Lina's frustration with her parents.

options.text_zh: 你妈妈不是不爱你，她是用她那个年代的坐标系来爱你。在她经验里，稳定等于安全，结婚等于幸福。但你们活在不同时代，成功的定义变了。代际冲突不是谁对谁错，而是两套经验在碰撞。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ māma bú shì bú ài nǐ, tā shì yòng tā nàge niándài de zuòbiāo xì lái ài nǐ. Zài tā jīngyàn lǐ, wěndìng děngyú ānquán, jiéhūn děngyú xìngfú. Dàn nǐmen huó zài bùtóng shídài, chénggōng de dìngyì biàn le. Dàijì chōngtū bú shì shéi duì shéi cuò, ér shì liǎng tào jīngyàn zài pèngzhuàng.
options.text_en: Your mom isn't unloving — she loves you through her generation's coordinate system. In her experience, stability equals safety, marriage equals happiness. But you live in different eras, and success has been redefined. Generational conflict isn't right vs. wrong, it's two experience systems colliding.
options.feedback: 'Two experience systems colliding' — a precise, empathetic framing that validates both sides.

options.text_zh: 你就别听她的了，自己的人生自己做主。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ jiù bié tīng tā de le, zìjǐ de rénshēng zìjǐ zuòzhǔ.
options.text_en: Just don't listen to her — your life, your decisions.
options.feedback: Too simplistic — ignores the complexity of family bonds.

options.text_zh: 你妈说得也有道理，三十岁确实应该考虑一下了。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ mā shuō de yě yǒu dàolǐ, sānshí suì quèshí yīnggāi kǎolǜ yíxià le.
options.text_en: Your mom has a point — at thirty you really should start thinking about it.
options.feedback: Sides with the parent — invalidates her autonomy.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 两套经验系统碰撞……你这个比喻太准了。最难受的不是吵架，而是吵完以后她会哭。她一哭我就觉得自己是不是太自私了。但如果我为了让她不哭而放弃我想要的生活，那我的余生就要为她而活了。这个结怎么解？
turns.text_pinyin: Liǎng tào jīngyàn xìtǒng pèngzhuàng…… nǐ zhège bǐyù tài zhǔn le. Zuì nánshòu de bú shì chǎojià, ér shì chǎo wán yǐhòu tā huì kū. Tā yī kū wǒ jiù juéde zìjǐ shì bu shì tài zìsī le. Dàn rúguǒ wǒ wèile ràng tā bú kū ér fàngqì wǒ xiǎng yào de shēnghuó, nà wǒ de yúshēng jiù yào wèi tā ér huó le. Zhège jié zěnme jiě?
turns.text_en: Two experience systems colliding... that metaphor is spot on. The hardest part isn't the arguing — it's that she cries afterward. When she cries, I wonder if I'm being too selfish. But if I give up the life I want just so she won't cry, then I'll spend the rest of my life living for her. How do you untie this knot?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Offer wisdom on the impossible tension between self and family.

options.text_zh: 这个结也许不用解开，只要松一松。你不需要她完全理解你，也不必完全按她的方式来。但你可以让她看到你是认真的——不是叛逆，而是深思熟虑的选择。她的担心会慢慢变成信任。
options.text_pinyin: Zhège jié yěxǔ bú yòng jiěkāi, zhǐyào sōng yī sōng. Nǐ bù xūyào tā wánquán lǐjiě nǐ, yě bùbì wánquán àn tā de fāngshì lái. Dàn nǐ kěyǐ ràng tā kàn dào nǐ shì rènzhēn de — bú shì pànnì, ér shì shēnsī shúlǜ de xuǎnzé. Tā de dānxīn huì mànman biàn chéng xìnrèn.
options.text_en: Maybe this knot doesn't need untying, just loosening. You don't need her to fully understand you, nor follow her way entirely. But you can show her you're serious — not rebellion, but deliberate choice. Her worry will slowly become trust.
options.feedback: Mature and compassionate — reframes the conflict as a process rather than a problem to solve.

options.text_zh: 你妈妈那一代人不懂个人自由。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ māma nà yīdài rén bù dǒng gèrén zìyóu.
options.text_en: Your mom's generation doesn't understand personal freedom.
options.feedback: Dismissive of an entire generation — creates more division.

options.text_zh: 你有没有试过坐下来好好跟她谈一次？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu shì guò zuò xiàlái hǎohǎo gēn tā tán yī cì?
options.text_en: Have you tried sitting down and having a real talk with her?
options.feedback: Well-intentioned but naive — suggests a simple conversation can resolve deeply structural differences.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 担心慢慢变成信任……我希望是这样的。也许我需要的不是让她同意我，而是让她放心。谢谢你。吃完这碗面我回去给她打个电话。不聊那些争的东西，就问问她今天吃了什么、身体好不好。
turns.text_pinyin: Dānxīn mànman biàn chéng xìnrèn…… wǒ xīwàng shì zhèyàng de. Yěxǔ wǒ xūyào de bú shì ràng tā tóngyì wǒ, ér shì ràng tā fàngxīn. Xièxie nǐ. Chī wán zhè wǎn miàn wǒ huí qù gěi tā dǎ ge diànhuà. Bù liáo nàxiē zhēng de dōngxi, jiù wènwen tā jīntiān chī le shénme, shēntǐ hǎo bù hǎo.
turns.text_en: Worry slowly becoming trust... I hope so. Maybe what I need isn't her agreement, but her peace of mind. Thank you. After this bowl of ramen, I'll call her. Won't talk about the things we argue about — just ask what she ate today, how she's feeling.

tree.cultural_note: The tension between filial piety (孝) and individual autonomy is one of the defining struggles for young Chinese adults. The phrase「催婚」(cuī hūn, pushing for marriage) describes the relentless family pressure many face to marry by a certain age. Chinese parents' emotional strategies — especially a mother's tears — carry enormous weight in a culture where causing parental distress is deeply stigmatized. The emerging compromise — living autonomously while maintaining emotional connection — represents a new generation's attempt to honor both tradition and selfhood.

tree.setup: You visit your friend Yiming's studio. He's a calligrapher who left a lucrative tech job to practice his art full-time. His friends think he's lost his mind.
tree.setup_zh: 你去朋友一鸣的工作室。他是一个书法家，辞掉了高薪的科技公司工作，全职练习书法。他的朋友们都觉得他疯了。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 大家都说我浪费了名校的文凭和大厂的经验。但你看这个「永」字——王羲之说过，一个「永」字包含了书法所有的基本笔画。我每天写一百个「永」字，每次都不一样。这种感觉，写代码的时候从来没有过。
turns.text_pinyin: Dàjiā dōu shuō wǒ làngfèi le míng xiào de wénpíng hé dà chǎng de jīngyàn. Dàn nǐ kàn zhège 'yǒng' zì — Wáng Xīzhī shuō guò, yīge 'yǒng' zì bāohán le shūfǎ suǒyǒu de jīběn bǐhuà. Wǒ měi tiān xiě yībǎi ge 'yǒng' zì, měi cì dōu bù yíyàng. Zhè zhǒng gǎnjué, xiě dàimǎ de shíhou cónglái méiyǒu guò.
turns.text_en: Everyone says I've wasted my elite degree and big-company experience. But look at this character '永' — Wang Xizhi said a single '永' contains all the fundamental strokes of calligraphy. I write a hundred '永' characters every day, and each one is different. That feeling — I never had it writing code.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his choice and his art.

options.text_zh: 每个'永'字都不一样——这就是手工和机器的区别。代码追求可复制，书法追求不可复制。每一笔都带着你那一刻的呼吸和情绪。你没有浪费什么，你在用另一种语言表达自己。
options.text_pinyin: Měi ge 'yǒng' zì dōu bù yíyàng — zhè jiù shì shǒugōng hé jīqì de qūbié. Dàimǎ zhuīqiú kě fùzhì, shūfǎ zhuīqiú bù kě fùzhì. Měi yī bǐ dōu dài zhe nǐ nà yī kè de hūxī hé qíngxù. Nǐ méiyǒu làngfèi shénme, nǐ zài yòng lìng yī zhǒng yǔyán biǎodá zìjǐ.
options.text_en: Each character is different — that's the difference between craft and machine. Code pursues replicability; calligraphy pursues unreplicability. Each stroke carries your breath and emotion in that moment. You haven't wasted anything — you're expressing yourself in another language.
options.feedback: Sharp contrast between replicability and uniqueness — validates his art on its own terms.

options.text_zh: 但你靠书法能养活自己吗？
options.text_pinyin: Dàn nǐ kào shūfǎ néng yǎnghuó zìjǐ ma?
options.text_en: But can you make a living from calligraphy?
options.feedback: This reduces art to income — the question he hears from everyone else.

options.text_zh: 你可以一边工作一边练，不用辞职啊。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ yībiān gōngzuò yībiān liàn, bú yòng cízhí a.
options.text_en: You could practice while keeping your job, no need to quit.
options.feedback: Compromising advice that misses why full commitment matters to him.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 用一种不同的语言表达自己……谢谢你能这样看。很多人问我「书法有什么用」，但这个问题本身就是错的。你不会问一朵花有什么用、一首歌有什么用。美的存在不需要理由。但在一个什么都要有用的社会里，没有用的东西反而最珍贵。
turns.text_pinyin: Yòng yī zhǒng bùtóng de yǔyán biǎodá zìjǐ…… xièxie nǐ néng zhèyàng kàn. Hěn duō rén wèn wǒ 'shūfǎ yǒu shénme yòng', dàn zhège wèntí běnshēn jiù shì cuò de. Nǐ bú huì wèn yī duǒ huā yǒu shénme yòng, yī shǒu gē yǒu shénme yòng. Měi de cúnzài bù xūyào lǐyóu. Dàn zài yīge shénme dōu yào yǒuyòng de shèhuì lǐ, méiyǒu yòng de dōngxi fǎn'ér zuì zhēnguì.
turns.text_en: Expressing yourself in a different language... thank you for seeing it that way. Many people ask me 'what's the use of calligraphy,' but the question itself is wrong. You wouldn't ask what a flower is for, or what a song is for. Beauty doesn't need a reason to exist. But in a society where everything must be useful, useless things are paradoxically the most precious.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on the value of useless beauty.

options.text_zh: 庄子说过：'人皆知有用之用，而莫知无用之用也。'也许你书法最大的意义就在于它没有实用价值。它提醒我们，人不只是为了'有用'而存在。我们也需要无用的美和无目的的创造。
options.text_pinyin: Zhuāngzǐ shuō guò: 'Rén jiē zhī yǒuyòng zhī yòng, ér mò zhī wúyòng zhī yòng yě.' Yěxǔ nǐ shūfǎ zuì dà de yìyì jiù zàiyú tā méiyǒu shíyòng jiàzhí. Tā tíxǐng wǒmen, rén bù zhǐ shì wèile 'yǒuyòng' ér cúnzài. Wǒmen yě xūyào wúyòng de měi hé wú mùdì de chuàngzào.
options.text_en: Zhuangzi said: 'Everyone knows the use of the useful, but no one knows the use of the useless.' Maybe your calligraphy's greatest meaning is its lack of practical value. It reminds us we don't exist only to be useful. We also need useless beauty and purposeless creation.
options.feedback: Masterful use of Zhuangzi to validate his path — philosophically grounded and deeply affirming.

options.text_zh: 你的字确实写得很好看。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de zì quèshí xiě de hěn hǎokàn.
options.text_en: Your calligraphy really does look beautiful.
options.feedback: Surface compliment — doesn't engage with the philosophical dimension.

options.text_zh: 也许你可以在网上卖，现在很多人喜欢收藏。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ nǐ kěyǐ zài wǎng shàng mài, xiànzài hěn duō rén xǐhuan shōucáng.
options.text_en: Maybe you could sell online — lots of people like collecting calligraphy now.
options.feedback: Commodifies his art immediately after he explained why utility isn't the point.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 无用之用……庄子说到了我心里去了。来，我写一个字送给你。你想要什么字？
turns.text_pinyin: Wúyòng zhī yòng…… Zhuāngzǐ shuō dào le wǒ xīn lǐ qù le. Lái, wǒ xiě yīge zì sòng gěi nǐ. Nǐ xiǎng yào shénme zì?
turns.text_en: The use of the useless... Zhuangzi spoke right to my heart. Come, I'll write a character for you as a gift. What character would you like?

tree.cultural_note: Wang Xizhi (王羲之, 303-361 CE) is China's most celebrated calligrapher, and the character「永」(yǒng, eternity) is indeed considered to contain all eight basic strokes of Chinese calligraphy (永字八法). The Zhuangzi quote about「无用之用」(the use of the useless) is from a famous passage about a gnarled, 'useless' tree that survives precisely because no one cuts it down — a parable about the value of not being instrumentally useful. This philosophy profoundly resonates with artists in China's hyper-pragmatic modern economy.

title: What We Learn When Nobody Is Teaching
title_zh: 没人教的时候我们在学什么

tree.setup: You and your friend Xiaoqing, a kindergarten teacher, are discussing how children learn versus how adults learn. She's noticed something surprising in her classroom.
tree.setup_zh: 你和朋友小晴——一位幼儿园老师——在讨论孩子和成人学习方式的区别。她在教室里发现了一些有趣的事。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我发现了一个矛盾：孩子在自由玩耍的时候学到的东西，往往比我正式教他们的时候更多、更深。当我刻意去教的时候，他们在模仿我；但当我退后一步让他们自己探索的时候，他们在创造。教育最有效的时刻，也许恰恰是教育者消失的时刻。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ fāxiàn le yīge máodùn: háizi zài zìyóu wánshuǎ de shíhou xué dào de dōngxi, wǎngwǎng bǐ wǒ zhèngshì jiāo tāmen de shíhou gèng duō, gèng shēn. Dāng wǒ kèyì qù jiāo de shíhou, tāmen zài mófǎng wǒ; dàn dāng wǒ tuì hòu yī bù ràng tāmen zìjǐ tànsuǒ de shíhou, tāmen zài chuàngzào. Jiàoyù zuì yǒuxiào de shíkè, yěxǔ qiàqià shì jiàoyù zhě xiāoshī de shíkè.
turns.text_en: I've discovered a contradiction: what children learn during free play is often more and deeper than what they learn when I formally teach them. When I deliberately teach, they imitate me; but when I step back and let them explore, they create. Perhaps education's most effective moment is precisely when the educator disappears.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Engage with this paradox of teaching.

options.text_zh: 最好的老师不是给答案的人，而是创造条件让学生自己找到答案的人。你退后一步留出的空间，就是创造力的土壤。过度干预反而压缩了探索。教育的艺术在于知道什么时候不教。
options.text_pinyin: Zuì hǎo de lǎoshī bú shì gěi dá'àn de rén, ér shì chuàngzào tiáojiàn ràng xuéshēng zìjǐ zhǎo dào dá'àn de rén. Nǐ tuì hòu yī bù liú chū de kōngjiān, jiù shì chuàngzào lì de tǔrǎng. Guòdù gānyù fǎn'ér yāsuō le tànsuǒ. Jiàoyù de yìshù zàiyú zhīdào shénme shíhou bú jiào.
options.text_en: The best teacher isn't someone who gives answers, but creates conditions for students to find their own. The space you create by stepping back is soil for creativity. Over-intervention compresses exploration. The art of education is knowing when not to teach.
options.feedback: Precise and layered — extends her observation into educational philosophy.

options.text_zh: 但如果不教他们，他们怎么学到知识？
options.text_pinyin: Dàn rúguǒ bú jiào tāmen, tāmen zěnme xué dào zhīshi?
options.text_en: But if you don't teach them, how will they learn knowledge?
options.feedback: Misses her distinction between formal knowledge and deeper learning.

options.text_zh: 小孩子就是喜欢玩而已。
options.text_pinyin: Xiǎo háizi jiù shì xǐhuan wán éryǐ.
options.text_en: Little kids just like to play, that's all.
options.feedback: Dismisses play as unserious — exactly the mindset she's challenging.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 知道什么时候不教……这可能是最难学的教学技巧。我们整个体系都在训练老师「教得更多」，但很少有人思考「教得更少」的价值。我在想，也许成年人的很多问题就出在这里——我们被教了太多正确答案，以至于失去了自己提问的能力。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhīdào shénme shíhou bú jiào…… zhè kěnéng shì zuì nán xué de jiàoxué jìqiǎo. Wǒmen zhěnggè tǐxì dōu zài xùnliàn lǎoshī 'jiāo de gèng duō', dàn hěn shǎo yǒu rén sīkǎo 'jiāo de gèng shǎo' de jiàzhí. Wǒ zài xiǎng, yěxǔ chéngnián rén de hěn duō wèntí jiù chū zài zhèlǐ — wǒmen bèi jiāo le tài duō zhèngquè dá'àn, yǐzhì yú shīqù le zìjǐ tí wèn de nénglì.
turns.text_en: Knowing when not to teach... that may be the hardest teaching skill to learn. Our entire system trains teachers to 'teach more,' but rarely does anyone consider the value of 'teaching less.' I wonder — maybe many adult problems originate here. We were taught so many correct answers that we lost the ability to ask our own questions.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on how this affects adults.

options.text_zh: 完全同意。我们花了二十年接受教育，然后花余生试图找回教育前那个敢问「为什么」的自己。也许最好的教育不是增加什么，而是保护孩子天生就有的好奇心。不让它被标准答案覆盖掉。
options.text_pinyin: Wánquán tóngyì. Wǒmen huā le èrshí nián jiēshòu jiàoyù, ránhòu huā yúshēng shìtú zhǎo huí jiàoyù qián nàge gǎn wèn 'wèi shénme' de zìjǐ. Yěxǔ zuì hǎo de jiàoyù bú shì zēngjiā shénme, ér shì bǎohù háizi tiānshēng jiù yǒu de hàoqí xīn. Bú ràng tā bèi biāozhǔn dá'àn fùgài diào.
options.text_en: We spend twenty years being educated, then spend the rest recovering the self that dared ask 'why.' Maybe the best education isn't adding something, but protecting the curiosity children are born with. Not letting standard answers overwrite it.
options.feedback: Inversion — education as recovery, not acquisition.

options.text_zh: 但基础知识还是要教的，否则什么都不会。
options.text_pinyin: Dàn jīchǔ zhīshi háishì yào jiāo de, fǒuzé shénme dōu bú huì.
options.text_en: But basic knowledge still needs to be taught, otherwise they won't know anything.
options.feedback: Misses the nuance — she's not against teaching facts, but against suppressing inquiry.

options.text_zh: 你们幼儿园的课程是怎么安排的？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐmen yòu'éryuán de kèchéng shì zěnme ānpái de?
options.text_en: How is your kindergarten curriculum structured?
options.feedback: Bureaucratic — shifts from philosophy to scheduling.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 保护好奇心……也许这就是我真正的工作。不是教他们认字、算数，而是保护那个在他们眼睛里闪着光的东西。谢谢你，这次对话让我重新理解了我在做的事。
turns.text_pinyin: Bǎohù hàoqí xīn…… yěxǔ zhè jiù shì wǒ zhēnzhèng de gōngzuò. Bú shì jiāo tāmen rèn zì, suànshù, ér shì bǎohù nàge zài tāmen yǎnjīng lǐ shǎn zhe guāng de dōngxi. Xièxie nǐ, zhè cì duìhuà ràng wǒ chóngxīn lǐjiě le wǒ zài zuò de shì.
turns.text_en: Protecting curiosity... maybe that's my real job. Not teaching them characters and arithmetic, but protecting that light shining in their eyes. Thank you — this conversation helped me re-understand what I'm doing.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese education has traditionally emphasized teacher authority and knowledge transmission (传道授业解惑). The emerging counter-movement values play-based learning and inquiry, influenced by both Western educational philosophy (Montessori, Reggio Emilia) and indigenous Chinese traditions like the Socratic-style dialogues found in Confucian classics. The tension between exam preparation and genuine learning remains one of the most debated topics in Chinese educational reform.

tree.setup: Your friend Xiaohan has transformed an abandoned lot between apartment buildings into a community garden. Neighbors who never spoke before now share tools and recipes.
tree.setup_zh: 朋友小含把楼间的一块废弃空地改造成了社区花园。以前从不说话的邻居现在互相分享工具和食谱。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你知道最让我惊讶的是什么吗？不是这块空地变成了花园，而是人变了。三楼的李阿姨和五楼的王叔以前从来不打招呼，现在每天早上一起浇花。一块共享的土地创造了一种共享的关系。物理空间改变了社会空间。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhīdào zuì ràng wǒ jīngyà de shì shénme ma? Bú shì zhè kuài kòngdì biàn chéng le huāyuán, ér shì rén biàn le. Sān lóu de Lǐ āyí hé wǔ lóu de Wáng shū yǐqián cónglái bù dǎ zhāohu, xiànzài měi tiān zǎoshang yīqǐ jiāo huā. Yī kuài gòngxiǎng de tǔdì chuàngzào le yī zhǒng gòngxiǎng de guānxì. Wùlǐ kōngjiān gǎibiàn le shèhuì kōngjiān.
turns.text_en: You know what surprised me most? Not that this empty lot became a garden, but that people changed. Auntie Li on the third floor and Uncle Wang on the fifth never used to greet each other — now they water flowers together every morning. Shared land created shared relationships. Physical space changed social space.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on how space shapes community.

options.text_zh: 人际关系需要物理空间才能发生。高楼把人叠在一起，但没连在一起。花园给了陌生人自然开口的理由。社区不是组织出来的，是从合适的空间里长出来的。
options.text_pinyin: Rénjì guānxì xūyào wùlǐ kōngjiān cái néng fāshēng. Gāolóu bǎ rén dié zài yīqǐ, dàn méi lián zài yīqǐ. Huāyuán gěi le mòshēngrén zìrán kāikǒu de lǐyóu. Shèqū bú shì zǔzhī chūlái de, shì cóng héshì de kōngjiān lǐ zhǎng chūlái de.
options.text_en: Relationships need physical space to happen. High-rises stack people but don't connect them. A garden gives strangers a reason to naturally start talking. Community isn't organized — it grows from the right space.
options.feedback: Analytically sharp — connects micro-community building to urban planning principles.

options.text_zh: 你不怕物业来管吗？这块地有没有使用权的问题？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ bú pà wùyè lái guǎn ma? Zhè kuài dì yǒu méiyǒu shǐyòng quán de wèntí?
options.text_en: Aren't you worried about the property management? Are there land-use issues?
options.feedback: Bureaucratic concern that misses the social achievement.

options.text_zh: 挺好的，但能持续多久呢？
options.text_pinyin: Tǐng hǎo de, dàn néng chíxù duō jiǔ ne?
options.text_en: It's nice, but how long can it last?
options.feedback: Pessimistic — undermines what she's built before appreciating it.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 叠在一起但不连在一起——这正是我之前的感受。我在这栋楼住了三年，连隔壁邻居姓什么都不知道。我们共享墙壁，但不共享生活。这个花园做的事情其实很简单：它给了人一个一起做某件事的理由。不是一起消费，而是一起创造。
turns.text_pinyin: Dié zài yīqǐ dàn bù lián zài yīqǐ — zhè zhèng shì wǒ zhīqián de gǎnshòu. Wǒ zài zhè dòng lóu zhù le sān nián, lián gébì línjū xìng shénme dōu bù zhīdào. Wǒmen gòngxiǎng qiángbì, dàn bù gòngxiǎng shēnghuó. Zhège huāyuán zuò de shìqíng qíshí hěn jiǎndān: tā gěi le rén yīge yīqǐ zuò mǒu jiàn shì de lǐyóu. Bú shì yīqǐ xiāofèi, ér shì yīqǐ chuàngzào.
turns.text_en: Stacked together but not connected — that was exactly my feeling. I lived in this building for three years without knowing my next-door neighbor's surname. We share walls, but not lives. What this garden does is actually simple: it gives people a reason to do something together. Not consuming together, but creating together.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Discuss the difference between consuming and creating together.

options.text_zh: 这个区别太重要了。消费是并行的——我们坐在同一个餐厅但毫无交集。但创造是交互的——你必须协商、妥协、分享。一起种一棵树、修一堵矮墙，共同劳动产生的纽带比任何团建活动都强。
options.text_pinyin: Zhège qūbié tài zhòngyào le. Xiāofèi shì bìngxíng de — wǒmen zuò zài tóng yīge cāntīng dàn háo wú jiāojí. Dàn chuàngzào shì jiāohù de — nǐ bìxū xiéshāng, tuǒxié, fēnxiǎng. Yīqǐ zhòng yī kē shù, xiū yī dǔ ǎi qiáng, gòngtóng láodòng chǎnshēng de niǔdài bǐ rènhé tuánjiàn huódòng dōu qiáng.
options.text_en: That distinction is crucial. Consumption is parallel — sitting in the same restaurant without intersection. But creation is interactive — you must negotiate, compromise, share. Planting a tree together, repairing a wall together — bonds from shared labor are stronger than any team-building.
options.feedback: Sociological distinction — consumption isolates, creation connects.

options.text_zh: 你有没有想过把这个经验推广到其他小区？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu xiǎng guò bǎ zhège jīngyàn tuīguǎng dào qítā xiǎoqū?
options.text_en: Have you thought about expanding this to other neighborhoods?
options.feedback: Jumps to scaling before fully appreciating the local achievement.

options.text_zh: 但是每个人的审美不一样，种什么花不会吵架吗？
options.text_pinyin: Dànshì měi ge rén de shěnměi bù yíyàng, zhòng shénme huā bú huì chǎojià ma?
options.text_en: But everyone has different tastes — don't people argue about what flowers to plant?
options.feedback: Focuses on potential conflict rather than the community-building she's describing.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 在劳动中人是真实的……对。你无法假装种花。你的手脏了，你的膝盖疼了，你旁边的人帮你扶了一下花盆——这些真实的瞬间比任何社交软件上的互动都更有意义。也许我们不需要更多的社交平台，我们只需要更多的花园。
turns.text_pinyin: Zài láodòng zhōng rén shì zhēnshí de…… duì. Nǐ wúfǎ jiǎzhuāng zhòng huā. Nǐ de shǒu zāng le, nǐ de xīgài téng le, nǐ pángbiān de rén bāng nǐ fú le yíxià huāpén — zhèxiē zhēnshí de shùnjiān bǐ rènhé shèjiāo ruǎnjiàn shàng de hùdòng dōu gèng yǒu yìyì. Yěxǔ wǒmen bù xūyào gèng duō de shèjiāo píngtái, wǒmen zhǐ xūyào gèng duō de huāyuán.
turns.text_en: In labor, people are genuine... right. You can't fake planting flowers. Your hands get dirty, your knees hurt, the person beside you steadies a flower pot for you — these real moments are more meaningful than any interaction on social media. Maybe we don't need more social platforms. We just need more gardens.

tree.cultural_note: Community gardens (社区花园) are emerging in Chinese cities as grassroots responses to urban alienation. In traditional Chinese communities, shared courtyards (大院) naturally fostered neighborly bonds, but modern high-rise living has isolated people behind locked doors. The concept of「共建」(gòngjiàn, co-building) reflects a growing movement to reclaim shared spaces and rebuild community through collaborative action rather than consumption.

tree.setup: You're walking with an environmental journalist, Sister Ye, along a river that was once heavily polluted but has been partially restored. Fish have returned for the first time in twenty years.
tree.setup_zh: 你和环保记者叶姐沿着一条曾经严重污染但部分恢复了的河流散步。鱼二十年来第一次回来了。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看到水里那条鱼了吗？二十年前这条河是黑色的，工厂的废水直接排进来。现在鱼回来了。但很多人不知道的是，生态修复不是「修好了」就结束了——它需要持续的维护。自然有自愈的能力，但前提是我们停止伤害。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn dào shuǐ lǐ nà tiáo yú le ma? Èrshí nián qián zhè tiáo hé shì hēisè de, gōngchǎng de fèishuǐ zhíjiē pái jìnlái. Xiànzài yú huí lái le. Dàn hěn duō rén bù zhīdào de shì, shēngtài xiūfù bú shì 'xiū hǎo le' jiù jiéshù le — tā xūyào chíxù de wéihù. Zìrán yǒu zìyù de nénglì, dàn qiántí shì wǒmen tíngzhǐ shānghài.
turns.text_en: See that fish in the water? Twenty years ago this river was black — factory waste was dumped directly into it. Now the fish are back. But what most people don't know is that ecological restoration doesn't end when it's 'fixed' — it requires continuous maintenance. Nature has the ability to heal itself, but only if we stop the harm.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her observation about ecological recovery.

options.text_zh: '停止伤害'比'修复'更难。因为它意味着改变生产方式和消费习惯。鱼回来是好消息，也是提醒：自然的恢复力有限。不从根本上改变与自然的关系，今天回来的鱼明天还会消失。
options.text_pinyin: 'Tíngzhǐ shānghài' bǐ 'xiūfù' gèng nán. Yīnwèi tā yìwèi zhe gǎibiàn shēngchǎn fāngshì hé xiāofèi xíguàn. Yú huí lái shì hǎo xiāoxi, yě shì tíxǐng: zìrán de huīfù lì yǒuxiàn. Bù cóng gēnběn shàng gǎibiàn yǔ zìrán de guānxi, jīntiān huí lái de yú míngtiān hái huì xiāoshī.
options.text_en: 'Stopping harm' is harder than restoring. It means changing modes of production and consumption. Fish returning is good news but also a reminder: nature's resilience is limited. Without fundamentally changing our relationship with nature, today's fish will vanish again tomorrow.
options.feedback: Structural thinking — connects individual ecological recovery to systemic change.

options.text_zh: 现在环保法律越来越严了，应该会好的。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài huánbǎo fǎlǜ yuè lái yuè yán le, yīnggāi huì hǎo de.
options.text_en: Environmental laws are getting stricter now, it should improve.
options.feedback: Delegates responsibility to policy without engaging with the deeper relationship question.

options.text_zh: 这条鱼能吃吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè tiáo yú néng chī ma?
options.text_en: Can you eat this fish?
options.feedback: Consumptive reflex — exactly the mindset she's critiquing.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 改变与自然的关系……这才是根本。我采访过很多环保项目，最成功的不是技术最先进的，而是让当地人真正参与进来的。当一个渔民开始自发保护鱼群的时候，说明他不再把鱼看作资源，而是看作邻居。关系变了，行为才会变。
turns.text_pinyin: Gǎibiàn yǔ zìrán de guānxì…… zhè cái shì gēnběn. Wǒ cǎifǎng guò hěn duō huánbǎo xiàngmù, zuì chénggōng de bú shì jìshù zuì xiānjìn de, ér shì ràng dāngdì rén zhēnzhèng cānyù jìnlái de. Dāng yīge yúmín kāishǐ zìfā bǎohù yúqún de shíhou, shuōmíng tā bú zài bǎ yú kàn zuò zīyuán, ér shì kàn zuò línjū. Guānxì biàn le, xíngwéi cái huì biàn.
turns.text_en: Changing the relationship with nature... that's the root. I've reported on many environmental projects. The most successful ones aren't the most technologically advanced, but those that truly involve local people. When a fisherman starts voluntarily protecting fish stocks, it means he no longer sees fish as a resource but as a neighbor. When the relationship changes, behavior changes.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on seeing nature as neighbor rather than resource.

options.text_zh: 把鱼看作邻居——这是一种古老的世界观：人不是自然之外的存在，而是自然的一部分。我们用'征服自然'太久了，也许该换成'回到自然'。这条河教我们的不只是环保，更是谦逊。
options.text_pinyin: Bǎ yú kàn zuò línjū — zhè shì yī zhǒng gǔlǎo de shìjièguān: rén bú shì zìrán zhī wài de cúnzài, ér shì zìrán de yī bùfen. Wǒmen yòng 'zhēngfú zìrán' tài jiǔ le, yěxǔ gāi huàn chéng 'huí dào zìrán'. Zhè tiáo hé jiāo wǒmen de bù zhǐ shì huánbǎo, gèng shì qiānxùn.
options.text_en: Seeing fish as neighbors — that's an ancient worldview: humans aren't outside nature, but part of it. We've used 'conquering nature' too long; maybe it's time for 'returning to nature.' This river teaches us not just environmental protection, but humility.
options.feedback: Philosophically grounded — connects modern environmentalism to ancient wisdom.

options.text_zh: 个人的力量太小了，还是要靠政府和企业。
options.text_pinyin: Gèrén de lìliang tài xiǎo le, háishì yào kào zhèngfǔ hé qǐyè.
options.text_en: Individual efforts are too small — it still depends on government and corporations.
options.feedback: Surrenders agency — contradicts her point about local participation.

options.text_zh: 你做了这么多年环保报道，不觉得累吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zuò le zhème duō nián huánbǎo bàodào, bù juéde lèi ma?
options.text_en: After so many years of environmental reporting, don't you feel tired?
options.feedback: Shifts from substance to personal fatigue.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 谦逊……对。也许这条河恢复了以后，最大的收获不是鱼回来了，而是住在河边的人学会了谦逊。学会了不把自己当作这条河的主人，而是当作它的邻居。你看，那条鱼又跳起来了——它不知道二十年前这里发生了什么。但我们知道。知道了就有责任记住。
turns.text_pinyin: Qiānxùn…… duì. Yěxǔ zhè tiáo hé huīfù le yǐhòu, zuì dà de shōuhuò bú shì yú huí lái le, ér shì zhù zài hé biān de rén xuéhuì le qiānxùn. Xuéhuì le bù bǎ zìjǐ dàng zuò zhè tiáo hé de zhǔrén, ér shì dàng zuò tā de línjū. Nǐ kàn, nà tiáo yú yòu tiào qǐlái le — tā bù zhīdào èrshí nián qián zhèlǐ fāshēng le shénme. Dàn wǒmen zhīdào. Zhīdào le jiù yǒu zérèn jìzhù.
turns.text_en: Humility... yes. Perhaps after this river recovered, the greatest gain isn't that the fish returned, but that the people living by the river learned humility. Learned not to see themselves as the river's owner, but as its neighbor. Look, that fish jumped again — it doesn't know what happened here twenty years ago. But we know. And knowing brings the responsibility to remember.

tree.cultural_note: China's environmental restoration efforts represent one of the largest ecological rehabilitation programs in history. Rivers like the Yangtze have seen fishing bans (长江禁渔) to allow ecosystems to recover. Traditional Chinese philosophy, particularly Daoist concepts of「天人合一」(tiān rén hé yī, unity of heaven and humanity), offers philosophical foundations for environmental ethics that are being rediscovered alongside modern environmentalism.

tree.setup: You and your friend Liang are at dinner. He puts his phone face-down on the table — something he's been trying to do more often.
tree.setup_zh: 你和朋友亮在吃晚饭。他把手机屏幕朝下放在桌上——这是他最近在尝试的一个习惯。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我做了一个实验——记录自己每天看手机的时间。结果是六个小时。六个小时。我醒着的时间大概十六个小时，也就是说有三分之一我是在看一块屏幕。最可怕的不是数字本身，而是那六个小时里我几乎不记得自己看了什么。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ zuò le yīge shíyàn — jìlù zìjǐ měi tiān kàn shǒujī de shíjiān. Jiéguǒ shì liù ge xiǎoshí. Liù ge xiǎoshí. Wǒ xǐng zhe de shíjiān dàgài shíliù ge xiǎoshí, yě jiù shì shuō yǒu sān fēn zhī yī wǒ shì zài kàn yī kuài píngmù. Zuì kěpà de bú shì shùzì běnshēn, ér shì nà liù ge xiǎoshí lǐ wǒ jīhū bú jìde zìjǐ kàn le shénme.
turns.text_en: I ran an experiment — tracking my daily screen time. Result: six hours. Six hours. I'm awake about sixteen hours, which means one-third of my waking life is spent looking at a screen. The scariest part isn't the number itself, but that I can barely remember what I looked at during those six hours.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Engage with the implications of forgotten screen time.

options.text_zh: 六个小时不留下记忆——那些时间不是被'使用'了，而是被'消耗'了。真正的使用会留下痕迹：一本好书，一次好对话。但刷手机像精神热量消耗——你以为在摄入信息，其实只在消耗注意力。
options.text_pinyin: Liù ge xiǎoshí bù liú xià jìyì — nàxiē shíjiān bú shì bèi 'shǐyòng' le, ér shì bèi 'xiāohào' le. Zhēnzhèng de shǐyòng huì liú xià hénjì: yī běn hǎo shū, yī cì hǎo duìhuà. Dàn shuā shǒujī xiàng jīngshén rèliàng xiāohào — nǐ yǐwéi zài shèrù xìnxī, qíshí zhǐ zài xiāohào zhùyì lì.
options.text_en: Six hours leaving no memory — those hours weren't 'used,' they were 'consumed.' Real use leaves traces: a good book, a good conversation. Scrolling is mental calorie burn — you think you're taking in information, but you're just burning attention.
options.feedback: Sharp metaphor — 'consuming' vs 'using' time crystallizes the problem.

options.text_zh: 六个小时还好吧，很多人更多。
options.text_pinyin: Liù ge xiǎoshí hái hǎo ba, hěn duō rén gèng duō.
options.text_en: Six hours isn't that bad, many people use more.
options.feedback: Normalizes the problem by comparing downward.

options.text_zh: 你可以设一个使用限制啊。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ shè yīge shǐyòng xiànzhì a.
options.text_en: You can set a usage limit.
options.feedback: Technical fix that misses the deeper reflection he's pursuing.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 消耗注意力……太准了。而且你注意到了吗？手机不只偷走了我们的时间，还偷走了我们的「间隙」。以前排队的时候会发呆、等车的时候会看路人、无聊的时候会想事情。这些间隙是创造力的温床。现在所有的间隙都被屏幕填满了。我们再也没有机会无聊了。
turns.text_pinyin: Xiāohào zhùyì lì…… tài zhǔn le. Érqiě nǐ zhùyì dào le ma? Shǒujī bù zhǐ tōu zǒu le wǒmen de shíjiān, hái tōu zǒu le wǒmen de 'jiànxì'. Yǐqián páiduì de shíhou huì fādāi, děng chē de shíhou huì kàn lùrén, wúliáo de shíhou huì xiǎng shìqíng. Zhèxiē jiànxì shì chuàngzào lì de wēnchuáng. Xiànzài suǒyǒu de jiànxì dōu bèi píngmù tiánmǎn le. Wǒmen zài yě méiyǒu jīhuì wúliáo le.
turns.text_en: Consuming attention... spot on. And have you noticed? Phones don't just steal our time — they steal our 'gaps.' Waiting in line you used to daydream; waiting for the bus you'd watch passersby; when bored you'd think. Those gaps were the seedbed of creativity. Now all gaps are filled by screens. We never have the chance to be bored anymore.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on the lost art of boredom.

options.text_zh: 无聊是创造力的前奏——你说得完全对。所有伟大的想法都诞生在'无事可做'的时刻。我们害怕无聊，用手机填满每一秒。但也许我们失去的不只是时间，而是与自己内心对话的机会。
options.text_pinyin: Wúliáo shì chuàngzào lì de qiánzòu — nǐ shuō de wánquán duì. Suǒyǒu wěidà de xiǎngfǎ dōu dànshēng zài 'wú shì kě zuò' de shíkè. Wǒmen hàipà wúliáo, yòng shǒujī tiánmǎn měi yī miǎo. Dàn yěxǔ wǒmen shīqù de bù zhǐ shì shíjiān, ér shì yǔ zìjǐ nèixīn duìhuà de jīhuì.
options.text_en: Boredom as the prelude to creativity — you're right. All great ideas are born in 'nothing to do' moments. We fear boredom, filling every second with phones. But maybe what we've lost isn't just time, but the chance to dialogue with ourselves.
options.feedback: Builds on his insight beautifully — connects boredom to inner life and creativity.

options.text_zh: 我觉得手机也有好处啊，可以学很多东西。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde shǒujī yě yǒu hǎochù a, kěyǐ xué hěn duō dōngxi.
options.text_en: I think phones have benefits too — you can learn a lot.
options.feedback: Deflects with a 'both sides' argument instead of engaging with his specific point.

options.text_zh: 你把手机戒了吧，用功能机。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ bǎ shǒujī jiè le ba, yòng gōngnéng jī.
options.text_en: Just quit smartphones and use a basic phone.
options.feedback: Extreme solution that misses the nuance of his reflection.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 与自己内心对话的机会……嗯。也许今晚我们就从这顿饭开始。手机朝下放着，眼睛看着对面的人。这顿饭的每一分钟我都会记得，因为我没有在消耗，我在使用。
turns.text_pinyin: Yǔ zìjǐ nèixīn duìhuà de jīhuì…… en. Yěxǔ jīn wǎn wǒmen jiù cóng zhè dùn fàn kāishǐ. Shǒujī cháo xià fàng zhe, yǎnjīng kàn zhe duìmiàn de rén. Zhè dùn fàn de měi yī fēnzhōng wǒ dōu huì jìde, yīnwèi wǒ méiyǒu zài xiāohào, wǒ zài shǐyòng.
turns.text_en: The chance to dialogue with yourself... yes. Maybe tonight we start with this dinner. Phone face-down, eyes on the person across the table. I'll remember every minute of this meal, because I'm not consuming — I'm using.

tree.cultural_note: China has one of the world's highest smartphone usage rates, with the average person spending over 5 hours daily on mobile devices. The term「刷手机」(shuā shǒujī, scrolling) has become a recognized behavioral pattern. Chinese psychologists and cultural commentators increasingly discuss「注意力经济」(attention economy) and its effects on mental health. The practice of placing phones face-down (手机朝下) has become a symbolic gesture of presence in some Chinese social circles.

tree.setup: You meet a linguist, Dr. Zhou, who is documenting endangered dialects in southwestern China. She tells you about a village where only three elderly people still speak the local language.
tree.setup_zh: 你遇到了一位语言学家周博士，她正在记录中国西南地区濒危方言。她告诉你一个只有三位老人还在说当地语言的村庄。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 那个村子的语言没有文字。所有的知识——草药、天气预测、祭祀仪式——都存在于语言里。当最后一个说这种语言的人去世的时候，消失的不只是一种语言，而是一整套理解世界的方式。每一种语言都是人类认知的一个独特角度。
turns.text_pinyin: Nàge cūnzi de yǔyán méiyǒu wénzì. Suǒyǒu de zhīshi — cǎoyào, tiānqì yùcè, jìsì yíshì — dōu cúnzài yú yǔyán lǐ. Dāng zuìhòu yīge shuō zhè zhǒng yǔyán de rén qùshì de shíhou, xiāoshī de bù zhǐ shì yī zhǒng yǔyán, ér shì yī zhěng tào lǐjiě shìjiè de fāngshì. Měi yī zhǒng yǔyán dōu shì rénlèi rènzhī de yīge dútè jiǎodù.
turns.text_en: That village's language has no writing system. All knowledge — herbal medicine, weather prediction, ritual ceremonies — exists only within the language. When the last speaker dies, what disappears isn't just a language, but an entire way of understanding the world. Every language is a unique angle of human cognition.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the loss of linguistic diversity.

options.text_zh: 一整套理解世界的方式——这就是语言消亡最痛的地方。我们把语言当交流工具，但它远不止于此。每种语言都嵌入了几百年的观察和智慧。草药的名字可能就包含药性和采摘时节。
options.text_pinyin: Yī zhěng tào lǐjiě shìjiè de fāngshì — zhè jiù shì yǔyán xiāowáng zuì tòng de dìfang. Wǒmen bǎ yǔyán dāng jiāoliú gōngjù, dàn tā yuǎn bùzhǐ yú cǐ. Měi zhǒng yǔyán dōu qiàn rù le jǐ bǎi nián de guānchá hé zhìhuì. Cǎoyào de míngzi kěnéng jiù bāohán yàoxìng hé cǎizhāi shíjié.
options.text_en: An entire way of understanding the world — that's what makes language death painful. We treat language as a communication tool, but it's far more. Every language embeds centuries of observation and wisdom. A herb's name alone might encode its properties and harvest season.
options.feedback: Shows deep understanding of language as knowledge repository, not just communication tool.

options.text_zh: 大家都说普通话不是更方便吗？
options.text_pinyin: Dàjiā dōu shuō Pǔtōnghuà bú shì gèng fāngbiàn ma?
options.text_en: Isn't it more convenient if everyone speaks Mandarin?
options.feedback: Prioritizes convenience over cognitive diversity — the argument she's dismantling.

options.text_zh: 你可以把它录下来保存。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ bǎ tā lù xiàlái bǎocún.
options.text_en: You can record and preserve it.
options.feedback: Preservation is part of her work but doesn't engage with the philosophical dimension.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你说到了关键——语言是嵌入式的知识。我在录音的时候经常发现，有些概念在普通话里根本没有对应的词。比如那个村子的语言里有一个词，大概意思是「雨停了但空气还湿润着的那种安静」。这种精确的感受描述一旦消失，我们感知世界的维度就少了一个。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ shuō dào le guānjiàn — yǔyán shì qiàn rù shì de zhīshi. Wǒ zài lùyīn de shíhou jīngcháng fāxiàn, yǒuxiē gàiniàn zài Pǔtōnghuà lǐ gēnběn méiyǒu duìyìng de cí. Bǐrú nàge cūnzi de yǔyán lǐ yǒu yīge cí, dàgài yìsi shì 'yǔ tíng le dàn kōngqì hái shīrùn zhe de nà zhǒng ānjìng'.
