Zhè zhǒng jīngquè de gǎnshòu miáoshù yīdàn xiāoshī, wǒmen gǎnzhī shìjiè de wéidù jiù shǎo le yīge.
turns.text_en: You've hit the key point — language is embedded knowledge. While recording, I often find concepts that have no equivalent word in Mandarin. For example, this village's language has a word that roughly means 'the particular quiet when rain has stopped but the air is still moist.' Once such precise descriptions of feeling disappear, we lose a dimension of perceiving the world.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on what is lost when a word has no translation.

options.text_zh: 一个词的消亡就是一种感知能力的消亡。维特根斯坦说过'语言的界限就是世界的界限'。每失去一种语言，人类的世界就小了一圈。你保存的不是声音，而是人类理解现实的可能性。
options.text_pinyin: Yīge cí de xiāowáng jiù shì yī zhǒng gǎnzhī nénglì de xiāowáng. Wéitègēnsītǎn shuō guò 'yǔyán de jièxiàn jiù shì shìjiè de jièxiàn'. Měi shīqù yī zhǒng yǔyán, rénlèi de shìjiè jiù xiǎo le yī quān. Nǐ bǎocún de bú shì shēngyīn, ér shì rénlèi lǐjiě xiànshí de kěnéng xìng.
options.text_en: A word's extinction is the extinction of a perceptual ability. Wittgenstein said 'the limits of language are the limits of the world.' Each language lost shrinks the human world. What you're preserving isn't sound — it's humanity's possibility of understanding reality.
options.feedback: Philosophically rich — connects language death to the shrinking of human possibility.

options.text_zh: 全球化趋势下语言统一是不可避免的。
options.text_pinyin: Quánqiúhuà qūshì xià yǔyán tǒngyī shì bù kě bìmiǎn de.
options.text_en: Under globalization, language unification is inevitable.
options.feedback: Fatalistic — treats cultural loss as acceptable collateral of progress.

options.text_zh: 你研究这个能申请到科研经费吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yánjiū zhège néng shēnqǐng dào kēyán jīngfèi ma?
options.text_en: Can you get research funding for this?
options.feedback: This reduces urgent cultural preservation to funding logistics.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 嘴里装着一个无法复制的宇宙……这句话我要记住。下个月我要回去做最后一次完整的录音。也许那三位老人不会永远在，但至少他们的声音、他们的词、他们描述雨后安静的那种方式，会留下来。
turns.text_pinyin: Zuǐ lǐ zhuāng zhe yīge wúfǎ fùzhì de yǔzhòu…… zhè jù huà wǒ yào jìzhù. Xià ge yuè wǒ yào huí qù zuò zuìhòu yī cì wánzhěng de lùyīn. Yěxǔ nà sān wèi lǎorén bú huì yǒngyuǎn zài, dàn zhìshǎo tāmen de shēngyīn, tāmen de cí, tāmen miáoshù yǔ hòu ānjìng de nà zhǒng fāngshì, huì liú xiàlái.
turns.text_en: Mouths containing an irreplicable universe... I'll remember that phrase. Next month I'm going back for one final complete recording. Maybe those three elders won't be here forever, but at least their voices, their words, their way of describing post-rain quiet will remain.

tree.cultural_note: China has over 130 languages and countless dialects, many critically endangered. UNESCO estimates that a language dies approximately every two weeks globally. China's push for Mandarin (普通话) standardization, while economically beneficial, has accelerated dialect loss. Linguists and anthropologists increasingly frame this as a cognitive diversity crisis — each language encodes unique environmental knowledge, social structures, and ways of perceiving reality that are irreplaceable once lost.

title: The Meeting That Could Have Been an Email
title_zh: 本来可以是一封邮件的会议

tree.setup: You and your friend Xiaoli collapse into chairs at a coffee shop after surviving a particularly pointless three-hour meeting.
tree.setup_zh: 你和朋友小丽从一个特别没有意义的三小时会议出来后，瘫在了咖啡馆的椅子上。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 三个小时。三个小时的会议，真正有用的内容大概只有十五分钟。剩下的时间都在重复已经知道的信息、确认不需要确认的决定、以及轮流表态表忠心。这不是在工作，这是在表演工作。
turns.text_pinyin: Sān ge xiǎoshí. Sān ge xiǎoshí de huìyì, zhēnzhèng yǒuyòng de nèiróng dàgài zhǐ yǒu shíwǔ fēnzhōng. Shèng xià de shíjiān dōu zài chóngfù yǐjīng zhīdào de xìnxī, quèrèn bù xūyào quèrèn de juédìng, yǐjí lúnliú biǎotài biǎo zhōngxīn. Zhè bú shì zài gōngzuò, zhè shì zài biǎoyǎn gōngzuò.
turns.text_en: Three hours. Three hours of meeting, maybe fifteen minutes of actually useful content. The rest was repeating known information, confirming decisions that didn't need confirming, and taking turns performing loyalty through statements. This isn't working — it's performing work.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Engage with her critique of meeting culture.

options.text_zh: '表演工作'太精准了。很多组织里，可见性比产出更重要。安静高效地完成任务，没人注意；但在会议上滔滔不绝，大家觉得你'积极'。这种文化奖励的不是结果，而是姿态。
options.text_pinyin: 'Biǎoyǎn gōngzuò' tài jīngzhǔn le. Hěn duō zǔzhī lǐ, kějiàn xìng bǐ chǎnchū gèng zhòngyào. Ānjìng gāoxiào de wánchéng rènwù, méi rén zhùyì; dàn zài huìyì shàng tāotāo bùjué, dàjiā juéde nǐ 'jījí'. Zhè zhǒng wénhuà jiǎnglì de bú shì jiéguǒ, ér shì zītài.
options.text_en: 'Performing work' — devastatingly precise. In many organizations, visibility matters more than output. Quietly and efficiently finish tasks, nobody notices; talk at length in meetings, everyone thinks you're 'proactive.' This culture rewards posture, not results.
options.feedback: Systemic analysis — identifies the visibility-over-output dynamic driving meeting culture.

options.text_zh: 但你想想，那些表态的人也没办法吧，领导在场，谁敢不说两句？
options.text_pinyin: Dàn nǐ xiǎngxiang, nàxiē biǎotài de rén yě méi bànfǎ ba, lǐngdǎo zài chǎng, shéi gǎn bù shuō liǎng jù?
options.text_en: But think about it, those people making statements don't have a choice either, right? With the boss there, who dares not say a few words?
options.feedback: She just identified the system. You've redirected sympathy toward the performers instead of examining why the performance exists.

options.text_zh: 三个小时也不算特别长吧，上次我们那个季度总结开了五个小时。
options.text_pinyin: Sān ge xiǎoshí yě bú suàn tèbié cháng ba, shàng cì wǒmen nàge jìdù zǒngjié kāi le wǔ ge xiǎoshí.
options.text_en: Three hours isn't that long, is it? Last time our quarterly review went five hours.
options.feedback: She's critiquing the structure of meetings. You've turned it into a competition for who endures more.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 奖励姿态而不是结果……对。这就是为什么加班文化在中国这么根深蒂固。很多人加班不是因为工作没做完，而是因为早走会「不好看」。存在感比效率更重要。这种系统性的低效不是个人能改变的，它已经嵌入了整个评价体系。
turns.text_pinyin: Jiǎnglì zītài ér bú shì jiéguǒ…… duì. Zhè jiù shì wèi shénme jiābān wénhuà zài Zhōngguó zhème gēn shēn dì gù. Hěn duō rén jiābān bú shì yīnwèi gōngzuò méi zuò wán, ér shì yīnwèi zǎo zǒu huì 'bù hǎokàn'. Cúnzài gǎn bǐ xiàolǜ gèng zhòngyào. Zhè zhǒng xìtǒng xìng de dī xiào bú shì gèrén néng gǎibiàn de, tā yǐjīng qiàn rù le zhěnggè píngjià tǐxì.
turns.text_en: Rewarding posture over results... exactly. That's why overtime culture is so deeply rooted in China. Many people work late not because work isn't done, but because leaving early 'looks bad.' Presence matters more than efficiency. This systemic inefficiency isn't something individuals can change — it's embedded in the entire evaluation system.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Discuss how to resist performative work culture.

options.text_zh: 也许抵抗不是当面说'这可以是邮件'——而是在自己能控制的范围内重新定义好的工作。用结果说话，保护自己的时间，拒绝无意义的表演。改变不了系统，但别让它把你变成它的一部分。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ dǐkàng bú shì dāngmiàn shuō 'zhè kěyǐ shì yóujiàn' — ér shì zài zìjǐ néng kòngzhì de fànwéi nèi chóngxīn dìngyì hǎo de gōngzuò. Yòng jiéguǒ shuōhuà, bǎohù zìjǐ de shíjiān, jùjué wú yìyì de biǎoyǎn. Gǎibiàn bù liǎo xìtǒng, dàn bié ràng tā bǎ nǐ biàn chéng tā de yī bùfen.
options.text_en: Maybe resistance isn't saying 'this could be an email' — but redefining good work within your control. Let results speak, protect your time, refuse meaningless performance. You can't change the system, but don't let it make you part of it.
options.feedback: Pragmatic and empowering — offers resistance within constraints rather than futile confrontation.

options.text_zh: 我觉得关键是提高自己的效率，把能控制的部分做好。会议的事，改变不了就别想了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde guānjiàn shì tígāo zìjǐ de xiàolǜ, bǎ néng kòngzhì de bùfen zuò hǎo. Huìyì de shì, gǎibiàn bù liǎo jiù bié xiǎng le.
options.text_en: I think the key is to improve your own efficiency and handle what you can control. As for meetings, if you can't change them, don't dwell on it.
options.feedback: She's talking about resisting a culture. You've offered personal optimization advice that leaves the culture untouched.

options.text_zh: 其实领导也不容易，他们也有上面的压力，开会可能也是被逼的。
options.text_pinyin: Qíshí lǐngdǎo yě bù róngyì, tāmen yě yǒu shàngmiàn de yālì, kāi huì kěnéng yě shì bèi bī de.
options.text_en: Actually leadership has it tough too, they also have pressure from above, the meetings might be forced on them as well.
options.feedback: She's building toward agency. You've spread the helplessness upward through the hierarchy until nobody is responsible.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 不让系统把你变成它的一部分……我需要这句话。来，为了今天那三个小时，我请你喝咖啡。至少这十五分钟的对话，比那三个小时的会议有价值一万倍。
turns.text_pinyin: Bú ràng xìtǒng bǎ nǐ biàn chéng tā de yī bùfen…… wǒ xūyào zhè jù huà. Lái, wèile jīntiān nà sān ge xiǎoshí, wǒ qǐng nǐ hē kāfēi. Zhìshǎo zhè shíwǔ fēnzhōng de duìhuà, bǐ nà sān ge xiǎoshí de huìyì yǒu jiàzhí yī wàn bèi.
turns.text_en: Not letting the system make you part of it... I need that line. Come, for those three hours today, coffee's on me. At least this fifteen-minute conversation is ten thousand times more valuable than that three-hour meeting.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese workplace culture has been widely criticized for「形式主义」(xíngshì zhǔyì, formalism) — prioritizing the appearance of work over substance. The phrase「表演工作」(performing work) has become viral on Chinese social media. Long meetings, performative overtime (加班文化), and「内卷」(involution/rat race) are all manifestations of a system that rewards visibility over output. Young workers increasingly push back through「躺平」(lying flat) or seeking companies with more results-oriented cultures.

title: The Box That Sets You Free

tree.setup: You're talking with a musician friend, Yue, who composes music within strict traditional forms. She surprises you by saying constraints make her more creative, not less.
tree.setup_zh: 你在和音乐人朋友月聊天，她在严格的传统曲式里作曲。她出人意料地说限制让她更有创造力，而不是更少。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 很多人以为创造力就是完全的自由——想做什么就做什么。但我发现恰恰相反。当我面前有无限的选择时，我反而什么都写不出来。但当我给自己设定一个框架——比如只用五个音、或者固定在七拍——我的创造力反而被激发出来了。
turns.text_pinyin: Hěn duō rén yǐwéi chuàngzào lì jiù shì wánquán de zìyóu — xiǎng zuò shénme jiù zuò shénme. Dàn wǒ fāxiàn qiàqià xiāngfǎn. Dāng wǒ miànqián yǒu wúxiàn de xuǎnzé shí, wǒ fǎn'ér shénme dōu xiě bù chūlái. Dàn dāng wǒ gěi zìjǐ shèdìng yīge kuàngjià — bǐrú zhǐ yòng wǔ ge yīn, huòzhě gùdìng zài qī pāi — wǒ de chuàngzào lì fǎn'ér bèi jīfā chūlái le.
turns.text_en: Many people think creativity is total freedom — doing whatever you want. But I've found the opposite is true. When I face infinite choices, I can't write anything. But when I set a framework — like using only five notes, or fixing a seven-beat pattern — my creativity is actually triggered.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Explore the paradox of constraint and creativity.

options.text_zh: 这让我想到河流和河岸的关系。没有河岸的水只是漫无目的的洪水。正是因为两岸的限制，水才有了方向和力量。创造力也一样——框架不是牢笼，而是给能量一个流动的方向。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè ràng wǒ xiǎngdào héliú hé hé'àn de guānxì. Méiyǒu hé'àn de shuǐ zhǐ shì mànwú mùdì de hóngshuǐ. Zhèng shì yīnwèi liǎng àn de xiànzhì, shuǐ cái yǒu le fāngxiàng hé lìliang. Chuàngzào lì yě yíyàng — kuàngjià bú shì láolóng, ér shì gěi néngliàng yīge liúdòng de fāngxiàng.
options.text_en: This reminds me of a river and its banks. Water without banks is just aimless flood. The banks' constraint gives water direction and force. Creativity is the same — a framework isn't a cage, but gives energy a direction to flow.
options.feedback: Yue's eyes light up — she sets her pen down and leans forward, clearly wanting to keep going.

options.text_zh: 但太多限制不会扼杀创造力吗？
options.text_pinyin: Dàn tài duō xiànzhì bú huì èshā chuàngzào lì ma?
options.text_en: But wouldn't too many constraints kill creativity?
options.feedback: Reasonable question but misses the nuance of chosen vs imposed constraints.

options.text_zh: 我更喜欢自由发挥，不喜欢被规则限制。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ gèng xǐhuan zìyóu fāhuī, bù xǐhuan bèi guīzé xiànzhì.
options.text_en: I prefer free expression, I don't like being limited by rules.
options.feedback: Reflexive resistance — exactly the assumption she's challenging.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 河流和河岸……这个比喻太美了。你知道吗，中国传统诗词就是最好的证明。律诗要求五个字或七个字一行，押韵，平仄对仗——限制极其严格。但正是在这些限制里，李白和杜甫写出了人类历史上最自由的诗句。自由不是没有边界，而是在边界内舞蹈。
turns.text_pinyin: Héliú hé hé'àn…… zhège bǐyù tài měi le. Nǐ zhīdào ma, Zhōngguó chuántǒng shīcí jiù shì zuì hǎo de zhèngmíng. Lǜshī yāoqiú wǔ ge zì huò qī ge zì yī háng, yāyùn, píngzè duìzhàng — xiànzhì jíqí yángé. Dàn zhèng shì zài zhèxiē xiànzhì lǐ, Lǐ Bái hé Dù Fǔ xiě chū le rénlèi lìshǐ shàng zuì zìyóu de shījù. Zìyóu bú shì méiyǒu biānjiè, ér shì zài biānjiè nèi wǔdǎo.
turns.text_en: River and banks... what a beautiful metaphor. You know, Chinese classical poetry is the best proof. Regulated verse requires five or seven characters per line, rhyme, tonal patterns — extremely strict constraints. Yet within those constraints, Li Bai and Du Fu wrote some of the freest verses in human history. Freedom isn't the absence of boundaries, but dancing within them.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on freedom as dancing within boundaries.

options.text_zh: 在边界内舞蹈——也许这不只适用于艺术，也适用于人生。我们都有限制——时间有限、能力有限。但正是这些限制定义了我们的选择，让选择有意义。如果什么都可以做，做什么都没意义。
options.text_pinyin: Zài biānjiè nèi wǔdǎo — yěxǔ zhè bù zhǐ shìyòng yú yìshù, yě shìyòng yú rénshēng. Wǒmen dōu yǒu xiànzhì — shíjiān yǒuxiàn, nénglì yǒuxiàn. Dàn zhèng shì zhèxiē xiànzhì dìngyì le wǒmen de xuǎnzé, ràng xuǎnzé yǒu yìyì. Rúguǒ shénme dōu kěyǐ zuò, zuò shénme dōu méi yìyì.
options.text_en: Dancing within boundaries — maybe this applies not just to art but to life. We all have limits — time, ability. But limits define our choices and make them meaningful. If everything were possible, nothing would mean anything.
options.feedback: Extends the artistic principle into existential philosophy — limits create meaning.

options.text_zh: 你的音乐在哪里可以听到？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de yīnyuè zài nǎlǐ kěyǐ tīng dào?
options.text_en: Where can I listen to your music?
options.feedback: Shifts to promotion rather than engaging with the insight.

options.text_zh: 你有没有试过完全自由的即兴创作？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu shì guò wánquán zìyóu de jíxìng chuàngzuò?
options.text_en: Have you tried completely free improvisation?
options.feedback: Circles back to the assumption she's already addressed.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 每一步都是一个有重量的决定……对。也许这就是为什么有限的生命比永生更有价值。因为时间有限，所以每一天都有分量；因为能力有限，所以每一次努力都有意义。来，我弹给你听我最新写的那首曲子——五个音，七拍，但里面有整个宇宙。
turns.text_pinyin: Měi yī bù dōu shì yīge yǒu zhòngliàng de juédìng…… duì. Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì wèi shénme yǒuxiàn de shēngmìng bǐ yǒngshēng gèng yǒu jiàzhí. Yīnwèi shíjiān yǒuxiàn, suǒyǐ měi yī tiān dōu yǒu fènliàng; yīnwèi nénglì yǒuxiàn, suǒyǐ měi yī cì nǔlì dōu yǒu yìyì. Lái, wǒ tán gěi nǐ tīng wǒ zuìxīn xiě de nà shǒu qǔzi — wǔ ge yīn, qī pāi, dàn lǐmiàn yǒu zhěnggè yǔzhòu.
turns.text_en: Every step a weighted decision... yes. Maybe that's why a finite life is more valuable than immortality. Because time is limited, every day carries weight; because ability is limited, every effort has meaning. Come, let me play you my newest piece — five notes, seven beats, but it contains an entire universe.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese classical poetry (律诗, lǜshī) is one of the most strictly constrained literary forms in world literature — requiring exact character counts, tonal patterns (平仄), and rhyme schemes. Yet within these constraints, Tang Dynasty poets produced works of unparalleled emotional freedom and beauty. This paradox mirrors the broader Chinese aesthetic principle that form and freedom are not opposed but complementary — constraint channels creative energy rather than suppressing it.

title: The Question That Has No Rush

tree.setup: Late at night, you and your friend Xiaochen are the last two people in a small café. He's thirty-five and says he still doesn't know what his life is for.
tree.setup_zh: 深夜，你和朋友小辰是一家小咖啡馆里最后两个人。他三十五岁了，说他还是不知道自己活着是为了什么。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 三十五了。按照社会的标准，我应该已经找到了自己的「使命」或者「方向」。但说实话，我还是不知道。不是说我过得不好——工作稳定，朋友不少，身体健康。但总觉得少了什么，好像在等一个答案，但又不知道问题是什么。
turns.text_pinyin: Sānshí wǔ le. Ànzhào shèhuì de biāozhǔn, wǒ yīnggāi yǐjīng zhǎo dào le zìjǐ de 'shǐmìng' huòzhě 'fāngxiàng'. Dàn shuō shíhuà, wǒ háishì bù zhīdào. Bú shì shuō wǒ guò de bù hǎo — gōngzuò wěndìng, péngyou bù shǎo, shēntǐ jiànkāng. Dàn zǒng juéde shǎo le shénme, hǎoxiàng zài děng yīge dá'àn, dàn yòu bù zhīdào wèntí shì shénme.
turns.text_en: Thirty-five. By society's standards, I should have already found my 'mission' or 'direction.' But honestly, I still don't know. It's not that my life is bad — stable job, plenty of friends, good health. But something always feels missing, like I'm waiting for an answer without knowing the question.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his feeling of purposelessness.

options.text_zh: 也许'不知道'本身不是问题。我们被一种叙事绑架了——好像每个人都应该在某个年龄找到'人生使命'。但很多人一辈子都在寻找，过程本身就有意义。目的感不是找到的，是活出来的。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ 'bù zhīdào' běnshēn bú shì wèntí. Wǒmen bèi yī zhǒng xùshì bǎngjià le — hǎoxiàng měi ge rén dōu yīnggāi zài mǒu ge niánlíng zhǎo dào 'rénshēng shǐmìng'. Dàn hěn duō rén yíbèizi dōu zài xúnzhǎo, guòchéng běnshēn jiù yǒu yìyì. Mùdì gǎn bú shì zhǎo dào de, shì huó chūlái de.
options.text_en: Maybe 'not knowing' isn't the problem. We've been hijacked by a narrative — as if everyone should find their 'life mission' by a certain age. But many people search their whole lives, and the searching itself is meaningful. Purpose isn't found, it's lived into being.
options.feedback: Liberating — dismantles the pressure of 'finding purpose' and replaces it with daily authenticity.

options.text_zh: 你应该去做一些新的尝试，比如换个工作或者学一个新技能。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi qù zuò yīxiē xīn de chángshì, bǐrú huàn ge gōngzuò huòzhě xué yīge xīn jìnéng.
options.text_en: You should try something new, like changing jobs or learning a new skill.
options.feedback: Action-oriented but bypasses the existential depth he's expressing.

options.text_zh: 三十五岁还早啊，别着急。
options.text_pinyin: Sānshí wǔ suì hái zǎo a, bié zháojí.
options.text_en: Thirty-five is still young, don't worry.
options.feedback: Dismissive reassurance — doesn't engage with the real feeling.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 活出来的，不是找到的……这跟我一直在想的不一样。我以为目的感是一个答案——某一天你突然顿悟了，知道了自己应该做什么。但也许它更像种子——你不知道什么时候种下的，但有一天发现它已经在长了。
turns.text_pinyin: Huó chūlái de, bú shì zhǎo dào de…… zhè gēn wǒ yīzhí zài xiǎng de bù yíyàng. Wǒ yǐwéi mùdì gǎn shì yīge dá'àn — mǒu yī tiān nǐ tūrán dùnwù le, zhīdào le zìjǐ yīnggāi zuò shénme. Dàn yěxǔ tā gèng xiàng zhǒngzǐ — nǐ bù zhīdào shénme shíhou zhòng xià de, dàn yǒu yī tiān fāxiàn tā yǐjīng zài zhǎng le.
turns.text_en: Lived into being, not found... that's different from what I'd been thinking. I thought purpose was an answer — one day you suddenly have an epiphany and know what you should do. But maybe it's more like a seed — you don't know when it was planted, but one day you notice it's already growing.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Offer a closing thought about purpose as a growing thing.

options.text_zh: 也许你的种子已经在长了，只是你没认出它。每天帮推车的老人，周末给朋友做饭，今晚愿意说出困惑——这些不是在等待目的，这就是目的正在生长的方式。先活着，它会长出来。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ nǐ de zhǒngzǐ yǐjīng zài zhǎng le, zhǐ shì nǐ méi rèn chū tā. Měi tiān bāng tuī chē de lǎorén, zhōumò gěi péngyou zuòfàn, jīn wǎn yuànyì shuō chū kùnhuò — zhèxiē bú shì zài děngdài mùdì, zhè jiù shì mùdì zhèngzài shēngzhǎng de fāngshì. Xiān huó zhe, tā huì zhǎng chūlái.
options.text_en: Maybe your seed is already growing — you just haven't recognized it. Helping the old man every day, cooking for friends on weekends, voicing confusion tonight — these aren't waiting for purpose, they are purpose growing. Live first, it will grow.
options.feedback: Deeply compassionate and specific — shows him purpose is already present in his daily kindnesses.

options.text_zh: 你可以去读一些关于人生规划的书。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ qù dú yīxiē guānyú rénshēng guīhuà de shū.
options.text_en: You could read some books about life planning.
options.feedback: Turns an existential conversation into a self-help prescription.

options.text_zh: 我也不知道我活着是为了什么，但我不太想这些。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yě bù zhīdào wǒ huó zhe shì wèile shénme, dàn wǒ bú tài xiǎng zhèxiē.
options.text_en: I don't know what I'm living for either, but I don't think about it much.
options.feedback: Avoidant — he's trying to go deeper, not looking for someone to match his confusion.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 先活着，它自己会长出来……嗯。也许我一直在找的那个答案，其实一直在我身边。只是它太安静了，被我对宏大目标的执念遮住了。谢谢你。这杯最后的咖啡，是今晚最好的味道。
turns.text_pinyin: Xiān huó zhe, tā zìjǐ huì zhǎng chūlái…… en. Yěxǔ wǒ yīzhí zài zhǎo de nàge dá'àn, qíshí yīzhí zài wǒ shēnbiān. Zhǐ shì tā tài ānjìng le, bèi wǒ duì hóngdà mùbiāo de zhíniàn zhē zhù le. Xièxie nǐ. Zhè bēi zuìhòu de kāfēi, shì jīn wǎn zuì hǎo de wèidào.
turns.text_en: Live first, and it will grow on its own... yes. Maybe the answer I've been looking for has been beside me all along. It was just too quiet, obscured by my fixation on grand goals. Thank you. This last cup of coffee is the best taste of the evening.

tree.cultural_note: The question of purpose (人生意义) weighs heavily on many Chinese adults who grew up with clear, externally defined goals (study hard, pass exams, get a good job) but find themselves directionless once those milestones are achieved. The concept of「活出来」(huó chūlái, lived into being) echoes both existentialist philosophy and Chinese Buddhist practice — the idea that meaning emerges through engaged living rather than intellectual discovery. The midnight café conversation between friends is itself an example of purpose manifesting: authentic human connection.

title: The Mapmaker's Last Alley
title_zh: 制图师的最后一条巷子

tree.setup: You've wandered into a narrow Beijing hutong and found an elderly woman, Auntie Fang, carefully sketching a hand-drawn map of the neighborhood on rice paper. She explains she's been mapping every hutong in the district for thirty years — but this is the last one that hasn't been demolished.
tree.setup_zh: 你走进了北京一条狭窄的胡同，发现一位老太太——方阿姨——正在宣纸上仔细地绘制这片街区的手绘地图。她解释说她三十年来一直在绘制这个区里每一条胡同的地图——但这是最后一条还没被拆除的。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看这张图，每一条线都是我亲自走过的。有的巷子窄到只能侧身通过，但正因为窄，邻居之间反而亲近。现在这些线条大多数都变成了虚线——意思是已经不存在了。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn zhè zhāng tú, měi yī tiáo xiàn dōu shì wǒ qīnzì zǒu guò de. Yǒu de xiàngzi zhǎi dào zhǐ néng cè shēn tōngguò, dàn zhèng yīnwèi zhǎi, línjū zhī jiān fǎn'ér qīnjìn. Xiànzài zhèxiē xiàntiáo dàduōshù dōu biàn chéng le xūxiàn — yìsi shì yǐjīng bú cúnzài le.
turns.text_en: Look at this map — every line is a lane I walked myself. Some alleys were so narrow you could only pass through sideways, but precisely because they were narrow, neighbors felt close to each other. Now most of these lines have become dashed — meaning they no longer exist.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Say something to Auntie Fang about what her map is really preserving.

options.text_zh: 所以这不只是地图——是记忆的存档。等胡同都没了，也许只有您的图纸还能证明它们存在过。
options.text_pinyin: Suǒyǐ zhè bù zhǐ shì dìtú — shì jìyì de cúndàng. Děng hútòng dōu méi le, yěxǔ zhǐ yǒu nín de túzhǐ hái néng zhèngmíng tāmen cúnzài guò.
options.text_en: So this isn't just a map — it's an archive of memory. Once the hutongs are gone, maybe only your drawings will prove they ever existed.
options.feedback: She pauses her brush and looks up at you, holding eye contact for a moment longer than usual. Then she nods slowly and goes back to drawing.

options.text_zh: 这些虚线代表已经拆了的？那剩下几条还在？
options.text_pinyin: Zhèxiē xūxiàn dàibiǎo yǐjīng chāi le de? Nà shèngxià jǐ tiáo hái zài?
options.text_en: The dashed lines mean already demolished? How many solid ones are left?
options.feedback: She glances at the map, then back at you. "Just this one," she says quietly, and turns back to her drawing.

options.text_zh: 现在用手机导航就可以了，不需要手绘地图了吧？
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài yòng shǒujī dǎoháng jiù kěyǐ le, bù xūyào shǒuhuì dìtú le ba?
options.text_en: You can just use phone navigation now, no need for hand-drawn maps, right?
options.feedback: She doesn't look up from her paper. After a moment she says, "Maps tell you where to go. This tells you what was here."

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 抵抗？我没想过那么大的词。我只是觉得，一个地方如果连名字都没人记得了，那它就真的死了。你知道吗，很多胡同的名字本身就是故事——帽儿胡同是因为形状像帽子，百花深处是因为以前种满了花。
turns.text_pinyin: Dǐkàng? Wǒ méi xiǎng guò nàme dà de cí. Wǒ zhǐ shì juéde, yīge dìfang rúguǒ lián míngzì dōu méi rén jìde le, nà tā jiù zhēn de sǐ le. Nǐ zhīdào ma, hěn duō hútòng de míngzì běnshēn jiù shì gùshì — Màor Hútòng shì yīnwèi xíngzhuàng xiàng màozi, Bǎihuā Shēnchù shì yīnwèi yǐqián zhǒng mǎn le huā.
turns.text_en: Resistance? I never thought of such a grand word. I just feel that if no one even remembers a place's name, then it truly dies. Did you know, many hutong names are stories in themselves — Mao'er Hutong because it was shaped like a hat, Baihua Shenchu because it used to be full of flowers.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to what she said about names and the life of a place.

options.text_zh: 地名消失的方式很微妙。当百花深处变成写字楼的地址，名字还在，但含义已经空了。您的地图至少保留了名字和地点之间那层联系。
options.text_pinyin: Dìmíng xiāoshī de fāngshì hěn wéimiào. Dāng Bǎihuā Shēnchù biàn chéng xiězìlóu de dìzhǐ, míngzì hái zài, dàn hányì yǐjīng kōng le. Nín de dìtú zhìshǎo bǎoliú le míngzì hé dìdiǎn zhī jiān nà céng liánxì.
options.text_en: Place names disappear in subtle ways. When Baihua Shenchu becomes an office tower's address, the name survives but the meaning's been hollowed out. Your maps at least preserve that connection.
options.feedback: She tilts her head and murmurs, "Hollowed out — yes, that's exactly the right word for it," then writes something small in the corner of the map.

options.text_zh: 百花深处——我听说那里现在有一家很有名的咖啡馆。
options.text_pinyin: Bǎihuā Shēnchù — wǒ tīng shuō nàlǐ xiànzài yǒu yī jiā hěn yǒumíng de kāfēiguǎn.
options.text_en: Baihua Shenchu — I heard there's a famous café there now.
options.feedback: She gives you a long look. "Yes," she says. "There is."

options.text_zh: 北京现在的房价太高了，你觉得会降吗？
options.text_pinyin: Běijīng xiànzài de fángjià tài gāo le, nǐ juéde huì jiàng ma?
options.text_en: Housing prices in Beijing are too high now, do you think they'll go down?
options.feedback: She blinks, then quietly turns back to her map without answering.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 嗯……我有时候在想，如果我不画了，这些记忆会不会随着我一起消失？我今年七十八了，手也没以前稳了。我女儿说让我把地图扫描成电子版，但我总觉得纸上的东西有一种温度。
turns.text_pinyin: Ǹg... Wǒ yǒu shíhòu zài xiǎng, rúguǒ wǒ bú huà le, zhèxiē jìyì huì bu huì suízhe wǒ yīqǐ xiāoshī? Wǒ jīnnián qīshíbā le, shǒu yě méi yǐqián wěn le. Wǒ nǚ'ér shuō ràng wǒ bǎ dìtú sǎomiáo chéng diànzǐ bǎn, dàn wǒ zǒng juéde zhǐ shàng de dōngxī yǒu yī zhǒng wēndù.
turns.text_en: Mm... Sometimes I wonder, if I stop drawing, will these memories disappear along with me? I'm seventy-eight this year, and my hands aren't as steady as before. My daughter says I should scan the maps into digital copies, but I always feel that things on paper have a certain warmth.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you think about scanning versus keeping the paper originals?

options.text_zh: 两者不矛盾。电子版让更多人看到，但纸上的东西——哪里用力，哪里犹豫——扫描仪扫不出来。两种形式承载的信息不一样。
options.text_pinyin: Liǎng zhě bú máodùn. Diànzǐ bǎn ràng gèng duō rén kàn dào, dàn zhǐ shàng de dōngxī — nǎlǐ yònglì, nǎlǐ yóuyù — sǎomiáoyí sǎo bù chūlái. Liǎng zhǒng xíngshì chéngzài de xìnxī bù yīyàng.
options.text_en: They're not contradictory. Digital lets more people see them, but the things on paper — where you pressed hard, where you hesitated — a scanner can't capture that. The two carry different kinds of information.
options.feedback: She looks down at her own hand resting on the paper. "Where you hesitated," she repeats softly. "I never thought of it that way."

options.text_zh: 扫描也挺好的，电子版不会丢，以后还可以放大来看细节。
options.text_pinyin: Sǎomiáo yě tǐng hǎo de, diànzǐ bǎn bú huì diū, yǐhòu hái kěyǐ fàngdà lái kàn xìjié.
options.text_en: Scanning sounds good, digital copies won't get lost and you can zoom in on the details later.
options.feedback: She nods politely. "Yes, my daughter says the same thing," she says, and doesn't add anything more.

options.text_zh: 现在AI画画比人画得好多了，您可以试试。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài AI huàhuà bǐ rén huà de hǎo duō le, nín kěyǐ shìshi.
options.text_en: AI can draw much better than humans now, you should try it.
options.feedback: She looks up at you, then back at the map. She doesn't say anything, just keeps drawing.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你说得有道理。也许我应该两个都做。不过说实话，我最放不下的不是地图本身，是画地图的过程——每次走进一条胡同，都像在跟一个老朋友告别。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ shuō de yǒu dàolǐ. Yěxǔ wǒ yīnggāi liǎng ge dōu zuò. Búguò shuō shíhuà, wǒ zuì fàng bú xià de bú shì dìtú běnshēn, shì huà dìtú de guòchéng — měi cì zǒu jìn yī tiáo hútòng, dōu xiàng zài gēn yīge lǎo péngyǒu gàobié.
turns.text_en: You make a good point. Maybe I should do both. But honestly, what I can't let go of isn't the map itself, it's the process of making it — every time I walk into a hutong, it's like saying goodbye to an old friend.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the idea that mapping a hutong is a form of farewell.

options.text_zh: 也许告别本身也是一种陪伴。您没有假装什么都没变，而是选择把每一步都好好走完。等将来有人打开这张图，看到的不只是街道——还有一个人对这座城市的深情。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ gàobié běnshēn yě shì yī zhǒng péibàn. Nín méiyǒu jiǎzhuāng shénme dōu méi biàn, ér shì xuǎnzé bǎ měi yī bù dōu hǎohǎo zǒu wán. Děng jiānglái yǒu rén dǎkāi zhè zhāng tú, kàn dào de bù zhǐ shì jiēdào — hái yǒu yīge rén duì zhè zuò chéngshì de shēnqíng.
options.text_en: Maybe farewell is itself companionship. You didn't pretend nothing changed — you chose to walk every step to the end. When someone opens this map, they'll see not streets, but one person's deep feeling for this city.
options.feedback: Her hand stops moving. She looks up at you with something soft in her expression, then quietly says, "深情…… 是啊。" and turns back to the map.

options.text_zh: 那这条胡同画完之后，您打算做什么？
options.text_pinyin: Nà zhè tiáo hútòng huà wán zhīhòu, nín dǎsuàn zuò shénme?
options.text_en: After you finish mapping this hutong, what do you plan to do?
options.feedback: She pauses, then answers practically: "Rest my hand for a while, probably." She doesn't look up.

options.text_zh: 您应该去旅游，不要老是待在胡同里。
options.text_pinyin: Nín yīnggāi qù lǚyóu, búyào lǎo shì dāi zài hútòng lǐ.
options.text_en: You should go traveling, don't always stay in the hutongs.
options.feedback: She smiles briefly but it doesn't reach her eyes. "This is where I want to be," she says, and keeps drawing.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 深情……你这个年轻人说话倒是让人暖心。来，你帮我看看这个角落的比例画得准不准。这堵墙后面原来有一棵老槐树，去年被砍了。我还是把它画上了——在我的地图上，它永远都在。
turns.text_pinyin: Shēnqíng…… nǐ zhège niánqīngrén shuōhuà dào shì ràng rén nuǎnxīn. Lái, nǐ bāng wǒ kànkan zhège jiǎoluò de bǐlì huà de zhǔn bù zhǔn. Zhè dǔ qiáng hòumiàn yuánlái yǒu yī kē lǎo huáishù, qùnián bèi kǎn le. Wǒ háishì bǎ tā huà shàng le — zài wǒ de dìtú shàng, tā yǒngyuǎn dōu zài.
turns.text_en: Deep affection… you young people do say things that warm the heart. Come, help me check if the proportions of this corner are accurate. Behind this wall there used to be an old scholar tree — it was cut down last year. I drew it in anyway — on my map, it will always be there.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Say something about her choice to draw the tree even though it's gone.

options.text_zh: 这才是您的地图最珍贵的地方——它不只记录现在有什么，也记录了曾经有过什么。普通地图只呈现当下，您的包含了时间的厚度。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè cái shì nín de dìtú zuì zhēnguì de dìfang — tā bù zhǐ jìlù xiànzài yǒu shénme, yě jìlù le céngjīng yǒu guò shénme. Pǔtōng dìtú zhǐ chéngxiàn dāngxià, nín de bāohán le shíjiān de hòudù.
options.text_en: That's what makes your map most precious — it doesn't just record what exists now, but what once existed. Ordinary maps only show the present. Yours contains the thickness of time.
options.feedback: She pats the paper gently with one finger, right where the tree is drawn. "Thickness of time," she murmurs. "I like that."

options.text_zh: 画不存在的东西不就是造假吗？地图应该准确反映现实。
options.text_pinyin: Huà bù cúnzài de dōngxī bú jiù shì zàojiǎ ma? Dìtú yīnggāi zhǔnquè fǎnyìng xiànshí.
options.text_en: Drawing things that don't exist is just falsifying, isn't it? Maps should accurately reflect reality.
options.feedback: She looks at you steadily. "This is a memory map, not a navigation app," she says, and turns back to her work.

options.text_zh: 槐树被砍了太可惜了。是谁决定砍的？
options.text_pinyin: Huáishù bèi kǎn le tài kěxī le. Shì shéi juédìng kǎn de?
options.text_en: Such a shame the tree was cut down. Who decided to cut it?
options.feedback: She shakes her head slightly. "Does it matter now?" she says quietly, and keeps drawing.

tree.cultural_note: Hutong mapping has become a grassroots preservation movement in Beijing. As traditional neighborhoods are demolished for development, elderly residents and volunteers create hand-drawn maps that capture not just physical layouts but the social geography of communities — who lived where, which courtyards shared walls, where the communal water tap stood. These maps exist in tension with official cartography, preserving a human layer that satellite imagery cannot capture.

tree.setup: It's nearly midnight at a Taipei night market. Most stalls have closed, but a middle-aged woman named Sister Lin is still tending her scallion pancake stand. You're her last customer. She seems contemplative tonight, watching the empty street where crowds were bustling just an hour ago.
tree.setup_zh: 台北夜市快到午夜了。大多数摊位已经收摊，但一位中年女性——林姐——还在照看她的葱油饼摊。你是她最后一位客人。她今晚显得若有所思，望着一小时前还人潮涌动的空荡街道。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你知道吗，我最喜欢的其实不是人多的时候，是现在这个时间。灯还亮着，油烟的味道还没散，但人都走了。整条街突然变得很安静，像是在喘一口气。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhīdào ma, wǒ zuì xǐhuān de qíshí bú shì rén duō de shíhòu, shì xiànzài zhège shíjiān. Dēng hái liàng zhe, yóuyān de wèidào hái méi sàn, dàn rén dōu zǒu le. Zhěng tiáo jiē tūrán biàn de hěn ānjìng, xiàng shì zài chuǎn yī kǒu qì.
turns.text_en: You know, my favorite time isn't when it's crowded — it's right now. The lights are still on, the smell of cooking oil hasn't faded yet, but everyone's gone. The whole street suddenly goes quiet, like it's catching its breath.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to Sister Lin's observation about this quiet moment.

options.text_zh: 我能理解。白天的热闹是属于客人的，但这个安静的尾声才是属于你的。一个地方最真实的样子，往往是人群散去之后。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ néng lǐjiě. Báitiān de rènào shì shǔyú kèrén de, dàn zhège ānjìng de wěishēng cái shì shǔyú nǐ de. Yīge dìfang zuì zhēnshí de yàngzi, wǎngwǎng shì rénqún sàn qù zhīhòu.
options.text_en: I can understand that. The bustle belongs to the customers, but this quiet epilogue belongs to you. The most authentic side of a place often shows itself after everyone's gone.
options.feedback: She looks at you with a slight surprised smile, like you said something she's thought but never heard aloud. "Yes," she says. "Exactly that."

options.text_zh: 你应该早点收摊，这么晚对身体不好。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi zǎo diǎn shōu tān, zhème wǎn duì shēntǐ bù hǎo.
options.text_en: You should close up earlier, staying this late is bad for your health.
options.feedback: She nods with a patient expression. "My sister says the same thing," she says, and turns to wipe down the griddle.

options.text_zh: 这条街每天几点开始、几点结束？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè tiáo jiē měitiān jǐ diǎn kāishǐ, jǐ diǎn jiéshù?
options.text_en: What time does this street open and close every day?
options.feedback: She blinks, a little thrown by the turn. "Around five until midnight, usually," she says, then falls quiet again.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我妈以前也摆摊，就在这同一个位置。她教我说，做吃的这一行，重要的不是手艺多高超，而是你愿不愿意在别人看不到的时候也认真。比如收摊前的最后一张饼，跟第一张要一样用心。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ mā yǐqián yě bǎi tān, jiù zài zhè tóngyī ge wèizhì. Tā jiāo wǒ shuō, zuò chī de zhè yī háng, zhòngyào de bú shì shǒuyì duō gāochāo, ér shì nǐ yuàn bú yuànyì zài biérén kàn bú dào de shíhòu yě rènzhēn. Bǐrú shōu tān qián de zuìhòu yī zhāng bǐng, gēn dì yī zhāng yào yīyàng yòngxīn.
turns.text_en: My mom used to run a stall too, in this exact same spot. She taught me that in the food business, what matters isn't how skilled you are, but whether you're willing to be conscientious even when no one's watching. Like the last pancake before closing — it should have the same care as the first one.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the philosophy her mother taught her about craft.

options.text_zh: 这是很朴素但很深的职业伦理。不是为了被看见而认真，而是认真本身就是对食物和对自己的尊重。你妈妈的说法比「匠心」这种词更实在——就是一种日常的选择。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè shì hěn pǔsù dàn hěn shēn de zhíyè lúnlǐ. Bú shì wèile bèi kànjiàn ér rènzhēn, ér shì rènzhēn běnshēn jiù shì duì shíwù hé duì zìjǐ de zūnzhòng. Nǐ māma de shuōfǎ bǐ 'jiàngxīn' zhè zhǒng cí gèng shízài — jiù shì yī zhǒng rìcháng de xuǎnzé.
options.text_en: A plain but deep professional ethic. Not being careful to be seen, but careful as respect — for the food and yourself. Your mother's way of putting it is more grounded than 'artisan spirit' — a daily choice.
options.feedback: She stops wiping and goes still for a second. "Daily choice — she would have liked that," she says softly, half to herself.

options.text_zh: 你妈妈一直在同一个位置摆摊？那这个位置是固定租给你家的吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ māma yīzhí zài tóngyī ge wèizhì bǎi tān? Nà zhège wèizhì shì gùdìng zū gěi nǐ jiā de ma?
options.text_en: Your mom always ran this same spot? Is this location rented permanently to your family?
options.feedback: She looks a bit caught off guard. "Something like that," she says vaguely, and moves on.

options.text_zh: 我觉得现在的葱油饼不如以前好吃了，可能是油的质量下降了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde xiànzài de cōngyóubǐng bù rú yǐqián hǎochī le, kěnéng shì yóu de zhìliàng xiàjiàng le.
options.text_en: I think scallion pancakes aren't as good as they used to be, maybe the oil quality has dropped.
options.feedback: She glances at you, then at the griddle, then goes quiet. The mood shifts slightly.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你说到点子上了。现在很多人把「匠心」当成营销用语，一说匠心就涨价。但对我妈来说，那就是做人的底线。她不会跟你解释为什么要这样做——她觉得不需要解释。后来我接手以后才慢慢明白，她那种不解释的态度本身就是一种教育。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ shuō dào diǎnzi shàng le. Xiànzài hěn duō rén bǎ 'jiàngxīn' dāng chéng yíngxiāo yòngyǔ, yī shuō jiàngxīn jiù zhǎngjià. Dàn duì wǒ mā lái shuō, nà jiù shì zuòrén de dǐxiàn. Tā bú huì gēn nǐ jiěshì wèishéme yào zhèyàng zuò — tā juéde bù xūyào jiěshì. Hòulái wǒ jiēshǒu yǐhòu cái mànmàn míngbái, tā nà zhǒng bù jiěshì de tàidù běnshēn jiù shì yī zhǒng jiàoyù.
turns.text_en: You hit the nail on the head. Nowadays people use 'artisan spirit' as a marketing term — mention artisan spirit and prices go up. But for my mom, it was just the baseline of being a decent person. She wouldn't explain to you why she did things that way — she didn't think it needed explaining. It was only after I took over that I gradually understood: her refusal to explain was itself a form of education.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to this idea of teaching through doing rather than explaining.

options.text_zh: 「身教重于言教」。有时候把道理说出来反而变成教条。你妈妈用行动教你的东西，因为没被明确说出来，反而留给你空间自己去领悟。那种留白本身就是信任。
options.text_pinyin: 'Shēnjiào zhòng yú yánjiào'. Yǒu shíhòu bǎ dàolǐ shuō chūlái fǎn'ér biàn chéng jiàotiáo. Nǐ māma yòng xíngdòng jiāo nǐ de dōngxī, yīnwèi méi bèi míngquè shuō chūlái, fǎn'ér liú gěi nǐ kōngjiān zìjǐ qù lǐngwù. Nà zhǒng liúbái běnshēn jiù shì xìnrèn.
options.text_en: 'Teaching by example outweighs words.' When you state a principle, it can harden into dogma. What your mother taught through action left you room to understand it yourself. That blank space is trust.
options.feedback: She's quiet for a moment, her hands resting on the counter. "Trust," she repeats, nodding once. "That's a good way to put it."

options.text_zh: 那你为什么不去开一家正式的餐厅呢？比摆摊赚得多。
options.text_pinyin: Nà nǐ wèishéme bú qù kāi yī jiā zhèngshì de cāntīng ne? Bǐ bǎi tān zhuàn de duō.
options.text_en: Then why don't you open a proper restaurant? You'd make more money than running a stall.
options.feedback: She tilts her head. "My mom didn't care about making more money," she says simply. "Neither do I, really."

options.text_zh: 你的饼一直都是这个价格吗？最近没有涨价？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de bǐng yīzhí dōu shì zhège jiàgé ma? Zuìjìn méiyǒu zhǎngjià?
options.text_en: Has the pancake always been this price? No price increase recently?
options.feedback: She tells you the current price with a polite smile, and a small silence settles between you.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 信任——这个词用得好。她信任我会自己想明白。有时候我收摊的时候一个人站在这里，闻着那股油烟味，会突然很想她。不是因为难过，而是因为觉得她还在这里。这个摊位就像是我们之间的一根线。
turns.text_pinyin: Xìnrèn — zhège cí yòng de hǎo. Tā xìnrèn wǒ huì zìjǐ xiǎng míngbái. Yǒu shíhòu wǒ shōu tān de shíhòu yīge rén zhàn zài zhèlǐ, wén zhe nà gǔ yóuyān wèi, huì tūrán hěn xiǎng tā. Bú shì yīnwèi nánguò, ér shì yīnwèi juéde tā hái zài zhèlǐ. Zhège tānwèi jiù xiàng shì wǒmen zhī jiān de yī gēn xiàn.
turns.text_en: Trust — that's a good word for it. She trusted me to figure it out myself. Sometimes when I'm closing up, standing here alone, smelling the cooking oil, I suddenly miss her very much. Not because I'm sad, but because I feel she's still here. This stall is like a thread between us.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to how the stall keeps her connected to her mother.

options.text_zh: 也许这就是为什么你不愿意换地方或者改行。每一次揉面、每一次煎饼，都是在重复她教给你的动作。那种重复不是机械的——是一种仪式，每天用身体记住一个人。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì wèishéme nǐ bú yuànyì huàn dìfang huòzhě gǎiháng. Měi yī cì róu miàn, měi yī cì jiān bǐng, dōu shì zài chóngfù tā jiāo gěi nǐ de dòngzuò. Nà zhǒng chóngfù bú shì jīxiè de — shì yī zhǒng yíshì, měi tiān yòng shēntǐ jì zhù yīge rén.
options.text_en: Maybe that's why you don't want to move or change careers. Every time you knead dough, fry a pancake, you're repeating motions she taught you. That repetition isn't mechanical — it's ritual. Remembering someone with your body, every day.
options.feedback: She looks down at her hands, and her expression goes somewhere private for a moment. "Remembering with your body," she says quietly. "Yes."

options.text_zh: 你有考虑过注册商标吗？现在品牌很重要。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu kǎolǜ guò zhùcè shāngbiāo ma? Xiànzài pǐnpái hěn zhòngyào.
options.text_en: Have you considered registering a trademark? Branding is really important these days.
options.feedback: She blinks, and the warmth in her face retreats slightly. "I'll think about it," she says politely.

options.text_zh: 你的摊位是租的吗？这里的摊位费贵不贵？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de tānwèi shì zū de ma? Zhèlǐ de tānwèi fèi guì bú guì?
options.text_en: Is your stall space rented? Are the fees here expensive?
options.feedback: She gives you a brief answer on the fees, then turns to straighten something on the counter.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 仪式……对，就是这个意思。好了，这是你的饼，最后一张。你慢慢吃。我去收拾了。谢谢你今晚陪我聊了这么久，这条街上难得有人愿意在这个时间停下来。
turns.text_pinyin: Yíshì…… duì, jiù shì zhège yìsi. Hǎo le, zhè shì nǐ de bǐng, zuìhòu yī zhāng. Nǐ mànmàn chī. Wǒ qù shōushí le. Xièxiè nǐ jīn wǎn péi wǒ liáo le zhème jiǔ, zhè tiáo jiē shàng nándé yǒu rén yuànyì zài zhège shíjiān tíng xiàlái.
turns.text_en: Ritual… yes, that's exactly what I mean. Alright, here's your pancake — the last one. Take your time eating. I'll go clean up. Thank you for chatting with me this long tonight. It's rare for someone to stop at this hour on this street.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Say goodbye to Sister Lin at the end of the night.

options.text_zh: 谢谢你的饼，也谢谢今晚说的这些话。你妈妈如果看到你现在的样子，应该不需要多说什么了——你已经都懂了。明天见，林姐。
options.text_pinyin: Xièxiè nǐ de bǐng, yě xièxiè jīn wǎn shuō de zhèxiē huà. Nǐ māma rúguǒ kàn dào nǐ xiànzài de yàngzi, yīnggāi bù xūyào duō shuō shénme le — nǐ yǐjīng dōu dǒng le. Míngtiān jiàn, Lín jiě.
options.text_en: Thank you for the pancake, and for what you shared tonight. If your mother saw you now, she probably wouldn't need to say much — you already understand. See you tomorrow, Sister Lin.
options.feedback: She holds your gaze for a moment, then looks away and smiles to herself. "Goodnight," she says softly. She doesn't say anything else.

options.text_zh: 能不能给我一张名片？我下次带朋友来。
options.text_pinyin: Néng bù néng gěi wǒ yī zhāng míngpiàn? Wǒ xià cì dài péngyǒu lái.
options.text_en: Can you give me a business card? I'll bring friends next time.
options.feedback: "I don't have cards," she says pleasantly. "But we're here every night."

options.text_zh: 你这里可以用手机支付吗？我没带现金。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhèlǐ kěyǐ yòng shǒujī zhīfù ma? Wǒ méi dài xiànjīn.
options.text_en: Can I pay by phone here? I didn't bring cash.
options.feedback: She points to the QR code on the counter without missing a beat. The practical question breaks the spell of the evening.

tree.cultural_note: Taiwan's night markets are not just commercial spaces — they are intergenerational institutions. Many stalls occupy the same spot for decades, with recipes and locations passed down within families. The quiet hour after closing is a private time that vendors rarely share with outsiders. The phrase 身教重于言教 (teaching by example outweighs teaching by words) reflects a deep Confucian value about how wisdom is transmitted through lived practice rather than instruction.

title: The Library at the Edge of the District

tree.setup: You've discovered a small community library tucked into a ground-floor apartment in an aging residential compound. The volunteer librarian, Uncle Xu, a retired middle school teacher, has turned his own living room into a lending library for migrant workers' children in the neighborhood. It's late afternoon, and the last child has just left.
tree.setup_zh: 你在一个老旧居民小区的底层住宅里发现了一间小型社区图书馆。志愿图书管理员徐叔叔——一位退休的初中教师——把自己的客厅改造成了一个借阅图书馆，专门为附近外来务工人员的孩子服务。现在是傍晚，最后一个孩子刚刚离开。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你来得正好，刚安静下来。下午来了六个孩子，最小的才上二年级。他们的父母都在附近的工地或者工厂上班，晚上才回来。这里就是他们放学后的落脚点。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ lái de zhènghǎo, gāng ānjìng xiàlái. Xiàwǔ lái le liù ge háizi, zuì xiǎo de cái shàng èr niánjí. Tāmen de fùmǔ dōu zài fùjìn de gōngdì huòzhě gōngchǎng shàngbān, wǎnshàng cái huílái. Zhèlǐ jiù shì tāmen fàngxué hòu de luòjiǎodiǎn.
turns.text_en: You came at just the right time — it just quieted down. Six children came this afternoon; the youngest is only in second grade. Their parents all work at nearby construction sites or factories and don't come home until evening. This is their landing place after school.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to what Uncle Xu said about the role this place plays for the children.

options.text_zh: 所以这里不只是图书馆，更像是一个临时的家。对这些孩子来说，有个安全的地方待着、有人在旁边，可能比书本身更重要。
options.text_pinyin: Suǒyǐ zhèlǐ bù zhǐ shì túshūguǎn, gèng xiàng shì yīge línshí de jiā. Duì zhèxiē háizi lái shuō, yǒu ge ānquán de dìfang dāi zhe, yǒu rén zài pángbiān, kěnéng bǐ shū běnshēn gèng zhòngyào.
options.text_en: So this isn't just a library — it's more like a temporary home. For these children, having a safe place to be after school with someone present might matter more than the books themselves.
options.feedback: He nods slowly, hands on his knees. "That took me a while to figure out," he says. "Now I think you're right."

options.text_zh: 这些孩子能在公立学校上学吗？户口有没有问题？
options.text_pinyin: Zhèxiē háizi néng zài gōnglì xuéxiào shàngxué ma? Hùkǒu yǒu méiyǒu wèntí?
options.text_en: Can these children attend public schools? Do they have hukou issues?
options.feedback: He sighs. "Most of them manage to get in, but it's never simple," he says, and steers the conversation back to the library.

options.text_zh: 你这里的书多吗？有没有英语教材？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhèlǐ de shū duō ma? Yǒu méiyǒu Yīngyǔ jiàocái?
options.text_en: Do you have many books here? Any English textbooks?
options.feedback: He waves a hand at the shelves. "Some of everything. But the books aren't really the point," he says, watching to see if you understand.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 嗯……一开始我以为关键是让他们多读书，后来才发现，很多孩子来了根本不看书，就坐在角落里画画或者发呆。我一开始还着急，后来想通了——他们需要的不是被要求做什么，而是被允许什么都不做。
turns.text_pinyin: Ǹg... Yī kāishǐ wǒ yǐwéi guānjiàn shì ràng tāmen duō dú shū, hòulái cái fāxiàn, hěn duō háizi lái le gēnběn bú kàn shū, jiù zuò zài jiǎoluò lǐ huàhuà huòzhě fādāi. Wǒ yī kāishǐ hái zhāojí, hòulái xiǎng tōng le — tāmen xūyào de bú shì bèi yāoqiú zuò shénme, ér shì bèi yǔnxǔ shénme dōu bú zuò.
turns.text_en: Mm... At first I thought the key was getting them to read more, but later I realized many children come and don't read at all — they just sit in a corner drawing or staring into space. I used to worry about that, but eventually I understood: what they need isn't to be told to do something, but to be allowed to do nothing.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his insight about what the children actually need.

options.text_zh: 在学校要学习，在家可能要帮忙，到处都是任务和期待。一个不要求任何产出的空间反而成了最稀缺的东西。您提供的不是教育——是一种庇护。
options.text_pinyin: Zài xuéxiào yào xuéxí, zài jiā kěnéng yào bāngmáng, dàochù dōu shì rènwù hé qīdài. Yīge bù yāoqiú rènhé chǎnchū de kōngjiān fǎn'ér chéng le zuì xīquē de dōngxī. Nín tígōng de bú shì jiàoyù — shì yī zhǒng bìhù.
options.text_en: At school they have to study, at home they might have to help out — everywhere there are tasks and expectations. A space that demands nothing actually becomes the scarcest thing. What you provide isn't education — it's shelter.
options.feedback: He sits up a little straighter. "Shelter," he says quietly. "That's the word. I've been looking for that word."

options.text_zh: 但是不看书的话，来图书馆有什么意义呢？
options.text_pinyin: Dànshì bú kàn shū dehuà, lái túshūguǎn yǒu shénme yìyì ne?
options.text_en: But if they're not reading, what's the point of coming to a library?
options.feedback: He gives you a patient look. "I used to think that too," he says. "Then I watched them for a while."

options.text_zh: 我小时候也经常发呆，后来就好了。孩子嘛，长大就好了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ xiǎo shíhòu yě jīngcháng fādāi, hòulái jiù hǎo le. Háizi ma, zhǎng dà jiù hǎo le.
options.text_en: I used to space out a lot as a kid too, it got better later. Kids, they'll be fine when they grow up.
options.feedback: He nods but his expression tightens just slightly. "These kids have a little more going on than that," he says carefully.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 庇护这个词用得准。有一个小女孩，叫小雨，她爸爸在建筑工地受了伤，妈妈在超市打两份工。她每天来，不说话，就趴在桌子上写日记。有一天她给我看了一页——上面写着「今天图书馆很暖和」。就这一句话。
turns.text_pinyin: Bìhù zhège cí yòng de zhǔn. Yǒu yīge xiǎo nǚhái, jiào Xiǎoyǔ, tā bàba zài jiànzhù gōngdì shòu le shāng, māma zài chāoshì dǎ liǎng fèn gōng. Tā měi tiān lái, bù shuōhuà, jiù pā zài zhuōzi shàng xiě rìjì. Yǒu yī tiān tā gěi wǒ kàn le yī yè — shàngmiàn xiě zhe 'jīntiān túshūguǎn hěn nuǎnhuo'. Jiù zhè yī jù huà.
turns.text_en: Shelter — that word is accurate. There's a little girl called Xiaoyu. Her father was injured on a construction site, and her mother works two shifts at a supermarket. She comes every day, doesn't talk, just leans on the table writing in her diary. One day she showed me a page — it said 'The library was warm today.' Just that one sentence.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to what Xiaoyu's diary entry reveals.

options.text_zh: 那一句话比一整篇作文包含的东西都多。「暖和」可能不只是温度——是有人在，灯亮着，没人催她走。对每天要承受那么多的孩子来说，一个可以预期的安全的地方，本身就是最大的温暖。
options.text_pinyin: Nà yī jù huà bǐ yī zhěng piān zuòwén bāohán de dōngxī dōu duō. 'Nuǎnhuo' kěnéng bù zhǐ shì wēndù — shì yǒu rén zài, dēng liàng zhe, méi rén cuī tā zǒu. Duì měi tiān yào chéngshòu nàme duō de háizi lái shuō, yīge kě yùqī de ānquán de dìfang, běnshēn jiù shì zuìdà de wēnnuǎn.
options.text_en: That sentence holds more than an essay could. 'Warm' isn't temperature — it's someone being here, lights on, no one rushing her out. For a child carrying that much, a predictable safe place is the greatest warmth.
options.feedback: He's quiet for a moment. Then he gets up and straightens one of the chairs at the table — the one Xiaoyu probably sits at.

options.text_zh: 你应该把她的日记给她妈妈看，让她妈妈知道她的感受。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi bǎ tā de rìjì gěi tā māma kàn, ràng tā māma zhīdào tā de gǎnshòu.
options.text_en: You should show her diary to her mother, so her mother knows how she feels.
options.feedback: He shakes his head firmly. "That's her diary. She showed me because she trusted me. I wouldn't do that."

options.text_zh: 这个年纪的孩子应该多运动，不应该整天趴在桌子上。
options.text_pinyin: Zhège niánjì de háizi yīnggāi duō yùndòng, bù yīnggāi zhěng tiān pā zài zhuōzi shàng.
options.text_en: Children this age should exercise more, they shouldn't spend all day leaning on a desk.
options.feedback: He looks at you for a moment. "She's not here because she has too much energy," he says quietly.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你理解得很透。但说实话，我也有撑不下去的时候。这套房子是我自己的，不用交租，但书要买、暖气费要交、有时候还要给孩子们准备点零食。老伴觉得我在花冤枉钱，邻居觉得我在多管闲事。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ lǐjiě de hěn tòu. Dàn shuō shíhuà, wǒ yě yǒu chēng bú xiàqù de shíhòu. Zhè tào fángzi shì wǒ zìjǐ de, bú yòng jiāo zū, dàn shū yào mǎi, nuǎnqì fèi yào jiāo, yǒu shíhòu hái yào gěi háizi men zhǔnbèi diǎn língshí. Lǎobàn juéde wǒ zài huā yuānwǎng qián, línjū juéde wǒ zài duō guǎn xiánshì.
turns.text_en: You understand deeply. But honestly, there are times I can barely keep going. This apartment is mine so I don't pay rent, but I need to buy books, pay heating bills, and sometimes prepare snacks for the children. My wife thinks I'm wasting money, and the neighbors think I'm meddling in other people's business.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the isolation and criticism he faces for doing this work.

options.text_zh: 「多管闲事」这个标签特别伤人，因为它把善意定义成了越界。但您做的事情恰恰是因为「不是您的事」才更有意义——您选择去在乎跟自己没有关系的孩子。
options.text_pinyin: 'Duō guǎn xiánshì' zhège biāoqiān tèbié shāng rén, yīnwèi tā bǎ shànyì dìngyì chéng le yuèjiè. Dàn nín zuò de shìqíng qiàqià shì yīnwèi 'bú shì nín de shì' cái gèng yǒu yìyì — nín xuǎnzé qù zàihū gēn zìjǐ méiyǒu guānxì de háizi.
options.text_en: The label 'meddling in others' business' is hurtful because it redefines goodwill as overstepping. But what you're doing matters precisely because 'it's not your business' — you chose to care about children who have nothing to do with you.
options.feedback: He exhales slowly, and some tension goes out of his shoulders. "That's it," he says, almost to himself. "That's the thing I couldn't explain to my wife."

options.text_zh: 那你可以申请政府补贴啊，应该有这方面的政策。
options.text_pinyin: Nà nǐ kěyǐ shēnqǐng zhèngfǔ bǔtiē a, yīnggāi yǒu zhè fāngmiàn de zhèngcè.
options.text_en: You could apply for government subsidies, there should be policies for that.
options.feedback: He nods wearily. "I've looked into it. It's a lot of paperwork for something uncertain." He sounds like someone who's already tried.

options.text_zh: 你老伴说得也有道理，你也要照顾好自己。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ lǎobàn shuō de yě yǒu dàolǐ, nǐ yě yào zhàogù hǎo zìjǐ.
options.text_en: Your wife has a point too, you need to take care of yourself.
options.feedback: He gives you a small, tired smile. "She tells me the same thing," he says, and it's clear that's not what he needed to hear.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 谢谢你这么说。你知道吗，有时候支撑我继续做下去的不是什么大道理，就是一些很小的瞬间。比如小雨那句「图书馆很暖和」。比如有个男孩走的时候偷偷把书摆整齐了，以为我没看到。这些瞬间让我觉得，这件事虽然小，但不是没有意义的。
turns.text_pinyin: Xièxiè nǐ zhème shuō. Nǐ zhīdào ma, yǒu shíhòu zhīchēng wǒ jìxù zuò xiàqù de bú shì shénme dà dàolǐ, jiù shì yīxiē hěn xiǎo de shùnjiān. Bǐrú Xiǎoyǔ nà jù 'túshūguǎn hěn nuǎnhuo'. Bǐrú yǒu ge nánhái zǒu de shíhòu tōutōu bǎ shū bǎi zhěngqí le, yǐwéi wǒ méi kàn dào. Zhèxiē shùnjiān ràng wǒ juéde, zhè jiàn shì suīrán xiǎo, dàn bú shì méiyǒu yìyì de.
turns.text_en: Thank you for saying that. You know, what keeps me going sometimes isn't any grand principle — it's small moments. Like Xiaoyu's sentence about the library being warm. Like a boy who quietly straightened the books before leaving, thinking I didn't see. These moments make me feel that even though this work is small, it's not meaningless.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Close the conversation by saying something about the boy straightening the books.

options.text_zh: 那个男孩偷偷摆书，说明您的图书馆已经教会了他一些东西——不是从书里学的，是从您的态度里学的。他看见一个人安静地照顾一个空间，然后他也开始照顾了。这种影响看不见，但是真实的。徐叔叔，这件事不小。
options.text_pinyin: Nàge nánhái tōutōu bǎi shū, shuōmíng nín de túshūguǎn yǐjīng jiāohuì le tā yīxiē dōngxī — bú shì cóng shū lǐ xué de, shì cóng nín de tàidù lǐ xué de. Tā kàn jiàn yīge rén ānjìng de zhàogù yīge kōngjiān, ránhòu tā yě kāishǐ zhàogù le. Zhè zhǒng yǐngxiǎng kàn bú jiàn, dàn shì zhēnshí de. Xú shūshu, zhè jiàn shì bù xiǎo.
options.text_en: The boy straightening books proves your library taught him something — not from a book, but your attitude. He saw someone quietly caring for a space, and started caring too.
options.feedback: He sits very still for a moment. Then he nods, twice, slowly — like he's deciding to believe it.

options.text_zh: 你可以在网上众筹，现在这种故事很容易传播。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ zài wǎngshàng zhòngchóu, xiànzài zhè zhǒng gùshì hěn róngyì chuánbō.
options.text_en: You could crowdfund online, these kinds of stories spread easily nowadays.
options.feedback: He shakes his head. "I don't want it to become a story," he says simply. "I just want to keep doing it."

options.text_zh: 你这里有空调吗？夏天孩子们不会热吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhèlǐ yǒu kōngtiáo ma? Xiàtiān háizi men bú huì rè ma?
options.text_en: Do you have air conditioning here? Don't the kids get hot in summer?
options.feedback: "There's a fan," he says, and gestures to the corner. The moment breaks quietly.

tree.cultural_note: China's migrant worker population — estimated at nearly 300 million — often leaves children in a gap between rural hometown schools and urban access. Children who move with their parents to cities frequently face barriers to enrollment in local public schools due to the hukou (household registration) system. Grassroots community libraries and after-school spaces fill a critical gap, though they operate largely without institutional support, sustained by individual dedication.

tree.setup: You're sitting in a cluttered office above a locksmith shop in an old Beijing neighborhood. The owner, Old Qian, is known locally as someone who finds lost things — not a private detective exactly, but a retired postal worker who uses his encyclopedic knowledge of the neighborhood's rhythms to reunite people with lost possessions. He's studying a faded photograph someone dropped off this morning.
tree.setup_zh: 你坐在一个老北京社区锁匠店楼上的杂乱办公室里。店主老钱在附近很有名——他专门帮人找失物。他不算是私家侦探，而是一个退休邮递员，凭借他对社区节奏的百科全书般的了解，帮人们找回丢失的东西。他正在研究今天早上有人送来的一张褪色照片。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看这张照片，背面写着「1987年春，什刹海」。今天早上一个老太太拿来的，说是在搬家的时候弄丢了一整盒老照片，这是唯一找回来的一张。她不是要我找照片——照片已经没了——她想找照片里另外那个人。三十多年没联系了。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn zhè zhāng zhàopiàn, bèimiàn xiě zhe '1987 nián chūn, Shíchàhǎi'. Jīntiān zǎoshang yīge lǎo tàitai ná lái de, shuō shì zài bānjiā de shíhòu nòng diū le yī zhěng hé lǎo zhàopiàn, zhè shì wéiyī zhǎo huílái de yī zhāng. Tā bú shì yào wǒ zhǎo zhàopiàn — zhàopiàn yǐjīng méi le — tā xiǎng zhǎo zhàopiàn lǐ lìngwài nàge rén. Sānshí duō nián méi liánxì le.
turns.text_en: Look at this photo — on the back it says 'Spring 1987, Shichahai.' An elderly woman brought it in this morning. She said she lost a whole box of old photos while moving, and this is the only one she recovered. She doesn't want me to find the photos — they're gone — she wants to find the other person in the photo. They haven't been in contact for over thirty years.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Ask about how he goes about finding someone from thirty years ago.

options.text_zh: 三十多年……这不是一般的寻人启事能解决的。您平时怎么找人？不像是靠数据库，更像是靠一种对这片地方的直觉？
options.text_pinyin: Sānshí duō nián…… zhè bú shì yībān de xúnrén qǐshì néng jiějué de. Nín píngshí zěnme zhǎo rén? Bú xiàng shì kào shùjùkù, gèng xiàng shì kào yī zhǒng duì zhè piàn dìfang de zhíjué?
options.text_en: Over thirty years… a missing-person notice won't solve this. How do you usually find people? It doesn't seem like you rely on databases — more like some kind of intuition about this area?
options.feedback: He looks up from the photo with a small pleased expression. "Intuition — that's one word for it," he says. "Let me tell you what it actually is."

options.text_zh: 现在用互联网搜一下不就行了？何必这么麻烦。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài yòng hùliánwǎng sōu yīxià bú jiù xíng le? Hébì zhème máfan.
options.text_en: Just search on the internet, why make it so complicated?
options.feedback: He holds up the photo. "This woman is probably in her seventies. She's not online," he says, not unkindly.

options.text_zh: 三十年没联系，对方可能已经搬走了或者去世了吧？
options.text_pinyin: Sānshí nián méi liánxì, duìfāng kěnéng yǐjīng bān zǒu le huòzhě qùshì le ba?
options.text_en: After thirty years without contact, the other person might have moved away or passed away, right?
options.feedback: "Possibly," he says, and sets the photo down. "But the old lady wants to know for certain. That's enough reason for me to look."

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 直觉？也算吧。我当了三十年邮递员，每天骑自行车在同一片区域转。谁搬来了、谁搬走了、哪家生了孩子、哪家老人过世了——这些我都知道。人们觉得邮递员只是送信的，但其实我们是城市的毛细血管，最细微的变化都经过我们的手。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhíjué? Yě suàn ba. Wǒ dāng le sānshí nián yóudìyuán, měi tiān qí zìxíngchē zài tóngyī piàn qūyù zhuǎn. Shéi bān lái le, shéi bān zǒu le, nǎ jiā shēng le háizi, nǎ jiā lǎorén guòshì le — zhèxiē wǒ dōu zhīdào. Rénmen juéde yóudìyuán zhǐ shì sòng xìn de, dàn qíshí wǒmen shì chéngshì de máoxì xuèguǎn, zuì xìwēi de biànhuà dōu jīngguò wǒmen de shǒu.
turns.text_en: Intuition? You could call it that. I was a postal worker for thirty years, riding my bicycle through the same area every day. Who moved in, who moved away, which family had a baby, which family's elder passed away — I knew it all. People think postal workers just deliver mail, but really we're the city's capillaries — the most subtle changes pass through our hands.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his metaphor of postal workers as the city's capillaries.

options.text_zh: 毛细血管这个比喻太好了。动脉是大路，地铁线，而你们走的是地图上不标注的小路。正因为路线细到无人关注，反而观察到了整个城市最真实的脉动。
options.text_pinyin: Máoxì xuèguǎn zhège bǐyù tài hǎo le. Dòngmài shì dà lù, dìtiě xiàn, ér nǐmen zǒu de shì dìtú shàng bù biāozhù de xiǎo lù. Zhèng yīnwèi lùxiàn xì dào wú rén guānzhù, fǎn'ér guānchá dào le zhěnggè chéngshì zuì zhēnshí de màidòng.
options.text_en: The capillary metaphor is perfect. Arteries are main roads and subway lines, but you walked lanes that don't appear on maps. Because your routes were too fine to notice, you saw the city's most authentic pulse.
options.feedback: He taps the table once, satisfied. "That's right. Nobody ever watched us, so we watched everything."

options.text_zh: 现在还有人寄信吗？我觉得邮局快被淘汰了吧。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài hái yǒu rén jì xìn ma? Wǒ juéde yóujú kuài bèi táotài le ba.
options.text_en: Does anyone still send letters? I think the post office is becoming obsolete.
options.feedback: He gives you a slightly deflated look. "The post office still exists," he says. "But yes, it's changed a lot."

options.text_zh: 三十年同一条路线，不觉得无聊吗？
options.text_pinyin: Sānshí nián tóngyī tiáo lùxiàn, bù juéde wúliáo ma?
options.text_en: Thirty years on the same route — didn't that get boring?
options.feedback: He shakes his head. "The route was the same. The street was different every single day," he says.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 活的记忆——对，就是这个感觉。比如这张照片，1987年什刹海。我记得那几年什刹海边上有一家裁缝铺，老板姓周。如果照片里的女人当年住在附近，周师傅的老婆可能认识她——那时候邻里之间谁不认识谁呢？所以我现在要做的第一步是找到周师傅的后人。
turns.text_pinyin: Huó de jìyì — duì, jiù shì zhège gǎnjué. Bǐrú zhè zhāng zhàopiàn, 1987 nián Shíchàhǎi. Wǒ jìde nà jǐ nián Shíchàhǎi biān shàng yǒu yī jiā cáiféng pù, lǎobǎn xìng Zhōu. Rúguǒ zhàopiàn lǐ de nǚrén dāngnián zhù zài fùjìn, Zhōu shīfu de lǎopó kěnéng rènshì tā — nà shíhòu línlǐ zhī jiān shéi bú rènshì shéi ne? Suǒyǐ wǒ xiànzài yào zuò de dì yī bù shì zhǎo dào Zhōu shīfu de hòurén.
turns.text_en: Living memory — yes, that's exactly how it feels. Take this photo: 1987, Shichahai. I remember that in those years there was a tailor shop by Shichahai — the owner's surname was Zhou. If the woman in the photo lived nearby back then, Master Zhou's wife might have known her — in those days, who among the neighbors didn't know everyone? So my first step is to find Master Zhou's descendants.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the way he reasons through chains of people rather than chains of evidence.

options.text_zh: 你的推理跟警察完全不同。他们靠证据链，你靠的是关系链——谁认识谁，谁住在谁旁边。这种方法的前提是社区足够紧密，每个人都是别人记忆的一部分。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de tuīlǐ gēn jǐngchá wánquán bùtóng. Tāmen kào zhèngjù liàn, nǐ kào de shì guānxì liàn — shéi rènshì shéi, shéi zhù zài shéi pángbiān. Zhè zhǒng fāngfǎ de qiántí shì shèqū zúgòu jǐnmì, měige rén dōu shì biérén jìyì de yī bùfen.
options.text_en: Your reasoning differs from police work. They follow evidence chains; you follow relationship chains — who knew whom, who lived next to whom. It only works if the community is tight enough that everyone is part of someone's memory.
options.feedback: He points at you. "Exactly. And that's also why it's getting harder."

options.text_zh: 你应该开个抖音号，记录你的破案过程，肯定能火。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yīnggāi kāi ge Dǒuyīn hào, jìlù nǐ de pò'àn guòchéng, kěndìng néng huǒ.
options.text_en: You should start a Douyin account and record your cases, you'd definitely go viral.
options.feedback: He looks at you like you've said something from another world. "I don't want to go viral," he says flatly. "I want to find the woman."

options.text_zh: 找人的话，直接发朋友圈不是更快吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhǎo rén dehuà, zhíjiē fā péngyǒu quān bú shì gèng kuài ma?
options.text_en: If you're looking for someone, wouldn't posting on WeChat Moments be faster?
options.feedback: "The person I'm looking for is in her seventies. She's not on WeChat Moments," he says patiently.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你说到点子上了。我最近越来越难找了——不是因为我老了，是因为社区散了。以前一个裁缝铺可以连接半条街的人，现在大家住在不同的小区里，互相不认识。我的方法需要的原材料正在消失。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ shuō dào diǎnzi shàng le. Wǒ zuìjìn yuè lái yuè nán zhǎo le — bú shì yīnwèi wǒ lǎo le, shì yīnwèi shèqū sàn le. Yǐqián yīge cáiféng pù kěyǐ liánjiē bàn tiáo jiē de rén, xiànzài dàjiā zhù zài bùtóng de xiǎoqū lǐ, hùxiāng bú rènshì. Wǒ de fāngfǎ xūyào de yuáncáiliào zhèngzài xiāoshī.
turns.text_en: You've hit the nail on the head. It's been getting harder and harder for me lately — not because I'm getting old, but because the community has scattered. A tailor shop used to connect half a street's worth of people; now everyone lives in different residential compounds, strangers to each other. The raw material my method needs is disappearing.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the idea that the social fabric his method depends on is dissolving.

options.text_zh: 有点像一种悖论——您做的事情证明了社区联系的价值，但您能做这件事的前提，也就是社区本身，正在瓦解。您越成功，越让人意识到那种东西正在消失。
options.text_pinyin: Yǒudiǎn xiàng yī zhǒng bèilùn — nín zuò de shìqíng zhèngmíng le shèqū liánxì de jiàzhí, dàn nín néng zuò zhè jiàn shì de qiántí, yě jiù shì shèqū běnshēn, zhèngzài wǎjiě. Nín yuè chénggōng, yuè ràng rén yìshí dào nà zhǒng dōngxī zhèngzài xiāoshī.
options.text_en: It's almost a paradox — your work proves the value of community connections, but the premise for doing it — the community itself — is dissolving. The better you are at this, the more it highlights what's being lost.
options.feedback: He's quiet for a moment. He sets the photograph down and looks at it. "I hadn't thought about it that way," he says slowly. "But yes."

options.text_zh: 那你退休以后不是应该好好休息吗？何必给自己找事做。
options.text_pinyin: Nà nǐ tuìxiū yǐhòu bú shì yīnggāi hǎohǎo xiūxi ma? Hébì gěi zìjǐ zhǎo shì zuò.
options.text_en: Shouldn't you be resting properly after retirement? Why look for things to do?
options.feedback: He looks up at you. "Resting from what?" he says simply.

options.text_zh: 我觉得城市化是大趋势，没办法阻挡的。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde chéngshìhuà shì dà qūshì, méi bànfǎ zǔdǎng de.
options.text_en: I think urbanization is the big trend, there's no stopping it.
options.feedback: He nods without expression. "I know," he says. "I'm not trying to stop it. I'm just trying to find this one woman."

tree.cultural_note: China's rapid urbanization has fragmented the dense social networks that once characterized traditional neighborhoods. Postal workers in the pre-digital era served as informal social infrastructure — knowing residents by name, tracking moves and life events through address changes. The 'lost object detective' figure represents a form of community knowledge that has no digital equivalent: an embodied understanding of how lives intersect in physical space, built over decades of daily presence.

tree.setup: You meet a young sound artist, Xiao He, in a quiet park near a Beijing hutong. She's holding a portable recorder and headphones, capturing ambient sounds. She explains she's building an audio archive of vanishing neighborhood soundscapes — the creak of old wooden doors, the calls of street vendors, the rhythm of mahjong tiles.
tree.setup_zh: 你在北京胡同旁边一个安静的公园里遇到了一位年轻的声音艺术家小何。她手持便携录音机和耳机，正在采集环境声音。她解释说她在建立一个正在消失的社区声景音频档案——老木门的吱呀声、街头小贩的叫卖声、麻将牌碰撞的节奏。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你听——那个声音，是三轮车在石板路上颠簸的声音。再过几年，这条石板路大概率会被铺成柏油的。到时候这个声音就只存在于我的录音里了。很多人觉得我在做一件无聊的事，但我觉得声音比照片更能还原一个地方的灵魂。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ tīng — nàge shēngyīn, shì sānlúnchē zài shíbǎn lù shàng diānbǒ de shēngyīn. Zài guò jǐ nián, zhè tiáo shíbǎn lù dà gàilǜ huì bèi pū chéng bǎiyóu de. Dào shíhòu zhège shēngyīn jiù zhǐ cúnzài yú wǒ de lùyīn lǐ le. Hěn duō rén juéde wǒ zài zuò yī jiàn wúliáo de shì, dàn wǒ juéde shēngyīn bǐ zhàopiàn gèng néng huányuán yīge dìfang de línghún.
turns.text_en: Listen — that sound, it's a tricycle bumping along a flagstone road. In a few more years, this stone road will probably be paved over with asphalt. By then this sound will only exist in my recordings. Many people think what I'm doing is pointless, but I believe sound can restore a place's soul better than photographs.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to her claim that sound preserves place better than images.

options.text_zh: 我从来没有这样想过。但你说的有道理——我看老照片的时候会分析它，但是如果突然听到小时候巷子里的声音，我大概会直接愣住。声音好像比画面更难防备。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ cónglái méiyǒu zhèyàng xiǎng guò. Dàn nǐ shuō de yǒu dàolǐ — wǒ kàn lǎo zhàopiàn de shíhou huì fēnxī tā, dànshì rúguǒ tūrán tīng dào xiǎo shíhou xiàngzi lǐ de shēngyīn, wǒ dàgài huì zhíjiē lèng zhù. Shēngyīn hǎoxiàng bǐ huàmiàn gèng nán fángbèi.
options.text_en: I never thought about it that way. But you're right — old photos I analyze, but if I suddenly heard sounds from the alley where I grew up, I'd freeze. Sound is harder to defend against than images.
options.feedback: She pulls off one headphone. 'Harder to defend against — that's exactly it. Nobody puts up walls against a sound.'

options.text_zh: 你的录音机是什么牌子的？效果好吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de lùyīnjī shì shénme páizi de? Xiàoguǒ hǎo ma?
options.text_en: What brand is your recorder? Does it work well?
options.feedback: She answers — 'Zoom H5' — but her expression flattens a little. She was talking about the soul of a place.

options.text_zh: 我觉得照片也挺好的，可以发朋友圈。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde zhàopiàn yě tǐng hǎo de, kěyǐ fā péngyǒuquān.
options.text_en: I think photos are fine too — you can post them on WeChat Moments.
options.feedback: She smiles politely but doesn't respond. The conversation she wanted to have and the one you're having are two different conversations.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对，没有防备。我录过一段胡同里早晨的声音——自行车铃铛、有人倒洗脸水的声音、收废品的吆喝、远处学校的广播体操。单独听每一个声音都很普通，但混在一起的时候，它就变成了一种非常具体的「早上七点的北京胡同」。这个配方以后不会再有了。
turns.text_pinyin: Duì, méiyǒu fángbèi. Wǒ lù guò yī duàn hútòng lǐ zǎochén de shēngyīn — zìxíngchē língdāng, yǒu rén dào xǐliǎnshuǐ de shēngyīn, shōu fèipǐn de yāohè, yuǎnchù xuéxiào de guǎngbō tǐcāo. Dāndú tīng měi yīge shēngyīn dōu hěn pǔtōng, dàn hùn zài yīqǐ de shíhòu, tā jiù biàn chéng le yī zhǒng fēicháng jùtǐ de 'zǎoshang qī diǎn de Běijīng hútòng'. Zhège pèifāng yǐhòu bú huì zài yǒu le.
turns.text_en: Right, no defenses. I once recorded the sounds of a hutong morning — bicycle bells, someone pouring out wash water, the scrap collector's calls, school radio calisthenics in the distance. Each sound alone is ordinary, but mixed together they become a very specific 'seven a.m. in a Beijing hutong.' That recipe will never exist again.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: She used the word 'recipe' — respond to that idea.

options.text_zh: 「配方」——这个词用得好。少了收废品的那个声音，整个配方就变了。就像一道菜少了一味料，你吃得出来不对，但说不出哪里不对。
options.text_pinyin: 'Pèifāng' — zhège cí yòng de hǎo. Shǎo le shōu fèipǐn de nàge shēngyīn, zhěnggè pèifāng jiù biàn le. Jiù xiàng yī dào cài shǎo le yī wèi liào, nǐ chī de chūlái bùduì, dàn shuō bù chū nǎlǐ bùduì.
options.text_en: 'Recipe' — that's a good word for it. Take away the scrap collector's voice and the whole recipe changes. Like a dish missing one ingredient — you can taste that something's off, but you can't say what.
options.feedback: She nods slowly. 'That's the hardest thing to explain to people. It's not about any one sound. It's about what happens when they're all there at once.'

options.text_zh: 你录了这么多，最后会做成什么？展览？播客？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ lù le zhème duō, zuìhòu huì zuò chéng shénme? Zhǎnlǎn? Bōkè?
options.text_en: With all these recordings, what will you make from them? An exhibition? A podcast?
options.feedback: A reasonable question, but she was talking about loss, and you jumped to output. She answers — 'I haven't decided yet' — and goes quiet for a moment.

options.text_zh: 北京变化太大了，我都认不出来了。
options.text_pinyin: Běijīng biànhuà tài dà le, wǒ dōu rèn bu chūlái le.
options.text_en: Beijing has changed so much, I can barely recognize it.
options.feedback: True, but too general. She was being specific — a recipe, a particular combination — and you widened it into a cliché about change.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 其实最让我心疼的是那些正在消失的人声。比如磨刀师傅的吆喝，每个人的调子都不一样，像是一种个人签名。我录过五个不同的磨刀师傅，他们的吆喝里有方言的痕迹、有性格的色彩、有年龄的印记。但这个行业快消亡了，年轻人不会接手。
turns.text_pinyin: Qíshí zuì ràng wǒ xīnténg de shì nàxiē zhèngzài xiāoshī de rénshēng. Bǐrú mó dāo shīfu de yāohè, měige rén de diàozi dōu bù yīyàng, xiàng shì yī zhǒng gèrén qiānmíng. Wǒ lù guò wǔ ge bùtóng de mó dāo shīfu, tāmen de yāohè lǐ yǒu fāngyán de hénjì, yǒu xìnggé de sècǎi, yǒu niánlíng de yìnjì. Dàn zhège hángyè kuài xiāowáng le, niánqīngrén bú huì jiēshǒu.
turns.text_en: Actually what pains me most are the disappearing human voices. Like knife sharpeners' calls — each person's melody is different, like a personal signature. I've recorded five different knife sharpeners, and their calls carry traces of dialect, colors of personality, marks of age. But the profession is dying out, and no young people will take it over.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the loss of unique human voices.

options.text_zh: 五个不同的磨刀师傅，五种不同的吆喝。如果你不录下来，这些声音就跟着他们一起走了——不是消失在某个地方，是彻底没了。连模仿都模仿不出来，因为别人的人生不一样。
options.text_pinyin: Wǔ ge bùtóng de mó dāo shīfu, wǔ zhǒng bùtóng de yāohè. Rúguǒ nǐ bù lù xiàlái, zhèxiē shēngyīn jiù gēnzhe tāmen yīqǐ zǒu le — bú shì xiāoshī zài mǒu ge dìfang, shì chèdǐ méi le. Lián mófǎng dōu mófǎng bù chūlái, yīnwèi biéren de rénshēng bù yīyàng.
options.text_en: Five different sharpeners, five different calls. If you don't record them, those sounds leave with them — not vanishing somewhere, just gone completely. You can't even imitate them, because someone else's life is different.
options.feedback: She looks at you for a long moment. 'Most people say "that's sad" and move on. You actually heard what I meant.'

options.text_zh: 有人建议用AI模拟这些声音吗？技术上应该可以做到。
options.text_pinyin: Yǒu rén jiànyì yòng AI mónǐ zhèxiē shēngyīn ma? Jìshù shàng yīnggāi kěyǐ zuò dào.
options.text_en: Has anyone suggested using AI to simulate these sounds? Technically it should be possible.
options.feedback: She pauses. 'That's the question that bothers me most.' She was going to explain why, but you jumped to the solution before she finished describing the problem.

options.text_zh: 现在年轻人都不愿意做这种工作了，没办法。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài niánqīngrén dōu bú yuànyì zuò zhè zhǒng gōngzuò le, méi bànfǎ.
options.text_en: Young people today don't want to do this kind of work, nothing to be done.
options.feedback: She nods but doesn't continue. 'Nothing to be done' closes a door she was trying to open.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 有人建议我用AI合成这些声音来「保存」，但合成出来的声音没有灵魂。就像你可以用AI写一首诗，但那首诗里没有写诗的那个人的犹豫和挣扎。我录的不是声波数据，是一个具体的人在一个具体的下午发出的具体的声音。
turns.text_pinyin: Yǒu rén jiànyì wǒ yòng AI héchéng zhèxiē shēngyīn lái 'bǎocún', dàn héchéng chūlái de shēngyīn méiyǒu línghún. Jiù xiàng nǐ kěyǐ yòng AI xiě yī shǒu shī, dàn nà shǒu shī lǐ méiyǒu xiě shī de nàge rén de yóuyù hé zhēngzhá. Wǒ lù de bú shì shēngbō shùjù, shì yīge jùtǐ de rén zài yīge jùtǐ de xiàwǔ fāchū de jùtǐ de shēngyīn.
turns.text_en: Someone suggested I use AI to synthesize these sounds to 'preserve' them, but synthesized sound has no soul. Just like you can use AI to write a poem, but that poem doesn't contain the hesitation and struggle of the person who wrote it. What I record isn't sound wave data — it's a specific person making a specific sound on a specific afternoon.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: She's talking about the difference between data and lived sound.

options.text_zh: 「一个具体的人，一个具体的下午」——这个说法让我想到，你录的其实不是声音本身，是那个下午。那个下午不会再来了。所以你做的事情不是保存，更像是……见证？
options.text_pinyin: 'Yīge jùtǐ de rén, yīge jùtǐ de xiàwǔ' — zhège shuōfǎ ràng wǒ xiǎng dào, nǐ lù de qíshí bú shì shēngyīn běnshēn, shì nàge xiàwǔ. Nàge xiàwǔ bú huì zài lái le. Suǒyǐ nǐ zuò de shìqíng bú shì bǎocún, gèng xiàng shì…… jiànzhèng?
options.text_en: 'A specific person, a specific afternoon' — that makes me think what you're really recording isn't the sound itself, but that afternoon. That afternoon won't come again. So what you're doing isn't preservation, it's more like... witnessing?
options.feedback: She's quiet for several seconds. Then: 'Witnessing. I've been trying to find the right word for two years. That's it.'

options.text_zh: 我觉得AI以后肯定会越来越好，也许有一天真的能完美复制。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde AI yǐhòu kěndìng huì yuè lái yuè hǎo, yěxǔ yǒu yī tiān zhēn de néng wánměi fùzhì.
options.text_en: I think AI will keep getting better — maybe one day it really can make a perfect copy.
options.feedback: She shakes her head slowly. 'You're still thinking about the copy. I'm talking about what can't be copied.' The gap between your wavelengths is showing.

options.text_zh: 这个项目应该去申请艺术基金，肯定有机构会感兴趣的。
options.text_pinyin: Zhège xiàngmù yīnggāi qù shēnqǐng yìshù jījīn, kěndìng yǒu jīgòu huì gǎn xìngqù de.
options.text_en: You should apply for art funding — there must be organizations interested in this.
options.feedback: She smiles. 'People always say that.' She puts her headphones back on. You offered logistics when she was offering philosophy.

tree.cultural_note: Street vendor calls (吆喝 yāohè) are recognized as intangible cultural heritage in several Chinese cities. Each vendor developed a unique melodic call that served as both advertisement and neighborhood soundtrack. Sound preservation has become an emerging field in urban cultural conservation, as researchers recognize that the acoustic environment — from vendor calls to bicycle bells to the specific echo of narrow alleys — constitutes a layer of cultural identity that photographs alone cannot capture.

tree.setup: You've taken shelter from a sudden rainstorm under a covered wooden bridge in a small town in Zhejiang province. An elderly retired calligrapher, Teacher Shen, is already there, watching the rain. He has a thermos of tea and seems content to wait. The rain drums steadily on the old wooden roof.
tree.setup_zh: 你在浙江一座小镇的廊桥下躲避突如其来的暴雨。一位退休的老书法家沈老师已经在那里了，正在看雨。他带着一个保温壶的茶，似乎乐于等待。雨水稳稳地敲打着古老的木屋顶。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 别着急，这种雨来得快，走得也快。正好歇一会儿。你知道吗，以前的人造廊桥不只是为了过河，也是为了这种时刻——给路人一个停下来的理由。现在大家都赶路，没人愿意停了。
turns.text_pinyin: Bié zhāojí, zhè zhǒng yǔ lái de kuài, zǒu de yě kuài. Zhènghǎo xiē yīhuǐr. Nǐ zhīdào ma, yǐqián de rén zào lángqiáo bù zhǐ shì wèile guò hé, yě shì wèile zhè zhǒng shíkè — gěi lùrén yīge tíng xiàlái de lǐyóu. Xiànzài dàjiā dōu gǎn lù, méi rén yuànyì tíng le.
turns.text_en: Don't worry, this kind of rain comes fast and goes fast. Good chance to rest a bit. Did you know, people in the past built covered bridges not just for crossing rivers, but for moments like this — giving travelers a reason to stop. Nowadays everyone's rushing, no one wants to stop anymore.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to what Teacher Shen said about covered bridges as places for pausing.

options.text_zh: 我从来没从这个角度想过。廊桥不只是「通过」，也是「停留」。下雨是老天给你的暂停键，廊桥是人给你的接住这个暂停的空间。
