options.text_pinyin: Wǒ cónglái méi cóng zhège jiǎodù xiǎng guò. Lángqiáo bù zhǐ shì 'tōngguò', yě shì 'tíngliú'. Xià yǔ shì lǎotiān gěi nǐ de zàntíng jiàn, lángqiáo shì rén gěi nǐ de jiē zhù zhège zàntíng de kōngjiān.
options.text_en: I've never thought of it that way. A covered bridge isn't just for passing through — it's also for staying. Rain is nature's pause button, and the bridge is the human-made space that catches that pause.
options.feedback: He nods slowly, eyes still on the rain. "Pause button," he says. "Good. That's exactly it."

options.text_zh: 这座桥有多少年历史了？是文物保护单位吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè zuò qiáo yǒu duōshǎo nián lìshǐ le? Shì wénwù bǎohù dānwèi ma?
options.text_en: How old is this bridge? Is it a protected heritage site?
options.feedback: He answers briefly — several hundred years, yes, listed — then settles back into watching the rain, leaving you to the silence.

options.text_zh: 我看天气预报说今天不会下雨的，不太准。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ kàn tiānqì yùbào shuō jīntiān bú huì xià yǔ de, bú tài zhǔn.
options.text_en: The weather forecast said it wouldn't rain today, it's not very accurate.
options.feedback: He smiles slightly. "The bridge doesn't care about forecasts," he says, and leaves it at that.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 「暂停键」——这个比喻好。我退休以后最大的变化就是学会了跟「没事做」和平共处。以前每天都排得满满的，退休第一年反而很焦虑，觉得自己没用了。后来慢慢接受了「无用」也是一种状态，不比「有用」低一等。
turns.text_pinyin: 'Zàntíng jiàn' — zhège bǐyù hǎo. Wǒ tuìxiū yǐhòu zuìdà de biànhuà jiù shì xuéhuì le gēn 'méi shì zuò' hépíng gòngchǔ. Yǐqián měi tiān dōu pái de mǎnmǎn de, tuìxiū dì yī nián fǎn'ér hěn jiāolǜ, juéde zìjǐ méi yòng le. Hòulái mànmàn jiēshòu le 'wúyòng' yě shì yī zhǒng zhuàngtài, bù bǐ 'yǒuyòng' dī yī děng.
turns.text_en: 'Pause button' — good metaphor. The biggest change after I retired was learning to coexist peacefully with 'having nothing to do.' Before, every day was packed full; the first year of retirement I was actually anxious, feeling useless. Eventually I came to accept that 'uselessness' is also a state of being, not inferior to 'usefulness.'

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his journey from anxiety about uselessness to acceptance.

options.text_zh: 这触及了一个根本的问题——我们把自我价值绑定在了「有产出」上面。庄子说「无用之用」，但那需要先放下「有用才值得存在」这个念头，才能真正做到。听起来那一年挺难熬的。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè chùjí le yīge gēnběn de wèntí — wǒmen bǎ zìwǒ jiàzhí bǎngdìng zài le 'yǒu chǎnchū' shàngmiàn. Zhuāngzǐ shuō 'wúyòng zhī yòng', dàn nà xūyào xiān fàngxià 'yǒuyòng cái zhídé cúnzài' zhège niàntou, cái néng zhēnzhèng zuòdào. Tīng qǐlái nà yī nián tǐng nán áo de.
options.text_en: That touches something fundamental — tying worth to being productive. Zhuangzi talked about 'the usefulness of uselessness,' but you have to drop the idea that usefulness justifies existence first. That first year sounds rough.
options.feedback: He chuckles softly. "Rough is right. My wife thought I was becoming difficult." He pours a little more tea into his cup.

options.text_zh: 退休以后可以学点新东西啊，比如摄影或者旅游。
options.text_pinyin: Tuìxiū yǐhòu kěyǐ xué diǎn xīn dōngxī a, bǐrú shèyǐng huòzhě lǚyóu.
options.text_en: After retirement you could learn something new, like photography or travel.
options.feedback: He nods politely. "I suppose so," he says, in the tone of someone who has heard this suggestion many times.

options.text_zh: 你以前教书的学校在哪里？学生多吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǐqián jiāoshū de xuéxiào zài nǎlǐ? Xuéshēng duō ma?
options.text_en: Where was the school you used to teach at? Many students?
options.feedback: He answers briefly, then looks back at the rain. He seemed to want to talk about something else.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 庄子……是的。我教了一辈子书法，总是在追求「写好」。退休以后反而开始享受写「坏」的自由。有时候故意让笔锋歪一下，发现反而更有意思。年轻的时候追求控制，老了才知道放松也是一种力量。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhuāngzǐ…… shì de. Wǒ jiāo le yī bèizi shūfǎ, zǒng shì zài zhuīqiú 'xiě hǎo'. Tuìxiū yǐhòu fǎn'ér kāishǐ xiǎngshòu xiě 'huài' de zìyóu. Yǒu shíhòu gùyì ràng bǐfēng wāi yīxià, fāxiàn fǎn'ér gèng yǒu yìsi. Niánqīng de shíhòu zhuīqiú kòngzhì, lǎo le cái zhīdào fàngsōng yě shì yī zhǒng lìliàng.
turns.text_en: Zhuangzi… yes. I taught calligraphy my whole life, always pursuing 'writing well.' After retirement I actually started enjoying the freedom of writing 'badly.' Sometimes I deliberately let the brush tip go crooked, and find it's actually more interesting. When young I chased control; only in old age did I learn that looseness is also a kind of power.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his idea that deliberate imperfection is its own skill.

options.text_zh: 这让我想到侘寂——不完美本身可以是美。但你说的更进一步：不是被动地接受不完美，而是主动地选择它。那种选择只有先掌握规则的人才做得到。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè ràng wǒ xiǎng dào chà jì — bù wánměi běnshēn kěyǐ shì měi. Dàn nǐ shuō de gèng jìn yī bù: bú shì bèidòng de jiēshòu bù wánměi, ér shì zhǔdòng de xuǎnzé tā. Nà zhǒng xuǎnzé zhǐ yǒu xiān zhǎngwò guīzé de rén cái zuòdedào.
options.text_en: That reminds me of wabi-sabi — imperfection itself can be beautiful. But what you're describing goes further: it's not passively accepting imperfection, but actively choosing it. And only someone who's mastered the rules first can really make that choice.
options.feedback: He raises his cup slightly, like a small toast. "Exactly. A student who writes badly is just writing badly. What I'm doing is different."

options.text_zh: 写坏了那还叫书法吗？书法不是应该追求完美吗？
options.text_pinyin: Xiě huài le nà hái jiào shūfǎ ma? Shūfǎ bú shì yīnggāi zhuīqiú wánměi ma?
options.text_en: If you write badly, can you still call it calligraphy? Isn't calligraphy supposed to pursue perfection?
options.feedback: He looks at you with mild amusement. "That's exactly what my students used to say," he says.

options.text_zh: 你的书法作品卖吗？我想买一幅。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ de shūfǎ zuòpǐn mài ma? Wǒ xiǎng mǎi yī fú.
options.text_en: Do you sell your calligraphy? I'd like to buy a piece.
options.feedback: He waves a hand. "I write for myself now. That's the whole point," he says, not unfriendly.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 嗯——先掌握规则，再打破规则。这不是叛逆，是回归。就像这场雨，它不遵守任何节拍，但你听着不觉得乱，反而觉得自然。也许自然界本来就没有「对」和「错」，只有「刚好在这里」。
turns.text_pinyin: Ǹg — xiān zhǎngwò guīzé, zài dǎpò guīzé. Zhè bú shì pànnì, shì huíguī. Jiù xiàng zhè chǎng yǔ, tā bù zūnshǒu rènhé jiépāi, dàn nǐ tīng zhe bù juéde luàn, fǎn'ér juéde zìrán. Yěxǔ zìránjiè běnlái jiù méiyǒu 'duì' hé 'cuò', zhǐ yǒu 'gānghǎo zài zhèlǐ'.
turns.text_en: Mm — first master the rules, then break them. It's not rebellion, it's return. Like this rain — it follows no beat, but when you listen it doesn't feel chaotic, it feels natural. Perhaps nature never had 'right' and 'wrong,' only 'happens to be here.'

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his idea of "happens to be here" — nature without right or wrong.

options.text_zh: 「刚好在这里」——我觉得这也形容了我们现在的情况。两个陌生人因为一场雨停在同一座廊桥上，没有计划，但这半小时比很多刻意安排的对话更有意思。最好的相遇可能就是这样——无心插柳。
options.text_pinyin: 'Gānghǎo zài zhèlǐ' — wǒ juéde zhè yě xíngróng le wǒmen xiànzài de qíngkuàng. Liǎng ge mòshēngrén yīnwèi yī chǎng yǔ tíng zài tóngyī zuò lángqiáo shàng, méiyǒu jìhuà, dàn zhè bàn xiǎoshí bǐ hěn duō kèyì ānpái de duìhuà gèng yǒu yìsi. Zuìhǎo de xiāngyù kěnéng jiù shì zhèyàng — wúxīn chā liǔ.
options.text_en: 'Happens to be here' — that describes us now. Two strangers stopped at the same bridge by rain, no plan, but this half hour has been more interesting than many arranged conversations.
options.feedback: He looks sideways at you, then smiles — a real one. "Willows that plant themselves," he repeats. "Good. Sit down properly, the tea's not finished."

options.text_zh: 雨好像小了，我可以走了。谢谢啊。
options.text_pinyin: Yǔ hǎoxiàng xiǎo le, wǒ kěyǐ zǒu le. Xièxiè a.
options.text_en: The rain seems lighter, I can go now. Thanks.
options.feedback: He nods. "Safe travels," he says — and his expression is that of someone whose conversation just got cut short.

options.text_zh: 你觉得明天还会下雨吗？我后天要去杭州。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ juéde míngtiān hái huì xià yǔ ma? Wǒ hòutiān yào qù Hángzhōu.
options.text_en: Do you think it'll rain again tomorrow? I'm going to Hangzhou the day after.
options.feedback: He blinks and checks the sky. "Hard to say," he says pleasantly, and the previous thread drifts away.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 无心插柳……嗯，说得好。你看，雨已经小了。再过几分钟就可以走了。不过不急——茶还没喝完呢。来，我给你倒一杯。
turns.text_pinyin: Wúxīn chā liǔ…… ēn, shuō de hǎo. Nǐ kàn, yǔ yǐjīng xiǎo le. Zài guò jǐ fēnzhōng jiù kěyǐ zǒu le. Búguò bú jí — chá hái méi hē wán ne. Lái, wǒ gěi nǐ dào yī bēi.
turns.text_en: Willows planted without intention… well said. Look, the rain has eased. A few more minutes and you can go. But no rush — the tea isn't finished yet. Come, let me pour you a cup.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Accept the tea and say goodbye to Teacher Shen.

options.text_zh: 好，不急。谢谢您的茶，也谢谢这场雨。要是今天没下雨，我大概会匆匆走过去，注意不到桥上有这么好的风景——不是山水，是您。
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo, bú jí. Xièxiè nín de chá, yě xièxiè zhè chǎng yǔ. Yàoshi jīntiān méi xià yǔ, wǒ dàgài huì cōngcōng zǒu guò qù, zhùyì bú dào qiáo shàng yǒu zhème hǎo de fēngjǐng — bú shì shānshuǐ, shì nín.
options.text_en: Alright, no rush. Thank you for the tea — and for the rain. If it hadn't rained, I'd have hurried right across without noticing the best view on this bridge. Not the mountains. You.
options.feedback: He laughs — a short, genuine one — and hands you the cup. "Drink it while it's warm," he says. He looks pleased.

options.text_zh: 不用了，我赶时间，下次吧。
options.text_pinyin: Bú yòng le, wǒ gǎn shíjiān, xià cì ba.
options.text_en: No need, I'm in a hurry, next time.
options.feedback: He nods and tucks the cup back. "Next time," he says, and watches you go — the quiet irony of the whole conversation trailing behind you.

options.text_zh: 这是什么茶？龙井吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè shì shénme chá? Lóngjǐng ma?
options.text_en: What kind of tea is this? Longjing?
options.feedback: He tells you the tea name with a smile, but the warm closing note of the conversation slips away into tea trivia.

tree.cultural_note: Covered bridges (廊桥 lángqiáo) in southern China served as communal gathering spaces for centuries — travelers rested, merchants traded, and neighbors socialized under their roofs. The Taishun covered bridges in Zhejiang are UNESCO-recognized. The philosophical thread of this conversation draws on Daoist concepts of wuwei (non-action) and Zhuangzi's 'usefulness of the useless tree,' reflecting a tradition that values being over doing.

tree.setup: You're visiting a courtyard house (四合院) that's about to be converted into a boutique hotel. The last remaining resident, Grandma Wen, is sitting under a centuries-old ginkgo tree. She's been offered relocation money but hasn't left. The tree's golden leaves are falling around her.
tree.setup_zh: 你正在参观一座即将被改造成精品酒店的四合院。最后一位留下来的居民温奶奶正坐在一棵百年银杏树下。她已经被提供了搬迁费，但没有搬走。金黄的银杏叶正纷纷飘落在她周围。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你是来看房子的？还是来看树的？大多数人来看房子，看完就走了。但这棵银杏比这院子里任何一面墙都老。我爷爷的爷爷种的——五代人了。他们说改造以后会保留这棵树，但我知道，一棵树如果周围的一切都变了，它虽然还站着，其实已经不是原来那棵树了。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ shì lái kàn fángzi de? Háishì lái kàn shù de? Dàduōshù rén lái kàn fángzi, kàn wán jiù zǒu le. Dàn zhè kē yínxìng bǐ zhè yuànzi lǐ rènhé yī miàn qiáng dōu lǎo. Wǒ yéye de yéye zhǒng de — wǔ dài rén le. Tāmen shuō gǎizào yǐhòu huì bǎoliú zhè kē shù, dàn wǒ zhīdào, yī kē shù rúguǒ zhōuwéi de yīqiè dōu biàn le, tā suīrán hái zhàn zhe, qíshí yǐjīng bú shì yuánlái nà kē shù le.
turns.text_en: Are you here to see the house? Or the tree? Most people come for the house, take a look and leave. But this ginkgo is older than any wall in this courtyard. My grandfather's grandfather planted it — five generations. They say they'll keep the tree after the renovation, but I know: if everything around a tree changes, even though it's still standing, it's no longer the same tree.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you say to her?

options.text_zh: 如果院子变成了酒店大堂，这棵树就从「家里的树」变成了「装饰品」。物理上它没变，但它的意思完全变了。
options.text_pinyin: Rúguǒ yuànzi biàn chéng le jiǔdiàn dàtáng, zhè kē shù jiù cóng 'jiā lǐ de shù' biàn chéng le 'zhuāngshìpǐn'. Wùlǐ shàng tā méi biàn, dàn tā de yìsi wánquán biàn le.
options.text_en: If the courtyard becomes a hotel lobby, this tree goes from 'the family tree' to 'a decoration.' Physically it hasn't changed, but its meaning has completely shifted.
options.feedback: 温奶奶停顿了一下，轻声重复那个词：「装饰品。」她点点头，像是终于听到了一个说得出口的说法。

options.text_zh: 银杏树很值钱的，你可以要求他们多赔你一些。
options.text_pinyin: Yínxìng shù hěn zhíqián de, nǐ kěyǐ yāoqiú tāmen duō péi nǐ yīxiē.
options.text_en: Ginkgo trees are quite valuable — you could demand more compensation.
options.feedback: 温奶奶抬起眼睛看了你一会儿，没有说话，又低头看树叶。

options.text_zh: 也许保留树是个好的开始，以后慢慢会更好的。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ bǎoliú shù shì ge hǎo de kāishǐ, yǐhòu mànmàn huì gèng hǎo de.
options.text_en: Maybe keeping the tree is a good start — things might improve gradually.
options.feedback: 温奶奶淡淡地笑了笑：「他们也是这么跟我说的。」

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 装饰品——就是这个词。以后游客会在树下拍照，发朋友圈说「好有文化氛围」，但他们不知道这棵树下面埋过我奶奶的胎盘——以前的人有这个习俗，把孩子的胎盘埋在家里的树下，意思是根在这里。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhuāngshìpǐn — jiù shì zhège cí. Yǐhòu yóukè huì zài shù xià pāizhào, fā péngyǒu quān shuō 'hǎo yǒu wénhuà fēnwéi', dàn tāmen bù zhīdào zhè kē shù xiàmiàn mái guò wǒ nǎinai de tāipán — yǐqián de rén yǒu zhège xísú, bǎ háizi de tāipán mái zài jiā lǐ de shù xià, yìsi shì gēn zài zhèlǐ.
turns.text_en: Decoration — that's exactly the word. In the future tourists will take photos under the tree, post on WeChat Moments saying 'such cultural atmosphere,' but they won't know that my grandmother's placenta is buried under this tree. People used to have that custom — burying a child's placenta under the family tree, meaning one's roots are here.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you say to her?

options.text_zh: 游客看到的是一棵漂亮的老树，但您看到的是完全不同的东西。这种深度，改造之后是留不下来的。
options.text_pinyin: Yóukè kàn dào de shì yī kē piàoliang de lǎo shù, dàn nín kàn dào de shì wánquán bùtóng de dōngxī. Zhè zhǒng shēndù, gǎizào zhīhòu shì liú bù xiàlái de.
options.text_en: Tourists see a beautiful old tree. But what you see is something completely different. That depth can't survive the renovation.
options.feedback: 温奶奶把手放到树根上，轻轻摩挲了一下，没有说话。

options.text_zh: 现在还有人埋胎盘吗？这个习俗挺有意思的。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài hái yǒu rén mái tāipán ma? Zhège xísú tǐng yǒu yìsi de.
options.text_en: Do people still bury placentas now? That's quite an interesting custom.
options.feedback: 温奶奶停下来，带着一点困惑看着你：「你觉得……有意思？」

options.text_zh: 您可以把这些故事记录下来，留给后代看。
options.text_pinyin: Nín kěyǐ bǎ zhèxiē gùshì jìlù xiàlái, liú gěi hòudài kàn.
options.text_en: You could write these stories down and leave them for future generations.
options.feedback: 温奶奶苦笑了一下：「记录下来，然后呢？放在酒店的宣传册里？」

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你说到了关键——不可见的层次。我跟开发商谈过很多次，他们总是说「我们尊重历史」，但他们尊重的是历史的外壳，不是历史的内容。他们会保留灰砖、木梁、甚至门上的对联，但那只是表演。没有人住在里面，历史就是空的。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ shuō dào le guānjiàn — bùkě jiàn de céngcì. Wǒ gēn kāifāshāng tán guò hěn duō cì, tāmen zǒng shì shuō 'wǒmen zūnzhòng lìshǐ', dàn tāmen zūnzhòng de shì lìshǐ de wàiké, bú shì lìshǐ de nèiróng. Tāmen huì bǎoliú huī zhuān, mù liáng, shènzhì mén shàng de duìlián, dàn nà zhǐ shì biǎoyǎn. Méiyǒu rén zhù zài lǐmiàn, lìshǐ jiù shì kōng de.
turns.text_en: You've hit the key point — invisible layers. I've talked to the developer many times. They always say 'we respect history,' but what they respect is history's shell, not its content. They'll preserve the gray bricks, wooden beams, even the couplets on the door, but that's just performance. Without people living inside, history is empty.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you say to her?

options.text_zh: 为了「保护」这个院子，第一步就是把住在里面的人赶走。这种「保护」，其实是一种更精致的破坏。
options.text_pinyin: Wèile 'bǎohù' zhège yuànzi, dì yī bù jiù shì bǎ zhù zài lǐmiàn de rén gǎn zǒu. Zhè zhǒng 'bǎohù', qíshí shì yī zhǒng gèng jīngzhì de pòhuài.
options.text_en: To 'protect' this courtyard, the first step is to drive out the people who live in it. That kind of 'protection' is really a more refined form of destruction.
options.feedback: 温奶奶靠着树干，闭上眼睛：「精致的破坏。说得太准了。」

options.text_zh: 但是老房子确实不安全啊，有些结构已经很危险了。
options.text_pinyin: Dànshì lǎo fángzi quèshí bù ānquán a, yǒuxiē jiégòu yǐjīng hěn wēixiǎn le.
options.text_en: But old houses really aren't safe — some structures are already quite dangerous.
options.feedback: 温奶奶睁开眼，平静地说：「开发商也是这么说的。」

options.text_zh: 也许可以在酒店里设一个展览，专门介绍住过的家庭的历史。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ kěyǐ zài jiǔdiàn lǐ shè yīge zhǎnlǎn, zhuānmén jièshào zhù guò de jiātíng de lìshǐ.
options.text_en: Maybe they could set up an exhibit inside the hotel specifically about the families who lived here.
options.feedback: 温奶奶轻轻摇摇头：「展览。把我们变成展品。」

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 精致的破坏……说得太准了。好了，不说这些了。你来，我给你看一样东西。这棵树的树干上有个疤——是我小时候爬树摔下来，磕到树皮留下的。我的疤早就好了，但树的疤还在。有时候我觉得这棵树比我更忠实地记住了我的童年。
turns.text_pinyin: Jīngzhì de pòhuài…… shuō de tài zhǔn le. Hǎo le, bù shuō zhèxiē le. Nǐ lái, wǒ gěi nǐ kàn yī yàng dōngxī. Zhè kē shù de shùgàn shàng yǒu ge bā — shì wǒ xiǎo shíhòu pá shù shuāi xiàlái, kē dào shùpí liú xià de. Wǒ de bā zǎo jiù hǎo le, dàn shù de bā hái zài. Yǒu shíhòu wǒ juéde zhè kē shù bǐ wǒ gèng zhōngshí de jì zhù le wǒ de tóngnián.
turns.text_en: Refined destruction… that's exactly right. Alright, enough of that. Come, let me show you something. There's a scar on this tree trunk — from when I fell climbing as a child and hit the bark. My scar healed long ago, but the tree's scar is still there. Sometimes I feel this tree remembers my childhood more faithfully than I do.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you say to her?

options.text_zh: 人会愈合，会忘记，但树把每一个印记都保住了。也许这就是为什么我们需要树、需要老地方——我们把自己的记忆寄放在周围，因为知道自己终究会忘。
options.text_pinyin: Rén huì yùhé, huì wàngjì, dàn shù bǎ měi yīge yìnjì dōu bǎo zhù le. Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì wèishéme wǒmen xūyào shù, xūyào lǎo dìfang — wǒmen bǎ zìjǐ de jìyì jìfàng zài zhōuwéi, yīnwèi zhīdào zìjǐ zhōngjiū huì wàng.
options.text_en: People heal and forget, but the tree keeps every mark. Maybe that's why we need trees, why we need old places — we deposit our memories in our surroundings, knowing we ourselves will eventually forget.
options.feedback: 温奶奶把手指放在那个疤上，轻声说：「是。它替我记着。」

options.text_zh: 小时候爬树很危险的，现在小孩都不爬了。
options.text_pinyin: Xiǎo shíhòu pá shù hěn wēixiǎn de, xiànzài xiǎohái dōu bù pá le.
options.text_en: Climbing trees as a kid was dangerous — children don't really do that anymore.
options.feedback: 温奶奶收回手，有些茫然地点点头：「是，不爬了。」

options.text_zh: 银杏树的叶子秋天真好看，可以做书签。
options.text_pinyin: Yínxìng shù de yèzi qiūtiān zhēn hǎokàn, kěyǐ zuò shūqiān.
options.text_en: Ginkgo leaves are really beautiful in autumn — you could make bookmarks with them.
options.feedback: 温奶奶低头看了看脚边的落叶，没有回应。

tree.cultural_note: The practice of burying a newborn's placenta (胎盘) under a family tree was common in traditional Chinese culture, symbolizing that the child's roots (根) were anchored to the family home. The ginkgo tree (银杏) holds special significance in Chinese culture as one of the oldest living tree species — a 'living fossil' that can survive for over a thousand years. The conversion of siheyuan courtyard houses into boutique hotels represents one of the most visible tensions in Beijing's urban transformation.

tree.setup: On a rainy side street near an old residential quarter, you find an elderly man, Uncle Luo, sitting under a canvas awning repairing umbrellas. His tools are spread out on a folding table. Business is slow — most people just buy new cheap umbrellas. He's carefully mending a child's umbrella with a duck-shaped handle.
tree.setup_zh: 在老居民区附近一条下雨的小街上，你发现一位老人——罗叔叔——坐在帆布雨棚下修伞。他的工具摆放在一张折叠桌上。生意很清淡——大多数人只是买新的便宜伞。他正在仔细修补一把鸭子把手的儿童伞。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这把伞是一个小女孩的妈妈拿来的。伞骨断了两根，布也有个洞。买一把新的比修还便宜。但小女孩的妈妈说，这是孩子的外婆送的，外婆去年走了。所以这把伞不能扔。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè bǎ sǎn shì yīge xiǎo nǚhái de māma ná lái de. Sǎn gǔ duàn le liǎng gēn, bù yě yǒu ge dòng. Mǎi yī bǎ xīn de bǐ xiū hái piányi. Dàn xiǎo nǚhái de māma shuō, zhè shì háizi de wàipó sòng de, wàipó qùnián zǒu le. Suǒyǐ zhè bǎ sǎn bù néng rēng.
turns.text_en: This umbrella was brought by a little girl's mother. Two ribs are broken and the fabric has a hole. Buying a new one would be cheaper than repairing it. But the mother said it was a gift from the girl's grandmother, who passed away last year. So this umbrella can't be thrown away.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you say to him?

options.text_zh: 修理保留的不是功能，而是关系。一把新伞能挡雨，但它挡不住对外婆的记忆。您修的其实是那条线。
options.text_pinyin: Xiūlǐ bǎoliú de bú shì gōngnéng, ér shì guānxì. Yī bǎ xīn sǎn néng dǎng yǔ, dàn tā dǎng bú zhù duì wàipó de jìyì. Nín xiū de qíshí shì nà tiáo xiàn.
options.text_en: Repair preserves not function, but relationship. A new umbrella can block rain, but it can't hold the memory of grandmother. What you're actually mending is that thread.
options.feedback: 罗叔叔放下钳子，抬起头：「那条线——说得好。就是那条线。」

options.text_zh: 现在的伞质量太差了，以前的伞能用好多年。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài de sǎn zhìliàng tài chà le, yǐqián de sǎn néng yòng hǎo duō nián.
options.text_en: Umbrellas nowadays are such poor quality — umbrellas used to last many years.
options.feedback: 罗叔叔点了点头，礼貌地回应：「是，现在的料不行。」他继续低头修伞。

options.text_zh: 她没有给孩子买一把一样的鸭子伞？
options.text_pinyin: Tā méiyǒu gěi háizi mǎi yī bǎ yīyàng de yāzi sǎn?
options.text_en: Did she look for another duck umbrella just like it?
options.feedback: 罗叔叔抬起头，想了一下：「买一把一样的……不一样的。」

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 是这么回事。其实来找我修东西的人，十个里有八个不是因为东西贵、舍不得扔，而是因为东西有故事、不忍心扔。我修过一个老兵的军用水壶、一个老太太的缝纫机、一个大学生的破书包——他说那是他爸爸用过的。每一件东西背后都有一段不想放手的感情。
turns.text_pinyin: Shì zhème huí shì. Qíshí lái zhǎo wǒ xiū dōngxī de rén, shí ge lǐ yǒu bā ge bú shì yīnwèi dōngxī guì, shěbude rēng, ér shì yīnwèi dōngxī yǒu gùshì, bù rěnxīn rēng. Wǒ xiū guò yīge lǎobīng de jūnyòng shuǐhú, yīge lǎo tàitai de féngrènjī, yīge dàxuéshēng de pò shūbāo — tā shuō nà shì tā bàba yòng guò de. Měi yī jiàn dōngxī bèihòu dōu yǒu yī duàn bù xiǎng fàngshǒu de gǎnqíng.
turns.text_en: That's how it is. Actually, of the people who come to me for repairs, eight out of ten aren't because the thing is expensive and they can't bear to throw it away, but because it has a story and they can't bear to let go. I've repaired a veteran's military canteen, an old woman's sewing machine, a college student's worn backpack — he said it was his father's. Behind every object is a feeling someone isn't ready to release.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you say to him?

options.text_zh: 表面上您是在修东西，实际上是在帮人续延一段关系。来的人可能不知道怎么说自己的难过，但他们知道可以带一件破旧的东西来。
options.text_pinyin: Biǎomiàn shàng nín shì zài xiū dōngxī, shíjì shàng shì zài bāng rén xùyán yī duàn guānxì. Lái de rén kěnéng bù zhīdào zěnme shuō zìjǐ de nánguò, dàn tāmen zhīdào kěyǐ dài yī jiàn pòjiù de dōngxī lái.
options.text_en: On the surface you're fixing things, but really you're helping people extend a relationship. They may not know how to say what they're feeling, but they know they can bring a broken thing.
options.feedback: 罗叔叔把鸭子伞放下，看着它：「是这么回事。我一直没想到这个说法。」

options.text_zh: 断舍离不是很流行吗？有时候该扔还是得扔。
options.text_pinyin: Duàn shě lí bú shì hěn liúxíng ma? Yǒu shíhòu gāi rēng háishì děi rēng.
options.text_en: Isn't the decluttering trend popular? Sometimes you do just have to let things go.
options.feedback: 罗叔叔停了一下，温和地说：「那个女孩，才几岁。」

options.text_zh: 您有没有收徒弟？这个手艺不能失传。
options.text_pinyin: Nín yǒu méiyǒu shōu túdì? Zhège shǒuyì bù néng shīchuán.
options.text_en: Do you have any apprentices? This craft mustn't be lost.
options.feedback: 罗叔叔苦笑：「手艺好学，学了有什么用？」他重新拿起钳子。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 桥梁……你说得太好了。有时候修完东西，客人会在旁边坐一会儿，跟我讲那个物件的故事。我觉得他们需要的不只是修好东西，还需要有人听他们说。这个城市跑得太快了，没人有时间听别人讲一把旧伞的故事。
turns.text_pinyin: Qiáoliáng…… nǐ shuō de tài hǎo le. Yǒu shíhòu xiū wán dōngxī, kèrén huì zài pángbiān zuò yīhuǐr, gēn wǒ jiǎng nàge wùjiàn de gùshì. Wǒ juéde tāmen xūyào de bù zhǐ shì xiū hǎo dōngxī, hái xūyào yǒu rén tīng tāmen shuō. Zhège chéngshì pǎo de tài kuài le, méi rén yǒu shíjiān tīng biérén jiǎng yī bǎ jiù sǎn de gùshì.
turns.text_en: Bridge… that's beautifully put. Sometimes after I finish a repair, the customer sits beside me for a while and tells me the story behind the object. I think what they need isn't just the repair, but someone to listen. This city moves too fast — nobody has time to hear someone tell the story of an old umbrella.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you say to him?

options.text_zh: 人们不需要预约，不需要解释自己为什么难过，只需要带一件破旧的东西来。物件是钥匙，倾听才是门。
options.text_pinyin: Rénmen bù xūyào yùyuē, bù xūyào jiěshì zìjǐ wèishéme nánguò, zhǐ xūyào dài yī jiàn pòjiù de dōngxī lái. Wùjiàn shì yàoshi, qīngtīng cái shì mén.
options.text_en: People don't need an appointment, don't need to explain why they're sad. They just bring a broken thing. The object is the key — listening is the door.
options.feedback: 罗叔叔抬起头，把那句话在嘴里默念了一遍：「物件是钥匙，倾听才是门。我要记下来。」

options.text_zh: 您可以开个账号讲这些故事，肯定有人爱看。
options.text_pinyin: Nín kěyǐ kāi ge zhànghào jiǎng zhèxiē gùshì, kěndìng yǒu rén ài kàn.
options.text_en: You could start an account sharing these stories — people would definitely follow it.
options.feedback: 罗叔叔想了想：「把他们的事发到网上……他们跟我说，是因为我不会到处讲。」

options.text_zh: 现在心理咨询挺贵的，你算是免费的吧。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài xīnlǐ zīxún tǐng guì de, nǐ suàn shì miǎnfèi de ba.
options.text_en: Therapy is quite expensive now — you're basically offering it for free.
options.feedback: 罗叔叔皱了皱眉：「咨询……不一样的。」他没再接话。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 物件是钥匙，倾听才是门——你这句话我要记下来。好了，这把小伞修好了。你看，看不出来修过吧？这就是我的原则——修完之后不能留下明显的痕迹，要让东西看起来好像从来没坏过。因为对那个小女孩来说，这把伞代表的是外婆完整的爱。
turns.text_pinyin: Wùjiàn shì yàoshi, qīngtīng cái shì mén — nǐ zhè jù huà wǒ yào jì xiàlái. Hǎo le, zhè bǎ xiǎo sǎn xiū hǎo le. Nǐ kàn, kàn bù chūlái xiū guò ba? Zhè jiù shì wǒ de yuánzé — xiū wán zhīhòu bù néng liú xià míngxiǎn de hénjì, yào ràng dōngxī kàn qǐlái hǎoxiàng cónglái méi huài guò. Yīnwèi duì nàge xiǎo nǚhái lái shuō, zhè bǎ sǎn dàibiǎo de shì wàipó wánzhěng de ài.
turns.text_en: The object is the key, listening is the door — I need to remember that phrase. Alright, this little umbrella is repaired. See — you can't tell it was fixed, right? That's my principle: after repair, there should be no obvious trace. The thing should look like it was never broken. Because for that little girl, this umbrella represents her grandmother's whole love.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you say to him?

options.text_zh: 日本有个词叫「金继ぎ」，刻意让修补的痕迹可见。那是一种美学。但您的方式是相反的——让伤痕消失。对那个女孩来说，可能保护比面对更重要。
options.text_pinyin: Rìběn yǒu ge cí jiào 'jīnjì', kèyì ràng xiūbǔ de hénjì kějiàn. Nà shì yī zhǒng měixué. Dàn nín de fāngshì shì xiāngfǎn de — ràng shānghén xiāoshī. Duì nàge nǚhái lái shuō, kěnéng bǎohù bǐ miànduì gèng zhòngyào.
options.text_en: In Japan there's kintsugi — deliberately making the mending visible. That's one aesthetic. But yours is the opposite — making the damage disappear. For that girl, protection might matter more than facing it.
options.feedback: 罗叔叔把伞翻来翻去，若有所思：「金继ぎ……两种方式，都是尊重。」他轻轻点头。

options.text_zh: 这把伞的布料是什么材质的？防水性能还好吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè bǎ sǎn de bùliào shì shénme cáizhì de? Fángshuǐ xìngnéng hái hǎo ma?
options.text_en: What material is this umbrella's fabric? Is the waterproofing still good?
options.feedback: 罗叔叔抬起头，停顿了一下：「还行。」然后把伞叠起来包好。

options.text_zh: 您每天能修几把？这个生意够维持生活吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín měi tiān néng xiū jǐ bǎ? Zhège shēngyi gòu wéichí shēnghuó ma?
options.text_en: How many can you repair in a day? Is this business enough to live on?
options.feedback: 罗叔叔笑了笑，算是回答：「够用。」把话题带走了。

tree.cultural_note: The repair economy was once central to daily life in China — traveling cobblers, knife sharpeners, pot menders, and umbrella repairers were fixtures of every neighborhood. As disposable consumer goods became cheap and abundant, these trades nearly vanished. The few remaining repair people often find their role has shifted: they serve less as functional tradespeople and more as custodians of emotional attachment, helping people maintain connections to the past through physical objects.

tree.setup: You've stumbled into one of Beijing's last remaining traditional public bathhouses. The owner, Boss Ma, is a stocky man in his sixties who runs the place almost alone now. It's late afternoon — the quiet hours between the morning elderly crowd and the occasional evening visitor. Steam drifts through the tiled room as he wipes down benches.
tree.setup_zh: 你无意中走进了北京最后几家传统公共澡堂之一。老板马老板是一位六十多岁的壮实男人，现在几乎独自经营着这个地方。现在是下午——在早上老年人群和偶尔的晚间客人之间的安静时段。蒸汽在贴瓷砖的房间里飘荡，他正在擦拭长凳。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 年轻人难得来这种地方了。现在大家都在家洗，或者去那种日式汤泉——灯光暗暗的，放着轻音乐，一个人泡一个池子。我这里不一样，我这里是大通铺，谁都看得见谁。以前的人不觉得这有什么，现在的人受不了。
turns.text_pinyin: Niánqīngrén nándé lái zhè zhǒng dìfang le. Xiànzài dàjiā dōu zài jiā xǐ, huòzhě qù nà zhǒng Rìshì tāngquán — dēngguāng àn àn de, fàng zhe qīng yīnyuè, yīge rén pào yīge chízi. Wǒ zhèlǐ bù yīyàng, wǒ zhèlǐ shì dà tōngpù, shéi dōu kàn de jiàn shéi. Yǐqián de rén bù juéde zhè yǒu shénme, xiànzài de rén shòu bùliǎo.
turns.text_en: It's rare for young people to come to a place like this anymore. Now everyone showers at home, or goes to those Japanese-style hot spring spas — dim lighting, soft music, one person per pool. My place is different — open layout, everyone can see everyone. People in the past thought nothing of it; people now can't handle it.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you say to him?

options.text_zh: 在大通铺里，大家都一样，没有衣服来标识身份。这种平等感，私密的地方给不了。
options.text_pinyin: Zài dà tōngpù lǐ, dàjiā dōu yīyàng, méiyǒu yīfu lái biāozhì shēnfèn. Zhè zhǒng píngděng gǎn, sīmì de dìfang gěi bùliǎo.
options.text_en: In an open bathhouse, everyone is the same — no clothes to signal status. That kind of equality is something private spaces can't give you.
options.feedback: 马老板停下擦台布，点点头：「你说到点上了。就是这个。」

options.text_zh: 日式汤泉挺好的啊，环境比这里好多了。
options.text_pinyin: Rìshì tāngquán tǐng hǎo de a, huánjìng bǐ zhèlǐ hǎo duō le.
options.text_en: Japanese-style hot springs are great though — the atmosphere is much nicer.
options.feedback: 马老板笑了笑，没有反驳，继续擦台。

options.text_zh: 现在大家都很注意个人隐私，所以才不来这种地方。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài dàjiā dōu hěn zhùyì gèrén yǐnsī, suǒyǐ cái bù lái zhè zhǒng dìfang.
options.text_en: Everyone cares so much about personal privacy these days — that's probably why they don't come.
options.feedback: 马老板耸了耸肩：「是，不来了。」他没有接着说什么。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你说到点上了——平等。我记得小时候，我爸带我来澡堂，隔壁泡着的可能是局长，也可能是蹬三轮的。在热水里，谁也不比谁高。我爸说，澡堂是最诚实的地方，因为你什么都藏不住。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ shuō dào diǎn shàng le — píngděng. Wǒ jìde xiǎo shíhòu, wǒ bà dài wǒ lái zǎotáng, gébì pào zhe de kěnéng shì júzhǎng, yě kěnéng shì dēng sānlún de. Zài rè shuǐ lǐ, shéi yě bù bǐ shéi gāo. Wǒ bà shuō, zǎotáng shì zuì chéngshí de dìfang, yīnwèi nǐ shénme dōu cáng bú zhù.
turns.text_en: You've hit it exactly — equality. I remember when I was a kid, my dad would bring me to the bathhouse. The person soaking next to us might be a bureau chief or a rickshaw driver. In hot water, nobody's above anyone. My dad used to say, the bathhouse is the most honest place, because you can't hide anything.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you say to him?

options.text_zh: 「什么都藏不住」——字面上是身体，但我觉得那种氛围里，人也更容易说真话。放松了，防御就下来了。
options.text_pinyin: 'Shénme dōu cáng bú zhù' — zìmiàn shàng shì shēntǐ, dàn wǒ juéde nà zhǒng fēnwéi lǐ, rén yě gèng róngyì shuō zhēnhuà. Fàngsōng le, fángyù jiù xià lái le.
options.text_en: 'Can't hide anything' — literally the body, but I think in that atmosphere people also find it easier to say what's true. Once you relax, the defenses come down.
options.feedback: 马老板把抹布搭在肩上，认真点头：「你说得对。这里谈出来的事，比办公室多得多。」

options.text_zh: 您爸爸还健在吗？他也是开澡堂的吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín bàba hái jiànzài ma? Tā yě shì kāi zǎotáng de ma?
options.text_en: Is your father still alive? Did he also run a bathhouse?
options.feedback: 马老板停了一下：「走了。」他没有继续说下去。

options.text_zh: 局长和蹬三轮的坐在一起——那时候社会差距没那么大？
options.text_pinyin: Júzhǎng hé dēng sānlún de zuò zài yīqǐ — nà shíhòu shèhuì chājù méi nàme dà?
options.text_en: A bureau chief and a rickshaw driver in the same pool — was the gap between people smaller back then?
options.feedback: 马老板想了想：「差距一直都有。就是这里不分。」他继续擦长凳。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对，很多事在澡堂里比在办公室里好谈。因为热水让人放松，放松了防御就下来了。我这些年见过太多了——有人在澡堂里和好了，有人在澡堂里哭了，还有人每天来不是为了洗澡，就是为了有人说话。特别是那些独居的老人。
turns.text_pinyin: Duì, hěn duō shì zài zǎotáng lǐ bǐ zài bàngōngshì lǐ hǎo tán. Yīnwèi rè shuǐ ràng rén fàngsōng, fàngsōng le fángyù jiù xià lái le. Wǒ zhèxiē nián jiàn guò tài duō le — yǒu rén zài zǎotáng lǐ hé hǎo le, yǒu rén zài zǎotáng lǐ kū le, hái yǒu rén měi tiān lái bú shì wèile xǐzǎo, jiù shì wèile yǒu rén shuōhuà. Tèbié shì nàxiē dújū de lǎorén.
turns.text_en: Right, many things are easier to discuss in a bathhouse than in an office. Because hot water relaxes people, and once relaxed, defenses come down. Over the years I've seen too much — people reconciling in the bathhouse, people crying in the bathhouse, and people who come every day not to bathe, but just to have someone to talk to. Especially the elderly who live alone.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you say to him?

options.text_zh: 对那些老人来说，每天来泡个澡、跟人聊几句，可能是一整天唯一的社交。如果这里关了，那个空缺很难补上。
options.text_pinyin: Duì nàxiē lǎorén lái shuō, měi tiān lái pào ge zǎo, gēn rén liáo jǐ jù, kěnéng shì yī zhěng tiān wéiyī de shèjiāo. Rúguǒ zhèlǐ guān le, nàge kòngquē hěn nán bǔ shàng.
options.text_en: For those elderly people, a daily soak and a few words with someone might be their only social contact all day. If this place closes, that gap would be hard to fill.
options.feedback: 马老板把抹布放下，叹了口气：「这我知道。所以我一直没舍得关。」

options.text_zh: 老人们可以去社区活动中心啊，那里也有人陪。
options.text_pinyin: Lǎorén men kěyǐ qù shèqū huódòng zhōngxīn a, nàlǐ yě yǒu rén péi.
options.text_en: The elderly can go to community activity centers — there are people there to spend time with.
options.feedback: 马老板摇摇头：「活动中心要报名，要参加活动。这里不用。进来坐着就行。」

options.text_zh: 您这里的卫生条件怎么样？定期消毒吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín zhèlǐ de wèishēng tiáojiàn zěnmeyàng? Dìngqī xiāodú ma?
options.text_en: What are the hygiene conditions like here? Do you disinfect regularly?
options.feedback: 马老板直起腰，认真地说：「每天。卫生局每年来查，从来没出过事。」

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 无声的隔离——你说得真准。有个老李头，七十多岁了，老婆走了五年了，儿子在深圳。他每天下午两点准时来，泡到四点。有一次我问他为什么每天都来，他说「因为水是热的，人是活的」。就这两个条件——热水和活人——在家里他一个都没有。
turns.text_pinyin: Wúshēng de gélí — nǐ shuō de zhēn zhǔn. Yǒu ge Lǎo Lǐ tóu, qīshí duō suì le, lǎopó zǒu le wǔ nián le, érzi zài Shēnzhèn. Tā měi tiān xiàwǔ liǎng diǎn zhǔnshí lái, pào dào sì diǎn. Yǒu yī cì wǒ wèn tā wèishéme měi tiān dōu lái, tā shuō 'yīnwèi shuǐ shì rè de, rén shì huó de'. Jiù zhè liǎng ge tiáojiàn — rè shuǐ hé huó rén — zài jiā lǐ tā yīge dōu méiyǒu.
turns.text_en: Silent isolation — that's exactly right. There's an Old Li, over seventy, wife passed five years ago, son's in Shenzhen. He comes at exactly two every afternoon, soaks until four. Once I asked him why he comes every day. He said, 'Because the water is hot and the people are alive.' Just those two conditions — hot water and living people — at home he has neither.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you say to him?

options.text_zh: 「水是热的，人是活的」——就这两件事。这可能是我听过的对孤独最准确的描述。马老板，对他来说，您这里不是消费的地方，是他每天的理由。
options.text_pinyin: 'Shuǐ shì rè de, rén shì huó de' — jiù zhè liǎng jiàn shì. Zhè kěnéng shì wǒ tīng guò de duì gūdú zuì zhǔnquè de miáoshù. Mǎ lǎobǎn, duì tā lái shuō, nín zhèlǐ bú shì xiāofèi de dìfang, shì tā měi tiān de lǐyóu.
options.text_en: 'The water is hot and the people are alive' — that might be the most accurate description of loneliness I've heard. Boss Ma, for him this isn't somewhere to spend money — it's his reason to get up each day.
options.feedback: 马老板沉默了一会儿，轻声说：「所以我不关。」

options.text_zh: 他儿子应该多回来看看他，不能光在外面挣钱。
options.text_pinyin: Tā érzi yīnggāi duō huílái kàn kan tā, bù néng guāng zài wàimiàn zhèngqián.
options.text_en: His son should visit more — you can't just be out there making money.
options.feedback: 马老板点点头：「是。但儿子也不容易。」话题就此打住。

options.text_zh: 他可以养只猫或者狗，有个伴就好了。
options.text_pinyin: Tā kěyǐ yǎng zhī māo huòzhě gǒu, yǒu ge bàn jiù hǎo le.
options.text_en: He could get a cat or dog — having a companion would help.
options.feedback: 马老板想了想：「他说过，老了养不动了。」

tree.cultural_note: Traditional public bathhouses (澡堂 zǎotáng) were once essential social infrastructure in Chinese cities, serving as spaces of hygiene, relaxation, and community before most homes had private bathing facilities. Their decline reflects both improved housing and a cultural shift toward privacy and individualism. For elderly residents — particularly men who grew up with communal bathing culture — the bathhouse remains one of the last remaining 'third places' where social bonds are maintained without the formality of organized activities.

tree.setup: In a narrow lane off a quiet Shanghai street, you find a tiny watch repair shop that seems out of time. The clockmaker, Master Zhong, is in his seventies. Dozens of old clocks line the walls, ticking at different speeds. He's examining a pocket watch with a magnifying loupe. Strangely, the clock above his workbench has no hands.
tree.setup_zh: 在上海一条安静街道旁的窄巷里，你找到了一家仿佛脱离了时间的小型钟表修理店。钟表匠钟师傅七十多岁了。数十只老钟排列在墙上，以不同的速度滴答作响。他正用放大镜检查一只怀表。奇怪的是，他工作台上方的钟没有指针。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你注意到那个没有指针的钟了？那是我故意的。修了一辈子钟表，退休以后第一件事就是把自己的钟卸了指针。别人觉得奇怪，但我觉得这是我送给自己的自由——不再被时间追着跑。钟还在走，你听得到机芯的声音，但你不需要知道几点了。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhùyì dào nàge méiyǒu zhǐzhēn de zhōng le? Nà shì wǒ gùyì de. Xiū le yī bèizi zhōngbiǎo, tuìxiū yǐhòu dì yī jiàn shì jiù shì bǎ zìjǐ de zhōng xiè le zhǐzhēn. Biérén juéde qíguài, dàn wǒ juéde zhè shì wǒ sòng gěi zìjǐ de zìyóu — bú zài bèi shíjiān zhuī zhe pǎo. Zhōng hái zài zǒu, nǐ tīng de dào jīxīn de shēngyīn, dàn nǐ bù xūyào zhīdào jǐ diǎn le.
turns.text_en: You noticed the clock with no hands? That was deliberate. After a lifetime of repairing clocks, the first thing I did when I retired was remove the hands from my own clock. Others find it strange, but I think it's a gift of freedom I gave myself — no longer chased by time. The clock still runs — you can hear the movement — but you don't need to know what time it is.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you say to him?

options.text_zh: 您没有让钟停下来——那是否定时间。您只是卸掉了指针——那是从时间的计量中退出。这两者不一样。
options.text_pinyin: Nín méiyǒu ràng zhōng tíng xiàlái — nà shì fǒudìng shíjiān. Nín zhǐ shì xiè diào le zhǐzhēn — nà shì cóng shíjiān de jìliàng zhōng tuìchū. Zhè liǎng zhě bù yīyàng.
options.text_en: You didn't stop the clock — that would be denying time. You just removed the hands — that's withdrawing from time's measurement. Those two things are different.
options.feedback: 钟师傅放下放大镜，对你看了一会儿：「你说出了我自己都没说清楚的事。」

options.text_zh: 没有指针怎么知道什么时候吃饭？
options.text_pinyin: Méiyǒu zhǐzhēn zěnme zhīdào shénme shíhòu chīfàn?
options.text_en: Without hands, how do you know when to eat?
options.feedback: 钟师傅轻声笑了：「饿了就吃。」他低头继续看怀表。

options.text_zh: 这些老钟放在这里，是等着有人来修的吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhèxiē lǎo zhōng fàng zài zhèlǐ, shì děng zhe yǒu rén lái xiū de ma?
options.text_en: Are all these old clocks here waiting for someone to bring them in for repair?
options.feedback: 钟师傅扫了一眼墙上的钟：「有些是。有些是我自己的。」他没有展开。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 和解——对，就是和解。我年轻的时候恨时间，觉得它不够用。中年的时候怕时间，觉得它过得太快。现在老了，反而不急了。不是因为时间变慢了，而是因为我不再跟它赛跑了。你知道吗，所有的钟表其实都在说同一句话：「来不及了」。但我的这个没有指针的钟在说：「不急」。
turns.text_pinyin: Héjiě — duì, jiù shì héjiě. Wǒ niánqīng de shíhòu hèn shíjiān, juéde tā búgòu yòng. Zhōngnián de shíhòu pà shíjiān, juéde tā guò de tài kuài. Xiànzài lǎo le, fǎn'ér bú jí le. Bú shì yīnwèi shíjiān biàn màn le, ér shì yīnwèi wǒ bú zài gēn tā sàipǎo le. Nǐ zhīdào ma, suǒyǒu de zhōngbiǎo qíshí dōu zài shuō tóng yī jù huà: 'láibují le'. Dàn wǒ de zhège méiyǒu zhǐzhēn de zhōng zài shuō: 'bú jí'.
turns.text_en: Making peace — yes, exactly. When I was young I resented time, felt there wasn't enough. In middle age I feared time, felt it passed too fast. Now that I'm old, I'm not in a hurry anymore. Not because time slowed down, but because I stopped racing it. You know, all clocks are actually saying the same thing: 'Too late.' But my handless clock says: 'No rush.'

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you say to him?

options.text_zh: 我们看时间不是为了知道现在几点，而是为了算还剩多少。所有的计时器其实都是倒计时器。您把指针卸掉，钟就不再告诉您还剩多少了——只告诉您它还在转。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒmen kàn shíjiān bú shì wèile zhīdào xiànzài jǐ diǎn, ér shì wèile suàn hái shèng duōshǎo. Suǒyǒu de jìshíqì qíshí dōu shì dàojìshí qì. Nín bǎ zhǐzhēn xiè diào, zhōng jiù bú zài gàosù nín hái shèng duōshǎo le — zhǐ gàosù nín tā hái zài zhuǎn.
options.text_en: We check time not to know the hour, but to calculate how much is left. Every timer is a countdown. Remove the hands and the clock stops telling how much remains — it only says it's still going.
options.feedback: 钟师傅慢慢点头，目光落在那只没有指针的钟上：「倒计时器。是这样。」

options.text_zh: 您退休以后主要做什么？还在接修钟的单子吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín tuìxiū yǐhòu zhǔyào zuò shénme? Hái zài jiē xiū zhōng de dānzi ma?
options.text_en: What do you mainly do since retiring? Are you still taking repair orders?
options.feedback: 钟师傅指了指工作台上的怀表：「还接。但不赶了。」

options.text_zh: 您年轻的时候一定修了很多名表吧？
options.text_pinyin: Nín niánqīng de shíhòu yīdìng xiū le hěn duō míng biǎo ba?
options.text_en: You must have repaired a lot of famous watches in your younger years?
options.feedback: 钟师傅摇摇头：「名表也好，便宜表也好，心都一样不好受。」他岔开了话题。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 倒计时器——你说得太对了。但你知道我修钟表这么多年最大的感悟是什么吗？每一只钟表都有自己的性格。不是我在修它，是我在听它——听它哪里不舒服、哪个齿轮在挣扎。好的修理师不是技术好，是耐心好。你要等钟表自己告诉你问题在哪里。
turns.text_pinyin: Dàojìshí qì — nǐ shuō de tài duì le. Dàn nǐ zhīdào wǒ xiū zhōngbiǎo zhème duō nián zuìdà de gǎnwù shì shénme ma? Měi yī zhī zhōngbiǎo dōu yǒu zìjǐ de xìnggé. Bú shì wǒ zài xiū tā, shì wǒ zài tīng tā — tīng tā nǎlǐ bú shūfu, nǎge chǐlún zài zhēngzhá. Hǎo de xiūlǐ shī bú shì jìshù hǎo, shì nàixīn hǎo. Nǐ yào děng zhōngbiǎo zìjǐ gàosù nǐ wèntí zài nǎlǐ.
turns.text_en: Countdown timer — you're absolutely right. But do you know what my biggest insight is from all these years of repairing clocks? Every clock has its own personality. I'm not repairing it — I'm listening to it. Listening to where it's uncomfortable, which gear is struggling. A good repairman isn't skilled, he's patient. You have to wait for the clock to tell you where the problem is.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you say to him?

options.text_zh: 不急着修，先去听——这不只是修钟的方法吧。好像对人也是这样：先理解，再行动，解决方案往往自己就浮现了。
options.text_pinyin: Bù jí zhe xiū, xiān qù tīng — zhè bù zhǐ shì xiū zhōng de fāngfǎ ba. Hǎoxiàng duì rén yě shì zhèyàng: xiān lǐjiě, zài xíngdòng, jiějué fāng'àn wǎngwǎng zìjǐ jiù fúxiàn le.
options.text_en: Don't rush to fix it, just listen first — that's not only about clocks, is it. Seems true with people too: understand first, then act, and the solution often surfaces on its own.
options.feedback: 钟师傅把怀表放到一边，抬起头：「你说到了。我对人，也是这样。」

options.text_zh: 现在修钟表的年轻人越来越少了，以后这个手艺怎么传？
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài xiū zhōngbiǎo de niánqīngrén yuè lái yuè shǎo le, yǐhòu zhège shǒuyì zěnme chuán?
options.text_en: Fewer and fewer young people repair clocks now — how will this craft be passed on?
options.feedback: 钟师傅叹了口气：「传不下去，就算了。」他没有很在乎的样子。

options.text_zh: 智能手表坏了一般直接换，您这里接吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhìnéng shǒubiǎo huài le yībān zhíjiē huàn, nín zhèlǐ jiē ma?
options.text_en: Most people just replace a smartwatch when it breaks — do you take those here?
options.feedback: 钟师傅苦笑了一下：「那种东西，我打不开。」他重新拿起放大镜。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你说到了我心里。其实我对人也是这样。我老伴身体不好的那几年，我每天陪着她，不说什么，就是在旁边。她走了以后，我才发现陪伴不需要做什么，在场就够了。就像这些钟——你不需要给它们上发条，它们只是需要有人偶尔来听一听。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ shuō dào le wǒ xīnlǐ. Qíshí wǒ duì rén yě shì zhèyàng. Wǒ lǎobàn shēntǐ bù hǎo de nà jǐ nián, wǒ měi tiān péi zhe tā, bù shuō shénme, jiù shì zài pángbiān. Tā zǒu le yǐhòu, wǒ cái fāxiàn péibàn bù xūyào zuò shénme, zàichǎng jiù gòu le. Jiù xiàng zhèxiē zhōng — nǐ bù xūyào gěi tāmen shàng fātiáo, tāmen zhǐ shì xūyào yǒu rén ǒu'ěr lái tīng yī tīng.
turns.text_en: You've touched my heart. Actually, I'm the same way with people. During the years my wife was unwell, I sat with her every day, not saying anything, just being there. After she passed, I realized that companionship doesn't require doing anything — being present is enough. Like these clocks — you don't need to wind them, they just need someone to come listen once in a while.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you say to him?

options.text_zh: 听钟，陪妻子，守着这间小店——钟师傅，您这一辈子学的好像只有一件事：怎么好好地「在」。不追，不催，只是安静地在场。
options.text_pinyin: Tīng zhōng, péi qīzi, shǒu zhe zhè jiān xiǎo diàn — Zhōng shīfu, nín zhè yī bèizi xué de hǎoxiàng zhǐ yǒu yī jiàn shì: zěnme hǎohǎo de 'zài'. Bù zhuī, bù cuī, zhǐ shì ānjìng de zàichǎng.
options.text_en: Listening to clocks, sitting with your wife, keeping this little shop — Master Zhong, it seems like you've spent your whole life learning just one thing: how to truly be present. Not chasing, not rushing, just quietly there.
options.feedback: 钟师傅没有立刻说话。他看了看墙上那些滴答作响的钟，最后轻声说：「是。就这一件事。」

options.text_zh: 您一个人住不觉得寂寞吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín yīge rén zhù bù juéde jìmò ma?
options.text_en: Don't you feel lonely living alone?
options.feedback: 钟师傅朝墙上的钟摆了摆手：「有它们。」

options.text_zh: 这间店的租金贵吗？在上海开店不容易吧。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè jiān diàn de zūjīn guì ma? Zài Shànghǎi kāi diàn bù róngyì ba.
options.text_en: Is the rent for this shop expensive? Running a shop in Shanghai can't be easy.
options.feedback: 钟师傅想了想：「还行。房东也老了，不怎么涨。」他把话题带走了。

tree.cultural_note: Clock repair shops were once common in Chinese cities, where mechanical watches and clocks were valued possessions requiring regular maintenance. The decline of this trade mirrors broader shifts in consumer culture — from repairing to replacing, from mechanical to digital. The philosophical dimension of the clockmaker's relationship with time resonates with Daoist concepts of flowing with natural rhythms rather than measuring and controlling them.

tree.setup: You're at a dinner with a client, Mr. Huang, and your boss, Director Zhao. It's time to order. Everyone says '随便' (whatever) but nobody means it. Mr. Huang doesn't eat spicy food but won't say so. Director Zhao is vegetarian this month but hasn't announced it. You're the most junior person at the table, which means you're supposed to order, which means you'll be blamed if it's wrong.
tree.setup_zh: 你跟客户黄总、你的上司赵主任在餐厅吃饭。该点菜了。每个人都说'随便'，但没有人是真的随便。黄总不吃辣但不会说，赵主任这个月吃素但没宣布。你是桌上最年轻的，所以应该你来点，点错了就是你的问题。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 来来来，不用客气，随便点，我什么都行。黄总，您看看有什么想吃的？
turns.text_pinyin: Lái lái lái, bú yòng kèqi, suíbiàn diǎn, wǒ shénme dōu xíng. Huáng zǒng, nín kànkan yǒu shénme xiǎng chī de?
turns.text_en: Come come come, don't be polite, just order whatever, I'm fine with anything. Mr. Huang, see anything you'd like?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 赵主任太客气了。我也随便，你们点就好，我不挑食的。就是——最近胃不太好，太油腻的就别点了。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhào zhǔrèn tài kèqi le. Wǒ yě suíbiàn, nǐmen diǎn jiù hǎo, wǒ bù tiāoshí de. Jiùshì — zuìjìn wèi bú tài hǎo, tài yóunì de jiù bié diǎn le.
turns.text_en: Director Zhao is too kind. I'm easy too, you all order, I'm not picky. Just — my stomach hasn't been great lately, so nothing too greasy.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Two 随便's and one dietary restriction disguised as a health complaint. You need to navigate this.

options.text_zh: 那咱们先来几个清淡的？我看这家的清蒸鲈鱼不错，再来一个凉拌黄瓜开开胃。黄总，您平时口味偏清淡还是……？
options.text_pinyin: Nà zánmen xiān lái jǐ ge qīngdàn de? Wǒ kàn zhè jiā de qīngzhēng lúyú búcuò, zài lái yīge liángbàn huángguā kāi kāi wèi. Huáng zǒng, nín píngshí kǒuwèi piān qīngdàn háishì ...?
options.text_en: How about we start with some lighter dishes? The steamed bass here looks good, and a cold cucumber to start. Mr. Huang, do you generally prefer lighter flavors or...?
options.feedback: You've responded to the unspoken no-grease request, proposed safe dishes, and given Mr. Huang an opening to reveal his spice preference without having to volunteer it.

options.text_zh: 黄总，赵主任，你们随便点吧，我吃什么都行。
options.text_pinyin: Huáng zǒng, Zhào zhǔrèn, nǐmen suíbiàn diǎn ba, wǒ chī shénme dōu xíng.
options.text_en: Mr. Huang, Director Zhao, please just order, I'm fine with anything.
options.feedback: Three people have now said 随便. The menu is open, the waiter is waiting, and nobody has ordered anything. You've added a third layer of polite paralysis.

options.text_zh: 这家的水煮鱼很有名，我们来一份？再来一个麻婆豆腐？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè jiā de shuǐzhǔ yú hěn yǒumíng, wǒmen lái yī fèn? Zài lái yīge mápó dòufu?
options.text_en: Their boiled fish in chili oil is famous, shall we get that? And some mapo tofu?
options.feedback: Both dishes are aggressively spicy. Mr. Huang's 'stomach issues' were code for 'no spicy food.' You've ordered exactly what he can't eat.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 清蒸鲈鱼好，清淡又有档次。不过光吃鱼不够吧？要不要来个肉菜？我听说他们的红烧排骨做得不错——当然，如果黄总觉得太油就算了。
turns.text_pinyin: Qīngzhēng lúyú hǎo, qīngdàn yòu yǒu dàngcì. Búguò guāng chī yú bú gòu ba? Yào bú yào lái ge ròu cài? Wǒ tīngshuō tāmen de hóngshāo páigǔ zuò de búcuò — dāngrán, rúguǒ Huáng zǒng juéde tài yóu jiù suàn le.
turns.text_en: Steamed bass is good, light but classy. But just fish isn't enough, is it? Should we add a meat dish? I heard their braised ribs are good — of course, if Mr. Huang thinks it's too greasy, we'll skip it.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 排骨没问题没问题！我就是怕你们迁就我。其实我什么都能吃，真的。对了——你们喝酒吗？我今天可以少喝一点。
turns.text_pinyin: Páigǔ méi wèntí méi wèntí! Wǒ jiùshì pà nǐmen qiānjiù wǒ. Qíshí wǒ shénme dōu néng chī, zhēn de. Duì le — nǐmen hē jiǔ ma? Wǒ jīntiān kěyǐ shǎo hē yīdiǎn.
turns.text_en: Ribs are no problem! I was just worried you'd accommodate me. Actually I can eat anything, really. Oh — are you drinking? I can have a little today.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Mr. Huang just said 'I can have a little' which means he wants to drink but needs someone else to initiate. Your boss hasn't said whether he's drinking.

options.text_zh: 赵主任，今天难得跟黄总一起吃饭，要不要来一瓶白酒？当然如果您赶时间，喝啤酒也挺好的。黄总，您看呢？
options.text_pinyin: Zhào zhǔrèn, jīntiān nándé gēn Huáng zǒng yīqǐ chīfàn, yào bú yào lái yī píng báijiǔ? Dāngrán rúguǒ nín gǎn shíjiān, hē píjiǔ yě tǐng hǎo de. Huáng zǒng, nín kàn ne?
options.text_en: Director Zhao, it's rare to dine with Mr. Huang, shall we get a bottle of baijiu? Of course, if you're pressed for time, beer works too. Mr. Huang, what do you think?
options.feedback: You've put the decision on the boss, given him an easy downgrade to beer, and bounced it to the client. Everyone gets to look like they're deferring while the bottle is already on its way.

options.text_zh: 我不喝酒，你们随意吧。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bù hē jiǔ, nǐmen suíyì ba.
options.text_en: I don't drink, you go ahead.
options.feedback: In a client dinner, refusing to drink without offering an alternative changes the tone of the table. Mr. Huang's 'I can have a little' was an invitation, not a weather report.

options.text_zh: 来两瓶白酒吧，今天好好喝！
options.text_pinyin: Lái liǎng píng báijiǔ ba, jīntiān hǎohǎo hē!
options.text_en: Let's get two bottles of baijiu, let's drink well tonight!
options.feedback: Mr. Huang said 'a little.' Your boss hasn't said anything. You're the junior person ordering two bottles. This is deciding the drinking pace for both superiors.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 白酒可以，不过别喝太多，明天还要上班嘛。来半斤就行了。你看，菜也点了，酒也有了。小伙子安排得不错。
turns.text_pinyin: Báijiǔ kěyǐ, búguò bié hē tài duō, míngtiān hái yào shàngbān ma. Lái bàn jīn jiù xíng le. Nǐ kàn, cài yě diǎn le, jiǔ yě yǒu le. Xiǎohuǒzi ānpái de búcuò.
turns.text_en: Baijiu works, but let's not overdo it, we still have work tomorrow. Half a jin is enough. See, food's ordered, drinks are set. The young one arranged things nicely.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Director Zhao just publicly approved your ordering. Mr. Huang is nodding. You've survived the minefield. Close it out.

options.text_zh: 哪里哪里，都是赵主任和黄总好说话。要是你们谁有什么忌口的直接说啊——不然我就要开始猜了，猜错了可就不是点菜的问题了，是读心术的问题了。
options.text_pinyin: Nǎlǐ nǎlǐ, dōu shì Zhào zhǔrèn hé Huáng zǒng hǎo shuōhuà. Yàoshi nǐmen shéi yǒu shénme jìkǒu de zhíjiē shuō a — bùrán wǒ jiù yào kāishǐ cāi le, cāi cuò le kě jiù bú shì diǎncài de wèntí le, shì dúxīnshù de wèntí le.
options.text_en: Director Zhao and Mr. Huang are easy to work with. If anyone has dietary restrictions, say so directly — otherwise I'll have to guess, and if I guess wrong, it's not ordering anymore, it's mind-reading.
options.feedback: Self-deprecating, names the 随便 game without accusing anyone of playing it, and gets a laugh. The mind-reading line gives them permission to actually state preferences next time.

options.text_zh: 谢谢赵主任！希望菜合大家胃口。
options.text_pinyin: Xièxie Zhào zhǔrèn! Xīwàng cài hé dàjiā wèikǒu.
options.text_en: Thank you Director Zhao! I hope the food suits everyone's taste.
options.feedback: Safe and correct, but after navigating a three-way diplomatic negotiation disguised as dinner ordering, a stock phrase feels like you don't know what just happened.

options.text_zh: 不够的话我们再加。赵主任，还有什么需要的吗？
options.text_pinyin: Bú gòu de huà wǒmen zài jiā. Zhào zhǔrèn, hái yǒu shénme xūyào de ma?
options.text_en: We can add more if it's not enough. Director Zhao, anything else needed?
options.feedback: You've just closed a deal and you're reopening the menu. Let the order stand. Every new question is a new chance for someone to say 随便 again.

tree.cultural_note: Group dinner ordering (点菜) in Chinese business culture is an elaborate status negotiation. The most junior person typically orders, but must read unspoken preferences from senior diners who will only say '随便' (whatever). Dietary restrictions are often expressed indirectly through health complaints rather than direct statements. The phrase '少喝一点' (drink a little less) is rarely a statement of intent — it's a signal that drinking will happen. Successfully navigating a dinner order demonstrates social intelligence (情商) more clearly than any presentation.

tree.setup: You're having tea with Ms. Jiang, a recently retired high school Chinese literature teacher, in her small apartment filled with student letters. After thirty years in the classroom, she left because she could no longer reconcile her love of literature with what the education system demanded. She seems at peace with her decision but carries the weight of something unresolved.
tree.setup_zh: 你在蒋老师——一位刚退休的高中语文老师——的小公寓里喝茶。公寓里堆满了学生的来信。在课堂上教了三十年后，她离开了，因为她无法再调和自己对文学的热爱与教育体制的要求。她对自己的决定看似平静，但似乎还有什么未解的心结。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你知道我最后一堂课上了什么吗？鲁迅的《故乡》。我问学生：「闰土为什么变了？」标准答案是封建社会的压迫。但有个学生说：「因为他长大了，长大就是这样的。」我觉得那个回答比标准答案深刻一百倍，但考试只认标准答案。那一刻我突然觉得，我不是在教文学，我是在教应试技巧。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhīdào wǒ zuìhòu yī táng kè shàng le shénme ma? Lǔ Xùn de 'Gùxiāng'. Wǒ wèn xuéshēng: 'Rùntǔ wèishéme biàn le?' Biāozhǔn dá'àn shì fēngjiàn shèhuì de yāpò. Dàn yǒu ge xuéshēng shuō: 'Yīnwèi tā zhǎng dà le, zhǎng dà jiù shì zhèyàng de.' Wǒ juéde nàge huídá bǐ biāozhǔn dá'àn shēnkè yībǎi bèi, dàn kǎoshì zhǐ rèn biāozhǔn dá'àn. Nà yī kè wǒ tūrán juéde, wǒ bú shì zài jiāo wénxué, wǒ shì zài jiāo yìngshì jìqiǎo.
turns.text_en: Know what my last class was about? Lu Xun's 'My Old Home.' I asked: 'Why did Runtu change?' Standard answer: feudal oppression. But one student said: 'Because he grew up — that's what growing up is like.' A hundred times more profound. But exams only accept the standard answer. That moment I realized: I wasn't teaching literature, I was teaching test-taking.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you say to her?

options.text_zh: 那个学生用自己的经验去读文本，而不是套别人给的框架。但考试奖励的恰恰不是这个——它奖励的是你能不能准确地复现出那个框架。
options.text_pinyin: Nàge xuéshēng yòng zìjǐ de jīngyàn qù dú wénběn, ér bú shì tào biérén gěi de kuàngjià. Dàn kǎoshì jiǎnglì de qiàqià bú shì zhège — tā jiǎnglì de shì nǐ néng bù néng zhǔnquè de fùxiàn chū nàge kuàngjià.
options.text_en: That student read the text through his own experience, not someone else's framework. But the exam rewards exactly the opposite — it rewards whether you can accurately reproduce that framework.
options.feedback: 蒋老师放下茶杯，直起身：「对。准确复现。三十年里我改了多少份这样的卷子。」

options.text_zh: 但标准答案也是有道理的——封建压迫确实是鲁迅的主题之一。
options.text_pinyin: Dàn biāozhǔn dá'àn yě shì yǒu dàolǐ de — fēngjiàn yāpò quèshí shì Lǔ Xùn de zhǔtí zhī yī.
options.text_en: But the standard answer isn't wrong — feudal oppression really is one of Lu Xun's themes.
options.feedback: 蒋老师点点头：「没错。它也对。但那个学生的答案，也对。」她等着你往下想。

options.text_zh: 现在高考改革了很多，主观题的比重也在提高吧？
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài gāokǎo gǎigé le hěn duō, zhǔguān tí de bǐzhòng yě zài tígāo ba?
options.text_en: The gaokao has been reformed a lot — the proportion of subjective questions has been increasing, hasn't it?
options.feedback: 蒋老师苦笑：「主观题有标准答案。这个改革我等了三十年。」

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 服从性——你这个词用得太准了。三十年里，我看着一届又一届学生，进来的时候对世界充满好奇，出去的时候只关心分数。我不怪学生，也不完全怪体制——这是一个系统性的问题。当十三亿人需要一个「公平」的筛选机制，标准化就成了唯一的选择。但公平和深度之间，我们选了公平，代价就是扼杀了深度。
turns.text_pinyin: Fúcóngxìng — nǐ zhège cí yòng de tài zhǔn le. Sānshí nián lǐ, wǒ kàn zhe yī jiè yòu yī jiè xuéshēng, jìnlái de shíhòu duì shìjiè chōngmǎn hàoqí, chūqù de shíhòu zhǐ guānxīn fēnshù. Wǒ bú guài xuéshēng, yě bù wánquán guài tǐzhì — zhè shì yīge xìtǒngxìng de wèntí. Dāng shísān yì rén xūyào yīge 'gōngpíng' de shāixuǎn jīzhì, biāozhǔnhuà jiù chéng le wéiyī de xuǎnzé. Dàn gōngpíng hé shēndù zhī jiān, wǒmen xuǎn le gōngpíng, dàijià jiù shì èshā le shēndù.
turns.text_en: Compliance — that word is too accurate. Over thirty years, I watched class after class of students enter full of curiosity about the world and leave caring only about scores. I don't blame the students, and I don't entirely blame the system — it's a systemic problem. When 1.3 billion people need a 'fair' screening mechanism, standardization becomes the only option. But between fairness and depth, we chose fairness, and the cost was strangling depth.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you say to her?

options.text_zh: 任何允许主观判断的系统都会引入偏见。所以我能理解为什么选了标准化。只是——即使考试制度没法改，课堂上能不能让学生知道，那些不能得分的答案，有时候更真实？
options.text_pinyin: Rènhé yǔnxǔ zhǔguān pànduàn de xìtǒng dōu huì yǐnrù piānjiàn. Suǒyǐ wǒ néng lǐjiě wèishéme xuǎn le biāozhǔnhuà. Zhǐ shì — jíshǐ kǎoshì zhìdù méifǎ gǎi, kètáng shàng néng bu néng ràng xuéshēng zhīdào, nàxiē bù néng dé fēn de dá'àn, yǒu shíhòu gèng zhēnshí?
options.text_en: Any system allowing subjective judgment introduces bias — so I understand why standardization won. But even if the exam can't change, can the classroom at least let students know that unscored answers are sometimes the more honest ones?
options.feedback: 蒋老师慢慢点头：「我试过。」她停顿了一下。「这就是后来发生的事。」

options.text_zh: 那你为什么不去开自己的补习班？自己当老板，可以想怎么教就怎么教。
options.text_pinyin: Nà nǐ wèishéme bú qù kāi zìjǐ de bǔxí bān? Zìjǐ dāng lǎobǎn, kěyǐ xiǎng zěnme jiāo jiù zěnme jiāo.
options.text_en: Why not start your own tutoring center? Be your own boss — teach however you want.
options.feedback: 蒋老师轻声说：「补习班的家长，比学校家长更在乎分数。」

options.text_zh: 国外的教育体制也有很多问题，不只是中国这样。
options.text_pinyin: Guówài de jiàoyù tǐzhì yě yǒu hěn duō wèntí, bù zhǐ shì Zhōngguó zhèyàng.
options.text_en: Education systems abroad have plenty of problems too — this isn't unique to China.
options.feedback: 蒋老师笑了笑：「可能。但我教的是这里的孩子。」

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 体制的缝隙……我确实试过。有一段时间，我在课堂上花十分钟讲标准答案，剩下的时间带学生做自由讨论。但后来被家长投诉了——说我浪费时间，不讲考点。校长找我谈话，说理解我的想法，但让我「配合一下」。这个「配合一下」就是三十年——我配合了三十年，最后连自己都配合不了了。
turns.text_pinyin: Tǐzhì de fèngxì…… wǒ quèshí shì guò. Yǒu yī duàn shíjiān, wǒ zài kètáng shàng huā shí fēnzhōng jiǎng biāozhǔn dá'àn, shèng xià de shíjiān dài xuéshēng zuò zìyóu tǎolùn. Dàn hòulái bèi jiāzhǎng tóusù le — shuō wǒ làngfèi shíjiān, bù jiǎng kǎodiǎn. Xiàozhǎng zhǎo wǒ tánhuà, shuō lǐjiě wǒ de xiǎngfǎ, dàn ràng wǒ 'pèihé yīxià'. Zhège 'pèihé yīxià' jiù shì sānshí nián — wǒ pèihé le sānshí nián, zuìhòu lián zìjǐ dōu pèihé bùliǎo le.
turns.text_en: Cracks in the system… I did try. For a while, I spent ten minutes per class on the standard answers and used the remaining time for free discussion. But then parents complained — said I was wasting time, not covering test points. The principal talked to me, said he understood my ideas but asked me to 'cooperate a little.' That 'cooperate a little' lasted thirty years — I cooperated for thirty years, until finally I couldn't even cooperate with myself.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you say to her?

options.text_zh: 「配合一下」这四个字很可怕——它不是命令你改变，而是温和地请求你让步。每次让步都很小，小到觉得不值得抗争。三十年累积下来，就变成了一种自我背叛。
options.text_pinyin: 'Pèihé yīxià' zhè sì ge zì hěn kěpà — tā bú shì mìnglìng nǐ gǎibiàn, ér shì wēnhé de qǐngqiú nǐ ràngbù. Měi cì ràngbù dōu hěn xiǎo, xiǎo dào juéde bù zhíde kàngzhēng. Sānshí nián lěijī xiàlái, jiù biàn chéng le yī zhǒng zìwǒ bèipàn.
options.text_en: 'Cooperate a little' — insidious. Not an order to change, a gentle request to yield. Each concession so small it's not worth fighting. Thirty years of that accumulates into self-betrayal.
options.feedback: 蒋老师握着茶杯，看着桌面，过了一会儿才说：「自我背叛。对。就是这种感觉。」

options.text_zh: 其实每个行业都是这样的，不只是教育。
options.text_pinyin: Qíshí měige hángyè dōu shì zhèyàng de, bù zhǐ shì jiàoyù.
options.text_en: Actually every field is like this — it's not just education.
options.feedback: 蒋老师沉默了一下：「可能。但我说的是这三十年。」

options.text_zh: 您现在可以写一本关于教育的书，肯定有很多人想看。
options.text_pinyin: Nín xiànzài kěyǐ xiě yī běn guānyú jiàoyù de shū, kěndìng yǒu hěn duō rén xiǎng kàn.
options.text_en: Now you could write a book about education — I'm sure a lot of people would want to read it.
options.feedback: 蒋老师淡淡地笑了：「书。出版社也要审的。」

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 自我背叛……对，就是这种感觉。但你知道让我最矛盾的是什么吗？我的学生里有好几个考上了北大、清华，后来都过得不错。从结果来看，这个系统是「有效」的。但有效不等于正确。那些被筛掉的学生呢？那些不适应标准化的孩子呢？系统只展示它的成功案例，失败案例是沉默的。
turns.text_pinyin: Zìwǒ bèipàn…… duì, jiù shì zhè zhǒng gǎnjué. Dàn nǐ zhīdào ràng wǒ zuì máodùn de shì shénme ma? Wǒ de xuéshēng lǐ yǒu hǎo jǐ ge kǎo shàng le Běidà, Qīnghuá, hòulái dōu guò de búcuò. Cóng jiéguǒ lái kàn, zhège xìtǒng shì 'yǒuxiào' de. Dàn yǒuxiào bù děngyú zhèngquè. Nàxiē bèi shāi diào de xuéshēng ne? Nàxiē bù shìyìng biāozhǔnhuà de háizi ne? Xìtǒng zhǐ zhǎnshì tā de chénggōng ànlì, shībài ànlì shì chénmò de.
turns.text_en: Self-betrayal… yes, that's exactly it. But you know what troubles me most? Several of my students got into Peking University, Tsinghua, and went on to do well. From the results, the system 'works.' But effective doesn't mean right. What about the students who were filtered out? The children who couldn't adapt to standardization? The system only displays its success stories — the failure cases are silent.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you say to her?

options.text_zh: 我们用考上北大清华的故事来证明系统有效，但从来不追问被淘汰的人去了哪里。而且——即使是那些「成功」的学生，他们在这个过程中失去了什么？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒmen yòng kǎo shàng Běidà Qīnghuá de gùshì lái zhèngmíng xìtǒng yǒuxiào, dàn cónglái bù zhuīwèn bèi táotài de rén qù le nǎlǐ. Ér qiě — jíshǐ shì nàxiē 'chénggōng' de xuéshēng, tāmen zài zhège guòchéng zhōng shīqù le shénme?
options.text_en: We use Peking and Tsinghua success stories to prove the system works, but never ask where the filtered-out ones ended up. And even for the 'successful' students — what did they lose in the process?
options.feedback: 蒋老师把信封里厚厚的一叠信放到桌上：「这些是他们后来写给我的。你说的那个问题，很多人在信里问自己。」

options.text_zh: 总有人要被淘汰的，社会资源本来就有限。
options.text_pinyin: Zǒng yǒu rén yào bèi táotài de, shèhuì zīyuán běnlái jiù yǒuxiàn.
options.text_en: Someone always has to be filtered out — social resources are limited after all.
options.feedback: 蒋老师沉默了一会儿，平静地问：「那个被筛掉的，如果是你呢？」

options.text_zh: 您有没有跟那些考上名校的学生还保持联系？
options.text_pinyin: Nín yǒu méiyǒu gēn nàxiē kǎo shàng míngxiào de xuéshēng hái bǎochí liánxì?
options.text_en: Do you still stay in touch with the students who got into top universities?
options.feedback: 蒋老师指了指桌上那叠信：「写信的，大多数是当年成绩不好的那些。」

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 感受力的萎缩……你说到了我最心痛的地方。我见过太多「优秀」的学生，作文写得完美无缺，但你读完以后什么感觉都没有。因为那不是他们在写，是标准答案在写。他们的语言是借来的，情感是表演的。这不是学生的错，是系统教会他们：真实的感受不值钱，规范的表达才值钱。
turns.text_pinyin: Gǎnshòulì de wěisuō…… nǐ shuō dào le wǒ zuì xīntòng de dìfang. Wǒ jiàn guò tài duō 'yōuxiù' de xuéshēng, zuòwén xiě de wánměi wúquē, dàn nǐ dú wán yǐhòu shénme gǎnjué dōu méiyǒu. Yīnwèi nà bú shì tāmen zài xiě, shì biāozhǔn dá'àn zài xiě. Tāmen de yǔyán shì jiè lái de, qínggǎn shì biǎoyǎn de. Zhè bú shì xuéshēng de cuò, shì xìtǒng jiāohuì tāmen: zhēnshí de gǎnshòu bù zhíqián, guīfàn de biǎodá cái zhíqián.
turns.text_en: Atrophy of feeling… you've touched my deepest pain. I've seen too many 'excellent' students whose essays are technically flawless, but after reading them you feel nothing. Because it's not them writing — it's the standard answers writing. Their language is borrowed, their emotions performed. This isn't the students' fault — the system taught them: genuine feelings aren't worth anything, standardized expression is.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you say to her?

options.text_zh: 教会他们用一种不属于自己的语言说话，然后让他们误以为那就是自己的声音。等真正的语言退化以后，想找回来也找不到了。蒋老师，您愿意离开，说明您自己的声音还在。
options.text_pinyin: Jiāohuì tāmen yòng yī zhǒng bù shǔyú zìjǐ de yǔyán shuōhuà, ránhòu ràng tāmen wù yǐwéi nà jiù shì zìjǐ de shēngyīn. Děng zhēnzhèng de yǔyán tuìhuà yǐhòu, xiǎng zhǎo huílái yě zhǎo bù dào le. Jiǎng lǎoshī, nín yuànyì líkāi, shuōmíng nín zìjǐ de shēngyīn hái zài.
options.text_en: They were taught to speak in a language that isn't theirs, then led to mistake it for their own voice. Once the real language atrophies, you can't find it back. Ms. Jiang, your leaving proves your voice is still there.
options.feedback: 蒋老师低下头，过了一会儿，轻声说：「我不确定。但我想知道。这也是为什么我出来了。」

options.text_zh: 学生们还年轻，进了社会自然会找到自己的表达方式。
options.text_pinyin: Xuéshēng men hái niánqīng, jìn le shèhuì zìrán huì zhǎo dào zìjǐ de biǎodá fāngshì.
options.text_en: Students are still young — they'll naturally find their own way of expressing themselves once they enter society.
options.feedback: 蒋老师摇摇头：「有些人找到了。有些人以为那个借来的语言就是自己的，再也没有发现。」

options.text_zh: 现在自媒体那么发达，年轻人有很多地方可以表达自己。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài zì méitǐ nàme fādá, niánqīngrén yǒu hěn duō dìfang kěyǐ biǎodá zìjǐ.
options.text_en: With self-media so developed now, young people have so many places to express themselves.
options.feedback: 蒋老师苦笑：「有地方说话，和有话说——不一样的。」

tree.cultural_note: China's gaokao (高考) college entrance examination shapes the lives of approximately 10 million students annually. The standardized answer system (标准答案) for Chinese literature questions has been a persistent point of contention, with critics arguing that literary interpretation — inherently subjective — is reduced to memorizable templates. Teachers who attempt to introduce critical thinking often face resistance from parents focused on exam scores, creating what many educators describe as an irreconcilable tension between genuine learning and assessment.

tree.setup: You're sitting in a nearly empty restaurant in Chengdu with Chef Liao, a third-generation Sichuan cook who recently closed his family's restaurant after forty years. He now consults for chain restaurants but seems hollowed out by the work. He's drinking baijiu alone when you join him. The restaurant they're in serves a standardized version of the dish his family was famous for.
tree.setup_zh: 你和廖师傅——一位第三代川菜厨师——坐在成都一家几乎空了的餐厅里。他最近关了自家经营了四十年的餐馆。他现在为连锁餐厅做顾问，但这份工作似乎让他心力交瘁。你过来的时候他正独自喝着白酒。他们所在的这家餐厅供应的正是他家招牌菜的标准化版本。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你刚才点的那道回锅肉——你觉得好吃吗？说实话。这是用我提供的「标准化配方」做的。调味料精确到克，烹饪时间精确到秒。全国三百家分店，每一份的味道都一模一样。但我告诉你，这个味道跟我爸做的回锅肉之间的差距，大到你无法想象。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ gāngcái diǎn de nà dào huíguōròu — nǐ juéde hǎochī ma? Shuō shíhuà. Zhè shì yòng wǒ tígōng de 'biāozhǔnhuà pèifāng' zuò de. Tiáowèiliào jīngquè dào kè, pēngrèn shíjiān jīngquè dào miǎo. Quánguó sānbǎi jiā fēndiàn, měi yī fèn de wèidào dōu yīmú yīyàng. Dàn wǒ gàosù nǐ, zhège wèidào gēn wǒ bà zuò de huíguōròu zhī jiān de chājù, dà dào nǐ wúfǎ xiǎngxiàng.
turns.text_en: That twice-cooked pork you just ordered — do you think it's good? Be honest. It's made from the 'standardized recipe' I provided. Seasoning measured to the gram, cooking time measured to the second. Three hundred branches nationwide, every serving tastes identical. But I'll tell you: the gap between this taste and my father's twice-cooked pork is larger than you can imagine.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Ask what was different about his father's version.

options.text_zh: 配方一样，调料一样——那差距到底在哪里？是某种没办法写进配方的东西吗？
options.text_pinyin: Pèifāng yīyàng, tiáoliào yīyàng — nà chājù dàodǐ zài nǎlǐ? Shì mǒu zhǒng méi bànfǎ xiě jìn pèifāng de dōngxī ma?
options.text_en: Same recipe, same seasonings — so where's the gap? Is it something that can't be written into a recipe?
options.feedback: Chef Liao sets down his glass. He looks at you properly for the first time since you sat down.

options.text_zh: 我觉得挺好吃的啊，味道很正宗。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde tǐng hǎochī de a, wèidào hěn zhèngzōng.
options.text_en: I think it tastes pretty good, honestly — very authentic.
options.feedback: Chef Liao lets out a short, humorless laugh and refills his cup. He doesn't argue with you. That's almost worse.

options.text_zh: 你爸当时为什么不把配方写下来？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ bà dāngshí wèishéme bù bǎ pèifāng xiě xiàlái?
options.text_en: Why didn't your father write the recipe down at the time?
options.feedback: Chef Liao looks at you with a complicated expression — like you've asked the right question for the wrong reason. 'He did write it down,' he says quietly. 'That's what I gave them.'

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 不能被量化——你说到了关键。我爸炒回锅肉从来不称调料，全凭手感。他看一块肉的颜色就知道该怎么调火候。同样的猪肉，冬天和夏天的含水量不一样，他会微调。同一道菜，今天做的和明天做的稍微有点不同——不是失误，是适应。这种随机性就是生命力。标准化把这种随机性消灭了，味道就死了。
turns.text_pinyin: Bù néng bèi liànghuà — nǐ shuō dào le guānjiàn. Wǒ bà chǎo huíguōròu cónglái bù chēng tiáoliào, quán píng shǒugǎn. Tā kàn yī kuài ròu de yánsè jiù zhīdào gāi zěnme tiáo huǒhòu. Tóngyàng de zhūròu, dōngtiān hé xiàtiān de hánshuǐliàng bù yīyàng, tā huì wēi tiáo. Tóng yī dào cài, jīntiān zuò de hé míngtiān zuò de shāowēi yǒudiǎn bùtóng — bú shì shīwù, shì shìyìng. Zhè zhǒng suíjīxìng jiù shì shēngmìnglì. Biāozhǔnhuà bǎ zhè zhǒng suíjīxìng xiāomiè le, wèidào jiù sǐ le.
turns.text_en: Can't be quantified — you've hit the key. My dad never measured seasonings when cooking twice-cooked pork — all by feel. He'd know how to adjust the heat just by looking at the meat's color. Same pork has different moisture content in winter and summer, and he'd make micro-adjustments. The same dish, made today versus tomorrow, would be slightly different — not a mistake, but adaptation. That variability is vitality. Standardization killed that variability, and the taste died.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Reflect on the connection between variability and vitality in cooking.

options.text_zh: 就像现场演奏和录音室版本——录音室版完美、可重复，但现场版有呼吸、有即兴。你爸的菜是现场演奏，标准化配方是录音。
options.text_pinyin: Jiù xiàng xiànchǎng yǎnzòu hé lùyīnshì bǎnběn — lùyīnshì bǎn wánměi, kě chóngfù, dàn xiànchǎng bǎn yǒu hūxī, yǒu jíxīng. Nǐ bà de cài shì xiànchǎng yǎnzòu, biāozhǔnhuà pèifāng shì lùyīn.
options.text_en: Like a live performance versus a studio recording — the studio version is perfect, repeatable, but the live version breathes and improvises. Your father's cooking was live; the standardized recipe is the recording.
options.feedback: Chef Liao goes still for a moment. Then he nods slowly, staring at the table. 'Yeah,' he says. 'And I'm the one who turned it into a recording.'

options.text_zh: 但从商业角度来看，标准化是必须的，不然怎么扩张？
options.text_pinyin: Dànshì cóng shāngyè jiǎodù lái kàn, biāozhǔnhuà shì bìxū de, bùrán zěnme kuòzhāng?
options.text_en: But from a business standpoint, standardization is necessary — otherwise how do you scale?
options.feedback: Chef Liao looks at you like you've said something he's heard too many times from too many people he doesn't respect. He pours another drink.

options.text_zh: 你现在还自己做饭吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ xiànzài hái zìjǐ zuò fàn ma?
options.text_en: Do you still cook for yourself at home?
options.feedback: Chef Liao pauses. 'Not much,' he says, after a moment. It's an honest answer, but it lands like an admission he wasn't ready to make.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 现场演奏……这个比喻太准了。但你知道最讽刺的是什么吗？连锁店请我当顾问的时候，他们的原话是「我们要还原你爸的味道」。但还原是不可能的——因为那个味道不是一个固定的东西，它是一个活着的过程。你怎么还原一个过程？你只能还原一个结果的近似值。然后他们管这个近似值叫「正宗」。
turns.text_pinyin: Xiànchǎng yǎnzòu…… zhège bǐyù tài zhǔn le. Dàn nǐ zhīdào zuì fěngcì de shì shénme ma? Liánsuǒ diàn qǐng wǒ dāng gùwèn de shíhòu, tāmen de yuánhuà shì 'wǒmen yào huányuán nǐ bà de wèidào'. Dàn huányuán shì bùkěnéng de — yīnwèi nàge wèidào bú shì yīge gùdìng de dōngxī, tā shì yīge huó zhe de guòchéng. Nǐ zěnme huányuán yīge guòchéng? Nǐ zhǐ néng huányuán yīge jiéguǒ de jìnsìzhí. Ránhòu tāmen guǎn zhège jìnsìzhí jiào 'zhèngzōng'.
turns.text_en: Live performance… that analogy is spot on. But you know what's most ironic? When the chain hired me as a consultant, their exact words were 'We want to restore your father's taste.' But restoration is impossible — because that taste wasn't a fixed thing, it was a living process. How do you restore a process? You can only restore an approximation of a result. Then they call that approximation 'authentic.'

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the irony of calling an approximation 'authentic.'

options.text_zh: 「正宗」变成了营销词——不再指向真实的来源，而是指向消费者期待的口感。连锁店卖的不是你爸的味道，是你爸味道的「概念」。
options.text_pinyin: 'Zhèngzōng' biàn chéng le yíngxiāo cí — bú zài zhǐxiàng zhēnshí de láiyuán, ér shì zhǐxiàng xiāofèizhě qīdài de kǒugǎn. Liánsuǒ diàn mài de bú shì nǐ bà de wèidào, shì nǐ bà wèidào de 'gàiniàn'.
options.text_en: 'Authentic' has become a marketing word — no longer pointing to a real source, but to the taste range consumers expect. The chain isn't selling your father's flavor. It's selling the concept of your father's flavor.
options.feedback: Chef Liao laughs — a real one this time, short and sharp. 'That's exactly what they did. They even put his photo on the menu.'

options.text_zh: 其实消费者也分辨不出来，大多数人觉得好吃就行了。
options.text_pinyin: Qíshí xiāofèizhě yě fēnbiàn bù chūlái, dàduōshù rén juéde hǎochī jiù xíng le.
options.text_en: Consumers can't really tell the difference anyway — most people just care that it tastes good.
options.feedback: Chef Liao looks at you for a long moment. 'I know,' he says finally, very quietly. It's not agreement — it's resignation, and it's the worst thing you could have said to him.

options.text_zh: 你有没有在合同里限制他们怎么用「正宗」这个词？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu zài hétong lǐ xiànzhì tāmen zěnme yòng 'zhèngzōng' zhège cí?
options.text_en: Did you put any restrictions in the contract about how they use the word 'authentic'?
options.feedback: Chef Liao rubs his face. 'I should have had a lawyer,' he mutters. You've accidentally touched a real wound, but from entirely the wrong direction.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 品牌叙事……对，就是这个。他们甚至在菜单上写了我们家的故事——「三代传承，匠心独运」。但那个故事被简化成了三行字的广告文案。他们用我爸的照片，用我家的历史，来给一种工业化产品镀金。而我呢？我是那个亲手把配方交出去的人。有时候夜里睡不着，我觉得自己像是出卖了什么。
turns.text_pinyin: Pǐnpái xùshì…… duì, jiù shì zhège. Tāmen shènzhì zài càidān shàng xiě le wǒmen jiā de gùshì — 'sān dài chuánchéng, jiàngxīn dúyùn'. Dàn nàge gùshì bèi jiǎnhuà chéng le sān háng zì de guǎnggào wén'àn. Tāmen yòng wǒ bà de zhàopiàn, yòng wǒ jiā de lìshǐ, lái gěi yī zhǒng gōngyèhuà chǎnpǐn dùjīn. Ér wǒ ne? Wǒ shì nàge qīnshǒu bǎ pèifāng jiāo chūqù de rén. Yǒu shíhòu yèlǐ shuì bù zháo, wǒ juéde zìjǐ xiàng shì chūmài le shénme.
turns.text_en: Brand narrative… yes, exactly. They even wrote our family story on the menu — 'Three generations of tradition, artisan spirit.' But that story was reduced to three lines of ad copy. They use my father's photo, my family's history, to gild an industrialized product. And me? I'm the one who handed over the recipe with my own hands. Sometimes I can't sleep at night. I feel like I sold something.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to his guilt about handing over the family recipe.

options.text_zh: 你交出去的只是文字和数字——你爸的手感、他对食材的直觉，那些不在纸上。你出卖的是一个空壳，灵魂一直在你这里。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ jiāo chūqù de zhǐ shì wénzì hé shùzì — nǐ bà de shǒugǎn, tā duì shícái de zhíjué, nàxiē bú zài zhǐ shàng. Nǐ chūmài de shì yī ge kōng ké, línghún yīzhí zài nǐ zhèlǐ.
options.text_en: What you handed over was only words and numbers — your father's feel, his instinct for ingredients, those aren't on paper. What you sold was a shell. The soul has always been with you.
options.feedback: Chef Liao is quiet for a long time. He turns his cup in his hands. When he looks up his expression has shifted — not fixed, but slightly less heavy.

options.text_zh: 顾问费应该不少吧？至少经济上有保障了。
options.text_pinyin: Gùwèn fèi yīnggāi bù shǎo ba? Zhìshǎo jīngjì shàng yǒu bǎozhàng le.
options.text_en: The consulting fee must be decent, right? At least you're financially secure now.
options.feedback: Chef Liao looks at you with something close to pity. He doesn't respond. He just refills his glass and stares at it.

options.text_zh: 你有没有考虑过自己重新开一家小店？
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǒu méiyǒu kǎolǜ guò zìjǐ chóngxīn kāi yī jiā xiǎo diàn?
options.text_en: Have you ever considered opening a small place again on your own?
options.feedback: Chef Liao gives a short, tired sigh. 'Every week,' he says. Then silence. It's a real answer to a question that missed what he actually needed to hear.

tree.cultural_note: The industrialization of regional Chinese cuisine through chain restaurants has sparked intense debate about what 'authentic' (正宗) means. Traditional Sichuan cooking relies on what chefs call 'wok breath' (锅气) — the unique flavor that emerges from a specific wok, a specific flame, and a specific cook's rhythm. When chain restaurants standardize these recipes, they preserve the ingredients but lose the improvisational quality that distinguished each cook's version. The tension between scalability and authenticity is one of China's most visible cultural-commercial conflicts.

tree.setup: You're in a rural county seat in Guizhou, sitting in a tea house with a retired linguist, Professor Meng, who has spent her career documenting endangered Miao dialects. She has a worn notebook filled with phonetic transcriptions. The village where she recorded the last fluent speaker has recently been demolished for a reservoir.
tree.setup_zh: 你在贵州一个偏远的县城，和退休的语言学家孟教授坐在一家茶馆里。她一辈子都在记录濒危的苗族方言。她有一本磨损的笔记本，里面全是语音转录。她最后录下那位说流利方言的老人所在的村庄，最近为了修水库被拆了。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 那个村子不在了。但更让我心痛的不是村子——是声音。去年冬天，方明贵老人走了，他是巴拉河上游土语最后一个完全使用者。他走的时候，那种土语就死了。不是濒危、不是衰落——是死了。世界上再也不会有人用那种方式说话了。你知道一种语言死亡是什么感觉吗？不是图书馆着火，是一整座图书馆从来没有被建过。
turns.text_pinyin: Nàge cūnzi bú zài le. Dàn gèng ràng wǒ xīntòng de bú shì cūnzi — shì shēngyīn. Qùnián dōngtiān, Fāng Míngguì lǎorén zǒu le, tā shì Bālā hé shàngyóu tǔyǔ zuìhòu yī ge wánquán shǐyòngzhě. Tā zǒu de shíhou, nà zhǒng tǔyǔ jiù sǐ le. Bú shì bīnwēi, bú shì shuāiluò — shì sǐ le. Shìjiè shàng zài yě bú huì yǒu rén yòng nà zhǒng fāngshì shuōhuà le. Nǐ zhīdào yī zhǒng yǔyán sǐwáng shì shénme gǎnjué ma? Bú shì túshūguǎn zháo huǒ, shì yī zhěng zuò túshūguǎn cónglái méiyǒu bèi jiàn guò.
turns.text_en: That village is gone. But what hurts more isn't the village — it's the sound. Last winter, Elder Fang Minggui passed away, the last full user of the upper Bala River vernacular. When he died, that vernacular died. Not endangered — dead. No one will ever speak that way again. A language dying isn't a library burning — it's an entire library that was never built.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the image of a library that was never built.

options.text_zh: 「从来没有被建过」比「着火」更让人绝望——着火至少有过遗址，有过哀悼的对象。这里连那个都没有。那种土语里的认知方式，就这样消失了。
options.text_pinyin: 'Cónglái méiyǒu bèi jiàn guò' bǐ 'zháo huǒ' gèng ràng rén juéwàng — zháo huǒ zhìshǎo yǒu guò yízhǐ, yǒu guò āidào de duìxiàng. Zhèlǐ lián nàge dōu méiyǒu. Nà zhǒng tǔyǔ lǐ de rènzhī fāngshì, jiù zhèyàng xiāoshī le.
options.text_en: 'Never built' is more devastating than 'burned down' — a fire at least leaves ruins, something to mourn. Here there isn't even that. The ways of knowing inside that dialect just — vanished.
options.feedback: Professor Meng puts her hand on the notebook and looks out the window for a moment. Then she nods, slowly. 'You said it better than I usually do.'

options.text_zh: 您的录音资料保存得怎么样？至少那些还在。
options.text_pinyin: Nín de lùyīn zīliào bǎocún de zěnmeyàng? Zhìshǎo nàxiē hái zài.
options.text_en: How are your recordings being preserved? At least those still exist.
options.feedback: Professor Meng gives you a look that's more patient than irritated. 'Yes, they do,' she says. 'That's the question I keep asking myself.'

options.text_zh: 中国有多少种方言正在消失？
options.text_pinyin: Zhōngguó yǒu duōshǎo zhǒng fāngyán zhèngzài xiāoshī?
