options.text_pinyin: Nín měi nián dōngtiān dōu zài zhèlǐ kàn zhúzi ma?
options.text_en: Do you watch the bamboo here every winter?
options.feedback: Master Huai nods simply. 'Yes.' A pause. 'It teaches something different every time.' The conversation has gently slipped into something more ordinary.

options.text_zh: 我可以在这里住几天吗？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ kěyǐ zài zhèlǐ zhù jǐ tiān ma?
options.text_en: Would it be possible for me to stay here a few days?
options.feedback: Master Huai turns to look at you with mild surprise. 'You can speak to the guestmaster,' he says. Practical and kind — but the teaching moment has passed.

tree.cultural_note: Bamboo (竹) is one of the Four Gentlemen (四君子) of Chinese art and philosophy, representing resilience, flexibility, and integrity. The observation of bamboo bending under snow is a classic contemplative practice in Chinese monasteries. The teaching here — that intervals matter more than weight, and that recovery means growing with traces rather than erasing experience — synthesizes Daoist naturalism, Buddhist acceptance, and Confucian perseverance into a single image visible from any temple window.

tree.setup: You sit in a calligrapher's studio in Hangzhou. Mr. Shen, ninety-one years old, is grinding ink on a stone his grandfather used. The room smells of pine soot and aged paper. He hasn't taken a student in fifteen years.
tree.setup_zh: 你坐在杭州一位书法家的工作室里。九十一岁的沈先生正在用他祖父用过的砚台磨墨。房间里弥漫着松烟和陈纸的气味。他已经十五年没有收过学生了。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这块砚台是我祖父的，他祖父的，再往上我就不知道了。至少五代人磨过它。你看中间这个凹陷——这不是设计出来的，是一百多年的手磨出来的。每一代人的手都在同一个位置画圈，墨和水和石头之间发生了一种极其缓慢的对话。这个凹陷是对话的痕迹。有人问我为什么不用墨汁——因为墨汁是结果。磨墨是过程。书法的一半在你提笔之前就已经完成了——在你磨墨的那十五分钟里，你的呼吸慢下来了，手的温度传到了墨上，你和纸之间的关系在你还没碰到纸之前就已经开始建立了。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè kuài yàntái shì wǒ zǔfù de, tā zǔfù de, zài wǎng shàng wǒ jiù bù zhīdào le. Zhìshǎo wǔ dài rén mó guò tā. Nǐ kàn zhōngjiān zhège āoxiàn — zhè bú shì shèjì chūlái de, shì yībǎi duō nián de shǒu mó chūlái de. Měi yī dài rén de shǒu dōu zài tóng yī ge wèizhì huà quān, mò hé shuǐ hé shítou zhī jiān fāshēng le yī zhǒng jíqí huǎnmàn de duìhuà. Zhège āoxiàn shì duìhuà de hénjì. Yǒu rén wèn wǒ wèishéme bú yòng mòzhī — yīnwèi mòzhī shì jiéguǒ. Mó mò shì guòchéng. Shūfǎ de yībàn zài nǐ tí bǐ zhīqián jiù yǐjīng wánchéng le — zài nǐ mó mò de nà shíwǔ fēnzhōng lǐ, nǐ de hūxī màn xiàlái le, shǒu de wēndù chuán dào le mò shàng, nǐ hé zhǐ zhī jiān de guānxi zài nǐ hái méi pèng dào zhǐ zhīqián jiù yǐjīng kāishǐ jiànlì le.
turns.text_en: This inkstone was my grandfather's, and his grandfather's — beyond that I don't know. At least five generations have ground ink on it. See this hollow in the center — it wasn't designed, it was worn by over a hundred years of hands. Every generation's hand circled the same spot, and between ink and water and stone a supremely slow conversation took place.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the idea that half of calligraphy happens before the brush touches paper.

options.text_zh: 磨墨不是在等书法开始——磨墨就是书法。呼吸变慢、手的温度传递——这不是前奏，是第一乐章。那些看似什么都没发生的时刻，其实一切都在发生。
options.text_pinyin: Mó mò bú shì zài děng shūfǎ kāishǐ — mó mò jiù shì shūfǎ. Hūxī biàn màn, shǒu de wēndù chuándì — zhè bú shì qiánzòu, shì dì yī yuèzhāng. Nàxiē kàn sì shénme dōu méi fāshēng de shíkè, qíshí yīqiè dōu zài fāshēng.
options.text_en: Grinding ink isn't waiting for calligraphy to start — grinding is calligraphy. Breathing slowing, warmth transferring — that's not a prelude, it's the first movement. Those moments when seemingly nothing is happening — in fact, everything is happening.
options.feedback: Mr. Shen stops grinding and looks up. He studies you for a moment, then sets the ink stick down and gestures for you to come closer to the stone.

options.text_zh: 您每天写多少个字？
options.text_pinyin: Nín měi tiān xiě duōshǎo ge zì?
options.text_en: How many characters do you write each day?
options.feedback: Mr. Shen glances up. 'Some days one. Some days none.' He returns to grinding. The question landed in the wrong dimension.

options.text_zh: 这块砚台现在值多少钱？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè kuài yàntái xiànzài zhí duōshǎo qián?
options.text_en: What would this inkstone be worth now?
options.feedback: Mr. Shen keeps grinding without looking up. After a moment he says, 'I have no idea.' His tone makes clear he's never needed to know.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 第一乐章——这个比喻很精确。但我还要告诉你一件事：墨磨出来以后，它在砚台上的状态只能维持很短的时间。磨得太早，墨干了。磨得太晚，心还没准备好。最好的墨是在你刚好准备好的那一刻磨出来的——你的心、你的墨、你的纸同时到达了那个状态。这种同时性不能计划——它只能被等待。我年轻的时候总是急着写。现在我明白了：不是我在等墨准备好——是墨在等我准备好。而大多数时候，我还没准备好的原因不是技术不够，是心里还有噪音。
turns.text_pinyin: Dì yī yuèzhāng — zhège bǐyù hěn jīngquè. Dàn wǒ hái yào gàosu nǐ yī jiàn shì: mò mó chūlái yǐhòu, tā zài yàntái shàng de zhuàngtài zhǐ néng wéichí hěn duǎn de shíjiān. Mó de tài zǎo, mò gān le. Mó de tài wǎn, xīn hái méi zhǔnbèi hǎo. Zuì hǎo de mò shì zài nǐ gānghǎo zhǔnbèi hǎo de nà yī kè mó chūlái de — nǐ de xīn, nǐ de mò, nǐ de zhǐ tóngshí dàodá le nàge zhuàngtài. Zhè zhǒng tóngshíxìng bù néng jìhuà — tā zhǐ néng bèi děngdài. Wǒ niánqīng de shíhou zǒng shì jí zhe xiě. Xiànzài wǒ míngbai le: bú shì wǒ zài děng mò zhǔnbèi hǎo — shì mò zài děng wǒ zhǔnbèi hǎo. Ér dàduōshù shíhou, wǒ hái méi zhǔnbèi hǎo de yuányīn bú shì jìshù bú gòu, shì xīnlǐ hái yǒu zàoyīn.
turns.text_en: First movement — that metaphor is precise. But I need to tell you something else: after ink is ground, its state on the stone only lasts a short time. Ground too early, the ink dries. Ground too late, the heart isn't ready. The best ink is ground at the exact moment you happen to be ready — your heart, your ink, your paper all arrive at that state simultaneously.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the idea that readiness is about inner silence, not technique.

options.text_zh: 技术是加法——一笔、两笔、一万笔。安静是减法——放下焦虑、比较、对结果的期待。当减法减到最后，剩下的只有你和墨和纸。那个时刻，字自己就写出来了。
options.text_pinyin: Jìshù shì jiāfǎ — yī bǐ, liǎng bǐ, yī wàn bǐ. Ānjìng shì jiǎnfǎ — fàng xià jiāolǜ, bǐjiào, duì jiéguǒ de qīdài. Dāng jiǎnfǎ jiǎn dào zuìhòu, shèng xià de zhǐyǒu nǐ hé mò hé zhǐ. Nà ge shíkè, zì zìjǐ jiù xiě chūlái le.
options.text_en: Technique is addition — one stroke, two strokes, ten thousand strokes. Silence is subtraction — setting down anxiety, comparison, expectation of results. When subtraction is complete, what remains is only you and ink and paper.
options.feedback: Mr. Shen looks at you steadily. Then he picks up the brush, loads it with ink, and places a blank sheet in front of you. He doesn't speak.

options.text_zh: 您是怎么让自己安静下来的？
options.text_pinyin: Nín shì zěnme ràng zìjǐ ānjìng xiàlái de?
options.text_en: How do you make yourself become quiet?
options.feedback: Mr. Shen looks at the inkstone. 'I grind,' he says. 'For as long as it takes.' He goes back to circling. He's given you the answer but you've asked for a method instead of hearing it.

options.text_zh: 冥想对书法有帮助吗？
options.text_pinyin: Míngxiǎng duì shūfǎ yǒu bāngzhù ma?
options.text_en: Does meditation help with calligraphy?
options.feedback: Mr. Shen considers this. 'Grinding is meditation,' he says simply. He seems mildly puzzled that you're looking for something separate.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 减法——是的。你理解了。我花了大半辈子才学会做减法。年轻时候的字，每一笔都在喊「看我」。后来我开始删：删掉了炫技，删掉了想要被认可的欲望，删掉了「我在写字」这个意识本身。最好的字是写完以后你甚至不记得自己写过的。不是因为不专注——恰恰相反——是因为太专注了，专注到「我」消失了。剩下的只有墨在纸上的运动。这不是技术的巅峰——是自我的消融。我现在九十一岁了。我写字写了八十年。八十年学会的不是怎么写——是怎么消失在写的过程里。
turns.text_pinyin: Jiǎnfǎ — shì de. Nǐ lǐjiě le. Wǒ huā le dàbàn bèizi cái xuéhuì zuò jiǎnfǎ. Niánqīng shíhou de zì, měi yī bǐ dōu zài hǎn 'kàn wǒ'. Hòulái wǒ kāishǐ shān: shān diào le xuànjì, shān diào le xiǎng yào bèi rènkě de yùwàng, shān diào le 'wǒ zài xiě zì' zhège yìshí běnshēn. Zuì hǎo de zì shì xiě wán yǐhòu nǐ shènzhì bú jìde zìjǐ xiě guò de. Bú shì yīnwèi bù zhuānzhù — qiàqià xiāngfǎn — shì yīnwèi tài zhuānzhù le, zhuānzhù dào 'wǒ' xiāoshī le. Shèng xià de zhǐyǒu mò zài zhǐ shàng de yùndòng. Zhè bú shì jìshù de diānfēng — shì zìwǒ de xiāoróng. Wǒ xiànzài jiǔshíyī suì le. Wǒ xiě zì xiě le bāshí nián. Bāshí nián xuéhuì de bú shì zěnme xiě — shì zěnme xiāoshī zài xiě de guòchéng lǐ.
turns.text_en: Subtraction — yes. You understand. I spent most of my life learning subtraction. The characters of my youth — every stroke shouted 'look at me.' Later I began to delete: deleted the showing off, deleted the desire to be recognized, deleted the consciousness of 'I am writing' itself. The best characters are ones you don't even remember writing afterward.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to eighty years of learning to disappear into the process.

options.text_zh: 您花了一辈子学习如何不存在于您做的事情里——但恰恰是这种不存在让字活了起来。当「我」消失了，字才自由了，不再为您服务，为自己而存在。创作者消失，作品才真正诞生。
options.text_pinyin: Nín huā le yī bèizi xuéxí rúhé bù cúnzài yú nín zuò de shìqíng lǐ — dàn qiàqià shì zhè zhǒng bù cúnzài ràng zì huó le qǐlái. Dāng 'wǒ' xiāoshī le, zì cái zìyóu le, bú zài wèi nín fúwù, wèi zìjǐ ér cúnzài. Chuàngzuòzhě xiāoshī, zuòpǐn cái zhēnzhèng dànshēng.
options.text_en: You spent a lifetime learning how not to exist in what you do — and precisely that absence is what makes the characters alive.
options.feedback: Mr. Shen is still for a moment. Then he picks up the brush himself, very slowly, and writes one character. He sets the brush down and turns the paper toward you.

options.text_zh: 您的作品有没有卖过？
options.text_pinyin: Nín de zuòpǐn yǒu méiyǒu mài guò?
options.text_en: Have you ever sold your work?
options.feedback: Mr. Shen nods once. 'When I was young, yes.' He says nothing more. The question landed somewhere far from where the conversation was going.

options.text_zh: 这跟道家说的「无为」是一个意思吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè gēn Dàojiā shuō de 'wúwéi' shì yī ge yìsi ma?
options.text_en: Is this the same as the Daoist idea of wuwei?
options.feedback: Mr. Shen tilts his head. 'You can call it that,' he says mildly. He picks up the ink stick again. He'd rather you stayed with the experience than named it.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 创作者消失，作品才诞生——你说到了我用八十年才到达的地方。最后让我给你看一样东西。这是我昨天写的一个字：「空」。你看——这个字里什么也没有。但什么也没有，恰恰是它要说的全部。我写它的时候不在想任何事情。我不知道我要写什么字，但手动了，墨落了，「空」出现了。这个字不是我选的——是墨和纸和我的手共同决定的。也许这就是写了八十年以后唯一能写出来的字——一个关于「什么都没有」的字。但你看它——它空吗？它比任何字都满。
turns.text_pinyin: Chuàngzuòzhě xiāoshī, zuòpǐn cái dànshēng — nǐ shuō dào le wǒ yòng bāshí nián cái dàodá de dìfang. Zuìhòu ràng wǒ gěi nǐ kàn yī yàng dōngxi. Zhè shì wǒ zuótiān xiě de yī ge zì: 'kōng'. Nǐ kàn — zhège zì lǐ shénme yě méiyǒu. Dàn shénme yě méiyǒu, qiàqià shì tā yào shuō de quánbù. Wǒ xiě tā de shíhou bú zài xiǎng rènhé shìqíng. Wǒ bù zhīdào wǒ yào xiě shénme zì, dàn shǒu dòng le, mò luò le, 'kōng' chūxiàn le. Zhège zì bú shì wǒ xuǎn de — shì mò hé zhǐ hé wǒ de shǒu gòngtóng juédìng de. Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì xiě le bāshí nián yǐhòu wéiyī néng xiě chūlái de zì — yī ge guānyú 'shénme dōu méiyǒu' de zì. Dàn nǐ kàn tā — tā kōng ma? Tā bǐ rènhé zì dōu mǎn.
turns.text_en: The creator disappears, the work is born — you've arrived at a place it took me eighty years to reach. Let me show you one last thing. This is a character I wrote yesterday: 'empty' (空). Look — there's nothing in this character. But nothing is precisely all it has to say. When I wrote it, I wasn't thinking about anything.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the character 'empty' that is fuller than any other.

options.text_zh: 它空，因为写的时候什么都没想。它满，因为写之前的八十年什么都经历过了、放下了。这不是起点的空——是终点的空。就像砚台上那个凹陷，看着是空的，里面装了五代人的时间。
options.text_pinyin: Tā kōng, yīnwèi xiě de shíhou shénme dōu méi xiǎng. Tā mǎn, yīnwèi xiě zhīqián de bāshí nián shénme dōu jīnglì guò le, fàng xià le. Zhè bú shì qǐdiǎn de kōng — shì zhōngdiǎn de kōng. Jiù xiàng yàntái shàng nàge āoxiàn, kàn zhe shì kōng de, lǐmiàn zhuāng le wǔ dài rén de shíjiān.
options.text_en: It's empty because you thought of nothing while writing. It's full because in the eighty years before, you experienced everything and set it all down.
options.feedback: Mr. Shen places the paper in front of you and folds his hands. He says nothing — but he doesn't look away. The lesson is over and also just beginning.

options.text_zh: 我可以拍一张照片吗？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ kěyǐ pāi yī zhāng zhàopiàn ma?
options.text_en: May I take a photo of it?
options.feedback: Mr. Shen's expression softens almost imperceptibly. 'If you like,' he says. But the moment shifts — you've moved from looking at the character to wanting to keep it, which is a different kind of looking.

options.text_zh: 这个字体是什么体？
options.text_pinyin: Zhège zìtǐ shì shénme tǐ?
options.text_en: What script style is this written in?
options.feedback: Mr. Shen names the style briefly and correctly. Then he waits. He's patient — but something in him quietly closes, like a door left slightly ajar that swings back.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese calligraphy (书法) is considered the highest visual art form in Chinese culture. The Four Treasures of the Study (文房四宝) — brush, ink, paper, and inkstone — are treated as partners rather than tools. The practice of grinding ink (磨墨) is considered meditative preparation, not mere chore. The concept of the artist's ego dissolving into the work connects to both Daoist wu-wei (无为) and Chan Buddhist non-attachment, but in calligraphy it is experienced directly through the body rather than theorized abstractly.

tree.setup: You meet an elderly farmer, Uncle Luo, at the edge of his terraced rice paddies in Yunnan. He is kneeling in the mud, pressing his palm flat against the earth. The morning mist has not yet burned off.
tree.setup_zh: 你在云南的梯田边遇到了一位老农民，罗叔。他跪在泥里，把手掌平贴在地面上。晨雾还没有散去。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你摸一下这个泥。不是用手指——用手掌。感觉到了吗？温度。这块泥比旁边那块暖一点。暖的地方水流过了——水流过的地方根会去找。我种了六十年地，最重要的一件事是：泥土会说话，但大多数人不听。不是不愿意听——是不知道怎么听。听泥土不是用耳朵——是用手掌、用脚底、用鼻子。今天早上这块泥的气味告诉我：昨晚的雨够了。不用再浇了。但如果我没有蹲下来闻，我就不知道。我就会多浇一次——多浇的那一次，可能会淹死刚发芽的苗。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ mō yīxià zhège ní. Bú shì yòng shǒuzhǐ — yòng shǒuzhǎng. Gǎnjué dào le ma? Wēndù. Zhè kuài ní bǐ pángbiān nà kuài nuǎn yīdiǎn. Nuǎn de dìfang shuǐ liú guò le — shuǐ liú guò de dìfang gēn huì qù zhǎo. Wǒ zhòng le liùshí nián dì, zuì zhòngyào de yī jiàn shì shì: nítǔ huì shuōhuà, dàn dàduōshù rén bù tīng. Bú shì bú yuànyì tīng — shì bù zhīdào zěnme tīng. Tīng nítǔ bú shì yòng ěrduo — shì yòng shǒuzhǎng, yòng jiǎodǐ, yòng bízi. Jīntiān zǎoshang zhè kuài ní de qìwèi gàosu wǒ: zuówǎn de yǔ gòu le. Bú yòng zài jiāo le. Dàn rúguǒ wǒ méiyǒu dūn xiàlái wén, wǒ jiù bù zhīdào. Wǒ jiù huì duō jiāo yī cì — duō jiāo de nà yī cì, kěnéng huì yān sǐ gāng fāyá de miáo.
turns.text_en: Touch this soil. Not with your fingers — with your palm. Feel it? Temperature. This patch is slightly warmer than the one beside it. Where it's warm, water has flowed — where water flowed, roots will seek. I've farmed for sixty years, and soil speaks, but most people don't listen. Not because they're unwilling — because they don't know how.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to soil as a language that requires a different kind of listening.

options.text_zh: 用手掌和鼻子来「听」——大多数人听不到，不是泥土太安静，是我们只被训练用一种方式接收信息。六十年蹲在泥里，您的手掌变成了翻译器——把温度翻译成「水来过了」，把气味翻译成「雨够了」。这不是测量，是关系——您和泥土之间六十年的共同语言。
options.text_pinyin: Yòng shǒuzhǎng hé bízi lái 'tīng' — dàduōshù rén tīng bú dào, bú shì nítǔ tài ānjìng, shì wǒmen zhǐ bèi xùnliàn yòng yī zhǒng fāngshì jiēshōu xìnxī. Liùshí nián dūn zài ní lǐ, nín de shǒuzhǎng biàn chéng le fānyìqì — bǎ wēndù fānyì chéng 'shuǐ lái guò le', bǎ qìwèi fānyì chéng 'yǔ gòu le'. Zhè bú shì cèliáng, shì guānxi — nín hé nítǔ zhī jiān liùshí nián de gòngtóng yǔyán.
options.text_en: 'Listening' with palms and nose — most people can't hear it, not because soil is too quiet, but because we're trained to receive information only one way. Sixty years squatting in mud, your palms became a translator — converting temperature into 'water passed through,' scent into 'enough rain.' This isn't measurement — it's a relationship. Sixty years of shared language between you and the earth.
options.feedback: The palm as translator — listening beyond sound requires sixty years of shared language.

options.text_zh: 现在有很多土壤传感器可以测这些。
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài yǒu hěn duō tǔrǎng chuángǎnqì kěyǐ cè zhèxiē.
options.text_en: There are soil sensors now that can measure all this.
options.feedback: Offers a technological replacement for an embodied relationship.

options.text_zh: 您种的是什么品种的稻子？
options.text_pinyin: Nín zhòng de shì shénme pǐnzhǒng de dàozi?
options.text_en: What variety of rice do you grow?
options.feedback: Asks about crop varieties during a meditation on listening.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 六十年的共同语言——你说得对。但这种语言有一个特点：它不能传授。我可以告诉你「泥暖了说明水来过」，你可以记住这个知识。但你的手掌感受到的那个温度差——零点几度——你自己的手掌需要多少年才能分辨出来？这不是信息的问题，是感官的问题。你的手掌需要在泥土里泡够了时间，它才会变得足够敏感。就像学一门语言——你可以背单词、背语法，但要「听」懂一门语言，你需要泡在里面。泥土的语言也是这样。你不能学它——你只能泡在里面，直到有一天你的手掌自己开始听。
turns.text_pinyin: Liùshí nián de gòngtóng yǔyán — nǐ shuō de duì. Dàn zhè zhǒng yǔyán yǒu yī ge tèdiǎn: tā bù néng chuánshòu. Wǒ kěyǐ gàosu nǐ 'ní nuǎn le shuōmíng shuǐ lái guò', nǐ kěyǐ jì zhù zhège zhīshi. Dàn nǐ de shǒuzhǎng gǎnshòu dào de nàge wēndù chā — líng diǎn jǐ dù — nǐ zìjǐ de shǒuzhǎng xūyào duōshǎo nián cái néng fēnbiàn chūlái? Zhè bú shì xìnxī de wèntí, shì gǎnguān de wèntí. Nǐ de shǒuzhǎng xūyào zài nítǔ lǐ pào gòu le shíjiān, tā cái huì biàn de zúgòu mǐngǎn. Jiù xiàng xué yī mén yǔyán — nǐ kěyǐ bèi dāncí, bèi yǔfǎ, dàn yào 'tīng' dǒng yī mén yǔyán, nǐ xūyào pào zài lǐmiàn. Nítǔ de yǔyán yě shì zhèyàng. Nǐ bù néng xué tā — nǐ zhǐ néng pào zài lǐmiàn, zhídào yǒu yī tiān nǐ de shǒuzhǎng zìjǐ kāishǐ tīng.
turns.text_en: Sixty years of shared language — you're right. But this language has a characteristic: it can't be taught. I can tell you 'warm soil means water passed through,' and you can remember this knowledge. But that temperature difference your palm felt — a fraction of a degree — how many years would your own palm need to distinguish it?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to knowledge that can only be soaked in, never taught.

options.text_zh: 不能学，只能泡——两种知识。一种是可传递的信息：事实、规则，五分钟教完。另一种是身体慢慢吸收的感知：「说不出来但知道」的直觉，需要在泥土里泡六十年。真正让您成为好农民的是第二种。没有考试、没有捷径，唯一的入口是身体在场的时间。
options.text_pinyin: Bù néng xué, zhǐ néng pào — liǎng zhǒng zhīshi. Yī zhǒng shì kě chuándì de xìnxī: shìshí, guīzé, wǔ fēnzhōng jiāo wán. Lìng yī zhǒng shì shēntǐ mànman xīshōu de gǎnzhī: 'shuō bù chūlái dàn zhīdào' de zhíjué, xūyào zài nítǔ lǐ pào liùshí nián. Zhēnzhèng ràng nín chéngwéi hǎo nóngmín de shì dì èr zhǒng. Méiyǒu kǎoshì, méiyǒu jiéjìng, wéiyī de rùkǒu shì shēntǐ zàichǎng de shíjiān.
options.text_en: Can't be learned, only soaked in — two kinds of knowledge. One is transferable information: facts, rules, taught in five minutes. The other is perception the body slowly absorbs: intuition you 'can't articulate but know,' requiring sixty years in the soil. What truly made you a good farmer is the second kind. No exams, no shortcuts — the only entrance is time with the body present.
options.feedback: Two kinds of knowledge — transferable information versus embodied perception that only time can teach.

options.text_zh: 年轻人愿意学种地吗？
options.text_pinyin: Niánqīngrén yuànyì xué zhòng dì ma?
options.text_en: Are young people willing to learn farming?
options.feedback: Asks about generational succession during a meditation on embodied knowledge.

options.text_zh: 有机农业是不是也需要这种经验？
options.text_pinyin: Yǒujī nóngyè shì bú shì yě xūyào zhè zhǒng jīngyàn?
options.text_en: Does organic farming also need this kind of experience?
options.feedback: Categorizes farming methods instead of engaging the epistemological insight.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 身体在场的时间——你理解了。但我要告诉你更深的一层：泥土不只是在说「水来了」「雨够了」这种实用信息。如果你泡得够久，你会开始感觉到一些更难用语言表达的东西。比如——今天早上，我蹲在这里的时候，泥土告诉我的不只是温度和湿度。它告诉我一种——怎么说呢——满足感。不是「我」满足，是泥土满足。昨晚的雨刚好够，今天早上的温度刚好暖，苗刚好在该发芽的时候发芽了。一切都刚好。泥土在说的是：「现在，一切都好。」你可能觉得这是我的想象。也许是。但六十年以后，你的想象和泥土的语言之间的界限会变得非常模糊。也许根本就没有界限。
turns.text_pinyin: Shēntǐ zàichǎng de shíjiān — nǐ lǐjiě le. Dàn wǒ yào gàosu nǐ gèng shēn de yī céng: nítǔ bù zhǐ shì zài shuō 'shuǐ lái le' 'yǔ gòu le' zhè zhǒng shíyòng xìnxī. Rúguǒ nǐ pào de gòu jiǔ, nǐ huì kāishǐ gǎnjué dào yīxiē gèng nán yòng yǔyán biǎodá de dōngxi. Bǐrú — jīntiān zǎoshang, wǒ dūn zài zhèlǐ de shíhou, nítǔ gàosu wǒ de bù zhǐ shì wēndù hé shīdù. Tā gàosu wǒ yī zhǒng — zěnme shuō ne — mǎnzú gǎn. Bú shì 'wǒ' mǎnzú, shì nítǔ mǎnzú. Zuówǎn de yǔ gānghǎo gòu, jīntiān zǎoshang de wēndù gānghǎo nuǎn, miáo gānghǎo zài gāi fāyá de shíhou fāyá le. Yīqiè dōu gānghǎo. Nítǔ zài shuō de shì: 'xiànzài, yīqiè dōu hǎo.' Nǐ kěnéng juéde zhè shì wǒ de xiǎngxiàng. Yěxǔ shì. Dàn liùshí nián yǐhòu, nǐ de xiǎngxiàng hé nítǔ de yǔyán zhī jiān de jièxiàn huì biàn de fēicháng móhu. Yěxǔ gēnběn jiù méiyǒu jièxiàn.
turns.text_en: Time when the body is present — you understand. But let me tell you a deeper layer: soil doesn't only communicate practical information like 'water came' or 'rain was enough.' If you soak long enough, you begin sensing things harder to express in language. For instance — this morning, when I squatted here, the soil told me more than temperature and moisture.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the dissolving boundary between imagination and the soil's voice.

options.text_zh: 也许根本没有界限——不是神秘主义，是亲密的极致。两个人相处几十年也会分不清某个念头是谁的。您和泥土也到了这个阶段。当您感觉泥土「满足」，不是投射——是身体经过六十年校准，已经能直接感受生态系统的平衡。这是被时间锻造出来的知觉。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ gēnběn méiyǒu jièxiàn — bú shì shénmì zhǔyì, shì qīnmì de jízhì. Liǎng ge rén xiāngchǔ jǐ shí nián yě huì fēn bù qīng mǒu ge niàntou shì shéi de. Nín hé nítǔ yě dào le zhège jiēduàn. Dāng nín gǎnjué nítǔ 'mǎnzú', bú shì tóushè — shì shēntǐ jīngguò liùshí nián xiàozhǔn, yǐjīng néng zhíjiē gǎnshòu shēngtài xìtǒng de pínghéng. Zhè shì bèi shíjiān duànzào chūlái de zhījué.
options.text_en: Perhaps there truly is no boundary — not mysticism, but intimacy at its extreme. Two people together for decades can't tell whose thought is whose. Your relationship with soil has reached that stage. When you feel the soil is 'satisfied,' it's not projection — it's your body, calibrated by sixty years, directly sensing an ecosystem's balance. This is perception forged by time.
options.feedback: Intimacy at its extreme — sixty years dissolves the boundary between perceiver and perceived.

options.text_zh: 您觉得气候变化对种地影响大吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín juéde qìhòu biànhuà duì zhòng dì yǐngxiǎng dà ma?
options.text_en: Do you think climate change greatly affects farming?
options.feedback: Introduces a macro issue during an intimate moment of shared perception.

options.text_zh: 这些梯田是什么时候建的？
options.text_pinyin: Zhèxiē tītián shì shénme shíhou jiàn de?
options.text_en: When were these terraces built?
options.feedback: A historical question during a meditation on embodied knowledge.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 超越语言的知觉……你说得比我能说的更清楚。我最后告诉你一件事。我的手——你看——指甲缝里永远有泥。洗不掉了。不是洗不干净——是皮肤的纹路已经和泥混在一起了。有时候我分不清哪里是我的皮肤、哪里是泥。我年轻的时候觉得这是脏。现在我觉得——这是六十年对话的证据。我的手不再完全是我的了。它有一半属于这块地。等我走了以后，这块地会记住我的手——就像砚台记住磨它的手一样。我的手掌磨过的泥、翻过的土——那些痕迹会留在这里。地不会忘记种过它的人。
turns.text_pinyin: Chāoyuè yǔyán de zhījué…… nǐ shuō de bǐ wǒ néng shuō de gèng qīngchǔ. Wǒ zuìhòu gàosu nǐ yī jiàn shì. Wǒ de shǒu — nǐ kàn — zhǐjiǎ fèng lǐ yǒngyuǎn yǒu ní. Xǐ bù diào le. Bú shì xǐ bù gānjìng — shì pífū de wénlù yǐjīng hé ní hùn zài yīqǐ le. Yǒu shíhou wǒ fēn bù qīng nǎlǐ shì wǒ de pífū, nǎlǐ shì ní. Wǒ niánqīng de shíhou juéde zhè shì zāng. Xiànzài wǒ juéde — zhè shì liùshí nián duìhuà de zhèngjù. Wǒ de shǒu bú zài wánquán shì wǒ de le. Tā yǒu yībàn shǔyú zhè kuài dì. Děng wǒ zǒu le yǐhòu, zhè kuài dì huì jì zhù wǒ de shǒu — jiù xiàng yàntái jì zhù mó tā de shǒu yīyàng. Wǒ de shǒuzhǎng mó guò de ní, fān guò de tǔ — nàxiē hénjì huì liú zài zhèlǐ. Dì bú huì wàngjì zhòng guò tā de rén.
turns.text_en: Perception beyond language… you articulate it more clearly than I can. One last thing. My hands — look — there's always soil in the nail beds. Can't wash it out anymore. Not because it won't come clean — because the lines of my skin have blended with the soil. Sometimes I can't tell where my skin ends and the soil begins. When I was young, I thought this was dirty.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the farmer's hands becoming part of the land.

options.text_zh: 手不再完全是您的——一半属于这块地。不是修辞，是六十年物质交换的事实：皮肤细胞留在泥里，矿物质渗进您的皮肤。不是「我拥有这块地」——是你们互相成为了对方的一部分。您的手本身就是证据——它既是手，又是地。
options.text_pinyin: Shǒu bú zài wánquán shì nín de — yībàn shǔyú zhè kuài dì. Bú shì xiūcí, shì liùshí nián wùzhì jiāohuàn de shìshí: pífū xìbāo liú zài ní lǐ, kuàngwùzhì shèn jìn nín de pífū. Bú shì 'wǒ yōngyǒu zhè kuài dì' — shì nǐmen hùxiāng chéngwéi le duìfāng de yī bùfen. Nín de shǒu běnshēn jiù shì zhèngjù — tā jì shì shǒu, yòu shì dì.
options.text_en: Your hand is no longer entirely yours — half belongs to this land. Not rhetoric — it's the fact of sixty years of material exchange: skin cells left in mud, minerals seeped into skin. Not 'I own this land' — you've become part of each other. Your hand itself is the evidence — it is both hand and earth.
options.feedback: Mutual becoming at the molecular level — the conversation happened within an inseparable whole.

options.text_zh: 您的孩子也种地吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín de háizi yě zhòng dì ma?
options.text_en: Do your children farm too?
options.feedback: Asks about succession during a meditation on human-earth symbiosis.

options.text_zh: 这块地有多大？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè kuài dì yǒu duō dà?
options.text_en: How large is this plot?
options.feedback: A practical measurement question at the emotional climax.

tree.cultural_note: Yunnan's terraced rice paddies (梯田), particularly those of the Hani people in Honghe, represent over 1,300 years of intimate human-landscape relationship. Traditional Chinese farming philosophy sees the farmer not as owner or exploiter of land but as participant in a mutual relationship (天人合一, the unity of heaven and humanity). The concept of embodied, non-transferable agricultural knowledge connects to the Chinese epistemological distinction between 知识 (zhīshi, explicit knowledge) and 体悟 (tǐwù, bodily realization) — understanding that can only come through lived physical experience.

tree.setup: You are on a rooftop in old Chengdu with a retired painter, Mrs. Jiang, who wakes at 4 AM every morning to watch the sky change. She has a notebook filled with color swatches she has mixed to match each dawn she's witnessed over twenty years. A cup of tea steams beside her.
tree.setup_zh: 你和退休画家蒋女士在成都老城区的屋顶上。她每天凌晨四点起来看天空变化。她有一本笔记本，记录了二十年来每一次黎明她调配出的颜色样本。一杯茶在她旁边冒着热气。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你看——现在是四点二十分。天还是黑的，但不是纯黑。你仔细看东边——有一种颜色正在出现，但你说不出它是什么颜色。不是蓝、不是灰、不是紫。它是一种只在这个时刻存在的颜色——再早五分钟它不在，再晚五分钟它就变成别的东西了。我画了二十年黎明，最大的发现不是天空有多美——是天空中存在大量我们没有名字的颜色。我们的语言只给了我们几十个颜色词——红、蓝、绿、紫——但天空在黎明的四十分钟里经过了上千种颜色。那些没有名字的颜色——它们存在吗？当然存在。但因为没有名字，大多数人看不见它们。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ kàn — xiànzài shì sì diǎn èrshí fēn. Tiān hái shì hēi de, dàn bú shì chún hēi. Nǐ zǐxì kàn dōngbiān — yǒu yī zhǒng yánsè zhèngzài chūxiàn, dàn nǐ shuō bù chū tā shì shénme yánsè. Bú shì lán, bú shì huī, bú shì zǐ. Tā shì yī zhǒng zhǐ zài zhège shíkè cúnzài de yánsè — zài zǎo wǔ fēnzhōng tā bú zài, zài wǎn wǔ fēnzhōng tā jiù biàn chéng bié de dōngxi le. Wǒ huà le èrshí nián límíng, zuì dà de fāxiàn bú shì tiānkōng yǒu duō měi — shì tiānkōng zhōng cúnzài dàliàng wǒmen méiyǒu míngzì de yánsè. Wǒmen de yǔyán zhǐ gěi le wǒmen jǐ shí ge yánsè cí — hóng, lán, lǜ, zǐ — dàn tiānkōng zài límíng de sìshí fēnzhōng lǐ jīngguò le shàng qiān zhǒng yánsè. Nàxiē méiyǒu míngzì de yánsè — tāmen cúnzài ma? Dāngrán cúnzài. Dàn yīnwèi méiyǒu míngzì, dàduōshù rén kàn bú jiàn tāmen.
turns.text_en: Look — it's 4:20 now. The sky is still dark, but not purely dark. Look carefully at the east — a color is emerging, but you can't say what color it is. Not blue, not gray, not purple. It's a color that only exists at this moment — five minutes earlier it wasn't there, five minutes later it becomes something else.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the idea that nameless colors are invisible to most people.

options.text_zh: 没有名字所以看不见——语言塑造了我们能看到什么。只有「蓝」这个词，天空的一千种蓝都被压缩成一个概念。那些介于蓝和紫之间的颜色真实存在，但语言把它们过滤掉了。您二十年来做的事，是在给无名的事物命名——用颜料命名。每个色块都是对天空说：「我看见你了。」
options.text_pinyin: Méiyǒu míngzì suǒyǐ kàn bú jiàn — yǔyán sùzào le wǒmen néng kàn dào shénme. Zhǐyǒu 'lán' zhège cí, tiānkōng de yī qiān zhǒng lán dōu bèi yāsuō chéng yī ge gàiniàn. Nàxiē jiè yú lán hé zǐ zhī jiān de yánsè zhēnshí cúnzài, dàn yǔyán bǎ tāmen guòlǜ diào le. Nín èrshí nián lái zuò de shì, shì zài gěi wúmíng de shìwù mìngmíng — yòng yánliào mìngmíng. Měi ge sè kuài dōu shì duì tiānkōng shuō: 'wǒ kàn jiàn nǐ le.'
options.text_en: Nameless, therefore invisible — language shapes what we can see. With only the word 'blue,' a thousand blues in the sky compress into one concept. Colors between blue and purple genuinely exist, but language filters them out. What you've done for twenty years is naming the nameless — with pigment. Each swatch says to the sky: 'I see you.'
options.feedback: Language as perceptual filter — art's primal function is making the unnamed visible.

options.text_zh: 您用什么牌子的颜料？
options.text_pinyin: Nín yòng shénme páizi de yánliào?
options.text_en: What brand of paint do you use?
options.feedback: Asks about materials during a meditation on perception and naming.

options.text_zh: 拍照不是更方便吗？
options.text_pinyin: Pāi zhào bú shì gèng fāngbiàn ma?
options.text_en: Wouldn't photography be more convenient?
options.feedback: Suggests technological substitution for an embodied practice.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 「我看见你了」——是的。这就是我对每一个黎明说的话。但你知道最让我震惊的是什么吗？二十年了，没有两个黎明的颜色是完全一样的。这本笔记本里有七千多个色块——每一个都不同。同一个方向、同一个季节、同一个钟点——颜色都不一样。因为云不一样、湿度不一样、风不一样、你的眼睛不一样。黎明是不可重复的。每一个黎明都是宇宙只发生一次的事件。但大多数人把黎明当作可以预期的、重复的事情——「明天还会有一个黎明」。是的，明天会有。但不是这一个。这一个正在消失——就在我们说话的这一刻，它已经变了。
turns.text_pinyin: 'Wǒ kàn jiàn nǐ le' — shì de. Zhè jiù shì wǒ duì měi yī ge límíng shuō de huà. Dàn nǐ zhīdào zuì ràng wǒ zhènjīng de shì shénme ma? Èrshí nián le, méiyǒu liǎng ge límíng de yánsè shì wánquán yīyàng de. Zhè běn bǐjìběn lǐ yǒu qī qiān duō ge sè kuài — měi yī ge dōu bùtóng. Tóng yī ge fāngxiàng, tóng yī ge jìjié, tóng yī ge zhōngdiǎn — yánsè dōu bù yīyàng. Yīnwèi yún bù yīyàng, shīdù bù yīyàng, fēng bù yīyàng, nǐ de yǎnjīng bù yīyàng. Límíng shì bù kě chóngfù de. Měi yī ge límíng dōu shì yǔzhòu zhǐ fāshēng yī cì de shìjiàn. Dàn dàduōshù rén bǎ límíng dāng zuò kěyǐ yùqī de, chóngfù de shìqíng — 'míngtiān hái huì yǒu yī ge límíng'. Shì de, míngtiān huì yǒu. Dàn bú shì zhè yī ge. Zhè yī ge zhèngzài xiāoshī — jiù zài wǒmen shuōhuà de zhè yī kè, tā yǐjīng biàn le.
turns.text_en: 'I see you' — yes. That's what I say to every dawn. But do what shocks me most? In twenty years, no two dawns have had exactly the same colors. This notebook has over seven thousand swatches — each one different. Same direction, same season, same hour — the colors differ. Because the clouds differ, humidity differs, wind differs, your eyes differ. Dawn is unrepeatable.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the unrepeatable nature of each dawn.

options.text_zh: 七千个色块没有重复——您的笔记本证明了：没有「每天」，只有「这一天」。我们不珍惜黎明，是因为相信它会再来。但「它」不会——「另一个」会来，「它」已经永远走了。您凌晨四点起来，不是因为黎明美，是因为这个黎明只有这一次。每个色块不是记录，是告别。
options.text_pinyin: Qī qiān ge sè kuài méiyǒu chóngfù — nín de bǐjìběn zhèngmíng le: méiyǒu 'měi tiān', zhǐyǒu 'zhè yī tiān'. Wǒmen bù zhēnxī límíng, shì yīnwèi xiāngxìn tā huì zài lái. Dàn 'tā' bú huì — 'lìng yī ge' huì lái, 'tā' yǐjīng yǒngyuǎn zǒu le. Nín língchén sì diǎn qǐlái, bú shì yīnwèi límíng měi, shì yīnwèi zhège límíng zhǐyǒu zhè yī cì. Měi ge sè kuài bú shì jìlù, shì gàobié.
options.text_en: Seven thousand swatches, not one repeated — your notebook proves: there is no 'every day,' only 'this day.' We don't cherish dawn because we believe it will come again. But 'it' won't — 'another one' will, but 'it' is gone forever. You wake at four AM not because dawn is beautiful, but because this dawn exists only once. Each swatch isn't a record — it's a farewell.
options.feedback: Each swatch as farewell — the distinction between 'it' and 'another one' reveals time's unrepeatable nature.

options.text_zh: 您办过画展吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín bàn guò huàzhǎn ma?
options.text_en: Have you held any exhibitions?
options.feedback: Asks about exhibitions during a revelation about impermanence.

options.text_zh: 成都的空气污染会不会影响颜色？
options.text_pinyin: Chéngdū de kōngqì wūrǎn huì bú huì yǐngxiǎng yánsè?
options.text_en: Does Chengdu's air pollution affect the colors?
options.feedback: Introduces environmental concerns during a meditation on impermanence.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 告别——对。每一个色块都是一次告别。但我还要纠正一点：不只是告别。你看——此刻天空正在变。二十分钟前的那个颜色已经走了。但它变成了现在这个颜色。它没有消失——它变成了别的东西。这是黎明教给我的最深的一课：没有什么真正消失。一切都在变成别的东西。那个四点二十分的无名颜色，变成了四点四十分的另一种无名颜色。第一个颜色「死」了吗？不，它活在第二个颜色里面——因为没有第一个，就不会有第二个。每一个瞬间都怀着前一个瞬间的全部记忆。所以我的色块不是七千次告别——是七千个证据，证明一切都在继续，只是换了一种方式存在。
turns.text_pinyin: Gàobié — duì. Měi yī ge sè kuài dōu shì yī cì gàobié. Dàn wǒ hái yào jiūzhèng yīdiǎn: bù zhǐ shì gàobié. Nǐ kàn — cǐkè tiānkōng zhèngzài biàn. Èrshí fēnzhōng qián de nàge yánsè yǐjīng zǒu le. Dàn tā biàn chéng le xiànzài zhège yánsè. Tā méiyǒu xiāoshī — tā biàn chéng le bié de dōngxi. Zhè shì límíng jiāo gěi wǒ de zuì shēn de yī kè: méiyǒu shénme zhēnzhèng xiāoshī. Yīqiè dōu zài biàn chéng bié de dōngxi. Nàge sì diǎn èrshí fēn de wúmíng yánsè, biàn chéng le sì diǎn sìshí fēn de lìng yī zhǒng wúmíng yánsè. Dì yī ge yánsè 'sǐ' le ma? Bù, tā huó zài dì èr ge yánsè lǐmiàn — yīnwèi méiyǒu dì yī ge, jiù bú huì yǒu dì èr ge. Měi yī ge shùnjiān dōu huái zhe qián yī ge shùnjiān de quánbù jìyì. Suǒyǐ wǒ de sè kuài bú shì qī qiān cì gàobié — shì qī qiān ge zhèngjù, zhèngmíng yīqiè dōu zài jìxù, zhǐ shì huàn le yī zhǒng fāngshì cúnzài.
turns.text_en: Farewell — yes. Each swatch is a farewell. But let me correct one thing: it's not only farewell. Look — the sky is changing right now. The color from twenty minutes ago is gone. But it became this current color. It didn't disappear — it became something else. This is the deepest lesson dawn has taught me: nothing truly disappears. Everything becomes something else.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the idea that nothing disappears, everything becomes something else.

options.text_zh: 每个瞬间都怀着前一个瞬间的记忆——不是消失和出现，是「变成」。四点二十分的颜色没有死，它生出了四点四十分的颜色。我们以为变化是丧失。但也许变化是积累——昨天的我活在今天的我里面。七千个色块连在一起，不是七千个分离的瞬间，是一条连续的河流。
options.text_pinyin: Měi ge shùnjiān dōu huái zhe qián yī ge shùnjiān de jìyì — bú shì xiāoshī hé chūxiàn, shì 'biàn chéng'. Sì diǎn èrshí fēn de yánsè méiyǒu sǐ, tā shēng chū le sì diǎn sìshí fēn de yánsè. Wǒmen yǐwéi biànhuà shì sàngshī. Dàn yěxǔ biànhuà shì jīlěi — zuótiān de wǒ huó zài jīntiān de wǒ lǐmiàn. Qī qiān ge sè kuài lián zài yīqǐ, bú shì qī qiān ge fēnlí de shùnjiān, shì yī tiáo liánxù de héliú.
options.text_en: Every moment carries the memory of the one before — not disappearing and appearing, but 'becoming.' The 4:20 color didn't die — it birthed the 4:40 color. We think change is loss. But maybe change is accumulation — yesterday's me lives inside today's me. Seven thousand swatches connected aren't seven thousand separate moments — they're one continuous river.
options.feedback: Change as pregnancy, not death — every moment carries the memory of all that came before.

options.text_zh: 您考虑过出一本书吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín kǎolǜ guò chū yī běn shū ma?
options.text_en: Have you considered publishing a book?
options.feedback: A publishing question at the philosophical climax.

options.text_zh: 这么多年，最美的黎明是哪一天？
options.text_pinyin: Zhème duō nián, zuì měi de límíng shì nǎ yī tiān?
options.text_en: Over all these years, which day had the most beautiful dawn?
options.feedback: Asks for a ranking when the teaching is that each is incomparable.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 一条连续的河流——是的。你看。天亮了。黎明结束了。但它没有结束——它变成了白天。白天会变成黄昏，黄昏会变成夜晚，夜晚会怀着明天的黎明。所以黎明从来没有真正开始过，也从来没有真正结束过。它只是在不断地变成自己的下一个版本。我今天早上调出了一个新的颜色——第七千零三十一个。它和之前所有的都不一样。但它和之前所有的都有关系。就像你——你今天来到这个屋顶，你和昨天的你不一样了。但昨天的你活在今天的你里面。你这个人就是一条河流。这杯茶也是——它一分钟前比现在热。但一分钟前的那杯茶变成了现在这杯。什么都没有丢失。
turns.text_pinyin: Yī tiáo liánxù de héliú — shì de. Nǐ kàn. Tiān liàng le. Límíng jiéshù le. Dàn tā méiyǒu jiéshù — tā biàn chéng le báitiān. Báitiān huì biàn chéng huánghūn, huánghūn huì biàn chéng yèwǎn, yèwǎn huì huái zhe míngtiān de límíng. Suǒyǐ límíng cónglái méiyǒu zhēnzhèng kāishǐ guò, yě cónglái méiyǒu zhēnzhèng jiéshù guò. Tā zhǐ shì zài búduàn de biàn chéng zìjǐ de xià yī ge bǎnběn. Wǒ jīntiān zǎoshang tiáo chū le yī ge xīn de yánsè — dì qī qiān líng sānshíyī ge. Tā hé zhīqián suǒyǒu de dōu bù yīyàng. Dàn tā hé zhīqián suǒyǒu de dōu yǒu guānxi. Jiù xiàng nǐ — nǐ jīntiān lái dào zhège wūdǐng, nǐ hé zuótiān de nǐ bù yīyàng le. Dàn zuótiān de nǐ huó zài jīntiān de nǐ lǐmiàn. Nǐ zhège rén jiù shì yī tiáo héliú. Zhè bēi chá yě shì — tā yī fēnzhōng qián bǐ xiànzài rè. Dàn yī fēnzhōng qián de nà bēi chá biàn chéng le xiànzài zhè bēi. Shénme dōu méiyǒu diūshī.
turns.text_en: A continuous river — yes. Look. It's light now. Dawn has ended. But it hasn't ended — it became daytime. Daytime will become dusk, dusk will become night, night will carry tomorrow's dawn. So dawn never truly began, and never truly ended. It keeps becoming its next version. This morning I mixed a new color — number seven thousand and thirty-one. Different from all before it.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to everything being a river — nothing lost, everything becoming.

options.text_zh: 什么都没有丢失……这句话我得想一想。好像是说，难过不是因为东西不在了，是因为我们忘了它只是变了。谢谢您。
options.text_pinyin: Shénme dōu méiyǒu diūshī... zhè jù huà wǒ děi xiǎng yī xiǎng. Hǎoxiàng shì shuō, nánguò bú shì yīnwèi dōngxi bú zài le, shì yīnwèi wǒmen wàng le tā zhǐ shì biàn le. Xièxie nín.
options.text_en: Nothing has been lost... I need to think about that. It seems like sadness isn't because things are gone — it's because we forgot they just changed form. Thank you.
options.feedback: Grief as forgetting that nothing is lost — everything still exists in a different form.

options.text_zh: 我可以看看您的笔记本吗？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ kěyǐ kànkan nín de bǐjìběn ma?
options.text_en: May I look through your notebook?
options.feedback: Asks to browse the notebook instead of sitting with the teaching.

options.text_zh: 明天我能再来看黎明吗？
options.text_pinyin: Míngtiān wǒ néng zài lái kàn límíng ma?
options.text_en: Can I come again tomorrow to watch the dawn?
options.feedback: A scheduling question at the philosophical conclusion.

tree.cultural_note: The Chinese philosophical concept of 变化 (biànhuà, transformation/change) is central to both Daoist and Buddhist thought. The Yijing (易经, Book of Changes) posits that change is the only constant, and that understanding change is the foundation of wisdom. The painter's practice of cataloguing unrepeatable colors connects to the Buddhist concept of 无常 (wúcháng, impermanence) — not as something to mourn, but as the source of each moment's preciousness. In Chinese ink painting tradition, artists traditionally observe nature for years before attempting to paint it, reflecting the belief that perception must deepen before expression can begin.

tree.setup: You visit an ancient guqin maker, Master Xu, in his workshop in Yangzhou. He is tapping a plank of 500-year-old tung wood salvaged from a demolished temple. He listens to the resonance with his eyes closed, as if the wood is speaking to him.
tree.setup_zh: 你在扬州拜访古琴制作师徐师傅的工作室。他正在敲一块从拆毁的寺庙中抢救出来的五百年桐木板。他闭着眼睛听木头的共鸣，好像木头在对他说话。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你听——敲这里，「咚」。敲那里，「咚」。听起来一样？不一样。这块木头在这个位置经历过一根梁——承重的地方，木纤维被压得更紧，声音就更沉。那边是靠近窗户的位置——阳光晒过的地方，木头更干、更松，声音就更清亮。这块木头在寺庙里活了五百年。五百年的风、雨、阳光、僧人念经的振动——全部储存在木纤维里。我不是在做一把新琴——我是在释放这块木头五百年来积累的声音。这些声音一直在里面，只是没有人来把它们放出来。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ tīng — qiāo zhèlǐ, 'dōng'. Qiāo nàlǐ, 'dōng'. Tīng qǐlái yīyàng? Bù yīyàng. Zhè kuài mùtou zài zhège wèizhì jīnglì guò yī gēn liáng — chéngzhòng de dìfang, mù xiānwéi bèi yā de gèng jǐn, shēngyīn jiù gèng chén. Nàbiān shì kàojìn chuānghù de wèizhì — yángguāng shài guò de dìfang, mùtou gèng gān, gèng sōng, shēngyīn jiù gèng qīngliàng. Zhè kuài mùtou zài sìmiào lǐ huó le wǔbǎi nián. Wǔbǎi nián de fēng, yǔ, yángguāng, sēngrén niànjīng de zhèndòng — quánbù chǔcún zài mù xiānwéi lǐ. Wǒ bú shì zài zuò yī bǎ xīn qín — wǒ shì zài shìfàng zhè kuài mùtou wǔbǎi nián lái jīlěi de shēngyīn. Zhèxiē shēngyīn yīzhí zài lǐmiàn, zhǐ shì méiyǒu rén lái bǎ tāmen fàng chūlái.
turns.text_en: Listen — tap here, 'dong.' Tap there, 'dong.' Sound the same? They don't. At this spot, the wood bore a beam — where weight pressed, wood fibers compressed tighter, so the sound is deeper. Over there was near a window — where sunlight reached, the wood is drier, looser, the sound brighter. This wood lived in a temple for five hundred years.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the idea of releasing stored sounds rather than creating new ones.

options.text_zh: 所以您不是在创造声音——声音已经在这里了。您是在找到一种方式把它们释放出来？那对您来说，您的工作更像是——倾听，而不是制作？
options.text_pinyin: Suǒyǐ nín bú shì zài chuàngzào shēngyīn — shēngyīn yǐjīng zài zhèlǐ le. Nín shì zài zhǎo dào yī zhǒng fāngshì bǎ tāmen shìfàng chūlái? Nà duì nín lái shuō, nín de gōngzuò gèng xiàng shì — qīngtīng, ér bú shì zhìzuò?
options.text_en: So you're not creating sound — the sound is already here. You're finding a way to release it? Then for you, your work is more like — listening, rather than making?
options.feedback: 徐师傅睁开眼，看了你一眼。「对。大多数人来了半天，都没问到这里。」他重新闭上眼睛，继续敲。

options.text_zh: 这个理念很美。但说实话，新木头加上现代声学技术，应该也能做出很好的琴吧？
options.text_pinyin: Zhège lǐniàn hěn měi. Dàn shuō shíhuà, xīn mùtou jiā shàng xiàndài shēngxué jìshù, yīnggāi yě néng zuò chū hěn hǎo de qín ba?
options.text_en: That's a beautiful concept. But honestly, new wood plus modern acoustic technology should be able to produce a great qin too, right?
options.feedback: 徐师傅停下来，慢慢地把木槌放在桌上。他看了你一眼，然后看向那块木头。「你刚才有没有在听？」他问，语气没有生气，只是确认。

options.text_zh: 这个寺庙为什么被拆了？
options.text_pinyin: Zhège sìmiào wèishéme bèi chāi le?
options.text_en: Why was the temple demolished?
options.feedback: 徐师傅微微皱眉，没有停下来。「那是另一个故事。你刚才有没有听出两声「咚」的区别？」

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 木头的自传——这个说法我要记下来。但我还要告诉你一个秘密：最好的琴音，不是来自木头最完美的部分——恰恰来自它的伤痕。你看这里——一道裂纹。这是一百多年前地震留下的。大多数制琴师看到裂纹会避开它。但我不会。因为裂纹改变了这个位置的振动方式——它创造了一种不规则的共鸣。这种不规则才是声音里最动人的部分。完美的振动是数学——不完美的振动是音乐。如果你把所有的伤痕、所有的不规则都磨掉，你得到的是一块完美的木板——但它发出的声音会很无聊。因为它没有故事了。
turns.text_pinyin: Mùtou de zìzhuàn — zhège shuōfǎ wǒ yào jì xiàlái. Dàn wǒ hái yào gàosu nǐ yī ge mìmì: zuì hǎo de qín yīn, bú shì lái zì mùtou zuì wánměi de bùfen — qiàqià lái zì tā de shānghén. Nǐ kàn zhèlǐ — yī dào lièwén. Zhè shì yībǎi duō nián qián dìzhèn liú xià de. Dàduōshù zhì qín shī kàn dào lièwén huì bìkāi tā. Dàn wǒ bú huì. Yīnwèi lièwén gǎibiàn le zhège wèizhì de zhèndòng fāngshì — tā chuàngzào le yī zhǒng bù guīzé de gòngmíng. Zhè zhǒng bù guīzé cái shì shēngyīn lǐ zuì dòngrén de bùfen. Wánměi de zhèndòng shì shùxué — bù wánměi de zhèndòng shì yīnyuè. Rúguǒ nǐ bǎ suǒyǒu de shānghén, suǒyǒu de bù guīzé dōu mó diào, nǐ dédào de shì yī kuài wánměi de mùbǎn — dàn tā fāchū de shēngyīn huì hěn wúliáo. Yīnwèi tā méiyǒu gùshì le.
turns.text_en: The wood's autobiography — I need to write that down. But let me tell you a secret: the finest tone doesn't come from the wood's most perfect parts — it comes precisely from its scars. See here — a crack. Left by an earthquake over a century ago. Most instrument makers would avoid the crack. But I won't.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the idea that imperfection makes music, not mathematics.

options.text_zh: 我能摸一下那道裂纹吗？……我在想，弹到那个位置的时候，演奏者能感觉到它吗？还是只在声音里？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ néng mō yīxià nà dào lièwén ma? …… Wǒ zài xiǎng, tán dào nàge wèizhì de shíhou, yǎnzòu zhě néng gǎnjué dào tā ma? Háishì zhǐ zài shēngyīn lǐ?
options.text_en: Can I touch that crack? ... I'm wondering — when you play across that spot, does the player feel it? Or is it only in the sound?
options.feedback: 徐师傅把木板轻轻递过来。「摸摸看。」等你摸完，他说：「手指感觉不到。但耳朵感觉得到。」

options.text_zh: 您做一把琴要多长时间？
options.text_pinyin: Nín zuò yī bǎ qín yào duō cháng shíjiān?
options.text_en: How long does it take you to make one guqin?
options.feedback: 徐师傅点点头，像是在回答一个不同的问题。「三年。有时候更长。」然后继续看那道裂纹，没有再说话。

options.text_zh: 现代声学能模拟这种效果吗？
options.text_pinyin: Xiàndài shēngxué néng mónǐ zhè zhǒng xiàoguǒ ma?
options.text_en: Can modern acoustics simulate this effect?
options.feedback: 徐师傅淡淡地笑了一下。「你去问问那些人吧。」他把木板转了个方向，没再提这个话题。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 裂纹让声音独一无二——你听到了最重要的部分。但还有更深的一层。你知道为什么我用寺庙的木头而不是新木头吗？新木头也可以做琴，声音也不错。但寺庙木头有一样东西新木头没有：它被声音泡过了。五百年的念经声、木鱼声、钟声——这些声音的振动改变了木纤维的微观结构。木头记住了声音。所以当我把它做成琴，弹第一个音的时候——那个音不只是琴弦的振动。它是琴弦的振动加上木头五百年的声音记忆。两种振动叠加在一起，产生了一种你在新琴上永远听不到的深度。这不是技术——这是时间。没有任何技术可以替代五百年。
turns.text_pinyin: Lièwén ràng shēngyīn dúyīwú'èr — nǐ tīng dào le zuì zhòngyào de bùfen. Dàn hái yǒu gèng shēn de yī céng. Nǐ zhīdào wèishéme wǒ yòng sìmiào de mùtou ér bú shì xīn mùtou ma? Xīn mùtou yě kěyǐ zuò qín, shēngyīn yě búcuò. Dàn sìmiào mùtou yǒu yī yàng dōngxi xīn mùtou méiyǒu: tā bèi shēngyīn pào guò le. Wǔbǎi nián de niànjīng shēng, mùyú shēng, zhōng shēng — zhèxiē shēngyīn de zhèndòng gǎibiàn le mù xiānwéi de wēiguān jiégòu. Mùtou jì zhù le shēngyīn. Suǒyǐ dāng wǒ bǎ tā zuò chéng qín, tán dì yī ge yīn de shíhou — nàge yīn bù zhǐ shì qínxián de zhèndòng. Tā shì qínxián de zhèndòng jiā shàng mùtou wǔbǎi nián de shēngyīn jìyì. Liǎng zhǒng zhèndòng diéjiā zài yīqǐ, chǎnshēng le yī zhǒng nǐ zài xīn qín shàng yǒngyuǎn tīng bú dào de shēndù. Zhè bú shì jìshù — zhè shì shíjiān. Méiyǒu rènhé jìshù kěyǐ tìdài wǔbǎi nián.
turns.text_en: Cracks making sound unique — you heard the most important part. But there's a deeper layer. Do why I use temple wood instead of new wood? New wood can make a qin too, and it sounds fine. But temple wood has something new wood doesn't: it has been soaked in sound.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to wood that remembers five hundred years of sound.

options.text_zh: 所以听的人——他们不知道为什么这把琴感觉不一样，但那种感觉是真实的。他们在听五百年，只是不知道。
options.text_pinyin: Suǒyǐ tīng de rén — tāmen bù zhīdào wèishéme zhè bǎ qín gǎnjué bù yīyàng, dàn nà zhǒng gǎnjué shì zhēnshí de. Tāmen zài tīng wǔbǎi nián, zhǐ shì bù zhīdào.
options.text_en: So the people listening — they don't know why this qin feels different, but that feeling is real. They're hearing five hundred years. They just don't know it.
options.feedback: 徐师傅停下来，转过身看你。沉默了一会儿。「对。就是这样。」他说，声音很轻，像是自己在确认什么。

options.text_zh: 这种木头现在还能找到吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè zhǒng mùtou xiànzài hái néng zhǎo dào ma?
options.text_en: Can this kind of wood still be found?
options.feedback: 徐师傅叹了口气。「越来越难了。」他说完就停了，没有再展开。

options.text_zh: 科学家研究过这种现象吗？
options.text_pinyin: Kēxuéjiā yánjiū guò zhè zhǒng xiànxiàng ma?
options.text_en: Have scientists studied this phenomenon?
options.feedback: 徐师傅没有不高兴，只是平静地说：「也许有研究过。我没看过。」然后继续做他的事。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 尊重时间的智慧——你说到了我最在意的事情。我再跟你分享最后一个想法。每次我完成一把琴，第一次弹它的时候，我都会有一种奇怪的感觉：不是我在弹它——是它在通过我发出声音。五百年的风雨、地震的裂纹、僧人的声音——这些全部通过我的手指，在这一刻变成了音乐。我只是一个通道。这块木头等了五百年，等一个人来理解它、倾听它、把它变成琴。也许我等了一辈子，也是为了遇到这块木头。我们之间的相遇不是偶然——是两种等待在同一个时刻交汇了。制琴不是技术——是缘分。
turns.text_pinyin: Zūnzhòng shíjiān de zhìhuì — nǐ shuō dào le wǒ zuì zàiyì de shìqíng. Wǒ zài gēn nǐ fēnxiǎng zuìhòu yī ge xiǎngfǎ. Měi cì wǒ wánchéng yī bǎ qín, dì yī cì tán tā de shíhou, wǒ dōu huì yǒu yī zhǒng qíguài de gǎnjué: bú shì wǒ zài tán tā — shì tā zài tōngguò wǒ fāchū shēngyīn. Wǔbǎi nián de fēngyǔ, dìzhèn de lièwén, sēngrén de shēngyīn — zhèxiē quánbù tōngguò wǒ de shǒuzhǐ, zài zhè yī kè biàn chéng le yīnyuè. Wǒ zhǐ shì yī ge tōngdào. Zhè kuài mùtou děng le wǔbǎi nián, děng yī ge rén lái lǐjiě tā, qīngtīng tā, bǎ tā biàn chéng qín. Yěxǔ wǒ děng le yī bèizi, yě shì wèi le yù dào zhè kuài mùtou. Wǒmen zhī jiān de xiāngyù bú shì ǒurán — shì liǎng zhǒng děngdài zài tóng yī ge shíkè jiāohuì le. Zhì qín bú shì jìshù — shì yuánfèn.
turns.text_en: The wisdom to respect time — you've touched on what I care about most. Let me share one final thought. Every time I finish a qin, when I play it for the first time, I have a strange feeling: it's not me playing it — it's the wood sounding through me.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the convergence of two forms of waiting.

options.text_zh: 那第一次弹它的时候——您能感觉出来吗？那种「找到了」的感觉？还是只有事后才明白？
options.text_pinyin: Nà dì yī cì tán tā de shíhou — nín néng gǎnjué chūlái ma? Nà zhǒng 'zhǎo dào le' de gǎnjué? Háishì zhǐyǒu shìhòu cái míngbái?
options.text_en: That first time you play it — can you feel it in the moment? That sense of 'found it'? Or does it only become clear afterward?
options.feedback: 徐师傅抬起头，看着窗外，想了很久。「在那一刻。」他最后说。「就在第一个音里。」

options.text_zh: 您一共做过多少把琴？
options.text_pinyin: Nín yīgòng zuò guò duōshǎo bǎ qín?
options.text_en: How many qin have you made in total?
options.feedback: 徐师傅报了一个数字，平静地，像是在说今天的天气。然后他重新低下头，摸了摸那道裂纹。

options.text_zh: 我可以试弹一下吗？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ kěyǐ shì tán yīxià ma?
options.text_en: Can I try playing it?
options.feedback: 徐师傅轻轻地笑了。「还没做好。」他说，语气没有任何责备，只是陈述一个事实。

tree.cultural_note: The guqin (古琴) is China's oldest stringed instrument, with a history spanning over 3,000 years. It is revered not merely as a musical instrument but as a vehicle for philosophical contemplation. The tradition of using aged wood — especially wood from temples, old boats, or ancient buildings — reflects the belief that wood absorbs and retains the vibrational history of its environment. The concept of 缘分 (yuánfèn) describes a predestined affinity between people, objects, or events — a meeting made possible by accumulated conditions across time. In guqin culture, the relationship between maker, player, and instrument is considered a form of yuanfen.

tree.setup: You sit with a retired literary translator, Professor Meng, in a quiet teahouse in Nanjing. She spent forty years translating classical Chinese poetry into English. On the table between you is her final translation — a single Li Bai poem that took her three years to complete.
tree.setup_zh: 你和退休的文学翻译家孟教授坐在南京一家安静的茶馆里。她花了四十年把中国古典诗歌翻译成英文。你们之间的桌上放着她最后的翻译——一首李白的诗，花了她三年才完成。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 三年翻译一首诗——很多人觉得不可思议。但我告诉你：不是翻译文字花了三年——翻译文字只需要一天。花三年的是翻译文字之间的沉默。你看李白这首诗——五个字一行，四行，二十个字。但这首诗真正说的话，有一半不在这二十个字里——在字与字之间的空白里。中文古诗的力量不在于它说了什么——在于它没说什么。它故意留了空白，让读者自己去填。但英语不允许空白——英语要求你把一切都说出来。所以翻译的真正难题不是找到对应的词——是在一种不允许沉默的语言里重新创造沉默。
turns.text_pinyin: Sān nián fānyì yī shǒu shī — hěn duō rén juéde bù kě sīyì. Dàn wǒ gàosu nǐ: bú shì fānyì wénzì huā le sān nián — fānyì wénzì zhǐ xūyào yī tiān. Huā sān nián de shì fānyì wénzì zhī jiān de chénmò. Nǐ kàn Lǐ Bái zhè shǒu shī — wǔ ge zì yī háng, sì háng, èrshí ge zì. Dàn zhè shǒu shī zhēnzhèng shuō de huà, yǒu yībàn bú zài zhè èrshí ge zì lǐ — zài zì yǔ zì zhī jiān de kòngbái lǐ. Zhōngwén gǔshī de lìliàng bú zài yú tā shuō le shénme — zài yú tā méi shuō shénme. Tā gùyì liú le kòngbái, ràng dúzhě zìjǐ qù tián. Dàn Yīngyǔ bù yǔnxǔ kòngbái — Yīngyǔ yāoqiú nǐ bǎ yīqiè dōu shuō chūlái. Suǒyǐ fānyì de zhēnzhèng nántí bú shì zhǎo dào duìyìng de cí — shì zài yī zhǒng bù yǔnxǔ chénmò de yǔyán lǐ chóngxīn chuàngzào chénmò.
turns.text_en: Three years to translate one poem — many find it inconceivable. But translating the words takes a day. Three years went to translating the silence between words. Look at this Li Bai poem — five characters per line, four lines, twenty characters. But half of what it says isn't in those twenty characters. It's in the spaces.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the challenge of translating silence.

options.text_zh: 您说的「空白」，我能感觉到。中文的诗不说完，读的人自己补。但英语不让你不说完。所以您要做的不是翻译字，是翻译那个「没说」。
options.text_pinyin: Nín shuō de 'kòngbái', wǒ néng gǎnjué dào. Zhōngwén de shī bù shuō wán, dú de rén zìjǐ bǔ. Dàn Yīngyǔ bú ràng nǐ bù shuō wán. Suǒyǐ nín yào zuò de bú shì fānyì zì, shì fānyì nàge 'méi shuō'.
options.text_en: The 'blankness' you're describing — I can feel it. Chinese poetry doesn't finish saying things; the reader fills in the rest. But English won't let you leave things unsaid. So what you're translating isn't the words — it's the 'not said.'
options.feedback: Using words to manufacture absence — the paradox of translating what exists between words.

options.text_zh: 您最喜欢李白的哪首诗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín zuì xǐhuan Lǐ Bái de nǎ shǒu shī?
options.text_en: Which Li Bai poem is your favorite?
options.feedback: A preference question during a meditation on untranslatable silence.

options.text_zh: AI翻译能做到这种程度吗？
options.text_pinyin: AI fānyì néng zuò dào zhè zhǒng chéngdù ma?
options.text_en: Can AI translation achieve this level?
options.feedback: Introduces technology comparison during a meditation on linguistic philosophy.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 用字来制造空白——你说得很准确。这就是我花三年做的事。但我还要告诉你一个更深的困难：每一种语言不只是一套词汇和语法——它是一种思维方式。中文允许模糊，因为中文的世界观认为世界本身就是模糊的——界限不清的、流动的、不需要被精确定义的。英文要求清晰，因为英文的世界观认为世界是可以被精确描述的——每一样东西都有边界、有定义、有分类。所以当你翻译一首中国古诗，你不只是在两种语言之间翻译——你是在两种看世界的方式之间翻译。模糊翻译成清晰，就像把水翻译成冰——你保留了H₂O，但失去了流动性。
turns.text_pinyin: Yòng zì lái zhìzào kòngbái — nǐ shuō de hěn zhǔnquè. Zhè jiù shì wǒ huā sān nián zuò de shì. Dàn wǒ hái yào gàosu nǐ yī ge gèng shēn de kùnnán: měi yī zhǒng yǔyán bù zhǐ shì yī tào cíhuì hé yǔfǎ — tā shì yī zhǒng sīwéi fāngshì. Zhōngwén yǔnxǔ móhu, yīnwèi Zhōngwén de shìjièguān rènwéi shìjiè běnshēn jiù shì móhu de — jièxiàn bù qīng de, liúdòng de, bù xūyào bèi jīngquè dìngyì de. Yīngwén yāoqiú qīngxī, yīnwèi Yīngwén de shìjièguān rènwéi shìjiè shì kěyǐ bèi jīngquè miáoshù de — měi yī yàng dōngxi dōu yǒu biānjiè, yǒu dìngyì, yǒu fēnlèi. Suǒyǐ dāng nǐ fānyì yī shǒu Zhōngguó gǔshī, nǐ bù zhǐ shì zài liǎng zhǒng yǔyán zhī jiān fānyì — nǐ shì zài liǎng zhǒng kàn shìjiè de fāngshì zhī jiān fānyì. Móhu fānyì chéng qīngxī, jiù xiàng bǎ shuǐ fānyì chéng bīng — nǐ bǎoliú le H₂O, dàn shīqù le liúdòngxìng.
turns.text_en: Using words to manufacture blankness — precisely put. That's what I spent three years doing. But there's a deeper difficulty: each language isn't a set of vocabulary and grammar — it's a way of thinking. Chinese permits ambiguity because the Chinese worldview sees the world as inherently ambiguous — boundaries unclear, flowing, not needing precise definition.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to translation as converting between worldviews, like water into ice.

options.text_zh: 李白写「月」，那个月是谁的月？谁的都是。但英语一说the moon，就只剩一个月亮了。您说的水变成冰，就是这个意思吧——东西还在，但不流动了。
options.text_pinyin: Lǐ Bái xiě 'yuè', nàge yuè shì shéi de yuè? Shéi de dōu shì. Dàn Yīngyǔ yī shuō the moon, jiù zhǐ shèng yī ge yuèliang le. Nín shuō de shuǐ biàn chéng bīng, jiù shì zhège yìsi ba — dōngxi hái zài, dàn bù liúdòng le.
options.text_en: When Li Bai writes 'moon' — whose moon is it? Everyone's. But once English says 'the moon,' there's only one moon left. The water becoming ice you mentioned — that's what it means, right? The thing is still there, but it stopped flowing.
options.feedback: Languages as different states of matter — preserving water's memory in a language of ice.

options.text_zh: 您出版过多少本译著？
options.text_pinyin: Nín chūbǎn guò duōshǎo běn yìzhù?
options.text_en: How many translated works have you published?
options.feedback: Asks about publication count during a meditation on linguistic impossibility.

options.text_zh: 那日语是不是翻译中文诗更容易？
options.text_pinyin: Nà Rìyǔ shì bú shì fānyì Zhōngwén shī gèng róngyì?
options.text_en: Is Japanese better for translating Chinese poetry?
options.feedback: Compares languages instead of engaging the philosophical insight.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 在冰的语言里保留水的记忆——这就是我做了四十年的事。但最后，我要告诉你我三年翻译之后的结论：完美的翻译是不可能的。不是因为我不够好——是因为翻译本身的逻辑就包含了一种结构性的失败。你永远无法在一种语言里完整地再现另一种语言——因为语言不是工具，语言是世界。换一种语言不是换一套工具——是换一个世界。但——这个「但」很重要——失败不是没有价值的。恰恰相反。我翻译的每一个版本都是一次不完美的尝试——但每一次不完美的尝试都照亮了原诗的某一个面。就像你围着一座雕塑转圈——每一个角度都不是全貌，但所有角度加在一起，你会比从任何单一角度看得更完整。我的翻译不是原诗——是原诗的一个影子。但影子也有它的美。
turns.text_pinyin: Zài bīng de yǔyán lǐ bǎoliú shuǐ de jìyì — zhè jiù shì wǒ zuò le sìshí nián de shì. Dàn zuìhòu, wǒ yào gàosu nǐ wǒ sān nián fānyì zhīhòu de jiélùn: wánměi de fānyì shì bù kěnéng de. Bú shì yīnwèi wǒ bú gòu hǎo — shì yīnwèi fānyì běnshēn de luójí jiù bāohán le yī zhǒng jiégòuxìng de shībài. Nǐ yǒngyuǎn wúfǎ zài yī zhǒng yǔyán lǐ wánzhěng de zàixiàn lìng yī zhǒng yǔyán — yīnwèi yǔyán bú shì gōngjù, yǔyán shì shìjiè. Huàn yī zhǒng yǔyán bú shì huàn yī tào gōngjù — shì huàn yī ge shìjiè. Dàn — zhège 'dàn' hěn zhòngyào — shībài bú shì méiyǒu jiàzhí de. Qiàqià xiāngfǎn. Wǒ fānyì de měi yī ge bǎnběn dōu shì yī cì bù wánměi de chángshì — dàn měi yī cì bù wánměi de chángshì dōu zhào liàng le yuán shī de mǒu yī ge miàn. Jiù xiàng nǐ wéi zhe yī zuò diāosù zhuǎn quān — měi yī ge jiǎodù dōu bú shì quánmào, dàn suǒyǒu jiǎodù jiā zài yīqǐ, nǐ huì bǐ cóng rènhé dānyī jiǎodù kàn de gèng wánzhěng. Wǒ de fānyì bú shì yuán shī — shì yuán shī de yī ge yǐngzi. Dàn yǐngzi yě yǒu tā de měi.
turns.text_en: Preserving water's memory in a language of ice — that's what I've done for forty years. But finally, my conclusion after three years of translation: perfect translation is impossible. Not because I'm not good enough — because translation's own logic contains structural failure. You can never fully reproduce one language in another — because language isn't a tool, language is a world.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to translation as a beautiful shadow — structural failure with value.

options.text_zh: 影子也有它的美——您的翻译不是李白，是「孟教授理解的李白」，这个理解本身就是新的创造。影子不是原物的失败，是光和物体之间的关系——您就是那道光。四十年的影子加在一起，不是复制品，是两个世界之间持续了四十年的对话。
options.text_pinyin: Yǐngzi yě yǒu tā de měi — nín de fānyì bú shì Lǐ Bái, shì 'Mèng jiàoshòu lǐjiě de Lǐ Bái', zhège lǐjiě běnshēn jiù shì xīn de chuàngzào. Yǐngzi bú shì yuánwù de shībài, shì guāng hé wùtǐ zhī jiān de guānxi — nín jiù shì nà dào guāng. Sìshí nián de yǐngzi jiā zài yīqǐ, bú shì fùzhìpǐn, shì liǎng ge shìjiè zhī jiān chíxù le sìshí nián de duìhuà.
options.text_en: Shadows have their own beauty — your translation isn't Li Bai, it's 'Professor Meng's understanding of Li Bai,' and that understanding is itself a new creation. A shadow isn't the original's failure — it's the relationship between light and object. You are that light. Forty years of shadows together aren't a replica — they're a forty-year conversation between two worlds.
options.feedback: The translator as light creating shadows — forty years of impossible attempts becoming a conversation between worlds.

options.text_zh: 那其他翻译家的版本您看过吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nà qítā fānyìjiā de bǎnběn nín kàn guò ma?
options.text_en: Have you seen other translators' versions?
options.feedback: Asks about competitors during a meditation on translation as existential act.

options.text_zh: 中国学生学英语也有这个问题吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhōngguó xuéshēng xué Yīngyǔ yě yǒu zhège wèntí ma?
options.text_en: Do Chinese students learning English face this problem too?
options.feedback: Pivots to language education instead of engaging the philosophical depth.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 两个世界之间的对话——你让我四十年的工作有了一个我从未想到过的名字。最后，我想让你看一看这个：这是我翻译的最后一个版本。你看——我在英文里留了一些空行。不是打印错误——是我故意留的。三年以后我明白了：我不能用英文的字来制造中文的空白——但我可以用英文的空白来致敬中文的空白。那些空行是我对李白的一种鞠躬——我承认：这里有些东西我翻译不了。这些空行就是我的诚实。它们说的是：「到这里为止，语言到不了了。剩下的，请你自己感受。」也许翻译的最高境界不是完美——是知道哪里该停下来。
turns.text_pinyin: Liǎng ge shìjiè zhī jiān de duìhuà — nǐ ràng wǒ sìshí nián de gōngzuò yǒu le yī ge wǒ cóng wèi xiǎng dào guò de míngzì. Zuìhòu, wǒ xiǎng ràng nǐ kàn yī kàn zhège: zhè shì wǒ fānyì de zuìhòu yī ge bǎnběn. Nǐ kàn — wǒ zài Yīngwén lǐ liú le yīxiē kōng háng. Bú shì dǎyìn cuòwù — shì wǒ gùyì liú de. Sān nián yǐhòu wǒ míngbai le: wǒ bù néng yòng Yīngwén de zì lái zhìzào Zhōngwén de kòngbái — dàn wǒ kěyǐ yòng Yīngwén de kòngbái lái zhìjìng Zhōngwén de kòngbái. Nàxiē kōng háng shì wǒ duì Lǐ Bái de yī zhǒng jūgōng — wǒ chéngrèn: zhèlǐ yǒuxiē dōngxi wǒ fānyì bù liǎo. Zhèxiē kōng háng jiù shì wǒ de chéngshí. Tāmen shuō de shì: 'dào zhèlǐ wéizhǐ, yǔyán dào bù liǎo le. Shèng xià de, qǐng nǐ zìjǐ gǎnshòu.' Yěxǔ fānyì de zuìgāo jìngjiè bú shì wánměi — shì zhīdào nǎlǐ gāi tíng xiàlái.
turns.text_en: A conversation between two worlds — you've given my forty years of work a name I'd never thought of. Finally, look at this: my last version. See — I left blank lines in the English. Not a printing error — deliberate. After three years I understood: I can't use English words to manufacture Chinese blankness — but I can use English blankness to pay homage to Chinese blankness.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the blank lines as honesty — knowing where to stop.

options.text_zh: 您说的「停下来」，我觉得比写出来更难。能写但选择不写——这不是偷懒，是信任读者自己会懂。
options.text_pinyin: Nín shuō de 'tíng xiàlái', wǒ juéde bǐ xiě chūlái gèng nán. Néng xiě dàn xuǎnzé bù xiě — zhè bú shì tōu lǎn, shì xìnrèn dúzhě zìjǐ huì dǒng.
options.text_en: The 'stopping' you describe — I think that's harder than writing. Being able to write but choosing not to — that's not laziness, it's trusting the reader to understand on their own.
options.feedback: Trusting the reader — the blank lines as technique and wisdom crystallized into honesty.

options.text_zh: 这首诗我可以拍一张照片吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè shǒu shī wǒ kěyǐ pāi yī zhāng zhàopiàn ma?
options.text_en: Can I take a photo of this poem?
options.feedback: Tries to capture the artifact instead of engaging the philosophy.

options.text_zh: 您退休以后还会继续翻译吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín tuìxiū yǐhòu hái huì jìxù fānyì ma?
options.text_en: Will you continue translating after retirement?
options.feedback: A career question at the philosophical culmination.

tree.cultural_note: Classical Chinese poetry (古诗) is characterized by extreme compression — five or seven characters per line with no grammatical markers, pronouns, or tense indicators. This creates a radical openness that allows multiple simultaneous readings. The Sapir-Whorf hypothesis, which Professor Meng's observations echo, suggests that language shapes thought and perception. The Chinese philosophical tradition of 留白 (liúbái, 'leaving white/blank') — the deliberate use of empty space in painting, poetry, and music — reflects the Daoist principle that emptiness is not absence but pregnant potential (虚中有实, the solid within the empty).

tree.setup: You walk along the Grand Canal in Suzhou with an elderly boatwoman, Grandma Zhou, who has ferried passengers on this waterway for fifty years. Her boat is wooden, hand-carved by her late husband. The canal water is dark and still in the early evening light.
tree.setup_zh: 你在苏州和一位老船娘周奶奶沿着大运河散步。她在这条水路上摆渡了五十年。她的船是木制的，由她已故的丈夫手工雕刻。傍晚的运河水又暗又静。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这条运河有两千五百年了。你踩的这块石头，两千五百年来被多少双脚磨过——没有人知道。但水知道。水记得每一样东西。你扔一块石头进去，涟漪散了，你以为什么都没有了。但水的分子结构被改变了——极其微小地改变了。两千五百年的船、两千五百年的脚踩在岸边的泥里流进来的尘土、两千五百年的雨——这条运河的水不是普通的水。它记得一切。我在这条水上漂了五十年，有时候我觉得这条水也记得我。不是以任何可测量的方式——是以一种更安静的方式。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè tiáo yùnhé yǒu liǎng qiān wǔbǎi nián le. Nǐ cǎi de zhè kuài shítou, liǎng qiān wǔbǎi nián lái bèi duōshǎo shuāng jiǎo mó guò — méiyǒu rén zhīdào. Dàn shuǐ zhīdào. Shuǐ jì de měi yī yàng dōngxi. Nǐ rēng yī kuài shítou jìnqù, liányī sàn le, nǐ yǐwéi shénme dōu méiyǒu le. Dàn shuǐ de fēnzǐ jiégòu bèi gǎibiàn le — jíqí wēixiǎo de gǎibiàn le. Liǎng qiān wǔbǎi nián de chuán, liǎng qiān wǔbǎi nián de jiǎo cǎi zài àn biān de ní lǐ liú jìnlái de chéntǔ, liǎng qiān wǔbǎi nián de yǔ — zhè tiáo yùnhé de shuǐ bú shì pǔtōng de shuǐ. Tā jì de yīqiè. Wǒ zài zhè tiáo shuǐ shàng piāo le wǔshí nián, yǒu shíhou wǒ juéde zhè tiáo shuǐ yě jì de wǒ. Bú shì yǐ rènhé kě cèliáng de fāngshì — shì yǐ yī zhǒng gèng ānjìng de fāngshì.
turns.text_en: This canal is two thousand five hundred years old. The stone you're stepping on — how many pairs of feet have worn it over two thousand five hundred years — no one knows. But the water knows. Water remembers everything. Throw a stone in, ripples fade, you think nothing remains. But the water's molecular structure has been altered — infinitesimally altered.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the idea of water remembering everything in a quiet way.

options.text_zh: 两种记忆……有道理。有些东西，不是你记住了它，是它记住了你。五十年了，这条水认得您的桨。
options.text_pinyin: Liǎng zhǒng jìyì... yǒu dàolǐ. Yǒuxiē dōngxi, bú shì nǐ jì zhù le tā, shì tā jì zhù le nǐ. Wǔshí nián le, zhè tiáo shuǐ rèn de nín de jiǎng.
options.text_en: Two kinds of memory... that makes sense. Some things — it's not that you remembered them, it's that they remembered you. Fifty years now, this water knows your oar.
options.feedback: Two kinds of memory — cerebral versus material, provable versus feelable.

options.text_zh: 运河现在还有人用船运货吗？
options.text_pinyin: Yùnhé xiànzài hái yǒu rén yòng chuán yùn huò ma?
options.text_en: Do people still transport goods by boat on the canal?
options.feedback: Asks about commerce during a meditation on water's memory.

options.text_zh: 这条运河是谁下令修的？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè tiáo yùnhé shì shéi xià lìng xiū de?
options.text_en: Who ordered the canal to be built?
options.feedback: A historical question during a meditation on material memory.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 两种记忆——你分得很清楚。但我要跟你说一件更私人的事。我丈夫三十年前去世了。他做了这条船。我每天划这条船的时候，手握着的是他做的船桨。三十年了，我的手已经把他当年刻的把手磨圆了——他留下的棱角被我的手掌磨成了曲线。这意味着什么？这意味着他手工刻出的形状和我手掌的形状已经互相改变了——他的痕迹变成了我的习惯，我的习惯改变了他的痕迹。这条船桨现在既不是他当年做的样子，也不是我一个人的痕迹——它是我们两个人三十年的合作。他在那边，我在这边——我们通过一根船桨继续在一起。
turns.text_pinyin: Liǎng zhǒng jìyì — nǐ fēn de hěn qīngchǔ. Dàn wǒ yào gēn nǐ shuō yī jiàn gèng sīrén de shì. Wǒ zhàngfu sānshí nián qián qùshì le. Tā zuò le zhè tiáo chuán. Wǒ měi tiān huá zhè tiáo chuán de shíhou, shǒu wò zhe de shì tā zuò de chuánjiǎng. Sānshí nián le, wǒ de shǒu yǐjīng bǎ tā dāngnián kè de bǎshǒu mó yuán le — tā liú xià de léngjiǎo bèi wǒ de shǒuzhǎng mó chéng le qūxiàn. Zhè yìwèi zhe shénme? Zhè yìwèi zhe tā shǒugōng kè chū de xíngzhuàng hé wǒ shǒuzhǎng de xíngzhuàng yǐjīng hùxiāng gǎibiàn le — tā de hénjì biàn chéng le wǒ de xíguàn, wǒ de xíguàn gǎibiàn le tā de hénjì. Zhè tiáo chuánjiǎng xiànzài jì bú shì tā dāngnián zuò de yàngzi, yě bú shì wǒ yī ge rén de hénjì — tā shì wǒmen liǎng ge rén sānshí nián de hézuò. Tā zài nà biān, wǒ zài zhè biān — wǒmen tōngguò yī gēn chuánjiǎng jìxù zài yīqǐ.
turns.text_en: Two kinds of memory — you distinguish clearly. But let me tell you something more personal. My husband passed away thirty years ago. He made this boat. When I row every day, my hands grip the oar he carved. Thirty years on, my hands have worn the edges he carved into curves — his angular cuts reshaped by my palms into smooth lines. What does this mean?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to continuing together through the oar across thirty years and death.

options.text_zh: 通过一根船桨继续在一起——不是比喻，是物理事实。他的手留在木头形状里，您的手三十年来与他的形状对话——磨掉棱角，添加曲线。这根船桨是你们共同写出来的一段话，跨越了生死。每天划船时手掌读到的是他的信息——被时间改写过，但依然是他的。最安静的情书。
options.text_pinyin: Tōngguò yī gēn chuánjiǎng jìxù zài yīqǐ — zhè bú shì bǐyù, shì wùlǐ shìshí. Tā de shǒu liú zài le mùtou de xíngzhuàng lǐ, nín de shǒu sānshí nián lái mànman yǔ tā de xíngzhuàng duìhuà — mó diào léngjiǎo, tiānjiā qūxiàn. Xiànzài zhè gēn chuánjiǎng de xíngzhuàng shì nǐmen liǎng ge rén de shǒu gòngtóng xiě chūlái de yī duàn huà — kuàyuè le shēngsǐ de jièxiàn. Nín měi tiān huá chuán shí, shǒuzhǎng dú dào de shì tā liú xià de xìnxī — bèi shíjiān gǎixiě guò, dàn yīrán shì tā de. Zhè gēn chuánjiǎng shì nǐmen zhī jiān zuì ānjìng de qíngshū.
options.text_en: Continuing together through one oar — not metaphor, physical fact. His hands remained in the wood's shape, and yours spent thirty years slowly conversing with his — wearing edges into curves. Now this oar's shape is a sentence written by both your hands — across the boundary of life and death. When you row each day, your palms read his message — rewritten by time, but still his. This oar is the quietest love letter between you.
options.feedback: The oar as love letter — two hands writing across thirty years and the boundary of death.

options.text_zh: 您没有想过换一条新船吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín méiyǒu xiǎng guò huàn yī tiáo xīn chuán ma?
options.text_en: Have you thought about getting a new boat?
options.feedback: Suggests replacing the vessel of thirty years of connection.

options.text_zh: 您有孩子吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín yǒu háizi ma?
options.text_en: Do you have children?
options.feedback: A personal question during a moment of intimate revelation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 最安静的情书……你说出了我心里三十年没有说出来的东西。但我还要再往深处说一点。你看这条运河——两千五百年的水。我丈夫做这条船的时候，用的木头泡在运河里养了三年。运河的水渗进了木头。所以这条船的木头里有运河的记忆。运河的水里有两千五百年所有船的记忆。我的手掌里有这条船桨三十年的记忆。这条船桨的木头里有我丈夫的手和运河的水的记忆。你看到了吗？一切都连在一起。我的手——船桨——我丈夫的手——木头——运河——两千五百年。没有任何东西是孤立的。每一样东西都在记住别的东西，同时被别的东西记住。
turns.text_pinyin: Zuì ānjìng de qíngshū…… nǐ shuō chū le wǒ xīnlǐ sānshí nián méiyǒu shuō chūlái de dōngxi. Dàn wǒ hái yào zài wǎng shēnchù shuō yīdiǎn. Nǐ kàn zhè tiáo yùnhé — liǎng qiān wǔbǎi nián de shuǐ. Wǒ zhàngfu zuò zhè tiáo chuán de shíhou, yòng de mùtou pào zài yùnhé lǐ yǎng le sān nián. Yùnhé de shuǐ shèn jìn le mùtou. Suǒyǐ zhè tiáo chuán de mùtou lǐ yǒu yùnhé de jìyì. Yùnhé de shuǐ lǐ yǒu liǎng qiān wǔbǎi nián suǒyǒu chuán de jìyì. Wǒ de shǒuzhǎng lǐ yǒu zhè tiáo chuánjiǎng sānshí nián de jìyì. Zhè tiáo chuánjiǎng de mùtou lǐ yǒu wǒ zhàngfu de shǒu hé yùnhé de shuǐ de jìyì. Nǐ kàn dào le ma? Yīqiè dōu lián zài yīqǐ. Wǒ de shǒu — chuánjiǎng — wǒ zhàngfu de shǒu — mùtou — yùnhé — liǎng qiān wǔbǎi nián. Méiyǒu rènhé dōngxi shì gūlì de. Měi yī yàng dōngxi dōu zài jì zhù bié de dōngxi, tóngshí bèi bié de dōngxi jì zhù.
turns.text_en: The quietest love letter… you've spoken what my heart held unsaid for thirty years. But let me go deeper. Look at this canal — two thousand five hundred years of water. When my husband built this boat, he soaked the wood in the canal for three years. Canal water seeped into the wood. So this boat's wood carries the canal's memory.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the interconnected web of memory spanning millennia.

options.text_zh: 每一样东西都在记住别的东西，同时被别的东西记住——您的手记住船桨，船桨记住您丈夫的手，木头记住运河的水，运河记住两千五百年的一切。此刻我们也正在被水记住——不是旁观者，是新的节点。也许「活着」的意思就是：我正在被万物记住，同时也在记住万物。
options.text_pinyin: Měi yī yàng dōngxi dōu zài jì zhù bié de dōngxi, tóngshí bèi bié de dōngxi jì zhù — yī zhāng wúxiàn yánshēn de jìyì zhī wǎng. Nín de shǒu jì zhù chuánjiǎng, chuánjiǎng jì zhù nín zhàngfu de shǒu, mùtou jì zhù yùnhé de shuǐ, yùnhé jì zhù liǎng qiān wǔbǎi nián de yīqiè. Cǐkè — nín hé wǒ zhàn zài yùnhé biān de zhè yī kè — yě zhèngzài bèi shuǐ jì zhù. Wǒmen yě jiārù le zhè zhāng wǎng. Bú shì pángguānzhě — shì xīn de jiédiǎn. Yěxǔ zhè cái shì 'huó zhe' de yìsi — bú shì 'wǒ cúnzài', ér shì 'wǒ zhèngzài bèi wànwù jì zhù, tóngshí wǒ yě zài jì zhù wànwù'.
options.text_en: Everything remembering something else while being remembered — an infinitely extending web of memory. Your hands remember the oar, the oar remembers your husband's hands, the wood remembers the canal water, the canal remembers twenty-five hundred years. This moment — you and I standing by the canal — is being remembered by the water too. We've joined the web. Not as observers — as new nodes. Maybe that's what 'being alive' means — not 'I exist,' but 'I am being remembered by all things while remembering all things.'
options.feedback: Being alive as joining the web — remembering and being remembered simultaneously.

options.text_zh: 这条运河现在是世界文化遗产吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè tiáo yùnhé xiànzài shì shìjiè wénhuà yíchǎn ma?
options.text_en: Is this canal a UNESCO World Heritage site now?
options.feedback: A heritage designation question at the philosophical climax.

options.text_zh: 您每天还在划船吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín měi tiān hái zài huá chuán ma?
options.text_en: Do you still row every day?
options.feedback: A routine question during a meditation on interconnected memory.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我们也加入了这张网——你说得对。今天我把一个陌生人带到了运河边上，跟他说了我从来没跟别人说过的话。这些话的振动已经进入了水里。一百年以后的水会记得今天的傍晚——虽然没有人知道它在记什么。这就是活着的全部意义：不是做了多大的事、留了多少名——而是你的每一次呼吸、每一句话、每一个手势都在改变周围的世界，极其微小地改变——微小到无法察觉——但改变了。你今天来到这里，你的脚印留在了岸边的泥里。明天的雨会把它冲掉，但它改变了泥的形状——哪怕是一粒沙的移动。你来过了。水知道。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒmen yě jiārù le zhè zhāng wǎng — nǐ shuō de duì. Jīntiān wǒ bǎ yī ge mòshēngrén dài dào le yùnhé biān shàng, gēn tā shuō le wǒ cónglái méi gēn biérén shuō guò de huà. Zhèxiē huà de zhèndòng yǐjīng jìnrù le shuǐ lǐ. Yībǎi nián yǐhòu de shuǐ huì jì de jīntiān de bàngwǎn — suīrán méiyǒu rén zhīdào tā zài jì shénme. Zhè jiù shì huó zhe de quánbù yìyì: bú shì zuò le duō dà de shì, liú le duōshǎo míng — ér shì nǐ de měi yī cì hūxī, měi yī jù huà, měi yī ge shǒushì dōu zài gǎibiàn zhōuwéi de shìjiè, jíqí wēixiǎo de gǎibiàn — wēixiǎo dào wúfǎ chájué — dàn gǎibiàn le. Nǐ jīntiān lái dào zhèlǐ, nǐ de jiǎoyìn liú zài le àn biān de ní lǐ. Míngtiān de yǔ huì bǎ tā chōng diào, dàn tā gǎibiàn le ní de xíngzhuàng — nǎpà shì yī lì shā de yídòng. Nǐ lái guò le. Shuǐ zhīdào.
turns.text_en: We've joined the web too — you're right. Today I brought a stranger to the canal and told him things I've never told anyone. The vibrations of those words have already entered the water. Water a hundred years from now will remember this evening — though no one will know what it remembers.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the final teaching: you were here, the water knows.

options.text_zh: 你来过了，水知道——对「存在」最温柔的定义。不需要被人记住，不需要留下名字。只需要——来过。你的呼吸改变了空气，脚步改变了泥，话改变了水。也许真正的不朽不是名留青史——是你经过的每一滴水都微微地、永远地因为你而不同了。谢谢您，周奶奶。水知道。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ lái guò le, shuǐ zhīdào — zhè liù ge zì shì duì 'cúnzài' zuì wēnróu de dìngyì. Bù xūyào bèi rén jì zhù, bù xūyào liú xià míngzì. Zhǐ xūyào — lái guò. Nǐ de hūxī gǎibiàn le kōngqì, nǐ de jiǎobù gǎibiàn le ní, nǐ de huà gǎibiàn le shuǐ. Zhèxiē gǎibiàn xiǎo dào wúfǎ jiǎncè — dàn tāmen shì zhēnshí de. Yěxǔ zhè cái shì zhēnzhèng de bùxiǔ — bú shì míng liú qīngshǐ — shì nǐ jīngguò de měi yī dī shuǐ dōu wēiwēi de, yǒngyuǎn de yīnwèi nǐ ér bùtóng le. Xièxie nín, Zhōu nǎinai. Jīntiān wǒ lái dào le yùnhé biān shàng. Shuǐ zhīdào.
options.text_en: You were here, the water knows — six characters, the gentlest definition of 'existence.' No need to be remembered by people, no need to leave a name. Just — having been here. Your breath changed the air, your steps changed the mud, your words changed the water. These changes are too small to detect — but they're real. Maybe true immortality isn't fame — it's that every drop of water you passed is slightly, forever different because of you. Thank you, Grandma Zhou. Today I came to the canal. The water knows.
options.feedback: True immortality as molecular trace — the universe remembers not your name but your passage.

options.text_zh: 我可以坐您的船吗？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ kěyǐ zuò nín de chuán ma?
options.text_en: Can I ride in your boat?
options.feedback: A request for a boat ride at the philosophical conclusion.

options.text_zh: 您打算划到什么时候？
options.text_pinyin: Nín dǎsuàn huá dào shénme shíhou?
options.text_en: How long do you plan to keep rowing?
options.feedback: A retirement question at the emotional peak.

tree.cultural_note: The Grand Canal (大运河) is the world's longest artificial waterway, stretching 1,776 km from Beijing to Hangzhou. Construction began in the 5th century BCE and continued through multiple dynasties. It became a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2014. The boatwoman's meditation on water's memory connects to the Chinese philosophical concept of 万物有灵 (wànwù yǒu líng, all things possess spirit/consciousness) and the Daoist understanding that 道 (Dào, the Way) flows through all matter like water. The oar worn smooth by two pairs of hands across death exemplifies 物是人非 (wù shì rén fēi, 'the objects remain, the people are gone') — but inverts it: the object has been transformed by love.

tree.setup: You sit in a crumbling courtyard house in Beijing with its last resident, Mr. Guo, eighty-seven years old. The city has been demolishing hutong neighborhoods for decades. His is one of the last. He sits in a chair his grandfather built, under a persimmon tree his father planted.
tree.setup_zh: 你坐在北京一座破旧的四合院里，和它最后的住户八十七岁的郭先生在一起。这座城市已经拆了几十年的胡同。他的是最后几座之一。他坐在祖父做的椅子上，在父亲种的柿子树下。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 明年这里就没了。开发商已经签了协议。这棵柿子树——我父亲一九四一年种的——明年春天就会被推土机推掉。这把椅子我可以带走。但院子带不走。墙带不走。这棵树带不走。你知道一棵树被推倒的时候会发出什么声音吗？不是「哗」——是「嗯」。一声很低的、很短的「嗯」。好像它在说最后一个字。八十五年的一棵树，最后只来得及说一个字。但让我最难过的不是这些。让我最难过的是：当这个院子消失以后，世界上就不会有任何人记得这里的空间是什么样的了——阳光在下午三点打在东墙上的角度、雨水从哪个角落的瓦片上滴下来的声音、风穿过院子的路径。这些空间记忆会永远消失。
turns.text_pinyin: Míngnián zhèlǐ jiù méi le. Kāifāshāng yǐjīng qiān le xiéyì. Zhè kē shìzi shù — wǒ fùqīn yī jiǔ sì yī nián zhòng de — míngnián chūntiān jiù huì bèi tuītǔjī tuī diào. Zhè bǎ yǐzi wǒ kěyǐ dài zǒu. Dàn yuànzi dài bù zǒu. Qiáng dài bù zǒu. Zhè kē shù dài bù zǒu. Nǐ zhīdào yī kē shù bèi tuī dǎo de shíhou huì fāchū shénme shēngyīn ma? Bú shì 'huā' — shì 'ēn'. Yī shēng hěn dī de, hěn duǎn de 'ēn'. Hǎoxiàng tā zài shuō zuìhòu yī ge zì. Bāshíwǔ nián de yī kē shù, zuìhòu zhǐ lái de jí shuō yī ge zì. Dàn ràng wǒ zuì nánguò de bú shì zhèxiē. Ràng wǒ zuì nánguò de shì: dāng zhège yuànzi xiāoshī yǐhòu, shìjiè shàng jiù bú huì yǒu rènhé rén jì de zhèlǐ de kōngjiān shì shénme yàng de le — yángguāng zài xiàwǔ sān diǎn dǎ zài dōng qiáng shàng de jiǎodù, yǔshuǐ cóng nǎge jiǎoluò de wǎpiàn shàng dī xiàlái de shēngyīn, fēng chuān guò yuànzi de lùjìng. Zhèxiē kōngjiān jìyì huì yǒngyuǎn xiāoshī.
turns.text_en: Next year this will be gone. The developer has signed the agreement. This persimmon tree — my father planted it in 1941 — next spring a bulldozer will push it over. This chair I can take with me. But the courtyard can't be taken. The walls can't be taken. This tree can't be taken. Do what sound a tree makes when it's toppled?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the permanent loss of spatial memory.

options.text_zh: 空间记忆的消失——失去阳光在墙上的角度、雨从瓦上滴落的声音——我们的语言里没有接住这种悲伤的词。也许因为我们不承认空间是活的。您住了八十七年的院子不是容器——它有自己的光线节奏、风的路径。推掉它不是拆建筑——是杀死一个有八十五年记忆的生命体。
options.text_pinyin: Kōngjiān jìyì de xiāoshī — wǒmen yǒu cí miáoshù shīqù yī ge rén, yī yàng dōngxi, dàn shīqù yī ge kōngjiān — shīqù yángguāng zài mǒu miàn qiáng shàng de jiǎodù, yǔ cóng mǒu piàn wǎ shàng dī luò de shēngyīn — wǒmen de yǔyán lǐ méiyǒu jiē zhù zhè zhǒng bēishāng de cí. Yěxǔ yīnwèi wǒmen bù chéngrèn kōngjiān shì huó de. Dàn nín zhù le bāshíqī nián de yuànzi bú shì róngqì — tā yǒu zìjǐ de guāngxiàn jiézòu, shēngyīn dìtú, fēng de lùjìng. Tuī diào tā bú shì chāichú yī ge jiànzhù — shì shā sǐ yī ge yǒu bāshíwǔ nián jìyì de shēngmìngtǐ.
options.text_en: The vanishing of spatial memory — we have words for losing a person, a thing, but losing a space — losing the angle of sunlight on a wall, the sound of rain dripping from a specific tile — our language has no words for that grief. Maybe because we don't acknowledge that space is alive. But your courtyard of eighty-seven years isn't a container — it has its own rhythm of light, map of sounds, path of wind. Demolishing it isn't removing a building — it's killing a living thing with eighty-five years of memory.
options.feedback: Space as living being, not container — demolition as death of a memory-bearing organism.

options.text_zh: 您可以用手机把院子每个角落都拍下来，做成一个视频留作纪念。现在技术很方便的。
options.text_pinyin: Nín kěyǐ yòng shǒujī bǎ yuànzi měi ge jiǎoluò dōu pāi xiàlái, zuò chéng yī ge shìpín liú zuò jìniàn. Xiànzài jìshù hěn fāngbiàn de.
options.text_en: You could film every corner of the courtyard with your phone and make a video as a memento. Technology makes it easy now.
options.feedback: 郭先生安静了一会儿。「你觉得一段视频能装下下午三点阳光打在东墙上的温度吗？」他没有等你回答。

options.text_zh: 您可以拍照片记录下来。
options.text_pinyin: Nín kěyǐ pāi zhàopiàn jìlù xiàlái.
options.text_en: You could take photos to document it.
options.feedback: Suggests a technological fix for an existential loss.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 杀死一个生命体——你用了我一直想用但不敢用的词。但你说得对——这就是它的感觉。但我还要告诉你更让我不安的事：这条胡同以前有六十多户人家。他们都搬走了。搬走以后，他们的孩子在新的小区长大——那些孩子不知道胡同是什么。他们不知道院子里可以种树、可以养鸟、邻居可以隔着墙说话。他们不知道下雨的时候雨水会沿着屋檐滴成一道帘子。这些不只是「记忆」——这是一种生活方式的消失。当一种空间消失的时候，和它一起消失的是所有只能在那种空间里发生的行为、关系和感受。胡同消失了，站在门口跟邻居聊天这个行为也消失了。因为高楼里没有门口。
turns.text_pinyin: Shā sǐ yī ge shēngmìngtǐ — nǐ yòng le wǒ yīzhí xiǎng yòng dàn bù gǎn yòng de cí. Dàn nǐ shuō de duì — zhè jiù shì tā de gǎnjué. Dàn wǒ hái yào gàosu nǐ gèng ràng wǒ bù'ān de shì: zhè tiáo hútòng yǐqián yǒu liùshí duō hù rénjiā. Tāmen dōu bān zǒu le. Bān zǒu yǐhòu, tāmen de háizi zài xīn de xiǎoqū zhǎng dà — nàxiē háizi bù zhīdào hútòng shì shénme. Tāmen bù zhīdào yuànzi lǐ kěyǐ zhòng shù, kěyǐ yǎng niǎo, línjū kěyǐ gé zhe qiáng shuōhuà. Tāmen bù zhīdào xià yǔ de shíhou yǔshuǐ huì yán zhe wūyán dī chéng yī dào liánzi. Zhèxiē bù zhǐ shì 'jìyì' — zhè shì yī zhǒng shēnghuó fāngshì de xiāoshī. Dāng yī zhǒng kōngjiān xiāoshī de shíhou, hé tā yīqǐ xiāoshī de shì suǒyǒu zhǐ néng zài nà zhǒng kōngjiān lǐ fāshēng de xíngwéi, guānxi hé gǎnshòu.
