Hútòng xiāoshī le, zhàn zài ménkǒu gēn línjū liáotiān zhège xíngwéi yě xiāoshī le. Yīnwèi gāolóu lǐ méiyǒu ménkǒu.
turns.text_en: Killing a living thing — you used the word I've always wanted to use but didn't dare. You're right — that's exactly how it feels. But let me tell you something more unsettling: this hutong once had over sixty households. They all moved away. After moving, their children grew up in new apartment complexes — those children don't know what a hutong is.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to space shaping behavior — when the hutong vanishes, the behaviors it enabled vanish too.

options.text_zh: 空间塑造行为——有些行为不是人「选择」的，是空间「邀请」的。胡同门口邀请你站着聊天，尺度刚好。高楼走廊邀请你低头走过，设计就是让你经过不停留。失去的不只是建筑——是被空间邀请出来的人际关系。隔墙聊天的亲密、院子里晒太阳的安静——只能在那种空间里生长。
options.text_pinyin: Kōngjiān sùzào xíngwéi — yǒuxiē xíngwéi bú shì yóu rén 'xuǎnzé' de, shì yóu kōngjiān 'yāoqǐng' de. Hútòng de ménkǒu yāoqǐng nǐ zhàn zhe liáotiān, yīnwèi chǐdù gānghǎo. Gāolóu de zǒuláng yāoqǐng nǐ dītóu zǒu guò, yīnwèi shèjì jiù shì ràng nǐ jīngguò ér bú shì tíngliú. Wǒmen shīqù de bù zhǐ shì jiànzhù xíngshì — shì yī zhěng tào bèi kōngjiān yāoqǐng chūlái de rénjì guānxi. Gé zhe qiáng liáotiān de qīnmì gǎn, yuànzi lǐ yīqǐ shài tàiyáng de ānjìng — zhèxiē guānxi zhǐ néng zài nà zhǒng kōngjiān lǐ shēngzhǎng. Bǎ kōngjiān huàn le, guānxi jiù zhǎng bù chūlái le.
options.text_en: Space shapes behavior — some behaviors aren't 'chosen' by people, they're 'invited' by space. A hutong doorway invites you to stand and chat because the scale is just right. A high-rise corridor invites you to walk past with head down because it's designed for passing, not staying. What we've lost isn't just an architectural form — it's an entire set of human relationships invited into existence by space. The intimacy of chatting through walls, the quiet of sunbathing together in a courtyard — these relationships can only grow in that kind of space. Change the space, and the relationships can't take root.
options.feedback: Space as invitation — relationships are grown by architecture, not just chosen by people.

options.text_zh: 政府有保护胡同的计划吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhèngfǔ yǒu bǎohù hútòng de jìhuà ma?
options.text_en: Does the government have plans to protect hutongs?
options.feedback: Asks about policy during a meditation on spatial epistemology.

options.text_zh: 新的小区也有社区活动啊。
options.text_pinyin: Xīn de xiǎoqū yě yǒu shèqū huódòng a.
options.text_en: New apartment complexes have community activities too.
options.feedback: Offers a substitute that misses the point about organic versus organized community.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 空间邀请了关系——你看得比很多建筑师都透。但最后我想说一件最个人的事。我坐在这把椅子上，在这棵柿子树下，已经坐了——我算算——大概七十年了。七十年的每个秋天，柿子会从树上掉下来，砸在地上——「啪」。小时候我等这个声音等得很开心——因为柿子可以吃。后来我等这个声音变成了一种习惯。再后来我等这个声音变成了一种安慰——在所有东西都在变化的时候，柿子还是每年秋天掉下来。明年秋天这棵树就不在了。那声「啪」会从世界上消失。不是说世界上没有别的柿子树了——是这棵树的柿子掉在这个院子的这块地上发出的那个特定的「啪」——那个声音只属于这里。属于我。明年秋天它就不在了——我也不知道我还在不在。
turns.text_pinyin: Kōngjiān yāoqǐng le guānxi — nǐ kàn de bǐ hěn duō jiànzhùshī dōu tòu. Dàn zuìhòu wǒ xiǎng shuō yī jiàn zuì gèrén de shì. Wǒ zuò zài zhè bǎ yǐzi shàng, zài zhè kē shìzi shù xià, yǐjīng zuò le — wǒ suàn suàn — dàgài qīshí nián le. Qīshí nián de měi ge qiūtiān, shìzi huì cóng shù shàng diào xiàlái, zá zài dì shàng — 'pā'. Xiǎo shíhou wǒ děng zhège shēngyīn děng de hěn kāixīn — yīnwèi shìzi kěyǐ chī. Hòulái wǒ děng zhège shēngyīn biàn chéng le yī zhǒng xíguàn. Zài hòulái wǒ děng zhège shēngyīn biàn chéng le yī zhǒng ānwèi — zài suǒyǒu dōngxi dōu zài biànhuà de shíhou, shìzi hái shì měi nián qiūtiān diào xiàlái. Míngnián qiūtiān zhè kē shù jiù bú zài le. Nà shēng 'pā' huì cóng shìjiè shàng xiāoshī. Bú shì shuō shìjiè shàng méiyǒu bié de shìzi shù le — shì zhè kē shù de shìzi diào zài zhège yuànzi de zhè kuài dì shàng fāchū de nàge tèdìng de 'pā' — nàge shēngyīn zhǐ shǔyú zhèlǐ. Shǔyú wǒ. Míngnián qiūtiān tā jiù bú zài le — wǒ yě bù zhīdào wǒ hái zài bú zài.
turns.text_en: Space invited relationships — you see more clearly than many architects. But let me say one last, most personal thing. I've sat in this chair, under this persimmon tree, for — let me think — about seventy years. Every autumn for seventy years, persimmons fall from the tree and hit the ground — 'pa.' As a child I waited for that sound with excitement — because persimmons could be eaten.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the particular 'pa' that belongs only to this place and this person.

options.text_zh: 那个「啪」——从期待到习惯到安慰。声音没变，变的是您和它的关系。小时候是甜的：食物和快乐。中年是稳的：世界还在运转。现在是痛的：即将失去。同一个「啪」装了三种情感。也许「家」不是地址不是墙——是一组只属于你的声音和七十年积累的意义。树不在了，您的耳朵也许还是会「听到」。
options.text_pinyin: Nàge tèdìng de 'pā' — cóng qīdài dào xíguàn dào ānwèi. Shēngyīn méi biàn, biàn de shì nín hé tā zhī jiān de guānxi. Xiǎo shíhou tā shì tián de: dàibiǎo shíwù hé kuàilè. Zhōngnián tā shì wěn de: dàibiǎo shìjiè hái zài zhèngcháng yùnzhuǎn. Xiànzài tā shì tòng de: dàibiǎo jíjiāng shīqù. Tóng yī ge 'pā' zhuāng le sān zhǒng wánquán bùtóng de qínggǎn. Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì 'jiā' — bú shì dìzhǐ, bú shì qiáng. Jiā shì yī zǔ zhǐ shǔyú nǐ de shēngyīn hé tāmen qīshí nián jīlěi de yìyì. Jíshǐ shù bú zài le, měi nián qiūtiān nín de ěrduo yěxǔ hái shì huì 'tīng dào'. Yīnwèi nàge shēngyīn yǐjīng bù zhǐ cúnzài yú kōngqì zhōng — tā cúnzài yú nín de qīshí nián lǐ.
options.text_en: That particular 'pa' — from anticipation to habit to comfort. The sound didn't change; your relationship to it did. As a child it was sweet: food and joy. In middle age it was steady: the world still working. Now it's painful: impending loss. One 'pa' holding three entirely different emotions. Maybe that's what 'home' is — not an address, not a wall. Home is a set of sounds that belong only to you and their seventy years of accumulated meaning. Even when the tree is gone, every autumn your ears may still 'hear' it. Because that sound no longer exists only in air — it exists in your seventy years.
options.feedback: Home as accumulated meaning in sound — the 'pa' lives in seventy years, not in air.

options.text_zh: 您搬到新家以后能种一棵新的柿子树吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín bān dào xīn jiā yǐhòu néng zhòng yī kē xīn de shìzi shù ma?
options.text_en: Can you plant a new persimmon tree at your new home?
options.feedback: Suggests replacement when the point is irreplaceability.

options.text_zh: 您可以录下来这个声音。
options.text_pinyin: Nín kěyǐ lù xiàlái zhège shēngyīn.
options.text_en: You could record the sound.
options.feedback: Offers a technological preservation for an existential loss.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 存在于我的七十年里——是的。也许你说得对。也许那个「啪」已经不需要树了——它已经住在我的耳朵里了。但你知道吗……说了这么多关于失去和消失的话，我突然意识到一件事：今天你来了。你坐在我的院子里。你听到了柿子树下面的风声——就是现在这个风声。你看到了下午的阳光打在东墙上的样子——就是现在这个样子。这些空间记忆——我以为会永远消失的——你带走了一份。不完整的一份，但是一份。一个人来到一个地方，认真地看了、听了、感受了——这个地方就在那个人心里活了下来。也许这就是为什么我今天愿意跟你说这些话。不是为了保存——是为了让这个院子再多活在一个人的心里。
turns.text_pinyin: Cúnzài yú wǒ de qīshí nián lǐ — shì de. Yěxǔ nǐ shuō de duì. Yěxǔ nàge 'pā' yǐjīng bù xūyào shù le — tā yǐjīng zhù zài wǒ de ěrduo lǐ le. Dàn nǐ zhīdào ma…… shuō le zhème duō guānyú shīqù hé xiāoshī de huà, wǒ tūrán yìshí dào yī jiàn shì: jīntiān nǐ lái le. Nǐ zuò zài wǒ de yuànzi lǐ. Nǐ tīng dào le shìzi shù xiàmiàn de fēng shēng — jiù shì xiànzài zhège fēng shēng. Nǐ kàn dào le xiàwǔ de yángguāng dǎ zài dōng qiáng shàng de yàngzi — jiù shì xiànzài zhège yàngzi. Zhèxiē kōngjiān jìyì — wǒ yǐwéi huì yǒngyuǎn xiāoshī de — nǐ dài zǒu le yī fèn. Bù wánzhěng de yī fèn, dànshì yī fèn. Yī ge rén lái dào yī ge dìfang, rènzhēn de kàn le, tīng le, gǎnshòu le — zhège dìfang jiù zài nàge rén xīnlǐ huó le xiàlái. Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì wèishéme wǒ jīntiān yuànyì gēn nǐ shuō zhèxiē huà. Bú shì wèi le bǎocún — shì wèi le ràng zhège yuànzi zài duō huó zài yī ge rén de xīnlǐ.
turns.text_en: Existing in my seventy years — yes. Maybe you're right. Maybe that 'pa' no longer needs the tree — it already lives in my ears. But … having said so much about loss and vanishing, I suddenly realize something: today you came. You sat in my courtyard. You heard the wind beneath the persimmon tree — this wind right now.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the courtyard living on in one more heart.

options.text_zh: 让这个院子再多活在一个人的心里——不是拍照——而是坐在这里认真地呼吸，让风声、光线、柿子树的影子进入我的感官。我带走的拷贝不完整。但也许这就是记忆——永远不完整，但是活的。照片是完整的拷贝，但它是死的。我心里的这个院子会和我一起继续呼吸。谢谢您，郭先生。
options.text_pinyin: Ràng zhège yuànzi zài duō huó zài yī ge rén de xīnlǐ — bú shì pāi zhào, bú shì lùyīn — ér shì zuò zài zhèlǐ, rènzhēn de hūxī, ràng fēng shēng, guāngxiàn, shìzi shù de yǐngzi jìnrù wǒ de gǎnguān. Wǒ dài zǒu le yī fèn bù wánzhěng de kǎobèi. Dàn yěxǔ zhè jiù shì jìyì de běnzhì — yǒngyuǎn bù wánzhěng, dàn tā shì huó de. Zhàopiàn shì wánzhěng de kǎobèi, dàn tā shì sǐ de. Wǒ xīnlǐ de zhège yuànzi suīrán móhu — dàn tā shì huó de, huì hé wǒ yīqǐ jìxù hūxī. Jīntiān wǒ tīng dào le fēng, kàn dào le guāng. Xièxie nín, Guō xiānshēng.
options.text_en: Letting this courtyard live in one more heart — not photos, not recordings — but sitting here, breathing deliberately, letting the wind, the light, the persimmon tree's shadow enter my senses. I'm taking away an incomplete copy. But maybe that's the nature of memory — always incomplete, but alive. A photo is a complete copy, but dead. This courtyard in my heart is blurry — but alive, and will keep breathing with me. Today I heard the wind, saw the light. Thank you, Mr. Guo.
options.feedback: Incomplete but alive versus complete but dead — embodied memory as the truest preservation.

options.text_zh: 有记者采访过您吗？
options.text_pinyin: Yǒu jìzhě cǎifǎng guò nín ma?
options.text_en: Have any journalists interviewed you?
options.feedback: Asks about media coverage during the emotional conclusion.

options.text_zh: 您搬到哪里去住？
options.text_pinyin: Nín bān dào nǎlǐ qù zhù?
options.text_en: Where will you move to?
options.feedback: A logistics question at the moment of farewell.

tree.cultural_note: Beijing's hutong (胡同) neighborhoods date back to the Yuan Dynasty (13th century). Since the 1990s, rapid urbanization has demolished over half of Beijing's traditional courtyard houses (四合院, sìhéyuàn). The courtyard house embodies Confucian family structure: rooms arranged around a central open space, generations living together, daily life organized by the architecture itself. The loss of hutongs represents not merely an architectural change but the disappearance of an entire relational ecology — what urban theorists call 'spatial affordance.' The persimmon tree (柿子树) is common in northern Chinese courtyards, symbolizing good fortune (柿 shì sounds like 事 shì, 'matters/affairs').

tree.setup: You sit in a small tailor's shop in Quanzhou with Mrs. Lin, seventy-three years old, who has been mending clothes for fifty years. She is repairing a child's coat with a needle and thread. The thread is red. Outside, a light rain falls on the old maritime quarter.
tree.setup_zh: 你坐在泉州一家小裁缝铺里，和七十三岁的林婶在一起。她缝补衣服已经五十年了。她正在用针线修补一件小孩的外套。线是红色的。外面，细雨落在老海港区上。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这件衣服的主人是一个五岁的小男孩。他妈妈说他每天都要穿这件外套——别的不肯穿。穿了两年，袖口磨破了，肘部有个洞。他妈妈想给他买一件新的，但他哭了。他说「我要这件」。所以他妈妈带来给我补。你看——我用红线补。为什么用红线？因为对一个五岁的孩子来说，补丁不应该是一个需要藏起来的东西——补丁应该是好看的。如果补丁比原来的布还好看，那这个洞就不是一个坏消息了——它是一个让衣服变得更特别的机会。我这辈子补过上万件衣服。最重要的一课就是：每一个洞都是一次让某样东西变得比原来更好的机会。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè jiàn yīfu de zhǔrén shì yī ge wǔ suì de xiǎo nánhái. Tā māma shuō tā měi tiān dōu yào chuān zhè jiàn wàitào — bié de bù kěn chuān. Chuān le liǎng nián, xiùkǒu mó pò le, zhǒu bù yǒu ge dòng. Tā māma xiǎng gěi tā mǎi yī jiàn xīn de, dàn tā kū le. Tā shuō 'wǒ yào zhè jiàn'. Suǒyǐ tā māma dài lái gěi wǒ bǔ. Nǐ kàn — wǒ yòng hóng xiàn bǔ. Wèishéme yòng hóng xiàn? Yīnwèi duì yī ge wǔ suì de háizi lái shuō, bǔdīng bù yīnggāi shì yī ge xūyào cáng qǐlái de dōngxi — bǔdīng yīnggāi shì hǎokàn de. Rúguǒ bǔdīng bǐ yuánlái de bù hái hǎokàn, nà zhège dòng jiù bú shì yī ge huài xiāoxi le — tā shì yī ge ràng yīfu biàn de gèng tèbié de jīhuì. Wǒ zhè bèizi bǔ guò shàng wàn jiàn yīfu. Zuì zhòngyào de yī kè jiù shì: měi yī ge dòng dōu shì yī cì ràng mǒu yàng dōngxi biàn de bǐ yuánlái gèng hǎo de jīhuì.
turns.text_en: The owner of this coat is a five-year-old boy. His mother says he insists on wearing this coat every day — refuses any other. After two years the cuffs are frayed, there's a hole at the elbow. His mother wanted to buy a new one, but he cried. He said, 'I want this one.' So she brought it to me. Look — I'm using red thread. Why red?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to every hole as a chance to make something better.

options.text_zh: 每一个洞都是一次机会——红色的补丁不是修复损坏，是利用损坏创造新的美。那个男孩的衣服补好了不是变旧——是变得更独特。它有了故事：这里曾经有一个洞，现在有一朵红色的花。修补和替换的根本区别——替换说「换新的」；修补说「让伤痕变成新的美」。
options.text_pinyin: Měi yī ge dòng dōu shì yī cì jīhuì — nín bǎ dòng kàn zuò yī zhǒng kōngjiān, yī ge hái méiyǒu bèi tiánchōng de kěnéngxìng. Hóngsè de bǔdīng bú shì zài xiūfù sǔnhuài — shì zài lìyòng sǔnhuài chuàngzào xīn de měi. Nàge nánhái de yīfu bǔ hǎo le bú shì biàn jiù le — shì biàn de gèng dútè le. Tā yǒu le yī ge gùshì: zhèlǐ céngjīng yǒu yī ge dòng, xiànzài zhèlǐ yǒu yī duǒ hóngsè de huā. Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì xiūbǔ hé tìhuàn de gēnběn qūbié — tìhuàn shuō 'huàn yī ge xīn de'; xiūbǔ shuō 'ràng wǒmen bǎ shānghén biàn chéng yī zhǒng xīn de měi'.
options.text_en: Every hole is a chance — you see a hole as space, an unfilled possibility. The red patch isn't repairing damage — it's using damage to create new beauty. That boy's coat, once mended, didn't get older — it got more unique. It gained a story: here there was once a hole, now here there's a red flower. Maybe that's the fundamental difference between mending and replacing — replacing says 'get a new one'; mending says 'let's turn this scar into a new kind of beauty.'
options.feedback: Holes as unfilled possibility — mending turns scars into stories.

options.text_zh: 现在还有人找您补衣服吗？
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài hái yǒu rén zhǎo nín bǔ yīfu ma?
options.text_en: Do people still come to you for mending?
options.feedback: Asks about business during a meditation on the philosophy of repair.

options.text_zh: 这种缝法叫什么？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè zhǒng féng fǎ jiào shénme?
options.text_en: What's this sewing technique called?
options.feedback: A technical question during a philosophical exchange.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 修补和替换的区别——你说得很到位。但我要告诉你一件事：修补衣服的时候，最重要的不是技术——是理解这件衣服和它的主人之间的关系。那个男孩为什么不肯穿别的外套？因为这件外套不只是一件衣服——它是他的安全感。也许他爸爸给他穿上的第一天是某个重要的日子，也许这件外套陪他度过了第一天上幼儿园的恐惧。我不知道具体的原因——但我知道这件衣服对他来说不可替代。所以我补的时候，我不只是在缝布——我是在修复他和这件衣服之间的关系。如果我补得好，他穿上以后会觉得：它还在。我的东西还在。世界还是安全的。
turns.text_pinyin: Xiūbǔ hé tìhuàn de qūbié — nǐ shuō de hěn dào wèi. Dàn wǒ yào gàosu nǐ yī jiàn shì: xiūbǔ yīfu de shíhou, zuì zhòngyào de bú shì jìshù — shì lǐjiě zhè jiàn yīfu hé tā de zhǔrén zhī jiān de guānxi. Nàge nánhái wèishéme bù kěn chuān bié de wàitào? Yīnwèi zhè jiàn wàitào bù zhǐ shì yī jiàn yīfu — tā shì tā de ānquán gǎn. Yěxǔ tā bàba gěi tā chuān shàng de dì yī tiān shì mǒu ge zhòngyào de rìzi, yěxǔ zhè jiàn wàitào péi tā dùguò le dì yī tiān shàng yòu'éryuán de kǒngjù. Wǒ bù zhīdào jùtǐ de yuányīn — dàn wǒ zhīdào zhè jiàn yīfu duì tā lái shuō bù kě tìdài. Suǒyǐ wǒ bǔ de shíhou, wǒ bù zhǐ shì zài féng bù — wǒ shì zài xiūfù tā hé zhè jiàn yīfu zhī jiān de guānxi. Rúguǒ wǒ bǔ de hǎo, tā chuān shàng yǐhòu huì juéde: tā hái zài. Wǒ de dōngxi hái zài. Shìjiè hái shì ānquán de.
turns.text_en: The difference between mending and replacing — well said. But let me tell you: when mending clothes, n't technique — it's understanding the relationship between this garment and its owner. Why won't the boy wear another coat? Because this coat isn't clothing — it's his security.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to mending as repairing the relationship between person and object.

options.text_zh: 修复他和衣服之间的关系——「它还在，世界还是安全的」。大多数成年人忘了：物品不只是物品。对五岁的孩子来说，这件外套就是世界的一部分。在一个所有东西都可以被即时替换的时代，您是少数还在说「让我们修一修」的人。这是一种价值观：相信关系值得被修复。
options.text_pinyin: Xiūfù tā hé yīfu zhī jiān de guānxi — 'tā hái zài, shìjiè hái shì ānquán de'. Dàduōshù chéngnián rén wàng le: wùpǐn bù zhǐ shì wùpǐn. Duì wǔ suì de háizi lái shuō, zhè jiàn wàitào jiù shì shìjiè de yī bùfen. Yěxǔ háizi shì duì de — wǒmen hé wùpǐn zhī jiān de guānxi běnlái jiù yīnggāi shì shēn de, bù kě tìdài de. Zài yī ge suǒyǒu dōngxi dōu kěyǐ bèi jíshí tìhuàn de shídài, nín shì shǎoshù hái zài shuō 'ràng wǒmen xiū yī xiū' de rén. Zhè bù zhǐ shì jìnéng — shì yī zhǒng jiàzhíguān: xiāngxìn guānxi zhíde bèi xiūfù.
options.text_en: Repairing the relationship — 'it's still here, the world is still safe.' Most adults have forgotten: objects aren't just objects. For a five-year-old, this coat is part of the world itself. Maybe children are right — our relationship with objects should be deep and irreplaceable. In an age where everything can be instantly replaced, you're among the few still saying 'let's mend it.' That's not just a skill — it's a value system: believing relationships are worth repairing.
options.feedback: Children's wisdom about object attachment — mending as a value system in a disposable age.

options.text_zh: 现在的衣服质量是不是比以前差了？
options.text_pinyin: Xiànzài de yīfu zhìliàng shì bú shì bǐ yǐqián chà le?
options.text_en: Is clothing quality worse now than before?
options.feedback: Asks about manufacturing quality during a meditation on human-object bonds.

options.text_zh: 快时尚对您的生意影响大吗？
options.text_pinyin: Kuài shíshàng duì nín de shēngyì yǐngxiǎng dà ma?
options.text_en: Has fast fashion affected your business?
options.feedback: A business question during a philosophical exchange.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 相信关系值得被修复——你说出了我五十年工作的核心。但让我再告诉你一件事——这是关于线的。你看这根红线——它从一个线团上来。这个线团可以缝很多件衣服。同一根线团上的线，连接了不同的人的不同的衣服。上个月我用同一个线团的线补了一件老奶奶的棉袄、一条小女孩的裙子、一个工人的手套。这三个人互相不认识——但他们身上有一根来自同一个线团的线。如果你把所有这些线都连起来看——我五十年用过的线——它们组成了一张巨大的看不见的网。这座城市里有上万件衣服上有我缝过的线。那些线在穿过街道、穿过人群，互相靠近又远离——但它们都从我的手里经过。我有时候觉得这座城市是被我的线缝在一起的。
turns.text_pinyin: Xiāngxìn guānxi zhíde bèi xiūfù — nǐ shuō chū le wǒ wǔshí nián gōngzuò de héxīn. Dàn ràng wǒ zài gàosu nǐ yī jiàn shì — zhè shì guānyú xiàn de. Nǐ kàn zhè gēn hóng xiàn — tā cóng yī ge xiàntuán shàng lái. Zhège xiàntuán kěyǐ féng hěn duō jiàn yīfu. Tóng yī gēn xiàntuán shàng de xiàn, liánjiē le bùtóng de rén de bùtóng de yīfu. Shàng ge yuè wǒ yòng tóng yī ge xiàntuán de xiàn bǔ le yī jiàn lǎo nǎinai de miánǎo, yī tiáo xiǎo nǚhái de qúnzi, yī ge gōngrén de shǒutào. Zhè sān ge rén hùxiāng bù rènshi — dàn tāmen shēn shàng yǒu yī gēn lái zì tóng yī ge xiàntuán de xiàn. Rúguǒ nǐ bǎ suǒyǒu zhèxiē xiàn dōu lián qǐlái kàn — wǒ wǔshí nián yòng guò de xiàn — tāmen zǔchéng le yī zhāng jùdà de kàn bú jiàn de wǎng. Zhè zuò chéngshì lǐ yǒu shàng wàn jiàn yīfu shàng yǒu wǒ féng guò de xiàn. Nàxiē xiàn zài chuān guò jiēdào, chuān guò rénqún, hùxiāng kàojìn yòu yuǎnlí — dàn tāmen dōu cóng wǒ de shǒu lǐ jīngguò. Wǒ yǒu shíhou juéde zhè zuò chéngshì shì bèi wǒ de xiàn féng zài yīqǐ de.
turns.text_en: Believing relationships are worth repairing — you've spoken the core of my fifty years. But let me tell you one more thing — about thread. Look at this red thread — it comes from a spool. This spool can mend many garments. Thread from the same spool connects different people's different clothes.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the invisible web of thread connecting the city.

options.text_zh: 一座城市被您的线缝在一起——那个老奶奶和小女孩身上有同一个线团的线，被看不见的线连着，虽然永远不会知道。五十年来成千上万的衣服从您手里经过，每件都带走了一段线和您手的温度。也许「社区」最原始的含义不是住在同一个地方，而是被同一双手照顾过。
options.text_pinyin: Yī zuò chéngshì bèi nín de xiàn féng zài yīqǐ — nàge lǎo nǎinai hé nàge xiǎo nǚhái zǒu zài tóng yī tiáo jiē shàng shí, tāmen shēn shàng yǒu lái zì tóng yī ge xiàntuán de xiàn, bèi yī gēn kàn bú jiàn de xiàn liánjiē zhe, suīrán yǒngyuǎn bú huì zhīdào. Nín de shǒu shì zhè zhāng wǎng de zhōngxīn. Wǔshí nián lái, chéng qiān shàng wàn de yīfu cóng nín shǒu lǐ jīngguò, měi yī jiàn dōu dài zǒu le yī duàn xiàn hé nín shǒu de wēndù. Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì 'shèqū' zuì yuánshǐ de hányì — bú shì zhù zài tóng yī ge dìfang, ér shì bèi tóng yī shuāng shǒu zhàogù guò.
options.text_en: A city stitched together by your thread — when that grandmother and that little girl walk on the same street, they carry thread from the same spool, connected by an invisible line they'll never know about. Your hands are the center of this web. Over fifty years, thousands of garments passed through your hands, each carrying away a length of thread and your warmth. Maybe that's the most original meaning of 'community' — not living in the same place, but being cared for by the same hands.
options.feedback: Community as being cared for by the same hands — the tailor as weaver of invisible bonds.

options.text_zh: 您一天能补几件衣服？
options.text_pinyin: Nín yī tiān néng bǔ jǐ jiàn yīfu?
options.text_en: How many garments can you mend in a day?
options.feedback: A productivity question during a meditation on invisible connection.

options.text_zh: 您有社交媒体账号吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín yǒu shèjiāo méitǐ zhànghào ma?
options.text_en: Do you have social media accounts?
options.feedback: Asks about digital presence during a meditation on physical connection.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 被同一双手照顾过——这就是社区。你说到了我心坎里。最后我想给你看一样东西。这是我自己的一件衣服——你看，上面有很多补丁。不同颜色的补丁。每一个补丁都是在不同的年份补的。最早的这个——蓝色的——是我妈妈补的，那时候我还是小女孩。这个绿色的是我丈夫补的——他针脚很粗，但很结实。这个白色的是我女儿学缝纫的时候补的——歪歪扭扭的，但是是她的第一次。这件衣服上有三代人的针脚。我穿着它的时候，我感觉三代人都在抱着我。每一个补丁都是一双手说的「我在乎你」。这件衣服值多少钱？一分钱不值。但你能说它没有价值吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Bèi tóng yī shuāng shǒu zhàogù guò — zhè jiù shì shèqū. Nǐ shuō dào le wǒ xīnkǎn lǐ. Zuìhòu wǒ xiǎng gěi nǐ kàn yī yàng dōngxi. Zhè shì wǒ zìjǐ de yī jiàn yīfu — nǐ kàn, shàngmiàn yǒu hěn duō bǔdīng. Bùtóng yánsè de bǔdīng. Měi yī ge bǔdīng dōu shì zài bùtóng de niánfèn bǔ de. Zuì zǎo de zhège — lán sè de — shì wǒ māma bǔ de, nà shíhou wǒ hái shì xiǎo nǚhái. Zhège lǜsè de shì wǒ zhàngfu bǔ de — tā zhēnjiǎo hěn cū, dàn hěn jiēshi. Zhège báisè de shì wǒ nǚ'ér xué féngrèn de shíhou bǔ de — wāiwāiniǔniǔ de, dànshì shì tā de dì yī cì. Zhè jiàn yīfu shàng yǒu sān dài rén de zhēnjiǎo. Wǒ chuān zhe tā de shíhou, wǒ gǎnjué sān dài rén dōu zài bào zhe wǒ. Měi yī ge bǔdīng dōu shì yī shuāng shǒu shuō de 'wǒ zàihu nǐ'. Zhè jiàn yīfu zhí duōshǎo qián? Yī fēn qián bù zhí. Dàn nǐ néng shuō tā méiyǒu jiàzhí ma?
turns.text_en: Cared for by the same hands — that's community. You've spoken to my heart. One last thing to show you. This is my own garment — look, it has many patches. Different colors. Each patched in a different year. The earliest — blue — was my mother's, when I was still a little girl. This green one was my husband's — his stitches were rough but strong.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the garment with three generations of patches — worthless but invaluable.

options.text_zh: 一分钱不值，但无价——蓝色是母亲的保护，绿色是丈夫笨拙的温柔，白色是女儿的第一次尝试。这件衣服不是衣服——是用针脚写的家书。每个补丁是一双手穿越时间留下的拥抱。他们的手不在了，但力度和温度都在针脚里。衣服最原始的功能不是遮体——是被爱包裹。谢谢您，林婶。
options.text_pinyin: Yī fēn qián bù zhí, dàn wújià — lán sè de shì mǔqīn de bǎohù, lǜsè de shì zhàngfu bènzhuō de wēnróu, báisè de shì nǚ'ér de dì yī cì chángshì. Zhè jiàn yīfu bú shì yīfu — shì yī fēng yòng zhēnjiǎo xiě chéng de jiā shū. Měi yī ge bǔdīng dōu shì yī shuāng shǒu chuānyuè shíjiān liú xià de yōngbào. Tāmen de shǒu bú zài le, dàn shǒu de lìdù, jiǎodù, wēndù dōu bǎocún zài zhēnjiǎo lǐ. Yěxǔ zhè cái shì yīfu zuì yuánshǐ de gōngnéng — bú shì zhē tǐ — shì bèi ài bāoguǒ. Xièxie nín, Lín shěn.
options.text_en: Worthless but priceless — blue is a mother's protection, green is a husband's clumsy tenderness, white is a daughter's first attempt. This garment isn't clothing — it's a family letter written in stitches. Each patch is a hug left by hands reaching across time. Their hands are gone, but the pressure, angle, warmth are preserved in every stitch. Maybe clothing's most original purpose isn't covering the body — it's being wrapped in love. Thank you, Auntie Lin.
options.feedback: Clothing as love made wearable — three generations' touch preserved in stitches.

options.text_zh: 您会把这件衣服传给您女儿吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín huì bǎ zhè jiàn yīfu chuán gěi nín nǚ'ér ma?
options.text_en: Will you pass this garment to your daughter?
options.feedback: Asks about inheritance during the emotional climax.

options.text_zh: 您能教我缝纫吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín néng jiāo wǒ féngrèn ma?
options.text_en: Can you teach me to sew?
options.feedback: A skill-learning request at the philosophical conclusion.

tree.cultural_note: Quanzhou (泉州) was historically China's greatest maritime port and a starting point of the Maritime Silk Road. The Chinese tradition of mending and reusing clothing (缝缝补补, féngfeng bǔbu) reflects both economic necessity and a philosophical value system that prizes maintenance over replacement. The red thread (红线) carries deep cultural significance in Chinese tradition — the Red Thread of Fate (红线, hóngxiàn) is said to connect those destined to be linked. The tailor's use of red thread transforms repair into connection. The concept of 惜物 (xīwù, cherishing objects) is central to traditional Chinese domestic philosophy — treating objects as partners in life rather than disposable commodities.

tree.setup: You visit a classical garden in Suzhou with its elderly gardener, Mr. Qian, who has tended it for forty years. He is pruning a pine tree that was shaped by his predecessor. The late afternoon sun creates long shadows through the lattice windows. A single cup of tea sits on a stone table.
tree.setup_zh: 你在苏州和一位老园丁钱师傅参观一座古典园林。他在这里养护了四十年。他正在修剪一棵由他的前任塑形的松树。午后的阳光透过漏窗投下长长的影子。一杯茶放在石桌上。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这棵松树是我师父种的——不对，不是种的。是他从另一棵松树上取了一根枝条，花了十二年把它培养成现在这个形状。他退休的时候把它交给了我，说了一句话：「我花了十二年让它长成这样。你的工作是花接下来的四十年让它保持这样。」你听清楚了吗？不是让它变得更好——是让它保持。十二年的创造，四十年的保持。保持比创造难得多。因为创造有兴奋感——你在做一样新的东西。但保持没有兴奋感。保持是每天做同样的事情——浇水、修剪、检查虫害——日复一日，年复一年，四十年。保持需要一种特殊的耐心——不是等待某个结果的耐心——是接受永远不会有结果的耐心。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè kē sōngshù shì wǒ shīfu zhòng de — bú duì, bú shì zhòng de. Shì tā cóng lìng yī kē sōngshù shàng qǔ le yī gēn zhītiáo, huā le shí'èr nián bǎ tā péiyǎng chéng xiànzài zhège xíngzhuàng. Tā tuìxiū de shíhou bǎ tā jiāo gěi le wǒ, shuō le yī jù huà: 'wǒ huā le shí'èr nián ràng tā zhǎng chéng zhèyàng. Nǐ de gōngzuò shì huā jiēxiàlái de sìshí nián ràng tā bǎochí zhèyàng.' Nǐ tīng qīngchǔ le ma? Bú shì ràng tā biàn de gèng hǎo — shì ràng tā bǎochí. Shí'èr nián de chuàngzào, sìshí nián de bǎochí. Bǎochí bǐ chuàngzào nán de duō. Yīnwèi chuàngzào yǒu xīngfèn gǎn — nǐ zài zuò yī yàng xīn de dōngxi. Dàn bǎochí méiyǒu xīngfèn gǎn. Bǎochí shì měi tiān zuò tóngyàng de shìqíng — jiāo shuǐ, xiūjiǎn, jiǎnchá chónghài — rì fù yī rì, nián fù yī nián, sìshí nián. Bǎochí xūyào yī zhǒng tèshū de nàixīn — bú shì děngdài mǒu ge jiéguǒ de nàixīn — shì jiēshòu yǒngyuǎn bú huì yǒu jiéguǒ de nàixīn.
turns.text_en: This pine was planted by my master — no, not planted. He took a cutting from another pine and spent twelve years training it into this shape. When he retired, he handed it to me and said one thing: 'I spent twelve years making it grow like this. Your job is to spend the next forty years keeping it this way.' Did you hear that?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to maintenance as harder than creation — patience without a result.

options.text_zh: 接受永远不会有结果的耐心——我们崇拜创造者，但很少崇拜维护者。创造是一瞬间的光芒；维护是四十年的恒温。没有人为「松树今天和昨天一样」鼓掌——但这种「一样」需要四十年如一日的看护。您做了四十年没有掌声的工作——不是缺乏野心——是对已经存在的美的持续忠诚。
options.text_pinyin: Jiēshòu yǒngyuǎn bú huì yǒu jiéguǒ de nàixīn — wǒmen chóngbài chuàngzàozhě, dàn hěn shǎo chóngbài wéihùzhě. Chuàngzào shì yī shùnjiān de guāngmáng; wéihù shì sìshí nián de héngwēn. Méiyǒu rén huì wèi 'zhè kē sōngshù jīntiān hé zuótiān yīyàng' gǔzhǎng — dàn zhèng shì zhè zhǒng 'yīyàng' xūyào sìshí nián rú yī rì de kānhù. Chuàngzào dédào zànměi, wéihù bú bèi kàn jiàn. Nín zuò le sìshí nián méiyǒu zhǎngshēng de gōngzuò — zhè bú shì quēfá yěxīn — shì yī zhǒng gèng gāojí de chéngnuò: duì yǐjīng cúnzài de měi de chíxù zhōngchéng.
options.text_en: Patience accepting there will never be a result — we worship creators but rarely maintainers. Creation is a flash of brilliance; maintenance is forty years of steady warmth. No one applauds 'this pine looks the same as yesterday' — but that 'sameness' requires forty years of daily care. Creation gets praise; maintenance goes unseen. Your forty years of work without applause isn't lack of ambition — it's a higher commitment: sustained loyalty to beauty that already exists.
options.feedback: Maintenance as sustained loyalty — the invisible heroism of keeping what already exists.

options.text_zh: 这个园林每天有多少游客？
options.text_pinyin: Zhège yuánlín měi tiān yǒu duōshǎo yóukè?
options.text_en: How many visitors does this garden get daily?
options.feedback: Asks about tourism during a meditation on invisible devotion.

options.text_zh: 您退休以后谁来接手？
options.text_pinyin: Nín tuìxiū yǐhòu shéi lái jiēshǒu?
options.text_en: Who will take over when you retire?
options.feedback: A succession question during a meditation on patience.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对已经存在的美的持续忠诚——你用了一个很好的词：忠诚。但让我告诉你忠诚的代价。四十年来，我每天早上五点到花园。不是因为我想——是因为植物不等人。如果我今天不浇水，它不会明天补回来。如果我今天不修剪，一根新枝可能会改变十二年的造型。四十年没有一天假——不是因为规定——是因为花园不放假。你知道最难的时候是什么时候？不是下雨天、不是冬天——是春天。因为春天所有东西都在拼命生长，而我的工作是控制它们。春天来的时候，每一根枝条都在说「让我长」，而我必须说「不，你只能长到这里」。我不是在反对生长——我是在引导生长。但引导生长需要你每天都在场，每天都做决定——这根枝留、那根枝剪——四十年的每一天都在做这种微小的决定。
turns.text_pinyin: Duì yǐjīng cúnzài de měi de chíxù zhōngchéng — nǐ yòng le yī ge hěn hǎo de cí: zhōngchéng. Dàn ràng wǒ gàosu nǐ zhōngchéng de dàijià. Sìshí nián lái, wǒ měi tiān zǎoshang wǔ diǎn dào huāyuán. Bú shì yīnwèi wǒ xiǎng — shì yīnwèi zhíwù bù děng rén. Rúguǒ wǒ jīntiān bù jiāo shuǐ, tā bú huì míngtiān bǔ huílái. Rúguǒ wǒ jīntiān bù xiūjiǎn, yī gēn xīn zhī kěnéng huì gǎibiàn shí'èr nián de zàoxíng. Sìshí nián méiyǒu yī tiān jià — bú shì yīnwèi guīdìng — shì yīnwèi huāyuán bú fàngjià. Nǐ zhīdào zuì nán de shíhou shì shénme shíhou? Bú shì xià yǔ tiān, bú shì dōngtiān — shì chūntiān. Yīnwèi chūntiān suǒyǒu dōngxi dōu zài pīnmìng shēngzhǎng, ér wǒ de gōngzuò shì kòngzhì tāmen. Chūntiān lái de shíhou, měi yī gēn zhītiáo dōu zài shuō 'ràng wǒ zhǎng', ér wǒ bìxū shuō 'bù, nǐ zhǐ néng zhǎng dào zhèlǐ'. Wǒ bú shì zài fǎnduì shēngzhǎng — wǒ shì zài yǐndǎo shēngzhǎng. Dàn yǐndǎo shēngzhǎng xūyào nǐ měi tiān dōu zàichǎng, měi tiān dōu zuò juédìng — zhè gēn zhī liú, nà gēn zhī jiǎn — sìshí nián de měi yī tiān dōu zài zuò zhè zhǒng wēixiǎo de juédìng.
turns.text_en: Sustained loyalty to existing beauty — you used a good word: loyalty. But let me tell you the cost of loyalty. For forty years, I arrive at the garden at 5 AM every morning. Not because I want to — because plants don't wait. If I don't water today, it won't make up for it tomorrow. If I don't prune today, one new branch might alter twelve years of shaping.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to forty years of tiny daily decisions that guide growth.

options.text_zh: 四十年的微小决定——留还是剪，长还是停。美不是一次性创造的——是无数不被注意的决定累积而成的。就像一段好的关系——不是因为一次盛大的告白——是因为每天一句问候、一杯茶、一个选择继续在场的决定。您对这棵松树的四十年，就是一种爱。安静的、日复一日的爱。
options.text_pinyin: Sìshí nián de wēixiǎo juédìng — liú háishì jiǎn, zhǎng háishì tíng. Měi bú shì yīcìxìng chuàngzào de — shì yóu wúshù bú bèi zhùyì de juédìng lěijī ér chéng de. Zhè kē sōngshù zhī suǒyǐ měi, shì yīnwèi sìshí nián lái měi yī tiān yǒu yī ge rén zuò le yī ge xiǎoxiǎo de zhèngquè de juédìng. Jiù xiàng yī duàn hǎo de guānxi — bú shì yīnwèi yī cì shèngdà de gàobái — shì yīnwèi měi tiān de yī jù wènhòu, yī bēi chá, yī ge xuǎnzé jìxù zàichǎng de juédìng. Nín duì zhè kē sōngshù de sìshí nián, jiù shì yī zhǒng ài. Ānjìng de, bù qiú huíbào de, rì fù yī rì de ài.
options.text_en: Forty years of tiny decisions — keep or cut, grow or stop. Beauty isn't created all at once — it accumulates from countless unnoticed decisions. This pine is beautiful because for forty years, every day, one person made one small correct decision. Like a good relationship — not because of one grand confession — but because of each day's greeting, a cup of tea, a choice to keep showing up. Your forty years with this pine tree is a kind of love. Quiet, unrequited, day after day.
options.feedback: Beauty as accumulated daily decisions — forty years of showing up as the purest form of love.

options.text_zh: 松树盆景是怎么训练的？
options.text_pinyin: Sōngshù pénjǐng shì zěnme xùnliàn de?
options.text_en: How are pine bonsai trained?
options.feedback: A horticultural technique question during a meditation on devotion.

options.text_zh: 苏州有几个古典园林？
options.text_pinyin: Sūzhōu yǒu jǐ ge gǔdiǎn yuánlín?
options.text_en: How many classical gardens are there in Suzhou?
options.feedback: A factual question during a philosophical exchange.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 安静的、日复一日的爱——你理解了。但我还想说一件事。这座花园最重要的设计原则不是任何一棵树或一块石头——是「看不见的时间」。你看这面墙上的青苔——你以为那是自然长的？不是。是我师父的师父故意在墙上涂了一种特殊的液体，让青苔在那里生长——但你看不出来是人为的。这块石头旁边的这棵兰花——看起来像是偶然长在那里的？不是。是二十年前有意种在那里的——但种的方式让它看起来像野生的。这座花园最高级的技术就是：让人看不出有技术。让一切看起来像是自然发生的——但每一个「自然」都是几十年精心维护的结果。最好的花园看起来像没有园丁。但没有园丁，花园一年就没了。
turns.text_pinyin: Ānjìng de, rì fù yī rì de ài — nǐ lǐjiě le. Dàn wǒ hái xiǎng shuō yī jiàn shì. Zhè zuò huāyuán zuì zhòngyào de shèjì yuánzé bú shì rènhé yī kē shù huò yī kuài shítou — shì 'kàn bú jiàn de shíjiān'. Nǐ kàn zhè miàn qiáng shàng de qīngtái — nǐ yǐwéi nà shì zìrán zhǎng de? Bú shì. Shì wǒ shīfu de shīfu gùyì zài qiáng shàng tú le yī zhǒng tèshū de yètǐ, ràng qīngtái zài nàlǐ shēngzhǎng — dàn nǐ kàn bù chūlái shì rénwéi de. Zhè kuài shítou pángbiān de zhè kē lánhuā — kàn qǐlái xiàng shì ǒurán zhǎng zài nàlǐ de? Bú shì. Shì èrshí nián qián yǒuyì zhòng zài nàlǐ de — dàn zhòng de fāngshì ràng tā kàn qǐlái xiàng yěshēng de. Zhè zuò huāyuán zuìgāojí de jìshù jiù shì: ràng rén kàn bù chū yǒu jìshù. Ràng yīqiè kàn qǐlái xiàng shì zìrán fāshēng de — dàn měi yī ge 'zìrán' dōu shì jǐ shí nián jīngxīn wéihù de jiéguǒ. Zuì hǎo de huāyuán kàn qǐlái xiàng méiyǒu yuándīng. Dàn méiyǒu yuándīng, huāyuán yī nián jiù méi le.
turns.text_en: Quiet, day-after-day love — you understand. But one more thing. This garden's most important design principle isn't any tree or stone — it's 'invisible time.' See the moss on this wall — you think it grew naturally? It didn't. My master's master deliberately applied a special liquid to the wall to encourage moss growth there — but you can't tell it's man-made.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the highest art being invisible — looking like no gardener exists.

options.text_zh: 最好的花园看起来像没有园丁——最高级的技术让自己消失。四十年劳动的目标是「看不见劳动」的效果。游客觉得「真美，好像天然的」——这其实是对您四十年最高的赞美，虽然没人知道在赞美您。您不署名、不留痕迹——但花园每一寸都是您存在过的证据。
options.text_pinyin: Zuì hǎo de huāyuán kàn qǐlái xiàng méiyǒu yuándīng — zuìgāojí de jìshù ràng zìjǐ xiāoshī. Zhè shì yī ge shēnkè de bèilùn: sìshí nián de láodòng mùbiāo shì zhìzào 'kàn bú jiàn láodòng' de xiàoguǒ. Yóukè juéde 'zhēn měi, hǎoxiàng tiānrán de' — zhè qíshí shì duì nín sìshí nián gōngzuò zuìgāo de zànměi, suīrán méi rén zhīdào tāmen zài zànměi nín. Nín bù shǔ míng, bù liú hénjì, bú yāoqiú bèi kàn jiàn — dàn huāyuán de měi yī cùn dōu shì nín cúnzài guò de zhèngjù.
options.text_en: The best garden looks like it has no gardener — the highest technique makes itself disappear. A profound paradox: forty years of labor aimed at producing the effect of 'invisible labor.' Visitors think 'how beautiful, it looks natural' — that's actually the highest praise for your forty years, though no one knows they're praising you. You don't sign your name, leave no trace, don't ask to be seen — but every inch of the garden is evidence you existed.
options.feedback: The paradox of invisible mastery — the gardener's highest praise is 'it looks natural.'

options.text_zh: 这种技术有书籍记录吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè zhǒng jìshù yǒu shūjí jìlù ma?
options.text_en: Are these techniques recorded in books?
options.feedback: Asks about documentation during a meditation on invisible mastery.

options.text_zh: 日本庭园也是这种理念吗？
options.text_pinyin: Rìběn tíngyuán yě shì zhè zhǒng lǐniàn ma?
options.text_en: Do Japanese gardens follow the same philosophy?
options.feedback: Compares to another tradition instead of engaging the insight.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 每一寸都是我存在过的证据——但这种证据只有另一个园丁才能读懂。普通游客看到的是美。但如果另一个园丁来，他会看到：「啊，这根枝条是这样引导的。这块青苔是刻意培养的。这棵兰花的位置不是偶然的。」他会看到我的手迹——不是在纸上——是在活的植物上。这就是我的语言。我不写字、不画画、不作曲——我用活的植物来表达。但活的植物和纸上的字有一个根本的区别：字写在纸上不会变——但植物每天都在变。所以我的「作品」永远是未完成的。每一天它都是新的——每一天它都需要我重新「写」一遍。四十年来，我每天早上都在重写同一幅画。从来没有「完成」的那一天。也许永远不会有。
turns.text_pinyin: Měi yī cùn dōu shì wǒ cúnzài guò de zhèngjù — dàn zhè zhǒng zhèngjù zhǐyǒu lìng yī ge yuándīng cái néng dú dǒng. Pǔtōng yóukè kàn dào de shì měi. Dàn rúguǒ lìng yī ge yuándīng lái, tā huì kàn dào: 'a, zhè gēn zhītiáo shì zhèyàng yǐndǎo de. Zhè kuài qīngtái shì kèyì péiyǎng de. Zhè kē lánhuā de wèizhì bú shì ǒurán de.' Tā huì kàn dào wǒ de shǒujì — bú shì zài zhǐ shàng — shì zài huó de zhíwù shàng. Zhè jiù shì wǒ de yǔyán. Wǒ bù xiě zì, bù huà huà, bú zuòqǔ — wǒ yòng huó de zhíwù lái biǎodá. Dàn huó de zhíwù hé zhǐ shàng de zì yǒu yī ge gēnběn de qūbié: zì xiě zài zhǐ shàng bú huì biàn — dàn zhíwù měi tiān dōu zài biàn. Suǒyǐ wǒ de 'zuòpǐn' yǒngyuǎn shì wèi wánchéng de. Měi yī tiān tā dōu shì xīn de — měi yī tiān tā dōu xūyào wǒ chóngxīn 'xiě' yī biàn. Sìshí nián lái, wǒ měi tiān zǎoshang dōu zài chóngxiě tóng yī fú huà. Cónglái méiyǒu 'wánchéng' de nà yī tiān. Yěxǔ yǒngyuǎn bú huì yǒu.
turns.text_en: Every inch evidence of my existence — but evidence only another gardener can read. Ordinary visitors see beauty. But if another gardener visits, they'll see: 'Ah, this branch was guided this way. This moss was deliberately cultivated. This orchid's placement wasn't accidental.' They'll see my handwriting — not on paper — on living plants. That's my language. I don't write, don't paint, don't compose — I express through living plants.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the work that is rewritten daily and never completed.

options.text_zh: 永远未完成——不是遗憾，是活的艺术和死的艺术的根本区别。画完成了就死了，不再需要你。但花园永远不会完成——所以永远需要你。也许这就是您每天五点到这里的原因——花园每天在说「我还需要你」。被需要，也许比被赞美更深。四十年每天五点你们互相需要。谢谢您，钱师傅。
options.text_pinyin: Yǒngyuǎn wèi wánchéng — zhè bú shì yíhàn — shì huó de yìshù hé sǐ de yìshù zhī jiān de gēnběn qūbié. Yī fú huà wánchéng le jiù sǐ le, bú zài xūyào nǐ. Dàn zhè zuò huāyuán yǒngyuǎn bú huì wánchéng — suǒyǐ yǒngyuǎn huó zhe, yǒngyuǎn xūyào nǐ. Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì nín měi tiān wǔ diǎn dào zhèlǐ de zhēnzhèng yuányīn — huāyuán měi tiān zǎoshang dōu zài shuō: 'wǒ hái xūyào nǐ.' Bèi xūyào — yěxǔ zhè shì bǐ bèi zànměi gèng shēn de mǎnzú. Sìshí nián lái měi tiān zǎoshang wǔ diǎn nǐmen hùxiāng xūyào. Zhè shì zuì ānjìng de àiqíng. Xièxie nín, Qián shīfu.
options.text_en: Forever unfinished — not a regret — the fundamental difference between living art and dead art. A painting once finished is dead, no longer needing you. But this garden will never be finished — so it's forever alive, forever needing you. Maybe that's the real reason you arrive at five every morning — the garden says each day: 'I still need you.' Being needed — maybe that's a deeper satisfaction than being praised. For forty years, every morning at five, you've needed each other. This is the quietest love story. Thank you, Master Qian.
options.feedback: Living art versus dead art — being needed as the quietest love story.

options.text_zh: 您每个月工资多少？
options.text_pinyin: Nín měi ge yuè gōngzī duōshǎo?
options.text_en: What's your monthly salary?
options.feedback: A compensation question at the philosophical climax.

options.text_zh: 这座园林被列为世界遗产了吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè zuò yuánlín bèi liè wéi shìjiè yíchǎn le ma?
options.text_en: Is this garden a World Heritage site?
options.feedback: A designation question at the emotional conclusion.

tree.cultural_note: Suzhou's classical gardens (苏州园林) are UNESCO World Heritage Sites representing the pinnacle of Chinese garden art. Unlike Western formal gardens, Chinese gardens aim for 虽由人作，宛自天开 (suī yóu rén zuò, wǎn zì tiān kāi) — 'though made by human hands, as if opened by heaven.' This principle of concealed artifice requires generations of careful maintenance. The role of the gardener (园丁, yuándīng) in Chinese classical gardens is one of perpetual stewardship — not creation but continuation. The philosophical foundation draws on Daoist concepts of 无为 (wúwéi, non-forcing) and 顺势 (shùnshì, following natural momentum), working with rather than against living systems.

tree.setup: The community center needs a new welcome sign. Old Zhou has volunteered to write it. Auntie Fang and Xiao Mei are watching.
tree.setup_zh: 社区中心要做一个新的欢迎牌。老周要写。方阿姨和小梅在看。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我来写！我的字很好！
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ lái xiě! Wǒ de zì hěn hǎo!
turns.text_en: I'll write it! My handwriting is great!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你们看！“欢迎大家”！好不好？
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐmen kàn! 'Huānyíng dàjiā'! Hǎo bù hǎo?
turns.text_en: Look! 'Welcome everyone'! How is it?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: “大”写反了。
turns.text_pinyin: 'Dà' xiě fǎn le.
turns.text_en: The character 'big' is backwards.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Zhou asks you what you think of his sign.

options.text_zh: 字很大，大家都能看到。
options.text_pinyin: Zì hěn dà, dàjiā dōu néng kàn dào.
options.text_en: The characters are big — everyone can see them.
options.feedback: Nice diplomacy — you found something true and positive to say without lying.

options.text_zh: 很好！没问题！
options.text_pinyin: Hěn hǎo! Méi wèntí!
options.text_en: Great! No problem!
options.feedback: Enthusiastic, but there IS a problem — the character is backwards.

options.text_zh: 你写错了。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ xiě cuò le.
options.text_en: You wrote it wrong.
options.feedback: True, but blunt. Xiao Mei already pointed it out — no need to pile on.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 大好！大家都能看到！
turns.text_pinyin: Dà hǎo! Dàjiā dōu néng kàn dào!
turns.text_en: Big is good! Everyone can see it!

tree.cultural_note: In Chinese social settings, directly pointing out mistakes can cause loss of face (丢面子). Finding something genuinely positive to say is a valued social skill.

tree.setup: It's a hot day. Teacher Luo, Uncle Bai, and you are sitting outside. Someone needs to go buy water. Nobody wants to go.
tree.setup_zh: 天很热。罗老师、老白和你坐在外面。要有人去买水。没有人想去。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我们要买水！现在！很热！
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒmen yào mǎi shuǐ! Xiànzài! Hěn rè!
turns.text_en: We need to buy water! Now! It's very hot!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 热...是吗？我觉得还好。
turns.text_pinyin: Rè... shì ma? Wǒ juéde hái hǎo.
turns.text_en: Hot... is it? I think it's fine.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 三十八度！三十八！
turns.text_pinyin: Sānshíbā dù! Sānshíbā!
turns.text_en: Thirty-eight degrees! Thirty-eight!

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Teacher Luo wants someone to go buy water. What do you say?

options.text_zh: 我去吧。你们要喝什么？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ qù ba. Nǐmen yào hē shénme?
options.text_en: I'll go. What do you want to drink?
options.feedback: You just volunteered — Teacher Luo is visibly relieved, Uncle Bai nods wisely as if this was his plan all along.

options.text_zh: 老白，你去吧。
options.text_pinyin: Lǎo Bái, nǐ qù ba.
options.text_en: Uncle Bai, you go.
options.feedback: Direct delegation. Uncle Bai will find a reason why he shouldn't — but it's a fair try.

options.text_zh: 我不热。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bú rè.
options.text_en: I'm not hot.
options.feedback: You just accidentally joined Uncle Bai's side. Teacher Luo's eye is twitching.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 很好。我在这里等。
turns.text_pinyin: Hěn hǎo. Wǒ zài zhèlǐ děng.
turns.text_en: Good. I'll wait here.

tree.cultural_note: Volunteering for small tasks is a way to build social goodwill (人情) in Chinese communities.

tree.setup: Old Zhou made baozi and is offering them to everyone. Auntie Fang has tasted one.
tree.setup_zh: 老周做了包子，给大家吃。方阿姨吃了一个。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我做的包子！好吃吗？好吃吧？
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ zuò de bāozi! Hǎo chī ma? Hǎo chī ba?
turns.text_en: My baozi! Delicious, right? Delicious, right?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 很特别。
turns.text_pinyin: Hěn tèbié.
turns.text_en: Very... unique.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对！我放了很多东西！
turns.text_pinyin: Duì! Wǒ fàng le hěn duō dōngxi!
turns.text_en: Yes! I put a lot of things in them!

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Zhou offers you a baozi. What do you say?

options.text_zh: 谢谢！我吃一个。
options.text_pinyin: Xièxie! Wǒ chī yí gè.
options.text_en: Thanks! I'll try one.
options.feedback: Brave and polite. Auntie Fang gives you a look that might be respect. Or sympathy.

options.text_zh: 我不饿。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bú è.
options.text_en: I'm not hungry.
options.feedback: A safe retreat. Zhou looks briefly disappointed, then offers one to someone else.

options.text_zh: 方阿姨觉得好吃吗？
options.text_pinyin: Fāng āyí juéde hǎo chī ma?
options.text_en: Does Auntie Fang think they're good?
options.feedback: Clever deflection, but you just put Auntie Fang in an awkward position. She already said 'unique' — that was her answer.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 他很高兴。
turns.text_pinyin: Tā hěn gāoxìng.
turns.text_en: He's very happy.

tree.cultural_note: 说'很特别' (very unique) is a classic Chinese way to say something is bad without saying it directly.

tree.setup: Manager Qin has put up a new sign: 'Please do not eat in the meeting room.' Teacher Luo is pleased. Old Zhou is confused.
tree.setup_zh: 秦经理贴了一个新的通知：请不要在会议室吃东西。罗老师很高兴。老周不明白。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 请看。新的规定。
turns.text_pinyin: Qǐng kàn. Xīn de guīdìng.
turns.text_en: Please look. New rule.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 很好！这很重要！
turns.text_pinyin: Hěn hǎo! Zhè hěn zhòngyào!
turns.text_en: Very good! This is important!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 可是...我带了包子。
turns.text_pinyin: Kěshì... wǒ dài le bāozi.
turns.text_en: But... I brought baozi.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Zhou looks at you with his baozi. What do you suggest?

options.text_zh: 我们去外面吃吧。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒmen qù wàimiàn chī ba.
options.text_en: Let's eat outside.
options.feedback: Perfect — you follow the rule AND save Zhou's baozi. Manager Qin nods approvingly.

options.text_zh: 你可以问秦经理。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ wèn Qín jīnglǐ.
options.text_en: You can ask Manager Qin.
options.feedback: Procedurally correct. Qin will say no, but at least it went through channels.

options.text_zh: 快吃！
options.text_pinyin: Kuài chī!
options.text_en: Eat it quick!
options.feedback: Teacher Luo is already looking at you. The sign is right there.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: ...规定就是规定。
turns.text_pinyin: ...Guīdìng jiù shì guīdìng.
turns.text_en: ...Rules are rules.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese workplaces often post written notices (通知) for rules. The formality of a posted rule carries more weight than a verbal request.

tree.setup: There are two chairs and three people: you, Auntie Fang, and Teacher Luo. Someone has to stand.
tree.setup_zh: 有两把椅子，三个人：你、方阿姨和罗老师。一个人要站着。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 只有两把椅子！两把！
turns.text_pinyin: Zhǐ yǒu liǎng bǎ yǐzi! Liǎng bǎ!
turns.text_en: Only two chairs! Two!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我可以站着。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ kěyǐ zhàn zhe.
turns.text_en: I can stand.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you do about the chair situation?

options.text_zh: 方阿姨，您坐。我站着。
options.text_pinyin: Fāng āyí, nín zuò. Wǒ zhàn zhe.
options.text_en: Auntie Fang, please sit. I'll stand.
options.feedback: Respectful to an elder. Auntie Fang sits without protest — she was testing you.

options.text_zh: 我去找一把椅子。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ qù zhǎo yì bǎ yǐzi.
options.text_en: I'll go find another chair.
options.feedback: Problem-solver approach. Good, but Teacher Luo wants this resolved NOW.

options.text_zh: 我先坐吧。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ xiān zuò ba.
options.text_en: I'll sit first.
options.feedback: With an elder standing? Auntie Fang says nothing, which says everything.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 嗯。
turns.text_pinyin: Ǹg.
turns.text_en: Mm.

tree.cultural_note: Offering your seat to elders (让座) is a deeply ingrained social expectation in Chinese culture.

tree.setup: Someone in the neighborhood lost a cat. Manager Qin is posting a notice. Old Zhou wants to help.
tree.setup_zh: 小区里有人丢了猫。秦经理在贴通知。老周想帮忙。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这是找猫的通知。请大家看看。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè shì zhǎo māo de tōngzhī. Qǐng dàjiā kàn kan.
turns.text_en: This is a lost cat notice. Please everyone take a look.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我帮你找！我很会找东西！
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ bāng nǐ zhǎo! Wǒ hěn huì zhǎo dōngxi!
turns.text_en: I'll help you find it! I'm great at finding things!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: ...你知道猫长什么样吗？
turns.text_pinyin: ...Nǐ zhīdào māo zhǎng shénme yàng ma?
turns.text_en: ...Do you know what the cat looks like?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_pinyin: ...Shì māo.
turns.text_en: ...It's a cat.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: How do you help the situation?

options.text_zh: 通知上有猫的照片吗？
options.text_pinyin: Tōngzhī shàng yǒu māo de zhàopiàn ma?
options.text_en: Is there a photo of the cat on the notice?
options.feedback: Practical question. Manager Qin points to the notice — there is indeed a photo. Zhou studies it intently.

options.text_zh: 老周，加油！
options.text_pinyin: Lǎo Zhōu, jiā yóu!
options.text_en: Old Zhou, you can do it!
options.feedback: Supportive but unhelpful. Zhou beams. The cat remains lost.

options.text_zh: 我没有看到猫。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ méi yǒu kàn dào māo.
options.text_en: I haven't seen the cat.
options.feedback: True but not particularly helpful. Manager Qin was hoping for a bit more engagement.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese residential communities (小区) often have a manager or committee that handles shared notices and community matters.

tree.setup: The office air conditioner is broken. Teacher Luo thinks this is a crisis. Xiao Mei disagrees — quietly.
tree.setup_zh: 办公室的空调坏了。罗老师觉得这是大问题。小梅不同意——但她什么也没说。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 空调坏了！已经坏了两个小时了！两个小时！
turns.text_pinyin: Kōngtiáo huài le! Yǐjīng huài le liǎng gè xiǎoshí le! Liǎng gè xiǎoshí!
turns.text_en: The AC is broken! It's been broken for two hours! Two hours!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 今天二十度。
turns.text_pinyin: Jīntiān èrshí dù.
turns.text_en: It's twenty degrees today.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这不是温度的问题！这是原则的问题！
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè bú shì wēndù de wèntí! Zhè shì yuánzé de wèntí!
turns.text_en: This isn't about the temperature! It's about the principle!

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: How do you respond to Teacher Luo?

options.text_zh: 你说得对，我去告诉秦经理。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ shuō de duì, wǒ qù gàosu Qín jīnglǐ.
options.text_en: You're right, I'll go tell Manager Qin.
options.feedback: You validated her concern AND took action through proper channels. Teacher Luo is satisfied. Xiao Mei looks at you with something approaching approval.

options.text_zh: 可是今天不热啊。
options.text_pinyin: Kěshì jīntiān bú rè a.
options.text_en: But it's not hot today.
options.feedback: You just said the same thing Xiao Mei said. Teacher Luo was already annoyed about that. Now she's annoyed at two people.

options.text_zh: 我们可以开窗户。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒmen kěyǐ kāi chuānghù.
options.text_en: We can open the windows.
options.feedback: Practical solution, but Teacher Luo didn't want a workaround. She wanted someone to care that the system is broken.

tree.cultural_note: 原则 (principles) matter in Chinese professional culture — sometimes the issue isn't the inconvenience itself but whether standards are being maintained.

tree.setup: Old Zhou has an idea for the community garden. He wants to grow watermelons. Auntie Fang has concerns.
tree.setup_zh: 老周有一个关于社区花园的想法。他想种西瓜。方阿姨有一些想法。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我想在花园里种西瓜！大家夏天都可以吃！
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng zài huāyuán lǐ zhòng xīguā! Dàjiā xiàtiān dōu kěyǐ chī!
turns.text_en: I want to grow watermelons in the garden! Everyone can eat them in summer!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 花园很小。
turns.text_pinyin: Huāyuán hěn xiǎo.
turns.text_en: The garden is small.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 小的西瓜也很好吃！
turns.text_pinyin: Xiǎo de xīguā yě hěn hǎo chī!
turns.text_en: Small watermelons are also delicious!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 花园只有两个平方。
turns.text_pinyin: Huāyuán zhǐ yǒu liǎng gè píngfāng.
turns.text_en: The garden is only two square meters.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: What do you think of Zhou's watermelon plan?

options.text_zh: 可以先种一个试试。
options.text_pinyin: Kěyǐ xiān zhòng yí gè shì shi.
options.text_en: We could try growing one first.
options.feedback: Diplomatic. You didn't kill Zhou's dream but set realistic expectations. Fang gives a small nod.

options.text_zh: 好主意！我们种吧！
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo zhǔyì! Wǒmen zhòng ba!
options.text_en: Great idea! Let's grow them!
options.feedback: Zhou is thrilled. Fang looks at you the way she looks at Zhou.

options.text_zh: 两个平方种不了西瓜。
options.text_pinyin: Liǎng gè píngfāng zhòng bù liǎo xīguā.
options.text_en: You can't grow watermelons in two square meters.
options.feedback: Factually correct. But Fang already said this more gently. Zhou's face falls.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好！试试！我明天就开始！
turns.text_pinyin: Hǎo! Shì shi! Wǒ míngtiān jiù kāishǐ!
turns.text_en: Good! Let's try! I'll start tomorrow!

tree.cultural_note: 先...试试 (try first) is a common Chinese diplomatic move — it neither commits to a plan nor rejects it.

tree.setup: Teacher Luo wants to file a complaint about noise. Manager Qin has a form for that. Uncle Bai has opinions.
tree.setup_zh: 罗老师想投诉噪音的问题。秦经理有一张表要填。老白有自己的看法。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 楼上太吵了！每天晚上！我要投诉！
turns.text_pinyin: Lóu shàng tài chǎo le! Měi tiān wǎnshang! Wǒ yào tóusù!
turns.text_en: Upstairs is too noisy! Every evening! I want to file a complaint!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好的。请先填这个表。
turns.text_pinyin: Hǎo de. Qǐng xiān tián zhè ge biǎo.
turns.text_en: Okay. Please fill out this form first.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我觉得吧...楼上的人也不容易。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde ba... lóu shàng de rén yě bù róngyì.
turns.text_en: I think... the people upstairs have it hard too.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你住在楼上！
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ zhù zài lóu shàng!
turns.text_en: You live upstairs!

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: The situation is getting tense. What do you do?

options.text_zh: 我们先看看表上写什么吧。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒmen xiān kàn kan biǎo shàng xiě shénme ba.
options.text_en: Let's first look at what the form says.
options.feedback: Redirecting to procedure — Manager Qin approves. The form becomes neutral ground.

options.text_zh: 老白，你晚上在做什么？
options.text_pinyin: Lǎo Bái, nǐ wǎnshang zài zuò shénme?
options.text_en: Uncle Bai, what are you doing in the evenings?
options.feedback: Direct investigation. Uncle Bai says '锻炼身体' (exercising). At 11pm. Teacher Luo's eye twitches.

options.text_zh: 楼上确实很吵。
options.text_pinyin: Lóu shàng quèshí hěn chǎo.
options.text_en: Upstairs is indeed very noisy.
options.feedback: You just took sides in front of Uncle Bai. He looks betrayed. Manager Qin sighs.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 表要两天才能处理。这是规定。
turns.text_pinyin: Biǎo yào liǎng tiān cái néng chǔlǐ. Zhè shì guīdìng.
turns.text_en: The form takes two days to process. That's the rule.

tree.cultural_note: Noise complaints between floors (楼上楼下) are one of the most common neighbor disputes in Chinese apartment buildings.

tree.setup: The shared kitchen is dirty. Xiao Mei, Old Zhou, and you are the only ones who used it today. Someone should clean it.
tree.setup_zh: 公用厨房很脏。小梅、老周和你是今天用过厨房的人。有人应该打扫。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 厨房有点脏。但是！我做了很好吃的菜！
turns.text_pinyin: Chúfáng yǒu diǎn zāng. Dànshì! Wǒ zuò le hěn hǎo chī de cài!
turns.text_en: The kitchen is a bit dirty. But! I made really delicious food!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我用了一个杯子。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ yòng le yí gè bēizi.
turns.text_en: I used one cup.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: The kitchen needs cleaning. What do you suggest?

options.text_zh: 我们三个人一起打扫吧。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒmen sān gè rén yìqǐ dǎsǎo ba.
options.text_en: Let's all three clean together.
options.feedback: Fair solution. Zhou agrees enthusiastically (he'll do 20% of the work while talking the whole time). Mei nods once.

options.text_zh: 老周，你做了饭，你打扫。
options.text_pinyin: Lǎo Zhōu, nǐ zuò le fàn, nǐ dǎsǎo.
options.text_en: Zhou, you cooked, you clean.
options.feedback: Logical but blunt. Zhou's smile freezes. Mei's cup suddenly becomes very interesting to examine.

options.text_zh: 明天再说吧。
options.text_pinyin: Míngtiān zài shuō ba.
options.text_en: Let's talk about it tomorrow.
options.feedback: The kitchen will not clean itself. And by tomorrow there will be a notice from Manager Qin.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我洗我的杯子。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ xǐ wǒ de bēizi.
turns.text_en: I'll wash my cup.

tree.cultural_note: Shared kitchen etiquette is a common tension point in Chinese dorms and shared apartments. 一起 (together) is often the safest diplomatic approach.

tree.setup: It's Auntie Fang's birthday. Old Zhou has planned a surprise. Xiao Mei was supposed to keep it secret.
tree.setup_zh: 今天是方阿姨的生日。老周准备了一个惊喜。小梅应该保密。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 都准备好了吗？方阿姨不知道吧？
turns.text_pinyin: Dōu zhǔnbèi hǎo le ma? Fāng āyí bù zhīdào ba?
turns.text_en: Is everything ready? Auntie Fang doesn't know, right?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 她问我了。我说了‘没什么’。
turns.text_pinyin: Tā wèn wǒ le. Wǒ shuō le 'méi shénme'.
turns.text_en: She asked me. I said 'nothing.'

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 很好！她一定不知道！
turns.text_pinyin: Hěn hǎo! Tā yīdìng bù zhīdào!
turns.text_en: Great! She definitely doesn't know!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: ...她买了蛋糕。
turns.text_pinyin: ...Tā mǎi le dàngāo.
turns.text_en: ...She bought a cake.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Fang clearly knows about the surprise. What do you say to Zhou?

options.text_zh: 没关系，我们还是可以一起吃蛋糕。
options.text_pinyin: Méi guānxi, wǒmen háishì kěyǐ yìqǐ chī dàngāo.
options.text_en: It's okay, we can still eat cake together.
options.feedback: You saved the party by reframing it. Zhou is only briefly disappointed before pivoting to cake.

options.text_zh: 我觉得方阿姨已经知道了。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde Fāng āyí yǐjīng zhīdào le.
options.text_en: I think Auntie Fang already knows.
options.feedback: Honest. Zhou's face falls, then brightens: '那她一定很高兴！' Zhou's optimism is unbreakable.

options.text_zh: 小梅！你说了！
options.text_pinyin: Xiǎo Méi! Nǐ shuō le!
options.text_en: Xiao Mei! You told her!
options.feedback: Mei blinks once. '我说了没什么。' Technically true. Mei did say 'nothing.' Fang figured it out herself.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese people often express surprise even when they already know (假装惊喜) — it's a social courtesy to honor the effort of the person who planned it.

tree.setup: An old tree in the courtyard blocks light to some apartments. Manager Qin has received complaints. Uncle Bai thinks the tree must stay.
tree.setup_zh: 院子里有一棵老树，挡住了一些住户的光线。秦经理收到了投诉。老白觉得树不能砍。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 楼上有三家说树挡了光线。按规定，可以申请把树砍掉。
turns.text_pinyin: Lóu shàng yǒu sān jiā shuō shù dǎng le guāngxiàn. Àn guīdìng, kěyǐ shēnqǐng bǎ shù kǎn diào.
turns.text_en: Three families upstairs say the tree blocks their light. According to regulations, they can apply to have it cut down.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这棵树比他们都早来。树比人先到，凭什么人说了算？
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè kē shù bǐ tāmen dōu zǎo lái. Shù bǐ rén xiān dào, píng shénme rén shuō le suàn?
turns.text_en: This tree was here before all of them. The tree arrived first — why do people get to decide?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 因为树不填表。
turns.text_pinyin: Yīnwèi shù bù tián biǎo.
turns.text_en: Because the tree doesn't fill out forms.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Both sides have a point. What do you think?

options.text_zh: 能不能修剪一下，不用砍掉？
options.text_pinyin: Néng bù néng xiūjiǎn yíxià, bú yòng kǎn diào?
options.text_en: Could we trim it instead of cutting it down?
options.feedback: A compromise both sides can live with. Qin checks his regulations. Bai nods as if this was his idea.

options.text_zh: 树在这里这么久了，不应该砍。
options.text_pinyin: Shù zài zhèlǐ zhème jiǔ le, bù yīnggāi kǎn.
options.text_en: The tree has been here so long, we shouldn't cut it.
options.feedback: Emotional appeal. Bai agrees loudly. But the three families still have dark apartments.

options.text_zh: 三家都说要砍，那就砍吧。
options.text_pinyin: Sān jiā dōu shuō yào kǎn, nà jiù kǎn ba.
options.text_en: If three families want it cut, then cut it.
options.feedback: Majority rules, but you just dismissed something that matters to the community's character. Bai looks at you with genuine disappointment.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 修剪...也行。但是谁来剪？这个树不是随便能剪的。
turns.text_pinyin: Xiūjiǎn... yě xíng. Dànshì shéi lái jiǎn? Zhè ge shù bú shì suíbiàn néng jiǎn de.
turns.text_en: Trimming... okay. But who does the trimming? This tree can't be trimmed by just anyone.

tree.cultural_note: Old trees in Chinese communities often have emotional significance. 砍树 (cutting a tree) can feel symbolic of destroying shared history.

tree.setup: Old Zhou opened a small noodle stand for the community fair. He asks Xiao Mei and Auntie Fang for their honest opinions.
tree.setup_zh: 老周在社区集市开了一个小面摊。他问小梅和方阿姨的意见。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 大家觉得我的面怎么样？我觉得非常好！
turns.text_pinyin: Dàjiā juéde wǒ de miàn zěnmeyàng? Wǒ juéde fēicháng hǎo!
turns.text_en: What does everyone think of my noodles? I think they're fantastic!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 面有点硬。汤有点咸。
turns.text_pinyin: Miàn yǒu diǎn yìng. Tāng yǒu diǎn xián.
turns.text_en: The noodles are a bit hard. The soup is a bit salty.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 那是因为有嚼劲！汤是浓的！
turns.text_pinyin: Nà shì yīnwèi yǒu jiáo jìn! Tāng shì nóng de!
turns.text_en: That's because they're chewy! The soup is rich!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 老周的面...有自己的风格。
turns.text_pinyin: Lǎo Zhōu de miàn... yǒu zìjǐ de fēnggé.
turns.text_en: Old Zhou's noodles... have their own style.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Zhou turns to you for your opinion. The noodles are indeed hard and salty.

options.text_zh: 下次可以煮久一点，会更好吃。
options.text_pinyin: Xià cì kěyǐ zhǔ jiǔ yì diǎn, huì gèng hǎo chī.
options.text_en: Next time try cooking them a bit longer — they'll be even better.
options.feedback: Constructive without crushing. The 更好吃 (even better) implies they're already okay. Zhou nods — he heard 'even better,' not 'not done.'

options.text_zh: 挺好的！
options.text_pinyin: Tǐng hǎo de!
options.text_en: Pretty good!
options.feedback: Kind but unhelpful. Zhou's noodles won't improve. Fang gives you a look.

options.text_zh: 小梅说得对，太硬了。
options.text_pinyin: Xiǎo Méi shuō de duì, tài yìng le.
options.text_en: Xiao Mei is right, they're too hard.
options.feedback: True, but now you're piling on. Mei already said it — she didn't need backup. Zhou's smile finally wavers.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 下次让我来放盐。
turns.text_pinyin: Xià cì ràng wǒ lái fàng yán.
turns.text_en: Next time let me handle the salt.

tree.cultural_note: 有自己的风格 (has its own style) is a classic Chinese diplomatic phrase that sounds positive but is actually neutral — a way to acknowledge effort without endorsing quality.

tree.setup: The community wants to take a group photo. Teacher Luo is directing. Getting everyone in position is proving difficult.
tree.setup_zh: 社区想拍一张集体照。罗老师在指挥。让大家站好很难。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 大家站好！高的站后面，矮的站前面！这不难吧？
turns.text_pinyin: Dàjiā zhàn hǎo! Gāo de zhàn hòumiàn, ǎi de zhàn qiánmiàn! Zhè bù nán ba?
turns.text_en: Everyone stand properly! Tall in back, short in front! This isn't hard, is it?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我站中间吧。中间比较重要。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ zhàn zhōngjiān ba. Zhōngjiān bǐjiào zhòngyào.
turns.text_en: I'll stand in the middle. The middle is more important.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你不高也不矮。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ bù gāo yě bù ǎi.
turns.text_en: You're neither tall nor short.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 所以我应该站中间。
turns.text_pinyin: Suǒyǐ wǒ yīnggāi zhàn zhōngjiān.
turns.text_en: So I should stand in the middle.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 中间是给谁的？中间是给——算了，你就站那儿吧。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhōngjiān shì gěi shéi de? Zhōngjiān shì gěi — suàn le, nǐ jiù zhàn nàr ba.
turns.text_en: The middle is for who? The middle is for — forget it, just stand there.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Teacher Luo asks where you want to stand.

options.text_zh: 罗老师，你说我站哪儿就站哪儿。
options.text_pinyin: Luó lǎoshī, nǐ shuō wǒ zhàn nǎr jiù zhàn nǎr.
options.text_en: Teacher Luo, I'll stand wherever you say.
options.feedback: Perfect — you gave the director her authority back. Luo places you precisely and is satisfied at last.

options.text_zh: 我站老白旁边吧。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ zhàn Lǎo Bái pángbiān ba.
options.text_en: I'll stand next to Uncle Bai.
options.feedback: Teacher Luo sighs but allows it. At least someone is standing still.

options.text_zh: 我不想拍照。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bù xiǎng pāi zhào.
options.text_en: I don't want to take a photo.
options.feedback: Teacher Luo's reaction: '不行！这是集体照！集体！' You're in the photo whether you like it or not.

tree.cultural_note: 集体照 (group photos) are taken seriously in Chinese communities and workplaces. Standing position reflects status — center and front are for important people.

tree.setup: Manager Qin has proposed quiet hours from 10pm-7am. Teacher Luo supports it enthusiastically. Uncle Bai thinks the concept of quiet hours is philosophically questionable.
tree.setup_zh: 秦经理提议晚上十点到早上七点是安静时间。罗老师非常支持。老白觉得'安静时间'这个概念值得讨论。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我建议每天晚上十点到早上七点是安静时间。大家同意吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ jiànyì měi tiān wǎnshang shí diǎn dào zǎoshang qī diǎn shì ānjìng shíjiān. Dàjiā tóngyì ma?
turns.text_en: I suggest 10pm to 7am as quiet hours every day. Does everyone agree?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 非常同意！终于！应该早就有了！
turns.text_pinyin: Fēicháng tóngyì! Zhōngyú! Yīnggāi zǎo jiù yǒu le!
turns.text_en: Absolutely agree! Finally! This should have existed long ago!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 可是...什么叫'安静'？这个标准是谁定的？
turns.text_pinyin: Kěshì... shénme jiào 'ānjìng'? Zhè ge biāozhǔn shì shéi dìng de?
turns.text_en: But... what counts as 'quiet'? Who decides the standard?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 不吵就是安静。
turns.text_pinyin: Bù chǎo jiù shì ānjìng.
turns.text_en: Not being noisy is quiet.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 但是一个人觉得安静，另一个人不一定觉得安静。
turns.text_pinyin: Dànshì yí gè rén juéde ānjìng, lìng yí gè rén bù yīdìng juéde ānjìng.
turns.text_en: But what one person considers quiet, another person might not.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Bai's philosophical objection is stalling the vote. How do you move things along?

options.text_zh: 老白说得有道理。但是我们可以先试一个月，看看效果。
options.text_pinyin: Lǎo Bái shuō de yǒu dàolǐ. Dànshì wǒmen kěyǐ xiān shì yí gè yuè, kàn kan xiàoguǒ.
options.text_en: Uncle Bai has a point. But we could try it for a month and see how it goes.
options.feedback: You acknowledged his concern without letting it derail the vote. A trial period satisfies everyone: Luo gets her rules, Bai gets his philosophical wiggle room, Qin gets a process.

options.text_zh: 我同意秦经理的建议。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ tóngyì Qín jīnglǐ de jiànyì.
options.text_en: I agree with Manager Qin's suggestion.
options.feedback: Simple and clear, but Bai feels unheard. He'll bring up the philosophy of quietness again next meeting.

options.text_zh: 老白，你晚上十点以后在干什么？
options.text_pinyin: Lǎo Bái, nǐ wǎnshang shí diǎn yǐhòu zài gàn shénme?
options.text_en: Uncle Bai, what are you doing after 10pm?
options.feedback: You just made it personal. Bai's philosophical defense was theoretical — now he has to justify himself. Awkward silence from Qin.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 试一个月...也行。但是这个问题本身值得深入讨论。
turns.text_pinyin: Shì yí gè yuè... yě xíng. Dànshì zhè ge wèntí běnshēn zhíde shēnrù tǎolùn.
turns.text_en: One month trial... fine. But this question itself deserves deeper discussion.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese community decisions often use a 试行 (trial period) approach as a compromise between immediate action and deliberation.

tree.setup: Four people need to decide where to eat lunch. It has been thirty minutes and no one can agree.
tree.setup_zh: 四个人要决定去哪里吃午饭。已经讨论了三十分钟，还没有决定。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 那家新开的火锅店！我听说很好吃！
turns.text_pinyin: Nà jiā xīn kāi de huǒguō diàn! Wǒ tīngshuō hěn hǎo chī!
turns.text_en: That new hotpot place! I heard it's really good!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你上次听说的那家店，我们排了一个小时的队。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ shàng cì tīngshuō de nà jiā diàn, wǒmen pái le yí gè xiǎoshí de duì.
turns.text_en: The last place you 'heard was good,' we waited in line for an hour.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 但是味道不错啊！
turns.text_pinyin: Dànshì wèidào búcuò a!
turns.text_en: But the food was good!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你点了两份一样的菜。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ diǎn le liǎng fèn yíyàng de cài.
turns.text_en: You ordered two of the same dish.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 随便。
turns.text_pinyin: Suíbiàn.
turns.text_en: Whatever.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: The group needs a decision. What do you suggest?

options.text_zh: 我们去楼下那家面馆吧，近又快。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒmen qù lóu xià nà jiā miànguǎn ba, jìn yòu kuài.
options.text_en: Let's go to the noodle shop downstairs — close and fast.
options.feedback: Decisive and practical. Mei stands up immediately. Fang nods. Zhou agrees but is already planning next time.

options.text_zh: 大家都想吃什么？
options.text_pinyin: Dàjiā dōu xiǎng chī shénme?
options.text_en: What does everyone want to eat?
options.feedback: That's what they've been discussing for thirty minutes. Mei's expression doesn't change but she is judging you.

options.text_zh: 老周想去就去吧。
options.text_pinyin: Lǎo Zhōu xiǎng qù jiù qù ba.
options.text_en: If Zhou wants to go, let's go.
options.feedback: You broke the deadlock by siding with Zhou. Fang accepts this without enthusiasm. Mei says '随便' again.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 讨论了四十分钟。吃什么都一样。
turns.text_pinyin: Tǎolùn le sìshí fēnzhōng. Chī shénme dōu yíyàng.
turns.text_en: Forty minutes of discussion. It's all the same.

tree.cultural_note: 说'随便' (whatever) when asked about food is extremely common in China — it sounds easygoing but often makes decisions harder for everyone else.

tree.setup: It's raining. There are two umbrellas and four people: you, Zhou, Mei, and Luo. Someone needs to share or get wet.
tree.setup_zh: 下雨了。有两把伞，四个人：你、老周、小梅和罗老师。有人要合用或者淋雨。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 只有两把伞！两把！下这么大的雨！
turns.text_pinyin: Zhǐ yǒu liǎng bǎ sǎn! Liǎng bǎ! Xià zhème dà de yǔ!
turns.text_en: Only two umbrellas! Two! And it's raining this hard!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 没关系！我不怕淋雨！
turns.text_pinyin: Méi guānxi! Wǒ bú pà lín yǔ!
turns.text_en: No problem! I'm not afraid of rain!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: ...一把是我的。
turns.text_pinyin: ...Yì bǎ shì wǒ de.
turns.text_en: ...One is mine.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: How do you handle the umbrella situation?

options.text_zh: 罗老师，我们可以一起打一把伞。老周和小梅一把。
options.text_pinyin: Luó lǎoshī, wǒmen kěyǐ yìqǐ dǎ yì bǎ sǎn. Lǎo Zhōu hé Xiǎo Méi yì bǎ.
options.text_en: Teacher Luo, we can share one umbrella. Zhou and Mei share the other.
options.feedback: Everyone covered. Luo accepts with dignity. Mei holds her umbrella and lets Zhou stand under it. Barely.

options.text_zh: 老周说他不怕，让他淋雨吧。
options.text_pinyin: Lǎo Zhōu shuō tā bú pà, ràng tā lín yǔ ba.
options.text_en: Zhou said he's not afraid — let him get wet.
options.feedback: Zhou did say that. He walks in the rain, smiling. He gets completely soaked and is cheerful about it. You feel a tiny bit guilty.

options.text_zh: 我跑回去！
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ pǎo huíqù!
options.text_en: I'll run back!
options.feedback: Brave but impractical. You arrive soaked. Teacher Luo shakes her head. She's taking attendance of who solved this and who didn't.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: ...别碰我的伞。
turns.text_pinyin: ...Bié pèng wǒ de sǎn.
turns.text_en: ...Don't touch my umbrella.

tree.cultural_note: Sharing an umbrella (合打一把伞) in China is normal among friends and colleagues. It's a practical, natural gesture.

tree.setup: Old Zhou didn't get the promotion he expected. He's pretending not to mind. Auntie Fang sees through it. Xiao Mei is present.
tree.setup_zh: 老周没有得到他期待的升职。他假装不在意。方阿姨看得很清楚。小梅在场。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 没关系！升不升职不重要。重要的是工作开心！
turns.text_pinyin: Méi guānxi! Shēng bù shēng zhí bú zhòngyào. Zhòngyào de shì gōngzuò kāixīn!
turns.text_en: It's fine! Getting promoted or not doesn't matter. What matters is being happy at work!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你上个月跟我说你买了新衬衫准备庆祝。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ shàng gè yuè gēn wǒ shuō nǐ mǎi le xīn chènshān zhǔnbèi qìngzhù.
turns.text_en: Last month you told me you bought a new shirt to celebrate.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 那个...那个衬衫是给别的场合买的。
turns.text_pinyin: Nà ge... nà ge chènshān shì gěi bié de chǎnghé mǎi de.
turns.text_en: That... that shirt was for another occasion.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你今天穿的就是那件。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ jīntiān chuān de jiù shì nà jiàn.
turns.text_en: You're wearing it right now.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Zhou is clearly hurt. How do you respond?

options.text_zh: 老周，你的工作大家都看在眼里。以后的机会还有的。
options.text_pinyin: Lǎo Zhōu, nǐ de gōngzuò dàjiā dōu kàn zài yǎn lǐ. Yǐhòu de jīhuì hái yǒu de.
options.text_en: Zhou, everyone can see the work you do. There will be more opportunities.
options.feedback: You acknowledged his feelings without making him admit them. The shirt comment is left alone. Zhou's smile becomes slightly more genuine.

options.text_zh: 确实不公平。你应该升职的。
options.text_pinyin: Quèshí bù gōngpíng. Nǐ yīnggāi shēng zhí de.
options.text_en: It really isn't fair. You should have been promoted.
options.feedback: Validating but risky — you just confirmed he has a reason to be upset, which makes it harder for him to pretend he's fine.

options.text_zh: 衬衫不错。
options.text_pinyin: Chènshān búcuò.
options.text_en: Nice shirt.
options.feedback: You accidentally joined Mei and Fang in exposing the thing he was trying to hide. The shirt is now the elephant in the room.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 衬衫确实不错。
turns.text_pinyin: Chènshān quèshí búcuò.
turns.text_en: The shirt really is nice.

tree.cultural_note: In Chinese culture, maintaining face (面子) when disappointed is important. Good friends acknowledge feelings indirectly rather than forcing someone to admit vulnerability.

tree.setup: Manager Qin has organized a mandatory team-building event: karaoke. Teacher Luo is excited. Xiao Mei would rather not exist.
tree.setup_zh: 秦经理组织了一次团建活动：唱歌。罗老师很兴奋。小梅希望自己不存在。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这次团建是卡拉OK。按规定，所有人都要参加。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè cì tuánjiàn shì kǎlā OK. Àn guīdìng, suǒyǒu rén dōu yào cānjiā.
turns.text_en: This team building is karaoke. According to regulations, everyone must participate.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 太好了！我准备了三首歌！
turns.text_pinyin: Tài hǎo le! Wǒ zhǔnbèi le sān shǒu gē!
turns.text_en: Wonderful! I've prepared three songs!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我有事。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ yǒu shì.
turns.text_en: I'm busy.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 什么事？
turns.text_pinyin: Shénme shì?
turns.text_en: What's the matter?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: ...还没想好。
turns.text_pinyin: ...Hái méi xiǎng hǎo.
turns.text_en: ...Haven't figured that out yet.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: How do you handle the karaoke situation?

options.text_zh: 小梅，我们可以一起唱，这样就不紧张了。
options.text_pinyin: Xiǎo Méi, wǒmen kěyǐ yìqǐ chàng, zhèyàng jiù bù jǐnzhāng le.
options.text_en: Xiao Mei, we can sing together — that way it's less nerve-wracking.
options.feedback: You offered a lifeline without making her admit she's nervous. Mei doesn't smile, but she doesn't object. That's her version of a yes.

options.text_zh: 我也不太想去。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ yě bú tài xiǎng qù.
options.text_en: I don't really want to go either.
options.feedback: Solidarity with Mei, but Manager Qin said 'regulations.' You just put yourself on the wrong side of procedure.

options.text_zh: 罗老师唱什么歌？
options.text_pinyin: Luó lǎoshī chàng shénme gē?
options.text_en: What songs is Teacher Luo singing?
options.feedback: You just opened Pandora's box. Luo launches into a detailed description of all three songs, including the key changes. Mei stares at you.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 一起唱！更好！我来选歌！
turns.text_pinyin: Yìqǐ chàng! Gèng hǎo! Wǒ lái xuǎn gē!
turns.text_en: Sing together! Even better! I'll pick the song!

tree.cultural_note: 团建 (team building) is a regular feature of Chinese workplace culture. Karaoke (KTV) is the most common choice. Refusing to participate can seem antisocial.

tree.setup: Uncle Bai submitted a report. Manager Qin found issues. Teacher Luo has strong feelings about the quality.
tree.setup_zh: 老白交了一份报告。秦经理发现了一些问题。罗老师对报告的质量有很强烈的看法。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 老白，你的报告...第三页跟第五页的数据不一样。
turns.text_pinyin: Lǎo Bái, nǐ de bàogào... dì sān yè gēn dì wǔ yè de shùjù bù yíyàng.
turns.text_en: Uncle Bai, your report... the data on page three doesn't match page five.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 那是因为从不同的角度看，数据会有不同的表现。
turns.text_pinyin: Nà shì yīnwèi cóng bùtóng de jiǎodù kàn, shùjù huì yǒu bùtóng de biǎoxiàn.
turns.text_en: That's because from different perspectives, data can appear differently.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 那是同一组数据！不能从不同角度变成不同的数字！
turns.text_pinyin: Nà shì tóng yì zǔ shùjù! Bù néng cóng bùtóng jiǎodù biàn chéng bùtóng de shùzì!
turns.text_en: It's the same data set! It can't become different numbers from different angles!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我觉得你对'数据'的理解比较狭窄。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde nǐ duì 'shùjù' de lǐjiě bǐjiào xiázhǎi.
turns.text_en: I think your understanding of 'data' is somewhat narrow.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: How do you help resolve this?

options.text_zh: 老白，可能是打字的时候打错了。我们一起看看？
options.text_pinyin: Lǎo Bái, kěnéng shì dǎ zì de shíhou dǎ cuò le. Wǒmen yìqǐ kàn kan?
options.text_en: Uncle Bai, maybe it was a typo. Let's look at it together?
options.feedback: You gave Bai an exit — 'typo' lets him fix it without admitting the philosophical defense was nonsense. Qin nods. Luo is unsatisfied but contained.

options.text_zh: 老白说得也有道理...
options.text_pinyin: Lǎo Bái shuō de yě yǒu dàolǐ...
options.text_en: Uncle Bai has a point...
options.feedback: He does not have a point. The numbers are just wrong. Luo stares at you. Qin checks whether your reports also have 'perspective-dependent' data.

options.text_zh: 罗老师说得对，应该改过来。
options.text_pinyin: Luó lǎoshī shuō de duì, yīnggāi gǎi guòlái.
options.text_en: Teacher Luo is right, it should be corrected.
options.feedback: Correct, but you sided publicly against Bai. He won't forget this. His next report will have a footnote about 'different perspectives on accuracy.'

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 请在周五之前修改好。我需要签字。
turns.text_pinyin: Qǐng zài zhōuwǔ zhīqián xiūgǎi hǎo. Wǒ xūyào qiān zì.
turns.text_en: Please have the corrections done by Friday. I need to sign off.

tree.cultural_note: In Chinese workplaces, giving someone a face-saving exit (台阶) when they've made an error is an important social skill. Blaming a 打字错误 (typo) is a common graceful out.

tree.setup: The office bought a new coffee machine. Nobody can figure out how to use it. Zhou claims he can. Fang has doubts.
tree.setup_zh: 办公室买了一台新咖啡机。没有人会用。老周说他可以。方阿姨有疑虑。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我来！我以前用过这种机器！
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ lái! Wǒ yǐqián yòng guò zhè zhǒng jīqì!
turns.text_en: I'll do it! I've used this kind of machine before!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你以前用过？哪种？
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǐqián yòng guò? Nǎ zhǒng?
turns.text_en: You've used one before? What kind?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 就是...那种有按钮的。
turns.text_pinyin: Jiù shì... nà zhǒng yǒu ànniǔ de.
turns.text_en: You know... the kind with buttons.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 所有咖啡机都有按钮。
turns.text_pinyin: Suǒyǒu kāfēi jī dōu yǒu ànniǔ.
turns.text_en: All coffee machines have buttons.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 对！所以我都会用！
turns.text_pinyin: Duì! Suǒyǐ wǒ dōu huì yòng!
turns.text_en: Right! So I can use all of them!

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Zhou is about to press buttons randomly. What do you do?

options.text_zh: 我先看看说明书吧。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ xiān kàn kan shuōmíngshū ba.
options.text_en: Let me check the manual first.
