options.feedback: The responsible move. Zhou watches impatiently while you read. Fang approves silently. The coffee comes out correctly.

options.text_zh: 老周，你先试试。
options.text_pinyin: Lǎo Zhōu, nǐ xiān shì shi.
options.text_en: Zhou, you try first.
options.feedback: The machine makes a loud noise. Something brown comes out. It is not coffee. Zhou declares it 'a different kind of coffee.'

options.text_zh: 我们还是喝茶吧。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒmen háishi hē chá ba.
options.text_en: Let's just drink tea instead.
options.feedback: Retreat is sometimes wise. But the machine cost 3000 yuan and Manager Qin expects a utilization report.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 说明书在盒子里。老周已经扔了。
turns.text_pinyin: Shuōmíngshū zài hézi lǐ. Lǎo Zhōu yǐjīng rēng le.
turns.text_en: The manual was in the box. Zhou already threw it away.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese offices often have shared appliances that become communal responsibility. The person who first figures out how to use something becomes the unofficial expert.

tree.setup: Two residents are arguing over a parking space. Manager Qin is mediating. Both think they have priority.
tree.setup_zh: 两个居民在争一个停车位。秦经理在调解。两个人都觉得自己有优先权。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 根据小区管理条例，停车位是先到先得。但老张说他每天都停这里。
turns.text_pinyin: Gēnjù xiǎoqū guǎnlǐ tiáolì, tíngchē wèi shì xiān dào xiān dé. Dàn Lǎo Zhāng shuō tā měi tiān dōu tíng zhèlǐ.
turns.text_en: According to community rules, parking is first-come first-served. But Old Zhang says he parks here every day.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 习惯不等于权利。
turns.text_pinyin: Xíguàn bù děngyú quánlì.
turns.text_en: Habit doesn't equal right.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 但是习惯也是一种...社会契约。
turns.text_pinyin: Dànshì xíguàn yě shì yì zhǒng... shèhuì qìyuē.
turns.text_en: But habit is also a kind of... social contract.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你又要讲哲学了。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ yòu yào jiǎng zhéxué le.
turns.text_en: You're going to philosophize again.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: How do you think the parking dispute should be resolved?

options.text_zh: 可以画线标号，每家一个固定的车位。这样最公平。
options.text_pinyin: Kěyǐ huà xiàn biāo hào, měi jiā yí gè gùdìng de chēwèi. Zhèyàng zuì gōngpíng.
options.text_en: We could paint lines and number them — one fixed spot per household. That's fairest.
options.feedback: Systematic solution. Qin reaches for his notebook to draft the procedure. Fang nods. Bai says '这个方法也有它的问题' but nobody asks what problems.

options.text_zh: 方阿姨说得对，规定就是规定。
options.text_pinyin: Fāng āyí shuō de duì, guīdìng jiù shì guīdìng.
options.text_en: Auntie Fang is right — rules are rules.
options.feedback: Legally correct but leaves Old Zhang feeling dismissed. He's been parking there for three years. His frustration is real even if his claim isn't.

options.text_zh: 停车问题太复杂了，不好解决。
options.text_pinyin: Tíngchē wèntí tài fùzá le, bù hǎo jiějué.
options.text_en: Parking problems are too complex — hard to solve.
options.feedback: You just channeled Uncle Bai. Fang looks at you with the expression she usually saves for him.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 画线...需要申请。我去查一下流程。
turns.text_pinyin: Huà xiàn... xūyào shēnqǐng. Wǒ qù chá yíxià liúchéng.
turns.text_en: Painting lines... requires an application. Let me check the process.

tree.cultural_note: 停车位 (parking spaces) are one of the most contentious issues in Chinese residential communities. 先到先得 (first come first served) is the default, but informal 'ownership' through habit is common.

tree.setup: The community needs volunteers for a weekend cleanup. Manager Qin has a sign-up sheet. Zhou signed up three times. Mei hasn't signed up.
tree.setup_zh: 小区周末需要志愿者打扫卫生。秦经理有报名表。老周报了三次名。小梅没有报名。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 目前有五个人报名。其中三次是老周。
turns.text_pinyin: Mùqián yǒu wǔ gè rén bàomíng. Qízhōng sān cì shì Lǎo Zhōu.
turns.text_en: Currently five people have signed up. Three of them are Old Zhou.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我想多做一点！
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng duō zuò yì diǎn!
turns.text_en: I want to do more!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 一个人只能报一次名。这是规定。
turns.text_pinyin: Yí gè rén zhǐ néng bào yí cì míng. Zhè shì guīdìng.
turns.text_en: Each person can only sign up once. That's the rule.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 所以其实只有三个人报名。
turns.text_pinyin: Suǒyǐ qíshí zhǐ yǒu sān gè rén bàomíng.
turns.text_en: So actually only three people signed up.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: The cleanup needs more volunteers. What do you do?

options.text_zh: 我报名。小梅，你也一起来吧？
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ bàomíng. Xiǎo Méi, nǐ yě yìqǐ lái ba?
options.text_en: I'll sign up. Xiao Mei, will you join too?
options.feedback: You led by example and made it easy for Mei to join without feeling pressured. She pauses. '...好吧。' That's a yes.

options.text_zh: 让老周做三个人的工作不就行了？
options.text_pinyin: Ràng Lǎo Zhōu zuò sān gè rén de gōngzuò bù jiù xíng le?
options.text_en: Can't Zhou just do the work of three people?
options.feedback: Zhou agrees enthusiastically. Qin explains that insurance only covers one volunteer per registration. Zhou's enthusiasm is now a liability issue.

options.text_zh: 这周末我有事。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè zhōumò wǒ yǒu shì.
options.text_en: I'm busy this weekend.
options.feedback: Mei's exact move. Now there are two of you not volunteering and three of Zhou.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 太好了！我们一起打扫！我负责最大的区域！
turns.text_pinyin: Tài hǎo le! Wǒmen yìqǐ dǎsǎo! Wǒ fùzé zuì dà de qūyù!
turns.text_en: Great! We'll clean together! I'll take the biggest area!

tree.cultural_note: Volunteering in Chinese communities often works through social pressure — one person stepping forward makes it easier (or harder to avoid) for others.

tree.setup: The community has 5000 yuan left in the annual budget. Teacher Luo wants to fix the community library. Old Zhou wants to throw a party. Manager Qin has a spreadsheet.
tree.setup_zh: 社区年度预算还剩五千块。罗老师想修社区图书室。老周想办一个联欢活动。秦经理有一份电子表格。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 图书室的灯坏了三个月了！三个月！大家都在黑暗中看书！
turns.text_pinyin: Túshūshì de dēng huài le sān gè yuè le! Sān gè yuè! Dàjiā dōu zài hēi'àn zhōng kàn shū!
turns.text_en: The library lights have been broken for three months! Three months! Everyone's reading in the dark!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 但是！大家工作了一年，应该开心一下！联欢活动能让大家更团结！
turns.text_pinyin: Dànshì! Dàjiā gōngzuò le yì nián, yīnggāi kāixīn yíxià! Liánhuān huódòng néng ràng dàjiā gèng tuánjié!
turns.text_en: But! Everyone's worked for a year — they deserve to have fun! A party will bring everyone together!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 修灯预计需要三千块。联欢活动至少要四千五。预算只有五千。
turns.text_pinyin: Xiū dēng yùjì xūyào sān qiān kuài. Liánhuān huódòng zhìshǎo yào sì qiān wǔ. Yùsuàn zhǐ yǒu wǔ qiān.
turns.text_en: Light repairs are estimated at 3000 yuan. A party needs at least 4500. The budget is only 5000.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 那就办一个简单的联欢活动！不用那么多钱！
turns.text_pinyin: Nà jiù bàn yí gè jiǎndān de liánhuān huódòng! Bú yòng nàme duō qián!
turns.text_en: Then let's have a simple party! It doesn't need that much money!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 简单的联欢还不如不办。要办就要办好。
turns.text_pinyin: Jiǎndān de liánhuān hái bùrú bù bàn. Yào bàn jiù yào bàn hǎo.
turns.text_en: A simple party is worse than no party. If you do it, do it properly.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: How do you think the 5000 yuan should be spent?

options.text_zh: 先修灯，剩下的两千块可以买一些零食，在图书室里开一个小型茶话会。
options.text_pinyin: Xiān xiū dēng, shèng xià de liǎng qiān kuài kěyǐ mǎi yìxiē língshí, zài túshūshì lǐ kāi yí gè xiǎoxíng cháhuàhuì.
options.text_en: Fix the lights first, then use the remaining 2000 for snacks and have a small tea party in the library.
options.feedback: Both needs met within budget. Luo gets her lights. Zhou gets his gathering. Qin's spreadsheet balances. This is governance.

options.text_zh: 灯比较重要，应该先修灯。
options.text_pinyin: Dēng bǐjiào zhòngyào, yīnggāi xiān xiū dēng.
options.text_en: The lights are more important — fix them first.
options.feedback: Correct priority, but Zhou is disappointed. His 'no problem!' smile doesn't reach his eyes.

options.text_zh: 都重要，可以跟上面申请追加预算。
options.text_pinyin: Dōu zhòngyào, kěyǐ gēn shàngmiàn shēnqǐng zhuījiā yùsuàn.
options.text_en: Both are important — we could apply for additional budget from above.
options.feedback: Qin's face suggests this has been tried before. The answer from 'above' was, apparently, no.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 茶话会...我查一下有没有相关的审批流程。
turns.text_pinyin: Cháhuàhuì... wǒ chá yíxià yǒu méi yǒu xiāngguān de shěnpī liúchéng.
turns.text_en: Tea party... let me check if there's a relevant approval process.

tree.cultural_note: Budget allocation in Chinese communities often involves balancing 面子 (face) considerations with practical needs. A 茶话会 (tea party) is a low-key alternative to expensive formal events.

tree.setup: The team interviewed a candidate. Auntie Fang, Xiao Mei, and Old Zhou have different opinions. You're the deciding vote.
tree.setup_zh: 团队面试了一个候选人。方阿姨、小梅和老周有不同的看法。你是决定性的一票。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 很好！态度很积极！问什么都说'我可以学'！我觉得应该录取！
turns.text_pinyin: Hěn hǎo! Tàidù hěn jījí! Wèn shénme dōu shuō 'wǒ kěyǐ xué'! Wǒ juéde yīnggāi lùqǔ!
turns.text_en: Great! Very positive attitude! Said 'I can learn' to everything! I think we should hire them!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 说'我可以学'是因为他现在不会。他回答了八个问题，有七个是'我可以学'。
turns.text_pinyin: Shuō 'wǒ kěyǐ xué' shì yīnwèi tā xiànzài bú huì. Tā huídá le bā gè wèntí, yǒu qī gè shì 'wǒ kěyǐ xué'.
turns.text_en: He said 'I can learn' because he doesn't know how right now. He answered eight questions — seven were 'I can learn.'

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 简历上写了'精通Excel'。你问他什么是数据透视表，他说'我可以学'。
turns.text_pinyin: Jiǎnlì shàng xiě le 'jīngtōng Excel'. Nǐ wèn tā shénme shì shùjù tòushì biǎo, tā shuō 'wǒ kěyǐ xué'.
turns.text_en: His resume says 'proficient in Excel.' When asked what a pivot table is, he said 'I can learn.'

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 态度比能力更重要！
turns.text_pinyin: Tàidù bǐ nénglì gèng zhòngyào!
turns.text_en: Attitude is more important than ability!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 能力是零的时候，态度再好也没有用。
turns.text_pinyin: Nénglì shì líng de shíhou, tàidù zài hǎo yě méi yǒu yòng.
turns.text_en: When ability is zero, no amount of attitude helps.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: You're the deciding vote. What's your assessment?

options.text_zh: 他确实态度不错，但是这个岗位需要马上能上手的人。我们可以留着他的简历，下次有初级岗位的时候联系他。
options.text_pinyin: Tā quèshí tàidù búcuò, dànshì zhè ge gǎngwèi xūyào mǎshàng néng shàngshǒu de rén. Wǒmen kěyǐ liú zhe tā de jiǎnlì, xià cì yǒu chūjí gǎngwèi de shíhou liánxì tā.
options.text_en: His attitude is good, but this role needs someone who can start immediately. We can keep his resume and contact him when there's a junior position.
options.feedback: Balanced. You respected Zhou's observation about attitude, accepted Fang's evidence about skills, and found a constructive middle path.

options.text_zh: 我同意方阿姨。不应该录取。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ tóngyì Fāng āyí. Bù yīnggāi lùqǔ.
options.text_en: I agree with Auntie Fang. We shouldn't hire him.
options.feedback: The right call, but Zhou feels outnumbered. He'll mention the candidate's 'positive energy' two more times this week.

options.text_zh: 给他一个试用期的机会吧。
options.text_pinyin: Gěi tā yí gè shìyòng qī de jīhuì ba.
options.text_en: Let's give him a probation period.
options.feedback: Kind, but you just hired someone who can't do 7/8 of the job. Fang makes a note. In three months, she'll remind you of this moment.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 保留简历...行。但是下次面试，老周不负责打分。
turns.text_pinyin: Bǎoliú jiǎnlì... xíng. Dànshì xià cì miànshì, Lǎo Zhōu bù fùzé dǎ fēn.
turns.text_en: Keep the resume... fine. But next interview, Zhou doesn't do the scoring.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese hiring often weighs 态度 (attitude) and 能力 (ability) as separate dimensions. The tension between 'will learn' and 'already knows' is a genuine disagreement, not just a formula.

tree.setup: A family on the third floor is renovating. The noise starts at 8am. Teacher Luo filed a complaint. The family says they're following the rules. Both are right.
tree.setup_zh: 三楼一家人在装修。噪音从早上八点开始。罗老师投诉了。这家人说他们遵守了规定。双方都有道理。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 每天早上八点！电钻的声音！我在家工作，根本无法集中精力！
turns.text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang bā diǎn! Diànzuàn de shēngyīn! Wǒ zài jiā gōngzuò, gēnběn wúfǎ jízhōng jīnglì!
turns.text_en: Every morning at 8am! The sound of drills! I work from home — I can't concentrate at all!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我查了规定。装修时间是早上八点到晚上六点，周末不允许。他们没有违规。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ chá le guīdìng. Zhuāngxiū shíjiān shì zǎoshang bā diǎn dào wǎnshang liù diǎn, zhōumò bù yǔnxǔ. Tāmen méi yǒu wéiguī.
turns.text_en: I checked the regulations. Renovation hours are 8am to 6pm, not allowed on weekends. They're not violating any rules.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 规定不代表合理！合规不代表合情！
turns.text_pinyin: Guīdìng bù dàibiǎo hélǐ! Hé guī bù dàibiǎo hé qíng!
turns.text_en: Following rules doesn't mean it's reasonable! Legal doesn't mean considerate!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 但是在管理层面，我只能依据规定处理。
turns.text_pinyin: Dànshì zài guǎnlǐ céngmiàn, wǒ zhǐ néng yījù guīdìng chǔlǐ.
turns.text_en: But at the management level, I can only act based on regulations.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Both sides have valid points. What do you suggest?

options.text_zh: 也许可以跟三楼商量一下，每天装修前先通知一声，让大家有心理准备。不改时间，但加一个沟通环节。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ kěyǐ gēn sān lóu shāngliang yíxià, měi tiān zhuāngxiū qián xiān tōngzhī yì shēng, ràng dàjiā yǒu xīnlǐ zhǔnbèi. Bù gǎi shíjiān, dàn jiā yí gè gōutōng huánjié.
options.text_en: Maybe we could talk to the third floor — give a heads-up before starting each day, so people can prepare. Don't change the schedule, but add a communication step.
options.feedback: Elegant. You preserved the rule AND addressed Luo's real complaint (the feeling of powerlessness). Qin notes this as a potential new procedure.

options.text_zh: 罗老师可以戴耳塞。
options.text_pinyin: Luó lǎoshī kěyǐ dài ěrsāi.
options.text_en: Teacher Luo could wear earplugs.
options.feedback: You just told the person with the complaint to adapt. Luo's face suggests you have made an enemy for life.

options.text_zh: 规定确实说八点可以开始。没办法。
options.text_pinyin: Guīdìng quèshí shuō bā diǎn kěyǐ kāishǐ. Méi bànfǎ.
options.text_en: The rules do say 8am is allowed. Nothing to be done.
options.feedback: Technically true, but you took the rule's side against the person. Luo isn't wrong that 合规不等于合情. Some situations need more than rules.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 提前通知...这个可以做。我起草一个沟通模板。
turns.text_pinyin: Tíqián tōngzhī... zhè ge kěyǐ zuò. Wǒ qǐcǎo yí gè gōutōng múbǎn.
turns.text_en: Advance notice... that's doable. I'll draft a communication template.

tree.cultural_note: 合规 (legal compliance) vs 合情 (emotional reasonableness) is a genuine tension in Chinese community life. Good solutions often address both.

tree.setup: The community is having a cooking competition. Zhou is confident. Fang is skeptical. Bai is judging, which no one asked for.
tree.setup_zh: 社区在办厨艺比赛。老周很自信。方阿姨持怀疑态度。老白自己当了评委，没人邀请他。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这次我做我的拿手菜！红烧肉！绝对没问题！
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè cì wǒ zuò wǒ de náshǒu cài! Hóngshāo ròu! Juéduì méi wèntí!
turns.text_en: This time I'm making my specialty! Braised pork! Absolutely no problem!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 上次你做红烧肉，把锅烧了一个洞。
turns.text_pinyin: Shàng cì nǐ zuò hóngshāo ròu, bǎ guō shāo le yí gè dòng.
turns.text_en: Last time you made braised pork, you burned a hole through the pot.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 那是锅的质量问题！不是我的问题！
turns.text_pinyin: Nà shì guō de zhìliàng wèntí! Bú shì wǒ de wèntí!
turns.text_en: That was a quality issue with the pot! Not my cooking!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 作为评委，我觉得应该给每个人机会。不过，老周，你的红烧肉我不敢打高分。这不是因为不好，是因为我有自己的评判标准。
turns.text_pinyin: Zuòwéi píngwěi, wǒ juéde yīnggāi gěi měi gè rén jīhuì. Búguò, Lǎo Zhōu, nǐ de hóngshāo ròu wǒ bù gǎn dǎ gāo fēn. Zhè bú shì yīnwèi bù hǎo, shì yīnwèi wǒ yǒu zìjǐ de píngpàn biāozhǔn.
turns.text_en: As a judge, I believe everyone deserves a chance. However, Zhou, I wouldn't dare score your braised pork high. Not because it's bad, but because I have my own judging criteria.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 谁让你当评委了？
turns.text_pinyin: Shéi ràng nǐ dāng píngwěi le?
turns.text_en: Who made you a judge?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: How do you weigh in?

options.text_zh: 老周，你做你想做的菜。比赛的重点是参与，不是结果。方阿姨，你也参加吧？
options.text_pinyin: Lǎo Zhōu, nǐ zuò nǐ xiǎng zuò de cài. Bǐsài de zhòngdiǎn shì cānyù, bú shì jiéguǒ. Fāng āyí, nǐ yě cānjiā ba?
options.text_en: Zhou, make whatever you want. The point of the competition is participation, not winning. Auntie Fang, will you enter too?
options.feedback: You redirected the energy. Zhou stays motivated. Fang's eyebrow rises — she's interested. Bai's self-appointed judge position goes unchallenged, which is its own kind of community management.

options.text_zh: 老白，评委应该是秦经理决定的吧？
options.text_pinyin: Lǎo Bái, píngwěi yīnggāi shì Qín jīnglǐ juédìng de ba?
options.text_en: Uncle Bai, shouldn't Manager Qin decide who the judges are?
options.feedback: Procedurally correct. Bai looks slightly offended. Qin, when reached, says he'll need to create a judge selection process. The competition may be delayed.

options.text_zh: 方阿姨做得比较好吃，她来做吧。
options.text_pinyin: Fāng āyí zuò de bǐjiào hǎo chī, tā lái zuò ba.
options.text_en: Auntie Fang's cooking is better — she should do it.
options.feedback: You just publicly ranked their cooking. Zhou's face crumples. Fang says '我没说我要参加' (I didn't say I was entering). Bai marks something on an imaginary clipboard.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 参与确实很重要。这正是我当评委的理由。
turns.text_pinyin: Cānyù quèshí hěn zhòngyào. Zhè zhèng shì wǒ dāng píngwěi de lǐyóu.
turns.text_en: Participation is indeed important. That's precisely why I'm a judge.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese community events often involve informal self-appointment (自封) of roles. Challenging it directly is awkward; redirecting the energy is more effective.

tree.setup: Someone's delivery was left at the wrong door. Teacher Luo received a package that isn't hers. Xiao Mei received nothing. Manager Qin is investigating.
tree.setup_zh: 有人的快递送错了门。罗老师收到了一个不是她的包裹。小梅什么都没收到。秦经理在调查。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这不是我的包裹！上面写的名字也不是我的！快递员怎么能犯这种错误？
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè bú shì wǒ de bāoguǒ! Shàngmiàn xiě de míngzì yě bú shì wǒ de! Kuàidìyuán zěnme néng fàn zhè zhǒng cuòwù?
turns.text_en: This isn't my package! The name on it isn't mine either! How can a delivery person make this kind of mistake?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 上面写的是谁的名字？
turns.text_pinyin: Shàngmiàn xiě de shì shéi de míngzì?
turns.text_en: Whose name is on it?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_pinyin: ...Xiǎo Méi.
turns.text_en: ...Xiao Mei.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 那是我的包裹。
turns.text_pinyin: Nà shì wǒ de bāoguǒ.
turns.text_en: That's my package.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 快递记录显示，1403号和1304号经常搞混。要不要我跟快递公司反映？
turns.text_pinyin: Kuàidì jìlù xiǎnshì, yāo sì líng sān hào hé yāo sān líng sì hào jīngcháng gǎo hùn. Yào bú yào wǒ gēn kuàidì gōngsī fǎnyìng?
turns.text_en: Delivery records show units 1403 and 1304 are frequently confused. Should I report this to the delivery company?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: How do you help resolve this?

options.text_zh: 秦经理，可以在门口贴一个更明显的门牌号，快递员看到大号数字就不容易搞错了。
options.text_pinyin: Qín jīnglǐ, kěyǐ zài ménkǒu tiē yí gè gèng míngxiǎn de ménpái hào, kuàidìyuán kàn dào dà hào shùzì jiù bù róngyì gǎo cuò le.
options.text_en: Manager Qin, we could put more visible unit numbers on the doors — delivery people won't mix them up if the numbers are bigger.
options.feedback: Root cause solution. Qin immediately starts drafting a signage improvement proposal. Luo is satisfied. Mei takes her package.

options.text_zh: 罗老师，你可以先把包裹给小梅。
options.text_pinyin: Luó lǎoshī, nǐ kěyǐ xiān bǎ bāoguǒ gěi Xiǎo Méi.
options.text_en: Teacher Luo, you can just give the package to Xiao Mei.
options.feedback: Immediate fix but doesn't address the pattern. Luo hands over the package but is still upset about the principle.

options.text_zh: 这种事经常发生，没什么大不了的。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè zhǒng shì jīngcháng fāshēng, méi shénme dà bù liǎo de.
options.text_en: This happens all the time, it's not a big deal.
options.feedback: You just minimized Luo's concern to her face. She collects herself. 'Not a big deal?' Her voice is very quiet. This is worse than when she yells.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 谢谢。
turns.text_pinyin: Xièxie.
turns.text_en: Thanks.

tree.cultural_note: 快递 (delivery) problems in Chinese apartments are extremely common. Systematic solutions like clear numbering earn community respect.

tree.setup: The community center found old photographs from 20 years ago during renovation. The photos show the neighborhood before modernization. Auntie Fang, Uncle Bai, and Teacher Luo react very differently.
tree.setup_zh: 社区中心装修时发现了二十年前的老照片，拍的是这个街区现代化之前的样子。方阿姨、老白和罗老师的反应非常不同。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这是那个面馆。在这张照片里你还能看到后面的巷子。现在那里是停车场。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè shì nà ge miànguǎn. Zài zhè zhāng zhàopiàn lǐ nǐ hái néng kàn dào hòumiàn de xiàngzi. Xiànzài nàlǐ shì tíngchēchǎng.
turns.text_en: This is that noodle shop. In this photo you can still see the alley behind it. Now it's a parking lot.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 那个时候比较好。人跟人之间有温度。现在每个人回去就关门，跟邻居一年说不上三句话。
turns.text_pinyin: Nà ge shíhou bǐjiào hǎo. Rén gēn rén zhījiān yǒu wēndù. Xiànzài měi gè rén huíqù jiù guān mén, gēn línjū yì nián shuō bù shàng sān jù huà.
turns.text_en: It was better back then. There was warmth between people. Now everyone goes home and closes the door — you might not say three sentences to your neighbor in a whole year.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 但是那个时候没有暖气，没有独立卫生间。怀旧不能忽略事实。
turns.text_pinyin: Dànshì nà ge shíhou méi yǒu nuǎnqì, méi yǒu dúlì wèishēngjiān. Huáijiù bù néng hūlüè shìshí.
turns.text_en: But back then there was no heating, no private bathrooms. Nostalgia can't ignore facts.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你说的是物质。我说的是精神。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ shuō de shì wùzhì. Wǒ shuō de shì jīngshén.
turns.text_en: You're talking about material things. I'm talking about spirit.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你那个时候也关门。我记得很清楚。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ nà ge shíhou yě guān mén. Wǒ jì de hěn qīngchǔ.
turns.text_en: You closed your door back then too. I remember clearly.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: The photos have triggered a real conversation. What do you contribute?

options.text_zh: 两个说法都有道理。现在条件好了，但是有些东西确实不一样了。也许我们可以把这些照片在社区展出，让大家都看看以前是什么样的。
options.text_pinyin: Liǎng gè shuōfǎ dōu yǒu dàolǐ. Xiànzài tiáojiàn hǎo le, dànshì yǒuxiē dōngxi quèshí bù yíyàng le. Yěxǔ wǒmen kěyǐ bǎ zhèxiē zhàopiàn zài shèqū zhǎnchū, ràng dàjiā dōu kàn kan yǐqián shì shénme yàng de.
options.text_en: Both views make sense. Conditions are better now, but some things are genuinely different. Maybe we could exhibit these photos in the community — let everyone see what it used to be like.
options.feedback: You honored both positions and turned the disagreement into something constructive. Bai nods as if vindicated. Luo thinks the exhibit needs proper signage. Fang says nothing, which is her version of 'not bad.'

options.text_zh: 老白说得对，以前确实比较有人情味。
options.text_pinyin: Lǎo Bái shuō de duì, yǐqián quèshí bǐjiào yǒu rénqíng wèi.
options.text_en: Uncle Bai is right — there really was more human warmth before.
options.feedback: Luo's jaw tightens. Fang just pointed out that Bai's own behavior doesn't match his nostalgia. You're romanticizing alongside someone whose memories are selective.

options.text_zh: 发展是好事，不能光看过去。
options.text_pinyin: Fāzhǎn shì hǎo shì, bù néng guāng kàn guòqù.
options.text_en: Development is good — we can't just look at the past.
options.feedback: True but dismissive. Fang was sharing a specific memory of a real place. Reducing it to 'development is good' is exactly the kind of statement she would dismantle.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 展出可以。但是不要配那种'时代变了我们不变'的标语。让照片说话就行。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhǎnchū kěyǐ. Dànshì bú yào pèi nà zhǒng 'shídài biàn le wǒmen bú biàn' de biāoyǔ. Ràng zhàopiàn shuōhuà jiù xíng.
turns.text_en: An exhibit is fine. But don't add those 'times change but we don't' slogans. Let the photos speak.

tree.cultural_note: Nostalgia for old neighborhoods (老街区) is a genuine tension in rapidly modernized Chinese cities. The debate between material progress and community warmth (人情味) has no clean resolution.

tree.setup: Manager Qin installed a community suggestion box. After one week, he has 47 suggestions. Most are from Old Zhou.
tree.setup_zh: 秦经理在小区装了一个意见箱。一周后收到了四十七条建议。大部分是老周写的。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 四十七条建议。其中三十二条是老周的。我认得他的字。
turns.text_pinyin: Sìshíqī tiáo jiànyì. Qízhōng sānshí'èr tiáo shì Lǎo Zhōu de. Wǒ rèn de tā de zì.
turns.text_en: Forty-seven suggestions. Thirty-two are from Old Zhou. I recognize his handwriting.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我关心社区！每天都有新想法！
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ guānxīn shèqū! Měi tiān dōu yǒu xīn xiǎngfǎ!
turns.text_en: I care about the community! I have new ideas every day!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 第十七条建议是在停车场种西瓜。第二十三条也是种西瓜，但是措辞不同。
turns.text_pinyin: Dì shíqī tiáo jiànyì shì zài tíngchēchǎng zhòng xīguā. Dì èrshísān tiáo yě shì zhòng xīguā, dànshì cuòcí bùtóng.
turns.text_en: Suggestion #17 is to grow watermelons in the parking lot. Suggestion #23 is also watermelons, but worded differently.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 有一条不是老周的。上面只写了一个字：'嗯'。
turns.text_pinyin: Yǒu yì tiáo bú shì Lǎo Zhōu de. Shàngmiàn zhǐ xiě le yí gè zì: 'ǹg'.
turns.text_en: There's one that isn't Zhou's. It just says one word: 'mm.'

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: ...小梅，那是你写的吗？
turns.text_pinyin: ...Xiǎo Méi, nà shì nǐ xiě de ma?
turns.text_en: ...Xiao Mei, was that yours?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 嗯。
turns.text_pinyin: Ǹg.
turns.text_en: Mm.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Qin needs help making the suggestion box actually useful. What do you recommend?

options.text_zh: 可以设一个规则：每人每周最多提三条建议，每条不超过一百个字。这样大家都有机会发言，也鼓励大家认真想。
options.text_pinyin: Kěyǐ shè yí gè guīzé: měi rén měi zhōu zuìduō tí sān tiáo jiànyì, měi tiáo bù chāoguò yìbǎi gè zì. Zhèyàng dàjiā dōu yǒu jīhuì fāyán, yě gǔlì dàjiā rènzhēn xiǎng.
options.text_en: We could set a rule: max three suggestions per person per week, max 100 characters each. That gives everyone a voice and encourages thoughtful submissions.
options.feedback: Structure without killing participation. Zhou considers his three weekly slots carefully. Fang murmurs that 'one would be sufficient.' Qin writes the new policy.

options.text_zh: 让老周的建议也正式讨论。也许有一两条真的可行。
options.text_pinyin: Ràng Lǎo Zhōu de jiànyì yě zhèngshì tǎolùn. Yěxǔ yǒu yì liǎng tiáo zhēn de kěxíng.
options.text_en: Let's formally discuss Zhou's suggestions too. Maybe one or two are actually feasible.
options.feedback: Generous. Qin schedules a review session. Zhou is thrilled. After three hours, one suggestion (better lighting in stairwell B) is approved. The watermelon proposals are tabled indefinitely.

options.text_zh: 也许意见箱不适合我们小区。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ yìjiàn xiāng bú shìhé wǒmen xiǎoqū.
options.text_en: Maybe the suggestion box isn't right for our community.
options.feedback: Qin spent two weeks installing and designing the system. You just suggested scrapping it. His expression remains neutral, but a muscle in his jaw moves.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 三条限制...我需要做一个新的告示。还有，西瓜的事，我正式记录一下：已读，暂不执行。
turns.text_pinyin: Sān tiáo xiànzhì... wǒ xūyào zuò yí gè xīn de gàoshi. Háiyǒu, xīguā de shì, wǒ zhèngshì jìlù yíxià: yǐ dú, zàn bù zhíxíng.
turns.text_en: Three-suggestion limit... I need to make a new notice. Also, regarding the watermelons, let me officially note: read, not implemented at this time.

tree.cultural_note: 意见箱 (suggestion boxes) are common in Chinese institutions. They often become dominated by a few enthusiastic contributors, creating a participation imbalance.

tree.setup: The community garden plot is being divided. Some residents want individual plots. Others want a shared garden. Both approaches have real advantages and real problems.
tree.setup_zh: 社区花园要分配了。有的居民想要个人地块，有的想要共享花园。两种方案都有真正的优点和真正的问题。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 分开的地块才公平！每个人管好自己的，责任清楚，不会有人偷懒。上次共享花园，杂草长了两个月没人拔！
turns.text_pinyin: Fēnkāi de dìkuài cái gōngpíng! Měi gè rén guǎn hǎo zìjǐ de, zérèn qīngchǔ, bú huì yǒu rén tōu lǎn. Shàng cì gòngxiǎng huāyuán, zácǎo zhǎng le liǎng gè yuè méi rén bá!
turns.text_en: Individual plots are fair! Everyone takes care of their own, responsibilities are clear, no freeloaders. Last time with the shared garden, weeds grew for two months with nobody pulling them!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 分开以后就不是'社区花园'了，是'私人花园挨在一起'。花园的意义是大家一起种，一起看着长。
turns.text_pinyin: Fēnkāi yǐhòu jiù bú shì 'shèqū huāyuán' le, shì 'sīrén huāyuán āi zài yìqǐ'. Huāyuán de yìyì shì dàjiā yìqǐ zhòng, yìqǐ kàn zhe zhǎng.
turns.text_en: Once you divide it, it's not a 'community garden' anymore — it's 'private gardens next to each other.' The point of a garden is growing things together, watching them grow together.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你上次一棵草都没拔。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ shàng cì yì kē cǎo dōu méi bá.
turns.text_en: You didn't pull a single weed last time.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我在观察。有些草不一定是杂草。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ zài guānchá. Yǒuxiē cǎo bù yīdìng shì zácǎo.
turns.text_en: I was observing. Some of those might not have been weeds.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 两个方案都有问题。分开了没有社区感。共享了没有人负责。关键是：谁愿意真正花时间？不是口头上说，是实际去做。
turns.text_pinyin: Liǎng gè fāng'àn dōu yǒu wèntí. Fēnkāi le méi yǒu shèqū gǎn. Gòngxiǎng le méi yǒu rén fùzé. Guānjiàn shì: shéi yuànyì zhēnzhèng huā shíjiān? Bú shì kǒutóu shàng shuō, shì shíjì qù zuò.
turns.text_en: Both plans have problems. Divide it and you lose community. Share it and nobody takes responsibility. The real question is: who's actually willing to put in time? Not talking — doing.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: How do you think the garden should be managed?

options.text_zh: 可以两个方案结合：三分之二共享种菜，三分之一分成小块给想自己种的人。共享部分每周排一个值日表，一家一天。
options.text_pinyin: Kěyǐ liǎng gè fāng'àn jiéhé: sān fēn zhī èr gòngxiǎng zhòng cài, sān fēn zhī yī fēn chéng xiǎo kuài gěi xiǎng zìjǐ zhòng de rén. Gòngxiǎng bùfen měi zhōu pái yí gè zhírì biǎo, yì jiā yì tiān.
options.text_en: We could combine both: two-thirds shared for community vegetables, one-third divided into small plots for individuals. The shared part gets a weekly duty roster — one household per day.
options.feedback: Structural compromise with accountability built in. Luo gets her clear responsibilities. Bai gets his community ideal (partially). Fang nods — you answered her question about who actually does the work.

options.text_zh: 老白说得对，应该保持共享。
options.text_pinyin: Lǎo Bái shuō de duì, yīnggāi bǎochí gòngxiǎng.
options.text_en: Uncle Bai is right — we should keep it shared.
options.feedback: Noble but Luo's point stands: last time, nobody weeded. Ideals without structure produce weedy gardens.

options.text_zh: 分开比较简单，不容易吵架。
options.text_pinyin: Fēnkāi bǐjiào jiǎndān, bù róngyì chǎojià.
options.text_en: Dividing is simpler — less arguing.
options.feedback: Pragmatic but reductive. Bai looks personally wounded. Fang says '确实简单' in a tone that means 'and simplistic.' The garden is more than a conflict-avoidance problem.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 值日表...我可以做。但是谁来监督执行？算了，我来监督。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhírì biǎo... wǒ kěyǐ zuò. Dànshì shéi lái jiāndū zhíxíng? Suàn le, wǒ lái jiāndū.
turns.text_en: Duty roster... I can make that. But who monitors compliance? Never mind, I'll monitor.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese communities frequently face the 公共 vs 私人 (public vs private) management tension. Hybrid solutions with 值日表 (duty rosters) are a practical compromise that honors both individual accountability and collective spirit.

tree.setup: Someone proposed putting a 'civilization standard' sign in the elevator. Teacher Luo drafted one. Auntie Fang has notes. The sign has fourteen rules.
tree.setup_zh: 有人提议在电梯里贴文明标语。罗老师草拟了一份。方阿姨有一些意见。标语上有十四条规则。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我写了十四条。涵盖了所有情况。从按钮到站位到说话音量到宠物到外卖到购物袋——全面覆盖。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ xiě le shísì tiáo. Hángài le suǒyǒu qíngkuàng. Cóng ànniǔ dào zhànwèi dào shuōhuà yīnliàng dào chǒngwù dào wàimài dào gòuwù dài — quánmiàn fùgài.
turns.text_en: I wrote fourteen rules. They cover all situations. From buttons to standing positions to speaking volume to pets to takeout to shopping bags — comprehensive coverage.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 十四条。在电梯里。电梯里只有两面墙。
turns.text_pinyin: Shísì tiáo. Zài diàntī lǐ. Diàntī lǐ zhǐ yǒu liǎng miàn qiáng.
turns.text_en: Fourteen rules. In an elevator. The elevator has two walls.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 可以用小号字体。
turns.text_pinyin: Kěyǐ yòng xiǎo hào zìtǐ.
turns.text_en: We can use smaller font.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 那谁看得见？
turns.text_pinyin: Nà shéi kàn de jiàn?
turns.text_en: Then who can read it?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 没有人看电梯里的标语。
turns.text_pinyin: Méi yǒu rén kàn diàntī lǐ de biāoyǔ.
turns.text_en: Nobody reads elevator signs.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 那是因为标语写得不好！好的标语人们会看的！
turns.text_pinyin: Nà shì yīnwèi biāoyǔ xiě de bù hǎo! Hǎo de biāoyǔ rénmen huì kàn de!
turns.text_en: That's because the signs are poorly written! Good signs people will read!

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: How do you help resolve the elevator sign debate?

options.text_zh: 不如只留最重要的三条。短一点，大家更容易记住。罗老师选三条最关键的？
options.text_pinyin: Bùrú zhǐ liú zuì zhòngyào de sān tiáo. Duǎn yì diǎn, dàjiā gèng róngyì jì zhù. Luó lǎoshī xuǎn sān tiáo zuì guānjiàn de?
options.text_en: How about keeping only the three most important ones? Shorter means more memorable. Teacher Luo, could you pick the three most critical?
options.feedback: You preserved Luo's authority while solving Fang's objection. Asking Luo to choose respects her expertise. She agonizes — all fourteen feel essential — but eventually distills them. The final three are actually effective.

options.text_zh: 小梅说得对，标语没什么用。
options.text_pinyin: Xiǎo Méi shuō de duì, biāoyǔ méi shénme yòng.
options.text_en: Xiao Mei is right — signs don't really work.
options.feedback: Luo's face cycles through three emotions in two seconds. She spent a week on this. Mei's observation was accurate but she didn't mean it as a policy recommendation. You weaponized her deadpan.

options.text_zh: 十四条也可以，分两面墙贴。
options.text_pinyin: Shísì tiáo yě kěyǐ, fēn liǎng miàn qiáng tiē.
options.text_en: Fourteen is fine — put them on both walls.
options.feedback: Logistically creative but Fang's point was about readability, not wall space. The elevator will feel like a regulation museum.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 三条。三条如果选得好，可以管得住十四条要管的事。
turns.text_pinyin: Sān tiáo. Sān tiáo rúguǒ xuǎn de hǎo, kěyǐ guǎn de zhù shísì tiáo yào guǎn de shì.
turns.text_en: Three. Three well-chosen rules can govern everything the fourteen were trying to cover.

tree.cultural_note: 文明标语 (civilization slogans) are ubiquitous in Chinese public spaces. The gap between the number of rules posted and the number actually followed is a widely recognized irony.

tree.setup: A stray cat has been living in the community garden. Some residents feed it. Others want it removed because it scares birds and damages plants. Both sides feel strongly.
tree.setup_zh: 一只流浪猫住在社区花园里。有的居民喂它，有的人想赶走它因为它吓鸟、破坏植物。双方都很坚持。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这只猫在这里比我们大多数人都久。它有权利待在这里。而且，猫的存在本身就是一种——
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè zhī māo zài zhèlǐ bǐ wǒmen dàduōshù rén dōu jiǔ. Tā yǒu quánlì dāi zài zhèlǐ. Érqiě, māo de cúnzài běnshēn jiù shì yì zhǒng —
turns.text_en: This cat has been here longer than most of us. It has a right to be here. Besides, the very existence of a cat is a kind of —

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 不要说'一种哲学'。上次关于树的讨论你就说了'一种哲学'。
turns.text_pinyin: Bú yào shuō 'yì zhǒng zhéxué'. Shàng cì guānyú shù de tǎolùn nǐ jiù shuō le 'yì zhǒng zhéxué'.
turns.text_en: Don't say 'a kind of philosophy.' You said that during the tree discussion last time.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: ...一种生态平衡。
turns.text_pinyin: ...Yì zhǒng shēngtài pínghéng.
turns.text_en: ...A kind of ecological balance.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 实际情况是：猫把王奶奶种的花全刨了。王奶奶八十二了，每天照顾那些花。这不是哲学的问题。
turns.text_pinyin: Shíjì qíngkuàng shì: māo bǎ Wáng nǎinai zhòng de huā quán páo le. Wáng nǎinai bāshí'èr le, měi tiān zhàogù nàxiē huā. Zhè bú shì zhéxué de wèntí.
turns.text_en: The reality is: the cat dug up all of Grandma Wang's flowers. She's eighty-two and tends those flowers every day. This isn't a philosophy problem.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我查了一下，宠物管理条例里没有涉及流浪猫。这在规定的灰色地带。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ chá le yíxià, chǒngwù guǎnlǐ tiáolì lǐ méi yǒu shèjí liúlàng māo. Zhè zài guīdìng de huīsè dìdài.
turns.text_en: I checked — the pet management regulations don't cover stray cats. This is in a regulatory gray area.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Both sides care for legitimate reasons. What do you suggest?

options.text_zh: 能不能联系动物救助组织，看看有没有人愿意领养？这样猫有了家，王奶奶的花也能保住。如果找不到人领养，可以在花园的角落给猫搭一个小窝，远离花坛。
options.text_pinyin: Néng bù néng liánxì dòngwù jiùzhù zǔzhī, kàn kan yǒu méi yǒu rén yuànyì lǐngyǎng? Zhèyàng māo yǒu le jiā, Wáng nǎinai de huā yě néng bǎo zhù. Rúguǒ zhǎo bú dào rén lǐngyǎng, kěyǐ zài huāyuán de jiǎoluò gěi māo dā yí gè xiǎo wō, yuǎnlí huātán.
options.text_en: Could we contact an animal rescue organization to see if someone would adopt it? That way the cat gets a home and Grandma Wang's flowers are safe. If no one adopts, we could build a small shelter in the garden corner, away from the flower beds.
options.feedback: Tiered solution that respects the cat AND the gardener. Bai's 'ecological balance' gets a practical form. Fang's concern for Grandma Wang is addressed. Qin starts looking up rescue organizations.

options.text_zh: 王奶奶八十二了，她的花更重要。应该想办法把猫送走。
options.text_pinyin: Wáng nǎinai bāshí'èr le, tā de huā gèng zhòngyào. Yīnggāi xiǎng bànfǎ bǎ māo sòng zǒu.
options.text_en: Grandma Wang is eighty-two — her flowers are more important. We should find a way to relocate the cat.
options.feedback: Understandable priority, but 'send it away' is vague and unsettling to the people who've been feeding it. Where does it go?

options.text_zh: 一只猫而已，不用这么认真吧。
options.text_pinyin: Yì zhī māo éryǐ, bú yòng zhème rènzhēn ba.
options.text_en: It's just a cat — no need to take it so seriously.
options.feedback: Fang's face. She just told you an eighty-two-year-old woman's daily purpose was destroyed. Bai is also offended, for philosophical reasons. You managed to unite both sides against you.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 领养也好。但是领养的人一定要理解：这只猫不是普通的猫。它有——
turns.text_pinyin: Lǐngyǎng yě hǎo. Dànshì lǐngyǎng de rén yīdìng yào lǐjiě: zhè zhī māo bú shì pǔtōng de māo. Tā yǒu —
turns.text_en: Adoption works. But the adopter must understand: this is not an ordinary cat. It has —

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 是一只猫。
turns.text_pinyin: Shì yì zhī māo.
turns.text_en: It's a cat.

tree.cultural_note: Community stray cat debates are common across Chinese cities. The tension between compassion (同情心) and practical damage is genuine and ongoing.

tree.setup: Manager Qin wants to start a community newsletter. Old Zhou volunteered to be editor-in-chief. Auntie Fang has read Zhou's previous writing. Teacher Luo has editorial standards. Xiao Mei was asked to contribute.
tree.setup_zh: 秦经理想办一份社区通讯。老周自告奋勇当主编。方阿姨看过老周以前写的东西。罗老师有编辑标准。小梅被邀请投稿。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 社区通讯可以增强居民的归属感。我已经拟定了发刊计划，每月一期，每期八页。
turns.text_pinyin: Shèqū tōngxùn kěyǐ zēngqiáng jūmín de guīshǔ gǎn. Wǒ yǐjīng nǐdìng le fākān jìhuà, měi yuè yì qī, měi qī bā yè.
turns.text_en: A community newsletter can strengthen residents' sense of belonging. I've drafted a publication plan — monthly, eight pages per issue.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我来当主编！我有很多想法！第一期我想写十二篇文章！
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ lái dāng zhǔbiān! Wǒ yǒu hěn duō xiǎngfǎ! Dì yī qī wǒ xiǎng xiě shí'èr piān wénzhāng!
turns.text_en: I'll be editor-in-chief! I have lots of ideas! For the first issue I want to write twelve articles!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你上次写的通知，把'三楼'写成了'三搂'。而且一篇通知用了八个感叹号。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ shàng cì xiě de tōngzhī, bǎ 'sān lóu' xiě chéng le 'sān lǒu'. Érqiě yì piān tōngzhī yòng le bā gè gǎntànhào.
turns.text_en: The last notice you wrote, you wrote '三楼' (third floor) as '三搂' (third hug). And one notice used eight exclamation marks.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 如果要办通讯，就必须有基本的编辑标准。格式、校对、内容审核，一样都不能少。否则不如不办。
turns.text_pinyin: Rúguǒ yào bàn tōngxùn, jiù bìxū yǒu jīběn de biānjí biāozhǔn. Géshì, jiàoduì, nèiróng shěnhé, yíyàng dōu bù néng shǎo. Fǒuzé bùrú bú bàn.
turns.text_en: If we're doing a newsletter, there must be basic editorial standards. Format, proofreading, content review — nothing can be skipped. Otherwise don't bother.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你们让我写什么？
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐmen ràng wǒ xiě shénme?
turns.text_en: What do you want me to write?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 写什么都行！你可以写一个专栏！比如'小梅看世界'！
turns.text_pinyin: Xiě shénme dōu xíng! Nǐ kěyǐ xiě yí gè zhuānlán! Bǐrú 'Xiǎo Méi kàn shìjiè'!
turns.text_en: Write anything! You could have a column! Like 'Xiao Mei's View of the World'!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: ...每期多少字？
turns.text_pinyin: ...Měi qī duōshǎo zì?
turns.text_en: ...How many characters per issue?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 随便！五百、一千、两千都行！
turns.text_pinyin: Suíbiàn! Wǔbǎi, yìqiān, liǎngqiān dōu xíng!
turns.text_en: Whatever! 500, 1000, 2000 — all fine!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 五十。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǔshí.
turns.text_en: Fifty.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: The newsletter needs structure. How do you help organize it?

options.text_zh: 我建议这样分工：秦经理负责发行和流程管理，罗老师负责编辑审核，老周负责采访和收集素材，小梅可以写一个简短的观察专栏。主编可以轮流做，或者让秦经理统筹。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ jiànyì zhèyàng fēngōng: Qín jīnglǐ fùzé fāxíng hé liúchéng guǎnlǐ, Luó lǎoshī fùzé biānjí shěnhé, Lǎo Zhōu fùzé cǎifǎng hé shōují sùcái, Xiǎo Méi kěyǐ xiě yí gè jiǎnduǎn de guānchá zhuānlán. Zhǔbiān kěyǐ lúnliú zuò, huòzhě ràng Qín jīnglǐ tǒngchóu.
options.text_en: I'd suggest dividing labor: Qin handles distribution and process, Luo handles editorial review, Zhou handles interviews and gathering material, Mei writes a short observation column. Editor-in-chief could rotate, or Qin could coordinate overall.
options.feedback: Everyone's strengths deployed. Zhou gets to be enthusiastic without controlling quality. Luo gets her standards. Mei's column will be the best-written fifty characters in the newsletter. Qin has process oversight.

options.text_zh: 让老周当主编吧，他最有热情。
options.text_pinyin: Ràng Lǎo Zhōu dāng zhǔbiān ba, tā zuì yǒu rèqíng.
options.text_en: Let Zhou be editor — he's the most enthusiastic.
options.feedback: Enthusiasm is not the same as editorial competence. Fang's single raised eyebrow communicates volumes about '三搂.' The first issue will be... energetic.

options.text_zh: 这个工作量太大了，不现实。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè ge gōngzuò liàng tài dà le, bú xiànshí.
options.text_en: This is too much work — not realistic.
options.feedback: Qin spent two weeks on the publication plan. Four people just volunteered (in various degrees of willingness). You're the only one who didn't contribute.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 分工方案我接受。我现在就起草编辑流程手册。
turns.text_pinyin: Fēngōng fāng'àn wǒ jiēshòu. Wǒ xiànzài jiù qǐcǎo biānjí liúchéng shǒucè.
turns.text_en: I accept the division of labor. I'll draft the editorial process manual now.

tree.cultural_note: Chinese community organizations often struggle with the gap between enthusiasm (热情) and execution capability (执行力). Effective delegation requires putting people where their strengths serve, not where they volunteer.

tree.setup: The property management company proposed a 15% maintenance fee increase. Manager Qin has the numbers. Teacher Luo is furious. Uncle Bai has a theory about inflation. Auntie Fang asks the question nobody wants to answer.
tree.setup_zh: 物业公司提出物业费涨百分之十五。秦经理有数据。罗老师很愤怒。老白有一套关于通胀的理论。方阿姨问了一个谁都不想回答的问题。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 物业公司提出从明年起物业费上调百分之十五。他们的理由是人工成本上涨和设备老化。我核实了一下，这两点基本属实。
turns.text_pinyin: Wùyè gōngsī tíchū cóng míngnián qǐ wùyè fèi shàng diào bǎi fēn zhī shíwǔ. Tāmen de lǐyóu shì réngōng chéngběn shàngzhǎng hé shèbèi lǎohuà. Wǒ héshí le yíxià, zhè liǎng diǎn jīběn shǔshí.
turns.text_en: The property company proposes a 15% maintenance fee increase starting next year. Their reasons are rising labor costs and aging equipment. I verified — both points are basically accurate.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 电梯坏了三次没人修！楼道灯从十月份就不亮！他们连基本服务都做不好，凭什么涨价？涨价可以，先把该做的做好！
turns.text_pinyin: Diàntī huài le sān cì méi rén xiū! Lóudào dēng cóng shí yuèfèn jiù bú liàng! Tāmen lián jīběn fúwù dōu zuò bù hǎo, píng shénme zhǎng jià? Zhǎng jià kěyǐ, xiān bǎ gāi zuò de zuò hǎo!
turns.text_en: The elevator broke three times with no repairs! The hallway lights haven't worked since October! They can't even provide basic service — on what basis do they raise prices? Fine, raise prices — but first do what you're supposed to do!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 涨价这个事情吧，表面上看是物业费的问题，其实本质上是一个更深层次的经济结构问题。通胀不是物业公司能控制的。从宏观角度看——
turns.text_pinyin: Zhǎng jià zhè ge shìqíng ba, biǎomiàn shàng kàn shì wùyè fèi de wèntí, qíshí běnzhì shàng shì yí gè gèng shēn céngcì de jīngjì jiégòu wèntí. Tōngzhàng bú shì wùyè gōngsī néng kòngzhì de. Cóng hóngguān jiǎodù kàn —
turns.text_en: This price increase, on the surface it's a maintenance fee issue, but fundamentally it's a deeper structural economic problem. Inflation isn't something the property company can control. From a macro perspective —

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 老白，你交得起百分之十五吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Lǎo Bái, nǐ jiāo de qǐ bǎi fēn zhī shíwǔ ma?
turns.text_en: Uncle Bai, can you afford the 15%?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这个...不是能不能的问题，是应不应该的问题。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè ge... bú shì néng bù néng de wèntí, shì yīng bù yīnggāi de wèntí.
turns.text_en: That's... not a question of ability, it's a question of principle.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 五楼的老太太靠退休金过日子。百分之十五对她是一个月的菜钱。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǔ lóu de lǎo tàitai kào tuìxiū jīn guò rìzi. Bǎi fēn zhī shíwǔ duì tā shì yí gè yuè de cài qián.
turns.text_en: The elderly woman on the fifth floor lives on her pension. Fifteen percent is a month's grocery money for her.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: The community needs a position for the negotiation with the property company. What do you propose?

options.text_zh: 我建议分两步走。第一，列出物业目前没有履行的服务项目，要求他们在涨价之前先达标。第二，如果服务达标了，可以接受涨价，但不是百分之十五——可以谈百分之八到十，而且对低收入住户设一个减免比例。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ jiànyì fēn liǎng bù zǒu. Dì yī, liè chū wùyè mùqián méi yǒu lǚxíng de fúwù xiàngmù, yāoqiú tāmen zài zhǎng jià zhīqián xiān dá biāo. Dì èr, rúguǒ fúwù dá biāo le, kěyǐ jiēshòu zhǎng jià, dàn bú shì bǎi fēn zhī shíwǔ — kěyǐ tán bǎi fēn zhī bā dào shí, érqiě duì dī shōurù zhùhù shè yí gè jiǎnmiǎn bǐlì.
options.text_en: I'd suggest a two-step approach. First, list the services the property company currently fails to provide — require them to meet standards before any increase. Second, if they meet standards, accept an increase but not 15% — negotiate 8-10%, with a reduced rate for low-income residents.
options.feedback: Structured, fair, and addresses Fang's concern about the fifth-floor grandmother. Luo's anger gets channeled into the accountability list. Qin has a framework to work with. Even Bai's macro point gets absorbed into the 'negotiate the percentage' piece.

options.text_zh: 不应该接受涨价。服务不到位就不应该涨。
options.text_pinyin: Bù yīnggāi jiēshòu zhǎng jià. Fúwù bú dào wèi jiù bù yīnggāi zhǎng.
options.text_en: We shouldn't accept any increase. If service isn't there, they shouldn't raise prices.
options.feedback: Emotionally satisfying and Luo agrees loudly. But Qin verified the cost pressures are real. Refusing any increase may lead to worse service or the company withdrawing. You need leverage, not a wall.

options.text_zh: 百分之十五不算多，工资都在涨，物业费涨一点也正常。
options.text_pinyin: Bǎi fēn zhī shíwǔ bú suàn duō, gōngzī dōu zài zhǎng, wùyè fèi zhǎng yì diǎn yě zhèngcháng.
options.text_en: 15% isn't that much — wages are going up too, a small fee increase is normal.
options.feedback: Fang's face. She just told you about a woman for whom this equals a month of food. 'Not that much' depends entirely on who's paying.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 两步走的方案我可以整理成正式文件。服务清单我明天就可以列出来。减免比例需要跟财务确认可行性。
turns.text_pinyin: Liǎng bù zǒu de fāng'àn wǒ kěyǐ zhěnglǐ chéng zhèngshì wénjiàn. Fúwù qīngdān wǒ míngtiān jiù kěyǐ liè chūlái. Jiǎnmiǎn bǐlì xūyào gēn cáiwù quèrèn kěxíng xìng.
turns.text_en: I can formalize the two-step proposal as an official document. The service checklist I can have by tomorrow. The reduced rate needs feasibility confirmation from finance.

tree.cultural_note: 物业费 (property management fees) are a major source of community tension in Chinese cities. Negotiations between residents and property companies often require balancing legitimate cost pressures against service quality and affordability.

tree.setup: The community is selecting a 'Good Neighbor of the Year.' Old Zhou nominated himself. Nobody else did. The committee (Fang, Luo, Qin, Mei, and you) must decide what to do.
tree.setup_zh: 社区在评选'年度好邻居'。老周提名了自己。没有别人提名他。评委会（方阿姨、罗老师、秦经理、小梅和你）需要决定怎么办。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 收到五份提名。三份提名的是方阿姨——分别来自楼上的张家、隔壁的李阿姨和三楼的小王。一份是罗老师提名的秦经理——就是我。一份是老周提名的老周。
turns.text_pinyin: Shōu dào wǔ fèn tímíng. Sān fèn tímíng de shì Fāng āyí — fēnbié láizì lóu shàng de Zhāng jiā, gébì de Lǐ āyí hé sān lóu de Xiǎo Wáng. Yí fèn shì Luó lǎoshī tímíng de Qín jīnglǐ — jiù shì wǒ. Yí fèn shì Lǎo Zhōu tímíng de Lǎo Zhōu.
turns.text_en: Five nominations received. Three for Auntie Fang — from the Zhang family upstairs, Auntie Li next door, and Xiao Wang on the third floor. One is Teacher Luo nominating Manager Qin — that's me. One is Old Zhou nominating Old Zhou.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 自己提名自己...有这个先例吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Zìjǐ tímíng zìjǐ... yǒu zhè ge xiānlì ma?
turns.text_en: Nominating yourself... is there precedent for this?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 规定上没有禁止自我提名。
turns.text_pinyin: Guīdìng shàng méi yǒu jìnzhǐ zìwǒ tímíng.
turns.text_en: The rules don't prohibit self-nomination.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 老周每天帮大家拿快递、浇花、打扫楼道。他确实做了很多事。只是没有人想到提名他。这件事本身值得想一想。
turns.text_pinyin: Lǎo Zhōu měi tiān bāng dàjiā ná kuàidì, jiāo huā, dǎsǎo lóudào. Tā quèshí zuò le hěn duō shì. Zhǐshì méi yǒu rén xiǎng dào tímíng tā. Zhè jiàn shì běnshēn zhíde xiǎng yi xiǎng.
turns.text_en: Zhou takes deliveries for everyone every day, waters plants, cleans the hallway. He genuinely does a lot. It's just that nobody thought to nominate him. That itself is worth thinking about.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 方阿姨有三票。
turns.text_pinyin: Fāng āyí yǒu sān piào.
turns.text_en: Auntie Fang has three votes.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: The committee needs a fair decision. What do you propose?

options.text_zh: 方阿姨三票最多，按规则她应该得奖。但方阿姨刚才说的话让我想到一件事——也许我们可以增设一个'社区服务奖'，专门表彰每天默默做事的人，第一个给老周。这样既尊重了投票结果，也不忽视老周的付出。
options.text_pinyin: Fāng āyí sān piào zuì duō, àn guīzé tā yīnggāi dé jiǎng. Dàn Fāng āyí gāngcái shuō de huà ràng wǒ xiǎng dào yí jiàn shì — yěxǔ wǒmen kěyǐ zēng shè yí gè 'shèqū fúwù jiǎng', zhuānmén biǎozhāng měi tiān mòmò zuò shì de rén, dì yī gè gěi Lǎo Zhōu. Zhèyàng jì zūnzhòng le tóupiào jiéguǒ, yě bù hūshì Lǎo Zhōu de fùchū.
options.text_en: Fang has three votes and should win by the rules. But what she just said made me think — maybe we could create a 'Community Service Award' specifically for people who quietly do things every day, and give the first one to Zhou. That respects the vote AND acknowledges Zhou's contributions.
options.feedback: Elegant institutional solution. Fang gets the award she earned. Zhou gets recognized for what he actually does. The new award becomes a permanent category. Qin immediately drafts the charter. Nobody loses face.

options.text_zh: 方阿姨票最多，就给方阿姨吧。
options.text_pinyin: Fāng āyí piào zuì duō, jiù gěi Fāng āyí ba.
options.text_en: Fang has the most votes — just give it to her.
options.feedback: Procedurally correct. But Fang herself raised Zhou's invisible labor. Ignoring her own observation to simply accept her victory is oddly un-Fang.

options.text_zh: 老周做了那么多事，应该把奖给他。
options.text_pinyin: Lǎo Zhōu zuò le nàme duō shì, yīnggāi bǎ jiǎng gěi tā.
options.text_en: Zhou does so much — we should give it to him.
options.feedback: Overriding three genuine community votes to give the award to a self-nominee undermines the whole process. Luo: '那以后大家都自己提名自己就行了？' (So from now on everyone just nominates themselves?)

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 社区服务奖。好。老周应该知道有人看到了他做的事。
turns.text_pinyin: Shèqū fúwù jiǎng. Hǎo. Lǎo Zhōu yīnggāi zhīdào yǒu rén kàn dào le tā zuò de shì.
turns.text_en: Community Service Award. Good. Zhou should know someone noticed what he does.

tree.cultural_note: Recognition for 默默付出 (quiet contribution) is deeply valued in Chinese culture but structurally underrecognized. Creating formal channels for acknowledging invisible labor is considered wise community governance.

tree.setup: The shared kitchen has descended into chaos. Manager Qin drafted a 22-page management policy. Teacher Luo thinks it's too lenient. Uncle Bai thinks the concept of kitchen rules is an affront to cooking culture. Auntie Fang wants to know who left fish in the sink for three days.
tree.setup_zh: 公共厨房已经乱套了。秦经理草拟了一份二十二页的管理办法。罗老师觉得太宽松了。老白觉得厨房规则的概念本身就是对烹饪文化的冒犯。方阿姨想知道是谁把鱼在水槽里放了三天。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我拟定了《社区公共厨房使用与管理暂行办法》，一共二十二页，涵盖使用时间、卫生标准、设备维护、投诉流程四个方面。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ nǐdìng le 'Shèqū gōnggòng chúfáng shǐyòng yǔ guǎnlǐ zhànxíng bànfǎ', yígòng èrshí'èr yè, hángài shǐyòng shíjiān, wèishēng biāozhǔn, shèbèi wéihù, tóusù liúchéng sì gè fāngmiàn.
turns.text_en: I've drafted the 'Provisional Measures for Community Shared Kitchen Use and Management' — twenty-two pages covering usage hours, sanitation standards, equipment maintenance, and complaint procedures.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 二十二页。用来管一间厨房。
turns.text_pinyin: Èrshí'èr yè. Yòng lái guǎn yì jiān chúfáng.
turns.text_en: Twenty-two pages. To manage one kitchen.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我看了。第七条太宽松了。'使用后应及时清理'——'应'是什么意思？是必须还是建议？如果是建议，等于没说。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ kàn le. Dì qī tiáo tài kuānsōng le. 'Shǐyòng hòu yīng jíshí qīnglǐ' — 'yīng' shì shénme yìsi? Shì bìxū háishì jiànyì? Rúguǒ shì jiànyì, děngyú méi shuō.
turns.text_en: I've read it. Article 7 is too lenient. 'Users should clean up promptly' — what does 'should' mean? Required or suggested? If suggested, it says nothing.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 做饭是一种创造性活动。你不能用管理条例来规范创造。历史上最好的菜，都不是在规定里诞生的。
turns.text_pinyin: Zuò fàn shì yì zhǒng chuàngzàoxìng huódòng. Nǐ bù néng yòng guǎnlǐ tiáolì lái guīfàn chuàngzào. Lìshǐ shàng zuì hǎo de cài, dōu bú shì zài guīdìng lǐ dànshēng de.
turns.text_en: Cooking is a creative activity. You can't regulate creativity with management policies. The best dishes in history were never born inside regulations.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 老白，水槽里那条鱼是你的吗？
turns.text_pinyin: Lǎo Bái, shuǐcáo lǐ nà tiáo yú shì nǐ de ma?
turns.text_en: Uncle Bai, is that fish in the sink yours?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: ...那条鱼是在腌制过程中。
turns.text_pinyin: ...Nà tiáo yú shì zài yānzhì guòchéng zhōng.
turns.text_en: ...That fish is in the process of being marinated.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 三天了。
turns.text_pinyin: Sān tiān le.
turns.text_en: For three days.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: The kitchen policy needs to work in reality. What do you suggest?

options.text_zh: 二十二页太长了，没有人会看。建议简化成五条核心规则贴在墙上：用完清理、食物当天带走、公共工具用完归位、有问题找秦经理、违反两次取消使用权。规则简单才有人遵守。细节可以放在附件里需要的时候查。
options.text_pinyin: Èrshí'èr yè tài cháng le, méi yǒu rén huì kàn. Jiànyì jiǎnhuà chéng wǔ tiáo héxīn guīzé tiē zài qiáng shàng: yòng wán qīnglǐ, shíwù dāngtiān dài zǒu, gōnggòng gōngjù yòng wán guī wèi, yǒu wèntí zhǎo Qín jīnglǐ, wéifǎn liǎng cì qǔxiāo shǐyòng quán. Guīzé jiǎndān cái yǒu rén zūnshǒu. Xìjié kěyǐ fàng zài fùjiàn lǐ xūyào de shíhou chá.
options.text_en: Twenty-two pages is too long — nobody will read it. Simplify to five core rules on the wall: clean after use, take food home same day, return shared tools, issues go to Qin, two violations loses kitchen access. Simple rules get followed. Details can live in an appendix for reference.
options.feedback: Luo gets her 'must' (not 'should'). Qin keeps his comprehensive policy as appendix. Bai's fish is covered by rule #2. Fang is satisfied — five rules she can point to. The kitchen may actually get cleaner.

options.text_zh: 秦经理的办法很全面，就按这个来吧。
options.text_pinyin: Qín jīnglǐ de bànfǎ hěn quánmiàn, jiù àn zhè ge lái ba.
options.text_en: Qin's policy is comprehensive — let's go with it.
options.feedback: Respectful of Qin's work, but Fang is right — twenty-two pages for a kitchen is the bureaucratic equivalent of Bai's three-day fish. Nobody will read it.

options.text_zh: 也许我们不需要规定，大家自觉就好了。
options.text_pinyin: Yěxǔ wǒmen bù xūyào guīdìng, dàjiā zìjué jiù hǎo le.
options.text_en: Maybe we don't need rules — everyone should just be self-disciplined.
options.feedback: There is a three-day-old fish in the sink. Self-discipline has been tested and found wanting.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 五条核心规则加附件...结构上可行。我重新排版。老白，鱼的事，按新规第二条处理。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǔ tiáo héxīn guīzé jiā fùjiàn... jiégòu shàng kěxíng. Wǒ chóngxīn páibǎn. Lǎo Bái, yú de shì, àn xīn guī dì èr tiáo chǔlǐ.
turns.text_en: Five core rules plus appendix... structurally workable. I'll reformat. Uncle Bai, the fish situation falls under new rule number two.

tree.cultural_note: The tension between 全面管理 (comprehensive management) and 可操作性 (operability) is a constant theme in Chinese institutional life. The best policies are short enough to be followed.

tree.setup: A pipe burst in the basement. The community needs emergency coordination. All six characters respond in exactly the way you'd expect.
tree.setup_zh: 地下室水管爆裂了。社区需要紧急协调。六个人的反应完全符合你对他们的预期。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 水管爆了！地下室都是水！我说了多少次要定期检修！多少次！这是可以预防的！完全可以预防的！
turns.text_pinyin: Shuǐguǎn bào le! Dìxiàshì dōu shì shuǐ! Wǒ shuō le duōshǎo cì yào dìngqī jiǎnxiū! Duōshǎo cì! Zhè shì kěyǐ yùfáng de! Wánquán kěyǐ yùfáng de!
turns.text_en: A pipe burst! The basement is flooded! How many times did I say we need regular maintenance? How many times! This was preventable! Completely preventable!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我已经联系了维修队，预计四十分钟到。同时我需要确认：一，受影响范围；二，是否有电气安全隐患；三，居民疏散是否必要。按应急预案第三章执行。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ yǐjīng liánxì le wéixiū duì, yùjì sìshí fēnzhōng dào. Tóngshí wǒ xūyào quèrèn: yī, shòu yǐngxiǎng fànwéi; èr, shìfǒu yǒu diànqì ānquán yǐnhuàn; sān, jūmín shūsàn shìfǒu bìyào. Àn yìngjí yù'àn dì sān zhāng zhíxíng.
turns.text_en: I've contacted the repair team — ETA forty minutes. Meanwhile I need to confirm: one, extent of affected area; two, any electrical safety hazard; three, whether resident evacuation is necessary. Executing per emergency plan chapter three.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我去修！我以前修过水管！给我一把扳手！
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ qù xiū! Wǒ yǐqián xiū guò shuǐguǎn! Gěi wǒ yì bǎ bānshǒu!
turns.text_en: I'll fix it! I've fixed pipes before! Give me a wrench!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你以前修的那个水管，后来又漏了。而且比原来漏得更厉害。
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ yǐqián xiū de nà ge shuǐguǎn, hòulái yòu lòu le. Érqiě bǐ yuánlái lòu de gèng lìhai.
turns.text_en: The pipe you fixed last time leaked again afterwards. Worse than before.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 水的问题本质上是时间的问题。管道老化是不可逆的。我们今天修好了，明天还会有新的地方出问题。与其修管道，不如反思我们跟基础设施的关系。
turns.text_pinyin: Shuǐ de wèntí běnzhì shàng shì shíjiān de wèntí. Guǎndào lǎohuà shì bùkě nì de. Wǒmen jīntiān xiū hǎo le, míngtiān hái huì yǒu xīn de dìfang chū wèntí. Yǔqí xiū guǎndào, bùrú fǎnsī wǒmen gēn jīchǔ shèshī de guānxì.
turns.text_en: The water issue is fundamentally a time issue. Pipe aging is irreversible. We fix it today, tomorrow something else breaks. Rather than fixing pipes, we should reflect on our relationship with infrastructure.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 地下室有水。维修队四十分钟到。现在需要有人去关总阀门。
turns.text_pinyin: Dìxiàshì yǒu shuǐ. Wéixiū duì sìshí fēnzhōng dào. Xiànzài xūyào yǒu rén qù guān zǒng fámén.
turns.text_en: There's water in the basement. Repair team arrives in forty minutes. Right now someone needs to go shut the main valve.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Xiao Mei has identified the immediate priority. How do you organize the response?

options.text_zh: 小梅说得对，先关阀门。老周，你知道总阀门在哪里吗？你去关。秦经理继续协调维修队和安全评估。罗老师，你能通知受影响楼层的住户吗？大家先别讨论原因，等水止住了再说。
options.text_pinyin: Xiǎo Méi shuō de duì, xiān guān fámén. Lǎo Zhōu, nǐ zhīdào zǒng fámén zài nǎlǐ ma? Nǐ qù guān. Qín jīnglǐ jìxù xiétiáo wéixiū duì hé ānquán pínggū. Luó lǎoshī, nǐ néng tōngzhī shòu yǐngxiǎng lóucéng de zhùhù ma? Dàjiā xiān bié tǎolùn yuányīn, děng shuǐ zhǐ zhù le zài shuō.
options.text_en: Mei's right — valve first. Zhou, do you know where the main valve is? Go shut it off. Qin, continue coordinating the repair team and safety assessment. Luo, can you notify the affected floors' residents? Everyone, let's not discuss causes until the water is stopped.
options.feedback: Clear command structure. Zhou's energy channeled to a task he can actually do (he does know where the valve is). Luo's urgency redirected to useful notification. Qin's process continues. Bai's philosophy gently shelved. Mei's observation became the plan.

options.text_zh: 等维修队来吧，他们专业。
options.text_pinyin: Děng wéixiū duì lái ba, tāmen zhuānyè.
options.text_en: Let's wait for the repair team — they're professionals.
options.feedback: Forty minutes of water flowing. Mei just said the valve needs to be closed NOW. Waiting is not a strategy when the basement is flooding.

options.text_zh: 我去关阀门，你们在这里等。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ qù guān fámén, nǐmen zài zhèlǐ děng.
options.text_en: I'll go close the valve — everyone wait here.
options.feedback: Action is good, but going alone while everyone else waits wastes available people. Luo could be notifying residents. Qin could be managing the repair team. Solo heroism is less effective than coordination.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 总阀门我知道！在地下室东边！我去！
turns.text_pinyin: Zǒng fámén wǒ zhīdào! Zài dìxiàshì dōngbiān! Wǒ qù!
turns.text_en: I know where the main valve is! East side of the basement! I'm going!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 他这次确实知道。
turns.text_pinyin: Tā zhè cì quèshí zhīdào.
turns.text_en: This time he actually does know.

tree.cultural_note: In Chinese communities, crisis response reveals true organizational dynamics. The difference between 讨论问题 (discussing the problem) and 解决问题 (solving the problem) is a frequent frustration.

tree.setup: A foreign delegation is visiting the community next week. The committee must plan a cultural exchange event. Everyone has a different idea of what 'Chinese culture' means to present. The stakes are civic pride, but the conversation reveals deeper questions about identity and representation.
tree.setup_zh: 下周有一个外国代表团要来参观社区。委员会要策划一个文化交流活动。每个人对于展示什么样的'中国文化'有不同的想法。涉及的是市民自豪感，但对话揭示了更深层次的身份认同和代表性问题。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我们表演太极拳！外国人最喜欢太极拳！我可以领队！
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒmen biǎoyǎn tàijí quán! Wàiguó rén zuì xǐhuān tàijí quán! Wǒ kěyǐ lǐng duì!
turns.text_en: We'll perform tai chi! Foreigners love tai chi! I can lead the group!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 你练了多久太极？
turns.text_pinyin: Nǐ liàn le duō jiǔ tàijí?
turns.text_en: How long have you practiced tai chi?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 看过很多视频！
turns.text_pinyin: Kàn guò hěn duō shìpín!
turns.text_en: I've watched lots of videos!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 文化交流不能马马虎虎！这代表的是我们社区的形象！应该展示真正有水平的东西——书法、茶艺、至少是能拿得出手的。
turns.text_pinyin: Wénhuà jiāoliú bù néng mǎmǎhūhū! Zhè dàibiǎo de shì wǒmen shèqū de xíngxiàng! Yīnggāi zhǎnshì zhēnzhèng yǒu shuǐpíng de dōngxi — shūfǎ, cháyì, zhìshǎo shì néng ná de chū shǒu de.
turns.text_en: Cultural exchange can't be done carelessly! This represents our community's image! We should showcase things with real quality — calligraphy, tea ceremony, at least things we can be proud of.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 为什么每次'展示中国文化'就是书法和茶艺？他们自己国家的唐人街也有书法和茶艺。我们社区的文化不是表演出来的——是早上六点那个面摊、是楼下那棵老树下每天下棋的四个人、是老周每天帮大家拿快递。那才是真的。
turns.text_pinyin: Wèishéme měi cì 'zhǎnshì Zhōngguó wénhuà' jiù shì shūfǎ hé cháyì? Tāmen zìjǐ guójiā de tángrénjiē yě yǒu shūfǎ hé cháyì. Wǒmen shèqū de wénhuà bú shì biǎoyǎn chūlái de — shì zǎoshang liù diǎn nà ge miàntān, shì lóu xià nà kē lǎo shù xià měi tiān xià qí de sì gè rén, shì Lǎo Zhōu měi tiān bāng dàjiā ná kuàidì. Nà cái shì zhēn de.
turns.text_en: Why is 'showcasing Chinese culture' always calligraphy and tea ceremony? Their own Chinatowns have calligraphy and tea ceremony. Our community's culture isn't performed — it's the noodle stand at 6am, the four people playing chess under the old tree every day, Zhou taking everyone's deliveries. That's what's real.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 方阿姨说到了本质。文化不是你'展示'的东西，是你'活着'的东西。但问题是：你怎么把'活着'的东西在一个下午展示给看不懂的人？
turns.text_pinyin: Fāng āyí shuō dào le běnzhì. Wénhuà bú shì nǐ 'zhǎnshì' de dōngxi, shì nǐ 'huó zhe' de dōngxi. Dàn wèntí shì: nǐ zěnme bǎ 'huó zhe' de dōngxi zài yí gè xiàwǔ zhǎnshì gěi kàn bù dǒng de rén?
turns.text_en: Auntie Fang hit the essence. Culture isn't something you 'showcase' — it's something you 'live.' But the question is: how do you present 'living' culture in one afternoon to people who don't share the context?

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 让他们来吃面。不用解释。
turns.text_pinyin: Ràng tāmen lái chī miàn. Bú yòng jiěshì.
turns.text_en: Have them come eat noodles. No explanation needed.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: The group needs a plan that's authentic, dignified, and doable. What do you propose?

options.text_zh: 小梅的想法其实最好。不要'表演文化'，让他们'参与生活'。安排他们早上去面摊吃面，看楼下下棋的人，跟大家一起包饺子。然后罗老师可以准备一个简短的介绍，讲社区的历史和变化。不需要表演，但需要组织好节奏和翻译。这样他们看到的是真实的社区，不是一台文化展览。
options.text_pinyin: Xiǎo Méi de xiǎngfǎ qíshí zuì hǎo. Bú yào 'biǎoyǎn wénhuà', ràng tāmen 'cānyù shēnghuó'. Ānpái tāmen zǎoshang qù miàntān chī miàn, kàn lóu xià xià qí de rén, gēn dàjiā yìqǐ bāo jiǎozi. Ránhòu Luó lǎoshī kěyǐ zhǔnbèi yí gè jiǎnduǎn de jièshào, jiǎng shèqū de lìshǐ hé biànhuà. Bù xūyào biǎoyǎn, dàn xūyào zǔzhī hǎo jiézòu hé fānyì. Zhèyàng tāmen kàn dào de shì zhēnshí de shèqū, bú shì yì tái wénhuà zhǎnlǎn.
options.text_en: Mei's idea is actually the best. Don't 'perform culture' — let them 'participate in life.' Have them eat noodles at the morning stand, watch the chess players downstairs, make dumplings with everyone. Then Luo can prepare a brief introduction about the community's history and changes. No performance needed, but rhythm and translation need to be organized well. That way they see a real community, not a cultural exhibition.
options.feedback: You synthesized everyone's inputs: Mei's instinct for authenticity, Fang's rejection of performance culture, Luo's standards, and even Zhou's desire to participate (he'll be making dumplings, enthusiastically). Qin starts building the logistics timeline.

options.text_zh: 还是做书法和茶艺吧，比较安全。
options.text_pinyin: Háishi zuò shūfǎ hé cháyì ba, bǐjiào ānquán.
options.text_en: Let's just do calligraphy and tea ceremony — it's safer.
options.feedback: Fang just argued passionately against exactly this. Safe isn't the same as good. The delegation has seen calligraphy demonstrations before. They haven't seen your specific community.

options.text_zh: 这种活动形式大于内容，随便搞一下就行了。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè zhǒng huódòng xíngshì dà yú nèiróng, suíbiàn gǎo yíxià jiù xíng le.
options.text_en: These events are more form than substance — just throw something together.
options.feedback: Luo's face darkens. Fang's observation about real vs performed culture was a genuine insight about identity and representation. Dismissing it as 'form over substance' misses the entire conversation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我来做时间表和翻译安排。需要跟面摊老板提前沟通。还有保险的问题——外国人在社区活动需要确认责任条款。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ lái zuò shíjiān biǎo hé fānyì ānpái. Xūyào gēn miàntān lǎobǎn tíqián gōutōng. Háiyǒu bǎoxiǎn de wèntí — wàiguó rén zài shèqū huódòng xūyào quèrèn zérèn tiáokuǎn.
turns.text_en: I'll handle the timetable and translation arrangements. Need to coordinate with the noodle stand owner in advance. Also the insurance question — foreign visitors doing community activities need liability terms confirmed.

tree.cultural_note: The tension between 'performance culture' (表演文化) and 'lived culture' (生活文化) is a genuine debate in Chinese community representation. Authentic experiences often leave stronger impressions than polished exhibitions.

tree.setup: The community is burying a time capsule to be opened in 25 years. Each person can contribute one item. The conversation about what to include reveals what each person values — and what they think the future should know.
tree.setup_zh: 社区要埋一个时间胶囊，二十五年后打开。每个人可以放一件东西。关于放什么的讨论，暴露了每个人看重什么——以及他们觉得未来应该知道什么。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我放一张我们的集体照！让二十五年后的人看到我们！我现在就拍！
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ fàng yì zhāng wǒmen de jítǐ zhào! Ràng èrshíwǔ nián hòu de rén kàn dào wǒmen! Wǒ xiànzài jiù pāi!
turns.text_en: I'll put in a group photo! Let the people in 25 years see us! I'll take it right now!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 应该放一些有教育意义的东西。比如今年的课程表，让未来的人知道我们的教育理念是什么样的。每一代人都应该向下一代留下精神遗产。
turns.text_pinyin: Yīnggāi fàng yìxiē yǒu jiàoyù yìyì de dōngxi. Bǐrú jīnnián de kèchéng biǎo, ràng wèilái de rén zhīdào wǒmen de jiàoyù lǐniàn shì shénme yàng de. Měi yí dài rén dōu yīnggāi xiàng xià yí dài liú xia jīngshén yíchǎn.
turns.text_en: We should include something educational. Like this year's curriculum, so future people know what our educational philosophy looked like. Every generation should leave a spiritual legacy for the next.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我放面摊老板的菜单。手写的那种。上面有油渍。那个比课程表真实。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ fàng miàntān lǎobǎn de càidān. Shǒuxiě de nà zhǒng. Shàngmiàn yǒu yóuzì. Nà ge bǐ kèchéng biǎo zhēnshí.
turns.text_en: I'll put in the noodle stand owner's menu. The handwritten one. With grease stains on it. That's more real than a curriculum.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我写一封信给二十五年后的人。告诉他们我们当时在想什么。不是大事，是日常的思考。比如我们怎么看这棵树，怎么看这条巷子，怎么跟邻居相处。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ xiě yì fēng xìn gěi èrshíwǔ nián hòu de rén. Gàosu tāmen wǒmen dāngshí zài xiǎng shénme. Bú shì dà shì, shì rìcháng de sīkǎo. Bǐrú wǒmen zěnme kàn zhè kē shù, zěnme kàn zhè tiáo xiàngzi, zěnme gēn línjū xiāngchǔ.
turns.text_en: I'll write a letter to the people 25 years from now. Tell them what we were thinking about. Not big things — daily thoughts. Like how we see this tree, this alley, how we get along with neighbors.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 一个U盘。装今天的日期、天气、物价、和'公共厨房管理暂行办法'。
turns.text_pinyin: Yí gè U pán. Zhuāng jīntiān de rìqī, tiānqì, wùjià, hé 'gōnggòng chúfáng guǎnlǐ zhànxíng bànfǎ'.
turns.text_en: A USB drive. With today's date, weather, prices, and the 'Provisional Kitchen Management Policy.'

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: U盘二十五年后不一定能读。需要考虑兼容性。另外，时间胶囊的防潮处理需要确认——我查一下有没有相关的行业标准。
turns.text_pinyin: U pán èrshíwǔ nián hòu bù yīdìng néng dú. Xūyào kǎolǜ jiānróng xìng. Lìngwài, shíjiān jiāonáng de fángcháo chǔlǐ xūyào quèrèn — wǒ chá yíxià yǒu méi yǒu xiāngguān de hángyè biāozhǔn.
turns.text_en: A USB drive might not be readable in 25 years. Need to consider compatibility. Also, the time capsule's moisture-proofing needs confirmation — let me check if there are relevant industry standards.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Everyone's contribution reflects who they are. What do you put in the time capsule?

options.text_zh: 我放一张今天的报纸和一张手写的清单：我们社区现在有什么——面摊、老树、老周的快递服务、罗老师的规则、秦经理的流程、方阿姨的沉默、小梅的'嗯'、老白的理论。让二十五年后的人看看，这些东西还在不在。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ fàng yì zhāng jīntiān de bàozhǐ hé yì zhāng shǒuxiě de qīngdān: wǒmen shèqū xiànzài yǒu shénme — miàntān, lǎo shù, Lǎo Zhōu de kuàidì fúwù, Luó lǎoshī de guīzé, Qín jīnglǐ de liúchéng, Fāng āyí de chénmò, Xiǎo Méi de 'ǹg', Lǎo Bái de lǐlùn. Ràng èrshíwǔ nián hòu de rén kàn kan, zhèxiē dōngxi hái zài bú zài.
options.text_en: I'll put in today's newspaper and a handwritten list: what our community has right now — the noodle stand, the old tree, Zhou's delivery service, Luo's rules, Qin's procedures, Fang's silences, Mei's 'mm,' Bai's theories. Let the people in 25 years see whether these things are still there.
options.feedback: You captured the community not as an idea but as specific people and specific things. Everyone recognizes themselves in the list. There's a moment of quiet. Even Mei looks at you for slightly longer than usual.

options.text_zh: 我同意老白的想法，写一封信。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ tóngyì Lǎo Bái de xiǎngfǎ, xiě yì fēng xìn.
options.text_en: I agree with Uncle Bai — write a letter.
options.feedback: A good idea — Bai's suggestion was actually one of his better ones. But you contributed his idea rather than your own. The time capsule should have your specific perspective too.

options.text_zh: 随便放什么都行，关键是做好防潮。
options.text_pinyin: Suíbiàn fàng shénme dōu xíng, guānjiàn shì zuò hǎo fángcháo.
options.text_en: Doesn't matter what we put in — the key is moisture-proofing.
options.feedback: You just Qin'd the conversation. Moisture-proofing is important, but everyone else is talking about what matters to them and what they want to leave behind. Your contribution is... logistics.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好。那就这些。够了。不用再多了。
turns.text_pinyin: Hǎo. Nà jiù zhèxiē. Gòu le. Bú yòng zài duō le.
turns.text_en: Good. That's enough. No need for more.

tree.cultural_note: Time capsules (时间胶囊) in Chinese communities often become unexpected spaces for authentic self-expression — what people choose to preserve reveals what they value.

tree.setup: The community center's blank wall is being painted with a mural. A young artist has been hired, but the committee must approve the design. The conversation reveals six different ideas about what a community's public art should say — and who it should say it to.
tree.setup_zh: 社区中心的一面空墙要画壁画。请了一位年轻艺术家，但设计需要委员会批准。讨论中暴露了六种不同的想法：社区的公共艺术应该表达什么——以及给谁看。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 艺术家提交了三个方案。方案一是抽象几何图案，现代风格。方案二是社区历史场景，写实风格。方案三是一棵大树，周围有各种小动物和人物。我个人没有偏好——我只负责确保流程合规和预算可控。
turns.text_pinyin: Yìshùjiā tíjiāo le sān gè fāng'àn. Fāng'àn yī shì chōuxiàng jǐhé tú'àn, xiàndài fēnggé. Fāng'àn èr shì shèqū lìshǐ chǎngjǐng, xiěshí fēnggé. Fāng'àn sān shì yì kē dà shù, zhōuwéi yǒu gè zhǒng xiǎo dòngwù hé rénwù. Wǒ gèrén méi yǒu piānhào — wǒ zhǐ fùzé quèbǎo liúchéng hé guī hé yùsuàn kě kòng.
turns.text_en: The artist submitted three proposals. Option one: abstract geometric patterns, modern style. Option two: community history scenes, realistic style. Option three: a large tree with various small animals and people around it. I personally have no preference — I'm only responsible for process compliance and budget control.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 第三个！大树！好看！而且每个人都能看懂！小朋友也喜欢！
turns.text_pinyin: Dì sān gè! Dà shù! Hǎo kàn! Érqiě měi gè rén dōu néng kàn dǒng! Xiǎopéngyǒu yě xǐhuān!
turns.text_en: Option three! The tree! Beautiful! And everyone can understand it! Kids will love it too!

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 第二个。壁画应该有内容、有深度、有教育意义。历史场景能让下一代知道这个社区是怎么走过来的。公共艺术不是装饰，是精神建设。
turns.text_pinyin: Dì èr gè. Bìhuà yīnggāi yǒu nèiróng, yǒu shēndù, yǒu jiàoyù yìyì. Lìshǐ chǎngjǐng néng ràng xià yí dài zhīdào zhè ge shèqū shì zěnme zǒu guòlái de. Gōnggòng yìshù bú shì zhuāngshì, shì jīngshén jiànshè.
turns.text_en: Option two. A mural should have content, depth, and educational meaning. Historical scenes let the next generation know how this community got here. Public art isn't decoration — it's spiritual construction.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: '精神建设'。每次一说'精神建设'，出来的东西就像宣传栏。你想让这面墙变成宣传栏吗？好的壁画不需要'教育'任何人。它在那里，你经过的时候看一眼，觉得这个地方值得住——就够了。
turns.text_pinyin: 'Jīngshén jiànshè'. Měi cì yì shuō 'jīngshén jiànshè', chūlái de dōngxi jiù xiàng xuānchuán lán. Nǐ xiǎng ràng zhè miàn qiáng biàn chéng xuānchuán lán ma? Hǎo de bìhuà bù xūyào 'jiàoyù' rèn hé rén. Tā zài nàlǐ, nǐ jīngguò de shíhou kàn yì yǎn, juéde zhè ge dìfang zhíde zhù — jiù gòu le.
turns.text_en: 'Spiritual construction.' Every time someone says 'spiritual construction,' the result looks like a propaganda board. Do you want this wall to be a propaganda board? A good mural doesn't need to 'educate' anyone. It's there, you glance at it as you walk by, you feel this place is worth living in — that's enough.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我觉得第一个方案有它的道理。抽象的东西不会过时。今天画的历史场景，十年后看起来就是旧的。但一个几何图案，二十年后看起来跟今天一样。艺术的生命力不在于你能不能看懂，而在于它能不能让你停下来。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde dì yī gè fāng'àn yǒu tā de dàolǐ. Chōuxiàng de dōngxi bú huì guòshí. Jīntiān huà de lìshǐ chǎngjǐng, shí nián hòu kàn qǐlái jiù shì jiù de. Dàn yí gè jǐhé tú'àn, èrshí nián hòu kàn qǐlái gēn jīntiān yíyàng. Yìshù de shēngmìng lì bú zàiyú nǐ néng bù néng kàn dǒng, ér zàiyú tā néng bù néng ràng nǐ tíng xiàlái.
turns.text_en: I think option one has merit. Abstract things don't go out of date. Historical scenes painted today will look old in ten years. But a geometric pattern looks the same in twenty years as it does now. Art's vitality isn't in whether you understand it, but whether it makes you stop.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 让艺术家决定。
turns.text_pinyin: Ràng yìshùjiā juédìng.
turns.text_en: Let the artist decide.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我们是委员会！委员会的职责就是做决策！不能把决策权交出去！
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒmen shì wěiyuánhuì! Wěiyuánhuì de zhízé jiù shì zuò juécè! Bù néng bǎ juécè quán jiāo chūqù!
turns.text_en: We're a committee! The committee's duty is to make decisions! We can't hand decision-making power away!

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Six different perspectives on what art should do. What position do you take?

options.text_zh: 小梅和方阿姨说到了关键。我们不是在选装饰，是在选一面墙要表达什么。但我们也不是艺术家——我们知道自己要什么感觉，但怎么实现是她的专业。建议这样：我们把今天讨论的内容整理成一份简报给艺术家——我们想让人走过的时候觉得这里值得住，我们不想要宣传栏，我们希望十年后看起来不过时。然后让她根据这些方向重新设计一版。审美方向由我们定，专业表达由她来。
options.text_pinyin: Xiǎo Méi hé Fāng āyí shuō dào le guānjiàn. Wǒmen bú shì zài xuǎn zhuāngshì, shì zài xuǎn yì miàn qiáng yào biǎodá shénme. Dàn wǒmen yě bú shì yìshùjiā — wǒmen zhīdào zìjǐ yào shénme gǎnjué, dàn zěnme shíxiàn shì tā de zhuānyè. Jiànyì zhèyàng: wǒmen bǎ jīntiān tǎolùn de nèiróng zhěnglǐ chéng yí fèn jiǎnbào gěi yìshùjiā — wǒmen xiǎng ràng rén zǒu guò de shíhou juéde zhèlǐ zhíde zhù, wǒmen bù xiǎng yào xuānchuán lán, wǒmen xīwàng shí nián hòu kàn qǐlái bú guòshí. Ránhòu ràng tā gēnjù zhèxiē fāngxiàng chóngxīn shèjì yì bǎn. Shěnměi fāngxiàng yóu wǒmen dìng, zhuānyè biǎodá yóu tā lái.
options.text_en: Mei and Fang identified the key point. We're not choosing decoration — we're choosing what a wall should say. But we're also not artists — we know how we want it to feel, but execution is her expertise. I suggest: we compile today's discussion into a brief for the artist — we want people walking past to feel this place is worth living in, we don't want a propaganda board, we want it to look timeless in ten years. Then let her redesign based on those directions. Aesthetic direction from us, professional execution from her.
options.feedback: You found the structural answer: separate what the committee can meaningfully decide (values, feeling, constraints) from what requires professional judgment (visual execution). Luo keeps her committee authority. Mei's instinct about trusting the artist is honored. Fang's anti-propaganda criterion becomes a design constraint. Everyone's input shapes the brief without anyone designing the mural.

options.text_zh: 投票决定吧，少数服从多数。
options.text_pinyin: Tóupiào juédìng ba, shǎoshù fúcóng duōshù.
options.text_en: Let's vote — majority rules.
options.feedback: Democratic but reductive. The conversation generated real insight about what public art should do. Reducing it to a vote between three pre-existing options ignores everything that was said.

options.text_zh: 这面墙不画也行。白墙也挺好看的。
options.text_pinyin: Zhè miàn qiáng bú huà yě xíng. Bái qiáng yě tǐng hǎo kàn de.
options.text_en: We don't have to paint it. A blank wall is fine too.
